raw_text: ABERDEEN, 7TH EARL OF, creat. 1682; VISCT. FROMARTINE, BARON HADDON, METHLIC, TARVES and KELLE, 1682; Bart., 1642 (Scot.); VISCT. GORDON OF ABERDEEN, 1814 (U.K.), under which title he sits in the House of Lords; P.C. (1886); G.C.M.G. (1895); JOHN CAMPBELL HAMILTON-GORDON, 3rd son of the 5th Earl.—B. 1847; succeeded his brother, 1870; lord-lieut. of Aberdeenshire, 1880; high comsnr. to gen. assem. Church of Scotland, 1881-6; lord-lieut. of Ireland, Feb. to Aug., 1886; gov.-gen. of Canada, 1893 to 1898; representative of Canada on Pacific Cable Board, 1899 to 1900.

ACKROYD, JOHN HENRY.—Third clk. registry, sup. ct., Apr., 1854; clk. to 2nd puisne judge, Apr., 1855; admitted solr. of sup. ct., Mauritius, Apr., 1859; pol. and stip. mag. for the lesser dependencies of Mauritius, 1876; special comsnr. to investigate the treatment and state of the liberated Africans at Seychelles, May, 1881; dist. judge, Seychelles, 1882; dist. mag., Mauritius, Sept., 1884; ag. asst. ool. sec., 1885.

ADAMS, P. TARGETT.—Ed. at Epsom Medical Coll., 1874 to 1879, Guy's Hosp. and Durham Univ., 1881 to 1886; res. med. offr., Kent County Ophthalmic Hosp. (9 years), and a further 3 years as hon. full surgeon; for 10 years asst. med. offr. of health of Borough of Maidstone and deputy M.O.H. and asst. pub. analyst for County of Kent; med. offr., G.P.O., London, 1897; asst. M.O.H., Bombay, 1898-1900; returned to med. dept., G.P.O., London, 1900; govt. analyst and bacteriologist, O.R.C., June, 1903; and deputy M.O.H.

ADDERLEY, SIR AUGUSTUS JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1886), C.M.G. (1884).—B. 1835; formerly a mem. of the legis. coun., Bahamas; special comsnr. for the Bahamas and Jamaica at the International Fisheries Exhibn., 1883; roy. comsnr., Col. and Ind. Exhib., 1885, and exec. comsnr. for West Indies and Br. Honduras sections.
ADDISON, GLENTWORTH WALSH FRASER.—Asst. gold comsnr., northern dist., N.S. Wales, 1858; pol. mag., Tenterfield, 1869; pol. mag., Maitland, 1875; stip. mag., Sydney, 1882.

ADDISON, R. H.—Clk. and interp. to R.M., Umzini, Natal, 1876; to R.M., Lion's River, 1882; lieut. Zulu Carbineers, 1883; asst. comsnr. and R.M., Ndwandwe dist., Zululand, 1887; ditto, Nqutu dist., 1889; mag., Estcourt, 1899.
ADOLPHUS, G. A.—Supervisor of customs Gold Coast Col., 1890; asst. treasr., July, 1896; ag. on several occasions as dist. comsnr., Axim and C. C. Castle; asst. treasr., Northern Nigeria, July, 1900.

ADRIAN, F.O., C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1836; Entered C.O. Sept. 1863; asst. clk., 2nd class, 28th Dec., 1870; 1st class asst. clk., 26th Jan., 1877; supt. of the correspondence branch, gen. dep. and clk. for legal instruments, 1st Oct., 1880; apptd. offr. of arms of the Order of St. Michael and St. George, May, 1882; ret. 1901.
AGBEBI, D.—Warden Lagos prison, 1885; 4th clk., treas., 1886; examg. offr., customs, Aug., 1893; clk. paymr. dept., Ibadan, May, 1897.

AIKINS, THE HON. JAMES COX.—Ed. at Victoria Univ., Canada; returned to Canadian Assem. in 1864, where he sat until 1861; elected to the legis. coun., 1862, and at the union of the B.N.A. Provinces in 1867, was called to the Senate by roy. proclaim.; sworn of the privy coun., 9th Dec., 1869, and was sec. of state and regisr.-gen. of Canada from that date until the resign. of Sir John A. Macdonald, in Nov. 1873; re-apptd. to same office in Sir John's new administration, 19th Oct., 1878; became min. of inland rev.; lieut.-gov. of Manitoba and Keewatin, Dec. 1882-88.
AITKEN, C. COLQUHOUN.—3rd class clk., col. sec.'s office, Jamaica, Feb., 1878; 2nd class clk., gen. register office, 1878; 1st class clk., 1880.

AKERMAN, SIR JOHN WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1825; many years mem. of the legis. coun., Natal; served on the imigrn. board, coun. of educ., &c.; nominated to the exec. coun., 1870; deleg. to the S. African confec. on confederation in 1876; elected speaker of the legis. coun. in 1880; mem. of the native comsnr. of 1882; re-elected speaker in 1882; and again to the enlarged coun. of 1883; again after dissolution of 1886; again in 1890-1; deleg. to the Capetown customs confere., 1888; is a J.P.; ret. 1893.

AKTOYE, DANIEL.—Ed. C.M.S. Coll. Institute, Lagos; copyist to Queen's Advocate, Lagos, Aug. 1886; was fourteen years regisr. of instruments affecting land; dep. regisr. of births, deaths, marriages, and aliens, Jan. 1895; ag. regisr. at intervals; clk. to commt. of lands, Apr. 1901.

ALBURY, JOSEPH BENSON, M.D.—Mem. House of Assem., Bahamas, 1869; J.P., 1873; public vaccinator, 1881; and public physician, 1883; comsnr. Col. and Ind. Exhib., 1883; mem. legis. coun., 1889; med. inspec. of col., 1900.

ALDOWORTH, JOHN RICHARD OLIVER.—Ed. at Cheltenham College; second asst. collector and magistrate, Klang, 23rd March, 1889; passed in Malay, 11th July, 1889; asst. coll. and mag., Rawang, 11th July, 1889; first asst. coll. and mag., Klang, 1st December, 1889; asst. dist. officer, Kuala Selangor, 23rd December, 1889; ag. dist. officer, Kuala Selangor, 14th August, 1890; ag. treasurer of Pahang, 12th July to 12th November, 1892; dist. officer, Klang, 3rd July, 1893; dist. officer, Ulu Langat, 1st December, 1895; ag. collector of land revenue, Seremban, 17th Sept., 1896, to 30th Sept., 1897; examiner in Malay, Negri Sembilan, 1896; ag. collector of land revenue, Kuala Lumpur, and registrar of titles, Selangor, 15th February, 1898; collector of land revenue, Seremban, and registrar of titles, Negri Sembilan, 15th June, 1900; sec. to High Commissioner, Fed. Malay States, 25th Sept., 1901; dist. offr. Kuala Kangsar, Aug., 1903.

ALEXANDER, ARTHUR HARVEY.—Ed. Univ. Abdn.; sub-inspr., Jamaica constab., Oct. 1867; 3rd class inspr., Apr., 1869; 2nd class inspr., Nov., 1869; 1st class inspr., Oct., 1870; one of the comsnrs. to inquire into the management of prisons, 1873; agt.-gen. of imigrn., Oct., 1873; official mem. of legis. coun., 1882; imigrn. agt.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1884.
ALEXANDER, THOMAS.—Sub-inspr., Jamaica constab., Jan. 1872; 3rd class inspr., Oct. 1873; 2nd class inspr., Nov., 1876, now 1st cl. inspr.

ALLAN, THE HON. GEORGE WILLIAM, D.C.L.—Ed. at the Upper Canada Coll.; called to the bar of Upper Canada, 1846; mayor of Toronto, 1855; elected to the legis. coun. of Canada, 1858; summoned to the Senate of the Dominion of Canada, 1867; lieut.-col. of the Canadian Militia; Chancellor of the Univ. of Trin. Coll., Toronto; speaker, Senate, 1888-9.

ALLARDYCE, W. L., C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1861; clk. and interp. Provincial Dept., Fiji, 1879; transf. to Rotumah, 1882; stip. mag., July, 1882; ast. nativ. comannr. and stip. mag. and inspr. of nativ. taxes, Kadavu, Jan., 1890; mem. exec. coun., Nov., 1893; nativ. lands comsnr. and mem. legis. coun., 1894; nativ. comsnr., 1895; ast. col. sec. and receiv.-gen., Dec., 1898; deputy gov., 14-20 Feb., 1901; adminstrd. govt., 18th July, 1901, to 9th Sept., 1902; col. sec. and receiv.-gen., 18th July, 1902; gov., Falkland Is., 23rd May, 1904; editor of nativ. newspaper "Na Mata," 1890-99; author of a digest of Native Regulns., 1877-1900.

ALLDER, W. H.—5th clk., C.S.O., Barbados, 1892; 4th do., 1898; 3rd do., 1900; 2nd cls. clk., P.O., 1904; ag. clk. exec. coun. and comtee., Apr. to Oct., 1900, Apl. to July, 1902, Jan. to Oct., 1904; sec. to Barbados quarantine comsnr., 1903; also to W.I. quar. conference, 1904.
ALLDRIDGE, T. E. LESLIE.—Ed. at Rugby and Weimar, Germany; priv. sec. to Sir W. Roberts-Austen, K.C.B., Royal Mint, London, 1897 to 1899; 2nd-cl. supervisor of customs, Gold Coast Col., 1899.

ALLDRIDGE, THOMAS J., F.R.G.S., F.Z.S., &c.—Ag. U.S. consular agt. at S. Leone, 1871-2; travelling comsnr. for col.; hon. inspr. of pol., and J.P., 1889; pioneer to remote interior and concluded numerous treaties between British Govt. and paramount chiefs in Mendi Land; dist. comm. Sherbro, 1893; comm. of Court of Requests; accompanied H.E. Sir F. Carlewood, K.C.M.G., upon his first interior tour of 617 miles in 1894 as astronomical observer and topographer; medal and clasp for Mendi rising, Sherbro, 1888, mentioned in despatches; hon. corr. sec. Roy. Col. Inst.; awarded the Cuthbert Peek grant, 1900, by the R.G.S. for geographical work done during the past ten years in the hinterland of the colony; author of "The Sherbro and its Hinterland" (Macmillan), 1901.
ALLEN, GEORGE JAMES.—Entered C.O. Oct., 1895; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. 2nd div. Civil Ser. and re-assigned to C.O. Oct., 1898.
ALLEN, H. T.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil ser. and assigned to the C.O. Mar., 1898.

ALLMAN, ROBERT.—C.M.G. (1901); L.R.C.S.E., L.R.C.P.I.; Prin. med. offr., Oil Rivers Prot. (now So. Nigeria), Sept., 1891, and organized med. dept.; prin. med. offr., Cross, Eket, and Okrika expeds., 1895-1896; prin. med. offr., Prot. troops, Benin City expdn., 1897 (desps., medal and clasp and recd. thanks of S. of S.); prin. med. offr., Oron., 1897; Ekuri, 1897-8 (desps.); Ubium and Ishan expeds., 1899; Aro expdn., 1901-2 (medal and clasp); complimented by S. of S. (late Marquis of Salisbury) on efficient organization of med. dept., 1897; mem. of comtee. at C.O. for re-orgniztn. of W. African med. staff, Oct., 1901, and recd. thanks of S. of S.

ALLT, ALLEN BRAVO.—Ch. clk. bond-w'house. cust. dept. Brit. Guiana, Sept., 1884; 4th clk. offr. Jan. 1886; 3rd, May, 1886; 2nd Oct., 1883; sub. comptlr. Berbice, harbrnr. and regist. of shipping, July, 1897; ch. clk. May, 1898; ag. comptlr. of cust. June to July, 1899.

ALLWOOD, JAMES.—Second clk., gov't's sec.'s office, Jamaica, July, 1862; ag. 2nd clk. in the exec. comttee. office, Feb., 1865, to May, 1866; clk. imigrn. dept., May to Oct., 1866; 1st class clk., finance office, Oct., 1866, to Dec., 1869; supervisor of dist. post offices, Oct., 1871, to Mar., 1876; afterwards ast. col. sec.

ALLWOOD, JAMES, C.M.G. (1903).—Solr. of sup. ct., Jamaica, and dep. clk. of the peace, parish of St. Mary, June, 1866; clk. of the peace, parish of St. Elizabeth, Oct., 1868; clk., Savannah Mar dist. ct., Nov., 1868; ditto, Montego Bay, Jan., 1869; also clk. of the circuit ct., July, 1875; clk., dist. ct., Jan., 1880; 1st class clk., col. sec. office, Nov., 1885; R.M., 1888; now collr. gen.

AMIRAYAN, G. G.—Called to the bar, Inner Temp., 1894; clk. and translator, consmr.'s office, Larnaca, Cyprus, Oct., 1878; translator, high ct. of justice and ct. of Temiyiz, 1881; registr., sup. ct., 1883; was sec. to consm. apptd. in 1888 to inquire into organizm. of Cyprus pol.
AMORY, H. W.—Extra rev. offr., St. Kitts, 1893; rev. offr., quarantine offr., and clk. for bd. of health, Nevis, 1895; cashier, treasy., St. Kitts, 1897.

AMPTHILL, 2nd BARON, creat. 1881, OLIVER ARTHUR VILLIERS RUSSELL.—Son of the 1st Baron; born 1869; succeeded his father, 1884; G.C.I.E., 1900. Ed. at Eton and New Coll., Oxon. Rowed in the Oxford eight, 1889-91; pres. of the Oxford Union, 1891; B.A., 1891; M.A., 1900; ast. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, Sec. of State forCols., June, 1895; priv. sec., June, 1897; one of the British delegates to the Internat. Sugar Conference, Brussels, May and June, 1898; lieut. Roy. 1st Devon Yeomanry Cavalry; capt. 3rd batt. Bedfordshire Regt.; J.P., and prov. grand master of Freemasons, Bedfordshire; Governor of Madras, Oct., 1900.

ANDERSON, CHARLES OSBORNE.—Indentured clk., pub. hosp., Bahamas, May, 1885; 2nd clk., G.P.O., Apr., 1888; ch. clk., July, 1890; ag. postmr., 1892; elected mem. legis. assem. 1895; re-elected 1896 for the septennial term; ag. res. justice and collr. of rev. dist. of Inagua, from Oct., 1896, to Feb., 1898.

ANDERSON, CHARLES WILGUS.—4th Gov't. survr., B. Guiana, 1887; govt. offr. (mining regns.), and J.P., 1890; ditto, dept. of mines, in charge No. 2 dist., 1893; govt. survr. in charge Kaieteur Conglomerate prospection expdn., May, 1895 (published map); 1st cls. gov't. survr., 1896; ag. ast. crown survr., Nov., 1900; govt. survr. attchd. to B. Guiana boundary consmr., Sept., 1901; on geological survey with Prof. J. B. Harrison, C.M.G., Nov., 1902; junior consmr. (to act with H. I. Perkins, I.S.O.) for delimitation of B. Guiana boundary, Sep., 1903.

ANDERSON, SIR JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1901), C.M.G. (1898).—B. 1858; M.A., Aberdeen, 1877; 1st class honours in mathematics; gold medal as most distinguished graduate of year; 2nd class clk., C.O., 30th June, 1879; Bacon scholar, Gray's Inn, 1887; Inns of Court studentship, 1888; joint consmr. with the late Sir J. F. Dickson, Nov. and Dec., 1891, to inquire into certain matters connected with the registry of the sup. court of Gibraltar; priv. sec. to Sir R. Meade, 17th Aug., 1892; attached to the staff of the Br. agent for the Behring Sea Arbitration in London and Paris, 1892-93; 1st class clk., 11th Mar., 1896; prin. clk., 3rd June, 1897; sec. to the conference between Mr. Chamberlain and the Colonial Premiers, June and July, 1897; visited Gibraltar to inquire into rates of pay in the civ. serv., May, 1899; apptd. C. O. representative on staff of H.R.H. the Duke of Cornwall and York, during his visit to the Colonies, 1901; sec. to Colonial Conference, 1902; received thanks of Canadian Gov't. and Confederation Med. for services in connection with Alaska Bound., 1903; govt. Straits Settlements, 1st Feb., 1904.
ANDERSON, JOHN GERARD, I.S.O.(1903), M.A.—Under sec. for public instruction, Queensland, Nov., 1878.

ANDERSON, CAPT. P. W.—Ed. privately and at St. Andrews University; entered the army as 2nd lieut. Derbyshire regt., 1888; lieut., 1890; capt., 1893; retired with gratuity, 1902; Lushai expdttn., 1890 (medal with clasp); expdttn. to India, 1901 (medal with clasp); ast. inspr. of signalling in Bengal and the Punjab, 1892-94; adjutant of aux. forces, 1895-1900; brigade maj., W.A.F.F., Aug., 1900, to Feb., 1902; apptd. 2nd cls. res., N. Nig., June, 1902; ag. sec. to the adminstr., Aug., 1902.

ANDERSON, SIR WILLIAM JOHN, KT. BACH. (1896).—B. 1846; ed. at Pembroke Coll., Oxford; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, Nov., 1869; judge of the sup. ct., Turks Islands, Feb., 1874; dist. judge, Jamaica, 1886; R.M., Kingston and St. Andrews, 1888; ch. justice, Br. Honduras, 1890; ch. justice, Trinidad, 1900; ret., 1903.

ANDERSON, RAIF WILLIAM.—Ed. at Marlborough and in Germany. Asst. examr. of accounts, audr.-gen.'s off., O.R.C., Sept., 1901; ch. clk. and senior examr. of accts., audr.-gen.'s office, 1st July, 1902; ast.-auditor, 1st Dec., 1902.

ANDREWS, MARTINDALE STEWART.—Ed. at Merchant Taylors' schl.; Eastern Tel. Co.'s staff, Alexandria, 1882; African dir. tel. co., Gambia, June, 1886; supt., Accra, Feb., 1887; dir. of tels., G. Coast, June, 1891; offl. mem. and vice-chrmn., Accra town coun., 1898; dir. of tels. with Ashanti Field Force, June to Dec., 1900 (medal, ment. in despe.); ag. P.M.G., Jan. to June, 1901, also from Apr. to Oct., 1904; title changed to ast. P.M.G., Jan., 1905.

ANGERS, HON. AUGUSTE REAL.—Solr.-gen., Quebec, 1874-6; atty.-gen., 1876-8; leader of upper house for three years; then puisne judge, super. ct., Quebec; lieut.-gov., Quebec, 1887; senator and min. of agricult., Canada, Dec., 1892, to 1895.
ANGOVE, THOMAS.—Probation clk., audit office, W. Australia, July, 1880; 2nd clk., Treasy., 1886.

ANSELL, WILLIAM JAMES DAVID.—Ast. supt. of port, Larnaca, Cyprus, March, 1880; ag. supt. of port Limassol, June to Sept., 1880, Apr. to Dec., 1881; tide surv. and dep. harbmr., Dec., 1881; passed exam. in Modern Greek, 1885; govt. and municipal inspr. of weights and measures, Apr., 1892, to Aug., 1898; 2nd div. clk. and ast. collr. of customs and excise, Mar., 1894; acted collr. of customs and excise, July to Nov., 1894; May to Oct., 1895, Nov., 1896, to Nov., 1897; ast. consmr., Nov. 1896, June, 1897, Nov., 1897; collr. of customs and excise, May, 1898; is also supt. of port, receiver of wreck, and reg. of shipping.

ANSON, MAJOR-GENERAL SIR ARCHIBALD EDWARD HARBORD, R.A., C.M.G. (1876), K.C.M.G. (1882).—B. 1826; entered the royal mil. acad., Woolwich, 1841; presented with sword for exemplary conduct; 2nd lieut., R.A., June, 1844; 1st lieut., 1st Apr., 1846; 2nd capt., 9th July, 1852; 1st capt., 1st Sept., 1855; brevet major, 21st July, 1864; lieut.-col., 12th May, 1865; brevet col., 12th May, 1874; col., 23rd Dec., 1875; ret. with the hon. rank of maj.-gen., 26th August, 1879; served at the siege of Sebastopol in 1855; Crimean medal and clasp, 5th class Medjidie, and Turkish medal; inspr.gen. of pol., Mauritius, Sept., 1858; was employed in 1862 as the responsible mem. of the Br. mission to the court of Radama II. of Madagascar, on the occasion of his coronation; lieut.-gov. of Prince of Wales Is., Feb., 1867; admstg. govt. Str. St. Llntts. 1871, 1877, and 1879; conducted the successful military operations for repelling the invasion of the state of Sungei Ujong, 1875-76 (medal and clasp); ret. 1882; J.P. for Sussex.

ANSON, E. R.—Asst. comsry. of taxation, Br. Guiana, 1881; dep. comsry. and inspr. of weights and measures, 1884; stip. mag. N.W. dist., 1890; ag. govt. agt. N.W. dist., Sept. 1898; stip. mag., Corentyne coast dist., Feb., 1901.

ANSON, FRANK CHARLES MONTRÉOR.—Asst. comsry. of taxn., Br. Guiana, May, 1881; dist. comsry. and inspr. of weights and measures, June, 1884; sec. to road arrears coman., 1889; mem. of coman. to inquire into and assess damages caused by riots, 1890; J.P., 1892; chairman bd. of examiners under weighers and gaugers ordinance, 1883; mem. cent. bd. of health and excise bd., and ag. ch. comsry., 1892-3, 1893-4, 1894-5; ag. stip. mag. Centl. Demerara judicial dist., Br. Guiana, Aug., 1898; capt. Br. Guiana Militia, 1896, and obtained certif. for drill and musketry from schls. of instruc., Chelsea Bks., Lon., and Hythe; Oct., 1898, ag. stip. mag. North-West Dist., Br. Guiana; May, 1900, ag. stip. mag. Sth Ess. River Jud. Dist., and pres. P.L.Bd., Wake-naam, Br. Guiana; Feb., 1901, treasurer and comptroller of customs, St. Lucia, ex officio member of exec. coun. and legislature; chrmn. quarantine authority, and registrar of shipping; treas. and mem. of ex. and leg. couns., Lagos, May, 1903.

ANTHONISZ, JAMES OLIVER.—Cadet, S. Stltlments, Oct., 1883; B.A., St. John's Coll., Camb. (Senior Optime); asst. Indian immigr. agt., Singapore, May, 1886; 3rd mag., Singapore, 1888; 2nd ditto, July, 1892; ag. official assignee and regisr. of deeds, Singapore, Oct., 1894; and Apr., 1897; ag. 1st mag., July, 1895; offl. assignee and regisr. of deeds, May, 1897; prn. munic. comsrs., Sing., Jan., 1901; comsrs., court of requests, Sing., 1st Jan., 1902; 1st magis., Sing., Aug., 1902, but continued to act as pres. of the munic. comsrs.; seconded for 1 year as pres. munic. comsrs., 1st Jan., 1903; ag. treas., Sept., 1904.

ANTHONISZ, RICH. GERALD.—Served in educ. dept. and regisr.-genl.'s dept., Ceylon; asst. regisr.-genl., 23rd Feb., 1892; seconded for serv. as examiner of Dutch records, 15th July, 1899; archivist and librarian, 1st Jan., 1902.

ANTON, GEORGE.—Cik., audit office, Grenada, Nov., 1872; 2nd cik. col. sec.'s office, Apr., 1878; ag. ch. cik., Apr. to Sept., 1879, and again in 1880; treasy. accntnt., St. Vincent, Jan., 1881; ag. auditor, Aug., 1888, to end of 1889; regisr. sup. ct., Dec., 1891; J.P., 30th July, 1892; regisr.-genl., 1st Jan., 1898; ag. treas. and paymaster, and mem. ex. and leg. coun., 10th June to 14th Nov., 1899; offl. mem., leg. coun., 8th Jan., 1901; coroner, 1st dist., 1st Apr., 1902; mag. 1st dist., 10th June, 1902.

ANTROBUS, REGINALD L., C.B. (1898).—B. 1853; ed. at Winchester and at New Coll., Oxford; scholar of New Coll., 1872; 1st class in classical mods., 1874; 2nd class in final classical school, 1876; B.A., 1876; apptd., after an open compet. exam., to be a cik. in the C.O., May 3rd, 1877; asst. priv. sec. to the Earl of Kimberley, 5th Dec., 1880, to Dec., 1882; priv. sec. to the Earl of Derby, 16th Dec., 1882, to 24th June, 1885; to Col. the Right Hon. F. A. Stanley, M.P. (now Earl of Derby), 24th June, 1885, to 6th Feb., 1886; and to Earl Granville, 6th Feb. to 3rd Aug., 1886; adminstr. the govt. of St Helena, 5th Nov., 1889, to 8th June, 1890; priv. sec. to Mr. Meade, 1st Feb., 1892, and to Mr. S. Buxton, M.P., 17th Aug., 1892; sen. cik., Jan., 1894; princ. cik., 1896; asst. under sec. of state, Oct., 1898.

APPLEYARD, CHARLES W.—Apptd., after compet. exam., to be a cik., 2nd div., civ. ser., and assigned to C.O., Mar. 14th, 1893; matric., Lond., 1895; temporary staff officer in aects. branch, 1900; ag. col. secretary, etc., Falklands, Apr., 1900, to Feb., 1901; returned to C.O., Mar., 1901; assist. acctnt. to high commissioner for South Africa, Nov. 30, 1901.

APTHORP, MAJOR K. P.—Ed. at Charterhouse; lieut. Royal Irish, 1881; capt., 1888; major, 1889; Nile expedn., 1885 (medal, with clasp and Khedive's star); Black Mountain expedn., 1888 (medal with clasp); S. African war, 1900-1 (medal with two clasps, King's medal, ment. in despatches); A.D.C. to lieut.-gov., Punjab, 1889-1891; S.S.O., Lucknow, 1892-1894; adjutant, Oudh Light Horse, 1894-1899; dist. comsrs., O.R.C., 1900; S.A. constab., 1901; sec., land settm. dept., O.R.C., 1st Oct., 1901.

ARCHER, F. BISSETT.—Prin. cik. col. sec.'s office, G. Coast, Mar., 1894; ch. cik., Jan., 1896; ag. asst. col. sec., Aug., 1896; cik. of coun., Sept., 1896; lieut. G. Coast certif. volrs., 1895; ag. adjt. June to Oct., 1896; F.O. certif., Wellington Barracks, 1897; asst. col. sec. Lagos, June, 1897; compiled Lagos offl. handbk.; in charge of the secretariat on many occasions, 1898-1902; ag. treas., Jan. to May, 1901; and mem. of ex. and legis. couns.; treas., Gambia, Jan., 1903; mem. of ex. and legis. couns., and J.P. of the colony; ag. col. sec., Feb. to Aug., 1903.
ARCHER, T., C.M.G. (1884).—B. 1823; ag.-gen. for Queensland, 1882 to 1884; reapptd. 13th June, 1888; resig. 1890.

ARCHER, P. L. H.—Ent. pub. service correspde. branch, col. sec.'s off., Barbados, 1883; promoted gen. post-office, 1884; customs, 1890; 2nd cls. supervisor, G. Coast, June, 1902; ag. ch. regisr. and sheriff, G. Coast, 11th Dec., 1902, to 8th June, 1903.

ARGYLL, Duke of, P.C., K.T., G.C.M.G.—Marquis of Lorne, The Rt. Hon. John Douglas Sutherland Campbell—Born 1845; married, 1871, H.R.H. Princess Louise Caroline Alberta, 4th daughter of H.M. the late Queen; sat in the House of Commons for Argyllshire from Feb., 1868, to Sept., 1878; priv. sec. to his father, the Duke of Argyll, at the India Office, Dec., 1868; is author of poetical and other works, including "Trip to the Tropics;" gov. gen. of Canada, 1878 to 1883; M.P. for S. Manchester, 1895; succeeded his father, 1900.

ARMBRISTER, PERCY W. D.—Ag. regisr. prothonotary, and cik. of Crown, Bahamas, 1884; ag. ch. cik., col. secs. dept., 1886; cik., pol. ct. Nassau, 1887; J.P., 1890; res. mag. and collr. of rev., Abaco, 1890; Eleuthera, 1894, Harbour Is., 1895, Inagua, 1896; ag. stip. and circuit mag., Bahamas, and judge of ct. of com. pleas., 1896 and 1897; regisr. of cts. prothonotary, and cik. of Crown, Jan., 1897; chairman of comtee. of prisons; presides in cases of marine casualties.
ARMOUR, Hon. J. D.—Pres. high ct. and ch. just., King's Bench, Ontario; ch. just. of Ont., and ch. just. of appeal, 1900; puisne judge, supr. ct., Canada, 1902.
ARNOLD, W. J. J.—Royal Univ., Ireland; B.A., 1887; M.B., 1894; civ. surgeon attached to R.A.M.C., St. Helena and S. Africa, 1900 to 1903; apptd. col. surgeon, St. Helena, Apl., 1903.

ARROWSMITH, W. C. G.—Apptd. 8th clk. G.P.O., Jamaica, May, 1866; 6th clk., audit off., Aug., 1869; promoted to 1st clk., Nov., 1870; acted as ch. elk. in aud. off., Feb., 1874, to Apl., 1875, and Mar. to July, 1877; apptd. clk. ch. June, 1884; deputed for spec. work of inspecting parochial acts., Oct., 1888; acted as aud. gen. on sev. occasions.

ARUNACHALAM, P.—Ed. Acad. Coll., Colombo, Ceylon, and Christ's Coll., Camb., of which he was a scholar; B.A. 1875; M.A. 1879; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1875; writer Ceylon civ. serv., Apr., 1875; comsrr. of requests, pol. mag., 1876; dist. judge, June, 1887; comsrr. of requests, Colombo, 1892; dist. judge, Kurunegala, Sept., 1897; regisr.-gen., Jan., 1898; supt. of census, Mar. 1900; officer of class I, dist. judge, Kurunegala, 23rd June, 1903.

ASHBURNHAM, JOHN ANCHITEL.—Clk. to admstr., Brit. Bechuana-land, Oct., 1885; sec. to Bech. admstr., Feb., 1888; accompanied high comsrr. to conference with pres., S.A.R., at Blignaut's Pont, Mar., 1890, and at Coleberg, Apl., 1893; sec. to concessions comsrr. for Bech. Prot., May, 1893; C.C. and R.M. at Taungs, Brit. Bech., Feb., 1894; C.C. and R.M. at Gordonia, June, 1894; asst. comsrr. for Bech. Prot., Nov., 1896; R.M., Bloemfontein, Sep., 1901; chrmn., land laws enquiry coman., O.R.C., Oct., 1901; ag. col. sec., O.R.C., Apl., 1904.

ASHLEY, RT. Hon. ANTHONY EVELYN MELBOURNE, P.C. (1896).—Son of the 7th Earl of Shaftesbury; born 1836; ed. at Harrow and Trin. Coll., Camb.; M.A., 1858; called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn, 1863; was for some time sec. to the late Lord Palmerston; is a mag. for Dorset, and was treas. of county cts. of Dorset from 1863 to 1874; is 2nd church estates comsrr.; parly. sec. to the Board of Trade, Apr., 1880; parly. under-sec. of state for the cols., 12th May, 1882, to 24th June, 1886; sat in the House of Commons for Poole from May, 1873, to Mar., 1880, and for the Isle of Wight from Apr., 1880, to Nov., 1885; is author of a Life of Lord Palmerston.

ASHMORE, ALEX. MURRAY, C.M.G. (1900).—B. 1856; writer, Ceylon Service, 1876; pol. mag., Panvila, 1878; office asst. gov't. agent, Western Provinces, 1883; ditto, Central Province, 1884; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., 1887; comsrr. of requests and pol. mag., Kandy, 1891; ag. col. sec., G. Coast, Feb., 1894; rec-gen., Cyprus, Mar., 1895; ag. ch. sec., June to Nov., 1896; Br. delegate of Evcaf, 1897; served on Transvaal Concessions coman. in S. Africa and Lond., 1900-1; govt. sec., Br. Guiana, 1900; admstl. govt., 1901.

ATCHLEY, C., I.S.O. (1902).—B. 1852; entered the C.O., Aug., 1868; served in ch. clk.'s, acctnt's, and gen. depts.; asst. clk., 2nd class, 1875; supt. of the library, 1st Oct., 1880; mem. libr. comttee, Imp. Instit., 1891; correps. mem. of Instt. Colonial Universal, 1892; librarian, C.O., 1900.

ATKINSON, JOHN MITFORD, M.B. (Lond.), 1881; M.R.C.S. (Eng.) and L.S.A., 1878, D.P.H. (Camb.), 1894.—Honours at Pret. Sc. M.B., 1896; prize man at London Med. Coll., 1896-1897; res. med. offr. St. Mary Abbot's Infirmary, Kensington, 1878-1888; med. offr. No. 3 dist. St. Mary Abbott's, Kensington 1885-1887; supt. govt. civil hospital, Hong Kong; med. offr. to small-pox hospital and to the govt. lunatic asylum, 1887; J.P. 1890; ag. col. surg., 1895; prin. civ. med. offr., 1897; received thanks of S. of S. for services during plague, 1898; pres. of the Sanitary Board 1897-1900, and again from August, 1901.

ATKINSON, THOS. HENRY.—Cadet, destitute poor dept., S. Aust., 1875; clk., 1878; 2nd visiting offr., 1884; 1st ditto, 1886; dep. supt. of dest. asylum, and visiting offr., 1889; supt. of dest. asylum, and sec. to bd., 1897; chrmn. of dest. bd., 1904.

ATTRILL, GEORGE.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of lower (now 2nd) div. of civ. ser., and assigned to C.O. June, 1884; asst. col. sec., G. Coast, Oct., 1893; ag. ch. assist. col. sec. Mar. to Oct., 1896, Dec., 1897, to June, 1898, Dec., 1898, to June, 1899; clk. of coun. Feb. to Sept., 1896, Nov., 1897, to June, 1898; comptroller of cust., Sept., 1899; acted as col. sec., and mem. of ex. and leg. councils, July, 1899; Sept. to Dec., 1899; Sept. to Dec., 1900; and July to Dec., 1901.

AUBER, STEVEN J.—Temporary outdoor offr. custome, S. Leone, June, 1878; tide waiter, Aug., 1879; landing waiter, Sep., 1880; sen. ag. measuring survr. of shipping, Aug., 1882; clk. of customa, Gambia, 1883; ch. clk. and cashier, 1892.
AUBERT, EDGAR, C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1850; poor law comsrr., Mauritius, 1892; mem. coun. of educm., 1892, of bd. of health, 1894; nominated mem. coun. of govt., 1896.
AUCKLAND, BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. M. R. NELIGAN.—Ed. Reading schl. and Trin. Coll., Dub.; B.A., 1884; deacon, 1886; priest, 1887; formerly Vicar of St. Stephen's, Westbourne Park.

AUSTIN, HENRY BOASE.—Ed. at Grey Coll., Bloem., O.R.C.; entered late O.F.S. serv. as clk. to surv. gen., 11th Oct., 1889; ch. clk., deeds reglay. off., 28th Dec., 1897; apptd. military reglar. of deeds, O.R.C., 15th Nov., 190C; asst. reglar. of deeds, 20th Dec., 1901; ag. reglar. of deeds, July to Dec., 1901.

AUSTIN, REV. PRESTON BRUCE.—1st div. Lond. matric. and deacon (Durh.), 1888; priest, 1890; asst. master Huddersfield Coll., Yorks, 1884-88; curate Georgetown Cathedral, Br. Guiana, 1888, and of Demerara River dist. 1892; ag. asst. master, Queen's Coll., Georgetown, 1889; J.P. for the Col., 1893; supt. of missions in Demerara and Essequebo river dists., 1897 to 1904; headmaster, Cape Coast govt. schl., G. Coast, May, 1904; ag. col. chaplain, 1904.

AYLIFFE, GEORGE HAMILTON.—Pol. trooper, S. Austral., Feb., 1859; clk. in G.P.O., Apr., 1866; clk. in pol. comsrr.'s office, May, 1866; inspr. under cent. bd. of health, May, 1875; sec. cent. bd. of health, May, 1883, to Aug., 1904; retg. offr. under Electoral Act (Dist. E. Adelaide), Dec., 1884, to Aug., 1903; registr.-gen. of births, deaths and marriages, Dec., 1886; is a J.P.; retg. offr. for the state, and retg. offr. for central dist. for leg. coun., Aug., 1903; also vaccination offr. and inspr. of anatomy, Aug., 1904.
AYTON, A. L.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. 2nd div. civil ser. and assigned to science and art dept., Mar., 1899; to C.O. July, 1899.
BACKHOUSE, ALFRED P.—Coron. prosecutor, N.W. dist., N.S. Wales, Oct., 1878; ditto, S. dist., Jan., 1881; ct. dist. judge, N.W. dist., 1884.

BADELEY, FRANCIS JOSEPH.—Ed. Clergy Orphan School, Canterbury, and Jesus Coll., Camb.; sen. opt. math. tripos, 1889; Hong Kong cadet, Oct., 1890; passed cadet, Jan., 1893; ag. dep. supt. of pol., June to Dec., 1893; ag. asst. postmr.-gen., May to July, 1894; ag. asst. registr.-gen., July, 1894; J.P., 1894; dep. supt. of pol. and asst. supt. fire brigade, July, 1895; capt. of pol., supt. fire brigade, and supt. Victoria gool., Apl., 1902; mem. of leg. coun., 1902.

BAILEY, ARTHUR W.—Ed. Dulwich Coll. and St. Cath. Coll., Camb. (scholar); 2nd sen. opt. math. tripos; B.A. 1895; cadet S.S., Nov., 1896; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Penang, Feb., 1899; passed fin. exam. in Chinese, June, 1899; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Sing., Mar., 1901; 4th mag. Singapore, May, 1901; 3rd mag. Penang, Jan., 1902; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Penang, Feb., 1902; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Sing., Aug., 1902, and June, 1904; dist. offr., Xmas. Is., 1st June, 1904.

BAILEY, DEMETRIUS J. S., B.A. (Camb.).—Served in Methuen's Horse; cadet Sarawak Service, Mar., 1888; asst. res., May, 1891; res., 2nd class, 2nd div., Jan., 1894; asst. res., May, 1891; res., 2nd cl., Jan., 1894.
BAILEY, EDWARD S.—2nd clk. col. treas., Barbados, 1880; ch. clk. 1892; acted as clk. to asst. ct. of appeal in 1892 and other occasions; ag. sec. emigrn. comsn., Oct., 1893; ag. col. treas., 1894.

BAILEY, WILLIAM H.—Entered the service June, 1875, as 3rd clk. customs dep., Barbados; promoted 2nd clk. Nov., 1876; ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, and clk. of legis. coun., May, 1882; acted for five months in 1892 as gov., Glendairy Prison; ag. audit.-gen., June, 1893, to Apr., 1894, Aug. to Dec., 1895, and July to Nov., 1896; ag. aud.-gen. May to Oct., 1897, and from June, 1898; registr., Jan., 1898; col. postmr., 1900.

BAKER, SIR RICHARD C., K.C.M.G. (1895); C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1842; ed. at Eton and Trin. Coll., Camb.; B.A. 1864; M.A. 1870; called to the bar, Linc.'s Inn, 1864; M.H.A., S. Australia, 1868; atty.-gen., 1870-1; M.L.C. since 1887; min. of educn. and leader of coun., 1884-5; special envoy from Austral. cols. to U.K. to negotiate Postal Union, 1885-6; member of Austral. fed. convention, Sydney, 1891, and chairman of Austral. fed. convention, Adelaide, Sydney, and Melbourne, 1896-7; pres. L.C., 1893 to 1901; mem. of Sth. Austral. Parl., 1901; pres. sen., 1901.

BALL, JAMES DYER, M.R.A.S.—Ed. King's Coll., Lond.; employed in registr.-gen.'s office, Hong Kong, Feb., 1875; in mag.'s, Mar., 1875; in educn. dept., Mar., 1876; passed in Chinese, ag. asst. interp. of Chinese, sup. ct., Oct., 1878; ch. interp., 1881; dep. marshal, V.A. ct. in 1881 and 1882; commr. of oaths, 1881; employed in consular ct., Canton, in connection with Logan case, 1883; judge's clk. sup. ct., Mar., 1890; ag. dept.-regir. sup. ct., Nov., 1894; marshal, Nov., 1894; ag. registr.-gen., Sept., 1898; mem. bd. of examns., Sept., 1898; ag. asst. registr.-gen. and dep. registr. of marriages, Feb., 1899; author of "Things Chinese," "The Cantonese made Easy" series, "Hakka made Easy," and other books on Chinese languages.
BALLANTINE, DAVID.—Sub. collr. customs, Br. N. Guinea, July, 1889; mag. for native affairs, Nov., 1890; collr. of customs, postmr., and treas., Dec., 1893.
BALLARD, H., C.M.G. (1901).—Capt. of the port and shipping master, Durban, Natal; ret., 1904.

BANBURY, GEORGE ALEXANDER LETHBRIDGE.—Apptd. to Legacy and Succession Duty Office, London, May, 1873 (upper grade), after compet. exam.; asst. col. sec. and treas., S. Leone, May, 1880; acted as postmr. from 1880 to 1882; ag. collr. of customs, 1882; ag. col. sec. and treas. and mem. of exec. and legis. couns., 1883 to 1884; asst. col. sec. and treas. offr., St. Helena, 1884; ag. col. sec. and mem. of exec. coun., Mar., 1885; collr. of dues and taxes, Seychelles, Sept., 1886; collr., treas., and manager of savings bank, 1889; registr.-gen. of Mauritius, Sept., 1899; provisional nom. mem. coun. of govt., June, 1900.

BAPTISTE, GEORGE ALBERT CLARENCE.—Ed. City Schl., Patna, and Patna Coll.; matric., Calcutta Univ., Dec., 1867; asst. commr., Patna div., Bengal; sub-dep. mag. and collr., Tirhoot, 1872; in 1873 passed the test in Hindustani, surveying and engineering, and law; salt supt., Chumparan, and special dep. collr. for irrig. purposes, Patna, Shahabad, and Gaya, 1873; in 1874 passed the higher exam. in law and Hindustani; 1875, dep. mag. and collr., Patna, but continued on special duty; 1877, on special duty for the acquisition of land for the Tirhoot State Rly.: stip. mag., Mauritius, July, 1877.

BARBADOS, BISHOP OF (founded 1825), RIGHT REV. WILLIAM PROCTOR SWABY.—Late Barry schl. and Dur. Exhib., Hatf. Hall, Durham; B.A. 1873, M.A. 1876, B.D. 1887, D.D. 1890; Cons. Lord Bish. of Guiana, 1893; transf. to Barbados, 1899; V. of Castletown, co. Durham, 1874-84; V. of St. Mark, Millfield, co. Dur. 1884-93.

BARKER, ALFRED J. GLANVILLE, M.R.C.S. Eng., 1884; certif. medico-psychol. assoc.—Ed. Univ. Coll. and Hosp.; 1st M.B., Lon.; late res. clin. asst. E. Lond. Hosp. for Children; house-surg., Tiverton Infr., Cent. Lond. Ophthalmic Hosp.; res. clin. asst., Bethlehem Royal Hosp.; temp. asst. med. offr., Surrey Co. Asyl., Tooting; ag. col. surg. and res., Penang, S. Stiltna, 1896; J.P. and coroner, Penang, 1896; prin. med. offr., Sarawak, May, 1897.

BARKER, GENERAL SIR G. DIGBY, K.C.B. (1900), C.B. (1888).—B. 1833; Ensign 78th Highlanders, 1853; adj. 1858; col. in the army, 1887; maj.-gen., 1887; lieut.-gen., 1895; gen., 1900; served in Persian war, 1857 (medal with clasp); in Indian Mutiny, 1857-8, including battle of Cawnpore; relief, defence, and capture of Lucknow (as D.A.Q.M.G.) (mentioned in despatches, medal with two clasps, brevet maj.), and a year's service; graduated Staff Coll., 1866; special service at army headqrs., 1867-8; D.A.Q.M.G., S. dist., 1867-73; prof. of mil. art. and history at the Staff Coll., 1874-6; asst. dir. of mil. educn. at army headqrs., 1877-83; A.A.G. and A.Q.M.G., western dist., 1884-87; commanded the troops in China and Hong Kong, 1890-95; ag. gov. of H. Kong, May to Dec., 1891; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Bermudas, 1896-1901.

BARLOW, G. PRATT.—Cadet, Sarawak civil service, May, 1878; res. (2nd class), 3rd div., Nov., 1886; res. Muka, Oct., 1890; ag. res. Baram, July, 1892; of Bintulu, Aug., 1893; res. Lower Rejang, Oct., 1894.
BARNARD, JAMES.—Ent. govt. service, 1864; sec. for customs, Tasmania, 1894.

BARNES, DOUGLAS DRURY.—Ag. sub. inspr. of pol., Br. Guiana, Sept., 1886; 2nd clk., inspr.-gen. office, Jan., 1887; acted as inspr. of pol. on several occasions; dist. inspr. of pol., Aug., 1891; Hythe extra certificate, Sept., 1891; attached to R.I.C. Depôt, Dublin, for two months in 1891; taken prisoner by Venezuelans while in command of Ururan Station, Cuyuni River, Jan., 1896; supt. of pol., Br. Honduras, June, 1895; J.P. for the col.; ag. dist. comsnr. Belize on various occasions from Feb., 1899, to Oct., 1900; capt. commdg. Br. Honduras Vols., Dec., 1897; major commdg., Jan., 1899; rec. Diamond Jubilee med. 1897; certificates of proficiency for captains and field officers, with special distinction, schl. of instruction, Chelsea Barracks, Feb., 1903; ag. regir. gen. and regir. of sup. ct., July, 1903.

BARNES, JOHN FREDERICK EVELYN, C.M.G. (1901), M.I.C.E.; M.I.C.E. Ire.—Asst. county survr., Antrim, 1872; of Abercorn Estates, 1873-9; gov't. survr., Natal, 1880; Durban-boro. engrnr. 1882; asst. col. engrnr. and surv. gen. Natal, 1888; ag. col. engrnr., with seats in exec. and legis. couns. 1889-90; engrnr., P.W. dept., 1894; ch. engrnr. of P.W., 1897.

BARNES-LAWRENCE, L. A. W., R.N.—Mid. of "Pearl," 1875, in an attack at Santa Cruz, S. Pacific, which resulted in the death of Commodore Goodenough; sub-lieut. of "Seagull," 1876, during expdn. up the river Gambia, W. Africa, to quell native disturbances; served in Nav. Brig. from "Active" during Kaffir war, 1877-8; sent for service under the Chinese govt., 1879; lieut. of "Iris" throughout the Egyptian campaign, 1882; 1st lieut. of "Kingfisher," 1884-8; employed slave cruising on E. Coast Africa and Persian Gulf; in comd. of "Swift" at Hanbow during missionary riots, 1892; comdr. naval intell. dept., Sept., 1892, to Dec., 1895; in comd. of "Rupert," Dec., 1896; capt. of the port and supt. of lights, Gibraltar, June, 1898; harbr. mast. and M.L.C., H. Kong, 1904.

BARNES, WALTER STANLEY, M.D., M.R.C.S., L.S.A.—Asst. med. sup. Lunatic Asyl., Berbice, 1888; ag. med. sup. 1890; med. sup. Leper Asylums, 1891; asst. res. surg., Georgetown Hosp., 1893; res. surg., 1896.

BARNES, WARREN DELABERE, B.A.—Ed. King's Coll. Sch. and Pemb. Coll., Camb., cadet, S.S., Nov., 1888; passed final exam. in Chinese, Nov., 1891; 3rd mag., Penang, 1893; ag. prot. of Chinese, Perak, Oct., 1893; warden of mines, Perak, Jan., 1896; warden of mines, prot. of Chinese and sen. mag., Pahang, Jan., 1899; asst. prot. of Chinese, Penang, Dec., 1901; ag. comsnr. of lands and mines, F.M.S., Aug., 1903; ag. Brit. res., Pahang, 4th Nov., 1903; ag. prot. of Chinese, S.S., Mar., 1904; sec. for Chinese affairs, S.S. and F.M.S., 1st May, 1904.

BARNETT, BERTRAM LEEDS THOMAS, M.A. (Honors in Nat. Sci. Tripos, 1896) Camb.; M.B.; B.C. Camb.; M.R.C.S. Eng.; L.R.C.P.; D.P.H. Lond.—Ed. Malvern Coll., St. John's Coll., Camb., and St. George's Hosp., Lond.; late senior house surg., Cheltenham hosp.; civ. surg., H.M. brigade of guards; asst. med. regir., St. George's Hosp.; asst. med. offr. of health, H. Kong, 1903.

BARNETT-CLARKE, H. P.—Temp. clk. H. of Assm., Cape, 1885-7; asst. lib., joint library of parlmt., 1887-97; asst. clk. of coun., clk. of the papers, and shorthand writer, 1897; clk. of coun. and offr. in charge of books, 1st Jan., 1904.

BARRETT, S.—Supt. of natives, Grahamstown, Cape, Oct., 1875; also inspr. of locations, Albany, 1876; commanded a Fingo levy in Gaika war, 1878; R.M. Quathing Basutoland, July, 1882; asst. comsnr., Leribe, Nov., 1886; Basutoland comsnr. on boundary delimitation between Basutoland and O.F.S., Sept., 1891.

BARRY, SIR JACOB DIRK, KNT. BACH (created 1878).—Ed. Chelt. Coll.; recorder of the high ct., Grigualand West, 1875; apptd. temporarily to administer the govt., 1875; mem. of both couns., 1875; judge in the E. dist., Cape, 1878; puisne judge, sup. ct., 1880; ret., 1902.

BARTON, THE RIGHT HON. SIR EDMUND, P.C. (1901), G.C.M.G. (1902).—Mem. legis. assem., N.S. Wales; atty.-gen. in Dibbs' ministry, 1891-3; mem. Fedn. Convention, 1897-8; leader of Opposition, N.S.W., 1898-9; chief Aust. del. to England in connection with passing of Federation Act, 1900; first Prime Minister of the Commonwealth of Australia and min. of state for External Affs., 1st Jan., 1901; judge of high court, Sept., 1903.

BATESON, EDWARD.—Ed. at Rugby and King's Coll., Camb.; exhibnr., 1888; schlr., 1889; sen. in the historical tripos, 1890; editor of vols. i. and ii. of a "History of Northumberland," issued under the direction of a comtee., 1891-4; called to the bar (Lincoln's Inn), 1896; dist. and stip. mag. in Mauritius, 1901; att.-gen., St. Lucia, 1903.
BATHFIELD, WM.—4th clk., regiay., sup. ct., Mauritius, 6th Sep., 1873; ch. clk. master's off., 9th Oct., 1896; ag. acctnt. in bankruptcy, 1898-99.
BATHURST, W. DUNDAS.—Ed. Lancing Coll.; agt. of Congo Free State, 1883-6; apptd. to col. audit branch exchequer and audit dept., Jan., 1889; local audr., Gibraltar, under C. and A. G., Jan., 1891.

BAUERLE, WALTER, B.A. (1889).—Ed. at St. John's Coll., Camb. (class. tripos, 1889); apptd. clk., col. audir. branch of E. and A. Dept., 4th Nov., 1901; asst. auditor, S. Nigeria, 4th Sept., 1902; ag. asst. treas., Oct., 1902; ag. local auditor, Mar. to Oct., 1903, and again in 1904.

BAUMGARTNER, G. A.—Writer Ceylon Civil Service, July, 1871; Class V., Feb., 1874; Class IV., Sept., 1876; Class III., Feb., 1883; Class II., Dec., 1894; govt. agt., prov. of Uva, Apl., 1897; dist. judge, Kurunegala, Oct., 1901; Class I., Jan., 1902; ag. dist. judge, Kandy, Apl., 1902; dist. judge, Galle, Apl., 1903.
BAUMGARTNER, H. V.—Cik., land settm't. dept., O.R.C., 16th Oct., 1902; clk., audit dept., 2nd Dec., 1902.

BAXENDALE, ARTHUR SALISBURY, M.I.C.E.—Ed. St. Paul's, Stony Stratford, and Leamington Coll. and at Schl. of Elec. Engrnrng.; for six years in service of Eastern and Eastern Extension Tel. Co.'s; supt. of posts and telegraphs, Selangor, Oct., 1888.

BAXENDALE, F. R. S.—Ed. at Charterhouse, cadet, Fiji civil service, 1884; passed cadet, 1887; European offr. armed native constab., 1886; 2nd clk. and interpr. prov. dept., 1888; stip. mag., 1889; res. comsnr. Cakaudrove Prov., 1892; ag. supt. of pol. and dep.-commdt. armed native constab., sheriff, and marshal of the admiralty division of the supreme court, 1896; stip. mag., Leonka, and ag. stip. mag., Tailern, 1897; also comsnr. and insp. of native taxes, Lomaiviti, 1899; ag. nat. comsnr., mem. ex. coun., Jan., 1903.
BAYER, CHAS. ALBERT.—Asst. survr., P.W.D. (Waterworks branch), S. Aust., 1882; sanitary engrn., 1888; hyd. engrn., 1902.

BAYLY, MAJOR GEORGE C.—(5th Batt. Rifle Brigade).—Passed school of instruction, Mar., 1879; 1st class extra certif., Hythe, 1886, and instructor of musketry, 1886; asst. inspr. G.C.C., Dec., 1883; dist. comsnr., Lagos and Badagry, 1884; ditto, Cape Coast, 1885; sub-inspr., Br. Honduras constab., Dec., 1888; mag., Orange Walk, Nov., 1889, to Nov., 1890; aide-de-camp to Sir A. Moloney, Sept., 1891, to Jan., 1897; inspr., 1893; ag. inspr. commandant, 1894; ch. of police and excise, Grenada, Feb., 1898; M.L.C., Mar., 1899; mem. of St. Vincent police comsnr., 1903, recd. thanks of S. of S.; local comdt. mil. police, and asst. comsnr., Cyprus, Dec., 1903.
BAYLY, LIEUT.-COL. Z. S., C.M.G. (1880).—Comdg. right wing, Cape Mounted Rifles; and ag. commdt.-gen. of Cape col. forces; commdt.-gen., 1881; ret., 1892.

BAYNES, EDWARD WILLIAM.—Ag. clk. comsnr.'s office, Montserrat, and clk. couns. Jan. and Feb., 1890, and May, 1897; ag. 4th clk. col. sec.'s office, Antigua, 1896, 1897, and 1898; ag. clk. audit office and federal acctnt., Dec., 1897; ag. treasy. office, Montserrat, Dec., 1898; 2nd clk., G.P.O., Antigua, Jan., 1899; ag. clk. G.P.O., Apr., 1899, to Feb., 1900; ag. clk. to mag. dist. A, Feb. to May, and June to Dec., 1900; clk. to comsnr., Virgin Islds., Jan., 1901, also clk. to treasy., clk. legis. coun., and registr. of births, etc., dist. D; survr. of ships, June, 1901.

BEACH, THE RIGHT HON. SIR MICHAEL EDWARD HICKS, 9th Baronet (created 1619), P.C., M.P.—B. 1837; ed. at Eton and Chr. Ch., Oxon, first class in law and modern history at the final exam., July, 1858; B.A., 1858; M.A., 1861; dep. lieut. for Gloucestershire, 1861; M.P. for E. Gloucestershire, July, 1864; partly sec. to the poor law bd., Feb., 1868; under S. of S. for the home dept., Aug. to Dec., 1868; ch. sec. to the lord-lieut. of Ireland, and sworn a mem. of the priv. coun., Feb., 1874; admitted to a seat in the cabinet, 1876; S. of S. forCols., 4th Feb., 1878, to 28th Apr., 1880; chancellor of the exchequer and leader of the House of Commons, 24th June, 1885, to Feb., 1886; ch. sec. to the lord-lieut. of Ireland, Aug., 1886, to Jan., 1887; pres. of the bd. of trade 1888-92; chancellor of the exchequer, 1895-1902; M.P. for W. Bristol since 1885.

BEARD, CHARLES HALMAN.—Called to the bar, Leeward Is., Mar., 1875; awarded equity prize by coun. of legal educn., Lond., 1881; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., Trin., 1882; ag. puisne judge, Leeward Is., on various occasions, 1886-1895; ag. solr.-gen., on various occasions, 1887-1899; ag. atty.-gen. (9 times), 1888-1898; apptd. solr.-gen., 15th Oct., 1889; res. mag., St. Mary, Jamaica, 28th Jan., 1898; registr. friendly societies, Antigua, Apl., 1889, to Oct., 1893; mem. leg. coun., Antigua, 1889, 1890, 1896, and 1897; J.P., Antigua, Sept., 1889; visiting justice of gaol, Antigua, Sept., 1889 to 1897; res. mag., St. Mary, Jamaica, 1898.

BEATTY, DAVID.—Cadet, S. Stttmnts., Nov., 1898; passed final exam. in Chinese, June, 1901; ag. 3rd magis., Sing., Nov., 1901; ag. dist. offr., Christmas Is., July, 1903; 3rd mag., Penang, Oct., 1903; but cont. to act as dist. offr.; ag. 2nd mag., Sing., May, 1904; ag. 2nd asst. prot. of Chinese, Sing., June, 1904.

BEAUCHAMP, 7th EARL (creat. 1815), VISCRT. ELMLEY (1815), BARON BEAUCHAMP (1806, U.K.).—K.C.M.G. (1889); WILLIAM LYGON; B. 1872; succeeded his father, 1891.—Ed. Eton and Chr. Ch., Oxford; D.L. Worcestershire; Mayor of Worcester, 1895-6; mem. London schl. bd., 1897-9; gov. N.S.W., 1899 to 1900.

BEAUMONT, W. H.—Ensign 75th (Stirlingshire) Regt., Aug., 1870; lieut. Oct., 1871; served on the "Langalibalele Expedition" in 1873; ret. Aug., 1875; priv. sec. to Colonel Milles (administering the govt. of Natal), May, 1873; to Sir Benjamin Pine (lieut.-gov.), and clk. to the exec. coun., July, 1873; ag. R.M., Umlazi Div., Aug., 1874; clk., col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1875; ag. gov.'s clk. and clk. to the exec. coun., Oct., 1875; R.M., Newcastle division, Feb., 1878; col. comdt. of Dist. No. 1, Natal, at the commencement of the Zulu War, Jan., 1879, during which time he raised levies called the "Newcastle Scouts"; R.M., Inanda, May, 1887; R.M., City div., Pietermaritzburg, 1896; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., for various periods, 1st Feb., 1895, to Oct., 1902; judge of special treason ct., Oct., 1901; puisne judge, sup. ct., 1st Nov., 1902.

BEDFORD, ADMIRAL SIR FREDERICK GEORGE DENHAM, G.C.B. (1902); K.C.B. (1895).—B. 1838; entered R.N., 1852; lieut., 1859; commander, 1871; capt., 1876; commander-in-chief at the Cape and West Coast of Africa, 1892-95; Lord of the Admiralty, 1889-92, and 1895-99; commander-in-chief on N. American and W. Indian station, 1899; gov. of W. Australia, 16th Jan., 1903.

BEDWELL, HORACE, B.A., Jesus Coll., Camb.—Apptd. asst. dist. comsnr. Niger (now S. Nigeria) Protec., June, 1896; wounded while on duty Qua Ibo Riv., Sept., 1896; ag. dist. comsnr. Old Calabar, Dec. 1896-June, 1897; apptd. dist. comsnr. Warri dist., Jan., 1898; sent up Niger to take over Asaba dist. from Roy. Niger Co., Dec., 1899; dist. comsnr. Asaba, Dec., 1899-Sept., 1900; ag. divsnl. comsnr. Cent. (Niger) div. S. Nigeria Protec., June, 1901, to Dec., 1901; transfd. to secretariat as ch. asst. sec., Feb., 1902; ag. sec. to govt., Mar.-Sept., 1902; ag. divsnl. comsnr. and ag. sec. to the govt., 8th Mar., 1902, to 24th Sept., 1902, and 17th Apr., 1903, to 5th Aug., 1904.

BELFIELD, HENRY CONWAY.—Ed. Rugby and Oriel Coll., Oxon; B.A., 1877; called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1880; went W. circuit; mag., collr. of land rev., and inspr. of schls., Selangor, 1884; ch. mag. and comsnr. of lands, 1888; ag. Br. res., June and July, 1889; sen. mag., Perak, 1891; ag. sec. to govt., Perak, July, 1891, to Jan., 1893; comsnr. of lands, Federated Malay States, July, 1896; ag. British res., Selangor, on various occasions from Mar. 1897 to Oct. 1899, and since Jan. 1, 1900; British res., Negri Sembilan, Apl., 1901; British res., Selangor, Aug., 1902; J.P. for Devonshire.

BELL, ARCHIBALD G., M.I.C.E.—Ed. at Felstead and Uppingham; employed on surveys Jamaica rlyw. extensions, 1887-8, and 1898-90; asst. to W. Shelford, Esq., M.I.C.E., Apr. to Oct., 1890; asst. to Messrs. Hawkshaw and Hayter, 1890-1891; asst. engrn. P.W.D., Br. Guiana, Oct., 1891; asst. col. civil engrn., Oct., 1900; col. civil engrn., Aug., 1901; apptd. official member ct. of policy, Feb., 1901.

BELL, EDWARD.—Served in the R.I.C. from Dec., 1885, to Aug., 1896; Hythe musketry certif., 1894; sergeant-major Leeward Islda. police, and drill instructor St. Christopher defence force, Aug., 1896; inspr. of L. Islda. police force and adjutant and drill instructor, Antigua defence force; J.P. for the Presidency of Antigua; inspr. of police, St. Kitts-Nevis, 1902; capt. and adjut. St. K.N. defence force; J.P. for Presidency of St. K.N.

BELL, GEORGE CECIL.—Ed. Bedford grammar and modern schl.; midshipman, R.N.K., 1898-1901; supernumerary sub-insp., Jamaica constab., Mar. to Nov., 1901; 3rd cls. clk., revenue dept., Jamaica, Dec., 1901, to Mar., 1902; asst. supt. of pol., B. Hond., Apr., 1902.

BELL, GEORGE GERALD.—Appd. to Imperial civ. serv. after compet. exam., Nov., 1894; bookkeeper, P.W.D., Lagos, Nov., 1897; clk. and acctnt., marine dept., So. Nigeria, Nov., 1902; asst. sec., So. Nigeria, May, 1904.

BELL, HARRY CHARLES PURVIS.—Ed. at Cheltenham Coll.; writer Ceylon civil service, July, 1873; pol. mag., Matale, Mar., 1876, landing and tide survr., Galle, Jan., 1878; office asst., Colombo, to the govt. agt.; W. Prov., Feb., 1878; 1st landing survr., customs, Colombo, May, 1883; asst. collr., customs, Galle, Apr., 1884; asst. agt. and additional dist. judge, Kegalla, June, 1885; ag. 2nd asst. to col. sec., May, 1886; ag. landing survr., Colombo, 1887; dist. judge, Ratnapura, Nov. 1888; ditto Kegalla, July, 1889; archæological commnr., Dec., 1895; officer of Class I, but to continue as arch. commnr., 23rd June, 1903.

BELL, H. HESKETH, C.M.G. (1903).—B. 1865; Ed. in Paris; 3rd clk. gov.-in-chief's office, Barbados, May, 1882; transferred to treasy. dept., Grenada, 1883; supervisor of customs, G. Coast, Jan., 1890; sen. asst. treas., G. Coast, Aug., 1891; ag. dist. commnr. and sheriff, Accra, 1892; ag. col. treas., 1893; rec.gen., Bahamas, Dec., 1894; represented Harbour Isld. in House of Assem., 1895-6; ag. col. sec., July to Oct., 1897, Apl. to Nov. 1898; admnrs. of Dominica, Aug., 1899; author of "Geography of the Gold Coast," "Obeah," &c.
BELL, THE HON. JAMES.—Min. without portfolio, Victoria, Feb., 1886; min. of defence, 1888-90.
BELL, VALENTINE G., C.M.G. (1903), B. 1839; M.I.C.E.—Ch. res. engr., Jamaica Govt. rwy., 1880-86; dir. of pub. wks., Mar., 1887; ex officio mem. legis. coun., mein. of priv. coun., Aug., 1886.
BELL, DR. J.—Asst. surgeon gov't. med. dept. Hong Kong, May, 1890; ag. supt. gov't. civil hos., 1896; ag. priv. civil med. offr., 1900-1; supt. gov't. civ. hosp., 1903.

BELL, W. G., M.A., Glasgow.—Cadet, S. Settlements, Oct., 1897; passed fin. exam. in Chinese, May, 1900; supintndt. army order branch and gov't. sav. banks, Singapore, July, 1901; 2nd asst. prot. of Chinese, Penang, May, 1902; ag. asst. postmr.-gen., Penang, May, 1903; ag. postmr.-gen., S.S., Oct., 1903.

BELLAIRS, Lieut.-Gen. Sir WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1862), C.B. (1878).—B. 1828; entered the army, 1846; adj., 1851; capt., 1854; major, 1855; lieut.-col., 1865; col., 1873; brig.-gen., 1881. Served with the 49th Regt., and on the staff of the army throughout the Crimean campaign, being present at the siege and fall of Sebastopol; medal with three clasps, Turkish medal, 5th class of Medjidie, knight of legion of honour; served throughout the Kaffir campaign, 1877-8, when he commanded the combined forces on the E. frontier; also throughout the Zulu campaign, 1879. Has also acted as inspr.-gen. of pol., Barbados, 1857-8; local inspr. of army schls., 1868-73; col. sec., Gibraltar, Aug. to Nov. 1873; admnrs. of Natal, Apr. to May, 1880; mem. of exec. coun., Transvaal, 1880-1; comdr. of the forces at Pretoria in the Transvaal, 1880-81; admnrs. of Transvaal, Apr. to June, 1881.

BELLAMY, CHAS. VINCENT, M.I.C.E., M.I.M.E., F.G.S., F.R.Met.Soc.—Ed. Plymouth Coll.; Queen's Prizeman, S. Kensington, 1885; dist. engrn. P.W.D., Ceylon, May, 1889; 2nd financial asst., Colombo, 1889; dist. engrn., Pallai, Jan., 1890; dist. engrn., Vavoniya, Vilankulam, Apr., 1890; dist. engrn., Jaffna, Jan., 1891; exec. engrn., Victoria Bridge, Colombo, Sept., 1893; col. engrn. and survr.-gen., Dominica, W.I., Nov., 1895; also commr. ordnance property, July, 1897; nom. mem. Roseau Town Board, July, 1896; on special service at Antigua, Aug., 1896; and at Montserrat, Jan. and Feb., 1897; dir. pub. wks., Cyprus, May, 1898; also dir. of irrigtn., July, 1900; on spec. serv. in Egypt, Nov., 1902; dir. pub. wks. and dir. govt. telegraphs, Lagos, Jan., 1904; received thanks of S. of S., April, 1898, for a report on the pub. wks. of Dominica; recd. thanks of govt., Jan., 1904, for services in Cyprus; author of "Rainfall of Dominica"; "Climate of Cyprus"; "Rainfall in relation to Irrigation, Cyprus"; "Economic Construction of Roads"; "Monograph of the Roads of Cyprus"; compiled geological map of Cyprus with guide; author of papers published by the inst. of civil engrns., the geological soc., and the royal met. soc., &c., &c.

BELMOROE, EARL OF, P.C. (Ireland, 1867), G.C.M.G. (1890), K.C.M.G. (1872), Somerset Richard Lowry Corry, fourth Earl and Viscount Belmore of the co. Fermanagh, Belmore, Viscount and Baron Belmore, of Castle Coole, co. Fermanagh, in the peerage of Ireland, of which he is a representative peer.—B. 1835; graduated M.A. (hon.) at Cambridge, 1856; elected a representative peer for Ireland, Jan. 1897; was under sec. of state for home dept. in Lord Derby's 3rd adminstr., July, 1866; gov. of N. S. Wales, Aug., 1867-1872; served as one of the Lords Justices of Ireland, 1885; again 1886.
BENJAMIN, Sir BENJAMIN, Kt. BACH. (1889).—B. 1834; three years Mayor of Melbourne.

BENKA-COKER, AMBROSE PETERSON.—Ed. Wesleyan High schl. and Educ. Inst., Freetown, S. Leone; pioneer offr. and offr. of customs (3rd cls.) of Southern Nigeria—then the Oil River Protectorate, July, 1891; attached to the preventive service and stationed at Ilu, Sep. to Dec., 1892; 2nd cls. offr., Mar., 1893; 1st cls. Apl., 1897; has been successively prin. customs offr. of Bonny, Warri, Old Calabar and New Calabar; transf'd. to Bathurst, Gambia, as chief landing waiter, etc., 1st Aug., 1900; actd. as tide survr., warehouse keeper and wharfmaster, 1st Nov., 1901, to 2nd Mar., 1902.

BENNET, JOHN LIGHT.—Ed. Bourton Acad., near Bath, Somerset; joined London pol., Scotland Yard, 1879; wt. offr., Southwark pol. ct., 1883; sergt. in charge of Holloway pol. statn., 1887; ag. inspr., 1888; sen. inspr. pol., Gibraltar, 1889; ch. of pol., 1895; sent to S. Africa on spec. serv., 1902.

BENNETT, C. KAYE.—Tempy. outdoor offr., S. Leone, July, 1887; recd. thanks of S. of S. with gratuity, 1889; warehouseman, 1890; ch. clk. of customs, Niger Coast Protectorate, 1891; supervisor of customs, Southern Nigeria, Jan., 1900.

BENNETT, Henry James Lee.—M.R.C.S., Eng., 1878; L.R.C.P. and L.M., Edin., 1878; med. offr., Grenada, 1882; late house-surg., gen. infirmary, Dewsbury, Yorks.; jun. res. med. offr., Royal Albert hospital and eye infirmary, Devonport, Devon; mem. Br. Med. Assoc.; J.P., 1890.

BENNETT, William Hart.—Entered C.O. Nov., 1878; ch. clk. ch. sec.'s office, Cyprus, Jan., 1884; ag. asst. to ch. sec., Aug., 1886; acted as ch. sec., July to Oct., 1883; ag. comsrs. Papho, May to July, 1894; asst. sec. to govt., June, 1895; ag. ch. sec. and mem. exec. coun., July, 1896, to Jan., 1896, Jan. to April, 1898, July to Dec., 1899; col. sec., Falklands, 1900; admntrd. govt., May to Nov., 1902, and June to Sept., 1904.

BERESFORD, M. H. De La Poer, I.S.O. (1904).—Rev. offr., Leeward dist., St. Vincent, Oct., 1883; acted as pol. mag. and coroner, Windward dist., Dec., 1884; confidential clk. and clk. of couns., Grenada, Jan., 1885, and in conjunction therewith priv. sec. to gov., Sir Walter J. Sendall, K.C.M.G., Nov., 1887, to Nov., 1889; ag. inspr. of prisons, Jan. to Mar., 1889; clk. of couns., and ch. clk. to gov., Windward Is., Nov., 1889; priv. sec. to Sir A. Moloney, K.C.M.G., in conjunction with appts. of clk. of couns., and ch. clk., gov.'s office, Nov., 1897, to May, 1900; asst. sec., N. Nigeria, 11th July, 1900; asst. sec. to admntrd., 1st Sept., 1900; ag. asst. sec. to admntrd., Sept., 1900, to Jan., 1901, Jan. to Aug., 1902, May to June, 1903; apptd. sec. to admntrd., 1st June, 1903.

BERKELEY, Sir George, K.C.M.G. (1881), C.M.G. (1874).—B. 1819; apptd. (1845) col. sec. and contr. of customa, Honduras; admntrd. the govt. of Dominica from Apr., 1860, to Dec., 1861; lieut.-gov. of St. Vincent, Apr., 1864; ag. admntr., Lagos, Dec., 1872; gov. W. Africa Sttlmts., 1873; gov. Leeward Is., 1874; ret., 1881.

BERKELEY, Sir Henry Spencer, Kt. Bach. (1896).—B. 1851; called to the bar, Inner Tem., June, 1873; ag. atty.-gen. of the Leeward Is., Aug., 1877; solr.-gen., Leeward Is., June, 1878; col. sec., Leeward Is. (ag.), 1883; atty.-gen., Fiji, 1885; ag. ch. justice, and ch. judicial commr. for W. Pacific, May, 1887, to July, 1888; ag. high commr. for W. P., Dec., 1887, to Feb., 1888; ch. justice of Fiji and ch. judicial commr. for W. P., Feb., 1889. Has admntrd. the govt. of Fiji and acted as high commr. W. P. on several occasions; recd. thanks of S. of S. for deep, relating to native affairs, 1894; atty.-gen., Hong Kong, May, 1901; ag. ch. just., Apl. to June, 1902, and Aug., 1904.

BERKELEY, Hubert.—Midshipman, R.N.R., 1881; to Perak, 1884; pol., 1886; supt. of Penghulus, 1889; ag. collr. and mag., Upper Perak, 1891; asst. mag., Gopeng, 1894; ag. dist. mag., Kuala Kangsar, 1896; dist. mag., Upper Perak, 1889; dist. offr., Klang, 1893.

BERKELEY, Maurice J.—Ed. at Cheltenham and priv. tuitin.; barrister-at-law (Lincoln's Inn), 1896; ag. pol. mag., Tobago, 1882; St. Vincent, 1884; Barbados, 1883-1892; inland rev. offr., Barbados, 1883; J.P., 1884; pol. mag. and petty debt ct. judge, Dist. "D," Barbados, 1892; Bridgetown, Jan., 1899; ag. ct. of appl. judge, 1900-01; S.J.P., Port of Spain, 1902.

BERNAYS, Lewin Adolphus, C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1831; F.L.S., F.R.G.S., and corrpdg. mem. of various scientific societies.—Ed. at King's Coll. and St. Thomas's Hosp., and was an offr. of Parl. in N.S. Wales, 1838-9; clk. of the legis. assem., Queensland, 1839; is sec. of the Brisbane Water Supply, and local dir. of various public instns.

BERRINGTON, Arthur T. D.—Ed. Clifton Coll. and Chr. Ch., Oxford; B.A., 1878; called Inner Tem., 1879; Oxford circuit; J.P. and D.L. for Monmouthshire; priv. sec. to Sir H. Bulwer in Cyprus, Oct., 1888, and to Sir Frederick Dickson in the S. Sttlmts., May, 1890; ch. mag., Selangor, 1891; sen. mag., Perak, July, 1896; legal adviser, F.M.S., Nov., 1902.

BERTEAU, F. C.—Ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey, 1865-72; ent. Newfoundland civ. ser. as clk. H.M. Customs, 1878; collr. of cust., Labrador, 1881; clk. in col. sec.'s off., 1889; ch. clk., 1890; J.P. for col., 1893; mission to Ottawa to inquire into Canadian fin. system, with view of applying same to Newfld., May, 1898; audr.-gen. Newfld., July, 1898.

BERTRAM, Anton.—Ed. at City of London sch., and Camb.; scholar of Caius Coll., 1887; Fellow, 1891; 1st cl. class. trip., part I., 1890; ditto, part II., 1891; Powis medal for Latin verse; Chancellor's medal for classics, 1891; B.A., 1890; M.A., 1892; president, Union Soc., 1891; Tanored law studentship, Lincoln's Inn, 1890; called, 1893; atty.-gen., Bahamas, 1902; elected to H. of Assem., June, 1903; ag. col. sec., May to Sept., 1903; joint author, Handbook to Workmen's Compensation Acts, 1897 and 1900.

BERTRAM, Louis J., C.M.G. (1904).—Ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey, 1870-7; apptd. to exchequer and audit dept., Mar., 1878; asst. auditor, G. Coast Coll. Oct., 1884; ag. auditor, 1885, 1886, 1887; auditor, 1888. Prepared scheme of store accounts introduced into G. Coast and Lagos, 1886; auditor of Windward Is., 1893; ditto, Leeward Is., 1896; ditto Jamaica, 1897.

BETHELL, Arnold Talbot.—Ed. Ardingley Coll., Sussex, and Ay. Hall, Camb.; asst. instr. Beckford and Smith's foundation school, Spanish Town, Jamaica, 1889; ent. civ. ser. Bahamas, July, 1900; asst. resident justice, Watling's; 3rd clk. G.P.O., May, 1901.

BETTINGTON, Bertie.—Natal Mounted Police, 1882; Cape Mounted Rifles, 1886; extra offr., Sarawak, May, 1890; asst. resident, Muka, Mar., 1897; res. 2nd cl., Jan., 1900; ag. inspr. police and prisons, June, 1900; res., 2nd cl., Oya, Sep., 1902; 2nd cls. res., Mukah, 1903.
BEVAN, J. F.—Apptd. spec. audtor for repatriation and transfd. from E. and A. dept., 29th November, 1902; asst. col. treas., O.R.C., 1st June, 1903.

BEVERLEY, Wm. Henry.—Ed. Bishop's high schl., Poona, India; F.R.G.S.; 2nd lieut., 4th Norfolk regt., 1869; lieut., 3rd Essex regt., 1896; capt., ditto, 1899; sub-commdt., R. Niger Co.'s constab., and Gour Asaba Gaol, 1899; Wing offr., N.C. Prot. Force (afterwards S. Nigeria regt.), 1900; intell. offr., S. Nig. regt., 1903; operations in Niger Co.'s territories, 1899 (medal and clasp); Obium expdn., 1901; Oran expdn., 1901; Aro expdn. (medal and clasp), 1901; Asaba hinterland, 1902; Uri Omonoha hinterland, 1903.
BICKNELL, W. A.—Ch. clk. col. sec.'s office, Singapore, Sep., 1885; auditor, Penang, July, 1888.

BIDDLE, Frederick Wm.—Ed. at Belize, Br. Honduras; copyist in col. sec.'s off., Feb., 1894; clk. to admntr., Apl. to Dec., 1895, and Jan. to Feb., 1897; 3rd clk. col. sec.'s off., June, 1898; clk. to audr., Jan., 1899, to June, 1900; ag. auditor, Sep., 1900; 4th clk. treasy. and cust. and cl. to comsrs. of currency, 1900.

BIDDULPH, Gen. Sir Robert, R.A., G.C.H., G.C.M.G. (1886), K.C.M.G. (1880).—B. 1833; apptd. to Roy. Artillery, 1833; served in Crimean campaign, 1854-56; Ind. Mutiny campaign. 1856-57; China war, 1860. Staff services:—Dep. asst. adj.-gen. in India, 1859-60; mil. sec. in China, 1860-61; mil. sec., Madras, 1861-65; dep. asst. qtrmr.-gen., Woolwich, 1868-71; priv. sec. to Mr. Cardwell when sec. of state for war, 1871-73; asst. adj.-gen., War Office, 1873-78; special service, Cyprus, 1878; commanding the troops, Cyprus, 1879; was an asst. boundary commr. for the Parly. Reform Act of 1867; was apptd., 1879, H.M.'s commr. for arranging the payment to be made to the Porte under the annex to the Convention of 4th June, 1878; and high commr. for Cyprus, 1879; inspr.-gen. of recruiting, 1886; dir.-gen. of mil. educn., 1st Mar., 1888; qtrmr.-gen., Jan., 1893; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Gibraltar, Oct., 1893, to 1900.

BINNIE, THOS. I.—Supervisor of roads, B. Cent. Africa Prot., 15th Mar., 1897; asst. survr., survey dept., 15th Mar., 1898; survr. to Anglo-Portuguese provisional boundary delimitation, 1899 and 1900; chief survr., 1st Dec., 1900.

BIRCH, SIR ARTHUR N., C.M.G. (1886), C.M.G. (1875).—B. 1837; clk. in the C.O., Feb., 1855, promoted to 3rd class, Oct., 1859; asst. priv. sec. to Sir E. Bulwer Lytton, 1858; priv. sec. to Mr. Chichester Fortescue from Sept., 1859, until Feb., 1864, when he was permitted, while still remaining on the establishment of the C.O., to accept the apptnt. of col. sec. of Br. Columbia; was sen. mem. of the exec. coun., and ex officio clk. of the same; also pres. of the legis. coun.; was admnstr. of the govt. of the col. from Sept., 1865, to Nov., 1866; promoted to asst. sen. clk., C.O., July 5, 1866; resumed his duties in C.O. in Sept., 1867; ag. lieut.-gov. of Penang and Province of Wellesley, Feb., 1871, returned to England, July, 1872; 1st class clk. C.O., Sept., 1872; col. sec., Ceylon, June, 1873; administered the govt. during the spring and summer of 1874 and 1875-6; lieut.-gov. Ceylon, 1876; resig., June, 1878; is agt. of the W. branch of the Bank of England, Burlington Gardens; exec. commr. for Ceylon at the Col. and Ind. Exhibn., 1886.

BIRCH, ERNEST WOODFORD, C.M.G. (1900)—B. 1837; ed. Harrow; cadet, S. Sttlmtn., Jan., 1876; employed in the C.O., Downing-street, to July, 1878; sec. to the pol. comm., 1879; passed final exam. in Malay, Aug., 1880; J.P. and a mag., Malacca; took charge Malacca land office, Feb., 1881; ag. collr. of land rev., Singapore, 1881, 2nd asst. col. sec., 1882; on special mission in H.M.S. "Espoir," to report on the Cocos-Keeling Is., 1885; mag. and collr. of land rev., Malacca, 1888; ag. Br. res., Selangor, May, 1892, to Jan., 1893; sec. to govt., Perak, July, 1893; ag. res., Perak, Sept., 1895, to July, 1896; Br. Res. Negri-Sembilan, June, 1897; govrn. and commander-in-chief of the col. of Labuan and of B.N. Borneo, Dec., 1900; Br. res., Perak, 10th Feb., 1904.

BIRCH, JAMES KORTHRIGHT.—Cadet, S. Sttlmtn., 1872; passed final exam. in Malay, Dec., 1873; asst. mag., coroner, and collr. land rev., P.W., Sept., 1874; collr. land rev., Penang and P.W., and Sttlmtn. offr., Trans Krian, Sept., 1877; collr. of land rev., Singapore, June, 1880; ag. pol. mag. and commr., ct. of requests, P.W., May, 1882; sen. dist. offr., P.W., 1888; 1st mag. and commr., ct. of requests, and supt. of prisons, Penang, June, 1890; ag. treas., S. Sttlmtns., Feb., 1894; 1st mag. Singapore, Apr., 1895, but continued to act as treas.; treas., Apr., 1897; ag. res. councillor, Penang, May, 1897, to Aug., 1898, and from Apr., 1901; res. councillor, Malacca, 1898; ag. res. coun., Penang, Oct., 1903; confirmed, July, 1904.

BIRD, HON. B. STAFFORD.—Has represented Franklin Dist. in House of Assm., Tasmania, since 1882; mem. of commsn. on educn. and on lunatic asyls., 1883; treas. of the col. in the Fysh Govt., 1887-92; and treas. of the col. in the Lewin Ministry, 1899-1900.

BIRD, CHRISTOPHER, C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1855; clk.; engrn.'s dept., Natal, Jan., 1874; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1880; 1st clk., Aug., 1881; clk., exec. coun., Apr., 1883, to July, 1885; J.P., 1887; asst. col. sec., Jan., 1888; apptd. prin. under-sec. on estabtment of responsible govt., Oct., 1893; mem. of civ. ser. bd., 1894; chrmn., civ. serv. bd., 1901; permitt. head of civ. serv., 1903.

BISHOP, KENELM CLARENCE.—Served in B.S.A. police, Bech. Prot. div., 1898; siege of Mafeking (medal and clasp); clk. to res. commr., Bech. Prot., 1899; ch. clk. and regiar. of res. commr.'s court, 1902.

BISHOP, WM. HOWARD.—Asst. clk., Georgetown prison, B. Guiana, 15th Oct., 1885; clk., ditto, 12th Aug., 1889; clk. H.M. penal settmtn., Massaruni, 2nd June, 1891; clk., Bartica pol. mag. court, 27th Oct., 1891; issuer, penal settmtn., Massaruni, 30th May, 1892; clk., ditto, 18th Aug., 1892; clk. and issuer, Georgetown prison, 12th June, 1894.

BLACK, G. G.—Ed. High Schl., Glasgow; Univ. Coll. Schl.; London and Glasgow Univ.; clk., audit dept., W. Australia, 1892; audit examr., 1894; ch. clk., P. W. Dept., 1896; ag. under sec., P.W., Jan. to Dec., 1902; is a J.P.

BLACKMORE, EDWIN GORDON, C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1837; ed. at King Edward VI. Gram. Schl., Bath; served with the Taranaki Rifle Volrs. in the New Zealand war, 1863-64, and was present, in reserve, at the action of Poutoko, 2nd Oct., 1863, and at the storming and capture of the rebel Maori strongholds at Ahuahu and Kaitake, Mar., 1864 (medal); parly. librn. to the legis. of S. Austral., Oct., 1864; clk. astt. and serj.-at-arms, house of assem., Dec., 1869; clk. of the house of assem., May, 1886; clk. leg. coun. and clk. of parlt., May, 1887; clk. of Austral. convention which met in Adelaide and Sydney, 1897, Melbourne, 1898, to agree to a bill for the federation of Australia; clk. of the constitutional comttee. to draft the bill; recd. thanks of conven.; clk. of sen. and clk. of parliaments, Austra. Commwth., 1901; several years examr. and lecturer in hist. for Univ. of Adelaide; organising sec. of movement to raise a Bushmen's corps for service in S. Africa, and mem. of sub-comttee. to fully equip the corps; author of "The Decisions of Mr. Speaker Denison and Mr. Speaker Brand on Points of Order, Rules of Debate, and the Gen. Practice of the House of Commons, 1857-84"; "The Decisions of Mr. Speaker Peel, 1884-95"; "Manual of the Practice, Procedure, and Usage of the House of Assembly of S. Australia"; "Manual of the Practice, Procedure, and Usage of the Legis. Coun. of S. Australia"; and the "Law of the Constitution of S. Australia."

BLACKWELL, LIONEL NORTON.—2nd lieut. 4th Batt. King's Shropshire L.I., Nov., 1888; lieut., Sept., 1889; transferred to Thanes Mil. Div., R.E., Mar., 1894; instructor of musketry, Apr., 1895; asst. inspr., Lagos Housa Force, Nov., 1895; ag. dist. comsrr., Oct., 1896; captain, July, 1898; received reward (50l.) after examination in the Yoruba language, 1898; Lagos representative—delimitation of the boundary Lagos; Northern Nigeria, July to August, 1901; appointed District Commissioner of the Colony and Acting Railway Commissioner, August, 1901.

BLACKWOOD, LORD BASIL TEMPLE.—Ed. at Harrow and Balliol Coll., Oxford; called to the Bar (In. Temp.), 1897; dep. judge advoc. S. Africa, Mar., 1900, to June, 1901; sec. to gov., O.R.C., 1901 to Mar., 1903; ag. col. sec., O.R.C., May to Sept., 1902; asst. ditto, 1903; ag. ditto, Apl. to Aug., 1903.

BLADEN, JAMES BUXTON.—Apptd. clk., store and timekeeper, harbour wks., Antigua, Jan., 1896; ag. 2nd clk., P.O., Oct. to Dec., 1896; ag. 1st clk., 1897; acctnt. of savings bank, Jan. to Oct., 1898; cashier of ditto, Oct., 1898 to Mar., 1902; ag. 1st indoor treasry. offr., Aug., 1901; ag. 2nd indoor ditto, on sev. occasions, 1899, 1900 and 1902; ag. 3rd indoor ditto (or cashier), 1901 and 1902; ag. 3rd outdoor ditto, and excise offr., in 1899 to 1902; jun. clk., audit dept., May, 1902; apptd. 2nd treasry. offr., and quarantine offr., Montserrat, Apl., 1903.
BLAINE, SIR CHARLES FREDERICK, KNT. BACH. (1889).—Deleg. for Cape Colony to S. African Customs Union Confe., 1882.

BLAINE, HERBERT FRANCIS.—Ed. at Harrow and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; called to Bar (In. Temp.), 1881; admitted advoc., sup. ct. of Cape, 1881; M.A., 1889; twice ag. sol.-gen. East dists. ct.; att.-gen., O.R.C., May, 1901.
BLAIR, HON. A. G.—Mem. for York in New Brunswick House of Assem.; premier and atty.-gen. from 1883 to 1896; min. of rlys. and canals in Mr. Laurier's govt., June, 1896.

BLAKE, THE HON. EDWARD, K.C., M.P.—B. 1833; called to the bar of Upper Canada, 1856; createda Q.C., 1864; entered Canadian parl't, 1867, and has also sat in Ontario assem.; was prime min. of Ontario from 20th Dec., 1871, until 25th Oct., 1872, when he resig.; sworn of the priv. coun. 7th Nov., 1873, but did not hold office; re-entered the cabinet, 19th May, 1875, as min. of justice, a position he exchanged in Sept., 1877, for that of pres. of the coun.; ret. from the cabinet, 31st Jan., 1878; declined the chancellorship of Ontario, Dec., 1862, also the ch. justiceship of the sup. ct. of the Dominion, May, 1875; is chancellor of the Univ. of Toronto, and Pres. of the Law Soc. of Upper Canada; leader of Liberal party in Canada, 1880-7; has sat in the Parl'mt. of U.K. since 1892; mem. of S. Afr. Comte., 1897.

BLAKE, SIR ERNEST EDWARD, K.C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1845; apptd. to a clerkship in the C.O., 4th July, 1863, after a compet. exam.; 1st jun. class, 20th Nov., 1869; 2nd class clk., 30th Sept., 1872; asst. priv. sec. to the Earl of Kimberley, 1st Oct., 1872; priv. sec., 27th Jan., 1874; 1st class clk. and head of the gen. dep., 1st May, 1879; crown agt. for the cols., 1st Jan., 1881.

BLAKE, SIR HENRY ARTHUR, G.C.M.G. (1897), K.C.M.G. (1888), C.M.G. (1887), F.R.G.S.—B. 1840; cadet Roy. Ir. Constaby., Feb., 1859; sub-inspr., Mar., 1859; apptd. res. mag., Feb., 1876; in Jan., 1882, was one of the five special res. mags. selected by Govt. to concert and carry out measures for the pacification of a large portion of Ireland; apptd. gov. of the Bahamas, Jan., 1884; Newfoundland, 1887; Queensland, Nov., 1888, but did not take up apptnt.; gov., Jamaica, Dec., 1888; gov., H. Kong, 1898; gov., Ceylon, 1903.
BLAKELY, DONALD QUENTIN.—Cltk., atty.-gen.'s off., B. Honduras, Apl., 1898.

BLAKELY, JAMES FRANCIS.—3rd clk., col. sec.'s off., B. Honduras, Feo., 1894; ag. 2nd clk., Apl., 1895; clk. to survt.-gen., Nov., 1896; asst. keeper, King's warehouse, May, 1897; clk. to col. engrn., Jan., 1899; ag. 2nd grade cust. and ex. offr., Mar., 1904.

BLAKENEY, CAPT. JOHN EDWD. CHAS.—Ed. at St. Peter's Coll., Camb.; lieut. 4th Batt. Essex Regt., Sept., 1886; capt., Feb., 1895; qualified for comen. in the line, Sept., 1889; Hy the certificate, 1894; Aldershot and Shoeburyness, 1895; tactics and mil. topography, 1894; asst. inspr. S. Leone frontier pol., Sept., 1895; now inspr. of pol.: is a J.P. for the col.; attached to Anglo-French bdry. coman., 1895-6; commd. detachment of frontier pol. H.M. Diamond Jubilee, 1897; ag. dist. comanr. Panguma dist., Aug., 1897, to Jan., 1899; commanded expedit. against Lopas and Bandia, 1898; recd. thanks of Govr. and S. of S.; subsequently commdng. the frontier pol. during the defence of Panguma against insurgents for two months until relieved by a column under Capt. Fairthought, D.S.O.; afterwards attacked and destroyed several Mendi strongholds; served on the Liberian frontier, Dec., 1898; West African med. and clsp., 1898-9; ag. dist. comanr., Falabu, Sept., 1899; adjt. frontier pol., Dec., 1899; ag. inspr.-gen., Feb., 1900.

BLANCHARD, VICTOR P.—Cltk., govt. office, Roseau, Dominica, 1889; clk. to valuation comsrs. and comsrs. of assessed taxes, Feb., 1895; ag. ch. outdoor offr., treaty and quarantine offr., July, 1895, to 1896; ag. sec. quarantine bd., 1896.
BLANCHET, J., K.C.—Prov'l. sec. and regis., Quebec; puisne judge, King's Bench div., sup. ct., Quebec, 1891.

BLAND, ROBERT NORMAN.—Ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Chelt. Coll.; B.A. Trin. Coll., Duh.; Cadet, S. Settlmts., Oct., 1882; passed in Malay, May, 1884; collr. and mag. Sri Monanti, June, 1886; dist. offr. southern dist., P.W., 1888; collr. of land rev., Penang, 1889; ditto, Singapore, 1890; offr. in charge, Sunjel Ujong, Dec., 1894; ditto, Negri Sembilan, Jan. to Apr., 1896; ag. offr. assignee of deeds, Singapore, June, 1896; inspr. of prisons, S. Settlmts., Mar., 1897; sen. dist. offr., prov. Wellesley, Apr., 1897; inspr. of prisons, July, 1897; ag. res. councillor, Malacca, April to Sept., 1900, Nov., 1901 to June, 1903, and Oct., 1903; treas., S.S., July, 1904, but continued to act as res. coun., Malacca.

BLAYNEY, J. ALBERT, L.R.C.P., Elin.; L.R.C.S., Edin.; L.F.P.S., Glas.—Govt. med. offr. Br. New Guinea; res. mag. central dist., 1895; mem. exec. and legis. couns., 1896; ag. regis. gen., and collr. of customs, Oct., 1898, to Jan., 1899; ch. med. offr., Oct., 1899.

BLISSETTI, HENRY FREDERICK, C.M.G. (1874)—B. 1847; clk., Roy. Ordnance Factories at Portsmouth, Jan., 1867; asst. comanr., African commissariat, Aug., 1869; priv. sec., 1870-1, to the admnr. of the G. Coast, and clk. of coun., 1872; served throughout the Ashanti Exped. of 1873-4, as control offr. with Capt. Glover's force; mentioned in despatches, and medal; acted on several occasions as auditor-gen. of the W. A. Sttlmts. from 1875 to 1881; comanr. to examine the accts. of the Falkland Is., 1882; sent on special service to the G. Coast as comanr. of accts., 1884.
BLOUNT, THE REV. CHAS., K., M.A.—Assistant minister, Falkland, Is., 1901.

BLOW, HORATIO JOHN HOOPER.—Arrived, N. Zealand, 1871; entered govt. service (P.W. dept.), 1873; record clk., 1878; ag. asst. under sec., Jan., 1885; asst. under sec., Oct., 1885; ag. under sec., June, 1891; under sec., Oct., 1891; under sec. for rlys. (in addition to P.W.), Jan., 1895.
BOAK, THE HON. SIR ROBERT, K.T. BACH. (1902)—B. 1822; Pres. of legis. coun., Nova Scotia; resigned, 1904.

BOAST, ALFRED.—Clk. in the mag.'s office, Umgeni Div., Natal, Jan. 1879; clk. and interp. to R.M., Umvoti, Nov., 1880; ag. admstr. of native law and border agent, Lower Tugela, Aug. to Nov., 1883; R.M., Zululand, 1890; mag., Eshowe divn., 1901.

BOAST, HENRY W.—Clk. and Zulu interp., Greytown, Natal, July, 1876; clk. to res. mag., Ladysmith, May, 1878; clk. and Zulu interp., Estcourt, Dec., 1880; ditto, Ixopo, 1886; regiar., native high ct., Apr., 1887; admnr. of native law, Tugela Valley, Jan., 1890; mag., Krantzkop, 1894.

BOLDERO, CAPT. A. H.—Ret. R.N., dep. master attendant, marine dept., Singapore, May, 1896; ag. master attendant, S. Sttlmts., Dec. to July, 1897, Feb. to Dec., 1901, and from Aug., 1902; master attendant, S.S., Dec., 1902.
BOLTON, JOHN GEORGE ELLIOTT, M.R.C.S.—Eng. Govt. med. offr. and comsrr. in lunacy, Mauritius, 21st Jan., 1870; sanitary warden, 15th Nov., 1895; ag. med. inspr. in 1900 and 1902.

BOLTON, WM. FERRALL.—Apptd. to crown agent's dept. 20th Nov., 1900; on spec. serv. under F.O. as ag. treas. of Somaliland, 22nd Nov., 1902, to 19th July, 1903; recd. thanks of S. of S.; 3rd asst. treas. East Africa Progr., 11th Jan., 1904.
BOLUS, PERCY REGINALD, M.B. (Lond.)—Asst. col. surgeon, Falkland Is., 1904.

BOND, THE RT. HON. SIR ROBERT, P.C. (1902), K.C.M.G. (1901)—B. 1857; Speaker, Newfoundland house of assem., 1884; mem. of exec. coun. and col. sec., 1889 to 1894; official deleg. to London on fisheries question, 1890, and to Washington on reciprocity question in the same year; again col. sec. 1895 to 1897; deleg. to Ottawa in connection with the confederation negotiations, 1895, and subsequently negotiated a loan for the col. in London in that year; prime min. and col. sec., 1900.

BONSER, RT. HON. SIR J. W., P.C. (1901), KT. BACH. (1894), M.A.—B. 1847; formerly fellow of Christ's Coll., Camb., sen. classic in 1870; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 18th Nov., 1872; att.-gen., S. Sttlmts., 1883; ch. justice, 1893; ch. justice, Ceylon, 1893; mem. jud. comtce., priv. coun., 1901.

BOOTHE, LEONARD WM.—Writer, Ceylon service, 1878; pol. mag., Avisawella, 1880; office asst. to govt. agt., Uva, 1886; ditto, Manaar, 1891; pol. mag., Kandy, 1895; ag. dist. judge, Kalutara, Oct., 1895; resumed duties as forest sttlmt. offr., Dec., 1895; ag. asst. govt. agt., Kegalla, Mar. to Oct., 1896; special offr. under Ordinance No.1 of 1897; govt. agt. Sabaragamuna, June, 1899; govt. agt. N.C. Province, Feb., 1900.

BOOTHE, ROBT. MALCOLM.—Cadet, Fiji, Nov., 1883; attached to col. sec. office, Feb., 1884; ditto stip. mag. office, Tai Levu, July, 1884; at Navua, Nov., 1884; att. gen.'s office, June, 1885, to Oct., 1888; passed cadet, 1887; stip. mag., Tai Levu, 1888; ditto, Loma Viti, 1893; ditto, Savu Savu and Bua, 1893; confirmed as stip. mag. and comm. of sup. ct., 1894; called to the bar Mid. Tem., 1899; stip. mag., sub.-coll. customs and tax inspr., Lautoka, 1901.

BOOTHEBY, JOSEPH, C.M.G. (1878).—B. 1837; clk. col. sec.'s office, S. Australia, 1853; clk. in audit office, 1854; ch. clk., 1856; ch. clk. in ch. sec.'s office, 1859; also govt. statist and supt. of census, 1860; astt. sec. and govt. statist, S. Australia, 1866; and under-sec. and govt. statist, 1868 to 1880; elected corrpdg. mem. of the statistical soc., Lond., 1869; trustee, savings bk., S. Australia, 1869; a comsrr. for international exhibns., 1872; joint edit. of a work "S.
