Australia: its History, Resources, and Productions," pub. by authority of govt., 1876; exec. comsrr. representing S. Australia at the Paris univl. exhibn. of 1878; elected hon. mem. of roy. geogr. socs. of Berlin and Vienna, 1878.

BORDER, THE HON. SIR FREDERICK WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1902), B.A., M.D.—B. 1847; grad. of Harvard Med. Schl.; has represented King's in the House of Commons since 1874, with the exception of one term, being defeated at the gen. elections of 1882; min. of Militia and Defence in Sir W. Laurier's govt., June, 1896.
BORN, EDWARD TURNER, M.B.—Asst. col. surgeon, St. Lucia, 1901; ditto, Falkland Is., 1903; col. surg., Falklands, 1904.
BOSMAN, JOHANNES JACOBUS.—Examiner of diagrams, survr.-gen.'s office, Cape, Aug., 1892.

BOSTON, REV. NATHANIEL HENRY.—Ed. C.M.S. Gram. Schl., Freetown, and Fourah Bay Coll., S. Leone; Durham Univ., L.H., 1878; B.A., 1890; deacon, 1880; priest, 1881; sec. of S. Leone C.M.S., 1880-1887; missionary and supt., Bullom mission, 1880-1888; delegate of S. Leone native church to England, 1885; J.P., S. Leone, 1890; editor of "Missionary Friend Magazine," 1876-1881; supt. of Lacca church and dist., 1898-1899; minister of St. Paul's church, Tasso Is., Jan.-Apl., 1900; priest in charge, St. Henry's parish, York, S. Leone, Apl. to Sep., 1900; astt. col. chaplain and regiar. of deaths, Cape Coast, 1st Sep., 1900; ag. col. chaplain 23rd Sep., 1900, to 7th Apl., 1901; also from 10th May, 1902.
BOTTOMLEY, W. C.—Ed. Owens Coll. and Trin. Coll., Camb., entrance maj. schlr., 1896; schlr., 1898; B.A. and 3rd wrangler, 1900; ent. C.O. after compet. exam. as 2nd cl. clk., 22nd Oct., 1901.

BOUCAUT, THE HON. SIR JAMES PENN, K.C.M.G. (1898).—B. 1831; entered parlmt., S. Australia, 1862. Held office in several ministries as atty.-gen., Oct., 1865, to Mar., 1866, and thence to May, 1867; as atty.-gen. and premier, and again as atty.-gen. in 1872; as premier and comsrr. of crown lands and pub. wks. from June, 1875, to June, 1876; as premier and treas., from 26th Oct., 1877, to 25th Sep., 1878, when he accepted a seat on the bench of the sup. ct.

BOULLE, VICTOR LOUIS.—Receiver of registn. dues, and conservator of mortgages, Oct., 1893; regiar. and officer of the civil status, Seychelles, Nov., 1897; ag. crown prosecutor, July, 1902, to Jan., 1903, and July to Oct., 1903.
BOULTON, J. F., Assoc. M.I.C.E. (1884).—Ast. engineer, p.w. dept., H. Kong, Apl., 1889; exec. engr., Mar., 1895; ag. asst. dir. p.w., July, 1902, to May, 1903, and 1904.

BOURKE, SOUTHWELL G. J.—Ent. Royal Navy as cadet, 1864; served in "Aurora," Canada, during fenian raid (medal); in "Satellite" during Abyssinian campaign (medal); in "Orontes" during Egyptian campaign, 1882 (medal, Khedive's star); called to bar (Mid. Tem.), 1887; stip. mag., Br. Guiana, 1897; interpr. in French (1st class, Navy), ag. pol. mag., Georgetown, Oct., 1900.

BOURNE, HUGH CLARENCE, M.A. (Balliol Coll., Oxford).—Asst. sec., charity organization soc., 1881-84; called to the bar (Inner Tem.). 1884; regier-gen. and examr. of titles, Trinidad, 1893; acted as judge of sup. etc., 1896-8; audr.-gen. Trinidad and Tobago, 1898; ag. col. sec., 1899, 1900, 1901, and 1902; admstd. govt., 10th to 13th Nov., 1901; ag. 1st class clk., C.O., Oct., 1902.
BOURNNE, J. O.—Regier. of titles, Queensland, 1890.

BOVELL, SIR HENRY ALLEYN, K.T. BACH. (1902).—B. 1854; Gilchrist scholar (London Univ.), Jan., 1873; LL.B. (Lond.), Jan., 1877, with 2nd class honours in jurisprudence and Roman law; called to the bar, Linc. Inn., Nov., 1876; solr.-gen., and escheator-gen., of Barbados, and J.P., Mar., 1882; M.L.C., July, 1883; mem. of comsn. to revise laws of Barbados, Mar., 1886; chancellor of the diocese, 1884; atty.-gen., Oct., 1886; atty.-gen. Br. Guiana, 1896; ag. legal asst. C.O., Dec., 1900 and 1901; ch. just., Br. Guiana, 1902.

BOVELL, JOHN R., F.L.S., F.C.S.—Parochial treas. and collr. of rates, parish of St. John, Barbados, Apr., 1882; supt. reformatory and industrial schl., Apl., 1883; mem. local comttee. Col. and Ind. exhib., 1885; supt. Botan. Stan., 1886; went to Antigua 1890 to start Skerrett's training schl.; vice-pres., Windward dist. agric. soc., 1890; mem. of comsn. to enquire into sugar cane borers, Jan., 1893; J.P., Mar., 1893; chairman emigrn. comttee., Apr., 1895.

BOVILL, ALFRED KARSLAKE.—Clk. of wks., pub. wks. dept., Cyprus, 1882-5; asst. to dir. of survey and principal forest officer, 1886-1895; inspr. of agric. industries, 1892-96; prin. forest offr., 1895.

BOWELL, THE HON. SIR MACKENZIE, K.C.M.G. (1895).—B. 1823; emigrated from Suffolk to Canada in 1833; well known in Canada as a journalist; has sat in Canadian House of Commons since 1867; sworn of the privy coun., 19th Oct., 1878, and apptd. min. of customs; min. of militia and defence, 1892; min. of trade and commerce, Dec., 1892; on special mission to Australia in connection with the development of inter-col. trade, 1893; pres. of col. confce. at Ottawa, 1894; prem. of Canada and vice-president of council, 1894-96.

BOWEN, ALFRED SYDNEY.—Ed. Queen's Roy. Coll., Trinidad, and Newton Coll., S., Devon; 2nd clk. crown lands office, July, 1879; 4th clk. rec. gen. s. office, July, 1881; branch savings bk. clc., Jan., 1883; ch. clk., surg.-gen.'s office, Jan., 1885; ch. clk., G.P.O., May, 1885; warden St. Ann's and Diego Martin, Dec., 1889; J.P., 1890; ag. priv. sec. to Sir F. Broome, Sept., 1896.

BOWEN, ERNEST F. S., F.S.I., A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; clk. audit office from Feb., 1882, to Apr., 1889; clk. post office from May, 1889, to Sep., 1890; ch. clk. and draughtsman, pub. wks. office from Oct., 1890, to Jan., 1895; supt. pub. wks., Barbados, Feb., 1896.

BOWER, SIR GRAHAM JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1892), C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1848; ret. comdr., R.N.; entered navy, 1861, and served on Mediterranean, E. coast of Africa, E. Indian, Home, and Australian stations, until 1880, when he was apptd. priv. sec. to Sir Hercules Robinson, gov. Cape of Good Hope, and high comsnr., for S. Africa; imperial sec. to the high comsnr., 1894-97; colonial sec., Mauritius, 1898; admstd. govt., July, 1900, to 1901, and Nov., 1903, to Aug., 1904.

BOWHILL, JAMES ALLEN PURSER.—Inspr. of schls., Tobago, Sept., 1884; prov. 2nd rev. offr. and keeper of the spirit warehouse, Nov., 1884; ag. inland rev. offr., Apr., 1885; ag. audit. and regiar., June, 1885.

BOYCE, ERNEST PEDDER.—Supernumerary clk., G.P.O., Barbados, June, 1878; notarial clk. col. sec.'s office, Apr., 1879; sec. to comsnrs. to inquire into the working of the G.P.O., 1879; ch. clk., pub. library, 1880; ag. libr., Sept., 1882; asst. petty debt clc. of St. Michael's, Nov., 1882; ag. ch. clk., May, 1883, to June, 1884; inland rev. offr., 1887.

BOYD, A. D.—Teacher, preparatory dept., Dominica Grammar Sch., Sept., 1894; junr. govt. off. treas., Jan., 1895; clk. govt. off., Portsmouth dist., Jan., 1896; govt. off. treas., Roseau, Sept., 1896; quar. offr., Oct., 1897.
BOYD, A. H.—Entered C.O. after exam., Apr., 1887; asst. clk., June, 1892; supplementary clc. in registry, Mar., 1900.

BOYLE, SIR CAVENDISH, K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1889).—B. 1849; ed. at Charterhouse; dist. mag., Leeward Is., Dec., 1879; col. sec. of Bermuda, and regier.-gen., 1882; col. sec., Gibraltar, 1888; govt. sec., Br. Guiana, 1894; admstd. govt. at various times, 1894-1900; was also chairman of the Br. Guiana bk., Apr., 1897, to Nov., 1898; govrn. of Newfoundland, Jan., 1901; govrn., Mauritius, 15th Apl., 1904.
BOYSE, V. H.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of 2nd div., and assigned to C.O., 17th July, 1902.

BRABANT, MAJOR-GENERAL SIR EDWARD YEWD, K.C.B. (1901), C.M.G. (1879).—B. 1839; Mem. of the legis. assem. for E. London, Cape of Good Hope; retired from Cape mounted rifles, as captain, 1870; field comdt. of col. forces, 1878; col., Cape yeomanry, 1879; in command of local forces in the S. African war, 1899-1901; ret. 1904.

BRACE, GEORGE KNIGHT KIRKWOOD.—Dispenser of medicines and gen. ast., N. P. asylum, Bahamas, 1883 to 1890; clk., regiar. of records offr., 1890 to 1896; 3rd clk., recvr.-genl.'s and treas.'s dept., 31st Dec., 1896; ag. regiar. of records, 26th Aug. to 10th Nov., 1901; ag. 2nd clk. and book-keeper, recvr.-genl.'s and treas.'s dept., Nov., 1903, to March, 1904, and from 5th May, 1904.

BRACE, T. E. D.—Dispenser N. P. Asylum, Bahamas, Sept., 1882; clk. pol. office, Aug., 1883; 2nd clk. col. sec.'s office, Sept., 1887; clk. to bd. of pub. wks., New Providence, Mar., 1900; ch. clk., col. sec.'s dept., Mar., 1904.
BRADFORD, M. G.—Asst., P.W.D., Sarawak, May, 1895; supt., July, 1901; ag. comnr., May, 1902.
BRADLEY, JOHN THOMAN, L.R.C.P. and S., Edin.—Asst. med. offr., Seychelles, 26th Aug., 1901; J.P., and chairman, local bd. of health, South Mahé.

BRADY, ALFRED BARTON, M.I.C.E.—Pupil and afterwards ch. draughtsman, ch. engrnr.'s dept. L. and Y. Rly., Manchester, Jan., 1872; asst. to G. W. Stevenson, M.I.C.E., 1879; surv. Docking Union and Hunstanton urban sanitary authority, Mar., 1881; engrnr. and survr., Maldon sanitary dist., Essex, Oct., 1882; asst. engrnr., rly. dept., Brisbane, Qnalld., Jan., 1885; asst. engrnr. of bridges (rlys.), July, 1887; engrnr. for bridges (main roads), P.W.D., June, 1889; govt. architect and engrnr. for bridges combined, Sept., 1891; under-secretary for public works, govt. architect and engrnr. for bridges combined, Feb., 1901.

BRAITHWAITE, H. W.—Petroleum locker and asst. excise locker, San Fernando, Trinidad, 1873; ch. clk. sub-treasy., 1874; supt. regiar. of births, 1878; J.P.; elected mun. coun., 1888; now ch. clk. med. dept.; sec. quarantine, and sec. bd. of health.

BRAMSTON, Sir John, G.C.M.G. (1900), K.C.M.G. (1897), D.C.L., C.B. (1886).—B. 1832; graduated B.A. at Balliol Coll., Oxford, 1854; elected fellow of All Souls, 1855; D.C.L., 1863; barrister, Mid. Tem., 1857; went to Queensland, 1859, as priv. sec. to Sir George Bowen; resig., 1861; was mem. of the legis. coun. of Queensland, 1863 to 1869, and of the exec. coun., 1863 to 1866. While in England in 1867, was asst. boundary commr. for Devon and Cornwall under the Reform Act of that year. Returned to Queensland, 1868, and became atty.-gen., 3rd May, 1870; resig., 31st Dec., 1873; represented the Burnett district in the legis. assem. of Queensland, Apr., 1871, to Dec., 1873; apptd. atty.-gen. of Hong Kong, 1873; was ex officio mem. of the legis. and exec. couns.; acted as judge of the sup. ct. of Hong Kong, Feb. to May, 1874; apptd. an asst. under-sec. of state, C.O., 30th June, 1876; employed on a mission to Berlin in connection with the Angra Pequena negotiations, July 1886; regisr. of the Order of St. Michael and St. George, Feb., 1892; ret. 14th Nov., 1897; sent out as roy. commr., in conjunction with Admiral Sir James Erskine, K.C.B., to inquire into matters relating to French treaty rights in Newfoundland, 1898; mem. roy. comm. for Paris Exhibn. of 1900.

BRANCH, Edmund Ralph.—M.B.Ch.B. Edin.; dist. med. offr., Dominica, 1902; ditto, Dieppe Bay, St. Kitts, 1902; med. suptt., Cunningham hosp., and surgeon of the gaol, St. Kitts, Feb., 1903; lieut., St. Kitts-Nevis naval def. force, 1904.

BRANCH, E. St. J., B.A., B.C.L., Durham Univ.—Called to the bar, Gray's Inn, May, 1892; ag. chief mag., St. Vincent, 1895 to Jan., 1898; asst. to atty.-gen., Leeward Islds., May, 1898; ag. atty.-gen., July to Nov., 1899; mem. legis. coun. and exec. coun. Dominica; mem. general legis. coun., Leeward Is., Dec., 1899; atty.-gen., Leeward Is., 12 Sept., 1902; ag. col. sec., 10 Oct., 1902.

BRANCH, E. W. R., M.B., C.M. (Edin.), B.A. (Durham).—Med. offr., Virgin Is., Oct., 1889; acted as commr., Virgin Is.; J.P. and M.L.C., Virgin Is.; med. offr., Montserrat, 1890; M.L.C., Montserrat; med. offr., St. Kitts, 1894.
BRANDON, The Very Revd. Dean, M.A.—Colonial chaplain, Falkland Is., 1877.

BRASSEY, Lord, 1st baron (created 1886), Sir Thomas Brassey, K.C.B., D.C.L. (Hon.) and M.A. (Oxon).—B. 1836; called to the bar Linc. Inn, 1866; M.P. for Devonport, 1865, and for Hastings, 1868 to 1886; lieut. comdg. roy. naval art. volrs., 1873; hon. comdr., 1880; col. 2nd Cinque Ports art. volrs., 1891; dep.-lieut. and J.P. for Sussex; was civil lord of the admty., 1880 to 1884, and sec. to the admty., 1884-5; pres. of the statistical soc., 1879-80; comdr. of the legion of honour, 1889; chairman of the roy. comm. on opium traffic, 1894-5; lord-in-waiting, 1893-5; gov. of Victoria, 1895-1900.

BRATT, J. Hicks Davson.—5th cl. clk., audit dept., British Guiana, 1889; 4th, 1892; 3rd, 1895; 2nd, 1897; sec. to comtee. of inquiry into travelling expenses, 1893; sec. to comtee. on pensions, 1898; asst. loc. auditor, N. Nigeria, Feb., 1901; loc. auditor, Sept., 1901; ch. asst. treas., Jan., 1903; ag. treas., Feb., 1903.

BREAKLEY, L. J.—Passed Lond. matric. and Oxford sen. local exams., June, 1899; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. and assigned to ld. chamberlain's office, Feb., 1900; transferred to C.O., 18th Feb., 1900.
BRETT, F. W.—Ent. service of United African Co. (afterwards Roy. Niger Co.), Mar., 1882; priv. sec. to Sir G. Goldie, 1883 to 1889; transferred to C.O. as supplementary clk., Jan., 1900.
BREW, Sam. H.—Ed. Wesleyan high schl., Cape Coast; clerical asst., gov. office, G. Coast, Apr., 1887; jun. clk., Jan., 1891; 3rd clk., Jan., 1893; now 2nd clk.

BREWSTER, Edward John.—Ed. at Brewood and Chelt.; asst. dist. offr., Matang, Perak, 1878; asst. supt., Lower Perak, 1878; asst. mag., Krian, 1879; ditto, Salama, 1880; ag. dist. offr., Krian, 1883-4; ditto, Larut, 1885; ditto, Knita, 1888; dist. offr., Krian, 1889; mag., Krian, 1889; state commr. of lands and regisr. of mines, Jan., 1892; ag. state auditor, Apr., 1893; dist. mag., Lower Perak, Aug., 1893.

BRIDGES, W. F.—Sub admistr.-gen., Berbice, Br. Guiana, Feb., 1885; ag. admistr.-gen., Feb., 1886, to Jan., 1887; commr. to inquire into road arrears under rd. ordinance, May, 1889; and into land titles, Oct., 1890; admistr.-gen., Br. G., Apr., 1899.
BRIDGMAN, Alfred H.—Entered C.O. after exam., Oct., 1889; apptd. asst. clk., May, 1892; shorthand writer to C.O., Sept., 1890-1900; asst. supt. of printing, Mar., 1900.

BRIGHT, Charles E., C.M.G. (1883).—B. 1829; a leading merchant in Victoria; has been chairman of the harbour trust and commr. for the Melbourne exhibn.; has also been commr. for Victoria at exhibns. in Europe.

BRISBANE, 3rd Bishop (Australia), Rt. Rev. William Thomas Thornhill Webber, D.D.—Consec. 1886; Pemb. Coll., Oxon, M.A., 1802; curate of Chiswick, 1860-4; vicar of St. John the Evang., Holborn, 1864-85; mem. Lon. schl. bd., 1882-5.
BRISTOW, Chas. Edwin.—Govt. printer, S. Australia, Aug., 1890.

BRISTOWE, Leonard Syer, M.A. (Oxon).—Called to the bar, In. Temp., 1882; puisne judge, sup. ct., Transvaal, 1903; joint author of "Law of Charities and Mortmain"; author of "The Mortmain and Charitable Uses Act, 1891," and of "The Hospital Legal Handbook"; joint editor of 2nd edtn. of "MacSwiney on Mines."

BRISTOWE, Lindsay W.—Clk. survr.-gen.'s office, Br. Honduras, 1880; in treas. and cust. dept., 1883-4; sec. crown lands bd., 1884; qualified land survr., clk., regisr.'s and record office, clk. of the petty debt ct., July, 1884; clk. of coun., 1885; regisr. V.A. court, 1886; provost-marshal, 1887; regisr. and keeper of records, 1887-1890; offl. admistr., 1890; dist. commr. Toledo dist., 1892; Belize dist., 1893; notary and commr. of the sup. ct.; editor Br. Honduras handbook, 1888-93; dist. commr. G. Coast, 1893; assigned to treas., Apl., 1894; ag. ch. asst. treas., Aug., 1894, to Jan., 1895; dist. commr., Accra, 1894; Elmina, 1895-96; chief regisr. and sheriff, 1897; dis. commr., Chama, Sekondi, 1898; Salt Pond, 1899; Winnebah, 1900; 3rd asst. col. sec., Oct., 1901; 2nd ditto, 1902; 1st ditto, 1903; ag. ch. asst., Feb., 1904; clk. ex. and leg. couns., Mar., 1904.

BRITTEN, Henry.—Apptd. probationer, Dec., 1896; 3rd cl. clk., magistrate's office, Ixopo, June, 1896; clk. and Zulu interpreter and sub-distributor of stamps, Ixopo, Apl., 1897; ag. clk. of the court on several occasions; served through the Boer War, 1899-1900, with the Border mounted rifles, including the siege of Ladysmith.
BRITTEN, CHAS. ALFRED.—Master, Royal Coll., Mauritius, 15th Nov., 1876; sec., ditto, 17th Feb., 1893; sec., college comteee, 12th May, 1896; inspr. and sec. to comtees, 18th Oct., 1903.

BROADRICK, EDW. GEO.—Ed. at Sherborne schl.; clk. lower divn. H.M.'s office of works, Nov., 1884; cadet S.S., 1887; dist. offr., Nebong Tebal, P.W., June, 1890; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, July, 1895; ag. 2nd asst. prot. of imgrts., May, 1896; 2nd mag. in sole charge of dept., Nov., 1897; dist. offr., Dindings, Apr., 1897; ag. collr. land rev.; offr. in charge of treasy., Malacca, May, 1897; ag. sen. dist. offr., P.W., Sept., 1898; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of coun., Dec., 1898, to Nov., 1900; collr. of land rev. and offr. in charge of treasy., Malacca, Dec., 1901; ag. inspr. of prisons, S.S., Feb., 1902; capt. Singapore volr. rifles, July, 1900; p.s. cert., schl. of instruction, Chelsea barracks, July, 1901; major and ag. commt. Singapore volr. corps, May, 1902; sen. dist. offr., Province Wellesley, Aug., 1902; mag. for, and visited Cocos Is., Nov., 1902; ag. 1st mag., Sing., May to Aug., 1903; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., Sept., 1903; ag. commdt., Singapore vol. corps, 6th Nov., 1903; pres., mun. commsrs., 1st June, 1904.

BROCKMAN, EDW. LEWIS.—Cadet, S. Stltlms, 1886; passed in Malay, 1888; ag. supt. of educn., Penang, 1888; 3rd mag. Penang, June, 1890; ag. col. of land rev., Penang, May, 1899; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Feb. to Oct., 1892; dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, Mar., 1892; ag. collr. land rev., Penang, July, 1895; 2nd asst. col. sec., June, 1896; also ag. collr. land rev., Singapore, June, 1896; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of coun. Aug., 1897; collr. land rev., Malacca, 1898; ag. 1st mag., Singapore, Feb., 1899; commr., court of requests, Singapore, Aug., 1902; asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., Sep., 1903.
BROCKMAN, FREDERICK SLADE.—Survr., lands and survey dept., W. Aust., 1884; survr., P.W. dept., 1887; survr., lands and survey dept., 1890; inspecting survr., ditto, 1891; chief ditto, ditto, 1894.

BRODHURST, HENRY WM. FRED. COTTINGHAM.—Ed. at Chel. Coll.; writer, Ceylon service, 1877; pol. mag., Matale, 1889; asst. to gov't. agt., N.W. Provs., 1889; ditto, Kegalla, 1891; asst. gov't. agt., Kalutara, 1891; dist. judge, Tangalla, Oct., 1898; Matara, Mar., 1899; asst. gov't. agt., Puttalam, Dec., 1899; ag. gov't. agt. and fiscal, S. Prov., Feb., 1902.
BRODIE, H. M.—Ed. Roy. Coll., Maur.; sworn ld. survr., Maur., Apr., 1900; asst. survr., Seychelles, May, 1901; town survr., 23rd Dec., 1901.

BROMLEY, ROBERT.—Apptd. hon. attache to the embassy at Washington, Sept. 23rd, 1897; resigned Feb., 1901; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, S. of S. for Colonies, Dec., 1901; admstr., St. Kitts-Nevis, July, 1904.

BROOK, HERBERT ARTHUR.—Ag. clk., pol. office, Nassau, May to Oct., 1874; libr., Nassau pub. lib., reading room and museum, Oct., 1875, to Oct., 1879; clk., pol. office, Oct., 1879; ag. ch. clk. col. sec. dept., May to Nov., 1881, and Oct., 1882; ag. priv. sec. to gov', Mar. to Apr., 1883; ch. clk. col. sec. dept., June, 1883; clk. bd. of pub. works and regis. of records, Sept., 1887; supt. Bahamas census, 1891; now regis. of records; is a J.P. and mem. of bd. of educn.
BROOKE, HIS HIGHNESS SIR CHARLES JOHNSON, G.C.M.G. (1888).—B. 1829; Rajah of Sarawak.

BROOKE, GILBERT E.—Ed. Pem. Coll., Ca. and Lond. Hosp.; B.A., 1894; M.A., 1896; L.R.C.S. and L.R.C.P. Edin., L.E.P.S. (Glas.), Fell. Roy. Inst. Publ. Health; Fell. Med. Soc. Lond., F.R.G.S.; late surg. Allan Line Co.; Furness S.S. Co.; govt. med. offr. and med. officer of health, Cockburn Harbour, Turks and Caicos Is.; ag. G.M.O. and M.O.H., Turks Is., Aug., 1899; J.P. for the col., June, 1899; asst. comsnr., Cockburn Harbour, Dec., 1900; May, 1900; dist. comsnr., Caicos Is., June, 1901; health officer, Singapore, 31st Jan., 1902.

BROOKS, G. L.—Joined Lond. and Metrop. pol. force, 1885; inspr. Sierra Leone, Oct., 1886; supt., sheriff and provost-marshal, July, 1888; ag. pol. mag., coroner and judge of ct. of request for Freetown, July to Dec., 1897.

BROOME, WILLIAM.—Entered Natal civil service, 1875; clk. exec. coun. and sec. defenc. comteee., 1878; ch. clk., atty.-gen.'s office, 1879; advoc., sup. ct., 1882; sec. coun. of educn., 1883; R.M., Newcastle div., 1889; acted several times as asst. col. sec., 1886-88; J.P. for the col., 1888; mem. of coun. of educn., 1891; master regis. sup. ct., 1889; offices of master and registrar separated 1897, retains that of master; mag. Durban, 1898; judge of spec. treas. ct., 1899; resumed duty as mag., Durban divn., 1902; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., 1904.
BROWN, SIR CHARLES GAGE, M.D., K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1889), Hon. LL.D.—B. 1826; was for many years med. adviser to the Col. Off. retired 1897.
BROWN, H. Y. L.—Govt. geologist, S. Australia, Dec. 1882.

BROWN, JOHN, C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1844; M.I.C.E.; asst. engrnr., Cape, 1873; maintenance engrnr., Western system, 1882; res. engrnr., Western and Midland systems of rly., 1884; ag. engrnr.-in-ch., June to Nov., 1888; ch. res. engrnr. (open lines), Dec., 1890; afterwards engrnr.-in-ch.: ret. 1904.
BROWN, JOHN FRANK.—Imperial service, 1st Sep., 1873; P.M.G., Natal, 14th Sep., 1900; let to Transvaal govt., 1st Oct., 1902; P.M.G., Transvaal, 3rd April, 1903; M.L.C.

BROWN, JOHN JAMES.—Ed. Liverpool Collegiate Instit. and Royal Coll., Mauritius, volr., col. sec's. office, Mauritius, Apr., 1880; 2nd clk. to sen. stap. mag., Aug., 1883; clk. to the ch. judge, May, 1864; curator of vacant estates, Oct., 1874; govt. agent before the forest lands purchase comsn. in conjunction with his office of curator, Nov., 1881, till Dec., 1882; and alone from Dec., 1882, to Feb., 1884; is marshal of the vice-admiralty ct. since Feb., 1873; asst. rec-gen., Dec., 1888; ag. collr. of customs May to Nov., 1891; collr. of customs, Jan., 1892; rec-gen., June, 1897; rec-gen., Jan., 1897; ag. col. sec., Sept., 1901.

BROWN, JONATHAN RICHARD.—Prison clk., B. Guiana, 1st Feb., 1878; promoted 1st clk. and served at penal settm., Massaruni, 18th Nov., 1884, to 30th Dec., 1887; ag. keeper, Fellowship pris., West Coast, Demerara, 3rd May to 3rd Nov., 1890; principal warder, penal settm., Massaruni, 19th June, 1894, confirmed; ag. chief warder, Georgetown pris., 4th July to 8th Dec., 1895; 2nd Dec., 1895, to 30th Mar., 1896, and 3rd Nov., 1896, to 12th July, 1898; ag. keeper, New Amsterdam prison, Berbice, 9th to 21st Dec., 1895; ag. ch. warder and asst. supt. at penal settm., Massaruni, 15th June to 14th Sep., 1897, and 12th Mar. to 16th June, 1900; keeper, Suddie pris., Essequibo, 1st Aug., 1900, to 31 Aug., 1903 (confirmed 1903). ch. warder, Georgetown pris., June, 1903, (confirmed, Mar., 1904); ag. supt., Georgetown prison, 14th Nov., 1903, to 13th Apr., 1904.
BROWN, MATTLAND.—Govt. res., Geraldton, W. Australia, 1886; ret. 1904.

BROWN, RICHARD MYLES.—Ed. Liverpool Coll. Instn., and Royal Coll., Mauritius (1st English scholarship); called to the bar, Mid. Tem., June, 1869; counsel to govt., Seychelles, 1872 to 1877; practised before sup. ct., Mauritius, 1877 to 1884; mem. of comsn. on laws and rules affecting civil jurisdiction and procedure of dist. cts. in Mauritius, Apr., 1882; dist. judge, Seychelles, Nov., 1884; judge of Seychelles, and sen. mem. exec. and legis. couns., 1889; adminstd. govt., Seychelles, Nov., 1891, to May, 1892; judge sup. ct., Mauritius, Aug., 1900.
BROWN, W.—Clk. of wks. for post and telegraph extension wks., Bloemfontein, O.R.C., 1st Sept., 1902.

BROWNE, ALBERT, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1860; apptd., after a compet. exam., to be a clk. of the lower (now div.) of the civil service, and assigned to the C.O., Sept., 1877, to Oct., 1880; clk. in ch. sec.'s office, Cyprus, 21st Oct., 1880; returned to duty in C.O., Jan., 1883; apptd. to act for three years as asst. acentnt. to the high comsnr. for S. Africa, Apr., 1891; has acted also as auditor, Bechuanaland, from Oct., 1892; mem. of bd. of inquiry into postal and telegraph adminstr., Bechuanaland, Apr., 1892; ag. Imperial sec., Jan. to Mar., 1893, and (jointly with Capt. Dawkins) Mar. to Aug., 1896; asst. to Imperial sec., and acentnt., Aug., 1895; ag. imp. sec., Apr. to June, 1900; finan. advr., O.R.C., August, 1900; col. treas., O.R.C., Dec., 1901.
BROWNE, ALFRED.—Apptd. asst. harbourmaster, Barbados, 1890.
BROWNE, DODWELL F.—Dist. judge, Colombo, 1893.

BROWNE, FRANK.—Student in Pharmaceut. Soc.'s Schl. in 1887; medallist in chemistry; elected research schlr. in 1888; apptd. a demonstrator in practical chemistry labortories, 1889 to 1893; apptd. asst. apothecary and analyst, Hong Kong, 1893; govt. analyst, 1898; sec. to liquor comsn., 1898; is Ph.C. and F.C.S.; J.P., 1903; author of several scientific papers.

BROWNE, GERALD.—Cadet, Fiji, July, 1879; sec. to high comsnr. for W. Pacific, Sept., 1879; also priv. sec. to gov. of N. Zealand, Nov., 1880; priv. sec. to gov. of Ceylon, Nov., 1883; ditto, Natal, Jan., 1886; clk. of exec. coun., Natal, Feb., 1886; sec. for Zululand on annexation of that country, and J.P., May, 1887; entd. Linc.'s Inn, 1889; priv. sec. to gov. of Ceylon, May, 1890, also sec. central irrigation bd., Ceylon, 1893; special mission to Sultan of Maldive Islds., May, 1894; sec. to govt. Selangor, Malay States, July, 1894; ex-officio mem. of state coun., 1898.
BROWNE, ROBERT.—Gen. Post Office, Barbados, 1890; customs, 1894; 2nd cls. supervisor of customs, Gold Coast, 1900; ag. aast. treas., Gold Coast, July to Sept., 1900; 1st cls. supervisor, May, 1902.

BROWNE, T. NEWTON.—Jun. clk. in col. sec.'s office, St. Vincent, 1866; ch. clk. to col. sec., 1871; confidential clk. to gov., and clk. of couns. and postmr. till 1881, when he resign. latter office; chief clk., gov.'s office, St. Lucia, 1882; acted as stip. mag. of the 1st dist. of St. Lucia in 1883; inspr. of pol. and inland rev. offr., Tobago, 1884; stip. mag. for Tobago; inspr. of pol. and marshal of prison, 1892; clk. of the peace, co. Caroni, Trinidad, 1895; ag. stip. J.P., co. Caroni, 1895.

BROWNING, JOHN GRANT, A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Catford Schl.; res. engr. Eastwood and Greasley sewage scheme; exec. dist. engrnr., P.W.D., Selangor, Malay States, Feb., 1896; engaged upon Pahang Trunk rly. construction.

BRUCE, SIR CHARLES, G.C.M.G. (1901), K.C.M.G. (1889), C.M.G. (1881).—B. 1837; ed. at Harrow; author of "Die Geschichte von Nala und Damayanté" (Sanscrit text, published by the Imp. Acad. of St. Petersburg), 1862; poems, 1866, and other wks.; asst. librnr., Br. Museum, 1863; prof. of Sanscrit, King's Coll., 1865; rector Royal Coll., Mauritius, 1868; dir. of pub. instruction, Ceylon, 1878; col. sec., Mauritius, 1882; adminstd. the govt. in 1883; lieut.-gov. and govt. sec., Br. Guiana, 1885; gov., Windward Is., 1893; gov. of Mauritius, 1897; ret., 1903.

BRUCE, ROBERT R.—Clk., Saint Helena, Mar., 1888; ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1894; dep. regisr. of births and deaths, Sept., 1886; J.P., Sept., 1896; dep. harb. mast., Oct., 1898; collr. of dues, 1887 to 1901; col. treas. and harb. mast., Jan., 1902.

BRUMELL, JOHN.—Admitted to Mid. Tem., Oct., 1872; called to the bar, June, 1875; practised his profession in Br. Guiana until Apr., 1882, when he was apptd. a stip. justice of the peace, stip. justice for New Amsterdam, and sheriff for the county of Berbice, 1883; acted on several occasions as pub. prosecutor, on two occasions as judge of the infr. crim. ct.

BRUNKER, HOWARD M.—2nd lieut., Donegal Artillery (militia), 1890; lieut., 1891 (passed schl. of instrn., Woolwich, 1893); resig., 1892, to join Bechuanaland border pol.; served in ditto, Oct., 1892, to Nov., 1895; served through Matabele campaign, 1893-4 (medal); apptd. inspr. of pol., B. Guiana, 12th Feb., 1896; promoted dist. inspr. 6th April, 1900; acted adjutant and musketry instr. to B. G. militia and police, April, 1901, to April, 1902; apptd. capt. in Donegal Artillery (militia), 18th June, 1902; passed schl. of musk., Hythe, 1902; promoted county inspr. of pol., B. G., 19th Nov., 1903.

BRYAN, HERBERT.—2nd lieut., Linc. regt., 18th June, 1892; lieut., 16th Jan., 1899; capt., Manchester regt., 26th July, 1899; brev. major, 29th Nov., 1900; served in W. Africa, 1897-8; employed in Lagos Hinterland; also in operations on the Niger, including expdt. to Bassema (ment. in desp., medal with clasp); served in N. Nigeria, 1900, slightly wounded (ment. in desp., clasp); served in operations in Ashanti, 1900; on the staff, D.A.A.G. (ment. twice in desp., medal); chief staff offr., Gambia expdt., 1901 (ment. in desp., medal with clasp); staff offr., W.A.F.F., 16th Oct., 1901; employed at C.O., 8th May, 1902, to 14th Nov., 1903; ag. col. sec., G. Coast, 29th Nov., 1903; ag. gov'r., 9th Feb., 1904, to 3rd Mar., 1904; apptd. col. sec., G. Coast, 14th Feb., 1904.

BRYANT, ALFRED THOMAS.—Ed. Tonbridge schl. and Wad. Coll., Oxon; cadet, S. Sttlnts., 1883; ag. collr. land rev., Penang, July, 1886; dist. offr., Malacca, 1887; ag. ditto, P.W., 1889; dist. offr. Dindings, June, 1890; ag. collr. of land rev. and offr. in charge of treas., Malacca, Mar., 1894; inspr. of schl., S. Sttlnts., Apr., 1897; 1st mag., Penang, 1898.

BUCHANAN, ALEXANDER.—Ed. St. Peter's, Adelaide, S. Australia, and Hofrlyl and Glasgow; admitted to S. Australian bar, 1884; stip. mag. Port Adelaide, 1891; master sup. ct., regist. in adm'y., regist. of companies, industrial and provident societies, and trades unions, 1891; industrial regisr. under Conciliation Act, 1895; also regisr. of probates, Aug., 1903.

BUCHANAN, SIR E. J., KNT. BACH. (1901).—B. 1844; called to the bar, Inner Temp., Jan., 1873; advoc. of sup. ct. of Cape Col., of high ct. of prov. of Griqualand West, and of vice-admiralty ct., Cape Col.; elected mem. for Worcester in house of assem., 1877; ag. atty.-gen. of Griqualand West, Nov., 1879; apptd. a puisne judge of the sup. ct. of the Cape Col., Apr., 1880; assigned to the ct. of the eastern dist., May, 1880, and to sup. ct., June, 1887.
BUCKELL, CHARLES PHILIP.—Govr.'s messenger and telegraph clk., Portland prison, 1st Mar., 1877; warder, Singapore pris., S. Stltlms., 29th Mar., 1883; supt. of telegraphs, Penang, 23rd Dec., 1892.

BUCKNILL, JOHN ALEXANDER STRACHEY, M.A. OXON.; J.P.—Barrister-at-law, Inner Temp., 1896; mid. circuit; advocate of sup. ct., Transvaal, 1902; comnrs. of patents, regisr. of trade marks and regisr. of companies, Transvaal, 5th June, 1902.
BUDGE, ALEX. CAMPBELL, J.F.—Entered the civ. ser. of N. S. Wales, Nov., 1888; clk., exec. coun., Oct., 1863.

BUKO, T. B.—Ed. at C.M.S. gram. school, Lagos; entd. civ. serv., Feb., 1888; clk. to ch. regisr., sup. ct., Jan., 1890; transf. to customs dept., Apl., 1891; dep. regisr. and interpreter, Leckie, east. dist., May, 1892; clk. to atty.-gen., Mar., 1901; 3rd cl. clk., col. sec's off., May, 1901.

BULLER, SIR WALTER LAWRY, K.C.M.G. (1886), C.M.G. (1875), F.R.S.—B. 1838; author of "The Birds of New Zealand"; called to the bar, Inner Temp., 1874; served in the Maori war, 1865 (medal); has served in various offices in the native affairs dept., and several times received the thanks of govt.; comnrs. for New Zealand at the Col. and Ind. exhibn., 1886; is Hon. D. Sc., Camb.; offr. of legion of honour for services at Paris exhibn., 1889; Kt. Comdr. of the Crown of Italy.
BULMER, J. A.—I.S.O. (1903); Postmnr., Cyprus, 27th July, 1878; postmtr.-gen., Trinidad, 1883; ret., 1903.

BULWER, SIR HENRY ERNEST GASCOYNE, G.C.M.G. (1883), K.C.M.G. (1874).—B. 1836; was one of the offl. residents under the lord high comnrs. in Ionian Is., from Oct., 1860, until the withdrawal of the Br. protectorate in June, 1864; apptd. treas. and rec. gen. for the Is. of Trinidad, in Feb., 1866; adminstr. the govt. of Dominica, Leeward Is., from Mar., 1867, to Jan., 1869; apptd. gov. of Labuan, and H.M. consul-gen. for Borneo, in Aug., 1871; lieut.-gov. of Natal, Aug., 1875, to 1880; gov. of Natal and special comnrs. for Zulu affairs, Dec., 1881; high comnrs. Cyprus, 1886; ret., 1892.

BUNDEY, THE HON. SIR WILLIAM HENRY, KT. BACH. (1904), K.C.—B. 1838; atty.-gen. of S. Australia, Sept., 1878; entered parlmt. in 1871; held office as min. of justice and educn., July, 1874, to Mar., 1875; 3rd judge of the sup. ct., 1884; also pres. of state bd. of conciliation, Jan., 1895, to Apl., 1896; ret., 1904.

BURBIDGE, HON. G. W., LL.D., K.C.—Called to the bar, New Brunswick, 1871; sec. to comnrs. to consolidate statutes of New Brunswick, 1876-7; dep. min. of justice, Canada, and solr. for Indian affairs, May, 1882; one of the comnrs. to revise the consolidated statutes of Canada, June, 1883, to June, 1885; judge, exchequer ct. of Canada, Oct., 1888.

BURCHELL, H. C.—Mem. Can. Soc. C.E.; mem. Am. Inst. M.E.; Mount Allison and McGill univs.; asst. engnr. to prov. engnr. of Nsw Scotia, 1875-8; govt. engnr., Newfndld., 1884; manager govt. telegraphs, 1893; man. Newfndld. rly., 1897; dep. min. and ch. engnr., P.W., and chmn. St. John's municipal coun., 1898.
BURGESS, PERCIVAL JAMES, M.A. CANTAB., F.C.S.—Govt. analyst and science lecturer, S. Stltlms., 19th Apl., 1900.

BURKE, HENRY LARDNER, K.C.—Ed. at Lincoln Coll., Oxford, 2nd cl. classical mods., 1871; B.A., 1873; M.A., 1901; LL.B., of Cape univ., 1880; admitted as an advocate of east. dist. ct., 1880, and of sup. ct., 1886; examiner in literature at Cape univ., 1888-1890; ag. solicitor-gen., 25th June to 25th July, 1895, 22nd Dec., 1896, to 28th Mar., 1897, and from 8th May to 12th June, 1897; apptd. crown prosecutor, Griqualand West, 14th June, 1897; apptd. Q.C., 13th May, 1898.

BURLEY, E.B.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. 2nd divn. civ. ser., and assigned to regisr.-gen.'s office, 21st Jan., 1896; transf. to C.O. 20th June, 1896; special service in office of high comnrs., South Africa, July, 1900, to Jan., 1902.

BURMESTER, CHARLES MANSEL.—Ed. at Dulwich Coll.; ent. serv. of Norddeutscher Lloyd SS. Co., Bremen, April, 1898; res. Dec., 1899; joined 58th Co. (Berks) Imp. Yeom., Jan. 29th, 1900; served in S. Afr. camp., 1900-1901; clk. to sec. O. R. C. Admn., Mar. 20th, 1901; clk. to col. sec., June, 1902.

BURNS, R. E.—2nd clk., registr.'s off., St. Kitts, June, 1898; ag. clk., town comnrs., Basseterre, June to Dec., 1899; clk., P.O., Oct., 1901; extra rev. offr., treas., Dec., 1899; ag. clk., treas., May, 1902, to Mar., 1903; ag. govt. clk., clk., ex. and leg. couns., clk., bd. of health, Apl., 1903, to Mar., 1904, June, 1904; ag. clk., treas., St. Kitts-Nevis, July, 1904.

BURNSIDE, SIR BRUCE LOCKHART, KT. BACH. (1884).—B. 1833; called to the bar, Linc.'s Inn, Apr., 1856; solr.-gen., Bahamas, May, 1864; was ag. atty.-gen. in 1863, 1866, 1869, and 1875; mem. of exec. coun. in 1866; and of legis. coun. in 1872; mem. of the local legislature from 1859; and was speaker of the House of Assem. in 1866; made Q.C. in 1874; atty.-gen., Sept., 1875; Queen's advoc., Ceylon, 1879; ch. justice, 1883; ret., 1893.

BURNSIDE, NIGEL BRUCE.—New Providence, Bahamas, 3rd clk., survr.-gen.'s dept., 1879 and 1881; ag. clk. bd. of pub. wks., 1890 to 1892; clk. legis. coun., 1881 to 1888; 2nd clk. survr.-gen.'s dept., 1882 to 1883; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s dept., 1883 to 1887; priv. sec. to Adminr. E. B. A. Taylor, C.M.G., 1886; clk., col. sec.'s dept., and clk., bd. of pub. wks., 1887 (still holding same); ag. col. sec., July to Nov., 1889, July to Oct., 1890, again in 1891.
BURNSIDE, R.B.—Called to the bar, Linc.'s Inn, 1884; admtd. to W. Australian bar, July, 1884; usher of black rod, legis. coun., Dec., 1890; crown solr., July, 1894; 3rd puisne judge, Dec., 1902.

BURROWS, STEPHEN MONTAGU, M.A.—Scholar of Eton, scholar of Exeter Coll., Oxford, Jan., 1876; graduated in classical hon., 1879; writer, Ceylon service, Oct., 1880; priv. sec. to Lieut.-Gov. Douglas, June, 1881; J.P. and additional coroner for Colombo, Nov., 1881; attached to the Queen's advoc. dept., June, 1882; J.P. and additional pol. mag., Galle, Aug., 1882; ag. office asst. to the govt. agt., N.W. prov., Apr., 1884; extra office asst. to the govt. agt., central prov., May, 1884; ag. office asst. to the govt. agt., north-central prov., and additional dist. judge, comnrs. of requests and pol. mag., Anuradhpura, Aug., 1884; ag. office asst., Badulla, July, 1888. ag. asst.-agt., Matale, June, 1887; ag. dist. judge, Kegalle, 1892; ag. fiscal agt. C.P., 1892; ag. asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, 1893; do., Nuwara Eliya, 1896; dir. of pub. instruction, 1899; govt. agent, N.W.P., 1902; author of "The Visitor's Guide to Kandy and Nuwara Eliya," and "The Buried Cities of Ceylon."

BURSLEM, W.—Ed. Manchester gram. schl. and Pemb. Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1879; M.A., 1882; asst. mast., Harrison Coll., Barbados, 1880; 2nd mast., Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad, 1891; head master, Lodge schl., Barbados, 1892; principal, Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad, 1895.

BURT, ALFRED EARLE.—Ck. to ch. justice, W. Australia, June, 1871; clk. col. sec.'s office, Dec., 1873; to gov., 1874; in audit office, Jan., 1875; draughtsman, marine survey dept., 1875; and in crown lands dept., Sept., 1877; acted as regis. of deeds and titles, Jan., 1880, to Mar., 1881; regis. of titles and deeds, June, 1890.

BURT, OCTAVIAN, I.S.O. (1903).—Ck. in gov.'s office, W. Australia, May, 1872; Jan., 1874, clk. to exec. coun. and priv. sec. to ag. gov.; Apr., 1874, to Dec., 1874, priv. sec. to Gov. Weld; May, 1875, clk. to exec. coun. and priv. sec. to Gov. Sir Wm. Robinson, K.C.M.G.; Sept., 1877, ch. clk. and keeper of records in survey office; res. mag., Newcastle, Apr., 1880; ditto, York, Apr., 1887; asst. col. sec., Apr., 1887; ag. col. sec., 1890, with seat in exec. and legis. couns., under sec. for W. Australia under responsible govt., 1891; ag. sec. to agent-gen., Apl., Oct., 1898; under-sec., May, 1899; sheriff, W.A., 1901.

BUSHE, ROBERT GERVASE.—Ed. at King's Col., Camb., where he obtained an exhibn. on entrance; elected to a foundation scholarship, 1874; was 27th wrangler in the math. tripos, 1875; grad. B.A., 1875; 2nd master Queen's Roy. coll., Trinidad, 1878; inspr. of schools, 1890; auditor-general, 1903.

BUSHE, R. J. SCOTT.—A.M.I.C.E., Ed. Queen's Roy. Coll., Trinidad, Malvern Coll., and Lancing Coll.; ag. and special draftsman, P.W. Dept., Trinidad, various occasions, 1882-6; draftsman light rly., Feb., 1880; survr. of loan wks., Grenada, Jan., 1899; ag. dir. pub. wks., Mar., 1891; engaged on loan wks., Trinidad, 1893; and in charge of S. div., P.W.D., 1894-6; draftsman, 1897; dist. offr., 1st grade, 1899; asst. col. civ. engr., B. Guiana, 1902.

BUTLER, ERNEST ORMOND.—Served in Bech. Border police and Bech. mounted pol., 1891-1897; Matabele war, 1893-4; served in B.S.A. police, Bech. Prot. div., 1898-1902; S. African war, 1899-1902 (medal and clasp, defence of Mafeking); ch. clk., headquarter off., Lt.-Gen. Baden Powell's force, 1900; customs offr., 1902-1903; asst. commr.'s clk., Bech. Prot., 1902; J.P., ditto, 1903.

BUTLER, F. G. A.—Exhibitioner of Trin. Coll., Oxford, 1892; 1st class classical moda., 1894; 1st class final classical schools, 1896; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in Admiralty, Oct., 1896; 2nd class clk. in C.O., Apl., 1897; sec. to W. Africa currency comte., 1899.

BUTLER, CAPT. P. J. H.—Late 3rd Batt. E. Lancs. Regt.; entered Natal civ. ser., Jan., 1890, customs dept.; sergt.-at-arms, House of Assem., Natal, Nov., 1895; volunt. for spec. serv., and as lieut. proceeded, Oct., 1899, with Hotchkiss gun detach. to Ladysmith, present during siege, medal and clasp.
BUTLER, RICHARD, M.L.A., S. Aust. (1890).—Min. of ed. and agric., 1898-9; treas., 1901; treas. and commr. of crown lands and immigrtn., 1902.

BUTLER, CAPT. VERE ALBAN.—Late R.M.L.I. and 7th Roy. Lanc. Militia; 2nd class inspr. of pol., Mauritius, June, 1880; pol. offr., with magisterial powers, Diego Garcia, Sept., 1885; reverted to former post on abolition of latter, Mar., 1889; ag. 1st class inspr. of pol., Jan., 1892; inspr., Sept., 1894; ag. ch. offr. of pol. on several occasions for Port Louis.

BUTLER, MAJ.-GEN. SIR WM. FRANCIS, K.C.B. (1886), C.B. (1874); B. 1838; served in Canada in Red River expedit., 1870; in Ashanti, 1874; Zulu War, 1878-9; Egypt, 1882; with Gordon Relief expedit., 1884-5; comdg. troops S. Africa, 1898; admstd. govt. Cape, Nov., 1898, to Feb., 1899; commanding troops W. Dist. England, 1899; do., Aldershott, 1900.

BUTTERWORTH, CAPTAIN ARCHIBALD WILLIAM.—Govt. storekeeper, Br. N. Guinea, Oct., 1890; headqrs. offr. armed constab., Feb., 1891; also port and harbmr., and native mag., promoted commandant constab. and res. mag., Oct., 1894; volunt. for serv. in S. Afr., and joined 4th Q.land conting., Mar., 1900, as sen. lieut.; prom. capt. at Pretoria, Nov., 1900; resig., Sept., 1901; apptd. capt., Lagos Batt., W. Afr. Front. Force, Sept., 1901.

BUXTON, SYDNEY CHARLES, M.P.—Ed. at Clifton Coll. and Trin. Coll., Camb.; was mem. of the London schl. bd. from 1876 to 1882; hon. sec. to Mr. Tuke's Irish emigrn. fund, 1882-4; author of the "Handbook to Political Questions," the "Political Manual," "Finance and Politics," "An Historical Study, 1783-1883," &c., and editor of the imp. parlmt. series; M.P. for Peterborough from June, 1883, to Nov., 1885, when he was unsuccessful; contested Croydon unsuccessfully in Jan., 1886; elected July, 1886, and re-elected in 1892, 1895, and 1900, for Poplar (Tower Hamlets), for which he still sits; parly. under-sec. of state for the cols., 17th Aug., 1892, to 28th June, 1895.

BUXTON, SIR THOMAS FOWELL, BART., G.C.M.G. (1899), K.C.M.G. (1895).—3rd Bart.; M.A., Camb.; B. 1837; dep. lieut. and J.P. for Essex and Norfolk; sheriff, 1875; M.P. for King's Lynn, 1865-68; lieut.-col., 2nd Tower Hamlets rifle volr. brigade, 1864; hon. col. since 1884; gov. of S. Australia, 1895-9.

BYNOE, A. D.—Ck., G.P.O., Barbados, 1874; rev. offr., Jan., 1879; landing waiter, May, 1879; 2nd clk., customs, June, 1887; ch. clk., customs, Dec., 1890; ag. compt. of cust., Apl., 1902, to Jan., 1903.
BYRNE, M.—Sec. to law dept., Victoria, Oct., 1896; formerly ch. clk. in same dept.

CADE, CHARLES SHERWOOD.—Capt. in the W. Cork artillery militia; served through the Zulu war, 1878-9; present at the battle of Ulundi; ast.-inspr., G.C.C., Mar., 1889; inspr., 11th Dec., 1882; adjt. and musketry instructor of the G.C.C., Sept., 1882, to May, 1883; local comdt. of pol., Cyprus, Sept., 1883; also asst. to commr., Nicosia, and gov., central prison, Oct., 1884; has acted several times as commr.

CADOGAN, 5TH EARL (Great Britain), GEORGE HENRY CADOGAN.—B. 1840; eldest son of the 4th earl, succeeded his father in 1873; was M.P. for Bath for a few months previous to his succession to the title; parl. under sec. for war, May, 1875, to 2nd March, 1878; parl. under sec. for the col., 2nd Mar., 1878, to 28th Apr., 1880; lord privy seal in Lord Salisbury's 1st and 2nd ministries; lord-lieut. of Ireland, 28th June, 1895-1902.
CALDER, CHARLES MACLEAR.—Admitted solr., sup. ct., Jamaica, 1881; clk. of cts., parish of
PORTLAND, APR., 1888; Ditto, parish of St. Mary's, Feb., 1889; res. mag., Trelawney, Jan., 1897.

CALVERT, JOHN J., I.S.O. (1904).—Ck., col. sec.'s office, N. S. Wales, 1853; clk. of select comtees, legis. assem., May, 1856; 1st clk. legis. coun., Apr., 1859; clk. asst., Jan., 1860; clk. of the parlms., Apr., 1871.

CAMERON, ALLAN DUNCAN.—Ed., Ireland; cler. asst., inspr. of villages, and sec., bd. of health, B.G., 14th Apr., 1890; 6th cls. clk.; inspr. of pris., Oct., 1893; 5th ditto, 9th Feb., 1896; gov't. offr., dep. of mines, Bartika, 8th Dec., 1896; acted as navg. offr. for Michael McTurk, C.M.G., 2nd Mar., 1897, to 8th Dec., 1898; ag. magst.'s clk., Bartika, 1897-98; transfld., same appt., to Koribae station, 19th Oct., 1899; J.P., 30th Oct., 1899; magst.'s clk., Corentyne jud. dist., 1st May, 1900; 3rd cls. clk., pol. dep., 1st Nov., 1901; commanded B. Guiana militia as lieut., 21st Nov., 1901; recommended by H.E. Sir J. A. Swettenham for appt. as hon. A.D.C. with rank of capt., Aug., 1902.

CAMERON, MAJ.-GEN. DONALD RODERICK, C.M.G. (1877).—B. 1834; entered R.A. 1856; capt. 1866, maj. 1875, lieut.-col. 1882, col. 1886; ret. 1888; instr. in gunnery, 1869-62, 15th Bde. R.A.; served throughout Bhootan campaign, 1864-66, as adjt. and as staff offr. of offr. comdg. R.A., Doonar Field Force (medal with clasp, thrice mentioned in despatches); adjt. 1st Bde. R.A., 1867-8; accompanied the Hon. W. McDougall, C.B., to Fort Garry, as a mem. of the exec. coun., N.W. Territories, 1869; was comsnr. internat. bdry. coman. and suptd. the expnd. which marked the internat. bdry. from the Lake of the Woods to the summit of the Rocky Mountains, 1872-6; reported on the Br.-Alaskan bdry., 1874; sec. to Canadian delegation at the Paris internat. confce., 1883, for the protection of submarine cables; reported on the E. bdry. of Br. Columbia, 1884; and again on the Alaskan bdry. in 1886; priv. sec. to Sir C. Tupper, Canadian plenip. to the fishery confce., 1881; principal, Roy. mil. coll., Kingston, Canada, 1888. Ret. 1896.

CAMERON, DONALD CHARLES.—Ed. at Rathmines School, Dublin; entd. Br. Guiana civ. ser., 1890; 5th class clk., secretariat, 1891; 4th ditto, 1895; 3rd class and despatch clk., 1895; 2nd class, 1899; priv. sec. to ag. gov'r., 1896, 1897, 1898 and 1901; ag. asst. gov. sec. and clk. of councils, 1900-1901, principal clk., secretariat, 1901; priv. sec. to gov'r. of Newfoundland (with permission of S. of S.) whilst on leave, 1902; asst. col. sec., Mauritius, July, 1904.

CAMERON, EDWARD J.—Ed. at Shrewsbury schl., Clifton Coll., and Merton Coll., Oxon; priv. sec. to Sir C. C. Lee, gov', Bahamas, Jan., 1882; and as gov. of the Leeward Is., Jan., 1884; asst. col. sec. and treas., S. Leone, Nov., 1884; J.P. for the Sttlmt., and visiting justice, Free-town Gaol; ag. col. sec. and treas., Dec., 1885, to July, 1886; pres., Virgin Is., Feb., 1887; mem. Leeward Is. legis. coun., 1887, and exec. coun., 1888; comsnr., Turks Is., Mar., 1893; ag. judge sup. ct., May to Dec., 1893, Jan. to June, 1895, and Apl. to July, 1898; admtr., St. Vincent, May, 1901.

CAMERON, MAJOR MAURICE ALEXANDER, C.M.G. (1900), R.E.—B. 1855; first coman. in Roy. engrs., Aug., 1874; dept. col. engrs., S. Sttlmt., Dec., 1883, to Apr., 1892; during which he acted for about two years and a half as col. engrn. and survr.-gen., and was a mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; apptd. one of the crown agts. for the colonies, July, 1895.

CAMPBELL, DOUGLAS GRAHAM.—2nd Surveyor, P.W.D., Selangor, S. Sttlmt., 1883; ditto, land office, 1885; asst. dist. offr., Rawang, 1887; sec. to res., 1888; dist. offr., Kuala Langat, 1890; ditto, Ulu Selangor, 1893; ditto, Klang, 1897; ditto and asst. Indian immigtn. agt., 1898; dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, 1899; sec. to res., 1901; res. of Negri Sembilan, 1904; has acted as res. on several occasions, and also as sec. to gov't.

CAMPBELL, SIR GEORGE W. R., K.C.M.G. (1891), C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1835; Ensign, Argyll and Bute rifles, Jan., 1855; lieut. same year; asst.-supt. Bombay rev. survey, 1896; on the outbreak of the mutiny in 1857, apptd. adjt. of the Ahmedabad Kol corps; asst. supt. of pol. and asst. mag. (has mutiny medal), supt. of pol., and mag. of the first class and comdt. of the Rutnaghrh rangers, Dec., 1859; promoted in 1863, and selected to reorganise the Canara pol. force; in 1866 in command of the Belgaum pol. Received numerous commendations from the Bombay gov't., and from the govt.-gen. and secs. of state for India for special services, and received two special donations of 3,500 rs. and 7,500 rs. for special services. Passed exams. in Hindostanee, Goojerathi, Marathi, and Canarese. In 1866 selected to take pol. charge of Ceylon, and to reorganise its constab., receiving a special gratuity of 1,000l. for this service. In 1868 confirmed as inspr.-gen.; ag. lieut.-gov., Penang, 1872-3; cholera comsnr. to Jaffna in 1866 and 1877; inspr.-gen. of prisons from Jan., 1880; was in charge of Egyptian exiles; ret. 1891.

CAMPBELL, H. C., L.S.O. (1903).—Second clk. in the deeds registry office, Natal, Apr., 1861; 2nd clk. audit office, Jan., 1863; 1st clk. R.M. ct., co. Pietermaritzburg, Mar., 1868; R.M., div. of Ixopo, Mar., 1876; ag. R.M. div. of Umguni, Nov., 1876; R.M., Inanda, and J.P. for col., 1880; master and regiar., sup. ct., 1882; col. audr., Apr., 1889; acted as judge native high ct., and as 2nd puisne judge and atty.-gen.; audit-gen., 1894; chmn. civ. ser. bd.; judge president, native high ct., 1899; judge, spec. treas. ct., 1900; resumed duty as judge pres., nat. high ct., 1901.

CAPE TOWN, ARCHBISHOP OF, MOST REV. WILLIAM WEST JONES, D.D.—Consec. 1874; metropolitan of the church of the prov. of S. Africa; formerly fellow of St. John's Coll., Oxon; preacher at Chapel Royal, Whitehall, and rural dean of Oxford.
CAPPER, THOMAS.—Ed. Trin. Coll., Camb.: B.Sc. (Lond.), 1876; B.A., Camb. (Math. and Nat. Sc. Triposes), 1879; inspr. of schools, Jamaica, Oct., 1880; official mem. of legis. coun., 1882.

CARBONE, SIR JOSEPH, LL.D. (1862), C.M.G. (1887), K.C.M.G. (1891), C.K.V.O. (1903).—B. 1839; received at the Malta Bar, 1863; reporter, ct. of commerce, 1868; crown advoc. and gov't. legal adviser, 1890; mem. of exec. and legis. coun., ex officio; ch. justice, Malta, 1895; vice-pres. legis. coun., 1898.

CARDEN, J.—Shorthand writer in office of asst. sec. (for India) to Roy. coman. Col. and Indian Exhib., 1886; diploma for services; entered service of Roy. Niger Company, Jan., 1887; priv. sec. to Sir G. Goldie, Jan., 1887, to Dec., 1889; transferred to C.O. as supplementary clk., Jan., 1900.

CARDEW, COL. SIR FRED., K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1894).—B. 1839; engaged in N.W. Frontier of India campaign, 1863, and Zulu and Transvaal campaigns, 1879-81; D.A.Q.M.G. in S. Africa, 1879-80; asst. mil. sec. China, 1882-3; sub-comsnr., Zululand, 1884-6; A.A.G., S. Africa, 1890; res. comsnr. Zululand, 1890; gov. S. Leone, 1894 to 1900.

CARLILE, EDWARD, K.C.—Gold medallist in law, Melbourne Univ., 1868; clk., census office, Victoria, May, 1861; regis.-gen.'s office, Jan., 1862; crown law offices, Mar., 1865; called to the bar, Victoria, 1871; parly. and professional ast. to crown law offs., 1873; parly. draftsman, July, 1879; clk.-astt., legis. assem., Apr., 1882; re-apptd. parly. draftsman with higher status, Apr., 1889; author of "Notes on Victoria Constitution," "Victorian Year Book, 1883-4."

CARLING, THE HON. JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1893).—B.1828; mem. for London, Ontario, 1857-74; elected to Dominion house of commons on confederation, 1867; rec-gen. of Canada, 1862; min. of wks. and agricul., Ontario, 1867-71; again elected for London, 1878; sworn of the privy coun. and made poetmr.-gen. in Sir J. Macdonald's govt., 1882; min. of agricul., 1865 to 1892; mem. of cabinet without portfolio, 1892; ret., 1894.

CARMODY, P.—Entered Imp. service after open competn., 1876; inland rev. laboratory scholarship, 1880; diploma (1st class) Roy. Schl. of Mines; astt. analyst and subsequently analyst, Somerset House, 1881-90; gov't. analyst, Trinidad, and prof. of chemistry, Queen's Roy. Coll. and Coll. of the Immaculate Conception, June, 1890; mem. central agricul. bd., 1890; mem. bd. of health, 1891; is F.I.C., F.C.S., mem. of soc. of chem. industry, and author of prize paper on "Recent Progress of Invention and Science," and other published papers on scientific subjects.
CARON, L. B.—Puissant judge, superior ct., Quebec, 1874.

CARON, THE HON. SIR J. P. R. A., K.C.M.G. (1885), K.C.—B. 1842; called to the bar of Lower Canada (now Province of Quebec) 1865; created a Q.C., 1876; entered the Canadian House of Commons, 1873, where he still sits; min. of militia and defence for the Dominion of Canada, 8th Nov., 1880; postmr.-gen., 1892 to 1896; one of representatives of Canada at col. confe., Ottawa, 1894.

CARENA, ALBERT EMANUEL E.O.—Ed. at St. Gregory's R.C. gram. schl., Lagos; astt. clk. har. dept., 1894; clk. and storekeeper, 1895; messenger and copyist, Queen's advocate's off., 1896; 2nd clk. gen. registry, 1897; Queen's advoc. clk., 1897; assisted as clk. in the trade comsn., 1898; sec. to the comsn. of inquiry on the conduct of Dr. D. J. Jones, 1899; promoted 3rd class clk., gov'r's office, 1901; sec. to the comsn. of inquiry on the railway accident at Owowo, 1901; sec. to the comsn. of inquiry on the customs dept., 1901; acted as regis. of corresp., secretariat, 1902; on spec. serv. to Ekiti countries, 1903; ag. conf. clk., clk. of ex. and leg. couns., 1904.

CARRINGTON, EARL (created 1895), VISCOUNT WENDOVER (U.K.), BARON CARRINGTON (Great Britain, 1797, and Ireland, 1796), CHARLES R. W. CARRINGTON, P.C. (1881), G.C.M.G. (1885).—B. 1843; ed. at Eton and Trin. Coll., Camb., B.A. 1863; late capt. Roy. Horse Guards, lieut.-col. 3rd batt. Oxford Light Infantry; capt. Queen's Body Guard, 1881 to 1885; gov. of N. S. Wales, 1885-90; lord chamberlain, 1892-95; joint hered. lord great chamberlain of England.

CARRINGTON, MAJOR-GEN. SIR FREDERICK, K.C.B. (1897), K.C.M.G. (1887), C.M.G. (1880).—B. 1844; ed. Chelt. Coll.; 24th regt.; comdr. of the F. L. H. in the Transkei war, S. Africa, 1877-78; commanded Transvaal volrs. against Sikukuni, 1878-79; commanded left wing C.M.R., 1879 to 1883; commanded col. forces in Basuto war, severely wounded; commanded 2nd mounted rifles, Bechuanaland field force, 1884; comdt. of pol., Br. Bechuanaland, 1889; comdt. native forces, Zululand, in 1888; again in command Bechuanaland pol., Dec., 1888-93; and again on special service, Nov., 1893, in connection with Matabele war; comdg. brigade at Gibraltar, 1895; commanded forces in Matabele war, 1896; resumed command at Gibraltar, 1897; commanded Rhodesian Field Force during S. Afr. war, 1900.

CARRINGTON, SIR JOHN WORRELL, KNT. (1897), C.M.G. (1888), K.C., F.R.G.S., D.C.L., LL.D.—B. 1847; ed. Codrington Coll., Barbados (S.C.C., 1866); and Line. Coll., Oxon (B.A., 1872; M.A., 1891); called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, June, 1872; acted as judge, astt. ct. of appeal, Barbados, 1874-5; J.P., 1874; mem. house of assem., 1874-8, and again 1881; M.L.C., 1878-81; solr.-gen., 1878; again acted as judge, astt. ct. of appeal, 1879; and as atty.-gen., 1880; hon. D.C.L., Durham, 1879; hon. LL.D., St. Andrews, 1894. Was pres. of the educn. bd., and of the bd. of health, and received the thanks of govt. for services in connection with educn.; ch. justice, St. Lucia and Tobago, 1882; on special duty as ag. col. sec., Grenada, 1882; admstd. gov't. of Tobago, 1883 to 1885; prepared revised edition Tobago laws; ag. ch. justice of Grenada, 1886; in 1887-9 prepared a new edition of the laws of St. Lucia; atty.-gen. Br. Guiana, Dec., 1888; chancellor of the diocese, 1890; engaged, 1891-2, in drafting for the govt. of Br. Guiana bills relating to the cts., criminal law and procedure, etc., of that col.; chmn. central bd. of health, 1889, of the opening-up-the-country comsn., 1891-2, and of the spirits ordinance comsn., 1893-4; maj. in the Br. Guiana militia, 1893. In 1894 prepared a new and revised edition of the laws of Br. Guiana; ch. justice, Hong Kong, 1896; ret. 1902.

CARTER, SIR GILBERT T., K.C.M.G. (1893), C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1848; entered navy 1864; paymr. of col. steamer "Sherbro." S. Leone, Aug., 1870; served on the G. Coast during a part of the Ashanti campaign; was one of the comsnrs. for valuing the ordnance and stores left behind by the Dutch at the transfer of Elmina; priv. sec. to gov. Leeward Is., Sept., 1875; collr. of customs and treas., G. Coast, Aug., 1879; treas. and postmr., Gambia, Oct., 1882; admstd. the govt. several times; admstr. of Gambia on separation from S. Leone, Nov., 1888; gov., Lagos, 1890-96; gov., Bahamas, 1898; gov., Barbados, 23rd July, 1904.

CARTER, WILLIAM STUART.—After compet. exam., apptd. copyist to the treasy., cust., and post office, Br. Honduras, Feb., 1894 ag. 5th clk. of same, Sep. to Dec., 1895; 4th clk. Mar. to June, 1896; ag. clk. to regis.-gen., June to Sep., 1898, Mar. to Oct., 1899, and Mar. to Oct., 1900; ag. clk. to col. engnr., Oct. to Feb., 1900.

CARTWRIGHT, THE RIGHT HON. SIR RICHARD JOHN, P.C. (1902), G.C.M.G. (1897), K.C.M.G. (1879).—B. 1835; entered Canadian parlmt., 1863; sworn of the privy coun., and was min. of finance, 7th Nov., 1873, until the resig. of the Mackenzie cabinet, Oct., 1878; min. of trade and commerce in Sir W. Laurier's govt., June, 1896.
CARTY, HON. M. H., K.C.—M.H.A. St. George, 1882-1897, Placentia, 1897, mem. exec. coun. (without portfolio), 1897.
CASALOFT, SIR L. N., KT. BACH. (1894).—B. 1822; Ch. justice super. ct., Quebec, 1894.

CASEY, THE HON. JAS. JOSEPH, C.M.G. (1878).—B. 1831; barrister-at-law; M.L.A., Victoria, 1861-1879; mem. exec. coun., 1868; min. of justice, 1868; solr.-gen., 1869; pres. of bd. of land and wks. and min. of agricul., 1872 to 1875; pres. of Victoria coman. to Paris Exhibn., 1878; exec. vice-pres. of Melbourne internat. exhibn., 1880; pres. of fed. bk. of Australia; is now judge of insolvency ct. and chmn. gen. sessions; judge of cts. of mines and co. cts.; is knight offr. of the legion of honour and of the crown of Italy.

CASOLANI, EDWARD ROBERT.—Apptd. clk., Sep., 1878, and higher div. clk., Mar., 1883, on the high comsnr's estab., Cyprus; transf. to the ch. sec.'s estab., Mar., 1884; ag. loc. comdt. of pol. and asst. comsnr., Nicosia, July to Sep., 1898; ch. clk. and sub. loc. comdt. of pol., Jan., 1899; again ag. loc. comdt., Nicosia, May to Oct., 1902.

CASOLANI, WILLIAM.—Clk. port dept., Malta, May, 1863; cust. dept., Jan., 1865; 2nd class clk., ch. sec.'s office, June, 1880; 1st class clk. and asst. clk. to the coun., Jan., 1886; dep. asst. sec. to govt. and ch. clk., July, 1891; asst. sec. to govt. and clk. to exec. coun. and coun. of govt., July, 1894; rec. gen. and director of contracts, 1902; mem. of ex. coun. and coun. of govt.
CASSERLY, JAMES MICHAEL.—3rd class clk., col. sec.'s office, Jamaica, 1878; 2nd class clk., Nov., 1885.
CASTOR, C. F., M.B., C.M., F.R.G.S.—Asst. col. surg., G. Coast Col., Feb., 1879; med. offr., Br. Guiana, 1888; med. supt. leper asylum, 1887; is J.P. and coroner.

CATTI, THE HON. A.—Mem. house of assem., S. Australia, since 1881; comsnr. of crown lands, S. Australia, June, 1881, to June, 1884; comsnr. of pub. wks., June, 1887, to June, 1889; chmn. of comtees., legis. assem., 1890.

CAVENDISH, A., B.A. Oxon.—Cadet, S.S., Jan., 1901; ag. 3rd mag., Penang; ag. 4th mag., Singapore; passed final exam in Malay, May, 1903; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, Oct., 1903; ag. dist. offr., Nibong Tebal, 23rd Jan., 1904.

CAYLEY, SIR RICHARD (KNT., 1882).—B. 1833; M.A. and scholar of St. John's Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, June, 1862; advoc. sup. ct. of Ceylon, June, 1863; dep. Queen's advoc., May, 1868; puisne judge of the sup. ct., 1873; Queen's advoc., 1876; ch. justice, 1879; resig., 1883; is a mag. for Rutland.

CELESTIN, LOUIS ALBERT.—Ed. govt. normal schl., Mauritius; teacher in edn. dept., 1874; ag. inspr. of schls., Nov., 1886, to Aug., 1889; dist. cashier, Dec., 1889, to Nov., 1893; ch. inspr. of distilleries, Nov., 1893; ag. supt. of distilleries, Apr. to Oct., 1895; dist. cashier, Flaq., June, 1898; Rose Hill, Jan., 1901; 2nd cls. clk., treasy., June, 1903.

CHADWICK, JOHN COURTENAY CHARSMAN.—Clk. and interp. to the R.M., Inanda div., Natal, Jan., 1868; clk. of the ct., Oct., 1870; acted as R.M. and admstr. of native law on various occasions, 1872-7, at Inanda; J.P. for the Inanda div., Aug., 1870; regist. of the native high ct., and sec. to native admstr. bd., Nov., 1876; admitted an atty. of the sup. ct., 1878; first class interp. on the staff of Maj.-Gen. Newdigate during Zulu War of 1879 (medal and clasp); admstr. of native law and border agt., Upper Tugela, 1880; R.M., Lions River div., and J.P. for the col., 1882; R.M. and admstr. of native law, Ixopo, Sep., 1886; mag., Lions River div., 1897-8; Umgeni divn., 1898.

CHADWICK, OSBERT, C.E., C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1834; has been employed by the C.O. to report on the drainage and water supply of various cols.; was consult. engrnr. for the Malta water wks. and Hong Kong water supply and drainage.

CHALMERS, A. J., M.D., Ch.B. (Victoria Univ., Eng.), F.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—Asst. col. surg., G. Coast, 1897; ag. ch. med. offr., 1900, and during siege of Kumassi, 1900 (ment. in desp., medal and clasp); registr., med. coll., Ceylon, 22nd Feb., 1901.

CHAMBERLAIN, THE RT. HON. JOSEPH, P.C. (1880), M.P., Hon. LL.D. (Camb.), Hon. D.C.L. (Oxford).—Eldest son of the late Mr. Joseph Chamberlain; born in London, 1836, and ed. at University Coll. schl., after which he was engaged in business in Birmingham till 1874, when he retired. In 1870 he was elected a mem. of the Birmingham town coun., and in the same year a mem. of the schl. bd., of which he became chmn. in 1873. In each of the three following years, 1874-5-6, he was elected mayor of the borough, and in June, 1876, on the retirement of Mr. Dixon, one of the three representatives of Birmingham, he was returned to Parlmt. At the general election of 1880 he was again returned with the late Mr. John Bright and Mr. Philip Muntz as his colleagues, and on the formation of Mr. Gladstone's adminstr. he was apptd. pres. of the bd. of trade, with a seat in the cabinet. While at the bd. of trade he prepared and succeeded in passing through Parlmt. the Bankruptcy Act, which is now in force, the Patents Act of 1883, and other measures. After the redistribution of seats in 1885 he was returned as mem. for W. Birmingham, and joined Mr. Gladstone's 3rd adminstr. in 1886, as pres. of the local govt. bd., but resigned in consequence of his disagreement with the premier's home rule policy. At the general election of 1886 he was returned unopposed for W. Birmingham, and at the end of 1887 proceeded to the U. States as plenipotentiary to negotiate, in conjunction with H.M.'s min. there and Sir Charles Tupper, a treaty for the settlement of the Atlantic fisheries question. The result of these negotiations was the convention of 1888, and though it was rejected by the U.S. senate, the modus vivendi established by it still regulates the fisheries. On the defeat of Lord Rosebery's govt. in June, 1895, Mr. Chamberlain joined Lord Salisbury's govt. as sec. of state for the col., and continued to hold that office in Mr. Balfour's cabinet, 1902. At the general elections of 1895 and 1900, in both of which he took a very prominent part, he was again returned for W. Birmingham. At the end of 1902 he visited S. Africa, to deal with questions connected with the settlement of the country; Lord Rector, Glasgow Univ., 1896; Chancellor, Birmingham Univ., 1901; resigned office in connection with the question of a new fiscal policy, Oct., 1903.

CHAMBERLAYNE, MAJOR TANKERVILLE JAMES.—Ed. Chelt. Coll.; ensign, Ceylon rifles, June, 1868; transf. to 80th regt., Oct., 1873; fort adjt., Hong Kong, June, 1875, to Mar., 1877; ag.-brig.-maj., Nov., 1875, to Feb., 1876; officiated twice as asst. mil. sec., China command; served in Transvaal and throughout Zulu war, 1878-79; present at Ulundi (medal and clasp, 1878-79); A.D.C. to gov. of Natal (Sir H. Bulwer, G.C.M.G.), 3rd Feb., 1882; priv. sec., Mar., 1882; also ag. clk. exec. coun., July, 1885; priv. sec. to high comsnr. of Cyprus, and clk., exec. coun., Mar., 1886; local comdt. of pol., 1892; col. of customs and excise and asst. to comsnr., Larnaca, 1894; ag. comsnr., Larnaca, June-Oct., 1895; Kyrenia, Nov., 1896-Apr., 1897; Nicosia, June-Nov., 1897; Kyrenia, Apr., 1898; comsnr. and local comdt. of pol., Kyrenia, 1899; ag. comsnr., Larnaca, June-Nov., 1900; do. Nicosia, Jan. to May, 1900, Nov., 1900, to Oct., 1901, and Aug., 1902, to Nov., 1903.

CHAMBERS, CECIL H.—Cashier, St. John's savings bank, Antigua, 1893; accountant, ditto, 1894; 4th out-door offr., treasy. and cust. dept., 1895; 3rd in-door offr., treasy., Antigua, 1897; audit clk., Dominica, 1900; audit clk., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1901; ag. chief audit clk. and federal acctnt., Antigua, 1902; ag. 1st treasy. offr., Montserrat; M.L.C.; comsnr. for oaths, Nov., 1903, to Mar., 1904.

CHAMBERS, JOHN RATCLIFFE.—2nd clk. to pres. St. Kitts, May, 1875; 3rd landing waiter, treasy., Dec., 1878; 1st clk. to regar. and provost-marshal, Apr., 1881; sec. of the comsn. to inquire into the general condition of Dominica, Nov., 1881; priv. sec. to gov. Leeward Is., 1882 to 1883; clk. to mag., dist. C. St. Kitts, July, 1883; dep. coroner, May, 1885; qtrmr. with St. Kitts militia, June, 1885; J.P., and visiting justice of the gaol; ag. mag. dist. C, 1899, 1902, 1903 and 1904.

CHANDLER, WILLIAM KELLMAN, C.M.G. (1902), B.A., LL.D.—B. 1857; St. John's Coll., Camb. (1875-79); law tripes, 1879-80; called to the bar. Inner Tem., June, 1879; ag. escheator-gen. of Barbados, 1880-81; J.P., 1880; apptd. comsnr. of probates, Dec., 1880; ag. pol. mag. of Bridgetown, Aug. to Nov., 1880; ag. solr.-gen., Nov., 1880, to Mar., 1881; ag. judge of asst. ct. of appeal, Mar., 1881, to June, 1883; master-in-chancery, May, 1882; judge of asst. ct. of appeal, June, 1883; mem. of house of assen., Mar., 1881; represented parish of St. Peter until Nov., 1884; M.L.C., 1884; represented Barbados in negotiations at Washington in connection with McKinley Tariff Act, 1891; ag. ch. just., Nov., 1901, to Apr., 1902; ag. col. sec., July, 1902, to Feb., 1903, and Apr. to Oct., 1903.

CHAPMAN, ARTHUR.—Assessor of taxes, Hong Kong, 1889; ag. asst. supt. fire brigade, 1891-92; J.P., 1894; lieut. Hong Kong volunteer corps, 1893; capt., 1897; major, 2nd in command, 1899; ag. comdt. on several occasions; thanks and medal for services during plague, 1894; mem. of comitee. on volunteer corps, 1898; commanded volunteers during disturbances in New Territory, 1899; commanded Hong Kong and China Coronation contingent, 1902.
CHAPMAN, FRANK.—3rd class clk., audit dept., Natal, Feb., 1898; 2nd class clk., Jan., 1900; 1st class clerk, Jan., 1901; asst. inspr., audit dept., July, 1901.

CHATHAM, WILLIAM, M.I.C.E.—Ed. Roy. High Schl. and Univ., Edin.; asst. to Messrs. Thos. Meik and Son, C.E., Edin., 1880; res. engrnr., Bo'ness drainage works, 1883; asst. engrnr. dock engrnr.'s office, Bristol, 1885; exec. engrnr. P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1890; ag. dir. of pub. wks., 1893-94-97-1901; mem. legis. coun., 1897; asst. dir. P.W., 1898; director, 1901; mem. exec. coun., 1901; pres. of san. bd., 1901.

CHERMSIDE, SIR HERBERT CHARLES, R.E., G.C.M.G. (1899), K.C.M.G. (1896), C.B.—B. 1850; Roy. Eng., 1870; during Russo-Turkish war, 1876-78, accompd. Turkish troops as mil. attache; served in Egy. camp, 1882; and was attached to Egy. army, 1883 to 1888; in 1896 became Brit. mil. comsnr. and commander of Brit. troops in Crete, and for services there was made K.C.M.G.; appointed to the Curragh district in 1899, and has since served in S. Afr. in commd. of 3rd infant. div.; gov. of Queensland, 1901; resigned 1904.

CHERRY, JAS. WM.—Ed. at Bedford Modern schl.; apptd. asst. ch. clk. and acctnt., P.W.D., G. Coast. 9th July, 1898; ch. clk. and acctnt., 1st Jan., 1900; off. mem. Accra town coun., 1898, 1899, 1900 and 1901; chrmn. streets and roads comte.; apptd. 2nd lieut. G. C. rifle volunteers, 16th Nov., 1898; lieut. 17th Aug., 1900; acted adjutant, 3rd May to 30th June, 1899, and 26th June, 1900 to 1st June, 1901; apptd. capt., 12th Oct., 1901; ag. offr. commanding, Dec., 1901, to July, 1902; certificate, army form, E 511, Chelsea barracks, Oct., 1901.

CHICHESTER, ATHOL AUGUSTUS.—Joined H.M.S. "Worcester" (training-ship), 1876; served in H.M. hospital ship, "Ganges," during Egyptian campaign, 1885; temp. trav. inspr. of bd. of agri. under cont. diseases acts, 1890 to 1892; apptd. asst. dist. comsnr. Niger Coast Prot., May, 1897; dist. comsnr. Sapeli, 1900-02; transf. to Asaba, Aug., 1902; trav. comsnr., 17th Jan., 1903; ag. divisional comsnr., Central Division, Apr. to Sept., 1903, and again in 1904.

CHICHESTER, CHARLES RENFREY.—Ed. Stonyhurst Coll. and Dublin Univ.; B.A. Dub.; L.R.C.P. and L.R.C.S., Ireland; diplomate in public health; licentiate in midwifery, Rotunda, Dub.; dipl. in pub. vaccination; asst. col. surg., Gambia, Mar., 1896; ag. col. surg., Apr. to Nov., 1896, May to Dec., 1898; pres. of bd. of health whilst ag. col. surg.; mem. quarantine bd.; sen. med. offr., So. Nigeria, 1904.

CHILD, LIEUT. HERBERT A.—Entered H.M. navy as cadet, 15th July, 1883; spechy. promoted to midshipman, 23rd July, 1885; served in H.M. ships, "Conquest," "Triumph," "Calypso," "Minotaur," "Duke of Wellington," "Pilot," and "Thrush"; spechy. promoted to lieut., July, 1891; landed with nav. brig. in Gambia exped., 1891-92 (West African medal and clasp); resigned comsnr., Feb., 1894, to take up appt. as ast. supt. marine in N. C. P., 4th May, 1894; took part in naval operations against Chief Nana of Benin river, 1894 (mentioned in desps., 2nd clasp); took part in naval operations against Brass chiefs, 1895 (3rd clasp); sup. of marine, S. Nig., 15th Jan., 1897; took part in naval operations against King of Benin, 1897 (mentioned in desps., 4th clasp); took part in Aro exped., 1901-2, having organisation and charge of marine transport arrangements (mentioned in desps., African gen. serv. medal and clasp); comsnr. as lieut. R.N. restored 21st Aug., 1902, on joining emergency list of retired officers, R.N.

CHIPPENDALL, GEORGE HERBERT.—Sub-lieut. 3rd foot, 1874; lieut. East Kent regt., 1874; capt. Nov., 1883; maj. W. Riding regt., Dec., 1893; lieut.-col., Nov., 1898; comdt. defence force, W. Australia, Dec., 1898; transf. to commonwealth govt., 1901.

CHITTY, LOUIS OGILVY.—Ed. Trin. Coll., Dub.; asst. mr., Harrison Coll., Barbados, 1883-91; headmr. gram. schl., St. Vincent, Sept., 1891, to Dec., 1893; inspr. of schls., St. Vincent, Jan., 1894, to Dec., 1896; inspr. of schls., Jamaica, Jan., 1897, to Mar., 1898; audr. and inspr. of schls., Seychelles, Apr., 1898; audr. only, 1902.
CHURCH, THE HON. C. E.—Mem. for Lunenburg in Novia Scotian legis. assem.; comsnr. for pub. wks. and mines.

CHURCHILL, JOHN SPENCER, C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1835; Ed. at Winchester; ensign, 46th regt., 1854; served at siege of Sebastopol (medal and clasp); capt. 36th regt., 1866; A.D.C. to Lord Lisgar, when lord high comsnr. of the Ionian Is., 1857; pres. of Virgin Is., 1879; ag. pres., Nevis, 1879, and of Dominica, 1882 to 1887; pres.. Montserrat, 1887; comsnr. of St. Kitts-Nevis, 1888; col. sec., Bahamas, 1895.

CLANLIS, J. B.—Chief clk., Port dept., Gibraltar; supplementary clk., Port dept., Sept., 1880; 3rd cl. clk. and clk. to regis. of shipping, Feb., 1883; 2nd cl. clk., Jan., 1892; 1st cl. clk. and clk. to shipping mr., Jan., 1898.

CLARE, HENRY LEWIS.—Ed. Rathmines schl. and Trin. Coll., Dub.; B.A. (1879), M.B., and B. Ch. (1880); M.D., 1897; D.P.H.; jun. res. med. offr., pub. hosp., Kingston, Jamaica, Apl., 1881; dist. med. offr., Dry River dist. and hosp., July, 1882; transf. to Vere dist., 1883; to Chapelton, 1888; to Spanish Town, 1892; surg. to St. Catherine dist. prison, and to Middlesex and Surrey cty. gaol; dist. med. offr., Kingston, June, 1896; mem. cent. bd. of health.
CLARENCE, RIVERLEY CHARLES.—Clk. to regis., sup. ct., Natal, Apl., 1875; asst. clk. of the peace, and prosecutor of the native high ct., Natal, Apl., 1880; clk. of the peace, 1896.
CLARK, ALFRED ALLEN.—Forester, Ceylon, 1st May, 1875; asst. conservator of forests, 1st Jan., 1889; ag. conservator, Jan., 1901, to June, 1902.
CLARK, ANDREW INGLIS.—Atty.-gen. of Tasmania in the Fysh govt., 29th Mar., 1887-92; mem. of the fed. coun. of Australasia, Jan., 1888; and of fed. convention, 1891; puisne judge, 1898.

CLARK, FRANCIS W., M.D. (Durham), 1900; M.B., 1892; D.P.H. (Camb.), 1891; M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (Lond.), 1886.—Ed. St. Paul's schl., and St. Barts. and Middlesex hosps.; med. offr. of health and supt. fever hosp., Lowestoft, 1893; med. offr. of health, Hong Kong, 1896; Dean, coll. of med. for Chinese; mem. sanitary board; J.P. 1896; mem. legis. coun., 1902.
CLARK, THOMAS.—Apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of civ. serv., and assigned to C.O., Apl., 1902.
CLARK, WM. MORTIMER, K.C.—Called to the bar, Ontario, 1861; Q.C., 1887; senator of Toronto univ.; chrmn. of bd. of management of Knox Coll., Toronto, 1880; lieut.-gov., Ontario, Apl., 1903.

CLARKE, SIR CHAS. MANSFIELD, Bart., G.C.B. (1901); G.C.V.O. (1903).—B. 1839; entd. Army, 1850; served in N. Zealand, 1861-66 (medal); Zulu war (promoted brevet-col., C.B., medal and clasp), 1879; Basutoland, 1880-81; commdt.-gen. of col. forces at Cape of Good Hope, 1880-82; A.A.G., War Office, 1884; D.A.G., Ireland, 1886-88; maj.-gen. commanding 3rd infantry brig., Aldershot, 1889-92; D.A.G. at headquarters, 1892-93; com.-in-chief and lieut.-gen. commanding the Forces, Madras, 1893-98; quartermaster-gen. to the Forces, 1899; gov., Malta, 2nd Mar., 1903.
CLARKE, ERNEST D.—Dep.-regis., sup. ct., Trinidad.

CLARKE, SIR FIELDING, KT. BACH. (1894), LL.B.—B. 1851; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., Easter, 1876; N.E. circuit; atty.-gen. of Fiji, 1881; ag. ch. just. and ch. judicial comsnr. for the W. Pacific, 1882 to 1883, and again 1884; confirmed, 1886; puisne judge, Hong Kong, 1888; ch. just., 1891; ch. just., Jamaica, 1896.
CLARKE, GEORGE H.—Asst. res. just., Nicoll's Town, Andros Is., Bahamas, Dec., 1902.

CLARKE, Lieut.-Col. Sir GEORGE S., R.E., K.C.M.G. (1893), C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1848; entered R.E., 1868; capt., 1880; major, 1887; served in Egypt, 1882 (medal and star); in Suakin expdn., 1885, mentioned in despatches (clasp); sec. col. defence comtee., 1885-92; supt. of carriage factory, Woolwich, 1893; gov. Victoria, 1901-1904; apptd. as one of a comtee. to advise as to the creation of a Board for the administrative business of the War Office, Nov., 1903; sec. to Imperial Defence comtee., 1904.

CLARKE, Lieut.-Col. Sir MARSHAL JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1886), C.M.G. (1880), (late R.A.).—B. 1841; res. mag., co. Pietermaritzburg, Natal, 1874; A.D.C. to Sir T. Shepstone, H.M.'s special comsnr., S. Africa, 1876; polit. offr. and special comsnr., Lydenburg, 1877; served in Transvaal campaign 1890-1, in command of Landdrost's office, Potchefstroom; mentioned in despatches; res. mag. Quithin, Basutoland, 1881; comsnr., Cape pol., King William's Town, 1882; col. comdg. Turkish regt. Egyptian gendarmerie, 1882; 3rd class order of Medjidie; ret. from the army, with rank of lieut.-col., 1883; res. comsnr., Basutoland, 1884; res. comsnr. and ch. mag., Zululand, 1883; res. comsnr., Rhodesia, 1898.

CLARKE, WILLIAM JAMES.—Joined Natal Mounted Police, 1878; served through Zulu war, 1879 (medal with clasp); accompd. ex-Empress Eugenie on her tour through Natal and Zululand, 1880; served in Basutoland war, 1880 (medal with clasp); served in Transvaal campaign, 1881, present at battle of Laing's Neck; accompd. mission to Pondoland, 1887; with mission to Tongoland, under Col. Sir R. Martin, in 1888, and with the Portuguese-Swazi bndry. comsnr., 1888; ag. res. mag. and admstr. of Native law for Ixopo div., 1889-90; apptd. crim. investig. offr., 1895; commanded Natal police field force on S. border during disturbances in E. Grigualand, 1897, and in Zululand, 1898; took part in Boer war, 1899-1902; commanded N.P. field force, in operations round Ladysmith, and during the siege; present at capture of Boer guns on Gun hill, and at battle of Cesar's Camp, on 6th Jan., 1900; with cav. brig. in attack on Botha's Pass, 1900; attached to the F.I.D.; was intell. offr. with Gen. Dartnell's column in the operations under Gen. French in E. Transvaal, and was sent by the latter gen. on spec. mis. to Zululand, 1901; joined Gen. Bullock's col. as intell. offr. in the E. Transvaal, 1901; ag. intell. offr. with General Dartnell's col. in O.R.C., 1901. In charge of sec. serv. dept. in Natal, 1902; sent by Gen. Bruce Hamilton on spec. mis. to Zululand, and was afterwards intell. offr. on that gen.'s staff, in the operations in S.E. Transvaal (medal with 4 clasps); accompd. the Prime Min. of Natal to England, in the capacity of sec., to attend the coronation, 1902.
CLAXTON, T. FOLKES, F.R.A.S.—Joined mag. and met. dept. R. Obser., Greenwich, Feb., 1890; apptd. asst. dir. Royal Alfred obser., Mauritius, Jan., 1896; dir., Dec., 1896; is also sec. met. soc., Maur.

CLAYTON, A. G.—Ed. Marlbro' Coll.; 3rd class clk., col. sec. office, Jamaica, Sept., 1886; ch. clk., col. sec. office, Br. Honduras, July, 1889; clk. of councils, Jan., 1898; priv. sec. to gov. of Br. Honduras, Jan., 1897, to Oct., 1898; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Ceylon, 1900; addtl. asst. col. sec., 1901.

CLAYTON, L. H.—B.A., Camb.; cadet, S. S. Stltmins., Nov., 1895; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese at Singapore, May, 1897; also of Penang, Aug., 1897; passed final exam. in Chinese, Mar., 1899; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, Feb., 1899; confirmed 4th mag., Aug., 1899; ag. asst. protector of Chinese, Singapore, Oct. 1899; dist. offr., Christmas L., May, 1901; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Penang, Apr., 1904; asst. ditto, Singapore, May, 1904, but cont. to act in Penang.
CLEARE, W. T.—Resident just. and collr. of cust., Bimini, Bahamas, Nov., 1899; ditto, Long Island, Mar., 1901.

CLELAND, W. L., M.B., A.M., Edin. (1876).—Asst. med. offr. Derby county asyl., Eng., 1877; res. med. offr. Parkside lun. asyl., S. Australia, and asst. col. surg., 1878; res. med. offr. Adelaide and Parkside lun. asyls., and col. surg., S. Australia, 1896; ceased to be res. med. offr., Adelaide Lun. Asyl., which was closed in 1903.

CLEMENTI, C.—Ed. St. Paul's schl. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford (demy, 1894; B.A., 1898; M.A., 1901), hon. mention Hertford schl., 1895; lst cl. mods., 1896; hon. mention Ireland and Craven schl., 1896; Boden Sanskrit scholar, 1897; prox. acc. Gaisford (Greek Prose), 1897; 2nd cl. lit. hum., 1898; prox. acc. Chancellor's Latin essay, 1899; cadet, Hong Kong, 1899; passed cadet, 1900; ag. asst. col. sec., 1900-1; ag. clk. of council, 1900 and 1901; asst. regiar.-gen., Aug., 1901; mem. bd. of examms. in Chinese, Apr., 1902; J.P., June, 1902; ag. asst. col. sec., and clk. of councils, Sept., 1902, to June, 1903; sec. interpretation bd., Dec., 1902; seconded for famine relief work in Kwang Si, April to June, 1903; mem. of land ct., New Territory, 1903-4.

CLEVELAND, ROBT. ACHILLES.—Ed. Univ. Coll. (Lond.); M.R.C.S. (England); L.R.C.P. (Lond.); D.T.M.H. (Camb.); med. offr., Cayon dist., St. Kitts, 1890; ditto, Georgetown, Saint Vincent, 1892; dist. med. offr., Larnaca, Cyprus, 1894; ditto, Nicosia, 1895; ag. ch. med. offr., Cyprus, 1895 and 1900; mem. of municipal coman., Nicosia; passed colloquial standard exam. in mod. Greek.

CLIFFORD, HUGH CHARLES, C.M.G. (1900).—B. 1866; joined Perak service, 1883; passed in Malay, Feb., 1885; collr. land rev., Kwala Kangsar, Mar., 1885; on special service to Pahang, 1887; ag. gov.'s agt. there, 1887-8; supt., Ulu Pahang, 1889; ag. Br. res. Pahang, Sept., 1890, to Mar., 1891, Apr. to Dec., 1891, and from Feb., 1893; sec. to govt., Selangor, Dec., 1894, but continued to act as res. of Pahang; res. Pahang, July, 1896; gov. of Br. North Borneo and Labuan, 1899 to 1901; Br. res., Pahang, 1901; ag. col. sec., Trinidad and Tobago, Sept., 1903; admtd. govt., 26th Mar. to 30th Aug., 1904; col. sec., Trinidad and Tobago, 14th Sept., 1904.

CLIFTON, ROBERT CECIL, I.S.O.(1904).—Landing waiter and postmr., Bunbury, W. Australia, 1873; 2nd clk., lands and survey dept., 1874; 2nd clk., customs, 1876; ch. clk., land and survey dept., Apr., 1880; under sec. for lands, 1891.

CLINTONETT, R.
