J.—Entered provost marshal's office, Barbados, as jun. clk. in 1869; accntn. in 1870; acted as provost marshal from Feb. to Sept., 1879; again in 1880; resig. 1880; apptd. official assignee, 1st Oct., 1882; served as gen. of health and quarantine bds., from 10th Oct., 1899, to 6th May, 1901; acted as mast. in chancery, from Nov., 1901, to Apr., 1902; mem. of edcn. bd.; gov. of "Harrison" and "Queen" Colls. and Combermere schl.; represents parish of St. Michael in house of assem.
CLUTSAM, S. H. O.—Called to bar, Bahamas, July, 1868; ag. sec. to bd. of educn., 1882; ag. stip. and circuit mag. in 1887; asst. clk. and sergt.-at-arms, house of assem., 1866; ch. clk., 1890.

COATES, ARTHUR ROBERT.—Clk., immigr. dept., Fiji, 1889; inspr. int. immigr.; inspr. nat. lab., 1892; stip. mag., Savu Savu and Taviuni, 1900; provi. inspr., Macuata and Bua, 1901; agt.-gen., immigr. and supt., poly. depot, 1904.

COBHAM, C. DELAYAL, C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1842; ed. Rugby, and Univ. Coll., Oxon; B.A. (honours), 1866; B.C.L., M.A., 1869; M.R.A.S. ast. comsnr., Larnaca, Cyprus, Oct. 1, 1878; comsnr. of Larnaca, Mar. 18, 1879; ag. ch. sec. to govt., 1880; ag. ch. collr. of customs, 1886; has edited a Bibliography of Cyprus, and "Excerpta Cypria," and translated Mariti's "Travels in Cyprus," the story of "Omm Haram," and Graziani's narrative of the siege of Nicea and Famagusta.

COBLEY, WALTER HENRY, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1850; A.M.I.C.E.; Asst. engrmr., Natal rly., Feb., 1876; ag. maintenance engrmr., Jan., 1880, to 1881; maintenance engrmr., 1882; dist. engrmr., 1883; suptg. engrmr. of surveys and construction, 1897.

COCHRANE, C. S., M.I.C.E.—Grad. at Trin. Coll., Dub., 1868; was employed on rly. wks. in S. America, 1871-74; asst. engrmr. on the Cape Govt. rly., 1874; ag. dist. engrmr., 1877; engrmr. in the pub. wks. dept., Trinidad, 1878; engrmr. in charge of surveys, 1879.

COCHRANE, HON. THOMAS, H.A.E., M.P.—Ed. at Eton; served in 93rd Highlanders and Scots Guards; hon. major, 4th Batt. Argyll and Sutherland Highlanders; J.P., Fife; D.L. for Renfrewshire; M.P. for North Ayrshire; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, June, 1895, to Feb., 1901; on active service in the S. African War, 1900; U.S. of S., Home Office, 1902.

COCKBURN, THE HON. SIR JOHN ALEX., K.C.M.G. (1900); M.D. (Lond.).—B. 1850; mem. of house of assem., S. Australia, 1884-1898; min. of educn., 1885, to June, 1887; premier and ch sec., 1889-90; again min. of educn. and agricul., 1893; agt.-gen. for S. Australia in London, 1898-1901.

CODRINGTON, STEWART.—Cadet, S. Sttlmts., Nov., 1898; ag. sub.-inspr. of schls., Malacca, May, 1900; ag. supt. Malay coll., Mar., 1900; ag. distr. offr., Malacca, Oct., 1900; distr. offr., Penang, Aug., 1902; ag. dist. offr., B. Mertajam, June, 1904.
COEN, GEORGE.—Deputy min. of finance and sec. treasy. board, Newfoundland, 1898.

COGHLAN, T. A., I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1866; public service comanr. of New S. Wales; ed. Sydney gram. schl.; apptd. to pub. wks. dept., 1873; assoc. mem. Inst. of Civil Engrs., 1882; asst. engnr. harbours and rivers dept., 1884; govt. statistician, 1886; registr. of friendly societies, 1892; honry. fellow roy. statis. soc., 1893; mem. of pub. ser. bd., 1896; justice of the peace, 1896; roy. comanr. to inquire into the working of the marine bd., 1896; roy. comanr. to inquire into working of Sydney water supply and sewerage bd., 1897; supervised the N. S. Wales census of 1891; pres. of the confce. of Australian statisticians to determine the population of the colonies, and to arrange for a uniform census of Australasia, 1900; author of "Wealth and Progress of N. S. Wales," "The Seven Colonies of Australasia," "Childbirth, a Study in Statistics," "Notes on the Financial Aspect of Australian Federation," "Report on the Eleventh Census of N. S. W.," "Treatise on the Deaths of Women in Childbirth," &c.
COHEN, HON. H. E.—Col. treas., N. S. Wales, Dec., 1877, to Dec., 1878; judge, metropolitan ct., May, 1881; min. of jus., Jan., 1883, to 1885.
COHEN, W. S.—Ed. at Clifton Coll. and Trin. Coll., Camb.; 2nd class tripes, 1893; M.A.; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple; sec., imgrn. (temporary), O.R.C., 1902.

COKER, S. D. A.—Aast. clk., treasy., Gambia, May, 1873; 2nd landing waiter, customs, Aug., 1875; asst. clk., treasy., Feb., 1879; ch. clk., treasy., and P.O., Mar., 1883; ag. gov.'s clk.; clk. to leg. comr. and dep. coroner, June to Nov., 1889; cashier, Jan., 1892; in charge of the treasy., June to Nov., 1892.

COLE, FRED. EDWARD.—Ed. at Marlbro' Coll. and King's Coll., Lond.; called to the bar, Mid. Term., Feb., 1886; dist. commr., G. Coast Col., May, 1888; clk. of cta., St. Elizabeth, Jamaica, Nov., 1890; ag. res. mag., Apr., 1891; apptd. res. mag., St. Thomas, Jamaica, 1902.
COLE, G.—Inspr. of pub. schls., Bahamas, and sec. to the educn. bd., and J.P., Sept. 1882.

COLENBRANDER, HERMAN JAMES.—Clk. for imigrn., Zulu border agency, Lower Tugela div., Natal, Nov., 1881; dep. clk. of peace, Umsinga, Nov., 1887; clk. and interp. to R.M., Umvoti, Aug., 1889; clk. of the ct., Klip River div., 1895; ch. clk., Durban magistracy, 1901; second asst. mag., Durban div., 1902; J.P. for colony, 1904.

COLES, THE HON. SIR JENKIN, K.C.M.G. (1894).—B. 1842; Mem. of house of assem., S. Australia, 1875-8, and since 1881; commr. cr. lds., June, 1884, to Feb., 1885; June, 1887, to June, 1889; comsnr. P.W., Feb. to June, 1885; speaker house of assem., S. Australia, 1890.

COLL, ANTHONY MICHAEL.—Ed. Haileybury, and Exeter Coll., Oxon.; M.A. and B.C.L., 1888; called to the bar, Inner Temp., Jan., 1887; pol. mag., Gibraltar, 1892; atty.-gen., 1901; K.C., 1902; ag. ch. just., and ag. col. sec., on various occasions.

COLLARD, WILLIAM ALFRED.—Clk. treasury, Oct., 1882; promoted to custome outdoor dept. as 6th examining officer, Cape Town, Sept., 1888; second class clk. and asst. acctn. to paymaster-gen., treasury, Jan., 1889; priv. sec. to Hon. J. X. Merriman, treasurer, July, 1890; priv. sec. to the Hon. Sir J. Gordon Sprigg, treasurer, May, 1893; and prin. clk., treasury, Oct., 1893; asst. stamping commr., Nov., 1895, to June, 1896; priv. sec. to the Hon. Sir J. Gordon Sprigg, prime minister, Jan., 1896; chief clk., treasury, July, 1896; accounting officer, 1st July, 1898; deputy asst. treasurer, 1st July, 1900; asst. treas., Nov., 1903.

COLLENS, JAMES HENRY, V.D. (1902).—Principal, govt. training school, Port of Spain, Trinidad, 1878, and govt. statist., 1900; lieut. Trinidad volunteers, 1880; capt., 1885; major, 1902; elected on first bd. of directors, civil service widows' and orphans' fund, 1890; author of "Guide to Trinidad," and other works; ag. supt. govt. printing dept., Dec., 1891; ag. postmr.-gen., 1893, 1895, 1897, 1898; ag. insprr. of schls., 1893, 1900, 1901, 1902. Commanded the Trinidad lt. inf. contingent at His Majesty's coronation, 1902.

COLLET, WILFRED, C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1856; ed. at Univ. Coll. schl., Lond.; 1st class hon. inter., LL.B., Lond., 1889; provisional 2nd clk., native dept., Fiji, Jan., 1881; inspr. of imigrts. and asst. native commr., Jan., 1883; sec. W. Pacific high commr., Oct., 1884; dist. commr. Cyprus, 1897; ag. rec.-gen., 1900-01; again 1902.
COLLIE, A.—Clk., accts. branch, repatriation dept., O.R.C., 14th July, 1902; examr. of accts. audit dept., 1st Dec., 1902.
COLLIE, J.—Apptd. clk. in financial adviser's office, O.R.C., 6th June, 1900; examr. of accts. audit dept., 1st July, 1901; ch. clk. and acetnt., treasy., 1st July, 1902.

COLLIER, FREDERICK WILLIAM.—Third clk., govt. sec.'s office, Br. Guiana, Nov., 1869; 2nd clk., Dec., 1870; record clk., Jan., 1877; ch. clk. and sec. to the quarantine bd., Dec., 1879; has acted as asst. govt. sec. and clk. to the ct. of policy and the combined ct. on several occasions since July, 1890; postmr.-gen., 1888.

COLLINS, A. E.—Ed. at the City of Lond. schl. and Camb.; L. Rothschild scholar and foundation scholar of Trin. Coll., 1890; prizeman, 1891-2; 1st class classical tripus, 1893; B.A., 1893; M.A., 1893; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in office of S. of S. for Coll., June, 1894; priv. sec. to Mr. (now Sir Edward) Wingfield, Mar., 1897; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, Feb., 1898; 1st class clk., Nov., 1898; sec. of S.S. currency comtee., 1902-3.
COLLINS, HON. GEORGE.—Chief secretary in the present Lewis Ministry, Tasmania.

COLLINS, JAMES ALLISON.—Asst. regir., high cts., O.F.S., 21st June, 1879; representative to orphan master, 9th Feb., 1885; landlroost, Ladybrand, 19th Nov., 1887; ditto, Winburg, 9th Mar., 1894; under govt. sec., 23rd July, 1897; res. mag., Bloem, O.R.C., 17th Mar., 1900; asst. sec. to O.R.C. admin. (afterwards asst. col. sec.), 1st Sept., 1901; master of the high ct., 20th Feb., 1903.

COLLINS, ROBT. MURRHEAD, C.M.G. (1904).—Retired lieut. R.N.; entered R.N., 1866; served in flying squadron in 1868 and 1872; sub-lieut., June, 1872; served on Channel and Australian stations to 1876; lieut., June, 1876; apptd. to Victorian naval forces (Australia), Dec., 1878; comdr. Victorian naval forces, 1884; sec. for defence for Victoria, 1888; sec. to dept. of defence, Commonwealth of Aust., 1st Mar., 1901.
COLLS, ARTHUR STANLEY.—Ed. Rugby; asst. engineer, Midland Rlwy., 1883 to 1886; dist. engr., P.W.D., Ceylon, 1886; dist. engr., Pretoria dist., Transvaal, 1902.

COLLYER, W. R., L.S.O.(1903).M.A. Cantab.—B. 1842; Ag. ch. just., S. Leone, 1879; ag. puisne judge, G. Coast, 1880, and ag. Queen's advoc., 1890; Queen's advoc., Cyprus, 1881; puisne judge, S. Stltlns., 1892; atty.-gen., S. Stltlns., 1893; ag. puisne judge, Oct., 1894, to Apr., 1895; ag. ch. just., S. Stltlns., July to Dec., 1897.

COLLYMORE, H. W.—Supernum. G.P.O., Barbados, Oct., 1876; ag. 3rd clk., 1878; 3rd clk., record branch, col. sec.'s office, Mar., 1879; 3rd clk., correspondence branch, Jan., 1881; ag. 2nd clk., 1883; ag. pub. librnr., 1884; 2nd clk. correspondence branch, col. sec.'s office, July, 1884; ag. ch. clk., June, 1893, to Apr., 1894; Aug. to Dec., 1895, and July to Nov., 1896; sec. highways commr., 1893-6; ch. clk. col. sec.'s office, and clk. of legal coun., Jan., 1898.
COLMAN, EUGENE ERNEST.—B.A. Cantab.; cadet, S. Stltlns., Oct., 1902; ag. 4th mag., Sing., Oct., 1903; passed final exam. in Malay, July, 1904.

COLMER, JOSEPH G., C.M.G. (1888).—B. 1856; priv. sec. to first high commr. for Canada, and sec. of emigrn. dept. of Canadian govt. offices, Lond., 1880; apptd. sec. to office of high commr., 1881; acted as interim sec. to colonization bd. since its formation, Dec., 1888, and formally apptd. as such Nov., 1891; res., Mar., 1903.

COLOMB, SIR JOHN C. R., M.P., K.C.M.G. (1888), C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1838; was M.P. for Bow and Bromley, 1886 to 1892; formerly capt., R.M.A., in which corps he served 1854-69; has been variously employed with naval, military, and auxiliary forces; author of many works and lectures on imperial defence and protection of commerce in war; received the thanks of gov. of Br. Columbia, 1877.
COLOMBO, 4th BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. REGINALD STEPHEN COPLETON, D.D.—Formerly fellow and tutor, St. John's Coll., Oxford. Consec. Dec., 1875.
COLUMBIA, BRITISH, 2nd Bishop, Right Rev. William Willcox Perrin.—Trin. Coll., Oxon, B.A., 1870; M.A., 1873; vicar of S. Luke's, Southampton, 1891-93. Consec. 1893.
COLVILLE, J. W.—Ck., lands dept., Victoria, apptd. 1863; head of registry of that dept., Jan., 1878; sec. cent. bd. of health, May, 1885.
COMMISSIONG, ALBERT.—Ck., col. sec.'s office, Grenada, Oct., 1879; 2nd ck., regis.'s office, Jan., 1883; ck., audit office, May, 1883; ch. ck., ditto, June, 1885.
COMMISSIONG, W. S.—Admitted to the bar, Grenada, 1867; escheator-gen., July, 1872; has acted on various occasions as atty.-gen. and as ch. just.; is an unofficial member of legis. coun.
CONRAN, JOSEPH GEORGE.—Emigration agent at Madras for Mauritius, 1st Jan., 1890.
CONROY, J. G., K.C.—Cent. dist. ct. judge, Newfoundland, 1880.
COOK, ALEXR.—Auditor Br. N. Borneo, Oct., 1880; treas. and aud.-gen., Aug., 1881; mem. of coun., June, 1883.
COOK, HENRY AUSTIN.—Ck. of wks., P.W.D., O.R.C., June, 1903.

COOK, WALLACE HAYNES.—Ck. Penal Settlement, Massaruni, Br. Guiana, Feb., 1881; steward, July, 1883; ag. sec. to poor law commrs., and aspt. of girls' reformatory, Apr., 1890, to Jan., 1891, and May, 1895, to Feb., 1896; ck. of supplies prison dept. and sec. to bd. of prisons, Nov., 1890; supt. almshouse, Georgetown, June, 1892; amalgamated offices of supt. almshouse, and sec. to poor law commrs., May, 1899.

COOKE, EBENEZER.—Accountant (afterwards manager), English & Australian Copper Co., S. Australia, 1862-82; represented Flinders in Parlt., 1875-82; J.P., 1875; mem. of various select comtees.; chmn., roy. comm. on finance, 1880-2; mem., roy. comm. on educn., 1882-3; 1st commr. of audit, 1882.

COOKE, JOHN HAMILTON, B.A., M.B., B.Ch.—Trin. Coll., Dub., honours in classics, 1882-3; in anatomy, 1885-6; sen. house surg. Western gen. dispensary, Marylebone, Lon., 1890-1; med. offr. dist. C., Dominica, 1891; dist. St. Kitts and Nevis, 1892; Yaws hosp., 1892-7; mem. bd. of health, 1893; surg.-cap. of local forces, 1898.
COOKE, WILLIAM ERNEST, M.A., F.R.A.S.—Cadet, Adelaide observatory, 1878; B.A. Adel. Univ., 1883; asst. astronomer, Adel. observ., 1884; M.A., Adel. Univ., 1889; govt. astronr., W.A., 1896.

COOKMAN, NATHANIEL GEORGE, L.R.C.S.I., L.R.C.P.L.—Med. offr., Portsmouth dist., Dominica, and surg. hosp., 1892-6; commr., regis., mag., sen. med. offr., Virgin Is., 1896; mem. gen. leg. coun., Leewards.
COOM, JOHN.—M.I.C.E., engrn., N. Zealand, P.W.D., 1876 to 1881; res. engrn. working rly., 1881 to 1897; inspecting engrn. for rly., 1897 to 1899; ch. engrn., 1899.

COOMBS, W. H., F.R.G.S., F.R.A.S.—Comdr. Roy. Navy; assoc. inst. C.E.; entered R.N. 1866; served on the Mediterranean, N. Amer., W. Indies, China, and other stations; lieut., 1875; served under Indian govt., 1876 to 1884 as asst. supt. marine surveys, chiefly on harbours, Madras, Colombo, Chittagong, etc.; passed as interp. in Hindi, Urdu, and Persian, 1877-8; ret. as comdr. R.N. 1894; apptd. by S. of S. for Cols. prot. of imigrts., Trinidad, Feb., 1896; M.L.C. and J.P. for the isld.

COOPER, FRANCIS ALFRED, C.M.G. (1901), M.I.C.E.—B. 1860; fellow Sanitary Inst., Gt. Britain; ed. at Rossall and Loughborough gram. schl., asst. to Mr. James Mansergh, C.E., Westminster, 1879; sanit. survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1887 to 1890; survr. to sanitary bd., 1888 to 1892; inspr. of buildings, P.W.D., 1888 to 1890; ag. asst. engrn., 1889; ag. survr.-gen., 1889; ag. asst. survr.-gen., 1889 to 1890; res. engrn. in charge of water and drainage dept., 1890 to 1892; dir. of pub. wks., Oct., 1891; mem. exec. and legis. couns., Mar., 1892; dir. pub. wks., Ceylon, 1897; pres. of sanitary bd., Hong Kong, 1895-7; recd. thanks of govt. for services in settlement of claims under the Taipingsham resumption ordnance, 1895; and from S. of S. for Cols. for services during the plague, 1896; M.L.C.; commr. of rds., waterwks., engrn., and mem. of harb. bd. and mun. coun., Colombo, 1897.
COOPER, P. A.—Atty.-gen. Queensland, Dec., 1880; northern judge, 5th Jan., 1883; 1st puisne judge, sup. ct.
COPELAND, THEHON.H.—Mem. legis.assem., N.S. Wales; sec. for lands in Dibbs' ministry, 1891-4; agt.-gen. for N.S.W. in London, 1900.

CORBET, FREDERICK H. M.—Hon. cor. philos. soc., etc.; priv. sec. to Mr. Justice Lawrie at various periods from 1885 to 1888; librnr. Colombo museum, June, 1886; hon. sec. to gen. comtee., Ceylon, for Paris Exposn., 1889; ag. sec., cent. irrigation bd., June, 1890, to May, 1891; resig. librnrship, Dec., 1893; has represented col. at Imp. Inst. as hon. exec. offr., since July, 1893; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, Jan., 1897; recd. jub. gold med. of Ceylon, 1897.

CORK, PHILIP CLARKE, C.M.G. (1904).—Ck. immigr. dept., Jamaica, May, 1875; Hindust. interpreter to the agt.-gen., 1876; inspr. of imigrts., 1877; prot. of imigrts., Grenada, 1881; ditto, Jamaica, 1884; commr. under the Kingston Improv't. Laws, 1894; nomtd. mem. of legis. coun., 1896; asst. sec., 1897; col. sec., Br. Hond., 1901; administg. the govt., 8th May, 1903.
CORK, WM.—Ck. and locker, Jamaica, Aug., 1868; 3rd class asst. collr. of taxes, Jan., 1869; 3rd class collr. of taxes and parochial treas., 1870; 2nd class collr. of taxes, 1879; now 1st class collr.

CORNEY, BOLTON GLANYLL, I.S.O. (1904).—Ed. at Fontainebleau, in London, and at Schwerin; St. Thomas's Hosp., 1870; M.R.C.S., Eng., 1874; entered the col. service in 1877 as govt. med. offr., Fiji; also health offr., Suva, 1877; med. offr. for the immigr. dept., 1881; agt.-gen. of immigr., Jan., 1886, to Mar., 1887; M.L.C. and of native regulative bd., 1885; ch. med. offr., June, 1887; M.L.C., June, 1888; ag. supt. lun. asyl., 1887; ag. rec.gen., Jan. to Mar., 1892; commr. to inquire into decrease of native populn., 1893; commr. to inquire into misgovt. of Prov. Tailenr., May, 1895; in 1897 rec. thanks of coral comtee. of roy. soc. for assistance to the geolog. expdn. to Funafuti, Ellice Islds.; then from the roy. soc. for renewed services to expdn., 1898; agt. gen. for immigr., Dec., 1902, to Mar., 1903.
CORNISH, CHARLES COX.—Ck., land titles office, S. Australia, 1864; ch. ck., crim. business, 1865 to 1887; sec. to atty.-gen., law dept., 1888.

CORNWALL, C. F.—Graduated B.A. Magdalen Coll., Camb., 1857; called to bar of Inner Tem., 1861; many years mem. of legislature of col. of Br. Columbia; on confederation of that prov. with Canada in 1871, summoned by H.M. to the Senate of the Dominion; lieut.-gov. of Br. Columbia, 1881-6.
CORNWELL, G. F.—Called to the bar (Linc.'s Inn), 1859; one of H.M.'s counsel for Gibr., 1885; coroner and J.P., 1895; inspr. of schools, 1902.
COSTIGAN, THE HON. JOHN.—Mem. of Canadian house of commons and of privy coun.; min. of inland rev., 1888; sec. of state for Canada, Dec., 1892; min. of marine and fisheries, 1894 to 1896.
COTTLE, H. C.—2nd astt. govt. printer, Ceylon, Nov., 1888; astt. govt. printer, 1888; ag. govt. printer, Aug., 1901.

COULL, W.—Enrolled law student, Leeward Is., 1875; clk. col. sec.'s office, Leeward Is., Mar., 1878; acted on occasions as ch. clk. and as clk. of the Antigua and federal exec. and legis. coun.; dep. coroner for district A, Oct., 1880; ag. mag., dist. B, Antigua, Jan. to Apr., 1882; mag. and coroner for district E, Roseau, and regis.-gen. of births, marriages, and deaths, Dominica, Aug., 1882; mem. of the bd. of poor law guardians; a visiting justice of the gaol, and nominated M.L.A., 1883; escheator-gen., 1886; mem. exec. coun., 1896; off. mem. legis. coun., 1896; in charge of out-door poor relief, 1898.
COULTER, ROBERT MILLER, M.D.—Deputy of postmr.-gen. of Canada, Aug., 1897.
COUNSEL, E. A.—Secretary for lands and surveyor-gen., Tasmania, 1889.

COURTNEY, JOHN MORTIMER, C.M.G. (1897), I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1838; asst. sec. and acctnt. to the treas., Canada, 1869; dep. min. of finance, dep. rec.-gen., and sec. to treas., 1878; mem. of comsn. of inquiry into the civ. ser. of Canada, 1891.

COURTNEY, Rt. Hon. LEONARD H., P.C. (1889).—B. 1832; ed. St. John's Coll., Camb.; B.A. (2nd wrangler) 1855; called to the bar, Linc.'s Inn, 1858; resptd. Liskeard, Dec., 1876, to Dec., 1885, when he was returned for the co. div. in which that borough was merged until 1900; partly under-sec. for the home dept., Dec., 1880; partly under-sec. for the cols., Aug., 1881; sec. to the treas., May, 1882; resig., 1885; chmn. of ways and means, 1886-92.
COUZENS, CHARLES.—Apptd., after exam., 3rd cl. messenger C.O., Jan., 1889; promoted 2nd cl. mess., Nov., 1899; King's mess., 1st Feb., 1902.

COWAN, JAMES.—Clk. to res. mag., York, W. Australia, 1864; clk. to pol. mag., Perth, 1870; clk. to legis. coun., 1874; regis. and master sup. ct., &c., Nov., 1878; also curator of intestate estates, Nov., 1883; R.M., Guildford, Mar., 1886; govt. res., N. dist., 1889; pol. mag., Perth, 1890; mag., Perth local, etc., 1897.
COWELL, H. RUSSELL, B.A.—Ed. Malvern and Cambridge; scholar of Clare, 1896-9; apptd., after compet. exam., clk., sec.'s dept., inland revenue, Oct., 1901; 2nd cl. clk., C.O., 27th Mar., 1902.

COWPER, SYDNEY, C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1854; clk. in office of comrsn. exhibn. of 1851, in 1871, in educn. dept. S. Kensington, 1875; 3rd class clk. in col. sec.'s office, Capetown, 1879; priv. sec. to premier, 1880-1; 2nd class clk., 1881; priv. sec. to premier, 1884-5; 1st class clk., 1885; sec. to prime min., 1887; ret. on abolition of office, 1891; prin. clk., dept. of agricult., 1892; sec. to premier (office re-established), 1897; J.P. for Cape and Wynberg; civ. ser. examiner (1899-1901), and art trustee; Basuto med. (1880); major, 2nd in commd., 4th batt. Cape Peninsula regt. (1901); ret., 1904.

COWPER, VERY REV. WILLIAM MACQUARIE, Magd. Hall, Oxon.—B.A., 1833; M.A., 1836; d. 1833; p. 1834; dean and archdeacon of Sydney, 1858; vicar-gen., 1877; formerly chapl. of St. Petrox, Dartmouth, 1836-6; chapl. to A.A. Co., Port Stephen's, 1836-56; principal, Moore Coll., Liverpool, 1856; incumbent of St. John's Bishopsthorpe, 1856-8; of St. Philip's, Sydney, 1858.

COX, CHARLES T., C.M.G. (1903).—B. 1838; 3rd clk., govt. sec.'s office, Br. Guiana, July, 1874; 2nd clk., 1876; record clk., 1879; sen. clk., 1883; ch. clk. and sec. to the quarantine bd., 1888; sec. to the royal comsn. on admnstr.-gen.'s dept. of B. Guiana, 1882; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., 1899; compiled "Index to Laws of Br. Guiana," 187 (2nd edition, 1891), also "Some Notes for Govt. Officers," 1894; astt. govt. sec., and clk. to ct. of policy and combined ct., 1889; also clk. of exec. coun., 1892; ag. govt. sec., in 1893, 1894, 1896, and 1898, with seat in exec. coun. and legislature; chmn. of comte. on pensions, 1898; ag. audr.-gen. on several occasions; admnstr. of St. Kitts and Nevis, 1899-1904; ag. gov., Leeward Is., July to Oct., 1903, and May to Sept., 1904; govt. sec. and ag. gov. B. Guiana, Sept., 1904.
COX, E.A.W.—Cadet, Sarawak div. ser., Mar., 1885; astt. res., May, 1891; of Lundr., June, 1896; res., 2nd class, Mar., 1898, of Truan; again May, 1899.
COX, F. J. D. C.—Cadet, Sarawak ser., Feb., 1884; astt. res., May, 1891; res., 2nd cls., June, 1901; 2nd cls. res., Lundu., 1904; mag., ct. of requests, Kuching, 1904.

COX, HUGH BERTRAM, C.B. (1902).—B. 1861; ed. at Westminster schl. and Chr. Ch., Oxford; 1st class classical mods., 1881; 1st class final cases, schl., 1883; M.A. and B.C.L., 1884; called to the bar, 1885, and joined S. Wales circuit; assisted the attv.-gen. (Sir R. Webster) in partly. and offl. work, 1886-1897; jun. counsel to the treas. in peergate cases, 1892, and to H.M. Customs, 1896; employed in connection with the Br. Guiana bndry. question, and as one of the jun. counsel in the preparations of the Br. case in the artn.; legal astt. under-sec., C.O., Nov., 1897.

COX, SIR WILLIAM HENRY LIONEL, Kt. BACH. (1896).—B. 1864; substitute procureur and advoc.-gen., Mauritius, Feb., 1880; puisne judge of the sup. ct., Aug., 1880; procureur and advoc.-gen., Aug., 1886; ch. justice, S. Stlntz, 1893.

CRAIGIE-HALKETT, MONTAGU C.—Ag. priv. sec. to gov. Falkland Ialda, Mar., 1897; writer, col. sec.'s off., Apl., 1897; priv. sec. to admnstr., Apl., 1897; clk., col. sec.'s off., Oct., 1897; ag. priv. sec. to gov., Nov., 1897; treas. clk., 1899.

CRAWFORD, HENRY LEIGHTON.—Ed. at Clifton and Corp. Christi Coll., Camb. (scholar); writer, Ceylon service, 1877; pol. mag., Kalpitiya, 1883; ditto, Gampola, 1883; offic. astt. to govt. agt., W. Prov., 1884; 2nd astt., col. sec. and clk. of legis. coun., 1890; priv. astt. col. sec., Apl., 1896; comsnr. under "The Buddhist Temporalities Ordee," 1889," Nov., 1900; ag. govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Nov., 1901; ag. prin. collr. of cust., 10th Nov., 1902; offr. of class I., 4th May, 1903.

CRAWFORD, WALLACE.—4th clk., imigr. dept., Br. Guiana, Sept., 1890; 3rd clk., Aug., 1894; 2nd clk., Oct., 1895; acted as sub-agt. of imigr., June, 1883, to June, 1894, and Oct., 1895, to Jan., 1896; Sept., 1896, to July, 1898; July, 1898, to Mar., 1899; ag. emigr. agt., Barbados, Oct., 1890, to Nov., 1891; ag. sen. sub-agt., July, 1892; awarded 1st certif. for proficiency in Hindustani, Mar., 1886.

CREAGH, C. V., C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1842; barrister-at-law (Mid. Tem., called May, 1892); was astt. dist. supt. of the 1st class in the Punjab pol.; in 1866, passed the highest pol. examn., and the 2nd standard in Oriental languages; in 186 raised the Sikh pol. for the Hong Kong govt.; dep. supt., 1867; J.P. in 1868; sheriff in 1874; received thanks of govt. for services in typhoon, Sept., 1874; acted as supt. of pol. in 1869-70 and 1877-78; ag. A.D.C. in 1878; supt. of fire brigade, 1878; acted as pol. mag. and coroner, 1878 to 1880; passed with credit the six exams. in Chinese colloquial prescribed by the govt.; in 1879, mem. of comtees. of public gambling, and on the defence of Hong Kong; arbitrator under the opium ordinance (7 of 1879); asst. Br. res., judge, and mem. of state coun., Perak, Mar., 1883; gov. and comr.-in-ch. and ch. judicial off., Br. N. Borneo, Mar., 1888, to 1895; also gov. Labuan, 1889 to 1895, when he ret.

CREASE, SIR HENRY PERING PELLEW, K.T. BACH. (1896).—B. 1823; grad. at Clare Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1847; called to the bar at the Mid. Temp., June, 1849; admitted to the bar, Br. Columbia, 1858; atty.-gen., 1869; puisne judge from 1870 to 1896, when he ret.; served on various legal comms.

CREASY, LEONARD, M.I.C.E.—Pioneer offr., Ceylon, 1874; suptdng. offr., 1877; dist. engnr., Feb., 1886; prov. engnr. N. Prov., July, 1890; financial and office asst., Nov., 1894; prov. engnr. E. Prov., Jan., 1895; ditto, Prov. Uva, May, 1895; on deputation to Seychelles, Nov. to Dec., 1896; prov. engnr., Cent. Prov. Kandy, Sept., 1897; ag. dir. pub. wks., 15th Mar., 1903.
CREED, HENRY.—Apptd., after exam., 3rd class messenger in C.O., July, 1900; 2nd class, 1st Feb., 1904.

CRESWELL, MARGARET S.—Apptd. postmistress of Gibraltar and supt. of govt. telegraphs, Imp. P.O., Sept., 1877, as a mark of the estimation of the services of her late father, the dep. postmstr.-gen. at Gib.; transf. to col. govt. in Jan., 1886, when the col. assumed the control of the local P.O.

CROFTON, FRANCIS BLAKE.—B.A. Trin. Coll., Dublin, 1863; provincial librarian, Nova Scotia, Jan., 1883; author of "The Major's Big-Talk of Stories," "Haliburton, the Man and the Writer," "Hairbreadth Escapes of Major Mendax," "For Closer Union"; a vice-pres. of the internat. library conf., 1897.

CROOK, JOHN ROWLAND, A.M.I.C.E.—Entered Crystal Pal. Engng. Schl., 1881; asst. to E. F. Griffith, C.E. (consulting sanitary engnr.), 1883; supt. of drawing office, C. P. Engng. Schl., 1888; gained 1st prize for civ. engnr., City of Lon. Coll., 1889; asst. engnr. water and drainage dept., Hong Kong, 1891; in charge of new sewerage wks., city of Victoria, 1891-94; sanitary surv., 1892; exec. engnr. P.W.D., 1893; and water authority during absence of dir. of pub. wks., 1893-94.

CROSS, J. W.—Cltk., Zulu interp., and sub-distributor of stamps in the res. mag.'s office, Ixopo, Natal, 23rd Oct., 1876; dep. clk. of the peace for Ixopo, 2nd Mar., 1877; and on the 3rd Mar., 1879, apptd. in command of the Ixopo, Natal, native foot contingent, 800 strong; and subsequently transf. to command of the mounted contingent during the Zulu war; acted on several occasions as res. mag. of Ixopo; clk. to R.M., Weenen, July, 1886; admtr. of native law, Pagadi's location, 1889; mag., Lower Tugela div., 1894; R.M., Alfred div., 1895; mag., Lions River div., 1896; Umvoti div., 1904.
CROSSKILL, HERBERT.—Dep. prov. sec., Nova Scotia, 1868-78; again since 1882.
CROUCHER, F., M.B.—Housesurg., gen. hosp., Singapore, 1893; col. surg., Malacca, Jan., 1897.

CROWTHER, FRANCIS G.—Apptd. draughtsman, P.W.D., G. Coast, July, 1898; inspr. of schls., Aug., 1901; mem. bd. of ed., Jan., 1902; trav. comsrr., Apl., 1902; compiled statement of govt. lands, July to Sep., 1902; ag. dir. of educ. and sec. to bd. of educ., Nov., 1902, to Apl., 1903; trav. comsrr., Dec., 1903; ag. regir. of deaths, Apl., 1904; trav. comsrr., July, 1904; ag. dir. of educ., June, 1904.

CUDEDFORD, WM.—Employed on financial mission in Bahamas, May, 1885, to May, 1886; supervisor of customs, G. Coast, Jan., 1887; local auditor, Lagos, under C. and A.G., London, Dec., 1889; ditto, S. Leone and Gambia, Sept., 1893.
CUNLIFFE-OWEN, EDWARD, C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1847; for services in connection with the Col. and Ind. Exhib., 1886.

CUNNINGHAM, J. FRANCIS, F.R.G.S., F.Z.S.—Ed. King's Coll., Lond.; sec. to B.C. Africa adminstr., 24th Sept., 1892; organised printing dept., and was apptd. editor of offl. gazette, June, 1894; served as vol. in campaigns against Zaraifi, etc., 1895 (recd. Central African medal); won gold medal offered by Zomba rifle club, 1897, and the Edwards Cup, 1898; ch. Br. comsrr. for delimitation of Anglo-Portuguese boundary in B.C. Africa, 24th May, 1899; priv. sec. to Sir H. Johnston's spec. miss. to Uganda, 24th Aug., 1899; passed exams. at Mid. Temp. and admitted a law student, 29th June, 1901; admitted to Gray's Inn, 1st July, 1901; sec. to Uganda adminstr., 1st Apl., 1901; apptd. judl. offr., 19th July, 1900; carried on duties of ch. judl. offr. and regir. in Uganda, 10th Apl., 1900, to 19th Dec., 1900; acted at headquarters for spec. comsrr. during his tour of inspection, 28th May to 8th June, 1900; and during the dep. comsrr.'s absence with Nandi expedition, 9th Jan. to 16th Feb., 1901; present sec. to Uganda adminstr.; registr.-gen. of marriages, 1st Nov., 1902; joined Uganda vol. reserve, 1903.

CUNYNGHAM, SIR PERCY F., BART.—B. 1867; cadet, Sarawak civ. ser., July, 1886; asst. res. of Trusan, May, 1890; res. (2nd class), Mar., 1896; lieut. 3rd battn. Middlesex Rifles, May, 1897; ag. res., Limbang, May, 1899; res., 2nd class, Upper Rejang, Aug., 1901; commt., Sarawak Rangers, July, 1903; res., 1st div., and mem. of sup. coun., May, 1904.

CURLEWIS, JOHN STEPHEN, B.A., LL.B. (Cape).—Cape civ. serv., 1883-1888; advocate, sup. ct., Cape Colony, 12th Oct., 1887; advocate, high ct. (late S.A.R.), May, 1888; counsel for Imperial govt. in arbitn. with late S.A.R. re Asiatic question, 1895; ag. judge of high ct. (late S.A.R.), 15th Apl., 1899, to 31st Jan., 1900; pres. of crim. ct. for Witwatersrand, 2nd Nov., 1899, to 31st Jan., 1900; puisne judge, sup. ct., Transvaal, 1st Sep., 1903.
CURLING, C. NAPIER.—Apptd. res. just. and collr. of Elenthua, Bahamas, Aug., 1895; ch. regir., G. Coast, 12th Jan., 1899; dist. comsrr., Mar., 1899; ch. regir. and sheriff, 1901.

CURRY, CHARLES.—Entered the col. sec.'s office, Cape, June, 1871; apptd. 3rd class elk., Aug., 1872; 2nd class, July, 1874; promoted dept. of crown lands and pub. wks., Oct., 1879; ch. elk., office of crown lands and pub. wks., Apr., 1881; sec. to the diamond mining comsrr., Sept., 1881; from 1882 has frequently acted as asst. comsrr. of crown lands and pub. wks.; asst. comsrr. of crown lands and pub. wks., Apr., 1892; permanent head of dept. of lands, mines, and agricult., Sept., 1892; under sec. for agricult., 1893; mem. of geol. comsrr.

CURRIE, SIR DONALD, G.C.M.G. (1897), K.C.M.G. (1881), C.M.G. (1877).—B. 1825; chmn. of the Union-Castle Mail Packets Co. In July, 1876, at the time of the negotiations between the Earl of Carnarvon and Pres. Brand, he assisted by his good offices in bringing about the success of these negotiations, and thus ending the long dispute between the Br. Govt. and the Orange Free State in reference to the diamond fields; formerly M.P. for Perthshire.

CUSACK-SMITH, Sir Berry, K.C.M.G. (1898).—B. 1859; ed. at Eton; barrister, Middle Temp., 1884; law scholarship, 1883; formerly lieut. 1st V.B., Essex regt.; apptd. H.M. Consul at Samoa, Jan., 1890, and H.M. dep. commr. for the W. Pacific at Samoa, May, 1890; proceeded in H.M.S. "Curacoa" to Union Group to report on land claims, Aug., 1892; dep. commr. for the Samoan and Union Groups, and authorised to perform the duties of res. in the Union Group, Oct., 1892; special judicial commr., Apr., 1893; apptd. to license and superintend employment of Gilbert Is. natives in Samoa, Jan., 1894; consul-gen., Valparaiso, 1897.

CUSCADEN, W. A.—Sen. Sophister, Trin. Coll., Dub.; capt., 4th Roy. Dub. Fus.; asst. inspr. of constab., G. Coast, 1879; civil commr., Taquah, 1881; ch. inspr. of pol., Singapore, 1883; asst. supt. of pol., Penang, 1884; ag. pol. supt., Malacca, 1889, and July, 1897; Singapore, 1891, and Apr., 1893; asst. supt. of pol., P.W., 1893; supt. pol., Malacca, July, 1897; ag. supt. pol., Singapore, Apr., 1898; ag. inspr. gen. pol., S.S., Mar., 1901, to Aug., 1902, and Nov., 1903, to Mar., 1904; supt. of pol., Sing., Aug., 1902.
CUTHERBERT, Sir Henry, M.L.C., K.C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1829; min. of justice, Victoria, 1886-90; solr.-gen., 1894.
DAETH, John.—Ed. at King's Coll., Lond.; asst. draughtsman, P.W.D., Jamaica, Jan., 1873; ch. ditto, Mar., 1875; dist. engnr., N. (now W.) Dist., Jan., 1883; engnr., Kingston Dist., 1886.

DALE, Augustus Charles, I.S.O. (1903).—Entered Cape service, Aug., 1880; promoted to 2nd class, Jan., 1873, and to 1st class, 1881; prin. clk., col. sec.'s office, 1881; regir. of statistics, col. sec.'s office, 1891; regir.-gen. of statistics, 1st July, 1902; also asst. dir. of census, Dec., 1902.

DALE, Charles Ernest.—Ed. Greenwich and King's Coll., London; fell. soc. accents, and auditors, fell. roy. statistical soc.; apptd. dir. customs Niger Coast Protec., Jan., 1895; asst. postmr.-gen., Apr., 1896; ag. dir.-gen. customs and postmr.-gen., Feb. to Sept., 1896, Oct., 1897, to June, 1898, Oct., 1899, to Feb., 1900; Dec., 1900, to July, 1901; treas. Southern Nigeria, Feb., 1898; ag. sec., Feb. to April, 1900; Jan. to Apr., 1903, dir. pub. officers guarantee fund, Apr., 1900; ag. treas., Lagos, Sept., 1904.

DALE, H. E.—Ed. at St. Paul's Schl. and at Oxford; class schol., Balliol Coll., 1894; Hertford schol., 1895; Craven schol., 1895; Ireland schol., 1896; 1st class moda., 1896; 1st class lit. hum., 1898; B.A., 1898; Fell. New Coll., 1898; Derby schol., 1899; M.A., 1901; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd class clk., C.O., Oct., 1898; sent on special mission to Br. Hond., Nov., 1903.

DALGARNO, James.—Clerk in the G.P.O. at Sydney, 1860; ch. clk., 1869; now asst. sec.; visited England, America, and New Zealand, as sec. to Sir Saul Samuel, who was specially comand by the N.S. Wales govt. to organise a mail serv. between the U.K. and the Australasian cols. by way of the U.S.A.; elected vice-pres. art. soc. N.S.W., 1885; and pres. Sydney mechanics' sch. of arts, 1886.

DALLAS, Francis Henry.—Ed. Univ. Coll. School; asst. sec. Penang municipality, 1895; ag. sec., May, 1896; Sarawak civ. ser.; ag. editor "Sarawak Gazette," and manager, Sarawak govt. printing office, 1897; ag. treas. and clk., supreme coun., June, 1902; treas. and recorder of sup. coun., 1903; mem. of comte. of adminstr., 1903-4.

DALTON, The Rev. Canon John Neale, M.A., C.M.G. (1882).—B. 1839; was gov. to T.R.H. Princes Albert Victor and George of Wales, and ag. chaplain to H.M.S. "Bacchante"; chaplain in ord. to Her Majesty, 1886; canon of Windsor, 1885.
DALY, Sir Malachy Bowers, K.C.M.G. (1900).—B. 1836; Lieut.-Governor of Nova Scotia, 1890.
DALY, The Hon. Thomas Mayne, K.C.—Mem. for Selkirk, Manitoba, in Dominion house of commons; min. of interior, Canada, 1892-6.

DANAVALL, John Martin.—Barrister, Mid. Temp., 1877; ag. solr.-gen., Leeward Is., 1883; again in 1884; ag. mag. Dist. C, 1883, and again in 1885; 2nd puisne judge, Leeward Is., June, 1888; also dep.-judge of the vice-admiralty, 1889.

DANDY, Joseph Henry.—Ed. Oakes Inst. Liverpool, certif. as inspr. of nuisances and sanitary inspr. from sanitary inst., 1896; 1st cl. advanced hygiene, S. Kensington, 1897; surveying (1st cl.) and certif. of sanitary knowledge, Victoria Univ., 1898; certif. schl. of hygiene, Univ. Coll., 1898; practical bacteriology, Univ. Coll., 1899; mem. of san. inst., 1900; san. inspr., Liverpool, chief sanitary inspr., Hong Kong, June, 1890.

DANE, Roft., M.R.L.S., L.S.A. (1887), L.R.C.P. (1889).—House physician, Middlesex hosp., 1890; house surg., gen. hosp., Singapore, June, 1890; col. surg., Prov. Wellesley, Mar., 1893; col. surg., Sing., March, 1901.
DARLEY, Cecil W., I.S.O. (1903).—Engnr.-in-ch. for harbours and river navig. and water supply, N. S. Wales, 1891.

DARLEY, Sir Fred. Matthew, G.C.M.G. (1901), K.C.M.G. (1897), KT. Bach. (1887).—B. 1830; Ed. Dungannon Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; B.A., Hon. LL.D., T.C., 1903; barrister, Ireland, 1853, N. S. Wales, 1862, Queensland, 1886; Q.C., 1878; M.L.C., N. S. Wales, 1888-86; vice-pres., exec. coun., N. S. Wales, 1881-23; ch. just., N. S. Wales, 1886; lict.-gov., N. S. Wales, 1891; acted as such six times for periods amounting in all to nearly 3 years, besides frequently as deputy for different governors; apptd. one of the commrs. to inquire into South African War, 1902.
DARNLEY, E. R.—Scholar of Trin. Coll., Camb., 1883; of Lond. Univ., 1897; B.A. (sixth wrangler) and B.Sc., 1897; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd class clk., C.O., 11th Oct., 1898.
DARTNELL, Major-Gen. Sir J. G., K.C.B. (1901), C.M.G. (1881).—B. 1838; late of the 27th and 80th regts.; comdt. of the vol. and mounted pol., Natal, 1874; ret., 1903.
DASHWOOD, Chas. James.—Govt. res. and judge of the N. territory of S. Australia, 1892; mem. H. of Assem., S. Australia, 1887-92.

DAUBENY, Chas. W. (late 3rd batt. Cheah regt., Jan., 1890).—Joined Sarawak civ. ser., Apr., 1883; offr. in charge, Baram, Apr., 1886; inspr. of pol. and prisons, Aug., 1888; supt. of pol. and prisons, Mar., 1901.

DAVENDPORT, Sir Samuel, K.T. Bach.(1884), K.C.M.G., and LL.D. (1886).—B. 1818; Crown nominee of the legis. coun., S. Australia, 1846-7; non-official mem. of the partly elective legis. coun., which passed the Constitution Act, 1856-6; elected mem. of legis. coun., 1857-66; twice min. of pub. wks.; hon. exec. comsnr. at the Lond. Internat. Exhibn., 1851; Philadelphia, 1876; Sydney, 1879; Melbourne, 1880; Col. and Ind., Lond., 1886; seven years pres. of the Royal Agricul. and Horticol. Soc. of S. Australia, and nineteen years pres. of the Adelaide Chamber of Manufacturers.
DAVEY, William B.—2nd clk., Antigua treas., Dec., 1878; a landing waiter, Sept., 1879; now 1st landing waiter.

DAVIDSON, Andrew, M.D., F.R.C.P. Edin.; M.R.C.S. Eng.; Fellow Royal Physical Soc.—Formerly physician to the ct. of Madagascar, visiting and suptndg. surg. civil hosp., Mauritius, Feb., 1877, and govt. analyst, Dec., 1877; acted as pol. and prison surg. in 1878; prof. of chem., Roy. Coll., Apr., 1879; supl. univ. asstl., Nov., 1879; author of numerous med. wks.; sec. to cattle plague comsn., etc.; medical adviser of the C.O., Edinburgh, 1897.

DAVIDSON, Walter Edward, C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1859; scholar and exhibitioner of Christ's Coll., Camb.; writer, Ceylon civ. ser., Aug., 1880; attached to Kandy Kachcheri, Dec., 1880; writer col. sec.'s office, Apr., 1881; transf'd. to Colombo Kachcheri, Sept., 1881; attached to the Negombo pol. ct., May, 1882; acted as asst. agt., Negombo, July to Oct., 1882; extra office asst., Colombo Kachcheri, May, 1883; and agt. office asst., Nov., 1884; sec. to Ceylon comsn., col. exhibn., 1886; ag. asst. govt. agt., Kegalla, Apl., 1888, Mar., 1892, and Feb., 1895; ag. pol. mag., Kalutara, Apl., 1899; Galle, Dec., 1890; pol. mag., Matale, Feb., 1887; Haldummulla, Oct., 1890; ag. dist. judge, Kalutara, Apl., 1899; agt. govt. agt. and fiscal, prov. of Sabaragamuwa, July, 1896; asst. govt. agt., Kegalla, Oct., 1896; N. Eliva, Feb., 1899; chmn. mun. coun., and mayor of Colombo, Apl., 1898; resig. as chmn. of mun. coun. on going on leave; asst. sec. to administrator, Transvaal, 1901; col. sec., 1902-3; gov., Seychelles, 15th Apl., 1904.

DAVIES, Hon. Sir Louis Henry, K.C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1845; called to the bar, 1866; Q.C. in 1880; was one of the Canadian counsel before the internat. fishery comsn. at Halifax in 1877; solr. gen. of P.E. Is., 1869, and again in 1872-3; led the opposition in P.E. Is. Legislature until Sept., 1876, when he became premier and atty. gen.; resig. in Mar., 1879, and was defeated at the gen. elections which followed; was first returned to the House of Commons at the gen. elections in 1882, and has since sat for Queen's; min. of marine and fisheries in Sir W. Laurier's govt., June, 1896; mem. joint high comsn., Quebec and Washington, 1898-9; puisne judge of sup. court, 1901.

DAVIES, The Hon. Sir Matthew Henry, K.T. Bach. (1890).—B. 1850; solr.; late mayor of Prahran; mem. exec. coun. Victoria; min. without portfolio, Feb., 1886; speaker, legis. assem., 1887-92; vice-pres. and an exec. comsnr. for the centennial internat. exhibn., 1888.

DAVIES, WM. Rees.—Ed. Eton and Trin. Hall, Camb.; B.A., 1885; called to bar (Inner Temp.), 1887; went South Wales circuit; J.P. and dep. lieut. Pembrokeshire; M.P. for Pemb., 1892-8; asst. priv. sec. to chancellor of exchequer, 1893-5; atty.-gen., Bahamas, Dec., 1897; administered the govt., June, 1901; ag. ch. just., May to Aug., 1902; King's advocate, Cyprus, 1902.

DAVIS, BECCLES.—Ed. C.M.S. grammar schl., Sierra Leone, and Fourah Bay Coll. (Dunelm); 1st class customs officer, 1894; ag. statistical officer of customs, Niger Coast Prot., Apr. to Oct., 1894; P.C.O. at Opobo, Brass, and Bonny, 1894 to 1899; supervisor of customs, Southern Nigeria, 1900; transf'd. to pub. wks. dep., 1903; accnt., P.W.D., 1903.

DAVIS, C. G. H.—Ent. col. ser. of Br. Guiana as asst. clk. audit office, Nov., 1887; 5th cl. clk., Apr., 1889; 4th Sept., 1889; 3rd Jan., 1894; sec. to comte. of combined court on col. expenditure, 1893-4; 2nd cl. clk., Nov., 1896; ag. 1st cl. clk., 1895-6-7-8; ag. accnt. to sup. ct., July, 1899, to Sept., 1900; 1st cl. clk. and accnt., post office, Sept., 1900.

DAVIS, C. T.—Ed. Christ's Coll., Brecon, and Oxford; classical scholar, Balliol coll., 1892-6; 1st class classical moda., 1894; Jenkyns exhibn., 1896; 1st class lit. hum., 1896; apptd., after compet. exam., 1st cl. clk., accnt.-gen.'s dept., admiralty, Oct. 5th, 1896; transf'd. to sec.'s dept., inland rev., Mar., 1897; 2nd cl. clk., col. office, Apr. 15th, 1897; went to Gibraltar as sec. to Rt. Hon. Sir C. R. Thompson on a mission to inquire into the currency question, May and June, 1898; sec. Pacif. cable comte., 1900; priv. sec., to Sir M. Ommannay, June, 1902.

DAVIS, L. A. R.—Permanent supernum. aid waiter, cust. dept., Oct., 1882; 5th cl., Apr., 1883; 4th cl., Oct., 1884; 4th cl. offr. of cust., Jan., 1886; 3rd cl., Oct., 1887; 2nd cl., June, 1896; ag. 1st grade comsry. of taxation and offl. asst. to comptrlr. of cust. in excise matters, July, 1898, to Mar., 1899.

DAVIS, Nicholas Darnell, C.M.G. (1895).—B. 1846; was for some years in Grenada, in the priv. sec.'s office; priv. sec. to Admstr. Baynes for a short time; in Br. Guiana served as clerk in several depts.; Aug., 1870, to Feb., 1871, sec. to the comsrs. of inquiry into the treatment of imigrts.; sec. to roy. comsrs. of inquiry into the treatment of imigrts. in Mauritius, Feb., 1872; comdt., Br. Sherbro, S. Leone, 1874; postmr.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1876; acted as rec.-gen., Dec., 1880, to Sept., 1881; comptroller of customs, 1st Oct., 1881; ag. auditor.-gen., mem. of the ct. of policv, and sen. comsrs. of the Vlissingen estate, 1882; mem. ct. of policy again in 1886; auditor gen., 1st July, 1898; mem. exec. coun., Br. G., Feb., 1901; has acted as gov. sec. on eight occasions, and three times as dep.-govr.
DAVIS, S. Spencer.—Extra rev. offr., St. Kitts, 1893; 4th ditto, 1895; asst. treas., Gold Coast, 1901.

DAVSON, Charles Simon, B.A., LL.D.—Ed. at Westminster and Trin. Hall, Camb.; called to the bar, Middle Tem., Jan., 1881; admitted to bar of Br. Guiana, 1882; acted as solr.-gen. on many occasions; stip. mag., 1888; solr.-gen., 1898; ag. atty.-gen., Nov., 1898, to Oct., 1899; again Oct., 1900, to Apr., 1901.
DAWE, Hon. Chas.—Newfoundland; M.H.A., Harbour Grace, 1878-1889; for Port-de-Grave since 1898; mem. exec. coun. (without portfolio), 1894, and again in 1897.
DAWSON, J. B. H.—M.R.C.S. Eng., I.R.C.P. Lond., 1893, St. Mary's Hosp. med. schl., Lond.; dist. med. offr., Leeward Islds., Oct., 1894; dist. surg. of Sierra Leone, Aug., 1893.
DAWSON, John Wilding.—Aast. eng., drainage dept., Mauritius, 30th Nov., 1894; drainage authority, 15th August, 1898.
DAWSON, Samuel Edward.—Queen's printer, Canada, and controller of stationery, Nov., 1891; doc. of letters, Laval Univ., Quebec, and fell. of roy. soc. of Canada.

DAY, H. R. A.—Cadet, Sarawak civ. service, July, 1886; asst. res. Lundu, June, 1881; asst. res., Bitulu, June, 1893; res. 2nd cl., Mar., 1896; res. of Lundu, 1898; res., 2nd cl., Upper Sarawak, Jan., 1902.

DEACON, THOMAS.—Apptd., after open compet. exam., for the home civ. ser., to the Impl. Post Office, July, 1884; postm.-gen., Gold Coast Colony, Sept. 2nd, 1899; lieut., G. C. Rifle vols., 1899; capt., 13th Oct., 1901; ag. adjutant, 11th Apr., 1903.

DEAKIN, THE HON. ALFRED.—Comsnr. of pub. wks. and min. of water supply, Victoria, Mar. 8th, 1883; and also vice-pres. bd. of land and works; solr.-gen. Nov. 13th, 1885; pres. of roy. comsn. on water supply, Dec., 1884; ch. sec. and min. of water supply, 1886; resig., Nov., 1890; sen. deleg. to col. confce., 1887; one of the Austr. delegates sent to Eng. in connection with Austr. federation bill; joined 1st ministry of the Commonwealth as atty.-gen. and min. of justice, Jan., 1901; prime min. of Australia, Sept., 1903.

DEALY, THOMAS KIRKMAN.—Ed. at St. Mary's Coll., Hammersmith; Marquis of Ripon's 1st prize and a double first (head of each list), 1880; matric. (1st div.), Lond. Univ., 1883; 1st B.A. (1st div.), 1884; Diplômé (degré supérieur) de l'Alliance Française (Paris), 1902; J.P., 1904; headmnr. St. Mary's, Derby, 1881-84; asst. master, govt. central schl., now Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, 1884; hon. exam. in physics to coll. of medicine for Chinese, 1888; passed 2nd standard higher grade Chinese, Aug., 1890; ag. 2nd master, Victoria Coll., Apr., 1890, to Sept., 1891, Apr., 1894, to Apr., 1895, Mar., 1899, to Apr., 1900, and from May, 1900, to Oct., 1901; apptd. master in charge, pol. schl., Sept. 1st, 1900.

DEANE, W. M., C.M.G. (1890); B.A., Trin. Coll., Camb., 1862; M.A., 1866.—B. 1840; student interp., Hong Kong, Mar. 28th, 1862; extra A.D.C. to Sir H. Robinson, 1864; passed as interp., 1865; priv. sec. to admstr., 1865; J.P., 1865; ag. reglar.-gen., June, 1865; capt.-supt. of pol., July, 1868; wounded severely on duty, 1878; ag. col. treas., 1881; ag. col. sec., 1881 and 1882; mem. exec. coun., 1887, and of legis. coun., 1888; ret. 1892.

DE BOISSIERE, ARNAULD.—Entd. civ. serv., Trinidad, 1891; trooper, light horse, 1897; sub-lieut., 1901; lieut., 1903; ag. chief of fire brigade, Mar., 1903; J.P. for colony, and inspr. of Indian immigts.
DE CELLES, ALFRED DUCLOS.—Joint librarian of parlmt., Canada, 1885.

DE CHARMOY, L. G. J. D'EMMEREZ.—Admitted an atty.-at-law, sup. ct., Mauritius, May, 1879; clk. to the puisne judge of the sup. ct., Apr., 1884, and interp. procureur-gen.'s dept.; 2nd class clk., regist. sup. ct., Apr., 1893; ag. accntnt. in bankruptcy, Feb., 1890, to Sept., 1891, and Apr., 1893, to May, 1896; ch. clk. registry sup. ct., 1896.
DE CORIOLIS, GUSTAVE DE.—Govt. survr., Mauritius, 1st Aug., 1889; sup't. of pub. wks. and survr.-gen., 21st March, 1892; mem. of coun. of govt.

DE FREITAS, ANTHONY, B.A. (Cantab.).—Called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn; ag. pol. mag. and coroner for E. dist., Grenada, 1900 and 1901; ag. atty.-gen., St. Vincent, Nov., 1902; ag. pol. mag. and coroner, S. dist., Grenada, Nov., 1902; ag. regist., sup. ct., Grenada, Dec., 1902, to Jan., 1904; ag. pol. mag. and coroner, S. dist., Grenada, July, 1903; ag. ch. just., mem. ex. and leg. couns., and mem. bd. of educ., St. Vincent, May to Oct., 1904; pol. mag. and coroner, 1st dist., St. Lucia, Oct., 1904.
DE GRUCHY, A. NICOLLE, M.B., C.M., Edin.—Asst. med. offr., and J.P., Victoria, Seychelles, and outlying islands, 11th Sept., 1901.

DEIGHTON, H., M.A., F.R.A.S.—Formerly scholar of Queen's Coll., Camb., 21st wrangler, 1854; 2nd class in moral science tripos, 1855; prin., Queen's Collegiate schl., Trinidad, 1860; prin. of Harrison Coll., Barbados, Aug., 1872; sent in 1879 to inspect the schls. and report on the educn. of Grenada; examined and reported on the govt. gram. schl. in St. Vincent in 1886.
DE KRETSER, EDWARD, I.S.O. (19-3).—B. 1834; employed in col. sec.'s off., Ceylon, 8th May, 1872; asst. aud.-gen., 1st Jan., 1901.

DE LA CORNILLIERE, HENRI GASTON ERNEST.—Draughtsman, P.W.D. of late O.F.S., Nov., 1892, to Mar., 1900; architectural asst.; P.W.D., O.R.C., Mar., 1900, to July, 1902; head of architectural drawing off., ditto, July, 1902.

DELAFAYE, ST LOUIS VICTOR, KT. BACH. (1898).—B. 1842; called to bar Mid. Tem., 1863; mun. councillor, St. Louis, 1870; prov. judge, sup. ct., Mauritius, Mar., 1879; mem. prison bd., Jan., 1884; mem. civ. ser. enquiry comm., Feb., 1884; mem. civ. ser. comsn., 1884-95; ag. puisne judge, 1884-5, and May, 1890, to July, 1892; Q.C., Aug., 1892; puisne judge, Nov., 1894; ag. chief justice, June, 1898; chief judge, Dec., 1898.

DELFOSSIE, LOUIS EDWARD.—Ck. in H.M.'s naval dockyard from Aug., 1869, to Aug., 1871; 3rd cl. ck. in the med. office, Aug., 1871; 1st cl. ck. in head office of the island, med. dept., Jamaica, Dec., 1890.
DE MELLO, ALOYSIUS.—Cadet, S. Sttlms., 27th Nov., 1903.

DEMIPSTER, T. ERSKINE.—Ed. at Adelaide House Acad., Jersey, and Hartley Inst., Southampton; entered the "non-regulation comsn.", Bengal, 1872, as extra asst. comsnr., Hayaribagh in 1874, special duty during Bengal famine, dep. mag. and dep. collr., Dubluanga, Tirhoot; in charge treasy., judicial, and revenue work, 1873; extra asst. comsnr., Nya, Dumka; Sonthal Pergunnahs: the same year special duty as astt. sttlm. offr. during Sonthal sttlm., determining measurements and adjudicating land disputes; 1876, sub-divisional offr. in charge Jamtara, Sonthal Pergunnahs; stip. mag., Mauritius, 1877.

DENHAM, E. B.—Ed. at Malvern and Merton Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1899; cadet, Ceylon, Nov., 1899; extra off. asst. gov't. agt. West. Prov., Dec., 1899; asst. gov't. agt., Mannar, Feb., 1902; ag. 2nd astt. col. sec., May, 1904.
DENMAN, ROBERT.—Ed. Marlh. Coll. and Guy's Hosp.; M.R.C.S. Eng., 1886; L.S.A. Lond., 1886; govt. med. offr., Seychelles, 1896; ch. med. offr., 1900.

DENNY'S, HENRY LARDNER.—Mem. of land court, Hong Kong, apptd. 10th Jan., 1902; acted as sec. of H. Kong general cham. of commerce, 1877; sec., librarian, and curator of City Hall, 1877-1899; judge's clk. and priv. sec. to ch. just. Sir John Small, H. Kong, 1873; crown solicitor, 1896-1900; ag. crown solicitor, Apl., 1902.

DENTON, SIR GEORGE CHARLES, K.C.M.G. (1900), C.M.G. (1891).—B. 1851; ensign, 57th regt., Oct., 1869; lieut., May, 1871; adjt., Aug., 1876; capt., Jan., 1878; chief of pol., St. Vincent, Apr., 1880; apptd. one of the comsnrs. to enquire into the pol. force, Barbados, Oct., 1880; mem. of exec. coun., Oct., 1881; represented St. Vincent at the telegraphic confce. at Barbados, May, 1882; admstd. govt. St. Vincent, May to July, 1885; again in 1886, and from Nov., 1887, to Mar., 1888; ag. col. sec., 1886 to 1888; col. sec., Lagos, Mar., 1888; admstd. govt. on many occasions, 1889-1900; apptd. lieut.-governor, 1900; admnr. of Gambia, Nov., 1900; gov., Mar., 1901.
DE PIRO, GIUSEPPE LORENZO, MARCHESSINO, C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1858; late hon. sec., comtee. of privileges, Maltese nobility.
DERAVIN, EUGENE E.—Rev. offr., St. Kitts, 1893; 1st rev. and excise offr., 1897.

DERBY, 16TH EARL OF (England, created 1485); BARON STANLEY, 1832 (United Kingdom); BART. 1637 (England); BARON STANLEY OF PRESTON, 1886; G.C.B. (1886); A.D.C.—THE RIGHT HON. FREDERICK ARTHUR STANLEY, younger son of the 14th Earl of Derby, b. 1841; ed. at Éton; entered the Grenadier Guards in 1858, and was apptd. lieut. and capt. in 1862; retired from the army, and entered Parlmt. as mem. for Preston in 1865; was a Lord of the Admiralty from Aug. to Dec., 1868; elected mem. for N. Lancashire at the gen. election of 1868, and again in 1874, 1880, and 1885; was financial sec. for war from Feb., 1874, to Aug., 1877, when he was apptd. financial sec. to the treasury, Mar., 1878, sec. of state for war, which office he held till the retirement of Lord Beaconsfield's adminstr. in 1880; S. of S. for Cols., June, 1885, to Feb., 1886; resign., Feb., 1886; pres. bd. of trade in Lord Salisbury's 2nd adminstr., Aug., 1886; gov.-gen. of Canada, 1888-93; succeeded his brother as Earl of Derby, 1893; Lord Mayor, Liverpool, 1895.

DE SARAH, J.H., C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1844; writer to the govt. of Ceylon, 1865; pol. mag. of Balapiti-módara, 1866; dist. judge, Badulla, Oct., 1867; comsnr. of requests, Colombo, 1868; dist. judge, Kurunégala, 1875; regis.-gen., Colombo, 1886; dist. judge, Kalutara, Dec., 1883; regist.-gen., May, 1886; dist. judge, Jaffna, Aug., 1891; ditto, Galle, Dec., 1891; ditto, Kandy, Aug., 1893.
DESCROIZILLES, FRÉDÉRIC VIVIAN.—Ed. Royal coll., Mauritius; inspr. of distill., Mauritius, May, 1874; asst. recvr.-gen., 16th June, 1897.

DESHON, EDWARD, C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1836; mem. of land bd., Queensland; ensign in 68th L.I., June, 1854; lieut., Dec., 1854; served with 68th L.I. at siege and fall of Sebastopol, from Nov., 1854, to end of the war; medal and clasp, and Turkish medal; instructor of musketry to the 68th L.I. from Nov., 1857, to Oct., 1861; passed compet. exam. for admission to Staff Coll., in July, 1861; ret. by sale of coman. in Oct., 1861; audit.-gen., Queensland, 1890.

DE SMIDT, HENRY, C.M.G. (1901), B.A. (Cape Univ.), F.S.S.—B. 1845; entered the service in Mar., 1865; permanent under col. sec. of the col. of Cape of Good Hope, and perm. head of convicts and prisons dept., holding in addition the following offices: (1) accounting offr., col. sec.'s dept.; (2) controller printg. and statnry.; (3) mem. civ. ser. comsn.; (4) mem. tender bd.; (5) custodian stamps; (6) admstr. food and drugs act; (7) inspr. emigrt. coolie ships. Was specially charged with the duties of dir. of the census of 1891; received congratulations of the govt., and was awarded an honorarium of 500l. specially voted by parlmt.; elected fellow of roy. statistical soc., 1892; asst. treasr., July, 1898; ret. 1904.

DE VILLIERS, Rt. Hon. SIR JOHN, P.C. (1897), K.C.M.G. (1882), KT. BACH. (1877).—B. 1842; ch. justice of the sup. ct., C. of Good Hope, 1874; atty.-gen. from 1872 to 1874; for several years mem. of the legis. assem.; pres. of the legis. coun., and mem. of the coun. of the Univ. of C. of Good Hope in 1873; one of the roy. coman. for the settlement of the affairs of the Transvaal, 1881; represented the Cape at the col. confce., Ottawa, 1894; mem. of judicial comtee., H.M. privy coun. 1897.

DEW, ARTHUR TOMYNS.—Naval cadet, 1867; midshipman, 1869; sub-lieut., 1873; took part in the expds. to the Linggi and Lukut rivers in 1874, and in suppression of piracy on Malayan coast (Indian medal, with Perak clasp); mag. and collr., Perak, 1881; sup. Br. territory of Dindings, 1884; dep. master attendant, shipping master, Singapore, 1886; is a J.P.; mag., Singapore and Penang; comsnr. ct. of requests; mag. and collr., dist. mag. and harbrmr., Matang, Perak, 1888; dist. mag., Krian, Apr., 1899; ag. dist. mag., Lower Perak, July, 1901; dist. offr., Batang Padang, Perak, Nov., 1902.
DEWAR, H. J.—Apptd. clk., repatriation, O.R.C., 23rd April, 1902; ditto, British refugee aid, 1st July, 1903; ditto, treasy., 1st July, 1903.
DEWDNEY, THE HON. EDGAR.—Lieut.-gov. of N.W. territories, 1881; min. of interior, Canada, 1888; lieut.-gov., Br. Columbia, 1892-7.

DE WET, THE HON. SIR JACOBUS ALBERTUS, K.C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1840; mem. Cape house of assem. and exec. coun.; min. for native affairs, 1884; Br. agent, S.A. Republic, June, 1890; ret. on pension, Sept., 1896.

DE WILTON, MAJOR ALBERT WALTER.—lieut., Lanarkshire militia, 1881; lieut., Connaught Rangers, 1883 to 1886; lieut., Indian staff corps, 1886 to 1894; capt., ditto, 10th Mar., 1894; major, ditto, 10th July, 1891; served in Burma campaign, 1885, 1887, 1889 (medal and two clasps); insp.-gen. of police, Mauritius, 29th Oct., 1898; insp.-gen. of pol. and prisons, Ceylon, 18th Aug., 1902.

DE WOLF, JAN. A.—Med. offr., St. Joseph, Trinidad, 1876; ag. health offr. of shipping, med. inspr. of imigrts., Port of Spain, 1877-9 and 1883-4; ag. surg.-gen., Aug., 1884, to June, 1885; health offr. of shipping, med. inspr. of imigrts., &c., Aug., 1890.
DEWS, ALBERT, M.A.—Classical master, Codrington Coll., Barbados, 1893.
DIAS, FELIX REGINALD, M.A., LL.M. Trin. Hall, Camb.—Called to the bar, Inner Tem., May, 1879; pol. mag. and comanr. of requests, Gampola, Ceylon, Oct., 1889; crown counsel for the island, July, 1893.

DICK, Geo. Royer, M.A.—Ed. Royal Coll., Mauritius, and Gonville and Caius, Camb. (late Fellow), called to the bar, Linc.'s Inn, Mar. term, 1869; prof. of math., Roy. Coll., Mauritius, 1879; regibr.-gen., 1886; mem. legis. coun., 1890; census comntr., 1890; ag. auditor-gen., 1893; ag.rec.-gen., June to Oct., 1895, and Apr., 1896, to Mar., 1897; audr.-gen., Apr., 1899.
DICK, John Norman.—Govt. engmr. survr., Penang, 1888; ag. govt. marine survr., Singapore, June, 1895, to June, 1896; govt. mar. surv., Singapore, March, 1901.

DICKEN, Charles Shortt, C.M.G. (1891).—B. 1841; ed. at Charterhouse; ensign 87th Royal Irish fusil., July, 1859; lieut., 1861; 1st class certif., schl. of musketry, Hythe, Nov., 1861; ret. in 1864; ag. sub-inspr. of pol., Queensland, June, 1866; clk. of petty sessions, Springsure, Oct., 1867; pol. mag., Springsure, July, 1872; gold comsrr. and pol. mag., Ravenswood, July, 1874; pol. mag. and gold-fields warden, Charters Towers, May, 1875; pol. mag., Townsville, Dec., 1878, to May, 1880; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., June, 1883; hon. sec. to Queensland comn. in Ind. and Col. exhib., Lond., 1886; J.P. of the col., and sec. Queensland govt. office, Lond.; ag. agt.-gen., Oct., 1895, to Nov., 1896, Jan. to Aug., 1897, and Jan. to Apr., 1898.

DICKSON, Thomas.—Ed. at Fettes Coll., Edin.; served in Boer war in 1st cont., Ceylon M.I., Jan., 1900, to Jan., 1901 (medal and 3 clasps); and in S.A.C. from Jan., 1901, to June, 1902 (King's medal and 2 clasps); temp. apptd. ch. elk., land settm. dept., O.R.C., 10th Oct., 1901; asst. sec., land settm., 12th Aug., 1903.

DILLON, A. Barrow.—Ed. Queen's Roy. Coll., Trinidad; clk. to inspr. of schls., B. Hond., 1893; ag. clk. to auditor and in treasy. and customs, 1893; clk. to col. engnr., 1895; ag. inspr. of schls., 1895-8; 2nd clk. treasy. and cust., 1897; inspr. of schls., June, 1898.
DILLON, P. L., I.S.O. (19-14).—4th class landing waiter, Barbados, 1866; 3rd class, 1868; senior, 1880; landing survr., 1886; comprtr. of customa, Dec., 1890.

DISCOMBE, John.—Apptd., after compet. exam., 3rd class clk., sup. ct., Gibraltar; 2nd class clk. (supernumerary), Jan., 1899; ag. asst. regist., Nov., 1899; asst. regist. and dep. clk. of arraigns, Jan., 1900; ag. regist. and admstr.-gen., July to Sept., 1901.

DOBIE, Edward David.—Crown solr., Tasmania (also clk. of the peace and regist. of building socs.), May, 1887; barrister, etc., sup. ct., Tasmania, admitted July, 1882; joined civ. ser., Tasmania, Mar., 1883; was partly draftsman and afterwards solr.-gen., from Mar. to May, 1887.

DOBERCK, Dr. W.—Astronomer in charge of Markree Observ., co. Sligo, 1874-1883; dir. Hong Kong Observ., since 1883; has published seventeen volumes of observations and researches in Hong Kong, and over 150 papers on scientific subjects in transactions of learned societies and scientific periodicals in Europe and America.

DOBSON, Hon. Alfred, C.M.G. (1904).—Called to the bar, Inner Temp., Jan., 1875; mem. legis. assem., Tasmania, 1877-87; att.-gen. and mem. exec.coun.1877; speaker legis. assem., July, 1885; solr.-gen., June, 1887; mem. of coun. of edcn., and church advoc. for the diocese of Tasmania; agt.-gen. for Tasmania in London, 1901.
DOCKER, E. B.—Dist. court judge, N. S. Wales, June, 1884.

DODDS, The Hon. Sir John Stokell, K.C.M.G. (1901), Kt. Bach., C.M.G. (1889).—B. 1848; att.-gen., Tasmania, Dec., 1878, to Oct., 1879, and in the Giblin ministry, Oct., 1879; treas. from Dec., 1881, to Aug., 1884; att.-gen. in the Douglas ministry from Aug., 1884, to Mar., 1886; also premier, Mar. to Dec., 1886; judge, sup. ct., 1886; deleg. col. confce., 1887; ch. just., 1898; admstr. of govt., 1899, and again 1900-1; lt.-gov., 3rd Aug., 1903.
DODDS, William John, M.D. (Edin.), 1879, gold medallist, M.B. and C.M., 1876; 1st class honours, D.Sc., 1878.—Inspr. of asyls. and med. supt. of Valkenburg asyl., Cape, 1889.
DOHERTY, C. J.—Puine judge, super. ct., Quebec, 1891.
DONNAN, J., C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1837; mast. attendant, Colombo, Ceylon, 1863; was comdr. of govt. steamers, "Manchester" and "Pearl," from July 1859; retired, 1902.

DONNELLY, W. J. S.—Survr.-gen., Newfoundland, to 1882; rec.-gen., 1882; resig., 1889; M.L.A. for Placentia, 1893; rec.-gen. and mem. exec. coun., Apr. to Dec., 1894; M.H.A., Placentia, 1897; rec.-gen., 1898.

DONOVAN, Justin Foley, M.D., M.Ch.L.M.—Med. offr. and health offr., Killeagh dispensary, co. Cork, 1876 to 1879; surg., R.N., 1879; received thanks of the admiralty, 1882 and 1888, "for zeal and devotion to duty" at R.N. hosps., Port Royal and Malta; and of the med. dir.-gen., R.N., for professional work, 1882-4-6; retired from the navy 1889; med. attendant, lepers' home, pub. gen. hosp. and constab. training depot, Spanish Town, Jamaica, 1889; med. examr., cvr. ser. widows and orphans pension fund, 1890.

DOUGLAS, Archibald Campbell.—Ed. Radley Coll. and Oxford Military Coll.; apptd. a consular asst. on the Niger Coast Protec., Dec., 1894; asst. dist. comntr., Feb., 1896; student, Lincoln's Inn, 1898; transport offr. in Qua Ibo dist. punitive exped., Feb. and Mar., 1899 (West African medal); promoted to rank of 2nd cl. offr., S. Nig., Jan., 1901; served as political offr. with Aro field force, Jan. to Feb., 1902 (medal); ag. dist. comntr., Brass, Apl. to Sept., 1904.

DOUGLAS, Hon. Sir Adye, Kt. Bach. (1902).—B. 1815; was elected a member of the first Tasmanian parliament, with which he has been associated ever since; ch. sec., 1884-6, and 1892-4; agt.-gen. for the col., 1886-7; pres. of legis. coun., 1894-1900.
DOUGLAS, James Archibald, M.A.—Director of education, So. Nigeria, Apl., 1903.

DOUGLAS, The Hon. John, C.M.G. (1877).—B. 1828; ed. at Rugby and Durham Univ.; has filled many important political offices in Queensland, including those of agt.-gen. and prime min.; special comsrr. for Br. New Guinea, 1885-8, when he returned to duty as res. mag., Thursday Is., Nov., 1896; asst. ree., June, 1899; res. 2nd cl. Jan., 1902; 2nd cls. ree., Barain dist., 1904.
DOUGLAS-DE-FENZI, C. W. P.—Clk. asst. and librarian, Natal; legis. coun., Feb., 1889; clk., legis. coun., 1893.
DOW, The Hon. John Lamont.—Min. of lands and agricul., pres. bd. of lands and wks., Victoria, Feb., 1884-90.

DOWNER, The Hon. Sir J. W., K.C.M.G. (1887), K.C.—B. 1844; att.-gen., S. Australia, June, 1881, to June, 1884, and att.-gen. and premier, 1885-7; del. to col. confce., 1887; ch. sec. and premier, 1892-3; mem. of senate, Australian parl., 1901-1903.

DOWNES, Major-Gen. M. Francis, C.M.G. (1883); late R.A.—B. 1834; ret., Oct., 1894; served in the Crimea, till June, 1856; medal and clasps; Turkish medal; instructor of fortification, R.M. Coll., Sandhurst, for two years; commanded artillery in Mauritius and St. Helena for four years; instructor of gunnery at Woolwich five years; col.-comdt., S. Australia, June, 1877; mem. of roy. com. on defences, Sydney, 1881; sec. of defence, Victoria, April, 1885, to Mar., 1888; comdt., S. Australian forces, Apr., 1888-92.

DOWSON, R. W.—Acctnt., Mauritius rlya., Sept., 1876; jun. audit examr., June, 1878; ch. clk. rly. dept., Apr., 1882; ag. asst. man., July, 1884, to April, 1885; head acctnt., Sept., 1891; ag. gen. man., July to Sept., 1896; asst. gen. man., July, 1903.

DOYLE, E. A. Gaynes, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lond.; obstet. soc., Lond.; mem. Br. med. assoc.; late asst. house surg., Westminster hosp.; joined med. service, Trinidad, 1884; ag. dist. med. offr., 1886; res. surg., San Fernando hosp., Nov., 1892; ag. res. surg., col. hosp., Port of Spain, 1893.

DRAKE, The Hon. Jas. George.—Mem. leg. ass., Queensland, 1888, 1893, 1896, 1899; M.L.C., 12th Dec., 1899; sec. for pub. instn. and P.M.G., Queensland, Dec., 1899, to Feb., 1901; senator for Queensland, Commonwealth parl., Mar., 1901; P.M.G., Commonwealth, Feb., 1901, to Aug., 1903; min. of def., Aug.-Sept., 1903; atty-gen., Sept., 1903; vice-pres. of ex. coun., 1904.

DRAYSON, Matthew John.—Apptd., after compet. exam., a clerk in the lower (now 2nd) div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., May, 1883; asst. regisr., May, 1892; promoted to higher grade 2nd div., July, 1896; seconded for service as chief clerk, col. sec.'s off., Hong Kong, 11th Jan., 1902; acted as clk. of coun., Aug., 1902.

DRAYTON, Edward Rawle, C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1859; clk. col. sec.'s office, Barbados, Sept., 1878; 2nd clk. in office of gov.-in-charge of the Windward Is., July, 1879; ag. ch. clk., May, 1880, and May, 1881, to Jan., 1882; ch. clk., Jan., 1882; ag. col. sec., Grenada, Apr., 1883; priv. sec. to Maj.-gen. Browne, July to Oct., 1884; provost-marshal, Grenada, Apr., 1885; ag. col. regisr., Sept., 1885; regist. sup. ct., J.P., July, 1886; mem. legis. coun., May, 1887; inspr. of prisons, Mar., 1888; mem. exec. coun., Nov., 1888; treas., May, 1890; ag. col. sec., Oct., 1890, to June, 1892; col. sec. and regisr.-gen., June, 1892; admstr. gov't. of Grenada whenever gov. absent, 1890-1901; gov't. of St. Vincent, June, 1893, Feb. to Sept., 1897, and Feb., 1900, to April, 1901; and of Windward Is., Jan., 1897; delegate to W. Indian quarantine conf., Apr., 1904.

DRINKWATER, Henry Martyn.—Ed. St. John's Coll., Hurstpierpoint, and at Edin. Univ., 1887 to 1890; 2nd treasy. offr., Montserrat, June, 1891; govt. analyst for testing petroleum, June, 1894; quarantine and visiting offr., Mar., 1899; ag. 1st treasy. offr. on numerous occasions.

DRURY, Albert Victor, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1837; Supernum. clk., War Office, Feb., 1856; to Oct., 1861; clk. col. sec.'s office, Queensland, Mar., 1862; transf. to exec. coun., Oct., 1863; clk. to exec. coun., Jan., 1867; and priv. sec. to Gov. Sir W. W. Cairns, 1875-6; to admstr. Sir A. H. Palmer, May to Dec., 1886.

DRURY, Edward Herbert Merivale.—Trinity Hall, Camb., 1890; called to the bar, Inner Temp., 1896; priv. sec. to Hon. Sir R. G. W. Herbert, G.C.B., Mar., 1899, to June, 1900; priv. sec. to Govr. Sir C. A. Moloney, K.C.M.G., Windward Is., July, 1900; ditto, Trinidad, Dec., 1900, to July, 1902; ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, St. Helena, Oct., 1902; and clk. to ex. coun., Apr., 1903; J.P., Nov., 1902.

DRURY, R. Frederick, A.M.I.C.E.—Asst. land survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1887; asst. inspr. of bldgs. and asst. sanitary survr., 1888; ag. land survr. and asst. engrnr. on extraordinary pub. wks., 1890; annually recurrent wks., 1892 and 1894; duties in connection with black plague, 1894; ag. sanitary engrnr., 1895; ag. exec. engrnr. for annually recurrent wks., 1897.

DU BOULAY, Ed.—Ag. clk. to audr. and treas., St. Lucia, Jan. to May, 1890; 4th clk., treas., Mar. to May, 1892; 3rd clk., May to Dec., 1892; 2nd clk., treas., and acct., Jan., 1893, to Apr., 1894; ag. ch. clk., treas., Feb. to Nov., 1893; audit clk., Apr., 1894; ag. audr., St. Lucia, Mar. to Sept., 1901; attached to Somersett house for service in W. African colonies, Dec., 1901.
DUDER, T. C.—J.P. Newfoundland, 1874; M.H.A., Fogo, since 1893; financial sec., 1894; chmn. bd. of wks., 1894; survr.-gen. and chmn. bd. of agricult., 1897; min. agricult. and mines, 1898.

DUFF, Benjamin, I.S.O. (1903).—Postmr., Swellendam, Cape, Apr., 1863; telegraphist in charge of Swellendam, July, 1864; ch. clk. and acctnt., telegraph dept., Mar., 1875; on active service in Transkei, as lieut. P.A.O.C.V. Artillery, Nov., 1880, to Apr., 1881; ag. gen. man. of telegraphs, June, 1883, to Feb., 1884; ch. clk. postal dept., and supt. of telegraphs, Jan., 1886; ag. sec., G.P.O., June, 1890, to Jan., 1891; sec., G.P.O., and supt. of telegraphs, 1892. Is mem. of the inst. of electrical engrns.

DUFF, Grant, The Rt. Hon. Sir M. E., P.C., G.C.S.I., C.I.E., F.R.S. (1880).—B. 1829; ed. at Balliol Coll., Oxford; B.A. 1850; under-sec. of state for India, 1868 to 1874; lord rector of Aberdeen Univ., 1866 to 1872; parly. under-sec. of state for the cols., Apr., 1880, to Aug., 1881; M.P. for Elgin dist., 1857 to 1881; gov't. of Madras, 1881-6.

DUFF, Robert.—Record clk., imigrn. dept., Br. Guiana, Apr., 1883; 4th clk., Aug., 1884; 3rd clk., Oct., 1885; 1st class certificate in Hindustani, Mar., 1885; 2nd clk., Mar., 1893; ch. clk., Jan., 1894; has acted as sub-imigrn. agt. on several occasions, and also as sen. sub-imigrn. agt.; imigrn. agt. and ch. clk., July, 1898.

DUFFIELD, Thomas.—Entered crown lands office, S. Australia, Mar., 1867; ch. clk. and imigrn. offr., crown lands and imigrn. dept., 1877; capt. mil. force, 1881; sec. crown lands dept.; mem. of central land and pastoral bds., Mar., 1896.

DUFFY, The Hon. John Gavan.—Eldest son of the late Sir Charles Gavan Duffy. Ed. at Stonyhurst. Vice-chancellor's prize, English essay, Melbourne Univ. Succeeded his father in representation of Dalhousie in legis. assem. of Victoria, 1874. Pres. of bd. of land and wks. of Victoria, 1889; postmr.-gen. of Victoria, 1890; also atty-gen., 1892; mem. of cabinet without portfolio, 1892; resig. Jan. 1893; again postmr.-gen., 1894.

DURIE, Mansergh Pace, M.R.C.P., and L.R.C.S.I., L.M. (1875).—Ed. Academic inst., and Meath hosp., Dub.; med. offr., dist. 2, Dominica, 1875 to 1880; asst. surg., Gold Coast, 1880-1; med. offr., dist. 2, Montserrat, May, 1886; offr. mem. legis. coun., Jan., 1888; med. offr., dist. 1, and med. offr. of health, Oct., 1889; mem. exec. coun., Sept., 1895; mem. pub. lib. comtee., Oct., 1896; *x officio* mem. bd. of health,

DUNCAK, ANDREW, H. F. (late lieut. R.N.)—Joined R.N., July, 1868; served in Mediterranean, S. America, Cape, India, Australia, and W. Coast of Africa; resig. Nov., 1883; passed exam. in theoretical survey, July, 1883, and practical survey, 1884, at the Cape Univ., in honours (first of his year on both occasions); from Apr., 1884, employed by the survr.-gen. of the Cape, and in priv. practice; mem. land comm. of Br. Bechuana-land, Sep., 1885; survr.-gen. of that col., July, 1886; survr.-gen. to Br. S. Africa Co., 1891.

DUNCAN, PATRICK, C.M.G. (1904).—Ed. at Oxford, schol. Ball Coll.; clk., after compet. exam., incl. rev., 1894; priv. sec. to Sir A. Milner; prin. clk., 1898; treas., Transvaal, 1901; col. sec., 1st Dec., 1903; mem. ex. and leg. couns.; mem. I.C.C.

DUNCOMBE, H. FEVRESHAM.—Pay clk. army pay off commissariat, Bahamas, 1889; 2nd master, Nassau gram. schl., 1890; 4th clk. treas., 1891; 3rd ditto, 1896; ag. dist. justice and collr. of rev. in various dists. in 1895 and 1896; J.P., 1896; rev. justice and collr. rev., Abaca, 1897; dist. comnr., Lagos, 1898.
DUNEDIN, 2ND BISHOP OF (founded 1864), Rt. REV. SAMUEL T. NEVILLE, D.D.—Consec. 1871; Magdal. Coll., Camb., B.A. 1865; curate of Scarsbrick, Lancs., 1860-64; rector of Skelton, Staffs., 1864-71.

DUNKERLEY, REV. W. H., M.A.—Ed. Pembroke Coll., Oxon.; col. chaplain, Malacca, 1891; ag. col. chaplain, Singapore, May to Oct., 1892, and May, 1896; col. chaplain, Penang, July, 1897; do. Singapore, April, 1901; archdeacon, Dec., 1902.

DUNLOP, C. E.—Writer, Ceylon civ. ser., Apr., 1871; pol. mag., Galagdara, 1873; Kalpitiya and Puttalam, 1874; landing survr., Galle, 1874; office asst. to govt. agt., E. prov., 1877; dist. judge, Badulla, 1878; office asst. to govt. agt., N. Cent. prov., 1881; asst. collr. of customs, Galle, 1883; dep. ditto, Colombo, 1884; dist. judge, Tangalla, 1887; ditto, Kalutara, 1895.

DUNLOP, COLONEL SAMUEL R.A., C.M.G. (1884)—B. 1838; ag. comnr. of pol., S. Stilttna, Nov., 1870; ag. pol. mag. and comnr. of ct. of requests, P.W., Apr., 1871; ag. dep. consnr. of pol. and coroner, Apr., 1871; ag. dep. comnr. of pol., Singapore, fire comnr. and coroner, Aug., 1871; ag. inspr.-gen. of pol., Dec., 1872; comnr. to Perak, Jan., 1874. In Nov., 1874, comnr. with the forces sent to quell the disturbances in the native state of Sungai Ujong; inspr.-gen., Straits pol., May, 1875. In Nov., 1875, on the murder of Mr. Birch, apptd. special comnr. (temporarily) for Perak affairs; organized the expdn. which captured the Passir Salak stockades, and was present at the capture, Nov., 1875; comnr. to forces during operations in Perak, Dec., 1875; accompanied Gen. Colborne's force up the Perak river, and across country to Kinta; ag. rea. coun., Penang, 1884 to 1885; pres. of Singapore mun. comnr., 1889; ret., 1890.

DUNN, ROBERT ERNEST.—Clerk and interp. to the R.M., Umgeni div., Natal, 1880; to R.M., Lions River, Mar., 1883; to R.M., Alfred, Oct., 1887; to R.M., Upper Umkomazi, 1894; mag. for Colony, mag., Weenen div., 1901.
DUNN, S.A.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. and assigned to the C.O., Oct., 1899.

DUPIGNY, E. G. MORSON.—Asst. govt. off., Dominica, Feb., 1889; clk. comrs. of valuati., 1890; clk. mag., dist. F., 1890; do., presdt.'s off., Dom., 1890-92; do. regtr.'s off., 1892; do. treas., Antigua, 1896; stud. Mid. Temp., 1900.
DUPONT, LOUIS EVENOR.—4th clk., registry sup. ct., Mauritius, 1st Sept., 1864; ch. clk., regist. dept., 18th June, 1884; recvr. of regist. dues and conservator of mortgages, 6th May, 1901.
DURMAN, F. J.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv., and assigned to C.O., 15th April, 1901.

DU VERGE, L. S. R., I.S.O. (1903).—Inspr. of distilleries, Mauritius, 1868; ag. regist. gen., 1896-7; storekeeper-gen., June, 1897; ag. recvr.-gen., in 1901, 1902 and 1903; nom. mem. of coum. of gent.
DUVIVIER, AIMÉ.—Clk., archives office, Mauritius, 1st Nov., 1879; custodian of archives, 1st Jan., 1891.

DYER, JAMES HAROLD.—Ed. King William's Coll., I. of Man, King Edward VI. schl., Berkshirestead; read. his comnr. in 3rd Batt. S. Lanc. regmt., 1889; obtained his compy., 1893; resig. comnr., 1894; joined Matabele relief force, 1896, and served through the campaign till the force was disbanded in 1897; joined C.M.R., 1897, and served through siege of Mafeking; resig. Cape Police in July, 1901; received medals and clamps; apptd. ast. dist. comnr. S. Nigeria, 28th Dec., 1901; ag. dist. comnr., Ediba, 1902.

DYER, THOS. T. R.—Entd. pub. ser. of St. Vincent as cust. offr., Jan., 1890; ag. clk., treasy., 1891; ast. clk., P.O., 2nd clk. gov. off. to Apl., 1892; ag. 2nd clk. in off. of gov., Windward Isla., May, 1892; transf. to St. Lucia; 4th clk., treasy., May, 1892; 3rd clk., Jan., 1893; ag. 2nd clk., Feb. to May, 1893; 2nd clk., govt. off., May, 1893; also supt. of govt. printing; ag. 2nd clk. in off. of adminstr., Wind. Isla., 1894; hurricane relic offr., St. Vincent, Sept. to Dec., 1898; ag. ch. clk. and clk. of couns., St. Lucia, Oct., 1900, to Mar., 1901; apptd. ch. clk. to gov. Windward Is. (Grenada), 1st Apr., 1901; ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, and clk. of ex. and leg. couns., 16th Nov., 1902.

DYER, SIR WILLIAM T. THISELTON, M.A., F.R.S., K.C.M.G. (1899), C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1843; Chr. Ch., Oxon., 1873; asst. dir., Royal Botanic Gardens, Kew, 1873; dir., 1886; botan. adviser to S. of S. for Col.

DYETT, EDWARD F.—2nd treasy. offr., Montserrat, Aug., 1886; admasurer of vessels, 1889; 1st treasy. offr., June, 1891; comnr. oaths, Nov., 1895; clk. of waterworks comnrs., 1896; sec. bd. of health, Jan., 1897; holds comnr. to act as treasur. of Montserrat in absence of the comnr. and has so acted on numerous occasions since Dec., 1897; offl. receiver in bankruptcy, Aug., 1898; holds comnr., dated Feb., 1899, to admin. oaths of office to ag. comnr., in absence of comnr. and govr.; mem. legis. coun., Mar., 1900.
DYETT, HENRY.—Comnr.'s and mag.'s clk., Montserrat, Feb., 1896; ag. 2nd treasy. offr. in portions of Apl., May, Sept., 1897; clk. of exec. and legis. couns., Mar., 1898.

DYETT, RICHARD HENRY KORTRIGHT.—Servd in pres.'s office, Virgin Is., 1879 to 1881, and 1882 to 1884; clk., registr.'s office, Nevis, 1886; 2nd clk., regier.'s office, Antigua, 1887; clk. to trustee, supt. ct. lib., July, 1888; comsnr. to admnr. oaths, 1889; marshal V.-A. ct., L.I., Dec., 1889; clk. to mag., dist. A., 1890; dep. cor., dist. A., 1892; ag. postmr., May to Sept., 1894; 1st clk. regier.'s off., Jan., 1895; dep. cor., dist. B., Oct., 1896; ag. mag., dist. A., Jan., Feb., Apl. to Nov., 1897; sec. to H.M. Diamond Jubilee comteese., June, 1897; ag. mag., dist. B., Apl., 1898, to Jan., 1899; ag. mag., dist. B., May to Sept., 1899; and 25th Oct., 1899; ag. mag., dist. A., Nov., 1899, to May, 1900; during which period (Jan. to Apl.) acted also as mag., dist. B.; ag. mag., dist. A., June, 1900, to Mar., 1901; and Apl. to Nov., 1901; also in Mar., 1902; mem. of "Coronation" comteese., and hon. sec. to same, May, 1902; ag. mag., dist. A., Aug., 1902, to Feb., 1903; regier. and prov.-marshal and regier. of titles, St. Kitts-Nevis, 11th June, 1903; mem. of Basseterre town bd., June, 1903.
EAKIN, JAS. W.—B.A., M.D., M. Ch. Univ., Dublin; govt. med. offr., Trinidad; dist. med. offr., South Naparima, and consulting surg. Col. hosp., San Fernando; surg.-major, local force.
EARL, ROBT. STEPHEN, M.A., M.B., B.C. (Cantab., 1891).—Med. offr. Lasoyedist, Dominica, July, 1892; offr., No. 5 Dist. Antigua, 1896; comsnr. Virgin Is.

EARNSHAW, ALBERT.—Ed. at Univ. Coll., Durham, and Pemb. Coll., Oxford; held foundtn.; Newby and Univ. class. schlrshps., Durham, 1886-1887; 1st cls. classics, 1st year exam., 1886; 1st cls. clas. final, 1887; B.A., 1887; M.A., 1890; elected fellow of Durham, 1889; open class. schlr., Pemb. Coll., Oxford, Jan., 1888; 1st cls. clas. moda., 1889; 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1891; B.A. Oxford, 1891; called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1893; went N.E. circuit and W. Ridg. Yorks., sessions, etc., 1893-1904; common law lecturer, Yorks. Coll., Leeds, 1899-1900; pol. mag. and coroner, W. div., Grenada, Feb., 1904.

EATON, HENRY FRANCIS.—Clk., col. storekeeper's office, Melbourne, Jan., 1853; transf'd. to civil commissariat, Feb., 1854; acctnt., govt. stores, Feb., 1855; transf'd. to treasy., Mar., 1865; ag. acctnt., treasy., 1869-70, 1876, 1886-7; acctnt. to treasy., 1887; under treasy., Sept., 1889—1895; capt. volr. artillery, 1876; is in the commission of the peace of the Colony.

EDWARDS, COL. ALFRED HAMILTON MACKENZIE, C.B.—Served in 1st Dragoon Guards, Jan., 1883, to Apl., 1892; adjutant, Behar Light Horse Volunteers, India, Apl., 1892, to Apl., 1897; 1st and 5th Dragoon Guards and Imperial Light Horse, Apl., 1897, to Oct., 1900; Hazara (N.W. Frontier, India), 1888, asst. prov. marshal (ment. in desp., medal and clasp); commanded Imperial Light Horse, S. African war, Nov., 1899, to June, 1900; dist. comsnr., Potchefstroom and Krugersdorp dista., June to Oct., 1900; commanded "A" division, S.A.C., Oct., 1900, to end of war (ment. in desp., Queen's medal, 4 clasps, King's medal, 2 clasps); substantive lt.-col. in the army, dated 29th Nov., 1900; committ., Transvaal volunteers.

EDWARDS, CHAS. HENRY.—Ed. St. Bees gram. schl. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; exhibtnr. of Queen's Coll., B.A., 1894; B.C.L., 1901; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., 1898; dist. comsnr., G. Coast, Dec., 1899; dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius, 1904.
EGERTON, RICHARD.—Ed. Westminster and Chr. Ch., Oxford; B.A. 1867; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., Nov., 1869; asst. res. mag., Kingston, May, 1892; res. mag., Jamaica, Feb., 1893.

EGERTON, WALTER, C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1858; ed. Tonbridge Schl.; cadet, S.
