Sttlmte., Oct., 1880; asst. to the Chinese prot., Jan., 1881; passed final exam., Malay, May, 1882; collr. of land rev., Penang and P.W., Jan., 1883; 2nd mag. of pol., Penang, May, 1883; also official assignee under the Bankruptcy Ordinance (Sept., 1882); ag. sen. dist. offr. Butterworth, P.W., Apl., 1890; a.J.P. and coroner for S. Sttlmte., and a visiting justice for Penang; offr. in char., Sungai Ujong, July, 1893; ag. res., Pahang, May to July, 1894; offl. assignee and regier. of deeds, Singapore, June, 1896; inspr. of prisons, S. Sttlmte., May, 1897; dep.-pres. of mun. comsnrs., Singapore, Mar., 1897; 1st mag., Penang, Apl., 1897; ag. 1st mag., Singapore, Jan., 1898; ag. res. councillor, Malacca, Mar., 1898, and Mar., 1899; 1st mag., Singapore, Oct., 1898, but continued to act as res. coun.; ag. col. treas., S.S., Nov.-Dec., 1898; ag. col. sec., S.S., Apl., 1900; ag. res. councilr., Penang, Feb. to Apl., 1901; ag. col. treas., S.S., Aug.-Oct., 1902; res. Ngy. Semb., Aug., 1902; high comnr., S. Nigeria, Nov., 1903; and gov., Lagos, 30th July, 1904.

EGGETT, W. H.—Apptd., after a compet. exam., to be a clk. of the lower (now 2nd) div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O. Nov., 1880; promoted to higher grade, 2nd div., Apl., 1896; asst. accntnt., 6th Apl., 1899.

EHREHARDT, ALBERT.—Ed. King Edward's High Schl., B'ham, and Worc. Coll., Oxford; graduated with honours in mods. and lit. hum., 1886; Inner Tem. schlrshp., common law, 1888; called to bar, 1889; dist. comsnr., Lagos, 1896; travelling comsnr. on the E. bdry., Aug., 1897; ag. res. of Ibadan, 1898; ag. treas. and mem. exec. and legis. coun., June, 1900; ag. att. gen., Sept., 1900; rlwy. comsnr. at Aro, June, 1901; ag. treas., Aug., 1901, to Mar., 1902; ag. atty.-gen., Mar. to June, 1902; apptd. res., Ibadan, Feb., 1902; apptd. col. treas., May, 1902; atty.-gen., Fiji, mem. ex. and leg. couns., May, 1903; ag. ch. just., May, 1904.

ELCUM, JOHN BOWEN, B.A., Oxon.—Ed. Highgate Schl. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; scholar of Queen's Coll., 1878; cadet, S. Sttlmte., Oct., 1884; passed in Malay, 1886; ag. dist. offr., S. Malacca, 1887; dist. offr. Bukit Mertajam, P.W., Jan., 1890; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, Dec., 1890; sheriff, Penang, Mar., 1892; ag. sheriff, Singapore, June, 1893; 2nd mag., Penang, Nov., 1893; ag. collr. land revenue, Singapore, Dec., 1893, to Feb., 1894; ag. inspr. of schls. S. Sttlmte., June, 1895; also ag. official assignee and regier. of deeds, Singapore, Mar. to June, 1896; collr. of land rev. and offr. in charge of treasy., July, 1897; ag. 1st mag., Singapore, Mar., 1898; inspr. of schls., Aug., 1898, but continued to act as first mag. till Feb., 1899; dir. pub. instrn., Aug., 1901; inspr. of prisons, July, 1904; fin. comsnr., F.M.S., Oct., 1904.

ELDRIDGE, A. E. G.—Clk., P.O., Antigua, Mar., 1880; clk. to pres. and isld. sec. and clk. to exec. coun., 1882; postmr. of Antigua, Aug., 1891; ag. mag. and coroner, Dist. B., 1894, 1896, and 1897; ag. mag. and coroner, Dist. H., Nevis, Apl., 1899, to Feb., 1900; mem. St. Kitts-Nevis legis. coun., and gen. legis. coun., 1899.

ELGEE, Capt. C. H.—Ed. Lancing and Sandhurst; 1st commn., 16th Foot, Feb., 1892; capt., Oct., 1899; active service, Chitral, 1895 (medal with clasp); Ashanti campaign, relief of Kumasi, 1900 (medal); adjutant, Lagos Haussa Force, 1899; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to Sir W. Macgregor, gov. of Lagos, 1900 to 1902; and clk. of ex. coun.; ag. insp.-gen., 1902; trav. commn., Lagos, 1902; ag. col. treas., mem. of ex. and leg. couns., 1902; resident, Ibadan, 1st Jan., 1903.

ELIOT, EDWARD CARLTON.—Ed. Bradford Coll. and Uppingham; apptd. 5th class clk., govt. secretariat, British Guiana, 1895; seconded ag. clk. to govt. agent, N.W. dist., Br. Guiana, for periods 1896 to 1899; 4th class clk., govt. secretariat, 1899; pay and quartermr. to Haussa force, Gold Coast Col., 1900; apptd. senior asst. treas., Oct., 1902.

ELIOT, LAURENCE S., I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1845; clk. to mags., W. Australia; landing and tide waiter at Bunbury, 1863; also postmr. and asst. dist. regir.; clk. in col. sec.'s office, 1872; sec. to central bd. of educn., and asst. clk. in legis. coun., 1873; regir.-gen., regir. of deeds, and regir. of brands, 1876; ag. ch. clk., col. sec.'s dept., Jan., 1878, to Jan., 1880; Ist clk. and regir.-gen., col. sec.'s dept., Jan., 1880; apptd. to take the census, Apr., 1881, and to superintend its compilation; ch. clk. and accntnt., treas., Apr., 1881; ag. asst., col. sec., Dec., 1889, to Oct., 1890; under treas., Jan., 1891; is a J.P.

ELLENBERGER, JULES.—Clk. to asst. commn., Bech. Prot., 1890; interpreter to concessions commn., Bech. Prot., 1893; ditto to Sir S. Shippard's boundry. commn., 1894; J.P., 1893; asst. mag., 1898; served in S. African war, including relief of Mafeking; spec. commn. for oaths of allegiance in So. dist. of Bech. Prot. to burghers of late S.A.R. and O.F.S.; asst. commn., So. dist., Bech. Prot., 11th July, 1902.
ELLERY, R. L. J., F.R.S., C.M.G. (1889).—B. 1827; astronomer, Victoria, 1853.
ELLIOT, MAJOR SIR HENRY GEORGE, K.C.M.G. (1899), C.B. (1901), C.M.G. (1879).—B. 2826; ch. mag. of Tembuland, C. of Good Hope; in command of Tembu levies during Basuto war; ret., 1904.

ELLIOTT, Sir CHARLES BLETTERMAN, LL.B., K.C.M.G. (1901), C.M.G. (1894).—B. 1841; clk. col. sec.'s office, Cape, 1859; clk. to Justice Watermeyer, 1863; sec. to examir. in literature and science; clk., col. sec.'s office, 1867; ch. clk. dept. of lands and wks., Dec., 1872; advoc.sup., 1875; asst. commn., crown lands and pub. wks., 1876; gen. manager, rly., Dec., 1880; is mem. of coun. of Univ., Cape; examir. and moderator of examirs. in science; ret., 1904.

ELLIOTT, COL. JOHN, R.M.L.I., C.B. (1877), C.M.G. (1891).—B. 1824; entered R.N. n 1838; 2nd lieut., June, 1846; col., 1849; served in the China expdn., 1842 (medal); served in H.M.S. "Fox" during the Burmese war, 1852 (officially thanked); mentioned in despatches; comld. the Royal Marines in the steam flotilla on the Irrawaddy (mentioned in gov.-gen.'s despatch; medal with clasp for Pegu). Served at the bombardment and surrender of the forts of Bomarsund, in Aug., 1854 (Baltic medal), and during the siege of Sebastopol in 1855, the expdn. to Kertch and Yeni-Kale (medal with clasp, 5th class of the Medjidie, and Turkish medal); has also the medal for the Kaffir war. Served as maj. of brigade to the force of Royal Marines landed in Mexico in 1863; lieut.-col., Devonshire Rifle Volr. Corps, 1870; 1871 J.P. for Plymouth; in 1874 called to the bar, Mid. Temp., and joined the western circuit; in 1876 inspr. of prisons and stip. J.P., Br. Guiana; inspr.-gen. of pol., Barbados, 1881; adminstd. govt. of St. Vincent, 1884 and 1889; acted as col. sec., Barbados, 1886 and 1894; ret., 1895.

ELLIS, FRANCIS ROBT., C.M.G., B. 1849; writer in the Ceylon civ. ser., July, 1871; additional pol. mag., Kurunegala, 1874; office asst. to govt. agt. N. Prov., 1876; ag. pol. mag., Kandy, 1878; ag. asst. agt., Matale, 1879; ditto Mullaitivu, 1880; dist. judge, Ratnapura, 1st Apr., 1883; pol. mag., Colombo 1885; dir. and inspr.-gen. of prisons, 1891-6; ag. inspr.-gen. pol., May to Nov., 1893; ag. prin. collr. of cust., Jan., 1896; govt. agt., S. prov., Jan. to Mar., 1897; govt agt., W. prov., Sept., 1897; aud.-gen., Mar., 1902; ag. col. sec., 19th Nov., 1903.
ELLIS, FRANK THOM.—Cadet, S. Sttlmts., 27th Nov., 1903.

ELLIS, WALTER DEVONSHIRE.—Ed. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxon.; scholar 1890-89; prox. acc. Hertford Univ. scholarship, 1891; 1st class classical mods., 1892; Univ. jun. Greek testament essay, 1893; 1st class literae humaniores, 1894; B.A., 1894; M.A., 1897; chancellor's prize, Latin essay, 1895; apptd., after compet. exam., to be a clk. in the C.O., Apr., 1895; priv. sec. to Lord Selborne, June, 1897; 1st class clk., 1899.

ELLIS, WILLIAM GILMORE, M.D., M.R.C.S.—Med. supt., lunatic asylum, Singapore, 1888; ag. also as municipal health offr., Singapore, May, 1889, to Feb., 1892 again 1894; ag. col. rec. surg., in 1901, 1902 and 1903.

ELY, HERBERT WILLIAM.—Clk. in dept. of agt. gen. for Tasmania; ch. clk., 1895; sec., 1899; acted as priv. sec. & Rt. Hon. Sir Edward Braddon, P.C., K.C.M.G., ex-premier of Tasmania, during his visit to England for the Diamond Jubilee celebration, 1897.

EMERSON, GEO. HY. K.C.—Speaker, house of assem., Newfoundland, 1890-94; mem. of official delegation to London, 1890, and of delegation from legislature in 1891 on the fisheries question; mem. exec. coun., 1895; asst. judge, sup. ct., Nov., 1896.
EMMENS, JOHN HOWARD.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to C.O., 11th Mar., 1901.

EMTAGE, O. DE C., M.A.—Ed. Harr. Coll., Barbados; Barbados scholar, 1886; scholar, Wore. Coll., Oxon., 1887; 1st class math. mods., 2nd cls. finals, 1890; hon. nat. sc. schools, 1891; asst. mast., Harr. Coll., 1891; examiner, 2nd grade schools, Barbados, 1891-1902; ditto, gram. school, St. Lucia, 1894-96; ditto, Grenada, 1897-98; head master, The Lodge school, 1899.
EMTAGE, WM. THOM. ALLDER.—M.A., Oxon.; director of pub. instrn., Mauritius, 21st Aug., 1900; nom. mem. of coun. of govt.

ENGELBACH, A. H. H., I.S.O. (1902).—B. 1850; apptd., 29th Dec., 1869, after passing an exam.before the civ. ser. commrs., clk.in the accnt. branch in the office of the sec. of state for the cols., having acted in that capacity since Mar., 1863; 1st class asst. clk., 1st Sept., 1879; book keeper and sen. asst. to the financial clk., 1884; acted for financial clk., June, 1886, to Apr., 1887; accountant, Apr., 1890.

ENRAGHT-MOONY, F.—Sub-inspr., Basuto land police, Mar., 1884; acted as govt. sec., Basutoland, 1883-1885; commanded Bech. Prot native police with rank of capt., 1896; asst. commn., Basutoland, 1897; acted as govt. sec. 1900-1901; mentioned in com.-in-chief's desps., 1901; native comsnr., Zoutpansberg, Aug., 1901; commanded Ft. Edward and Spelonken dist., Northern Transvaal, and acted as intell. offr. until close of war; special comsnr., Swaziland, June, 1902.

ESNOUF, E. AMAND.—Dist. mag., Seychelles, 1870; Grand Port, Mauritius, 15 May, 1878; dist. mag., Moka, 1883; jun. dist. mag., Port Louis, Aug., 1884; ag. master sup. ct., May, 1886; and Dec., 1887, to Jan., 1888; ag. sen. dist. mag., Pt. Louis, Mar., 1890, to Mar., 1892; Dec., 1892, to Feb., 1893; May to Aug., 1894; ag. dist. mag., Pt. Louis, 3rd div., Feb., 1898; ag. dist. and stip. mag., Riv. du Rempart, Mar., 1898; dist. mag., Pt. Louis, 3rd div., Mar., 1899; ag. substitute mast., sup. ct., Aug., 1901.
EVANS, SIR DAVID, K.C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1849; Lord Mayor of London, 1891-2; raised funds for relief of distress caused by the Mauritius hurricane and the fire at St. John's, Newfoundland.

EVANS, FREDERICK, C.M.G. (1881), C.V.O. (1903).—B. 1849; entered the cin. ser., Admiralty, Somerset house, 13th Dec., 1867; transfd. to the sec.'s dept. at Whitehall, Jan., 1872; asst. col. sec. and treas., S. Leone, May, 1878; priv. sec. to Sir S. Rowe, K.C.M.G., Feb., 1879, to Feb., 1881; ag. clk. of legis. coun., Sept., 1879; acted as col. sec., Dec., 1879, to Feb., 1880; ch. sec. and ch. of the staff to Sir S. Rowe during threatened hostilities with Ashanti, 1881; asst. col. sec., G. Coast, 1882; ag. collr. of customs and treas., Oct., 1882, to Jan., 1883; ag. col. sec., Aug. to Dec., 1883, and on other occasions; clk. of legis. coun., Feb., 1882, to Apr., 1884; dep. gov., Lagos, July to Aug., 1883, and May, 1885, to Jan., 1886; dep. gov., G. Coast, Sept., 1883, and Apr., 1885; col. sec., Lagos, Jan., 1886; ag. adminstr., July, 1886; col. sec., G. Coast, 1887; Leeward Is., 1888; Jamaica, 1896; ag. 1st cl. clk., C.O., Feb., 1900; col. sec., Gibraltar, 1901.

EVANS, WILLIAM.—Cadet, S. Sttlmnts., Nov., 1882; ed. King's Coll., Camb.; ag. astt. protector of Chinese, Singapore, Dec., 1885; ag. collr. of land rev., Malacca, May, 1896; 2nd astt. prot. of Chinese, May, 1887; ag. astt. prot. Chinese, Singapore, Feb., 1888; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, July, 1888; passed exams. in Chinese and Malay; J.P., ag. dist. offr., Dindinga, 1888; astt. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, June, 1890; ag. prot. of Chinese, Penang, July, 1890; ditto, S. Sttlmnts., Nov. and Dec., 1891, to June, 1893; astt. prot. Chinese, Penang, 1893; prot. of Chinese, S. Sttlmnts., Apr., 1895; ag. audr.-gen., S.S., May, 1901, to July, 1902; pres., municipal comsrs., Singapore, Sept., 1903; seconded for 6 mos. spec. serv. in Transvaal as prot. of Chinese, 25th Dec., 1903.
EVELYN, EDWARD A.—2nd clk. to regisr., St. Kitts, 1899; govt. offr., Sandy Point, Apl., 1903.

EVELYN, EDWARD ERNEST.—Ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; ent. col. sec.'s off., Barbados, 1883; astt. clk., govt. savings bk., 1883; ch. clk., 1884; rev. offr., St. Lucia, Jan., 1885, to Nov., 1890; acnt. P.W.D., Nov., 1890, to Jan., 1893; audit clk., Jan., 1893, to Mar., 1894; ch. clk., govt. off., and clk. of coun., Apl., 1894; astt. col. sec., S. Leone, Nov., 1900.
EVELYN, E. F.—Acentnt. of post office, Barbados, Sept., 1880.

EVELYN, MERRIT.—2nd clk. to pres., St. Kitts, Dec., 1878; ch. clk., treasy., Nevis, 1881; ag. treas., Nevis, July, 1882-1883; rev. offr. in charge, Nevis, treasy., 1883; prot. of imigts., Nevis, 1883; land and house tax comsnr., 1892; ag. mag., Nevis, 1894-5, 1895-6; water comsnr., Nevis, 1901; mem. quarantine bd., Nevis, 1903.

EVERARD, W.—Supernum. col. sec.'s office, Barbados, Sept., 1868; landing waiter, customs, Dec., 1868; ag. inspr. of inl. rev. offrs., Jan., 1884, to Dec., 1885; ch. clk., P.O., Mar., 1885; sen. landing waiter, customs, June, 1886.
EVERETT, COL. SIR W., K.C.M.G.—B. 1844; apptd. one of Brit. comsrs. in Paris for settlement of W. African affairs, 1896-1898.
EVERY, THE REV. EDWARD FRANCIS, M.A.—Bishop of the Falklands, 1902.
EYLES, C. H.—Asst. col. surg., G. Coast Col., 1883; col. surg., Br. Honduras, 1887.

EZECIEL, P. H.—B.A., Bombay, 1889 (at 14); first in Woolwich competn., 1893; B.A., London, 1894 (2nd exhib. at matricn., 1892); major schlr., Trin. Coll., Camb., 1896; 4th wrangler and B.A., Camb., 1897; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. clk., C.O., 11th Oct., 1898; passed final exam. for Bar, 1902.
FABRE, HECTOR, C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1834; formerly senator, Canada; hon. comsnr. for Canada, Ind. and Col. exhib.; now Canadian agt., Paris.
FACEY, LUTHER HERBERT.—3rd cl. clk., prisons dept., Jamaica, June, 1877; 2nd cl. clk., June, 1879; 1st cl. clk., police and prisons dept., Nov., 1892.

FAGAN, JOSEPH PATRICK.—L.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., L.M., Ireland; dist. med. offr., Niger Coast Prot., 13th Apl., 1894; senior med. offr., 29th May, 1903 (medal and clasp); Benin River, 1894 (medal and clasp); Aro Field Force, 1901-2.

FAILLE, GEO. CUNNYNGHAM.—Ed. Stonyhurst Coll.; ag. 2nd clk. to gov., Leeward Is., May, 1889; 2nd clk. to col. sec., Jan., 1890; 2nd clk., gov.'s office, June, 1890; ch. clk. col. sec. and clk. exec. coun., Aug., 1891.

FAIRBAIRN, ROBERT.—Second master, boys' schl., Perth, W. Australia, 1859; clk. to bench of mags., landing waiter, and postmr., Vasse, 1862; res. mag., mag. of local ct., and dist. regar., 1875; Newcastle, 1875; Vasse, 1880; Kimberley, 1883; Fremantle, 1886.

FALCK, DAVID GEORGE ANOSI.—Joined post and tel. dept., O.F.S., Nov., 1881; postmr. and telegraphist, Winburg, Nov., 1881; landdrost's clk., Bethlehem, Aug., 1889; ag. landdrost, Heilbronn, Apl., 1890; sec., G.P.O., O.F.S., Aug., 1892; landdrost, Pietermaritzburg, 1894 (not assumed); postmr.gen., O.F.S., 1st Jan., 1895; ditto, O.R.C., Mar., 1900.

FALCONBRIDGE, HON. WILLIAM GLENHOLME.—Ed. Toronto Univ.; B.A. (gold medalist), 1866; M.A., 1870; barrister, 1871; registr., Univ. of Toronto, 1872 to 1881; elected mem. of senate of Univ., 1881; Q.C., 1888; bencher of law society of Upper Canada, 1885; judge of sup. ct., Ontario, Q.B. div., Nov., 1887; pres. high. ct. and ch. just., Q.B., 1900.
FALSHAW, PERCY SCOTT.—M.R.C.V.S., Govt. vet. surgeon, Singapore, S. Sttlmnts., 15th June, 1893.

FANNIN, JOHN EUSTACE.—R.M. and admstr. of native law, co. Umvoti, Natal, Mar., 1887; previously at Lower Tugela div., from Mar., 1883; had previously acted as R.M. of Umlazi and Umgeni divs.; was special border agt., Umvoti, during the Zulu war of 1879; a mem. of Natal native comsn., 1881, and of the comsn. to define bndry. between Natal and Orange Free State, 1884; received thanks of sec. of state for services on this occasion; mem. of special judicial comsn. for trial of Zulu chiefs, 1888; judge, native high ct., 1899.
FARRAR, NICHOLAS.—Supernum., P.W. dept., Br. Guiana, 1883; 3rd clk., Jan., 1885; 5th class clk., rec. gen.'s office, May, 1889; 4th class, Apr., 1891.
FARRELL, WILLIAM OWEN.—Asst. guard, Mauritius rly., 1882; goods clk., 1884.

FARRER, ROLAND J.—Ed. at Eton, and Balliol Coll., Oxon; cadet, S. Sttlmts., Nov., 1896; ag. dist. offr., Nibong Tebal, Mar., 1897, to Sep., 1898; Dec., 1898, to Mar., 1900; ag. collr. land rev., Penang, Mar., 1900; ag dist. offr., Dindings, May, 1900; ag. supt. educ., Penang, June to Aug., 1900; head of Malay Coll., Malacca, May, 1901; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., June, 1901; ag. dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, May, 1902; confirmed, July, 1904; ag. dep. pub. proscr., July, 1904.
FAULKNER, E.—Ed. at the gram. schl., Free-town; brigade office clk., S. Leone, 1876 to 1883; ch. clk. col. secretariat, 1883; asst. col. sec., 1889.
FAURE, HON. SIR P. H., K.C.M.G. (1898).—B. 1848; sec. for native affairs, Cape, July, 1890; col. sec., May, 1893; sec. for agricultr., Jan., 1896, to Oct., 1898; col. sec., 1902.
FAWCETT, WILLIAM, B.SC. (LOND.), F.L.S.—Asst. botanical dept., Br. Museum, 1880; dir. pub. gardens and plantations, Jamaica, 1886; mem. bd. of govs., Jamaica institute, 1887.

FAWKES, ARCHIBALD WALTER, K.C.—Ed. Repton schl.; B.A., St. John's Coll., Camb.; called to bar, Inner Tem., Jan., 1879; regizr., sup. ct., comsnr. of ct. of requests and of stamps, Malacca, 1884; ag. regizr., sup. ct., Penang, 1885; pol. mag. and coroner, Gibraltar, 1886; acted as atty.-gen., 1890 and 1891; atty.-gen., Gibraltar, 1892; puisne judge, O.R.C., 1901.
FEILDEN, CAPT. R. B.—Capt., R.A.; ed. Wellington Coll.; lieut. R.A., July, 1884; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to gov., Barbados, May, 1890; to high comsnr., Cyprus, 1892; and to gov. Br. Guiana, 1898.

FELL, T. E., B.A., Cantab., 1897—Apptd. ch. clk., col. sec.'s off., G. Coast, 2nd Oct., 1897; ag. asst. col. sec., 14th Sep., 1899; inspr., preventive services, 4th Dec., 1899; ag. trav. comsnr., 1900-1901; trav. comsnr., 16th June, 1902; ag. comsnr. for nat. affairs, 26th June, 1902.

FERGUSON, DONALD.—Elected to provincial legislature, Pr. Ed. Is., 1878; apptd. to provl. cabinet in 1879, with the portfolio of pub. wks.; resig. the latter office in 1880, when he was apptd. provl. sec. and comsnr. of pub. lands; served as deleg. to Downing st. in 1886, regarding communication between P. E. Is. and mainland.

FERGUSSON, RIGHT HON. SIR JAMES, BART., P.C., M.P., G.C.S.I. (1885), K.C.M.G. (1874), C.I.E. (1880).—B. 1832; ed. at Rugby and Univ. Coll., Oxford; was lieut. and capt. of the grenadier guards; served in the Crimean campaign, including the battles of Alma and Inkerman (wounded) and siege of Sebastopol; was M.P. for Ayrshire, 1854-57 and 1859-68; unsuccessfully contested Sandwich, 1859, Frome, 1875, Greenock, 1878; was under sec. of state for India and the home depts. in Lord Derby's 3rd, and Mr. Disraeli's 1st admnstrs.; gov., S. Australia, 1868; and gov. of N. Zealand, 1873; resig., 1874; gov. of Bombay, Mar., 1880, to Mar., 1885; M.P. for Manchester, 1885 and 1886; under sec. of state foreign affairs 3rd Aug., 1886; postmr.-gen., 1891-92.
FERNANDEZ, HENRY.—Clerical asst. treas., Br. Guiana, 1887; 5th class clk., 1889; 4th class, 1891.

FERNANDO, MARCUS H.—Ed. Royal Coll., Colombo, and Univ. Coll., Lond.; Ceylon Govt. Univ. and Gilchrist scholar, 1883; B. Sc., 1886; M.B., 1888; gold medallist in med. and forens. med.; M.D., 1889; fellow of Univ. Coll., Lond., 1890; col. surg., civil med. dept., Ceylon; regiz. of Ceylon Med. Coll., lecturer on pathology, 1890; and analyst to govt. of Ceylon; reported epidemics of malarial fever, 1895; also in Bomba, 1897, on epidemics of plague; physician, gen. hosps., Colomba, 1897.
FERREIRA, P. J., C.M.G. (1880).—Was comdt. of local cavalry in the Transvaal against Sikukuni.
FFOLIOTT, LIEUT.-COL. O. HUMAN.—Served in Imp. army, 1888-81; mil. sec. col. forces, Cape, June, 1882.
FFRENCH-MULLEN, VINCENT, L.R.C.S.I.—Dist. med. offr., Port Maria, Jamaica, Apr., 1881.

FIDDES, GEORGE VANDELUR, C.B. (1901), B.A.—B. 1858; ed. at Dulwich Coll., and late scholar of Brasenose Coll., Oxford; 2nd class classical mods., 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., to be a clk. in the office of the sec. of state for the cols., 25th Mar., 1881; priv. sec. to Earl of Onslow, Feb., 1887; and to Baron H. de Worms (afterwards Lord Pirbright), Feb., 1888, to Aug., 1892; priv. sec. to Sir R. Meade, 11th Mar., 1896; 1st class clk., 1896; imperial sec. and acctnt. to Sir A. Milner, high comsnr. for S. Africa, Sept., 1897; politica. sec. to Lord Roberts at Pretoria, June, 1900; sec. to the Transvaal administration, Dec., 1900; returned to C. O., 1902, as prin. clk.

FIDDIAN, ALEXANDER.—Ed. Univ. Coll., Cardiff, and Oxford; schlr. Pembroke Coll., 1885; 1st class classical honours, 1896; Goldsmiths co. exhib., 1895; 1st class lit. hum., 1897; B.A., 1897; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd class clk., C.O., Oct. 12th, 1897.

FIELDING, HON. WILLIAM STEVENS.—Mem. for Halifax, Nova Scotia assem., 1882; declined premiership, but entered govt. without office in that year, and continued in that position till May, 1884, when he resig.; premier, provincial sec., and treas., Aug., 1884; min. of finance in Sir W. Laurier's admnstr., June, 1896.

FINNEMORE, ROBERT ISAAC, F.R.G.S., F.Z.S., F.S.S., F.S.S.E., F.R.C.I., Hon. Corres. Mem. Vic. Inst.—Entered civ. ser., Natal, 1858; 2nd clk. survr.-gen.'s dept., Mar., 1859; govt. land survr., 1863; ch. clk. and draughtsman, 1864; transf. to the atty.-gen.'s dept., Dec., 1865; admitted to the bar, Jan., 1868; postmr.-gen., 1876; R.M., city div., 1877 to 1878; ag. col. treas., 1877; has acted also as survr.-gen.; edited Natal Almanac and Register, 1876-78; digest of decisions of sup. ct. author Natal Law Reports from 1879, &c.; master and regizr. sup. ct., and regizr. vice-admiralty ct., 1878; R.M. and admnr. of native law, Durban, Mar., 1881; J.P., 1881; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., 1883; collr. of customs, regizr. of shipping emigr. offr. and harbour comsnr., 1889; dep. chmn. Natal harbour bd.; crown solr. and part. draughtsman, 1894; mem. of civ. ser. bd.: F.R.A.S., roy. meteorog. soc., roy. hist. soc., mem. soc. arts, and various other Br. and foreign scientific societies; puisne judge, sup. ct., Natal, 1890; senior puisne judge, 1902; has acted as ch. just.

FIRMINGER, R. E.—Clk. in the House of Commons, 1874 to 1880; lieut., Roy. E. Middlesex Militia, 1877; asst. inspr. G. Coast constab., Apr., 1880; dist. comsnr., Axim, 1880-1; dita Quittah, 1882-3; inspr., Dec., 1882; 2nd comm. Assinee bindry. comsnr., 1883; on special miss. to Bey and Bagidah, 1884, and to Ashanti, 1886; supt. Colombo convict establt., Ceylon, 1889; inspr.-gen. of prisons on sev. occasions, 1892-189

FIRMSTONE, H. W.—Ed. at Rugby and Hertford Coll., Oxon (scholar); cadet, S. Sttlmts., 1890; passed final exam. in Chinese, Dec., 1893; ag. dist. offr., South Malacca, May, 1894; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Jan., 1896; dist. offr. P.W., Oct., 1896; asst. protec. Chinese, Singapore, Feb., 1897; ag. asst. protec. Chinese, superint. Indian immigr., and mun. comsrr. for Penang, Aug., 1898, to Feb., 1902, and from Aug., 1902; ag. prot. of Chinese, S.S., Feb. to July, 1902; ag. asst. ditto, Penang, Aug., 1902, to Feb., 1903, and from Aug., 1903.

FISHER, HON. SIDNEY ARTHUR.—B.A. of Trin. Coll., Camb. He first contested Brome unsuccessfully in 1880. In 1887 he was elected, but was defeated in 1891. Re-elected, in 1896, and became min. of agricul. in Sir W. Laurier's govt., June, 1896.

FISHER, STANLEY.—Ed. at Westminster and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; M.A., 1891; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple and Lincoln's Inn (called 1890); apptd. pres. of dist. ct. of Kyrena, Cyprus, May, 1902; acted as King's advocate, May to Oct., 1902.

FISHER, WILLIAM WOODHOUSE.—Called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn Nov., 1877; ag. crown counsel, N. circuit, Ceylon, Jan., 1885, to Feb., 1886; ag. dist. judge, Matara, June, 1886, to Feb., 1887; ag. crown counsel, Kandy, Feb., 1887; crown counsel, N.W. prov., Ceylon, Mar., 1887; sec. to comttee for drafting code of civ. procedure, June, 1887, to May, 1888; additional crown counsel, N. circuit, Jan., 1890; pres. dist. ct., Kyrena, Cyprus, 1891; ag. puisne judge, 1894; mag. Jamaica, 1895.
FITZGERALD, THE HON. G. P.—Mem. of cabinet, Tasmania, without portfolio, 1888-92.

FITZPATRICK, HON. CHARLES.—Born in Quebec, 1863; Dufferin medallist, law faculty, Laval Univ., 1876; one of the counsel for the defence of Louis Riel at Regina in 1885; first returned to the Quebec legis. assem. at the gen. election of 1894, and again in 1896; solr.-gen. in Sir W. Laurier's govt., June, 1896.

FLEISCHER, THEODORE T. C.—Out-door offr. of customs and treas. dept., 1st Aug., 1878; 4th clk., customs and treas. dept., 25th Oct., 1878; 3rd clk., 22nd June, 1879; 2nd clk., 16th May, 1882; apptd. 1st class clk. in the treas. dept. in 1886, on separation of the customs from treas.; ag. chief clk. and book-keeper, treas., 9th March, 1899, to 10th Jan., 1900; ag. ch. clk. and cashier, 5th June, 1900, to 31st May, 1901.

FLEMING, SIR FRANCIS, K.C.M.G. (1892), C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1842, called to the bar, Middle Tem., Nov., 1866; crown solr. for Mauritius, 1869; dist. and stip. mag., 1872; acted as dist. judge, Seychelles, 1874; dist. judge, Jamaica, 1876; atty.-gen., Barbados, 1878; acted as ch. just., Sept., 1878, to Mar., 1879; acted as ch. just., St. Lucia, July, 1879; priv. sec. to Sir G. C. Strahan, administering the govt. of the Cape, in 1880; puisne judge, Br. Guiana, 1881; acted as atty.-gen., Aug. to Nov., 1882; Queen's advoc., Ceylon, 1883; atty.-gen., 1884; acted as ch. just., Mar. to Dec., 1885; col. sec., Natal, July, 1886, but did not take up apptm., being apptd. col. sec. of Mauritius, Dec., 1886; adminstrd. govt., July, 1887, to Dec., 1888; col. sec. Hong Kong, 1889; adminstrd. the govt., Feb. to Dec., 1890; gov't. S. Leone, 1892; ditto, Leeward Is., 1895; ret. 1901.

FLEMING, SIR SANDFORD, K.C.M.G. (1897) C.E., C.M.G. (1877), LL.D. (St. Andrews), 1884—B. 1827; engnr.-in-ch., Northern Railway, Canada, 1856 to 1863; deleg. to Canadian and Br. govt. from Red River settlers, 1863, in reference to Pacific Rly.; nominated by provs. of Canada, N. Brunswick and N. Scotia; govt. engnr.-in-ch. inter-col. rly., 1863; engnr.-in-ch., Canadian Pacific rly., 1871 to 1880; elected chancellor, Queen's Univ., Canada, 1880; re-elected 1883, and again 1886; deleg. from Canadian inst. and Amer. meteorological soc., N. York, to internat. geograph. congress at Venice, 1881; representing the Dominion of Canada at internat. prime meridian confce., Washington, 1884; dir. Hudson Bay Co., Canadian Pacific rly., etc.; author of "The Intercol., an Historical Sketch," 1876, "Old to New Westminster," 1884, memoirs on "A Prime Meridian for all Nations," "Universal Time," and various other wks.; deleg. to col. confce., 1887; representative of Canada, col. confce., Ottawa, 1894; on special mission to Hawaii, 1894, with Mr. Mercer of the C.O., negotiating for landing place for Pacific cable.
FLETCHER, A. G. M.—Ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Trin. Coll., Oxford; cadet, Hong Kong, 1901; ag. asst. registr. gen., 1903-4.
FLETCHER, GEO. WM.—Temporarily employed in Imp. commissariat, Hobart, 1861; clk., col. treasy., Hobart, July, 1862; receiver and paymr., Jan., 1886.

FLETCHER, WM., D.S.O., Surgeon, capt. militia med. staff corps; 6 mos. in 1898 attached to regulars at R.A.M.C. depot, Aldershot; med. offr., Niger Coast Prot. (now So. Nigeria), 1898; served with Ashanti expdn., 1900 (medal and clasp); Aro expdn., 1901-2 (medal and clasp).

FLYNN, EDMUND JAMES, K.C., LL.D.—Born 1847, at Percé, Gaspe; ed. Quebec Seminary and Laval Univ. (L.L.L. with honours, 1873), LL.D. (Lav., 1878); Q.C. 1887; called to the bar 1873; prof. of Roman law, Laval Univ., since 1874; mem. legis. assem., Quebec, Apr., 1878; mem. exec. coun. and comsrr. crown lands in Chapleau govt., Oct., 1879, to July, 1882; comsrr. of rlys. in Ross govt., Feb., 1884, and solr.-gen., May, 1885.

FORD, HENRY FRANCIS.—Entered G.P.O., Cape, 1868; promoted to deeds office, 1872; apptd. to inaugurate deeds office at Kokstad, and served as clk. to ch. mag. Transkei; in charge of deeds office, King William's Town, 1884; ch. clk., insolvency branch, master's office, Capetown, Jan., 1888; registr. and mstr. high ct., Griqualand, July, 1890.

FORD, JOSEPH CHARLES.—Ed. in France (Académie de Douai), and by private tuition by Dr. Langley at Wolverhampton; sec. inst. of Jamaica internat. exhib., 1891, and spec. comsrr. for Bahamas at that exhib.; apptd. superdt. of govt. printing off., Feb., 1891; co-ed. and compiler of "Handbook of Jamaica."

FORD, SIR THEODORE THOMAS, KT. BACH. (1888).—B. 1829; called to the bar, Middle Tem., 1866; puisne judge, S. Sttlmts., Mar., 1874; ag. judge of Penang, 1874, to Apr., 1876; resign. and returned to England; re-apptd. senior puisne judge, 1876; ch. justice, 1886; ret., 1889.

FORDE, ROBERT MICHAEL.—Asst. col. surg., Gold Coast, Nov., 1891; ag. dist. comsrr., Axim, 1892; on special service on Anglo-French bndly. comsrr., Feb. to July, 1892; med. offr. special mission to Kumasi, Dec. 1894, to Jan., 1895; col. surg., Gambia, Feb., 1895.

FOREMAN, JOHN.—Mont., France, L.K.Q.C.P.I. and L.M.; med. offr., Virgin Is., 1878; ag. mag., Virgin Is., 1880; M.L.C., 1880, and mem. ex. coun., 1885; ag. med. offr., Dominica, 1882-1884; med. offr., Dieppe Bay, St. Kitts, 1889-1893; med. supt. leper asylum and Pogson hoosp., and med. offr., dist. 5, Sandy Point, St. Kitts, 1893; surg.-capt., St. Kitts-Nevis def. force.

FORGET, AMÉDÉE EMANUEL.—Ed. Marieville Coll., Canada; called to the bar, 1871; served as sec. to council of the bar, Quebec; clk. of coun. and priv. sec. to lieut.-gov., N.W. Territ., 1876; asst. comsnr. of Indian affairs for Manitoba and N.W.T., 1888; mem. of coun. of pub. instr., 1893; Indian comsnr., 1895; lieut.-gov., N.W.T., 11th Oct., 1898.

FORREST, THE RIGHT HON. SIR JOHN, P.C., G.C.M.G. (1901), K.C.M.G. (1891), LL.D. (Camb.), F.R.G.S., F.G.S., F.L.S., C.M.G. (1882).—B. 1847; Hon. Fellow of the Italian geog. soc.; Hon. Fellow of the imp. geog. societies of Vienna and St. Petersburg; knight of the Italian crown; entered the survey dept. of W. Australia, 1845; commanded exploring expdtns. in 1869 into the interior in search of the remains of Dr. Leichhardt; in 1870 from Perth to Adelaide along the S. Coast; and in 1874 from Champion Bay, on the W. Coast, to the overland telegraph between Adelaide and Port Darwin, a journey of nearly 2,000 miles. For these services received thanks of gov. and legis. coun., and gold medal of the Roy. Geog. Socy. of London, 22nd May, 1876, and a grant in fee of 5,000 acres of crown land; dep. survr.-gen. of W. Australia, 1876; in 1878 and 1882 conducted the trigonometrical surveys of the Nichol Bay district, and the Gascoyne and Lyons District, in N.W. Australia, 1878; acted as comsnr. of crown lands and survr.-gen.; comsnr. of crown lands and survr.-gen. of W. Australia, 1883 to Dec., 1890, during which time was mem. of the exec. and legis. couns.; in Mar., 1883, reported on the Kimberley dist.; in Dec., 1890, was sent for to form first ministry under responsible govt., in which he took the position of premier and treas., which he continuously held for over 10 yrs., resigning on 13th Feb., 1902, to join the 1st Commonwealth Govt. of Australia; was one of the representatives of W. Australia at the National Australian federation convention, held at Sydney, 1891; author of "Explorations in Australia," 1865, "Notes on Western Australia," 1883, 1884, and 1885; deleg. to col. confce., 1887; represented W. Australia at the federal convention at Adelaide, Mar., 1897, and in June of the same year was present as rep. of the col. in London at H.M.'s Diam. Jub.; again rep. W. Australia at fed. convention, Sydney, 1897; Melbourne, 1898; joined 1st Commonwealth ministry as postmaster-general, Jan., 1901; defence minister, 1901, which he only held a few days, and then took the portfolio of defence.

FORTH, J. OGILVIE.—Audit clk., Fiji, Aug., 1883; accntnt. native taxes and clk. nat. native accts., June, 1889; ch. clk. audit office, Oct., 1890; col. auditor, Nov., 1891; Oct., 1890, proceeded to Tonga to audit accts. of Tongan govt., for which he received thanks of King George Tubon's cabinet.

FOSBERY, WIDENHAM FRANCIS WIDENHAM.—Cons. agt., Niger Coast Prot., 1893; ag. vice-coms., 1894-5; dist. comsnr., 1896; ag. div. comsnr., 1897-8; pol. offr., central division expdtn., 1898 (severely wounded); res., Benin City, 1898; pol. offr., Benin Terr. expdtn., 1899 (medal with clasp); ag. div. comsnr., 1900; pol. offr., Ishan expdtn., 1901 (medal with clasp); div. comsnr., 1902; pol. offr., Asaba Hint expdtn., 1902; senior div. comsnr., 1903; pol. offr., Igarra expdtn., 1903; ag. high comsnr., 1903-4; ag. sec. to adminstr., 1904.

FOSTER, E. ALEXANDER.—I.S.O. (1903); at Kingswood Coll., Bath and Trin. Coll., Dublin; clk., audit office, Jamaica, Mar., 1866; 2nd clk., May, 1868; ch. clk., island med. office, Jun., 1882; sec. of cent. bds. of health and quarantine auditor-gen., Leeward Is., Apr., 1897; mem. gen. legis. coun., fed. exec. coun. and exec. an legis. couns., Antigua; J.P., ag. col. sec., Lewis Ward Is., Sept., 1897, Sept. to Oct., 1899, Nov., 1899, to Apr., 1900, and Aug. to Oct., 1901; admnstr., Antigua, in Jan., 1898, Dec., 1899, and Jan., Feb., Mar., 1900.

FOSTER, THE HON. GEO. E., B.A., D.C.L.—Ed. common and superior schla., King's Count.; New Bruns.; Douglas gold medallist, Univ. N.B., 1866; B.A., 1868; medallist, ancient rhetoric, Edin., 1873; prof. classics, Univ. N.B., 1873-9; returned to parlmt., 1882; min. of marine and fisheries for Dominica, Dec., 1885; min. of finance, 1888 to 1896; represented Canada at col. confce., Ottawa, 1894.
FOSTER, RICHARD WITTT, M.L.A., S. Aust., 1893.—Comsnr. of pub. wks., 1899; ditto, and min. of industry, 1902-1904.
FOSTON, EDMUND CHRISTOPHER.—L.R.C.P. (Edin.), L.F.P.S. (Glas.); col. surg., prov. Wellesley (South), S. Stltms., 21st Aug., 1891; supt. S.S. emigtn. depot, Negapatam, 17th Nov., 1900.

FOWLER, FRANK.—Asst. clk., pub. works dept., Brit. Guiana, Feb., 1878; 3rd clk., Jan., 1881; 2nd clk., Apr., 1884; 1st clk. and draftsman, govt. id. dept., Nov., 1884; 3rd govt. survr., May, 1886; senr. govt. survr., Apr., 1896; apptd. J.P., Nov., 1900; asst. comsnr. of lands and mines, 1st Apr., 1903; has held numerous acting appointments.

FOWLER, GEO. MERRICK.—Ed. Chelt. Coll.; writer, Ceylon service Jan., 1874; pol. mag., Galagedara, Sept., 1876; landing survr., Galle, Feb., 1878; pol. mag., Navalapitiya, Jan., 1880; asst. collr. customs, Galle, June, 1880; dist. judge, Matara, May, 1886; asst. govt. agt., Matara, 1887; ditto, Kalutara, 1888; ditto, N'Elia, 1891; ditto, Trincomalee, 1895; apptnt. confirmed, Dec., 1895; ag. dist. judge, Kalutara, May, 1897; Negombo, Oct., 1897; ag. prin. asst. col. sec., July, 1897; govt. agt., Ratnapura, June, 1898; ag. govt. agt., W. prov., June, 1899; govt. agt., S. prov., Feb., 1900; govt. agt., and fiscal, W. prov., Mar., 1902.

FOX, HENRY WILSON, B.A.—Ed. Charterhouse, Marlborough Coll., Univ. Coll., Lond., Trin. Coll. Cam.; exhibtr., Trin. Coll., 1882; foundation scholar, 1883; natural sci. trip.; exhibtr., Lincoln's Inn, 1888; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1888; priv. sec. to Sir C. Mills, K.C.M.G., 1887 to 1889; admitted to practise in sup. ct., Cape Colony, and high ct., So. Rhodesia, 1894; pub. prosecutor, So. Rhodesia, 1894-1897; manager B.S.A. Co., head office, London, 1898; served during 1896-97 in the Matabeleland and Mashona land rebellion (medal and clasp and ment. indespr.).
FOX, WALTER.—Asst. supt., botanic gardens, Singapore, 16th June, 1879; supt., forests and gardens, Penang, 7th Dec., 1903.

FOXON, CUTHBERT COLENSO.—Clk. and Zulu interp. to asst. comsnr. and res. mag. Ndwandw dist., Zululand, Sept., 1887; transf. to Katongani dist., Apr., 1891; to Lower Umfolozi dist., Feb., 1892; ag. res. mag. there Mar., 1892; ag. sub-inspr. Z'land. pol. for six months, May, 1892, and Apr., 1893; transf. as clk. and Zulu interp. to Nkandhla dist. Nov., 1892; sub-inspr. Z'land. pol., Apr., 1893; commanded detachments Z'land pol. with the special comsn. annexation of Sambanans and adjacent territories, May, 1895; Br. res. Amatongaland, Br. Protectorate, June, 1896; deputy comsnr., Sept., 1896; res. mag., Z'land, Dec., 1897; mag., Ukandhla, 1901; J.P. for colony, 1904.

FOXON, FRANK ERNEST.—3rd class clk. civ. serv., Natal, and 2nd clk. and Zulu interp., Umgeli div., Nov., 1890; ag. clk. of ct., regis. circuit ct., and sub-distributor of stamps, Wenen country, 1886; clk. of ct., dep.-clk. of peace, and sub-distributor of stamps, UpperUmkomang div., May, 1887; admstr. native law, Impendlele, Mar., 1889; ditto, Inanda Location, Jan., 1890; ag. mag., Lower Tugela div., Apr. to June, 1890; ag. R.M. at Verulam and Stanger, 1890 to 1894; R.M. for col., and mag., Ndwedwe div., July, 1894; J.P. for col., 1893; served at commencement of Zulu war, 1879, in imp. transport dept., afterwards as lieut. Natal N. pioneers (medal and clasp); served as levy leader in command of Sibepu's contingent and Yamela's mounted men against Dinuzulu in Zululand, 1888; lieut., Natal carbineers, Apr., 1889, capt., Dec., 1897; mag. Ixopo div., 1897; served through the Boer war, 1899-1900, including the siege of Ladysmith.
FRANCE, HENRY DUNLOP.—Rev. offr., Anguilla, 1896; govt. offr., St. Kitts, 1897; rev. offr., Nevis, 1899; tariff clk., treasy., St. Kitts, 1901; ag. audit clk., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1902.

FRANCIS, COL. CLAUDE AUGUSTUS.—Ed. Craven Coll. and Vienna; entd. Impl. Yeo, 1875; C.M.R., 1876; S. African War (medal with clasp) 1877-8; inspr. constab., and armed pol. and J.P., Br. Guiana, 1880; higher grade, 1883; county inspr., 1891; acted as dep. inspr.-gen., 1892, and from Feb., 1895, to Apr., 1896; ag. inspr.-gen., May to Oct., 1896; supt. fire brig., 1895-6; mem. excise, quarantine, and health bds.; author of "A Manual for the Use of Police and Constabulary," "A Criminal Code," 1895, and "A Police Catchism," 1898; sheriff-comdt., armed constab., supt. civ. pol., and J.P., Fiji, 1897; marshal, V.-A. ct.; comndt., European vol. force, which he raised; local col., 1898; M.L.C., 1905.
FRANCIS, CYRIL GERARD BROOKE.—Clk., G.P.O., Fiji, 1898; ditto, treasy., 1899; lieut., armed constab., 1901; in command detachment, Nadarivatu, 1902; ag. adjut., A.N.C., and inspr. pol., 1903.

FRANKLIN, WILLIAM.—Asst. regis., natives, Kimberley and De Beers, Mar., 1877; regis. deaths, Kimberley and De Beers, 1878; clk. to civil comsnr. and res. mag., Barkly West, Jan., 1879; issuer of passes, Mar., 1879; inspr. of claims, Barkly West, June, 1879; inspr. of Sivonelli's diggings, May-June, 1882; from Oct., 1882, to Aug., 1883, off. valuator of all diamonds exported from the dist. of Barkly West; apptl. regis. of claims, Barkly West, Sept., 1883; inspr. and regis. mines and diggings, Barkly West.

FRASER, F. BLACHE.—2nd clk. atty.-gen.'s office, Trinidad, Jan., 1865; promoted to col. sec.'s dept. as 3rd clk., Sept., 1867; 3rd landing waiter, customs dept., Jan., 1875; principal landing waiter, Jan., 1883.
FRASER, J. J., K.C.—Atty.-gen. and premier, N. Brunswick; ret., 1882; puisne judge, sup. ct., 1883.
FRASER, J. O.—Survr.-gen., Newfoundland, Dec., 1882; postmr.-gen., 1885.

FRASER, MALCOLM A. C.—Ed. at King Edward's schl., Bromsgrove; probation clk. in col. sec.'s office, W. Australia, Apr., 1876; 2nd clk., land and survey dept., June, 1876; transf'd to col. sec.'s office, Aug., 1877; clk. and assist. priv. sec. to gov., July, 1878; also ast. clk. to the exec. comm.; clk. to gov., Sir W. C. F. Robinson, K.C.M.G., Apr., 1890; corrpdg. clk., col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1881; also meteorological reporter; registr.-gen., 1891; also registr. of patents, trade mks., etc.; represented the govt. of W. Aust. at the conference of govt. statisticians apptd. to arrange for a uniform census of Australia, Sydney, Jan., 1900; supt. of census, Western Australia, 1900; govt. statistician, 1901; represented W.A. at conference of statisticians convened to arrange for uniformity in the collection and compilation of statistics throughout the Commonwealth of Aust.

FRASER, SIMON JAMES.—Barrister-at-law; a stip. mag. of Tobago and J.P., June, 1868; coroner for the Is., July, 1868; has acted on several occasions as atty.-gen. from 1868; changed to Windward magistracy, Apr., 1874; acted as inspr. of imigrts., 1875 and 1876; inspr. of schls., 1876 to 1884; ag. atty.-gen., Oct., 1883; comsnr. of roads, dist. No. 1, Dec., 1883; pol. mag., Leeward dist., Nov., 1884; chmn. Metayer coman., Dec., 1884; mem. bd. of educn. and chmn. bd. of health, Aug., 1885, and of quarantine bd., Sept., 1887; judge of petty debt court of Bridgetown, Barbados, Jan., 1894.

FRASER, MAJOR-GEN. SIR THOMAS, K.C.B. (1900), R.E., C.M.G. (1882).—B. 1840; apptd. C.M.G. for civ. services in connection with the settlement of the Transvaal question; served in expidry. force in Egypt, 1882, and in the Nile expidry. force, 1884, with brevet rank of col.; 4th class Osmanieh, and 3rd class Medjidie.

FREEER, GERALD DUDLEY, L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), 1888, M.B. (Lond.), 1898.—House surg. gen. hosp., Birmingham, 1890; col. surg. gen. hosp., Singapore, July, 1890; col. surg. Malacca, Feb., 1893; col. surg. res. Penang, Jan., 1897; ag. col. surg., Penang, June, 1900; col. surg., Singapore, 1st Mar., 1901.

FREMANTLE, ADMIRAL THE HON. EDMUND ROBERT, R.N., SIR, G.C.B., C.M.G. (1874).—B. 1836; apptd. to the Order of St. M. and St. G. for service during the Ashanti war, 1873-74, having been some time sen. naval offr. on the station.
FRENCH, C. W.—Clk. to the pol. mags., dist. D., St. Kitts, 1866.

FRENCH, MAJ.-GEN., SIR GEORGE ARTHUR, R.A., K.C.M.G. (1902), C.M.G. (1877).—B. 1841; ed. at Sandhurst and Woolwich; joined R.A. as lieut. in 1860; proceeded to North America in Dec., 1861, with expeditionary force sent out in consequence of the "Trent Affair"; adjt. R.A., Kingston, from 1862 to 1866; qualified as 1st class gunnery instructor in 1867; 1st class inspr. of warlike stores in 1868; apptl. I.S.W. at Quebec in 1869 on the withdrawal of the Imp. troops; inspr. of artillery, with rank of lieut.-col., in 1870; organised the permanent batteries of artillery in 1871; comsnr. N.W.M.P., and stip. mag. for the territories, in Dec., 1873; raised, organised, and equipped the force; commanded the expidn. sent from the Red River to the base of the Rocky Mountains in 1874; inspr. of warlike stores, Devonport, 1878 to 1883; comldt. Queensland forces, with rank of col., 1883-91; comldt., N. S. Wales forces, 1896.
FRENCH, SIR S. R., K.C.M.G. (1901), C.M.G., (1896).—B. 1849; sec. and accnttit., G.P.O., Cape, Aug., 1880; controller P.O. savings bk., 1884; postmr.-gen., 1892.
FRERE, Aubrey Temple.—Cadet, Sarawak service, Oct., 1886; asst. res., May, 1890; res., 2nd class, 4th div., Jan., 1894.

FRERE, Bartle Henry Temple, LL.B.—Ed. at Charterhouse school and Trin. Coll., Cam.; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1887; pres. dist. court, Cyprus, 1897; transf. to Gibraltar as pol. mag. and coroner, 1902.

FRETTZ, W. H.—L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S., Edin.; med. offr., dist. 2, Nevis, Feb., 1882; dist. 3, S. Kitts, May, 1886; dist. 1, S. Kitts, Aug., 1896; official mem. leg. coun., S. Kitts-Nevis, Nov., 1896; offl. mem., leg. coun., St. K.-N., Nov., 1896, to Dec., 1900; health offr. and analyst of vital statistics, Feb., 1903; mem., Basseterre town board, 1903; offl. M.L.C., 1903.
FROST, The Hon. Sir John, K.C.M.G. (1904); C.M.G. (1879).—B. 1828; M.L.A., C. of G., field comm. of Cape vols., late of C.M.R.; sec. for agricult., Cape Col., 1893-6; ditto, 1902.

FROUDE, Ashley A., B.A., C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1863; ed. Westminster and Oriel Coll., Oxon; priv. sec. (unpaid) to Sir Robert Herbert at the C.O., Nov., 1886; sec. to roy. comm. for the division of Malta into electoral dists., Jan., 1888; sec. to the Behring Sea comm., June, 1891, and to the joint Behring Sea comm. at Washington, Jan., 1892; on the staff of the Br. agt., Behring Sea arbitration, 1892-3.

FRY, R. S.—Trigonometrical asst., survey dept., S. Sttlntz., Jan., 1881; res. Aug., 1884; rejoined July, 1885; dist. survr., Sept., 1885; ch. survr., Dec., 1888; observer for time balls and dep. registr. of shipping, Jan., 1894.

FRY, Wm. Herbert, L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.).—House surgeon, S. Sttlntz., 13th Aug., 1896; supernumerary col. surg., Sing., 1st Apr., 1900; col. surg., prov. Wellesley, South, 17th Nov., 1900; ditto, prov. Wellesley, North, 1st Mar., 1901.
FULLARTON, R.—Harbourmr., Melbourne, 1877; ch. harbourmr., pres. pilot bd., and chmn. steam navig. bd., 1882; capt. comding. Victorian naval brigade, 1871; mem. of coun. of defence, 1884.
FULLER, F. C.—Cadet, Fiji, 1884; res. comsnr., Rotumah, 1889; dis. comsnr., Lagos, 1892; res. of Ibadan, 1897; col. treas., Lagos, 1901; asst. sec. to govt., Malta, 1902; mem. exec. coun.

FULLER, Sir Thos. Ekins, K.C.M.G. (1904); C.M.G. (1903).—Emigtn. agt. in Lond. for Cape of Good Hope, 1872; resigned, 1876; served as mem. of house of ass. for 23 years; agent-gen. in Lond. for Cape of Good Hope, 1st Jan., 1902; J.P., Cape Town; has served on various councils and comtees. in Cape Colony.
FURLONG, L. O'B.—M.H.A., St. John's, E. Newfild., 1893; mem. exec. coun. and chmn. bd. of wks., Apr. to Dec., 1894; M.H.A., St. John's, E., 1897.

FYSH, The Hon. Sir Philip Oakley, K.C.M.G., (1896).—B. 1835; treas. in Mr. Kennersley's ministry, Tasmania, 1873-5, and without portfolio to July, 1876; premier without office, Aug., 1877, to Mar., 1878, and member without office of Mr. Giblin's 1st administration; maj. comdg. Tasmanian volr. rifle regt., 1880-4; again premier, Mar., 1887, to Aug., 1892; treas. in the Braddon Ministry, 1894-9; agt.-gen. for the col. in London, 1890 to 1901.
GABRIEL, J. S., M.R.C.S.E.—Med. offr., Antigua, dist. 6, 1881.

GAGNON, Charles Antoine Ernest.—Ed. St. Ann's Coll.; notary public, 1869; since 1885 pres. of Quebec board of notaries; elected to Quebec legislature, 1878-81, and since 1883; prov. sec., 1887; sheriff of Quebec, 1890.

GALE, Charles Henry.—Assoc. M.L.C.E.; articled pupil, 1882-5; asst. and resident engr., 1889; ch. draughtsman P.W. dept., Hong Kong, Sept., 1890; asst. engrn., Jan., 1900, and ag. exec. engrn., May, 1900; exec. engrn., Feb., 1901; J.P., 1904.

GALE, Walter A.—Ed. high schl., Perth, St. Peter's Coll., Adelaide, and Exeter Coll., Oxon; 3rd master, high schl., Perth, 1885; asst. regis. coll. sec.'s office, W. Australia, 1886; sec. to cent. bd. of educn., 1888; regis.-gen.; regis. of patents, etc., Sept., 1890; in charge of census for 1891; clk. of legis. assem., 1891; transf. to Commonwealth govt., 1901.

GALGEY, Otho, M.R.C.P.I. and L.M.—Med. offr., No. 5 dist., St. Vincent, Feb., 1872; asst. col. surg., Castrics, St. Lucia, Nov., 1878; acted in 1888, 1893, 1894, 1897, and 1900 as col. surg., St. Lucia; J.P. and mem. quarantine bd.; M.O. in charge royal gaol, Jan., 1889; and of drug store, Nov., 1890; ag. mem. of legis. coun., 1900.

GALLWEY, Lt.-Col. Henry Lioncl, C.M.G. (1889), D.S.O. (1896).—B. 1850; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Sandhurst; entered army, May, 1878; promoted lieut., Mar., 1881; capt., Oct., 1887; brevet-maj., May, 1897; maj., Mar., 1899; and lt.-col., Apr., 1901; apptd. dep. commr. and vice-consul, Oil Rivers prot., Mar., 1891; dep. commr. and consul, Niger Coast prot., Jan., 1897; and divn. commr. Niger Coast prot. (now South. Nigeria). Apr., 1899; ag. consul-gen. Niger Coast prot., Aug. to Nov., 1896; Jan., 1897; Feb., 1898, to Jan., 1899; ag. high commr., South. Nigeria, Mar. to Dec., 1900; Brass expdn., 1895 (despa., medal with clasp, D.S.O.); Benin expdn., 1897 (despa., clasp, brevet majority); Aro expdn. as chief political officer, 1901-2 (despa.); gov., St. Helena, 1902.

GALLWEY, Sir Michael Henry, K.C.M.G. (1888), C.M.G. (1883).—B. 1826; ed. Trin. Coll. Dub.; B.A.; barrister, King's Inns, Dub., Trin. term, 1853; Munster circuit; atty.-gen., Natal, 1857; mem. exec. and legis. couns.; pres. Transvaal and Zululand bndry. comm., 1878; received the thanks of the sec. of state; ch. juat., Natal, 1890; ret. 1902.

GALLWEY, Lieut.-Gen. Sir Thomas Lionel J., R.E., K.C.M.G. (1889).—B. 1821; 2nd lieut. R.E., Mar., 1839; served in the West Indies from Jan., 1842, to June, 1845; employed in Ireland during the famine, 1846-47, as inspecting officer, under the bd. of works; served in Canada, 1849-58; employed 1858-62 in the construction of fortified position, Gosport; mem. of the ordnance select comte., 1862-65; mem. of special mil. comm. to the United States during the war of 1864; employed as C.R.E. in building wks. of defence at Quebec, 1865-68; comdt., schl. of mil. engineering at Chatham, 1868-75; comdg. roy. engnr., and col. on staff, Gibraltar, 1877-79; inspr.-gen. of fortifications, 1880-82; gov. and comdr.-in-ch. of Bermuda, 1882-88.

GARDINER, John.—Entd. govt. ser., S. Austral., in ch. sec.'s off., 1866; treasy., 1871; clk., engrn.-in-chief's dept., 1874; acctnt., 1876; ch. acctnt., 1888; sec. commr. P.W., and chmn. supply and tender bd., 1899; transf. to serv. of Commonwealth of Aust. as pub. serv. inspr., 1902.

GARDNER, Harry.—Major (ret.), Roy. Scots; served in Bechuanaland expdn., 1884, and Zululand, 1888; ag. col. sec. and rec.-gen., St. Helena, 1886-7; ag. treas., Zululand, 1890; confirmed 1891; acctng. offr. for prov. of Zululand, 1897; inspr., audit dept., 1899.

GARLAND, PATRICK JOSEPH.—Apptd. a sen. med. offr., Northern Territories, 1899; served on Fra Fra expdn., under Captain Donald Stewart, June and July, 1899; specially mentioned in despatches; served on Neutral Zone expdn., Feb., 1900; mentioned in despatches; served on Fra Fra expdn., Mar., 1900; served during Ashanti rebellion and siege of Kumasi; acted as P.M.O. to column during retirement of Sir F. M. Hodgson, governor; specially mentioned in despatches; apptd. dist. comsnr. of Axim Dist., 1901.

GARNEAU, PIERRE.—Merchant and mem. of bd. of trade, Quebec; late gov't. dir. of North Shore railway; mayor, 1870-73; M.L.A., Quebec, 1873-78 and 1881-86; mem. of prov. exec. coun. and ministry as comsnr. of agricult., pub. wks., and crown lands, 1874-78; M.L.C., Quebec, 1887, and again comsnr. of agricult., pub. wks., and crown lands to 1891.

GARRARD, C. G.—Ed. Haileybury; dep. regiar. sup. ct., Penang, Jan., 1892; ag. dep. regiar. sup. ct., Singapore, Feb. to Oct., 1893; ag. regiar. sup. ct., Penang, July, 1894; ag. dep. regiar. sup. ct., and mag., Malacca, Apr., 1895; also ag. collr., land rev., and offr. in charge of treas., Malacca, Aug., 1895; ag. dep. regiar. sup. ct., Penang, Mar., 1896; astt. regiar. and mag., Malacca, 1896.

GARRAWAY, DAVID G.—Entered the provost-marshal's office, Grenada, in Jan., 1867; ch. clk. col. sec.'s office, 1868; clk. of couns., 1868 to 1878; dep. sec., regiar., and clk. of the crown, and sec. bd. of educn., 1871 to 1878; comsnr. of census, 1871; ag. auditor, 1871 to 1872, and 1875; sec. to exec. comtee., 1875-6; ag. treas., 1876-7, 1880-1-2; and ex officio a mem. of the legislature; auditor, 1878; notary public, 1880; compiler of census, 1881; ag. pol. mag., St. Vincent, 1883-4; treas., St. Lucia, July, 1884; mem. ex. and leg. couns.; performed duties of col. sec. in conjunction with his own during visit of gov'. in-ch., 1889 and 1890; admnstrd. govt., part of Feb., 1899; comp. of custms.; Br. Guiana, Sept., 1900; mem. of excise bd.; cent. bd. of hith. and quar. bd., div. pub. offrs. guar. fund.

GARRAWAY, D. JULIAN.—Entd. pub. serv., St. Lucia, Mar., 1892, as supermry. clk., treas.; ag. 4th clk., Nov., 1892; clk., P.O., June, 1893; ag. clk. to admnstr.-in-chief, Windward Is., Aug., 1893; 2nd clk. regiar.'s off., June, 1895; ch. clk., Jan., 1898; ag. regiar., sheriff and adminstr.-gen., on 3 occasions, 1901-2-3; rev. offr., Grenada, June, 1903; ag. pol. mag., West Dist., Feb., 1904; additional coroner, West Dist., Mar., 1904; ag. pol. mag., North Dist., for spec. purpose of hearing appeals agst. town bd. assessmts. for 1904; astt. tax offr. in connectn. with hearing appeals agst. assessmts. for land and house taxes for 1904.
GARRAWAY, R.F.—Postmr., Dominica, June, 1881; sec. bd. of hith. and quar. bd., Sept., 1881; ag. dist. mag., dist. G., Apr., 1893.

GARRETT, MARK.—In F.A.M.P., Cape, July, 1876, to Nov., 1879; clk. to C.C. and R.M., King William's Town, Sept., 1880; clk. col. sec.'s office, Capetown, May, 1884; sec. civ. ser. comsn., Mar., 1886; prin. clk., atty.-gen.'s office, 1895; sub-comsnr., war losses compensation comsn., Mar. to Sep., 1903; census staff, col. sec.'s office, prin. clk., pol. branch, atty.-gen.'s office, Aug., 1904.

GARRICK, THE HON. SIR J. F., K.C., K.C.M.G. (1886), C.M.G. (1885).—B. 1836; sec. for public lands and mines, Queensland, 6th Feb., 1878; atty.-gen., Dec., 1878, to Jan., 1879; col. treas. and postmr.- gen., 13th Nov. to Dec., 1883, when he resig. the former office; aty.-gen. for that col. in London, June, 1884, to June, 1888; was deleg. to the col. confee., 1887; again atty.-gen., 1890-5.

GATT, C.—Supt. govt. printing office, Malta, Feb., 1882; was priv. sec. to Sir Richard Wood, H.M.'s diplomatic agt., and consul-gen., Tunis, Sept., 1868, to May, 1871; astt. sec., Gozo, 1892; rec.-gen., 1899; audr.-gen., 1902; mem. of ex. coun. and of coun. of govt., 1903.

GATT, L., C.M.G. (1901), C.E.—Entered the Malta ser., Mar., 1883, as land survr.; rly. inspr., 1884; astt. engrnr., waterwks., 1885; survr. P.W.D., 1888; manager and engrnr., Malta rly., 1895; ch. engrnr., waterwks., and elect. lighting dept., 1896; supt. of P.W., with a seat in exec. coun., and a seat in coun. of govt., 1897.

GATTY, SIR STEPHEN HERBERT, KT. BACH. (1904), K.C.—Scholar Winchester schl. and New Coll., Oxon; called to bar, Mid. Term., Nov., 1874; went the N.E. circuit; atty.-gen., Leeward Islands, June, 1883; ag. ch. justice and local comsnr., W. Ind. incumbered estates ct. for Antigua, June to Oct., 1884; chancellor of the diocese of Antigua, July, 1884; local comsnr. incumbered estates ct. for St. Kitts, Oct., 1884; atty.-gen., Trinidad, Dec., 1886; chancellor of diocese and admgt. advoc., 1887; chmn. roy. comsn. on franchise and electoral dista., 1888, and of royal comsn. on Metayor system in Tobago, 1890; puisne judge, S. Stlntna., 1892; chief just., Gibraltar, 1895.
GAVIN, J. C.—Sec. to treas., rec.-gen., and paymr.-gen., New Zealand, Mar., 1860; astt. comptroller and audr.-gen., 1890.

GEIKIE, R.—Ed. at Harrow and Cambridge; undergraduate scholarship, King's Coll., 1895; Winchester prizeman, 1896; 1st class hist. tripos, 1896; elected fellow King's Coll., 1900; apptd., after open compet. exam., 2nd class clk. Local Govt. Bd., Nov., 1897; 2nd class clk., C.O., Mar., 1899.
GELLIBRAND, HON. W. A. B.—Mem. legis. coun., Tasmania, since 1871; formerly pres. legis. coun.
GEMMEL, GEORGE.—Clk. treas. dept., Seychelles, Feb., 1887; clk. to admr., Feb., 1889; clk. of councils, Jan., 1897; 1st customs offr., May, 1900; ag. audr., Aug., 1903.

GENIS, MICHAEL CHRISTIAN.—Clk. to state atty., Transvaal, 1874; clk. to R.M., public prosecutor, and postmr., Christiana, 1876; C.C. and R.M., Christiana, 1880; ditto, Rustenburg, July, 1880; pensioned upon retrocession in 1881; regiar. of deeds, Stellaland, Sept., 1884; also treas. and distributor of stamps; clk. to land comsn., Br. Bechuana land, Nov., 1885; registry clk. and J.P., Vryburg, July, 1886.
GENNADIOUS, P.—Dir. of agricult., Cyprus, 1896.
GEORGE, WM. JAMES, J.P.—Ed. Midland Institute, Birmingham; M.L.A., W. Aust., 1895 to 1902; comsnr. of rlyws., July, 1902.

GIBBES, REGINALD PRESCOTT.—Cadet, S.S., Nov. 1889; passed final exam., Tamil, Jan., 1892; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, Aug. to Nov., 1892; ag. dist. offr., Nebong Tebal, P. W., Mar., 1894; passed exam. in Malay, 1894; 2nd astt. prot. of imigrts., Penang, May, 1896; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, Aug., 1897; transf'd. to Calcutta as (temp.) astt. emigtn. agt. for Trinidad, Mauritius, Fiji, Jamaica, &c., Aug., 1900; appt. confirmed, Aug., 1901; passed exam. in Hindustani, May, 1901; ag. emigtn. agt., 19th Feb. to 12th Oct., 1902; emigtn. agt., 14th Sep., 1903.
GIBBS, Lawrence.—A.M.I.C.E., grad. assoc., and county engrnr.; asst. engrnr. P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1890; ag. exec. engrnr., 1896.

GIBRALTAR, Lord Bishop of, Rt. Rev. Wm. Ed. Collins.—Late Exhib. of Selw. Coll., Camb.; B.A. (Jun. Opt.), 1887; Lightfoot schl., 1889; Prince Consort pri., 1890; M.A., 1891; B.D., 1902; D.D., 1903; F.C. of All Hallow's, Barking, Lond., 1890-1; lectr. of Selw. Coll., 1891-3; lectr. of St. John's Coll., Camb., 1891-4; exam. hist. trip., 1894-5; select pr., Camb., 1899-1902; prof. of eccles. hist., King's Coll., Lond., 1893-1904; C. of All Hallow's, Barking, 1894; exam. chap. to Bp. of St. Albans (Feating), 1897-1903; Bishop of Gibraltar, 25th Jan., 1904.

GIBSON, Fred. Allan, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1860; clk. Mauritius civ. ser., after exam., Oct., 1875; clk. despatch branch, Sept., 1878; ag. précis writer, Nov., 1882, to June, 1883; ag. ch. clk. and sec. to exec. and legis. couns., Mar., 1890; clk. exec. and legis. couns., Sept., 1892; ag. jun. astt. col. sec., Sept., 1892; ch. clk., Mar., 1894; ag. astt. col. sec., June, 1894, to Jan., 1895; Jun. to Dec., 1895, Mar. to Sept., 1896, Jan. to Nov., 1897; ag. regar. gen., Jan. to Sept., 1899; nom. mem. coun. of govt., Apl. to Sept., 1899; ag. astt. col. sec., July, 1900; astt. col. sec., Apr., 1901; collr. of cust., 9th Nov., 1903; ag. col. sec., 9th Nov., 1903, to 19th Aug., 1904; ditto, 19th Sep., 1904.

GIBSON, Jas. Young.—Clk. and Zulu interp. to ct. of R.M.; sub-distrib. of stamps, and regar. circuit ct., Newcastle, Natal, Apr., 1882; also dep. clk. of peace, Mar., 1887; astt. comsr. and R.M., Nqutu dist., Zululand, Feb., 1889; ditto, Ndwandwe dist., May, 1889; mag. for colony; mag., Umvoti div., 1900; J.P. for colony, 1902; ag. mast., sup. ct., 1904.

GILES, Mortimer.—Entd. survey dept., S. Australia, 1864; draughtsman, 1876; clk. and astt. draughtsman, lands titles off., 1876; draughtsman, 1889; draughtsman and 3rd dep. regar.-gen. of deeds, 1901; regar.-gen. of deeds and regar. of buildg. soca., 1904.
GILL, C. I.—Puise judge, super. ct., Montreal prov. of Quebec, 1879.
GILL, Thomas, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1849; entered col. service, S. Australia, Feb., 1865; accntnt., treas., Jan., 1883; under treas., 1894.
GILL, Walter, F.L.S., F.R.H.S.—Conservator of forests, S. Australia, July, 1890.

GILLIES, The Hon. Duncan.—Premier and treas. of Victoria, also min. of rly., and min. of mines; resig. Nov., 1890; pres. of the bd. of land and wks., and comsnr. of crown lands and surveys, May, 1868; held the office of comsnr. of rlys. and roads, June, 1872, to Aug., 1873; was comsnr. of crown lands, &c., Oct., 1873, to May, 1877; comsnr. of rlys., &c., Mar. to Aug., 1880; also Mar., 1883, to Feb., 1886, when he took office as premier and treas. on the retirement of Mr. Service; resig. 1891; was one of the representatives of Victoria at the Federation Convention, 1891; agt.-gen. for Victoria, 1894 to 1897.

GILMAN, E.W.E., B.A., Oxon.—Cadet, S.S., Nov., 1899; ag. 4th magis., Sing., Nov., 1901; passed final exam. in Tamil, June, 1902; ag. astt. supt. of Indian immigrants, Penang, Apl., 1902; confirmed, July, 1904.
GIROUARD, Desiré, K.C.—Ed. Montreal Coll.; called to the bar, Canada, 1860; K.C., 1876; represented Jacques Cartier co., 1878 to 1896; judge sup. ct., Canada, 1895.

GIROUARD, Lt.-Col. Sir Edward Percy Cranwill, K.C.M.G. (1900), D.S.O., R.E.—Served with Dongola expd., force, 1896 (desp., brevet-major, British medal, Khedive's medal, 2 clasps); Nile expd., 1897 (desp., clasp); rly. traff. men., Woolwich Arsenal, 1890-5; dir. of Soudan rlyws., 1898-8; pres. Egyptian rlyw. bd., 1898-9 (2nd cls. Medjidie); S. African war, 1899-1902; comsnr. of rlyws., Transvaal and O.R.C., 1902; mem. I.C.C.
GISBORNE, Hartley, A.M., Camb., C.E., M.A.I.E.E.—Ed. at Mercers' schl. and Windsor Coll., Canada; astt. geolog. survr., Canada, 1876; tel. engrnr. 1880; dist. tel. supt. Manitoba, 1882.

GLASGOW, 7th Earl of, the Right Hon. Sir David Boyle, G.C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1833; capt. (retired) R.N.; served in the Crimean and China wars, 1857 (medal and clasp); J.P. for Ayrshire, and chmn. of co. coun.; gov. of N.Z., 1892-97.

GLASIER, F. Bedford.—Ast. civ. engrnr., Bermuda harbr. wks., June, 1894; engrnr.-in-charge, Coatzacoalos harbr. wks., Mexico, May, 1896; dist. engrnr., S. Leone govt. rly., June, 1897; dist. engrnr., Tarkwa rly., Gold Coast, Oct., 1898; and gen. manager, S. Leone govt. rly., May, 1899; promoted gen. manager, Lagos govt. rly., Apr., 1900.

GLASS, David, K.C.—Ed. gram. schl., London (Ont.); called to the bar; Q.C. for Ontario, 1875; elected alderman in 1885; and mayor in 1858-64-65; mem. of commons for E. Middlesex, Canada, 1872; has been pol. mag., recorder, and also dep. judge of Middlesex, and bencher of law soc. Ont.; removed to Manitoba, 1882; solr. of Winnipeg, 1884; mem. legis. assem., Winnipeg, 1886; unanimously elected speaker 1887 to 1888.

GLYDE, Adolphus Yeovil.—Apptd. clk. in land titles dept., Perth, W. Australia, 1882; dep. regar. of titles, ag. regar. of deeds, sub. collr. of internal rev., astt. regar. of titles, 1893; ag. regar. of deeds, Nov., 1896, to Jan., 1897.
GOBEIL, Antoine, I.S.O. (1904)—Law clk. dept. of pub. wks., Canada, 1879 to 1885; then sec., dep. min. of pub. wks., Jan., 1891.

GODBOLT, Thomas.—Entd. N. S. Wales public serv., July, 1871, as jun. clk., govt. printing off.; astt. paymaster of imperial pensions, 1877; bd. of trade clk. and cashier, govt. shipping office, 1881; emergency offr., col. treasy., 1891; paymaster of imperial pensions and allowances, 1893; paymaster, N. S. Wales Imperial Bushmen's contingent (local pay and allowances), 1900, and chief paymaster (for local pay and allowances), N. S. Wales Naval Contingent to China, 1900.

GODFREY, Joseph Ed.—M.B. and C.M., Edin., 1882; gov. med. offr., B. Guiana, Feb., 1883; med. inspr. of estates hospitals, May, 1898; deputy regar. of births, deaths and marriages, 1898; deputy chmn., cent. bd. of health, 1902; acted on sev. occasions as surg.-gen., regar.-gen., and chmn. of cent. bd. of health; apptd. surg.-gen., regar.-gen. of h., d. and m., chmn. cent. bd. of health, and offl. mem. of ct. of policy, July, 1904.
GODFREY, Leopold Henry.—Active serv. volunteer in 1st batt., Royal Sussex regt., Feb., 1901, to May, 1902; architectural draughtsman, pub. wks. dept., O.R.C., June, 1902.

GOLDIE, Charles.—Apptd., after compet. exam., an examg. offr. H.M. customs, Greenock, Sept., 1863; inspr. of invoices and ch. landing survr. H.M. customs, Jamaica, Oct., 1869; one of the comsnrs. to inquire into the pilot service of Jamaica, Apr., 1870; ag. collr. of customs, Kingston, 1873, and 1875 to 1876; acted in 1874 as island treas., and as collr.-gen. of customs, 1875 and 1889; collr. of customs and shipping master, Kingston, 1883.

GOLDNEY, Sir John Tankerville, K.T. Bach. (1893).—B. 1846; ed. Harrow, and Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Inner Temp., 1869, N. circuit; atty.-gen., Leeward Is., Apr., 1880; ag. ch. just., May, 1881; puisne judge, Br. Guiana, June, 1883; judge sup. ct., S. Stltns., Mar., 1887; ch. just., Trinidad, 1892; retired, 1902.

GOLLAN, H. C.—Ed. at Charterhouse; M.A., Edin. (1887); called to the bar, Mid. Temp., Jan., 1891, N. circuit; priv. sec. to Col. Lugard, comsrr. and comdt., W.A.F.F., Sept. 1st, 1899; priv. sec. to H.C. of Nor. Nig., 1st Jan.-21st Sept., 1900; ag. sec. to the adminstr., 1st June-31st Aug., 1900; atty.-gen. of Nor. Nig., 21st Sept., 1900; ag. ch. just., 16th Nov., 1900-4th Apr., 1901; ch. just., 4th Nov., 1901; ch. just., Bermuda, 1904.

GOMPERTZ, H. H. J.—Ed. at Bedford schl. and Exeter Coll., Oxon (scholar); 2nd cl. lit. hum., B.A. (1890); cadet, S. Stltns., 1890; passed final exam. Chinese, Dec., 1893; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, Aug., 1894, to Dec., 1896; passed final exam. in Cantonese, 1895; actg. deputy-regist., sup. ct., Penang, Dec., 1896; passed final exam. in Hokkien, 1897; 2nd asst. prot. of Chinese, 1st Jan., 1897; transf. to Hong Kong as asst. registr.-gen., 1897; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, Easter, 1899; actg. sen. mag., Hong Kong, June, 1899, to 1900; asst. col. sec., Hong Kong, Jan. 1st, 1900; mem. land ct., Hong Kong, 1st June, 1900; pres. land ct., 10th Jan., 1902; ag. pol. mag., 1904-5.
GOODMAN, Clifford E.—Entered pub. library, Barbados, Sept., 1890; 5th clk., audr.-gen.'s office, Apr., 1891; ch. clk., insp.-gen.'s office, July, 1892; also regist. of births, dist. A.

GOODMAN, Gerald Aubrey.—Ed. at Lodge schl. and Harrison Coll., Barbados, and Univ. Coll., Lond.; 1st com. law schrl., Mid. Temp., Trinity term, 1885; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., June, 1889; ag. judge of petty debt ct., and of asst. ct. of appeal, Barbados, 1889; J.P., 1889; ag. solr.-gen. in 1890-1-2; M.H.A. since Oct., 1889; mem. bd. of educ. since Oct., 1891; solr.-gen., Mar., 1896; ag. atty.-gen., 1891, 1898, 1900-1-2-3-4; K.C. for Barbados, 1903; mem. of quar. bd. and gen. bd. of health, 1902.

GOODMAN, Sir William Meigh, K.T. Bach. (1902).—B. 1849; ed. at Univ. Coll.; graduated B.A. (honours) at the Univ. of Lond., 1867; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., Nov., 1870; went S.E. circ. and Surrey sessions; atty.-gen., Br. Honduras, 1883; ch. just., 1886; comsrr. to revise and consolidate the laws of the col., 1886; atty.-gen. Hong Kong, 1889; ag. col. sec., May, 1891, to Mar., 1892; ag. ch. just., Mar. to Aug., 1895, Jan. to May, 1896, and Apr., 1899, to Feb., 1900; K.C., 1900; ch. just., Hong Kong, 1st April, 1902.

GOODRIDGE, Hon. A. F.—Mem. of house of assem. for Ferryland, Newfoundland, 1880 to 1882, and 1882 to 1885; for Twillingate, 1885 to 1889; returned for same dist., 1893; mem. exec. coun., 1885 (without portfolio) to 1889, and again in 1894; after the resign. of the Whitney ministry in Apr., 1894, he was called upon to form a ministry, and became premier of the col., which office he held till Dec., 1894; was a mem. of the bd. of wks. and rly. comsrs. under the Thorburn admnstr. (1885-9); he is also a mem. of the Newfoundland chang. of com., and has been its pres. and vice-pres.
GOOLD, Albert Wm. Fisher.—Supernum. inspr. of pol., Mauritius, 19th Dec., 1881; inspr., 16th Sept., 1894; ch. offr., 1st Nov., 1898; inspr.-gen., 17th Aug., 1902.

GOOLD-ADAMS, Major Sir H. J., K.C.M.G. (1902), C.B. (1898), C.M.G. (1894).—B. 1858; Roy. Scots fus.; comdg. a troop Bechuanaland border police, Aug., 1885; comdt., 1888; major, 1889; again comdt., 1893-5; commanded field force against Matabele, 1893; res. comsrr. for Bechuanaland Protectorate; dep. admr., Orange River Col., Jan., 1901; lt.-gov., 7th Aug., 1901.
GORDON, Arthur Henry Wyndham.—Supt. of police, Mauritius, 28th Nov., 1889; ag. inspr.-gen., 2nd Aug., 1892; supt. of prisons and reformatory, 25th Oct., 1892.

GORDON, Arthur John Lewis, C.M.G. (1877).—B. 1847; priv. sec. to the gov. of Trinidad, 1866 to 1870; ag. col. sec., Trinidad, Apr., 1870; priv. sec. to gov. of Mauritius, 1870; priv. sec. to the gov. of Fiji, 1878-90; priv. sec. to gov.-gen. of Canada (Earl of Aberdeen), 1893.

GORDON, Brig.-Gen., J.M., C.B. (1901).—Lieut. R.A., from the R.M. Acad., Woolwich, 1875; lieut. staff instr. R.V. force, S. Australia, Dec., 1881; on the formation of a permanent artillery force in S. Australia, apptd. lieut.-in-comd., Sept., 1882; capt., Aug., 1883; maj., May, 1885; ag. D.A.A.G., Dec., 1885; and hon. A.D.C., May, 1886; comdt. S. Australia mil. forces, 1893; lieut.-col., 1892; col., 1895; brig.-gen., 1901; inspr. mil. stores for Australian cols., 1898-9; transf., with mil. def. dept., to serv. of Commonwealth of Aust., 1st Mar., 1901.
GORDON, James.—Admitted to S. Aust. bar, 1881; stip. mag., Adelaide, 1892; pol. mag., Adelaide, 1902; mem. of Adelaide licensing bench, 1892; chrmn. ditto, 1901; mem. central bd. of health, 1901.

GORDON, The Hon John Hannah, K.C.—Admitted to S. Aust. bar, 1876; M.L.C., 1888; min. of ed., 1889-90 and 1892; ch. sec., 1893-6; atty.-gen., 1899-1902; atty.-gen. and min. of ed., 1902-3; 3rd judge of sup. ct., 1903.

GORDON, William Montgomery.—Ed. Edin. Acad.; clk. to consul-gen. of W. Pacific, Aug., 1879; clk. of exec. coun. and ch. clk. of crown lands in col. of Fiji, May, 1880; also acted as sec. to lands comsrr.; res. comsrr. and stip. mag. for the island of Rotuma, May, 1882; comsrr. to inquire into claims of Europeans to land, Nov., 1882; stip. mag., Fiji, Apr., 1886; priv. sec. to gov., Ceylon, June, 1887; ch. clk. col. secy.'s off., Leewards, and clk. of fedn., exec. and legis. couns., and of the local legiu. coun. of Antigua, Apr., 1889; ag. comsrr., Montserrat, Dec., 1891, to Mar., 1892, Mar., 1894, to June, 1895, and July to Dec., 1899; ag. col. sec., Leeward Islds., Aug. to Nov., 1900; asst. col. sec., Trinidad, July, 1901; ag. col. sec., Trinidad, Aug.-Sep., 1903, Mar.-Aug., 1904.

GOTTLIBER, F. H. Valentine.—Entered govt. service, S. Stltns., Oct., 1880; ch. clk. G.P.O., Singapore, 1888; called to the bar, Mid. Term., 1892; dep. regisr. sup. ct., Singapore, Aug., 1895; ag. asst. regisr. sup. ct. and mag., Malacca, Mar., 1898; also ag. sherif, Sep., 1898, to Nov., 1899; asst. regisr., sup. ct., Penang, July, 1904.
GOUGE, HERBERT DILLON.—Assessor, land tax dept., S. Australia, Apl., 1885; pub. actuary, Mar., 1894.

GOUGH, FREDERICK.—Messenger in office of Woods and Forests, 12th Apl., 1869; apptd., after exam., meas. C.O., 20th Jan., 1871; Queen's mess., Mar., 1880; sent three times on foreign service; acted as Queen's page-in-waiting on col. premiers during diam. jub., 1897; recd. address of thanks, and jub. medal, 1897; res. off. keeper, C.O., Mar., 1898; apptd. by the S. of S. to be in attendance on col. premiers during their Coronation visit, 1902; (received joint letter of thanks, and Coronation medal).

GOULBURN, 1st BISHOP OF—RIGHT REV. MESAC THOMAS, D.D.—Ed. at Trin. Coll., Camb.; graduated B.A. 1840; M.A. 1843; ordained deacon, 1840; priest, 1841; vicar of Tuddenham St. Martin, Suffolk; sec. to the col. and continental church society. Goulburn formed part of the diocese of N. S. Wales until its separation in 1863.
GOULD, JULIAN BARING.—Cadet, Sarawak serv., Mar. 1897; asst. res., Nov., 1900; res., 2nd cls., Jan., 1902.

GOURDEAU DE BEAULIEU, MAJOR FRANZOS FREDERIC.—Priv. sec. to Hon. J. Cauchon; mem. of Mackenzie admtn., 1870; ch. clk. and acctnt. dept. of marine and fisheries; dep. min. of marine and fisheries of Canada, May, 1896.

GOWAN, JAMES ROBERT, LL.D., C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1815; lieut., 44th regt., North York, 1838; called to Canadian bar, 1839; judge of Simcoe, 1842-83; chairman of bd. of pub. instruction, 1844 to 1871; comnr. for consolidating statute law of Canada, 1858-59; chmn. of bd. of judges 1869-96; engaged in consolidation of the crim. law for Dominion, 1869; mem. of "The Law Reform Comn.", 1871; roy. comnr. to inquire into charges against the ministry in reference to the Pacific rly. contract, 1873; comnr. for consolidating the statutes of Ontario, 1876-77; apptd. to high ct. of just., 1882; a senator of the Dominion, 1886; chmn. of Barrie Collegiate Inst., by annual election, 1881-87; called to Irish bar, 1889.

GOWDEY, WM. WALLACE.—Solr., Barbados, 1881; mem. house of assem., 1883-93; mem. of comte. of inquiry into Dodd's reformatory and industrial schl., 1884; mem. bd. of health, 1884-92; J.P., 1885; mem. of financial inquiry coman., 1885; of general hosp. bd., 1889-92; pol. mag. and judge petty debt ct., Dist. E., Barbados, 1892; receiver of wrecks, Dist. E., July, 1897; pol. mag., Dist. A., Oct., 1901; visiting just., Lazaretto, Jan., 1902; receiver of wrecks, Dist. A., May, 1902; ag. judge, Bridgetown petty debt ct., May and June, 1902; ag. judge, asst. ct. of appeal, July, 1902, to Feb., 1903; comnr. of probates, Aug., 1902; judge, Bridgetown petty debt ct., Dec., 1902; ag. coroner, Dist. A., March to May, 1903; ag. judge, asst. ct. of appeal, April, 1903; ag. inspr. of prisons, May, 1903.

GRABHAM, MICHAEL, M.R.C.S. Eng., L.R.C.P. Lon., M.A., M.B., B.C., Camb., 1891.—Ed. St. John's Coll., Camb.; St Thomas's hosp., Lon.; B.A. Camb., 1887; 1st cl. honours nat. sciences, tripes and exhibitioner, St. John's Coll.; asst. med offr., city asylum, Birmingham, 1891; surg. to the lying-in hospital and general penitentiary, Kingston, Jamaica, 1892; late sec. med. council of Jamaica, 1897; mem. bld. of govern. Institute of Jamaica, 1902.

GRAHAM, FRED., C.B. (1899).—B. 1841; apptd. clk. in the office of the sec. of state for cols., after compet. exam., 30th May, 1870; 2nd class clk., 30th Sept., 1872; priv. sec. to Mr. Herbert, 1st Oct., 1872; to the Rt. Hon. M. E. Grant-Duff, M.P., 8th Jan., 1881, to 5th Aug., 1881; to Mr. L. H. Courtney, M.P., 6th Aug., 1881; to the Hon. Evelyn Ashley, 12th May, 1882; and to the Earl of Dunraven, 24th June, 1885; to Sir R. Herbert, 3rd Feb., 1886; and again to the Earl of Dunraven, 3rd Aug., 1886, to Feb., 1887; 1st class clk., Dec., 1887; ag. prin. clk., Feb., 1892; prin. clk., 11th Mar., 1896; asst. under sec. of state, 1st Mar., 1897.

GRAHAM, JOHN JAMES, C.M.G. (1899).—B. 1847; clk. to C.C. and R.M., Albany, 1864; clk. to regisr. E. dista. ct., May, 1865; asst. regisr., Jan., 1868; asst. regisr., sup. ct., Mar., 1872; regisr. and master, E. dista. ct., Feb., 1876; ch. clk. to atty.-gen. and clk. of peace, Capetown, Jan., 1878; sec., law dept., July, 1882; high sheriff and taxing offr., sup. ct., May, 1884; mem. of the col. tender bd.; a J.P. for the col.; sec. to law dept., 1889.
GRAINGER, HENRY ALLERDALE.—Mem. H. of Ass. S. Austr., 1884, 1890 and 1901; state agent in London for S. Austr., 1901; ag.-gen., 1902.

GRANDJEAN, REV. J. F. J.—Ed. at Bastogne and Floreffe (Belgium); student in theology at Paris, 1864 to 1870; prof. at Nice, 1870 to 1872; priest in 1871; missionary in France, 1872 to 1874; preacher at St. Martin's-le-Grand, London, 1874 to 1876; ag. curate of Grouville and St. Heliers (Jersey), 1876 to 1878; curate of Melton Mowbray, 1879; civil chaplain of the Seychelles, 1880; senior curate, St. Andrew and St. Philip, N. Kensington, 1891; returned to chaplaincy, Seychelles, July, 1892.
GRANGE, HERBERT CHARLES.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv., and assigned to bd. of educ., Aug., 1901; transfd. to C.O., Feb., 1902.
GRANNUM, ALLEN.—Asst. auditor of Sierra Leone and Gambia, Aug., 1897.

GRANNUM, CLIFTON.—Ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; 2nd clk. to gov. Windward Is., Sept., 1891; ditto, St. Vincent, Apr., 1892; supervisor of customs, G. Coast Col., Oct., 1893; asst. local audr., G. Coast and Lagos, May, 1895; local audr., S. Leone, June, 1896; J.P., S. Leone, 1897; ch. asst. treas., G. Coast., Apl., 1903; ag. treas. and mem. ex. and leg. couns., mem. bd. of educ., July, 1903-June, 1904.

GRANNUM, EDWARD ALLAN.—Passed Barbados, W. I., civ. ser. exam., 1886; 3rd clk. registrar's off., Jan. to Oct., 1886; 2nd clk., Oct., 1886, to Nov., 1887; clk. master in chancery off., Nov., 1887, to June, 1888; honours graduate, diploma Ontario Com. Coll., Canada, affiliated institute chartered accountants, Canada, Dec., 1889; practised public accountant, Barbados, W. I., to Aug., 1897; asst. auditor, Sierra Leone and Gambia, 25th Sept., 1897, to 15th Jan., 1901; acted auditor May to Nov., 1898 asst. auditor, Gold Coast and Lagos, Jan. to March, 1900; auditor, March, 1900; introduced system double entry accounts Gold Coast P.O., July, 1901. prepared scheme for introduction of double entry system of accounts for Accra town council, Aug., 1901.

GRANNUM, EDWARD T.—Representative of Bridgetown, Barbados Assem., 1883; J.P., 1886; mem. exec. comttee., 1886-89, and 1890-94; mem. gen. bd. of health, 1885; deleg. to Washington as to tariff arrangements under McKinley act, 1891; audr.-gen., Barbados, 1894; ag. col. sec. and mem. exec. coun., Aug. to Dec., 1895, and July to Nov., 1896; ag. col. sec., mem. of exec. coun., May to Sept., 1897.

GRANT, H. E. W.—Sec. to the educn. bd., Barbados, 1882; priv. sec. to Sir W. Robinson, 1883; and at Trinidad, 1885 to 1891; went on mission to Venezuela, 1887; sec. to special pub. wks. and road loan bd., Trinidad, 1890-1; priv. sec. to Sir W. B. Griffith, gov. of G. Coast, 1891-2; called to the bar, Inner Tem., Jan., 1896; res. just., Harbour Is., Bahamas, Apr., 1897; ag. ch. clk. col. sec.'s office, and ag. clk. of councils, Br. Honduras, Nov., 1899; apptd. confirmed, 1901; ag.-atty.-gen., B. Hond., Mar., 1902, to Oct., 1903.

GRANT, LIBUT.-COL. JAS. MURRAY.—Served as a volr. against Tambockies in 1857, being then lieut. 85th L.I.; employed in mil. survey of Transkeian territory, 1861-62; ret. from Imp. army in 1867; inspr. F.A.M.P. 1867; served in Basutoland, 1866, and on the frontier border, 1869; mem. of two special comsns. of inquiry into native affairs, 1872; 1st class inspr., F.A.M.P., 1873; in same year commanded a column sent in pursuit of the Zulus under Langalibalele; ag. ch. mag., E. Griqualand, 1876; employed in making a mil. survey of the country between the Kabousie and the Kei, 1877; staff offr., col. forces in Gcalekaland campaign, 1877; ag. R.M., Komga, 1878; and transf'd. to C.M.R., 1878, as sen. capt.; honorary majority, Aug., 1879; commanded C.M.R., Mohalie's Hoek, Oct., 1880, and in charge of base and lines of communication, Jan., 1881, to end of campaign; commanded left wing C.M.R., Sept., 1881, to Sept., 1883; on a special mission to the Pondo ch. Umquikela, Nov., 1881; in command of all col. troops east of the Kei, Sept., 1884; war medal with clasp (1877-9); lieut.-com., 1892.
GRAVES, THE HON. J. H.—M.L.A. for Delatite, Victoria; comsnr. of trade and customs, Victoria, 9th July, 1881, to Mar., 1883.
GRAVES, SILAS.—Clerk, procureur-gen'l's dept., Mauritius, 2nd May, 1859; deputy collr. of cust., 2nd Mar., 1896; ag. collr. in 1899, 1900 and 19-3.
GRAY, E. A. S.—Demy, Magd. Coll., Oxon; B.A. 1892; asst. mast., Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana, Jan., 1896; asst. dist. comsnr., Niger Coast prot., Sept., 1899; dist. comsnr., Feb., 1902.

GRAY, GEORGE SMITH BOOTH ERskine.—Conway, 1879-80; midshipman, R.N.R., Dec., 1881; mercantile marine offr., 1884 to 1894; sub-lieut., R.N.R., 1887-1894; Lieut., R.N.R., 1894; H.M.S. "Anson" and naval employment, 1894-1897; joined So. Nigeria marine, Jan., 1900; served Ubium expdn. and Aro campaign (medal); gov. of gaols, 1902.

GRAY, REGINALD.—Called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1875; compiled Bermuda laws, 1690-1883, in 1884; revising offr., Bermuda, 1889-93; counsel to statute law consolidation comttee., Bermuda, 1898-1900; atty.-gen., 1900.
GRAY, ROBERT J.—Under col. sec., Queensland, Jan., 1880; asst. comsnr. of rly.s., 1889; comsnr., 1898.

GRAY, ST. GEORGE.—B.A., M.B., B.C.H. (Univ. Dub.), L.M.(Rotunda); ag. col. asst. surg., St. Lucia, 1892; supernumerary ditto, 1894; col. asst. surg. and med. offr. in charge of Yaws hosp., 1898; med. offr. in charge of agric. schl., 1901; med. offr., W. Africa med. staff, Sierra Leone, 1903.

GRAY, SIR S. BROWNLOW, KT. BAUH. (1901), C.M.G. (1888).—B. 1823; called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn, 1847; inspr. of schls., Bermuda, 1848; atty.-gen. and advoc.-gen., 1861; ch. justice, May, 1900, ret., 1904.
