GRAY, THOMAS ALEXANDER.—Late sergt. R.I.C.; sergt.-major, Leeward Islds. police, Montserrat; gaoler, Jan., 1899.
GREAVES, DUDLEY, A., M.R.C.S., Edin., L.R.C.P., Edin.—Asst. med. offr. Virgin Is., March, 1901; deputy commr.

GREAVES, LIEUT.-GEN. SIR GEORGE RICHARD, K.C.B. (1885), K.C.M.G. (1881), C.B.—B. 1831; has held several important posts; ch. sec. to govt. in Cyprus, July, 1878, to July, 1879; adjt.-gen. in India, Oct., 1879, to Oct., 1884; ch. of the staff at Suakin, 1885; in command of Meerut div. of Indian army to 1892.

GREAVES, KENNETH J.—Ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; called to bar, Mid. Temple, 26th Jan., 1900; ag. deputy clk., house of assem., Barbados, May-Sept., 1900; ag. pol. mag., Bridgetown, Oct., 1900, to Feb., 1901; ditto, April, 1901; ditto, St. Thomas and St. James, Nov., 1901, to Aug., 1902; apptd. pol. mag., 1st Sept., 1902.

GREAVES, WILLIAM HERBERT, K.C.—B.A., Oxon, honours juris., 1879; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., 1890; ag. solr.-gen., Barbados, Aug., 1884, to July, 1886, and July, 1886, to Jan., 1887, when confirmed; J.P., Sept., 1885; escheator-gen., 1887; Q.C. for Barbados, 1890; mem. exec. coun., 1890; acted as atty.-gen., July to Dec., 1887, and June to Dec., 1891; mem. of house of assem. since 1882, and of bd. of educn.; atty.-gen., Mar., 1896; now ch. just.
GREEN, A. PIERCE.—Surg., A.M.D., 1878; med. offr., col. forces in Basuto war, 1881; regtl. surg., left wing, C.M.R., 1881-4; med. offr., Cape rly.s., 1885; ditto, Bechuanaland border pol., 1885.

GREEN, CHARLES FRANCIS J.—Cadet, Sts. Stlttns., Nov., 1898; ag. dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Mar., 1900; ag. 4th mag., Sing., June, 1901; passed final exam. in Malay, Oct., 1901; ag. dist. offr., Malacca, Feb., 1902; confirmed, July, 1903.
GREEN, GEORGE C.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil serv., and assigned to C.O., 26th Aug., 1901.

GREEN, JOHN FREDERICK NORMAN, B.A.—Scholar, Emman. Coll., Camb., 1890; bracketed 17th wrangler, 1893; B.A., 1893; 1st class, Part II., nat. science tripos, 1894; 2nd class clk. C.O. after compet. exam., Feb., 1896; priv. sec. to Sir E. Wingfield, Dec., 1898; to Sir M. Ommanney, 1900; 1st class clk., 16th June, 1902.
GREENE, GEOFFREY PHILIP.—Gen. mag. Ceylon rlyws., 11th Oct., 1901.

GRENE, GEORGE BALL.—Ed. in England; jun. clk., ld. dept., Br. Guiana, Nov., 1887; 5th class clk., Feb., 1888; 4th cl., secretariat, 1891; sec. to comttee. for enquiring into post-office, 1893; sec. pilottage comsn., and 3rd class, 1895; 2nd class, and despatch clk., 1899; 1st class clk., 1901; ag. prin. clk., 1902-3, Aug.-Sept., 1904; ag. asst. govt. sec. and priv. sec. to ag. gov., Sept., 1904.

GREENE, JOHN LE SUEUR.—Ed. Roy. Coll.; entered col. sec. office, Mauritius, Jan., 1886; jun. copyist, procur. gen.'s office, Mar., 1887; sen. ditto, Feb., 1888; govt. translator of laws, Nov., 1888; 5th class clk., July, 1890; 4th class clk., Mar., 1896.

GREENE, SIR WILLIAM CONYNGHAM, K.C.B. (1900).—B. 1854; ed. Harrow, and Pemb. Coll., Ox.; M.A. 1880; entrd. F.O. 1877; ag. 3rd sec. legation at Athens, 1880; ditto Stuttgart, 1883; ag. chargé d'affaires Stuttgart and Darmstadt at various times, 1883-9; 2nd sec. diplomatic service, 1887; 2nd sec. Hague, 1889-91; Brussels, 1891-3; sec. of legation and chargé d'affaires, Teheran, 1893-6; H.M. agt. at Pretoria, S.A. Republic, with rank of chargé d'affaires, 25th Aug., 1896, to 1899.
GREENE, W. H., M.I.C.E. (London.)—Asst. engrnr., Victorian rly., 1888; dist. engrnr., 1868; res. engrnr., 1868; ch. engrnr. of existing lines, 1882; Victorian rly. comsrr., 1887-92.

GREENER, HERBERT.—D.S.O., clk., sec.'s dept., G.P.O., Cape, 1884; in charge of money order dept., 1886; 1st clk., admstr.'s office, Be. Bechuanaland, 1888; recvr. of revenue, Vryburg, and P.M.G., Bechuanaland, 1889; examnr. of acts, acctnt. to high comsrr., Capetown, 1890; paymr. (rank captain), Bech. Br. pol., 1891, combined with prin. cust. offr., for Bech. Prot., 1896; specially employed in charge of finances, Marekeng siege, and financial ast. to mil.-govr., Bloemfontein, 1899-1900; transf. to S.A.C. as ch. paymtr. and acctnt.-gen. (rank major), Oct., 1900; promoted lt.-col., 1901.

GREENLEES, THOMAS DUNCAN, M.B. Edin.—Surg.-capt., Graham's Town 1st city volrs.; late ast. med. offr., counties asyl., Carlisle, and City of London asyl.; prizeman medico-psychological assn.; med. supr., Graham's Town asyl., and visiting surg. to the chronic sick hospital, Aug., 1890.
GREENWAY, THE HON. THOMAS.—Mem. of legis. assem., Manitoba; formerly pres. of exec. coun.; premier; min. of agric. and imigrn. and rly. comsrr.

GREENWOOD, ALFRED CRAVEN.—Exec. staff, inventions exhibn., 1885, and Col. and Ind. exhibn., 1886, and Royal Jubilee exhibn., Manchester, 1887; sec. to various charitable institns., 1887 to 1890; priv. sec. to Sir A. Shea, gov. Bahamas, and clk. to coun., 1891-4; treas. of Gibraltar, 1897; J.P. and mem. of sanitary comsrr., chrmn. at various times, 1898-1904, also contractg. offr., 1897; ag. capt. of port, July to Oct., 1901-1903; hosp. comsrr., 1903; chrmn. of comttee on wharfage tolls and storage waterport wharf, 1902; chrmn. of comttee on management, working and revenue of new comm'l. mole, 1904.

GREENWOOD, THOMAS J.—Inspr. Cyprus pol., Feb., 1887; ag. local comdt., Limasol, Oct. to Dec., 1894, and Apr. to July, 1895; bronze medal, roy. hum. soc., for saving and attempting to save life during Limasol floods, Nov. 12th, 1894; local comndtt., 1896.
GREGORY, SIR AUGUSTUS CHARLES, K.C.M.G. (1903); C.M.G. (1875).—B. 1819; survr.-gen. of Queensland; ret. Sept., 1879; trustee of the Queensland museum, 1876; mem. legis. coun., 1882.
GREGORY, HENRY.—Elected M.L.A. for Coolgardie goldfield, 1897; re-elected 1 mines 1901; resigned, 1904.

GRENFELL, GENERAL SIR FRANCIS WALLACE, G.C.B., G.C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1841; gov. and comdr-in-chief of Malta, 1898; A.D.C. to Sir A. Cunynghame; also staff offr. to Col. Glyn in the Transkei, 1878-7; dep. ast. adjt. and qrtmtr.-gen. in the Kaffir war, 1878; the same in the Zulu war, 1879; ast. qrtmtr.-gen. in the Boer war, 1881; served in the Egyptian campaign of 1882, and the Nile expdn. of 1884; was Sirdar of the Egyptian army from 1885 to 1892; commanded the troops employed at Suakim against the Dervishes; after filling various posts at home, went again to Egypt in 1897 to command the British troops; with Lord Kitchener in the Nile campaign, 1898; gov. of Malta, 1899 to 1903.

GRENIER, GEORGE.—Employed in Jaffa Kachcheri and regtns. dept., 1861-1872; in Queen's advocate's dept., April, 1872, to 1878; second dep.-registrar, sup. ct., 9th April, 1878; dep.-registrar, 15th June, 1885; registrar, 1st May, 1894.
GRENIER, JOSEPH.—Dist. judge, Colombo, Ceylon, 23rd April, 1903; has acted on various occasions since 1897 as sol.-gen., comsrr. of assize, and puisne justice.

GREY, 4TH EARL OF (creat. 1806).—VISCOUNT HOWICK, BABON GREY, BART.—G.C.M.G. (1904). Ed. Harrow, and Trin. Coll., Camb.; M.P. 1880-6; dir. B.S.A. Co.; admstr. of Rhodesia, 1896 to 1898; gov.-gen. of Canada, 26th Sept., 1904.

GREY, ROBT. CAMPBELL.—Junior offr. transy. Selangor, 1st Aug., 1888; ast. in land off., Krian, 14th June, 1889; passed final exam. in Malay, 6th Dec., 1890; ast. mag., Kinta, 24th Dec., 1890; ag. supt. of Penghulus, Lower Perak, 14th June, 1891; supt. ditto, 1st Nov., 1891; ast. mag. and collr. of land rev., Lower Perak, 1st Apr., 1893; ag. dist. mag., Kuala Kangsar, 1st Jan., 1894; ast. sec. to govt., Selangor, 28th Feb., 1896; ag. dist. mag., Krian, 9th Apr., 1896; dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, 1st May, 1897; ditto, Kuala Selangor, 1st Jan., 1899; ditto, Ulu Pahang, 1st Nov., 1899; dist. mag., Kuala Kangsar, 19th Jan., 1900; ag. sec. to govt., Selangor, 1st Oct. to 30th Dec., 1900; ag. dist. mag., Kinta, 10th Oct., 1901; resumed duties as dist. mag., Kuala Kangsar, 21st Nov., 1901; sec. to res., Selangor, 1903.

GREY-WILSON, SIR WM., K.C.M.G. (1904); C.M.G. (1891).—B. 1852; ed. at Chel. Coll.; priv. sec. to Sir William Grey, gov. of Jamaica, 1874; to Lieut.-Gov. Edwd. E. Rushworth, Mar., 1877; admstr., Major-Gen. J. R. Mann, R.E., Aug., 1877; to Lieut.-Gov. F. P. Barlee, Br. Honduras, Sept., 1877; and clk. of the exec. and legis. couns., June, 1878; ag. mag., Orange Walk, in charge of frontier scouts, Dec., 1879, to Apr., 1880, and June, 1880, to Mar., 1881; ast. col. sec. and treas., S. Leone, 1883; special comsrr., Sulymah, Dec., 1883, to June, 1884; 4th ast. col. sec., G. Coast Col., 1884; col. sec., St. Helena, July, 1886; ag. gov., 1887; gov., 1889; gov., Falklands, 1897; gov., Bahamas, 5th May, 1904.
GRIEVE, R., M.D., C.M.G. (1894).—Med. supt., lun. asyl., Berbice, Br. Guiana, Sept., 1873; ag. med. offr., imigrn. dept., Aug., 1886; surg. gen. of col., Oct., 1886; ret. 1894.

GRIFFIN, C. T.—M.R.C.S., Eng.; L.R.C.P., Edin.; L.S.A., Lond.; suptgd. med. offr., Dikova, Ceylon, 1st Jan., 1883; dist. med. offr., 17th Jan., 1888; govt. med. offr., 1st Sept., 1892; col. surg. prov. of Uva, 21st Sept., 1898; ast. prin. civ. med. offr. and insp. gen. of hospitals, 17th Jan., 1901.

GRIFFIN, EUGENE PATRICK.—Third clk., treas., Gibraltar (after compet. exam.), Feb., 1883; pol. clk., 1883; 3rd clk., col. sec.'s office, 1886; 2nd class clk., Jan., 1890; 1st class clk., Jan., 1893; ch. clk. and cashier, P.O., Nov., 1893; transf. to col. sec.'s office, July, 1895.

GRIFFIN, MARTIN J.—Called to the bar in 1868 from offices of Hon. James McDonald (now ch. just. of Nova Scotia) and of Hon. Wm. Miller (speaker of Senate of Canada); edited Halifax "Express" till 1874; contested Halifax co. for local legislature in 1874; edited Halifax "Herald" till 1878; apptd. asst. on fishery coman. under Washington Treaty in 1873; priv. sec. to min. of just. in 1878; sec. of civ. serv. coman. in 1881; edited Toronto "Mail" till Aug. 6, 1885; when appnt. joint partly. librarian.

GUERITZ, EDWARD PEREGRINE.—Cadet and dist. offr. and res., Sarawak govt., 1874 to 1877; asst. pres., Br. N. Borneo, 1882-84; collr. of land rev., Sungei Ujong, 1885; collr. and mag., Jelebu, June, 1885; ag. res. Labuan, May, 1890; asst. govt. sec., Br. N. Borneo, 1891; res. Labuan, 1893; ses. judge, Br. N. Borneo, 1894; mem. coun., 1894; judge of chief ct., 1895; govt. sec., 1898; apptd. one of the commrs. to admnr. the govt., Br. N. Borneo and Labuan.
GULLICK, JOSEPH WM.—Ck., examiner's office, Imp. customs, Dec., 1862; transf. to E. and A. dept., Somerset House, Feb., 1873; supt. col. audit, branch of that dept., Feb., 1892.

GUPPY, ROBERT.—Imperial postal serv., 31st Mar., 1892; ch. ck. and acctnt., post and tel. dept., G. Coast, 1900; ag. dir. of telegraphs, 2nd June to 26th July, 1901; acctnt., 1902; prepared system of accounting passed in Council and brought into force 1st Sept., 1902.

GURDON, Sir W. BRAMPTON, C.B., K.C.M.G. (1882).—B. 1840; ed. at Eton and at Trin. Coll., Camb., B.A. (1863); apptd. clk.in the treasury., 1863; and received the K.C.M.G. for services on special missions in 1879 and 1881 to S. Africa for the settlement of financial questions; resig. 1885.
GURNER, JOHN AUGUSTUS.—Ed. at Chel. Coll., and Jesus Coll. (Camb.), LL.B.; called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1877; parly. draughtsman, Victoria, May, 1882, now one of the crown prosecutors.
GUTTERON, W. B.—Rev. offr., Br. Honduras, Apr., 1883; keeper, Queen's warehouse, Jan., 1888; ch. ck. treasy., customs, and P.O., Feb., 1891; postmtr., 1897.
GWYNNE, J. W.—Puisne judge of sup. ct. and ct. of exch., Canada.

HABENS, REV. W. J., B.A. (Lond., 1862).—Minister, Congregational church, N. Z., 1864-78; sec., bd. of educn., Christchurch, Jan., 1877, to May, 1878; inspr.-gen. of schls., 1878; mem. (and sec.) of roy. coman. to enquire into operations of N.Z. Univ. and its relation to secondary schls., 1879-80; fellow N.Z. Univ., 1880; sec. and inspr.-gen., educn. dept., Wellington, 1888.

HADDON-SMITH, GEO. BASIL, C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1861; lieut. Royal Guernsey militia, 1879; attached 32nd (D.C.L.I.) regt., 1880; attached 107th (Royal Sussex) regt., 1881; asst. inspr., Lagos constab., 1886; adjt., 1887; priv. sec. to ag. gov. and clk. of couns., 1887, 1888, and 1893; ag. inspr.-gen. and sheriff of Lagos, 1888, 1890, 1891, and 1893; ag. dist. coman., 1887, 1888, 1889, and 1891; took part in Jeju expedition, 1892, mentioned in despatches (medal and clasp); political offr. on Sir G. Carter's mission to Yorubaland, 1893; asst. col. sec. Lagos, 1894; ag. col. sec., 1895; priv. sec. to Sir F. Scott in Ashanti expdn., 1896; ch. ass. col. sec., G. Coast, 1896; ag. col. sec., 1896-7; mem. of exec. and legis. coun., G. Coast col.; political offr. to Col. Sir J. Willcocks in the Ashanti expdn., 1900; took part in relief of Kumasi, spec. ment. in despa. (Ashanti medal and clasp); ag. gov. Gambia, July to Oct., 1901; col. sec. S. Leone, Oct., 1901.

HADEN, FRANCIS SEYMOUR, B.A., C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1850; ed. at Westminster schl., and at Chr. Ch., Oxford; priv. sec. to Sir Henry Bulwer, K.C.M.G., lieut.-gov. of Natal, Mar., 1877; ag. ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, Mar., 1880; asst. col. sec., Jan., 1881; acted as col. treas. and also as col. sec., 1885; col. sec., 1887; was chmn. of Natal trade coman., 1885-6; deleg. to customs and rly. confce. Cape Town, 1888; dep. gov., Natal and Zululand in Dec., 1890; dep. gov. in Aug., 1891; ret., 1893, on introduction of responsible govt.
HAGGART, THE HON. JOHN G.—Postmtr.-gen., Canada, 1888; min. of rlys. and canals, 1892-6.

HAHN, D. M., A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Realschool, Hamburg; apprenticeship under J. A. Biernatzki, architect, Altona; theoretically instructed at the technological instn., Hamburg; in charge govt. saw mill, Trindad, Jan., 1887; foreman, pub.wks., Jan., 1888; ag. asst. engr., N. Div. in 1889, 1890-1; asst. engr., N. Div., 1891; asst. engr. of works and buildings, Feb. 1894; asst. engr. office and wks., Nov., 1899.

HAINES, REV. FRANK WM., B.A. (Oxon).—Ed. Christ's Coll., Brecknock, and Jesus Coll., Oxford; govt. tutor and chaplain, Selangor, 1890; inspr. of schls., Selangor, 1891; col. chaplain, Malacca, 1899; also ag. headmnr., High schl. Malacca, 1899-1900; col. chap., Penang, Apr., 1901 (Surrogate), hon. chaplain, Penang volunteer corps.

HALCOMB, FRED., M.A. (Wadham Coll., Oxon).—Parly. librarian, S. Australia, Jan., 1870; clk., asst. and serg.-at-arms, legis. coun., Apr., 1874; clk. house of assem., May, 1887; clk. legis. coun., 1901.

HALE, ABRAHAM.—Ag. mag., Matang, 13th Dec., 1884; insp. of mines and asst. mag., Kinta, Feb., 1885; collector and mag., Tampin, 17th Oct., 1887; ag. sup. of Negri Sembilan, Mar., 1888; clk. of coun., 20th Oct., 1888; asst. Indian immigr. agent, Dec., 1889; ag. for resident, Feb. to Mar., 1900; offr. in charge, Mar. to Nov., 1894; dist. offr., Tampin, 1st Jan., 1896; dist. offr., Kuala Selangor, 26th Jan., 1897; passed higher standard in Malay, Apl., 1897; examiner in Malay, 1898; dist. offr., Klang, 9th Mar., 1899; collector of land rev., Kuala Lumpur, and regar. of titles, Selangor; chmn., pub. gardens comtee., Kuala Lumpur, and of museum comtee., 21st Nov., 1899; vice-chmn., Malay agric. sttlmt., 23rd Sep., 1900; dir. of W. and O. fund, 19th Apr., 1901.

HALL, CECIL G.—Volr. col. sec.'s office, Mauritius, Nov., 1866; clk., gen. bd. of health, June, 1867; clk. med. dept., Nov., 1868; acctn. immigr. dept., June, 1872; senior clk. Oct., 1890; and ag. ch. clk. several times from 1890 to 1896; inspr. of imigrts., 1886; ag. storekr.-gen., Aug. to Nov., 1898; do., Sept., 1901; ditto, 12th Aug. to 19th Aug., 1904.

HALL, GILBERT AMOS.—Ed. at Harrow schl.; cadet, Straits, 1888; passed final exam. in Malay. Apr., 1891; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, July, 1891; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, July to Sept., 1893; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, Mar., 1894; supt. of educn., Penang, Oct., 1894; ag. dist. offr., Malacca, Nov., 1896; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, May, 1897; sheriff and dep. regar., sup. ct., Singapore, July, 1897; ag. collr. ld. rev. Penang, Mar., 1898; 2nd asst. col. sec., S. Sttlmts., Aug., 1898, but continued to act as collr. ld. rev., Penang, till Mar., 1900; ag. collr. ld. rev. and offr. in charge of treasy, Malacca, Sept., 1900, and confirmed, Aug., 1902; sen. dist. offr., July, 1904; ag. P.M.G., Aug., 19'4.

HALL, THE HON. SIR JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1882).—B. 1824; emigrated to N. Z., 1852; mem. legis. assem., 1855; col. sec., 1856; mem. legis. com., 1862-6; postmtr.-gen., 1866-9; col. sec., 1872-3; premier, 1879-82.
HALLETT, W. CARDY.—Ck. to col. survr., Bermuda, Aug., 1881; asst. supt. pub. wks., Jan., 1887; supt., July, 1887; col. survr., Oct., 1887.
HALLEY, ROBT. HENRY.—Ed. Dollar Academy, Scotland; clk.; central repatriation bd. Bloemfontein, Apl., 1902, to May, 1903; clk. to col. sec., O.R.C., May, 1903.

HALLIFAX, EDWIN RICHARD.—Ed. Blundell's schl.; Blundell's scholar, Balliol Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1896; cadet, Hong Kong, 1897; passed cadet, 1900; ag. police mag., New Territory, 1899; J.P., 1900; asst. sup. of pol., and pol. mag., N.T., 1901; ag. regis.-genl., May-June, 1902; ag. deputy sup. of police, 1903-4.

HALLIFAX, F. J.—Cadet, S. Sttlmts., Mar., 1893; passed final exam. in Malay, June, 1894; ag. dist. offr. Penang, Apr., 1896, and Malacca, Nov., 1896; supt. of educn., Penang, July, 1897; dist. offr., June, 1898; ag. dist. offr., Dinding, June, 1901; ag. coll. of land revenue, Penang, Apr., 1902; also ag. regis. of deeds and regis. of bills of sale, Penang, May, 1902; asst. P.M.G., Penang, Aug., 1902, but continued to act as coll. of land rev.; ag. collr. of land rev., and offr. in charge of Treasury, Malacca, Apl., 1903.
HAMBLIN, R.A.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil ser. and temporarily employed in the science and art dept. from Mar. 21st to May 26th, 1899; assigned to C.O. July, 1899.

HAMILTON, CHARLES BOUGHTON, C.M.G. (1896).—B. 1860; entered the service, 1865; 2nd elk. rec.gen.'s office, Br. Guiana, 1873, 1877; col. book-keeper, 1879; ag. rec. gen., 1881; and ag. aud.-gen., 1883 to 1886; received thanks of coun. on leaving for post of rec.-gen., &c., Trinidad, 1886; J.P.; ag. audr.-gen., M.E.C., and M.L.C., 1886; mem. of bd. of educn., 1887; of bd. of health, 1888; M.L.C., 1889; chmn. of comtees for widows' fund, 1889; ag. col. sec., 1890; chmn. hbr. improvement comte., and ag. audr.-gen. and col. sec., 1891; mem. coll. coun., 1891; rec.-gen., Br. Guiana, Dec., 1892; ag. aud.-gen. and mem. ct. of policy, May, 1893, to May, 1895, and again Oct. 1896; sen. comsnr., Vlissingen estate; chmn. spirits comsnr., "Trotman," trust comte.; dir. widows' and orphans' fund, &c.; mem. exec. coun., Dec., 1895; off. mem. ct. of policy, July, 1898; chrmn., excise bd., 1901; chrmn., educn. comen., 1902.
HAMILTON, CHARLES EDWARD—Called to the bar, Ontario, 1865; elected mayor of Winnipeg, 1885; elected to Manitoba legislature, 1885; atty.-gen., Manitoba, Feb., 1887-8.
HAMILTON, R. W.—Ed. St. Paul's schl. and Trin. Hall, Camb.; B.A., 1889; M.A., 1892; sec. comsnr. of enquiry, Dominica, 1893-4; called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1895; dist. comsnr., Lagos, 1895.

HAMILTON, STR W. A. B., K.C.M.G. (1897), C.B. (1892), C.M.G. (1887),—B. 1844; ed. at Harrow; apptd. a jun. clk. in the C.O., May, 1864, after compet. exam.; sent on secret service to N. America, 1867; 2nd class clk., 30th Sept., 1st class clk., July, 1879; and priv. sec. to Mr. Knatchbull-Hugessen (now Lord Brabourne), M.P., 9th Oct., 1872; called to the bar (Inner Tem.), 18th Nov., 1872; priv. sec. to Mr. James Lowther, M.P., under sec. of state for the cols., 25th Feb., 1874, to 14th Feb., 1878; priv. sec. to the Right Hon. James Lowther, M.P., ch. sec. for Ireland, 15th Feb., 1878; capt in the E. Lothan yeomanry cavalry, 5th June, 1878; major, 18th June, 1883; hon. lieut.-col. and comdg. regt., 1894; comdg. 12th yeomanry brigade, 1895; author of "Mr. Montenello; a Romance of the Civil Service"; priv. sec. to Mr. Stanhope, 3rd Aug., 1886; and to Sir H. Holland (now Lord Knutsford), Jan., 1887, to Aug., 1892; sec. to col. confce., 1887; prin. clk., 1st Jan., 1894; ch. clk., Mar., 1896; apptd. offr. of arms of the Order of St. Michael and St. George, 1901.

HAMLEY, WILLIAM WYMOND, M.A., F.R.M.S.—Clare Coll., Camb. (hon. classics, 1873); 1st class prof., Roy. Coll., Mauritius, Jan., 1879; sen. prof., May, 1887; ag. rector, May, 1891, to July, 1892, and Apr. to Nov., 1894, again May, 1900; rector, 1901; ag. director of pub. instrn. in 1902, 1903 and 1904.
HAMMOND, E. J.—Govt. med. offr., Savanna Grande district, Trinidad, 1 Jan., 1876.

HAMPDEN, 2ND VISOR. (creat. 1884), BARON DAGRS (creat. 1307), G.C.M.G. (1889).—B. 1841 dep.-lieut. and J.P. for Sussex; J.P. Herts; was M.P. for Hertfordshire, 1868 to 1874; for Stroud, July, 1874 (unseated Dec. 1874); and again 1880-5; and for Mid-Gloucester, 1885-6; survr.-gen. of ordce., 1883-5; (Gov. N.S. Wales, 1895-9.

HANCOCK, HERBERT HENRY, M.A.—Scholar St. John's Coll., Oxon; 2nd class classical mods., 1876; 2nd class classical finals, 1878; B.A., 1879; M.A., 1883; asst. master Cheltenham Coll., 1879; 2nd master Bosworth schl., 1880; prof. classics and moral philos., Codrington Coll., Barbados, 1883; ag. prin., 1889-91; 2nd master, Queen's Roy. Coll., Trinidad, 1892; ag. prin., Apr. to Dec., 1894; elk. to coll. coun., 1892; lieut. Trinidad field artillery.

HANCOCK, WILLIAM J., M.I.E.E., A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Glasgow Univ.; supt. of telephones, W. Australia, 1885; supt. of telegraphs and telephones, 1890; hon. local sec. and treas. for W. Australia of the instit. of electrical engineers of London govt. electrician, 1894.
HANITSCH, KARL RICHARD, PH.D. (Jena).—Demonstrator of Zoology, Univ. Coll., Liverpool, 1887; curator and librarian, Raffles library and museum, Singapore, 1895.

HANLEY, ALLAN HASTINGS, C.M.G. (1903); F.R.C.S.—Med. offr., Niger Coast Prot. (now S. Nigeria), May, 1893; sen. med. offr., Apl., 1901; dep. prin. med. offr., May, 1903; ag. prin. med. offr., Dec., 1903 to Apl., 1904.

HANNAM, WILLOUGHBY, M.I.C.E.—Apprentice to M. Du Baya, C.E., Reading; ast. survr., Melb. and River Murray rly., Victoria, 1860; engrn. Moreton Bay tramway co., Brisbane, 1861-63; dist. engineer (S. dist.) Queensland govt. rlys., 1863-8; contractor's engrn. on same, 1868-72; dist. engrn. and in charge of surveys, cent. div., Queensland rlys., 1872-85; ch. engrn., N. div., 1885; has been engaged on survey of 1,060 miles of rly., and directed survey of 250 miles more.

HANSON, CHAS. RAISTRICK, L.S.O. (1903).—B. 1850; M.I.C.E.; Cape govt. rly. staff, 1874; Ceylon, ditto, 1879; res. engrn. and man., Perak state rly., 1887; res. engr. for rlys., Perak, 1890, and Prov. Wellesley, 1903.

HARBIN, JOHN ALPHONSE.—Ch. clk. to regisr., Grenada, July, 1884; ditto to regisr. sup. ct., July, 1886; marshal V.-A. ct., Mar., 1887; ag. regisr. sup. ct., and ag. regisr. in V.-A. ct., May, 1888, to Apr., 1890; acctnt., treasy., July, 1890; sec. to comn. pub. wks. dept. enquiry, Dec., 1890; ag. ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, Aug., 1892, to Feb., 1893; ag. inspr. of schools Feb., 1893, to Mar., 1894; inspr. of schools Mar., 1894; ag. col. postmaster, Aug.-Sept., 1894; mem. bd. of ed. Apr., 1895; ag. regisr. sup. ct., Aug.-Sept., 1901; ag. inspr. of schools, St. Vincent, Sept.-Nov., 1901; mem. bd. of ed., St. Vincent, Sept.-Nov., 1901; ag. pol. mag. and asst. treas., Carriacou, Dec., 1901, to Jan., 1902.
HARBOUR, E. W. H.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to bd. of trade, 22nd Jan., 1901; transf'd. to C.O., 22nd July, 1901.

HARCOURT, ALGERNON BERNARD.—App'td. cons. agnt. in Oil R. Protect., Oct., 1892; ag. v.-cons., Bonny dist., June, 1894, to July, 1895; dist. comsnr., Bonny, 1st Apr., 1896; ag. v.-cons. cent. div. on various occasions; transf'd. to Cross Riv. div., Dec., 1899; trav'ling. comsnr., S. Nig., 1st Apr., 1900; ag. dival. comsnr., Cross Riv. div., May-Dec., 1900; ag. res. Benin City Territories, 1901 and 1902; divl. comsnr., Cross River, 17th Jan., 1903.

HARDING, ALFRED J.—Ed. Christ's Coll., Brecon, and St. John's Camb.; foundtn. schlr., 1899; 1st class nat. sci. trip. part I., 1900; B.A., 1900; ent. C.O. after compet. exam. as 2nd cl. clk., Oct., 1901.

HARDING, EDWARD J.—Ed. Dulwich Coll. and Hertford Coll., Oxford (scholar, 1889); 1st cls. class. moda., 1901; 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1903; B.A., 1903; app'td. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., Marine dept., bd. of trade, 30th Nov., 1903; 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 31st May, 1904.

HARDY, WENTWORTH MARMDUKE.—Entered survey dept., S. Australia, Oct., 1865; accompanied the survr.-gen. as survr. and draughtsman to Port Darwin, Dec., 1868; rose step by step, till app'td. Jan., 1878, trigonometrical survr. In 1884, at the recommendation of the survr.-gen. of Victoria, and to carry out his suggested reformation, appointed inspr. of surveys and prof. adviser, survey dept., Tasmania.

HARE, FREDERICK ARTHUR.—Ed. St. Columba's Coll., Wicklow; clk. to mags., Vasse, W. Australia, 1880; clk. and asst. priv. sec. to Sir William Robinson, during various periods from 1880 to 1882; inspr. of pol. for the S. dists. of the col., 1882; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to Sir F. N. Broome; ag. govt. res., Wyndham, Cambridge Gulf, 1886-7; ag. govt. res., Albany, 1887-8; res. mag. York, 1888-97; warden and res. mag. East Coolgardie Goldfield, 1897-1900; comsnr. of police, 1900.

HARE, GEO. THOMPSON, C.M.G. (1902); I.S.O. (1904).—B. 1863; ed. Wadhams Coll., Oxon; cadet, S. Sttlmts., 1884; passed in Chinese, 1888; dist. offr., Jan., 1890; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, June, 1890; 2nd asst. prot. Chinese, Penang, Oct., 1891; ag. asst. prot. Chinese, Penang, 1893; asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Apl., 1895; sec. for Chinese affairs, Fed. Malay States, Feb., 1897; on spec. serv. in Weihaiwei, 1901-2; ag. prot. of Chinese, 20th Nov., 1903; ret., 1904.

HARE, REGINALD CHARLES.—Clk. treasy., W. Australia, Apl., 1881; pol. inspr. S. dists., June, 1886; ag. govt. res., Wyndham, May, 1888; ditto, Roebourne, June, 1889; sec., W. Australian govt. agency, London, May, 1890.

HARGROVE, HERBERT.—Ed. at Harrow, 1888-1891; admitted a solicitor of sup. ct., England, Jan., 1897; app'td. asst. dist. comsnr. (probationary), G. Coast, Apl., 1902; ag. dist. comsnr., Axim, May, 1902.

HARMAN, WM. HENRY.—App'td., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the clv. serv. and assigned to science and art dept., 10th Apl., 1900; and to bd. of agric., 6th June, 1901; transf'd. to C.O., 27th Jan., 1902.
HARPER, FRANCOIS HENRY.—Transferred from the service of the Royal Niger Co. as supplementary clk. to C.O., 1st Jan., 1900.
HARRAGIN, WILLIAM CAMPBELL.—Inspr. of pol., Br. Guiana, Sept., 1874; sen. inspr., Oct., 1883; acted as stip. mag. in 1885, and as inspr.-gen. in 1886 and 1888; dep. inspr.-gen., 1891.

HARRIOTT, ALEXIS WYNNS.—Ed. at Yale: B.A. 1856; M.A. and B.Ph., 1859; ag. col. surv. and inspr., P.W., Turks and Caicos Islds., Feb. to Apl., 1864; J.P., 1879; unoff. mem. legis. bd., 1881 to 1888, when app'td. asst. comsnr. Grand Turk; ag. comsnr., Apl. to July, 1888; manager savings bk., 1889; mem. bd. of educn., 1891; coroner, 1893; ag. auditor on various occasions, 1888-1902.

HARRIOTT, EDMUND COWLES.—Clk. to comsnr., and to legis. bd., Turks and Caicos Islds., Nov., 1887; inspected govt. schls., 1890, 1891, and 1902; ag. reg'rs. sup. ct., and of deeds, 1891 and 1894; ag. asst. comsnr. G. Turk, May to July, 1896; and Aug. to Nov., 1901; ag. auditor. Aug. and Sept., 1901; ag. dist. mag., visiting Caicos Is., July, 1902; ag. acctnt. and clk. (collr. of cust., postmtr. and cashier), comsnr.'s office, Apl. to June, 1903; confirmed July, 1903; is a J.P.

HARRIS, CHARLES ALEXANDER, C.B. (C.M.G. (1900); B.A.—B. 1855; scholar, prizeman, and Porteus medallist of Christ's Coll., Camb.; Tancred student in com. law, Linc.'s Inn, 1877; 12th classic, 1878; app'td. 2nd class clk., C.O. after open competn., 1st July, 1879; sec. to W. Indies Finance Comms., Dec., 1882; asst. sec. Sugar Bounties Comfse., 1889; priv. sec. to Mr. Buxton, M.P., Jan., 1894, to June, 1895; and to the Earl of Selborne, June, 1895; 1st class clk., 1896; specially engaged in work connected with Venezuelan bndry. arbitn., 1896-8; prin. clk., Nov., 1898; sent to Paris, 1899, in connection with the Venez. bndry. arbitn.; conducted British case in Brazilian-B. Guiana bndry. arbitn., 1901-4; author of numerous articles in Dict. of Pol. Econ. on economic and statistical subjects, and of lives of various colonial gov's and statesmen in Dict. of Nat. Biog.

HARRISON, C. W.—Ed. at Sherborne schl., Clifton Coll., Balliol Coll., Oxford, and with Walter Wren, Esq.; 3rd cl. classical moda., 1894; app'td. cadet, Sléngar, F.M.S., Nov., 1897; mag. 2nd cl., Kuala Langat, Feb., 1899; passed final exam. in Malay, Oct., 1899; ag. asst. dist. offr., Klang, May, 1899.

HARRISON, CUTHBERT E.—App'td. consular agt., Oil Rivers Prot., June, 1891; ag. vice-consul, 1892 to 1895; made dist. comsnr., and served through Brass punitive expedition (medal and clasp), 1895; app'td. political offr., Niger column, Aro Field Force, 1902 (medal and clasp); ag. div. comsnr., Cross River division, Apl.-Sept., 1904.

HARRISON, JAMES HERBERT HUGH.—Ed. Regent's Park Coll., and New College (Lond.), and at University Coll. and Hospital; F.R.C.P. (Lond.); M.R.C.S. (Eng.); dist. mag. and dist. surg., Cayo, Br. Honduras, 1890; ag. dist. surg., Orange Walk, 1891, 1892; dist. comsnr. and dist. surg., Orange Walk, June, 1892.

HARRISON, JOHN B., C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1856; Nat. Sc. scholar, Christ's Coll., Camb., B.A. (Nat. Sc. Tripos), 1887; M.A., 1887; prof. of chemistry and agricul. science, Barbados, Sept., 1879; govt. analyst and prof. of chem., Br. Guiana, Dec., 1889; is F.C.S., F.G.S., and F.I.C., and author of several papers relating to the agriculture and geology of the W. Indies.

HARRISSON, SYDNEY THIRWLAW.—Ed. Blackheath; app'td. asst. acct., P.W.D., G. C. Col., 9th Nov., 1894; ch. acct., 2nd June, 1897; asst. acct., W.A.F.F., 26th Feb., 1898; ch. acct., 8th Nov., 1899; treas. N. Nig., 1st Jan., 1900.

HART, ANTHONY DE LLANO.—Storekeeper, Trinidad, Oct., 1866; office of the ct. of intendant, 1868; office of stip. mag., Port of Spain, 1868; marshal's office, June, 1869; clk. at govt. house, Sept., 1869; promoted to the customs dept., 1871; landing waiter, 1872, and inspr. of Venezuelan vessels in addition to other duties, 1876.

HART, JOHN HINCHLEY.—Supt. King's house, gardens and grounds, Jamaica, during their formation, Nov., 1875; supt. govt. cinchona plantations, Oct., 1881; ag. dir. pub. gardens and plantations, July, 1886; ch. botanical dept.; Trinidad, Mar., 1887; is a fellow Linn. Soc.; author of a "Botanist's Ramble in Cent. America," a work on cacao culture, curing, and fermenting (1891), a report on forest conservation (1891), and pamphlets relating to the cultivation of W. Indian products; engaged by the govt. of the Windward Is. to inspect and report on the working of the botanical stations, St. Vincent and Grenada (1890); lieut. Trinidad rifle volra., Mar., 1889; capt., 1897; capt. reserve, 1898; govt. member, Trinidad public library; ex. off. mem. agri. soc., Trinidad; supt. botanic station, Tobago; author of paper on rubber cultivation in West Indies.
HART, W. EDWARD.—Librarian, public library and museum, Mauritius, 1st Oct., 1903.

HARTE, LOVELACE FRED. EDWARD RALPH.—First class certif. at I.C. Coll. in Dec., 1875; articled law student, 3rd Jan., 1876; ag. clk. of the peace the same year; Jan., 1877, clk. in customs dept., Trinidad; clk. in audit office, Sept., 1878; 7th clk., Jan., 1883, June, 1887; 2nd clk., regar., sup., 1887; ch. clk., 1897; also comsrr. of oaths for whole island; ag. dep. regar. on occasions in 1896-7-8.
HARTLEY, CHAS.—M.A., Cantab.; lecturer in English and modern languages, Royal Coll., Ceylon, 11th Dec., 1896; principal, Royal Coll., 23rd June, 1903.

HARTLEY, E. B., C.M.G. (1901).—Surg.-Lieut.-Col., C.M.R.; created V.C. for gallantry in the operations against the stronghold of the Basuto chief Moroe; prin. med. offr., Cape col. forces, 1878; served through the Gaika, Morosi, and Basuto wars, 1878-81; govt. med. offr., Basutoland, 1874-7; ret., 1904.

HARVEY, THE HON. A. W.—Formerly mem. Newfoundland legisla. coun.; mem. exec. coun. (without portfolio), 1889 to 1894, and again Dec., 1894, to Dec., 1895; mem. of off. and legislative delegations to London on fisheries question in 1890 and 1891 respectively; on special mission to Madrid, 1892, in connection with commercial negotiations with Spain.

HARVEY, GEORGE LEONARD, C.E.—Asst. dir. of wks., So. Nigeria, June, 1900; asst. dir. of pub. wks., 1901; deputy dir. of pub. wks., 1901; ag. dir. of pub. wks., Mar. to Nov., 1902, and Apr. to Nov., 1904.
HARWARD, JOHN.—Principal, Royal Coll., Ceylon, 1st Apl., 1892; dir. of pub. instn., 23rd June, 1903.

HASTINGS, W. C. HOLLAND.—H.M.'s navy, 1865-81; retired with rank of comdr.; inspr. of imigrts., Jamaica, Nov., 1881, to May, 1889; coroner, Nov., 1886, to May, 1888; ch. of pol. and ag. town warden, St. Lucia, May to Dec., 1889; also for two months harbourm. and pilot; asst. harbourm., Hong Kong, Dec., 1889, to Feb., 1899; ag. harbourm., Feb., 1891, to Apr., 1892; ag. pol. mag., Apr., 1892, to May, 1895; ag. capt. supt. of pol. and supt. fire brigade, May, 1895, to Apr., 1896; ag. harbourm., June to Dec., 1895; ag. pol. mag., Apr., 1896, to Apr., 1897, and Feb., 1898, to Feb., 1899; postmr.-gen., Feb., 1899; seconded to bury the dead in plague epidemic, 1894, June to Aug., inclusive; mem. legis. coun., Nov., 1891, to Apr., 1892, and again from June, 1895, to Oct., 1896; mem. of various comsrs. and comtees.
HATCHER, C. M.—Apptd., after compet exam., clk. 2nd div. civ. ser., and assigned to W.O., 11 Sept., 1893; transf'd to C.O., 18 Mar., 1896; astt. to chief regar., Jan., 1900.
HATHERLEY, GEORGE MOOR.—2nd lieut. roy. artilly., Nov., 1890; lieut., Nov., 1893; apptd. with local rank of capt. to commd. of permanent force at Albany, W. Australia, July, 1899.
HATHERTON, BARON.—Col. the Hon. Edward George Percy Littleton, C.M.G. (1880); b. 1842; late mil. sec. to the gov.-gen. of Canada.

HAVELOCK, SIR ARTHUR ELIRANK, G.C.M.G. (1896), G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E. (1896), K.C.M.G. (1884).—B. 1844; entered the army, 32nd lt. infy., Jan., 1862; capt., 1873; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to the offr. admin.ing. the govt. of Mauritius from July, 1873, to Jan., 1874; ag. ch. div. comsrr., Seychelles Is., from Apr., 1874, until Feb., 1875; mem. of the exec. and legis. couns. of Fiji, May, 1875; apptd. (provisionally) col. sec. and rec.-gen., Fiji, 1st Sept., 1875; pres. of Nevis, 1877; admin.ing. the govt. of St. Lucia from June, 1878, to Aug., 1879; ch. civ. comsrr., Seychelles, Oct., 1879; gov. W. Africa Sttlmts., 1881; Apr., 1881, sent on a mission to Paris in conjunction with Mr. (now Sir A.) Hemming, of the C.O., to assist in negotiating the settlement of certain questions at issue between Great Britain and France, with regard to territory on the W. Coast of Africa; apptd. H.M.'s consul for Liberia, Dec., 1881; negotiated the sttlmt. of a conterminous boundary between S. Leone and Liberia, and inquired into the claims of Br. subjects against the Liberian Govt., Mar., 1882; gov. of Trinidad, Dec., 1884; gov. of Natal and special comsrr. for Zulu affairs, 1885; also gov. of Zululand, 1887; attended internat. confee. at Brussels on the suppression of the slave trade, 1889; gov. of Ceylon, 1890; of Madras, 1895 to 1900; of Tasmania, 1901 to 1904.

HAY, SIR JAMES SHAW, K.C.M.G. (1889), C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1839; joined 89th (Princess Victoria's) regt. in 1858; served in India during the latter part of the Mutiny; was adjt. for some time, and afterwards instructor of musketry; ag. adjt. to the provisional depot batt. at Dum Dum in 1859; adjt. to the Glasgow Highland volrs. in 1868 at their formation; asst. inspr., G. Coast armed native pol., 1875; dist. comsrr. of Accra, Oct., 1873; inspr.-gen., G. Coast constaby., Apr., 1877; astt. col. sec. G. Coast col., 1878; had charge of several special missions to the tribes in the interior; ag. col. sec. from Oct., 1878, to Jan., 1880, and again, Aug., 1880; inspr.-gen. of pol., Mauritius; admstr. of the Gambia, 1886; administd. the govt. of the W. Africa Sttlmts. from July, 1896, to Dec., 1897; gov., Sierra Leone, Oct., 1888; served in the Yonnie expedit., 1887-8 (medal and clasp); ag. admstr., Barbados, 1891; gov., Barbados, 1892 to 1900.

HAYCRAFT, THOS. WAGTAFFE.—B.A., St. John's Coll., Oxon.; called to the bar, In. Tem., 1885; practised in S.E. circuit and Sussex sessions; examir. of the ct., 1889 to 1899; apptd. arbitrator on bd. of Lon. chain. of arbitration, 1897; pres. dist. ct., Larnaca, Cyprus, 1899; ag. Queen's advocate, Oct., 1900, to Feb., 1901; author of "Executive Powers in relation to Crime and Disorder."

HAYNES, THOS. BENJAMIN.—Apptd. dispenser, penal settlement, Massaruni, B. Guiana, 6th June, 1870; 1st cls. prison warder, 12th July, 1877; schlstr. to penal settmts., 1st Sept., 1878; dispenser in charge of leper asylum, Kaow Is., 27th Oct., 1879; clk. to penal settmts., 27th Oct., 1883; keeper, New Amsterdam prison, Berbice, 5th July, 1887; supt., Georgetown prison, 19th June, 1894.
HAYNES, W. LINDSAY H.—Ck. to ch. just., Barbados, 1874-8; ck. to comnrs. apptd. to investigate offences against rioters, 1876; inl. rev. offr., dist. B, 1878; now inspr. of inl. rev. offrs. regir.

HAZELAND, FRANCIS ARTHUR.—Ck. to puisne judge, Hong Kong, Nov., 1878; ag. dep. regir., Mar. to Nov., 1882; ck. to ch. just., Sept., 1883; ag. ch. clk. col. sec. office, Mar. to Oct., 1890; ag. clk. of coun., Mar. to Dec., 1890; dep. regir. and acctnt., Jan., 1896; J.P., 1894; has passed in Chinese (higher standard); called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, Hilary, 1900; ag. pol. mag., June, 1900, to June, 1901; 2nd pol. mag., 7th June, 1901.
HEALY, P. J.—Crown prosecutor, S. dist., N.S. Wales, Jan., 1878; ditto, S.W. dist., 1879; crown prosecutor, Sydney, Jan., 1882.
HEARSEY, H.—Apptd. med. offr., B. Cent. Africa Prot., 6th June, 1896; principal ditto, 18th June, 1902.
HECTOR, SIR JAMES, M.D., F.R.S., K.C.M.G. (1887), C.M.G. (1875).—B. 1834; dir. of geological surveys and curator of the col. museum, N. Zealand.
HEIDENSTAM, DR. F. C., C.M.G. (1884).—Ch. med. offr., Cyprus, 1882.

HELY-HUTCHINSON, THE HON. SIR WALTER FRANCIS, G.C.M.G. (1897), K.C.M.G. (1888), C.M.G. (1883).—B. 1849; B.A. of Trin. Coll., Camb., called to the bar by the soc. of the Inner Tem.; accompanied Sir Hercules Robinson to Fiji as special attaché on the occasion of the cession of the islands to Gr. Britain, in Sept., Oct., 1874; priv. sec. to Sir Hercules Robinson, 10th Oct., 1874, for Fiji affairs; priv. sec. for N.S. Wales affairs, Jan., 1875; col. sec. of Barbados, Nov., 1877; ch. sec. to govt. of Malta, 1883; and lieut.-gov. and ch. sec. to govt., 1884; gov. of Windward Is., 1889; gov. of Natal, 1893; gov. Cape, 1901.

HEMERY, PERCY.—lst. clk., govt. savings bank, Br. Guiana, July, 1880; 3rd clk., treas., Jan., 1881; cashier, Aug., 1881; ag. ch. clk., 1884 and 1885; acctnt., govt. savings bank, Apr., 1888; ag. col. book-keeper, July, 1888, and Aug., 1890; ag. asst. rec-gen., and ag. sub-admstr., Berbice, Feb., 1891; ag. dep. rec-gen., Apr., 1891, May to Dec., 1892, May, 1893-1894, twice in 1895-6; sec. cattle diseases coman., Oct., 1891; asst. rec-gen., Berbice, June, 1897; J.P., and comsnr. of oaths and affid., June, 1897; sub-admstr-gen., Berbice, Nov., 1897 (additional); ag. compt. of cust., 22nd Feb., 1904.

HEMMING, SIR A. W. L., G.C.M.G. (1900), K.C.M.G.(1890), C.M.G. (1885).—B. 1841; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. in the office of the sec. of state for the cols., Feb., 1860; promoted Oct., 1864, 1st junior class; priv. sec. to Sir F. Rogers (now Lord Blachford), 20th May, 1867, till 20th May, 1871; priv. sec. to Mr. Herbert, 21st May, 1871, till 29th Sept., 1872; apptd. a 2nd class clk., 30th Sept., 1872; 1st class clk., Nov., 1874; priv. sec. to the Earl of Cadogan, 2nd March, 1878, to 30th June, 1879; sent on a special mission to Paris, Apr., 1879; prin. clk., 1st July, 1879; again proceeded on special service to Paris, May, 1881; Br. deleg. to the W. African confce. at Berlin, 1884; on special service at Paris, 1889-90 and 1894, in connection with the delimitation of French and English possessions on the W. coast of Africa; gov. of Br. Guiana, Mar., 1896; gov. of Jamaica, 1898 to 1904.
HENDERSON, A.—Apptd. clk., refugee camps, O.R.C., 1st Nov., 1901; clk. attached to audit dept., April, 1902; clk., treasy., 20th Aug., 1902.
HENDERSON, J. T.—Shorthand reporter of debates, legis. coun., Natal, 1883.

HENDERSON, W. R., C.M.G. (1901), M.D., and M.C., Dublin (1878).—B. 1855; entered army med. staff, Mar., 1880; served in India, and in Egypt during Soudan and Nile campaigns; senr. med. offr., imperial troops, Cape Coast Castle, 1886, and at S. Leone, 1888; ret. from army as capt. Feb., 1889; now on reserve of officers; entered Lagos med. serv., Oct., 1889; prim. med. offr. of expedn. against the Jebus, 1892, ment. in desps. and name brought to the notice of S. of S., received medal and clasp; during serv. in Lagos was in charge of secretariat, ag. trav. comsnr., and polit. offr. at Jebu Ode; prim. med. offr. of col. troops, Ashanti expdn., 1895-6 (ment. in desps. and received star); apptd. prim. med. offr., G. coast, June, 1897; accompanied late Sir W. Maxwell on expdttn. to N. Territories, July to Nov., 1897, and received thanks of H.M.'s govt. for services; mem. of comttee at C.O. for re-organisation of W. African med. staff, Oct., 1901, and received thanks of S. of S.

HENRY, CHARLES LOWICK.—Sen. clerical ast. inspr., prisons office, Br. Guiana, 1881; ag. clk. of supplies and sec. to the bd. of prisons, July to Oct., 1885; July to Sept., 1888; and June, 1890, to Jan., 1891; issuer to convict prison, Rio Massaruni, Nov., 1890; clk. of supplies and sec. to bd. of prisons, June, 1892; ag. supt. orphan asyl., Nov. to Dec., 1895, and Feb. to July, 1896; ag. man. dir. orphan asyl., in addition to duties as clk. of supplies, from July to Nov., 1898, May, 1899, to Jan., 1900, Nov. to Dec., 1900; ag. supt., orphan asyl., July, 1901, to Jan., 1903; ag. supt., almshouse, and sec., poor law comnrs., June, 1903.
HENKEL, C. C. H.—Conservator of forests, Transkeian territories, Cape Col., July, 1889.

HERBERT, THE HON. SIR ROBERT G. W., G.C.B. (1892), K.C.B. (1882), D.C.L. (Oxon.), LL.D. (Camb.).—B. 1831; ed. at Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxon. of which he was elected scholar in 1849; Hertford scholar, 1861; Ireland scholar, 1852; Latin verse prize, 1852; Eldon scholar, 1854; elected fellow of All Souls, 1854; priv. sec. to the chanr. of the excheq. (The Right Hon. W. E. Gladstone), 1855; called to the bar at the Inner Tem., 1858; col. sec. of Queensland, 1859; premier of Queensland and mem. of legis. assem. from 1860 to 1865; one of the ast. secs. to the Bd. of Trade from 1868 to Feb., 1870, apptd. ast. under-sec. of state for the cols.; permanent under-sec. of state for the cols., 21st May, 1871, to 1st Feb., 1892; is a mag. and dep.-lieut. of the co. of Cambridge; seec. of the Order St. Michael and St. George, May, 1877; ret. 1st Feb., 1892; chancellor of the Order of St. M. and St. G., 1892; agt.-agt.-gen. for Tasmania, 1893-6; ag. under sec. of state for the col., Oct., 1899, to June, 1900.

HERCHENRODER, FURCY ALFRED.—Ed. Roy. Coll., Mauritius; called to bar, Mid.Tem., June, 1888; ag. crown pros., Mauritius, 1893-8; leg. adviser, pol. mag., crown pros., conservr. of mortgages, and curator of vacant estates, Seychelles, May, 1898; judge Seychelles, 1900.

HEREFORD, GEORGE A.—Cadet, S. Sttlmnts., Nov., 1898; ag. dist. officer, Balik Pulau, Penang, Apr., 1899; passed final exam. in Malay, Oct., 1901; supt. of educn., Penang, Nov., 1902; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, Jan., 1904.

HERVEY, DUDLEY FRANCIS AMELIUS, C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1849; ed. at Marlborough; cadet, S. Sttlmnts., May, 1867; passed in Malay in Nov., 1868; ag. priv. sec. to gov. in Mar., 1869; ch. clk. and interp. to the lieut.-gov., Penang, Jan., 1870, in which year he accompanied H.M.S. "Algerine" to inquire into various cases of piracy on the coast of Aceh; sen. sworn clk., sup. ct., Singapore, Jan., 1871, also clk. in bankruptcy, and J.P. and ag. mag., Sept., 1871; Aug., 1878, pol. mag., Malacca; has accompanied various expedts. to the different native states of the peninsula; J.P.; res. councillor, Malacca, 1882; also supt. of Negri Sembilan, 1883-6; ret., Dec., 1893.
HESKETH, GILBERT WOOD.—Ed. Birkenhead, and Caughton, Ches.; engineer, corporation, City of Cape Town engine works; joined service of P.W.D., O.R.C., 2nd July, 1903.

HEWITT-FLETCHER, STANLEY.—Passed exams. and admitted mem. of Institute of Chartered Actuaries, England and Wales, June, 1892; 2nd acctnt., B.C.A. prot., 1st June, 1893; ag. ch. acctnt., 12th Aug., 1894 to 31st Mar., 1896, 11th May, 1887 to 4th Jan., 1898, and 6th Sept., 1899 to 26th Mar., 1900; collr. and mag., Zomba, Apl., 1900; ag. British agent and vice-consul, Chinde, July, 1900 to 31st Dec., 1900; apptd. to that post, 1st Jan., 1901; ag. vice-consul, Quillimane, Apl., 1902 to 31st Dec., 1903; British agent and vice-consul, Chinde, 1904; has recd. the B.C.A. medal and clasp, 1894-8.

HEWICK, JOHN EDWIN.—Ed. Highgate Schl.; ast. supt. of pol., P.W., 1872; during the Perak war commanded an expdt. across the country in pursuit of Sultan Ismail. Called to the bar of the Mid. Tem., 1880; stip. mag. Br. Guiana, 1883; sheriff of Essequibo, 1884; sheriff of Berbice, 1889; stip. mag. east and west bank, New Amsterdam, 1892; Berbice, 1893.
HEYWOOD, A. W.—Dist. forest offr., Cape, 1888; conservator, Knyasa, Oct., 1895.
HEYWOOD, J. B.—Sec. to treas., N. Zealand, 1890.

HICKSON, ROBERT R. PURDON, M.I.C.E.—Res. engr., Carlingford harbour wks., Ireland, 1886 to 1872; res. engr., Barrow harbour wks., England, 1872-6; engrn.-in-ch. harbours and jetties, S. Australia, 1876 to 1881; astt. engrn. harbours and rivers, N.S. Wales, 1881-9; comsnr. and engrn.-in-ch. roads, bridges, and sewerage, 1889 to 1895; under sec. for pub. wks., and comsnr. for roads, 1896; vice-pres. of pub. wks. tender bd., and chmn. of deptmtl. bd. of reference, N.S.W.; J.P. for S.A. and N.S.W.

HILL, CHARLES W., F.R.G.S.—Ck., lower div., ch. sec.'s office, Cyprus, Jan., 1886; passed in Greek, May, 1888; acted as ck., exec. coun., Aug., 1889, to Mar., 1890; and as priv. sec. to admtr., Oct., 1889, to Mar., 1890; govt. clk., shipping master, clk. of couns., and J.P., Falkland Islds., 1892; also priv. sec. to gov.; ag. col. treas., collr. of customs, postmr., &c., 1893; mem. exec. and legis. couns.; ag. col. sec., &c., 1894; special comnr. on land question, W. Falkland, 1895; ag. inspr. of schools, 1896; special mission to S. Chili, 1897; ag. col. sec., Apr. to Oct., 1897; col. treas., collr. of cust., and postmr., Falkland Islds., Oct., 1897; ag. col. sec., May, 1899, to June, 1900; senr. astt. treas., G. Coast, 1901.

HILL, DAVID YOUNG CAMPBELL.—As 6th clk., treas., Br. Guiana, July, 1874; ag. 4th clk., Apr., 1875, to Feb., 1876; ag. 1st clk., Oct., 1877, to May, 1878; provisional 3rd clk., audit office, May, 1878; 2nd clk., treas., 1880; ch. clk., 1881; col. book-keeper, 1886; ag. receiver-gen., 1887; again 1888; dep. rec. gen., Apr., 1891; acted as rec.-gen. at various periods from 1891 to 1898; dir. pub. offrs. widows and orphans fund, 1896.

HILL, E. C. H.—Cadet, S. Stlitma, Mar., 1875; ag. treas., Malacca, Mar. to July, 1876; ag. inspr. of schls., June to Oct., 1877; ag. collr. of land revenue, Penang and Province Wellesley, Oct., 1877; ag. inspector of schools, Feb., 1879; supt. of educn., Penang, and ag. 2nd mag., June, 1880; ag. mag., P. W., 1881, inspr. of schls., S. S., 1883; ag. res. coun. Malacca, Apr., 1896, to Feb., 1896; 1st mag. Penang, 1897; auditor-gen., S. S., Apr., 1897.

HILL, J. K. D.—Entered treasy., Br. Guiana, Sept., 1879; 6th clk., Feb., 1881; 2nd book-keeper, aud. office, Apr., 1881; 1st ditto, Mar., 1882; 2nd class clk., Apr., 1889; 1st ditto, July, 1889; sec. P.W. dept. coman. of enquiry, 1889; ag. ch. clk., 1890, 1894, and 1895; ag. ch. clk., audit office, 1894-8; apptd., Sept., 1896, acctnt. sup. of civ. justice, and acctnt. regar.'s office; student Linc.'s Inn, July, 1898; passed final exam., Mar., 1899; stip. mag., J.P., and coroner, July, 1899.

HILL, P. H. R.—Ck. and book-keeper, pol. estabmt., Br. Guiana, Jan., 1867; sen. inspr. of pol., July, 1868; ag. inspr. gen. of pol., July, 1872, to Apr., 1873; ag. stip. mag., Apr., 1875, to Nov., 1876, and on other occasions; astt. pol. mag. for Georgetown, Aug., 1880; ag. sheriff of Essequibo and stip. mag., Dec., 1880, to Nov., 1881; ag. pol. mag. of Georgetown and sheriff of Demerara, 1882; chmn. poor law coman., Apr., 1885; ag. recr.-gen. at various times, 1883-5; dir. of public offrs. widows and orphans fund, 1896.
HILLMAN, GEORGE FRANCIS.—Ed. Highbury New Park Coll., Lond.; admitted to the bar of supreme ct., W. Aust., 1898; apptd. clk. of legis. coun. and clk., of parlts., June, 1901.
HILLIER, H. K.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk., of the 2nd div. of the civil service, and assigned to the C. O., July, 1900.

HIME, RT. HON. LIBUT.-COL. SIR A.H., P.C. (1902), K.C.M.G. (1900), C.M.G. (1876), (late R.E.).—B. 1842; entered roy. engrs. in 1861; became capt. in 1874; employed in Bermuda under col. govt. from 1869 to 1871 in construction of a causeway and iron swing bridge connecting the island of St. George with the main island, for which he received the thanks of the legislature and the acknowledgments of the S. of S. for the cols.; col. engrn. of Natal, May, 1875; mem. of exec. and legis. couns., 1876; employed on survey of boundary between Orange Free State and Natal, 1884-5; ret., 1893; mem. legis. assem., 1897; min. of canals and works, 1897; premier, Natal, 1899.
HISLOP, THE HON. T. W. THOMAS.—Col. sec., N. Zealand, 1889-90; apptd. offr. of legion of honour for services at Paris Exhib., 1889.

HITCHINS, ALFRED W.—Ck., col. sec.'s office, Jamaica, 1868; clk., customs, Kingston, 1869; landing waiter, 1870; astt. inspr. of invoices, 1877; ag. astt. survr., June, 1877; chief tide survr., Apr., 1882; landing survr., May, 1883; has acted on several occasions as collr. of customs, Kingston.

HIXSON, FRANCIS, late master, R.N.—Served 15 years on Australian station, principally in surveying and exploring service; supt. of pilots, lighthouses, and harbours, N. S. Wales, Jan., 1863; organised N. S. W. Naval Bde., 1863, and still commands it; pres. marine board, Apr., 1872.

HOAD, COLONEL JOHN CHARLES, C.M.G. (1900).—B. 1856; dep. adj.-gen. and chief staff offr., commonwealth forces of Australia. Lieut. 1884; adjt., mounted rifles, 1886; capt., 1887; maj., 1891; 2nd in command mtd. rif., 1892; astt.-adj.-gen. and lieut.-col., 1896; col., 1899; reprd. headqtrs. staff, Vict., at Queen's Jubilee, London, 1897; attached to personal staff of Field-Marshal Lord Roberts; S. Afr. War, 1899-1900, cmd. the first Austr. regt., converted regt. into mtd. inf., Bloemfontein, Johannesburg, Pretoria; A.A.G. mtd. inf.; mentd. in desps., C.M.G., three clasps; apptd. A.D.C. to H.E. the govr.-gen. of the commonw.; apptd. ch. of staff for all mil. arrangements for reception in Vict. of H.R.H. the Duke of Cornwall and York, and opening of first commonw. parl.
HOBBS, STEPHEN BARTON.—Lower mechanical clk., surrv.-gen's. dept., Mauritius, 1st Jan., 1866; govt. surrv., 25th Apr., 1892; on spec. duty at Seychelles, 1897-1900.

HOBSON, WILLIAM EDWARD.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in lower (now 2nd) div. of the civ. serv., and assigned to the office of the comsrs. of H.M.'s wrks. and pub. bldgs., 1st Jan., 1885; transf'd. to the C.O., April, 1891; asst. reg'ar., 1889; promoted to higher grade, Jan., 1900.

HOCKING, SIR HENRY HICKS, KT. BACH. (1895).—B. 1842; ed. at St. John's Coll., Oxford, where he graduated B.A. in 1864, and B.C.L. in 1867, 2nd class mods., 1st class law and history; Vinerian scholar, 1866; called to the bar, Inner Temp., Nov., 1867, and joined the home circuit; atty.-gen. for W. Australia, 1872; ag. ch. just., 1873; ag. ch. just., Gibraltar, 1879; atty.-gen., Jamaica, 1880; ret., 1896; acted as legal adviser to C.O. for some months in 1897.

HODGE, LEONARD PERCIVAL, F.S.I., Assoc. M.Inst.C.E.—Ed. Queen's Coll., Taunton, and King's Coll., Lond.; honors construc., 1886; 2nd cl. supt., P.W., Jamaica, 1891; 1st cl. ditto, and attached to head off., 1892; dir. of roads, G. Coast, 1894, but did not take up apptn.; col. engr. and surg. gen., Dominica, 1898.

HODGSON, SIR FREDERICK MITCHELL, K.C.M.G. (1899), C.M.G. (1891).—B. 1851; entered Imp. P.O. and apptd., after compet. exam., Feb., 1869; was employed in the sec.'s office in connection with the transfer of the telegraphs to the state, and apptd. clk. in that office, 1870; apptd., after further exam., to the 3rd class (grade I) of the sec.'s office, Aug., 1876; promoted to 2nd class, Aug., 1880; was sen. capt. of the 24th Middlesex (post office), R.V.; postm.-gen., Br. Guiana, Jan., 1882; col. sec., G. Coast Sept., 1888; adminstd. the govt., June, 1889, to Feb., 1890; June to Nov., 1891; Aug., 1893, to Mar., 1894; Apr. to Oct., 1896; and Dec., 1897, to March, 1898; major comdg. the G. Coast R.V., which he raised in 1892; gov., G. Coast, 1898; gov. Barbados, Oct., 1900; gov., Br. Guiana, 16th July, 1904.
HOFMEYR, JAN HENDRIK.—Formerly mem. for Stellenbosch in Cape house of assembly; mem. of exec. coun., 1881; deleg. to col. conf'ee., 1887 and 1894.

HOGARD, ALFRED HENRY.—Accompanied Lieut.-Col. Durnford, Langalibalele expdn., 1873; ag. clk. and French interp. to R.M., Durban, 1875-76; served in Natal Guides in Zulu War (medal and clasp); 3rd class clk. to R.M., Durban, 1885; 2nd class clk., 1890; rec. offr., govt. savings bk., Durban, and field cornet, 1891.

HOLDER, THE HON. FREDERICK WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1850; mem. of house of assem. S. Australia, 1887; treasr. 1889 to Aug., 1890; premier and treasr., June to Oct., 1892; comsrr. of public works, June, 1893, to Apr., 1894; treasr., April, 1894, to Dec., 1899; premier and treasr., Dec., 1899, to May, 1901; mem., lse. of represent., Aust'n. parl't., 1901; speaker, 1901.

HOLLAND, BERNARD H., C.B. (1904).—Ed. at Eton and Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to bar, Inner Temp., 1882; in office of charity comsrr., 1884-1890; priv. sec. to Duke of Devonshire, 1892-1894; sec. to royal comsrr. on financial relations between Great Britain and Ireland, 1894-1896; to Ld. Rothschild's comittee, on old-age pensions, 1896-1898; to Transvaal concessions comsrr., 1900-1901; to royal comsrr. on Port of London, 1901-2; and to royal comsrr. on war in S. Africa, 1902-3; apptd. priv. sec. to Right Hon. Alfred Lyttelton, S. of S. for the colonies, 1903; author of "Imperium et Libertas," etc.
HOLLAND, J.—Apptd. after exam., 3rd cl. messenger, C.O., 18th Aug., 1902; 2nd cl. ditto, 10th June, 1904.

HOLLINGSWORTH, A. H.—A.M. Inst. C.R.; ed. King's Coll., London, engineering dept.; art. pupil, borough and water engrn., Croydon; asst. borough and water engrn., Croydon; asst. engrn., P.W.D., Hong-Kong, Dec., 1896; ag. exec. engrn., Aug., 1900; ag. consulting sanitary survr., Sept., 1900; exec. engrn., 1st Jan., 1902.
HOLMES, JOHN RICHARD.—S.S.C., 1879; barrister, Mid. Tem., 1889; dist. comsrr., G. Coast, 1889; commanded detachment in Awoonah expdn., 1889-91; pres. dist. ct., Cyprus, 1899.
HOLROYD, SIR E. D., KT. BACH. (1903).—Puiane judge, Victoria.
HOLTZE, MAURICE WM., F.L.S., etc.—Govt. gardener, Port Darwin, 1878; botanic gardener, 1880; dir. botanic garden, Adelaide, 1891.

HOMAGE, JAMES FRANCIS.—Court messenger, St. Helena, 1889; clk. to ch. just., 1882; man. govt. savings bk., 1865; clk. of the peace, taxing master, and registr. sup. ct., 1867; legal adviser, crown prosecutor, and registr. V.-A., 1870; also supervisor of customs and registr. o shipping, in 1883; admly. advc., 1890.
HOMBURG, ROBERT.—Mem. of H. of Ass., S. Aust., since 1884; atty.-gen., 1890-92 and 1892-3; ditto and min. of educn., 1904.

HONEY, JOHN WM.—Served under Capt.gort., 1880 to 1901; transf'd. to Transvaal when collr. and prin. controller of cust. and prin. registr. of shipping; served in Gaika-Galeka and Basutoland campaigns, holding comsrr. in latter as lieut. Capetown Rifles; dir. of cust., Transvaal, and cust. adviser to high comsrr., 16th May, 1901; M.L.C., Mem. I.C.C.; chrmn., tender and post office investment boards.

HONIBALL, OSCAR DUNCOMBE, M.D., M.Ch., Queen's Univ. Ireland (scholar in mathematics and honourman in nat. phil.).—Govt. med. offr., Antigua, 1868; jun. and sen. med. offr., public hosp., Kingston, Jamaica, 1869-1870; gov't. med. offr., 1870-1872; med. supt., Imigrts. Union hosp., St. Thomas-in-the-E., Jamaica, 1872; dist. med. offr., imigrn. dept., Br. Guiana, 1879; is a fellow of the Obstetrical Soc. of Gt. Britain and Ireland; and J.P. and coroner for Br. Guiana.

HOOD, SAMUEL J.—Asst. warehouse keeper, Br. Hosp., Oct., 1887; 4th clk., treas. and cust., Mar., 1891; 3rd clk., Jan., 1893; clk., reg.-gen., June, 1893; superv. of cust., Gold Coast, Feb., 1894; inspgr. superv., Nov., 1898; asst. col. of cust., S. Leone, Jan., 1901; ag. collr., Feb. to Aug., 1901; asst. comp. of cust., Gold Coast, Oct., 1901; ag. compt., Feb. to June, and Aug. to Dec., 1902, and Aug. 1903 to Feb., 1904; collr. of cust., Lagos, June, 1904.

HOOD, THOS.—Ed. City of Lond. schl., Univ. Coll., and St. Bart.'s hosp., London; M.R.C.S. Eng.; L.R.C.P., Lond.; asst. col. surg., S. Leone, 1897; served on staff during native rising, 1898 (W. African medal and clasp, 1898); comsrr. of Bandajuma dist., 1899; senior med. offr., 1902; ag. prin. med. offr., Oct. 1903 to Mar., 1904; J.T. for the colony.

HOPE, J. W.—Med. offr., convict estabmt., Fremantle; med. supt., invalid dépôt; med. offr., native penal settlement, Rottnest Is.; and health offr., Port of Fremantle, W. Australia, July, 1844; dist. med. offr. and quarantine offr., Fremantle, 1895.

HOPKINS, EDWARD FRANCIS.—Ed. at Portora roy. schl., Enniskillen, and Trin. Coll., Dub.; roy. scholarship, 1869; 1st honors in classics; honors in mathematics; B.A. in 1873; writer, Ceylon civ. ser.; July, 1874; pol. mag., 1877; dist. judge, Matare, 1887; asst. govt. agt., Vavuniya and Mullaitivu, Dec., 1891; ditto, Hambantota, 1896; dist. judge, Kalutara, Apr., 1897; Negombo, Sept., 1898; Batticaloa, Dec., 1899; Negombo, June, 1900; regisr.-gen., 23rd Nov., 1903.

HOPEY, WILLIAM MUSGROVE.—B.A. (Can-tab.), 1878; called to bar (Inner Temple), 1878; joined Cape sup. ct. bar, 1878; ag. crown prosecutor (Grigualand W.), July, 1886; crown prosecutor (Grigualand W.), Feb., 1886; Q.C., 1890; a judge of the Cape sup. ct. and assigned to the high court of Grigualand, Mar., 1892.

HORE, ERNEST HENRY.—Entered Imp. postal ser., July, 1871; transf'd to Cyprus for special service, Oct., 1878; entered col. ser., Aug., 1882; as sub. postmr., Limasol; acted several times as ch. clk.; ch. clk., Feb., 1890; acted several times as Istd. postmr.; apptd. Istd. postmr., Apr., 1893.

HORNY-BRITTON, CHARLES.—Apptd. dist. comsrr., Lagos, 1897; 2nd asst. col. sec., 1901; senior asst. col. sec., 1903; ag. col. treas., 1902-1903; ag. col. sec., 1904; holds dormant comsrr. to sit as pol. mag., and sat as such in 1897, 1901, 1902 and 1903; on spec. serv. to Ijebu-Ode, 1901 and 1904, and to Oyo and also to Ife, 1904; pres. of pol. comsrr., 1901; mem. of riyw. comsrr., 1901; pres. of cust. comsrr., 1904.
HORNE, C. A.—C.C. and R.M., Bredasdorp, Cape Col., June, 1886.
HORNE, W. H.—Ch. clk. survr.-gen.'s office, Cape, Aug., 1897.

HORWOOD, SIR WM. HENRY, KT. BACH. (1904).—Called to the bar, 1886; bencher of the law society, Newfoundland, 1891; Q.C., 1895; elected to the legislature, 1894; acted as col. sec. 1894-5; delegate from the Newfoundland govt. to the Ottawa conf., Apr., 1896, on the subject of confedn. with Canada; mem. of ex. coun., 1894-97; ag. atty.-gen., 1897; elected to legislature to represent Harbour Grace, 1897; apptd. by ch. just., with minister of just. to act in conjunction with judges as committee to amend rules and procedure, sup. court, 1899; minister of just. and atty.-gen., 1900; ex officio mem. of ex. coun., and of treasy. board; acted as leader of govt. during sess. of 1901; and also in 1902; apptd. ch. just., July, 1902; administered govt., Sept., 1902.
HOSE, CHARLES, D.Sc.—Cadet Sarawak service, Mar., 1884; res. 2nd cl., 4th div., Jan., 1891; res. 3rd Div., and mem. of sup. coun., May, 1904.

HOWARD, E. C. C.—Ed. at Dulwich Coll.; cadet, St. Stilimta, 1890; passed final exam. in Malay, Dec., 1892; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, Mar., 1894; ag. sup't. of educn., Penang, Aug., 1894; collr. of land rev. Singapore, 1897; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, Nov., 1897; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, Aug., 1898, to June, 1899; ag. 1st mag., Penang, Sept. to Dec., 1901; 2nd mag., Penang, Apr., 1902; ag. comsrr., ct. of req., Sing., Nov., 1903; ag. 1st mag., Sing., Aug., 1904.
HOWARD, HON. OLIVER.—Honorary attache to legatn., at Tangier, June, 1899; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, 1901; 2nd cls. res., N. Nigeria, Apl., 1903.
HOWELL, A. G.—5th clk. corprudg. branch, C.S.O., Barbados, 1890; 4th clk., 1892; 3rd clk., Jan., 1898; ch. clk. comsrr. of agriculture, 1899.
HOWELL, C. G.—Sec. and actuary, Barbados savings bank, 1891.
HOWELL, E. H.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil ser. and assigned to the C.O., Mar., 1898.

HOWELL, J. B.—3rd clk., treasy., Barbados, Nov., 1886; 3rd clk., col. sec.'s office, Sept., 1891; ag. ch. clk., record branch, C.S.O., Sept., 1893, to Apr., 1894; ag. ch. clk., provost-marshal's office, Dec., 1897, to Apr., 1898; 2nd clk., C.S.O., and clk. of exec. coun. and comtee., Jan., 1898; ag. ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, May to October, 1900; ag. priv. sec. and A.D.C. to the gov. of Barbados, lat. Apr. to 6th July, 1902.
HOWES, SEYMOUR WYLDE.—Ag. 2nd treasy. offr., Montserrat, Nov., 1899; temp. offr., treasy. dept., May, 1900.

HOWLAND, SIR WM. PEARCE, K.C.M.G. (1879), C.B.—B. 1812; min. of finance for the prov. of Canada, May, 1862; rec-gen., May, 1863; postmr.-gen., Nov., 1864; min. of inland rev., 1867; lieut.-gov. of Ontario, 1868 to 1873.

HUDSON, ARTHUR.—Balliol Coll., Oxon; B.A., 1890; M.A., 1900; called to the bar (Inner Temp.), 1892; of S.E. circuit and W. Ham sessions; dist. comsrr., Sierra Leone protectorate, Oct., 1896; solr.-gen. of col. of S. L., Dec., 1897; ag. atty.-gen. on various occasions, 1897-1901; apptd. atty.-gen., 11th Aug., 1901.
HUDSON, C.—Dist. traffic man., Auckland rlys., N. Zealand, 1884; asst. gen. man., N.Z. rlys., Jan., 1895.

HUDSON, HERBERT HENRY.—Dep. registr. sup., Singapore, 1888; ag. registr., sup. ct., Penang, Mar. to Dec., 1892, and from Feb., 1893; ag. registr. sup. ct., Singapore, July, 1894; dep. registr. sup. ct. and mag., Malacca, Nov., 1894; asst. registr. sup. ct., Penang, Apr., 1896; ag. sol.-gen., Penang, May to Oct., 1900; sol.-gen., Penang, Apr., 1902.

HUGGINS, HENRY DANIEL.—4th clerk audit office, Trinidad, Nov., 1873; asst. clk. of peace, St. Joseph's, Jan., 1876; clk., Aug., 1877; ditto, and sub-registr. sup. ct., San Fernando, Sept., 1879; clk. of peace, Port of Spain, Nov., 1880; special J.P., co. St. George, Feb., 1885; asst. mag., Port of Spain, Jan., 1887; stip. J.P. and warden, counties Niagara and Nariva, Sept., 1887; stip. mag., Cedros, Feb., 1894.
HUGHES, D. B. B., M.B.C.M. (Edin.).—1st prizeman, senior surgery; medallist, anatomy and pathology; ag. surg. in charge, col. hosp., Grenada, 1900; dist. med. offr., Saint Vincent, Dec., 1900.

HULL, H.M., C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1861; ed. at Charterhouse; priv. sec. to gov., G. Coast, and clk. of legis. and exec. couns., May, 1888, to Mar., 1891; travelling comsrr., G. Coast, Mar., 1891; one of the Br. comsrrs. for the delimitation of bdry. between the G. Coast Col. and the German Protectorate of Togo, Jan., 1892; ag. dir. of telegraphs, July to November, 1892; apptd. Br. comsrr. to delimit the bdry. between the G. Coast and the Ivory Coast, 1894, the comsrr., however, did not meet; ag. dir. of telegraphs, Aug., 1895, to Feb., 1896; and mentioned in despatches for services during Ashanti expedition, 1895-96; asst. col. sec., G. Coast, Dec., 1899; ag. compt. of customs, Feb. to June, 1900; on sp. service to E. Akim during Ashanti rising, 1900, raised 3,500 native levies; ag. compr. of customs, Nov., 1901.
HULLETT, RICHMOND WM.—M.A. (Cantab); principal of Raffles Inst., S. Stltmbs., Feb., 1871; ag. insp. of schls., S.S., 1874-76; ag. dir. of pub. inst., 11th Apr., 1903.

HULSE, LEIGH R.—Ed. Belize, Br. Honduras; appren. in gen. registry, Belize, Apr., 1893; copyist, Apr., 1894; 3rd clk., col. sec.'s off., Nov., 1896; 4th clk. treas. and customs dept. and clk. to the commsn. of currency, June, 1898; 3rd clk., Sept., 1900; 2nd clk., and clk. to quarantine bd., Dec., 1901; ag. auditor, Apr., 1903.
HUMPHREYS, JOHN.—Mag.'s clk., Br. Guiana; stip. mag., May, 1897.

HUMPHRYS, OCTAVIUS.—Asst. clk., house of assm., Antigua, 1858; clk., 1865; clk. of legis. coun., 1867; clk. of commsn. of roads, 1870 to 1872; ag. provost-marshal and regiar., July, 1873; confirmed Dec., 1873; clk. of commsn. of patents, Aug., 1876; regiar. of V.-A. ct., May, 1877; nominated mem. of legis. coun., June, 1877, to Feb., 1882; also ag. mag. for Dist. A, 1882 to 1883.

HUNT, WALTER R.—Ed. at Charterhouse and King's Coll., Lond.; 2nd and audit clk. col. sec.'s office, Br. Honduras, Mar., 1887; auditor Bahamas, 1890; M.L.C., Apr., 1899; ag. col. sec., Aug. to Oct., 1901, and May, 1904; recvr.-gen. and treas. and mem. ex. coun., Mar., 1904.

HUNTER, C. H. P., I.S.O. (1903).—Clerk to auditor, St. Lucia, Nov., 1883; ag. 2nd clk., govt office, May to Nov., 1884; ag. ch. clk., treas., Nov., 1884, to Mar., 1885, and May, 1889, to Mar., 1890; 3rd clk., treas., Jan., 1886; 2nd clk. May, 1887; ch. clk., regiar.'s office, Grenada, June, 1890; clk. of couns., Sept., 1890; ag. regiar. sup. ct., Nov., 1890; marshal V.-A. ct., Feb., 1891; ag. priv. sec. to gov., Windward Is., Apr., 1891; 1st-class clk., col. sec.'s office, G. Coast, 1891; ch. clk., Aug., 1892; asst. treas., Jan., 1896; asst. col. sec., S. Leone, Oct., 1896; J.P. for the col., 1896; asst. col. sec., G. Coast Col., Oct., 1897; mentioned in despatches for services during Ashanti rebellion, 1900; ch. asst., col. sec., July, 1901; ag. col. sec., April to Dec., 1901.

HUNTER, SIR DAVID, K.C.M.G. (1901), C.M.G. (1888).—B. 1841; in service of N. Br. Rly. Co. 1853 to 1879; apptd. by S. of S. gen. man., Natal govt. rly., Sept., 1879; mentioned in despatches, 1881; commsn., Natal harbour bd., 1881; received thanks of lieut.-gen. commanding troops for services of his dept., 1882; mem. exec. coun., 1890; deleg. to Harrismith rly. confee., 1890; deleg. to S.A. Republic on rly. extension from Natal terminus at Charlestown to Johannesburg, 1892, when survey of line by Natal engineers was arranged; again in 1893-4 when agreements were completed for construction of Charlestown extension; member Capetown rly. confee., 1895.

HUNTER, HAMILTON.—Ed. at Winchester Coll.; apptd. mag. for Taviuni, Fiji, Oct., 1874; stip. mag., Cakandrovi, Sept., 1875; commsn. of sup. ct., Apr., 1876; ch. pol. mag. for Fiji, Mar., 1877; mem. of land coms., Sept., 1877; dep. commsn. for the W. Pacific, Nov., 1881; ag. regiar.-gen., dep. regiar. of titles, and dep. commsn. of stamps, Jan., 1892; also dep. judge V.-A. ct.; off. recor. in bankruptcy, 1894; regiar. sup. ct. and curator, intes. estates, 1895; special judl. commsn. to visit islda. in H.M.S. "Goldfinch," 1896; visited Cook Islda. and Pitcairn in H.M.S. "Rovalist," 1898; ag. consul., Samoa, 1899.
HUNTER, JOHN.—Apptd., after compet. exam. clk. of the 2nd div. and assigned to the C.O., April, 1899.

HUNTER, WILLIAM, M.B., C.M., Aberdeen, 1896 (Honours) (Univ. Aberdeen, Leipzig, Berlin, King's Coll. and London Hospital); James Anderson medallist and scholar, 1896; John Murray medallist and scholar, 1896; George Thompson travelling fellow, 1897-99; mem. of brit. med. assoc.; mem. of physiological and neurological societies; late asst. director of pathological inst., and asst. bacteriologist, London hospital; govt. bacteriologist, Hong Kong, 1902.
HURLEY, J. S. A.—Ent. C.S.O., Barbados, 1884; copyist 1888; 3rd clk. record branch, 1889; 6th clk. P.O. 1891; 5th do., 1895; 3rd do., 1902.
HUSSEY, M. G.—Ed. at St. Paul's schl. and Keble coll., Oxford; enlisted in Roy. Dragoons, and served in S. African war (med. and 3 clasps); cadet, G. Coast, 1903.

HUTCHINS, DAVID ERNEST.—Passed Indian forest service exam., Nov., 1889; three years at school of foresta, Nancy, France, and special course of forestry instruction in Scotland; asst. conservator of forests, India, 3rd grade, 1872; 2nd grade, 1875; 1st grade, 1878; selected for special duty with the inspr. gen. of forests and dep. conservator of forests, 2nd grade, 1882; transfd. temporarily to Cape Col., June, 1883; awarded diploma at the internat. forestry exhibn., Edinburgh, 1884; conservator of foresta, E. division, Cape, Jan., 1886; Knyana, Sept., 1888; selected to visit and report on forests of W. Virginia, 1889; accompanied Cooper's Hill students on official tour through S. German forests, 1890; conservator of forests, W. div., and consulting forest offr., Mar., 1892.

HUTCHINSON, SIR JOSEPH TURNER, KT. BACH. (1895).—B. 1850; M.A., Christ's Coll., Camb.; barrister, Mid. Tem., 1879; Queen's advoc., G. Coast, Apr., 1888; ch. just., Jan., 1889; ditto, Windward Is., 1894; ditto, Cyprus, 1898.

HUTSON, EYRE, B.A.—2nd clk. to gov. Leeward Is., Oct., 1886; asst. priv. sec., to gov. Barbados, Nov., 1886; priv. sec. to gov. Barbados, Mar., 1887; ditto, at Mauritius, Jan., 1890; at Br. Guiana, July, 1893; Jamaica, 1898; apptd. col. sec. and regiar.-gen. Bermuda, July, 1901; chrmn., schools coman., 1902; chrmn., St. George's harbour comsn., 1903.
HUTTON, C.W.—Treas., C. of Good Hope, May, 1881; ret., 1884; mem. of assem. for Fort Beaufort.

HUTTON, EDWARD MALIN, M.A.—Ed. Magdalen Coll. Schl.; scholar Queen's Coll., Oxon; 2nd class classical mods., 3rd class final classics; admitted solr., 1870; regiar. sup. ct. and ct. of bankruptcy, Gibraltar, 1891; J.P. for Gibraltar, 1892.

HYSLOP, DR. J., D.S.O. (1901); M.B., C.M. (Edin.).—Res. surg., lunatic asylum, Pietermaritzburg, Natal, 4th July, 1882; and surg., Natal carabiniers; formerly asst. physician, Morningside Asyl., Edinburgh, and of Border Counties Asyl., Melrose; prin. med. offr., volra., 1889; S. African war, 1899-1901 (ment. in despa.); chrmn., Natal bd. of health, 1903.

IEVERS, R. W., C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1850; M.A. (Dub.); writer, Ceylon, 1872; asst. govt. agt., Kegalla, Ceylon, Mar., 1878; asst. col. sec., 1885; govt agt., N. Cent. prov., Mar., 1889; prin. asst. col. sec., Dec., 1894; govt. agt., N. prov., June, 1896; ag. col. sec., Nov., 1901; resumed duties as govt. agt., Jan., 1902.

IM THURN, EVERARD F., C.B. (1900), C.M.G. (1892), M.A.-B. 1852; ed. at Marlborough and Exeter Coll., Oxon; special mag., Pomeroon Dist., Br. Guiana, 1882 to 1890; govt. agt., N.W. dists., 1890; ag. 1st cl. clk., C.O., Dec., 1899; col. sec., Ceylon, 1901; admstd. govt., Ceylon, 1902; govt., Fiji, and high coman., W. Pacific, 21st June, 1904; editor of the Guiana scientific journal "Timehri," 1882-7; author of "Among the Indians of Guiana," 1883 and papers on the anthropology, natural history, geography, and history of Guiana; in 1884 made first ascent of Mount Roraima in the interior of Br. Guiana.

INGLIS, JAMES.—Ed. Edinburgh Univ.; in India for twelve years; well known as an author under nom de plume of "Maori;" famine comsrs. in Bhangulpore, 1875; exec. comsrs. for the govt. of India at Melbourne Exhibn., 1881; comsrs. at Sydney, Calcutta, and other exhibns.; entered parliament of N.S.W., 1885; min. of educn. in 1887; author of "Sport and Work on the Nepaul Frontier," "Our Australian Cousins," "Our New Zealand Cousins," "Tent Life in Tiger Land," and other works.

INNES, SIR JAMES ROSE, K.C.M.G. (1901), K.C.—B. 1824; called to the bar of sup. ct., Cape Colony, 12th Feb., 1878; mem. for Victoria East house of assem., Cape, 1884; subsequently reprtd. Cape div.; atty.-gen., 1st Rhodes' ministry, 17th July, 1890 to May, 1893; selected by Imp. govt. to watch proceedings in connection with trial of reform comte. prisoners at Pretoria, 1896; attygen. 4th Sprigg ministry, 1900-2; dele. for Cape Colony to attend conf. in Lond. on final ct. of appeal, 1901; ch. just., sup. ct., Transvaal, 29th Mar., 1902.

INNES, JAMES ROSE, C.M.G. (1879).—B. 1824; was teacher in govt. schls., C. of Good Hope, 1842; clk. to supt.-gen. of educn., 1845; clk. in col. sec.'s office, 1847-56; mag. of Riversdale, 1856-63; ditto of Uitenhage, 1863-67; C.C. and R.M., Bedford div., Oct., 1867; also inspr. of roads; ag. admstr. of Grigualand W., 1880; under sec. for native affairs, 1881; ret. 1902.

INNES, JOHN ROBT., barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn.—Ed. Edin. and Brussels Univ.; cadet, S. Sttlmnts., Nov., 1886; passed in Malay, 1889; dist. offr., S. Malacca, June, 1890; ditto, N. Malacca, June, 1890; asst. Indian immigr. agt., Malacca, June, 1899; ag. collr. land rev. and mag., Malacca, June to Nov., 1892; passed exam. in Dutch, 1893; ag. collr. land rev., Singapore, Feb., 1894, acted also as mag., Oct. 1894, to July, 1896, and as offl. assignee and regiar. of deeds, July to Oct., 1895; ag. asst. col. sec. Oct. to Dec., 1895; ag. collr. land rev. and offr. in charge of treas., also ag. mag. and dep. registr., sup. ct., Malacca, Mar., 1896; collr. of land rev., Penang, May, 1897; ag. sen. dist. offr. prov. Wellesley, Mar., 1898; ag. 1st mag., Penang, Aug., 1898, to Apr., 1899; ag. inspr. prisons, S.S., June, 1900, to Jan., 1901, and from May to Dec., 1901; superdt. census, 1901; deputy public prosecutor, Sing., Jan., 1902; sec. to govt., Perak, Feb., 1904.

INNISS, W. E.—Machinist, survey dept., S. Leone, Dec., 1866; under gaoler, Freetown, June, 1871; foreman and machinist, harbour works, Dec., 1872; keeper, Freetown gaol, Aug., 1874; inspr. of pub. wks., Dec., 1882; served in Yonnie expdn. 1887; offr. in charge, Sulymah, 1888.

INSKIPP, PERCY SIDNEY.—Clk., admstr.'s offr., S. Rhodesia, 1891; ag. sec., 1892; sec. to admstr., 1893; under sec., 1897; joined London staff, B.S.A. Co., 1900; mem. of pioneer corps (occupation of Mashonaland), 1890; also served with the Mashonaland horse, Matabele rebellion, 1896 (medal), and Rhodesia field force, 1900 (medal and 2 clasps).

IRELAND, ARCHIBALD EDWARD.—M.R.C.S. Eng., L.R.C.P. Lond., D.P.H. Oxford; St. Bart's hosp., Lond.; late clinical asst., Birmingham and Midland skin and urinary hospital; res. med. offr., The Infirmary, B'ham.; ditto, City Hosp., B'ham.; asst. comsrs. and govt. med. offr., Salt Cay, Turks Is., 1903.

IRVING, CHARLES JOHN, C.M.G., (1881).— B. 1831; in col. land and emigr. office from June, 1862; clk., audit office, 1863; special clk., audit office, Mauritius, 1864; auditor-gen., S. Sttlmnts., 1867; res. councrl., Malacca, 1879; ditto, Penang, 1882; ag. lieut.-gov., Malacca, 1870 and 1871; and of Penang, 1879; ag. col. sec., 1871, 1875, 1878, and 1888; ret., 1887.

IRVING, EDWARD A.—Joined Perak, F.M.S. service, 25th Mar., 1891; passed final exam. in Malay, Oct., 1891; passed final exam. in Chinese (Hakkar dialect), Feb., 1895; ag. collr. land rev., Kinta dist., June, 1896; prot. of Chinese, Larut dist., July, 1896; warden of mines, Selangor, Feb., 1898; warden of mines, Perak, Jan., 1899; passed final exam. in law, July, 1900; inspr. of schools, Hong Kong, Apl., 1901; mem. of educn. comtes., 1901-2.

IRVING, HENRY EDWARD.—Ed. at Handelslehranstalt, Leipzig; trooper, 9th Lancers, 1899- 1902; served in S. African campaign; ch. clk. to central repatriation bd., O.R.C., Mar., 1902; clk. to col. sec., Oct., 1902.

IRVING, SIR HENRY T., G.C.M.G. (1888), K.C.M.G. (1878), C.M.G. (1874).—B. 1833; clk. in C.O., Nov., 1854; promoted to 3rd class, Oct., 1859; priv. sec. to Sir F. Rogers, now Lord Blachford, 1862; promoted to 2nd class, Apr., 1863; col. sec. of Jamaica, June, 1866; col. sec., Ceylon, May, 1869; gov. Leeward Is.; 1873; gov. Trinidad, July, 1874; gov. Br. Guiana, 1882; ret. 1888.

IRVING, WILLIAM HOWE.—Appd. stock inspr., Queensland, 1866; junr. offr., customsdept., Brisbane, 1867; 2nd offr., shipping master, etc., Maryborough, 1870; ch. clk., Brisbane, 1883; landing survr. on amalgamn. of offices, Apr., 1886; collr. of customs and ch. inspr. of distilleries, Queensland, 1893; mem. of immigr. bd., 1894.

ISAACS, CHARLES POWYS.—Clk., col. branch, ex. and audit dep., Aug., 1890; clk. in charge of acct., Jan., 1894; detached for service as loc. auditor, Uganda Prot., June, 1895, to Sept., 1897; and again as loc. audr., N. Igl., May, 1900, to Aug., 1901; prin. clk., Transvaal aud. dept., Apr., 1902.

ISAACS, HENRY BABER.—Clk. of petty sessions and sub-collr. of taxes, dist. No. 3, Tobago, July, 1878; ag. inland rev. offr., Leeward Dist., Oct., 1878, to Dec., 1878; ag. 2nd rev. offr., Mar., 1880; 1st rev. offr., May, 1882; rev. offr., and regir. of births and deaths, Carriacou, Nov., 1884; is a J.P.; pol. mag. and coroner, 2nd dist., St. Vincent, 1898.
ISRAEL, JOHN W.—Ent. govt. service, 1872, Tasmania; auditor-general, 1896.
IZARD, REV. HERBERT CRAWFORD.—B.A. (Oxon); col. chaplain, Malacca, S. Sttlmnts., 19th Oct., 1901.

JACKMAN, HENRY THOS.—Crystal Palace engng. schl., 1890-3; pupil and asst. on water and drainage wks. to Messrs. McLandesborough and Preston, civ. engrs., 1893-6; in charge of new sewerage wks., Ripon, 1896-7; asst. dist. engnr. on electric tramways, drainage wks., etc., at Sheffield, 1897-1903; exec. engr. on water and drainage wks., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1903-4; ag. sanitary survr., 1904.
JACKSON, BEDVER BLAINE LOWTHER.—Ed Eton and Univ. Coll., Oxford; clk. to atty.-gen., O.R.C., July, 1901.

JACKSON, CECIL GOWER.—Ag. clk. and student interpreter, sec. native affairs off., and as clk. in mag.'s off., Ixopo and Newcastle divs., Natal, from 1888 to 1892; 2nd clk. and sub-distributor of stamps, mag. ct., Newcastle, 1892; clk. of ct. and Zulu interp., and dep. clk. of peace, Impendhle div., 1894; ch. clk. and Zulu interp., mag. ct., Weenen, 1897; J.P., Weenen County; acted frequently as mag., Impendhle and Weenen divs. since 1894; mag. for colony, 2nd astt. mag., Durban div., 1904; J.P. for colony, 1904.

JACKSON, EDGAR MICHAEL.—Entered col. sec.'s office, Cape, 1876; 2nd class clk., 1879; joint sec. to tender bd., 1880-1; astt. regar., sup. ct., Aug., 1881; ch. clk., insolvency and law branch, master's office, Jan., 1888; ch. clk., convict branch, atty.-gen.'s office, Feb., 1889; ch. clk. and ch. accntn., col. sec.'s office, Nov., 1894; supt., govt. stationery off., May, 1904.

JACKSON, Sir Henry Moore, K.C.M.G. (1899), C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1849; Capt., late Royal Artillery; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to Sir Henry Irving; gov. of Trinidad, 1874 to 1876; and to Sir John Glover, gov. of Newfoundland, 1877 to 1879; inspr.-gen. of pol., S. Leone, Dec., 1890, to 1894, and A.D.C. and priv. sec. to gov. Sir A. Havelock, 1881-4; sent to the G. Coast, on special service in connection with the threatened Ashanti invasion, 1881; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to Sir A. Havelock, gov. of Trinidad, 1884; comsnr., Turks Is., 1885; col. sec., Bahamas, 1890; adminstr.-gov., June to Sept., and Nov., and Dec., 1891; July to Oct., 1892, and June to Oct., 1893; col. sec., Gibraltar, 1894; gov. Leeward Is., 1901; ditto, Fiji, and high comsnr., W. Pacific, 1902; gov. Trinidad and Tobago, 13th June, 1904.

JACKSON, Lt.-Col. Hugh Milbourne, R.E.—Survey of India, 1883-96; Burmese expdn., 1885-9 (ment. in desps.); ordinance survey of Great Britain, 1895-9; S.
