African war, 1899-1902; attached to army headqtrs. staff for mapping and reconnaissance; A.A.G. topography, Nov., 1900, to end of war (ment. in desps. and brevet); pres. of Natal-Transvaal boundy. coman., 1902; survr.-gen., Transvaal, Mar., 1903.

JACKSON, Maximilian James.—Special mag. and inspr. under N. Border, Protection Act, Oct., 1868, C. of Good Hope; was clk. at Victoria (East), 1868 to 1864; ditto at Richmond, 1864 to 1865; clk. at Murrayburg, 1865 to 1868; C.C. and R.M., Richmond, 1873; now C.C. and R.M., Knysna div.; res. mag. and collr. of cust., Simon's Town, June, 1901.
JACKSON, Thos. Beddard, M.J.I.—Ed. at Bromsgrove coll.; govt. shorthand writer, Trinidad and Tobago, 1st Jan., 1900; ditto, and clk. col. sec.'s office, 8th June, 1901.
JACKSON, Wm. Henry.—Cadet, Ceylon, 23rd Sept., 1879; pol. mag., 1st May, 1886; astt. gov't. agt., 21st June, 1895; prin. collr. of cust., 11th June, 1901.
JAMAICA, Bishop of, The Rt. Rev. Enos Nuttall, D.D.—Ordained and ag. incumbent, St. George's, Kingston, 1866; island curate there, 1867; elected bishop, 1880.

JAMES, Chas. Canniff, M.A.—Matric., Lond. Univ., 1879; M.A., Victoria Univ., Cobourg, Ontario, 1883 (1st class honours and gold medal in nat. sci.); astt. master, Cobourg Coll. Institute, Jan., 1883; prof. of chemistry, Ontario Agricultl. Coll. at Guelph; dep. min. of agricult., Ontario, and sec. of the bureau of industries, June, 1891.

JAMES, Thos. Reynolds, M.S.T.E.—Born in Cornwall, 1833; man., govt. telegraph dept., Victoria; entered Victorian service, Apr., 1854; hon. sec. and treas., society of telegraph engnr.s. and electricians, London.

JAMES, Walter Hartwell.—Ed. in Perth; state and high schls., admitted as barrister and solicitor, 1888; member for East Perth, 1894; represented W. Aust. at fed. convcn., 1897-8; hon. minister, 1901; K.C., 1902; premier and atty.-gen., 1902; agt.-gen. for W.A., 1904.

JAMESON, Adam.—M.B. (1883), M.D. (1897), Edin.; M.L.C., metropolitan suburban province, W. Aust., 1901; hon. minister, 1901; min. for lands, 1901-2; ditto, 1902; commr. of crown lands, Transvaal, 1903; mem. ex. and leg. couns.; mem. I.C.C.
JAMESON, Chas.—Called to the bar, Queensland, Feb., 1883; sec. to crown law offrs., Apr., 1886.

JANISCH, Norl.—Served under the St. Helena govt., Aug., 1870, to Apr., 1878; 3rd-class clk. ofce of comsnr. of crown lands and pub. wks., Capetown, Apr., 1878; on active service with Cape Volks, in the Transkei in 1879, and during Basuto war, 1881; 2nd-class clk., July, 1881; 1st class, Apr., 1887; ch. clk., 1892; sec. for pub. wks., Sept., 1892; additional mem. of tender bd., May, 1894; under col. sec., July, 1898; dir. of census, 1904.
JARRETT, Michael Lewis, M.R.C.S. (Lond.), L.R.C.F. (Edin.).—Astt. col. surg., Sherbro, W. Africa, 1882; is a J.P.
JARVIS, A. Leonard Fitzgerald.—Entered Canadian govt. service, 1868; priv. sec. to postm. gen., 1882, to min. of agricult., 1885; sec. to dept. of agricult., 1896.

JARVIS, Edward B.—2nd clk. col. sec.'s office, Leeward Is., 1890; clk. of gov.'s office, 1891; ag. 1st clk., 1891; ag. 1st clk. col. sec.'s office, Mar. to June, 1896. On reorganisation of office, apptd. 2nd clk.; ag. ch. clk., and clk. to fed., exec. and legis. couns. and local legis. coun. of Antigua, Apr., 1897, to Jan., 1898, and July to Dec., 1899; P.S. to ag. gov., June to Oct., 1898; ch. clk. col. sec.'s off., and clk. to fed., exec. and legis. couns. and local legis. coun., 3rd July, 1901; astt. col. sec., Leeward Is., Feb., 1903.

JARVIS, J. Leslie H.—Dep. regar. of marriages, births, and deaths, 1890-2; and census offr. for parish of St. George, Antigua; clk. to admstr., and to exec. coun., Dominica, Oct., 1896; ag. clk. bd. of health, 1896.

JEFFERY, GEORGE.—Scholarship, R. Coll. of Art, 1872; scholarship in architecture, R. Academy of London (1874); F.R.I.B.A., 1892; architect to Rt. Rev. Bishop in Jerusalem and the East, 1892; hon. corres. mem. of Imperial Institute of Archaeology of Russia, 1897; inspr. of pub. wks., Cyprus, 1898; curator of ancient monuments, Cyprus, 1903.

JEKYLL, Lieut.-Col. Sir Herbert, K.C.M.G. (1901), R.E., C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1846; lieut., R.E., Apr., 1866; employed under War Office and Trinity House in 1868 to 1870, and destruction of sunken ships, from submarine mining, and under G.P.O. in the telegraph dept., from 1870 to 1873, and 1874 to 1876; served in the campaign of 1873-74 on the G. Coast; priv. sec. to the Earl of Carnarvon, Jan., 1877, to Feb., 1878; sec. to the col. defence comte., 1878; sec. to the roy. comm. on the defence of Br. possessions and commerce abroad, 1879-82; specially employed to visit and report on the defences of Singapore and Colombo, 1883-4; sec. col. defence comte., 1883; priv. sec. to Lord Carnarvon, viceroy of Ireland, June, 1885, to Jan., 1886; sec. to roy. comm. for Melbourne centennial exhib., 1888; priv. sec. to lord-lieut. of Ireland, 1892-3; sec. to the roy. comsn. for the Paris exhibition, 1900.

JENKINS, SIR G. H., KT. BACH. (1904), C.M.G. (1891).—B. 1843; clk., govt. rlys., Victoria, 1861; clk. of priv. bills in the legis. assem., 1865; sec. to royal coman. on the subject of payment of members of the legislature, 1868; clk. of comtees., 1870; sec. to the royal coman. on the parlmt. buildings, 1877, and clk. astt. of the legis. assem., 1878; clk. of the legis. assem., 1882; sec. to the Australasian Federation Convention, 1890, and was accorded a special vote of thanks by that body; on May, 1891, apptd. clk. of the parlmts. of Victoria; is an hon. just. of the peace for the cols. of Victoria and S. Australia. On the termination of his 25th year of service in the legis. assem., the members presented him with a magnificent service of silver plate as a mark of their regard and esteem, and on his retiring from the position of clk. of the legis. assem., a special vote of thanks was passed for his prolonged and valuable services.
JENKINS, THE HON. JOHN GREELEY.—Mem. H. of A., S. Aust., 1887; min. of ed. and N. territory, 1891-2; commr. of pub. wks., 1892; ditto, 1894-9; chief sec., 1899-1901; premier and ch. sec., 1901.

JERNINGHAM, SIR HUBERT EDWARD HENRY, F.R.G.S., K.C.M.G. (1893), C.M.G. (1889), R. des Lettres, Paris.—B. 1842; attaché, 1866; passed an exam. and obtained an hon. certif., 1886; attaché at Paris, 1867, and was transf. to Constantinople, 1870; 3rd sec., 1870; on temporary duty at Athens, 1870; and was transf. to Karlsruhe and Darmstadt, 1872; ag. chargé d'affaires, 1873; 2nd sec., 1873; ag. chargé d'affaires, 1874, 1875, 1876, and 1877; was transf. to Vienna, 1877; ag. agr. and consul.-gen. at Belgrade, 1878; was unemployed from 1879 till 1881, when he resig.; M.P. for Berwick, 1881-5; col. sec., Br. Honduras, 1887; administered the govt. for three months in 1888; col. sec., Mauritius, 1889; administered the govt. Mar., 1892; lieut.-gov., Aug., 1892; gov. and comdr.-in-ch., June, 1893; gov., Trinidad, Jan., 1897, to 1900.
JETTE, SIR L. A., K.C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1836; puisne judge, Quebec, 1878; lieut.-gov., Quebec, 1897.
JEWELL, M.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Apr., 1899.

JEX, ROBERT A. N.—Ed. St. John Berchman's Coll., Belize, Br. Hond.; apptd., after compet. exam., copyist in col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1897; ag. 4th clk., Aug. to Nov., 1897; ag. 3rd clk., May, 1898; ag. 3rd clk., Feb., 1899, to Feb., 1900; ag. clk. to reg. genl. Oct. to Dec., 1900; ag. 2nd clk., Apr. to Sept., 1901; 3rd clk., May, 1901.

JOHNSON, DUDLEY.—Dist. offr., Montserrat, Aug., 1891; regiar.-gen. births, deaths, and marriages for dist. A., Oct., 1895; road survr. for dists. 2 and 3 north and 3 south, Jan., 1900; local agricultural instructor, July, 1900.

JOHNSON, EDWARD RICHARD.—Joined post. and tel. dept., O.F.S., Oct., 1890; asst. postmr. and telegraphist, Jagersfontein, Oct., 1890; ditto, Fauresmith, Nov., 1892; postmr. and telegraphist, Thaba' Nchu, June, 1894; ditto, Koffyfontein, Sep., 1894; ch. clk. to P.M.G., O.F.S., Dec., 1896; relieving offr., Feb., 1897; ch. clk., secretarial branch, dep. of poets and tels., O.R.C., Mar., 1900; prin. clk. and astt. sec., ditto, July, 1902; astt. sec., ditto, July, 1903.
JOHNSON, EDWIN.—Apptd. to educn. dept., N.S. Wales, 1855; inspr. of schla., 1862; ch. inspr., 1880; under-sec., educn. dept., 1884.

JOHNSON, E. O.—Ed. Epsom Coll. and Harr. Coll., Barbados; 2nd treasy. offr., Montserrat, 1884; 1st treasy. offr., 1886; 1st clk. treasy., St. Kitts-Nevia, 1891; 1st rev. offr., 1894; ag. treasr., Dec., 1896, to Sept., 1897; ast. treasr., S. Leone, Nov., 1897; col. treasr., Mar., 1899.
JOHNSON, F. E. G.—B.A.; astt. dist. comsnr., So. Nigeria, Apl., 1900; dist. comsnr., July, 1902; ast. sec. (temporarily), Feb. to Aug., 1904; ag. pol. mag., Calabar, Aug., 1904.

JOHNSON, GEORGE CUNYNGHAM.—2nd clk., treasy., St. Kitts, Apr., 1894; ag. extra rev. offr., May, 1895; 2nd clk. admistr.'s office, clk. of legis. coun., Mar., 1896; ag. acctnt. treasy., Jan., 1897; audit clk. and clk. legis. coun., St. Kitts-Nevia, May, 1897; clk. to admusr., exec., and legis. councils, and clk. to bd. of health, Jan., 1901; astt. priv. sec. (unpaid) to H.E. Chas. T. Cox, C.M.G., ag. gov., Leeward Is., 1903, 1904; ag. astt. treas. and controller of cust., offl. mem., legis. coun., St. Kitts-Nevia; chrmn., Basseterre poor-law bd., Apl., 1903 to Mar., 1904; mem. quarantine bd., 1903; compiled index to the laws of Leeward Is. and St. Kitts-Nevia, 1901.

JOHNSON, GEORGE WILLIAM, M.A.—Scholar of Trin. Coll., Camb.; 8th wrangler and 3rd-class classics, 1880; apptd., after a compet. exam., to be a clk. in the office of the sec. of state for the cols., Mar., 1881; sec. to Eastern currency comtee., 1893; astt. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, May, 1896; priv. sec. to Sir R. Meade, Sept., 1896; 1st-class clk., Mar., 1897; ag. prin. clk., July, 1900; mem. of S. S. currency comtee., 1902.
JOHNSON, HENRY STAFFORD BROWNLOW.—Cadet, Sarawak, 1897; astt. resid., 1901; 2nd cls. res. in charge of Upper Rejang, 1903.
JOHNSON, JOHN RICHARD.—Pharmaceutical chemist, mem. of phar. soc. of Gt. Britain; med. storekeeper, govt. med. dept., Jamaica; apptd. to act as prof. analytical chem., May, 1899.

JOHNSTON, HARRY F.—Contract survr., W. Australia, Jan., 1874; ag. staff survr., Nov., 1883; confirmed, Feb., 1885; surveyed and triangulated Kimberley dist., and discovered Elvire and Mary rivers, where goldfields have been found; in charge of constrn. of telegraph, Roebourne to Derby, 1886-9; survr.-gen., July, 1896.

JOHNSTON, LEWIS AUDLEY MARSH.—Ed. Derby gram. schl.; [B.A. Trin. Coll., Dub.; cadet, Straits, 1888; passed final exam., Malay, Oct., 1890; attached to G.P.O., Singapore, Oct., 1890, to June, 1892; ag. dist. offr., N. Malacca, June, 1892; dep. regier. deeds and official assignee, Singapore, June, 1894; astt. postmr.-gen., June, 1895; ag. postmr.-gen., S. S., Sept., 1896; ag. collr. ld. rev. and offr. in charge of treasy., Malacca, Aug., 1898; ag. postmr.-gen., S. S., Sept., 1900, to Jan., 1902; P.M.G. Hong Kong, 1902; ag. treas. and mem. of ex. and leg. couns., 1903-5.

JOHNSTON, R. FLEMING.—M.A., Magdalen Coll., Oxford (1901); Gray prizeman, Edin. Univ., 1894; prox. acc. Lord Rector's essay; 1st cl. certa. Eng. lit., mod. history and constitutional law, Edin.; exhibitioner, Magdalen Coll., 1894; mentioned hon. causa Stanhope essay, 1898; B.A. (hons.), 1898; cadet, Hong Kong, 1898; ag. clk. of councils, 1899; priv. sec. to H.E. the administrator; ag. astt. col. sec., 1900; priv. sec. to the governor, 1900-1901; passed cadet, Oct., 1901; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of councils, Nov., 1901; ag. sec. to govt., Weihaiwei, 1904.

JOHNSTON, ROBERT MACKENZIE, I.S.O. (1903), F.S.S. (1879).—Regist.-gen. and govt. statistician of Tasmania; ed. Andersonian Univ., Glasgow; apptd. to organise and superintend the working of the acctnt.'s and traffic audit branch of the first rly. opened in Tasmania (1870); transf'd. to service of Tasmanian Govt. (1872); ch. clk. in auditor-gen.'s office (1880); apptd. to reorganise and conduct the then combined offices of regist.-gen. and govt. statistician (1882); roy. commr. to inquire and report on the fisheries of the col. of Tasmania (1882); apptd. salmon fisheries commr. (1882); mem. of the fisheries bd. (1888-90); chmn. of boundaries bd. of advice (1890); author of "Systematic Account of the Geology of Tasmania" and other scientific wks.; fellow of the Roy. Statis. Soc. of Lond.; mem. of the coun. of the Roy. Soc. of Tasmania and of the Australasian Assoc. for Advancement of Science; fellow of the Roy. Geog. Soc. of Australasia.

JOHNSTONE, EDWARD.—Ed. Cheltenham and Westward Ho! colleges; lieut., 4th Batt. P.A., Somerset L.I., 1886-1887; asst. supt. of pol., Madras presidency, 1887-88; served in 1st Batt. Durham L.I. (68th), 1889-1890; asst. inspr., Lagos Hausa force, 1896; on spec. serv. to G. Coast hinterland, 1897 (Samoury expedition, W. Africa, medal and clasp); priv. sec. to Sir G. C. Denton, lt.-gov. of Lagos, 1900; commr. of police, Lagos, 1900.

JOHNSTONE, ROBERT.—Ed. Edinburgh; temporary clk., audit office, Jamaica, 1878; 3rd-class clk., col. sec.'s office, 1879; sec. to bd. of supervision, poor relief, Sept., 1886; is also sec. schools commn. from 1883; and asst. govt. meteorologist from 1880 to 1899; sec. to customs defalcations, 1889; conduct of two public offrs., 1890; Milk River, 1892; ed. in Jamaica, 1898; apptd. mem. and man. dir. of local bd. of Mans. Ditchfield (school) trust property, 1899; author of "Nelson and the West Indies," and of "Poverty and the Poor Law in Jamaica," &c.; F.R. Met. Soc., 1895.

JOHNSTONE, ROBERT.—Ed. Queen's Roy. Coll., Trinidad, and City of London Schl.; asst. warden, Naparima, Trinidad, 1879; of St. Ann's and Diego Martin, 1885; warden, Naparima, Nov., 1889; stip. J.P. of Naparima, Oropouche, and La Brea, Mar., 1891; is major Trinidad light volrs.

JOHNSTONE, ROBERT STEWART.—Ed. at Trin. Coll., Dublin; B.A. (honors and medal in history and political science), 1882; LL.B., 1884; M.A., 1895; Holt scholar, Gray's Inn, 1885; called to the Irish bar 1886; called to the bar at Gray's Inn, Jan., 1889; lieut., 3rd (Militia) Batt. Manchester Regt., 1883; capt., 1887; dist. commr., Lagos, 1889; served on Lagos and Porto Novo boundary commn. 1890; stip. and circuit mag. and judge of ct. of com. pleas, Bahamas, Jan., 1894.
JOLICEUR, PHILIPPE J., K.C.—Asst. sec. of the prov. of Quebec.
JOLLIVET, ARTHUR B. YVRS.—L.R.C.P., Glas.; L.R.C.S., Edin.; health offr., Mauritius, 20th Mar., 1885; med. supt., Barkly asylum, 15th Nov., 1895.

JOLY DE LOTBINIERE, SIR HENRY GUSTAVE, K.C.M.G. (1895).—B. 1829; ed. in Paris, and called to the bar of Quebec in 1855; elected to parlmt. of old Canada for Lotbiniere in 1861, and continued to represent the county till 1867, when he was returned both to the parlmt. of the Dominion and to the legislature of Quebec; in 1874 he ret. from parlmt. to devote his attention to provincial affairs; in 1878, on the dismissal of the De Boucherville cabinet, he was called on to form a ministry, which he did, holding power for one session by virtue of the vote of the speaker; resig. 1879, after defeat in the house of assen.; later he ret. from the leadership of the party; and in 1886, as a protest against the Riel agitation carried on by the provincial liberal party, ret. from public life; in 1877 was offered the portfolio of agricul. in the Mackenzie govt., with a seat in the senate; controller of inland rev., without a seat in the cabinet, in Sir W. Laurier's govt., June, 1896; lieut.-gov. of B. Columbia, 1900.

JONES, HON. ALFRED GILPIN.—Ed. Yarmouth Acad., Nova Scotia; W. Indian merchant; gov. Dalhousie Coll.; pres. N. S. Marine Insurance Co., and dir. Acadia Fire Insurance Co.; comdr. Halifax garrison artillery; for some years mem. of Dominion house of commons for Halifax, 1867-72, 1874-78, and again in 1887, sworn of privy coun., and min. of militia in Mackenzie admin., 1878; mem. Pacific Cable comtee., 1896; lieut.-gov. of Prov. of Nova Scotia, Aug., 1900.

JONES, EDWARD RUTTER.—Acted aidwaiter, customa, Antigua, 1875 to 1880; apptd. cashier, treasy., Antigua, Oct., 1880; landing waiter, customa, Antigua, 1881; acted ist clk., audit off., Leeward Is., 1887 and 1892; apptd. 1st indoor offr., treasy., Antigua, 1896; ag. treas., Antigua, Sept., 1899, Oct., 1900, July, 1901, May, 1902, and Mar. to Dec., 1903; treas. and compt., customa, St. Kitts-Nevis, Mar., 1904; ofl. M.L.C., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1904; chrmn., P.L. board and mem. quarantine board.
JONES, J. F., C.M.G. (1902).—Joined B. S. Africa Co. on its incorporation, 1889; asst. sec., 1897; sec., 1898; joint manager, in addition to sec., 1902; director of Rhodesia Rlyws., Ltd.
JONES, JAMES WM.—Survr., survey dept., S. Aust., 1865; chief survr., 1877; dep. survr.-gen., 1881; conservator of water, 1883; sec. to commr. of pub.wks., and chrmn. of supply and tender bd., 1882.

JONES, PATRICK N. H., A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. King's Coll., Lond., applied sciences; student I.C.E.; res. engr., Barbados Water Supply Co., 1889 to 1896; ag. D.P.W., Barbados, Sept. to Oct., 1894; thanked by leg. coun. for services in drafting waterworks bill, 1894; apptd. engr. in charge of waterworks surveys, Trinidad, 1895; ag. res. engr. of water and sewerage works, Feb. to Nov., 1898; and Jan. to Oct., 1900; in charge of construction of water and sewerage works, 1900 to 1903; mem. of comtee. on tech. educ. in the colony, 1902; asst. D.P.W., Hong Kong, 3rd April, 1903; ag. D.P.W. and mem. of ex. and leg. couns., 1904.
JONES, S. SALTO.—Asst. book-keeper, Br. Guiana, Oct., 1882; 4th clk., rec.-gen.'s office, Jan., 1884; 3rd clk., May, 1884; 1st-class clk., Apr., 1888; cashier, July, 1888.

JONES, S. T.—Ed. Wes. high schl., S. Leone: spec. prize for "marked proficiency" in studies; R. coll. of preceptors and Pitman's shorthand certificates; appointed 6th cls. clk., P.O., Niger Coast Prot., 1898; 3rd cls. clk., high commr.'s off., 1900; 2nd cls. clk., 1902; Aro medal and clasp, 1902; 1st cls. clk., secretariat, 1903; sen. clk., secretariat, S. Nigeria, 1904.

JONES, W. H. HYNDMAN.—Ed. Marlborough and Trin. Coll., Camb.; LL.B. honours, 1870; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1878; ag. judge of asst. ct. of appeal, Apr., 1880; ag. sen. pol. mag., Bridgetown, Aug., 1880; one of the commrs. to inquire into the expenditure of the pol. force in Barbados, Oct., 1880; stip. mag., St. Lucia, Oct., 1881; mem. of legis. coun., May, 1881; has acted several times as ch. just., St. Lucia and of Tobago; pol. mag., Grenada, 1887; and mem. exec. and legis. couns.; res. mag., Jamaica, Apr., 1888; puisne judge, S.S., 1896; acted as judicial comnr., Fed. Malay States, April, 1903.

JONES, SIR W. H. QUAYLE, KT. BACH.(1892).—B. 1854; temporary puisne judge, G. Coast Col., 1882 and 1883; mem. of Lond. exec. and chmn. of local comtee. for the Col. and Indian Exhibn., 1886; Queen's advoc., 1883; ch. just., W. Africa Stltnts., 1887; ret. 1896; chmn. of sessions.
JORDAN, ALBERT JOHN.—Late of Kew Gardens; agricultural instructor, Montserrat, 1899.
JOSCELYNE, R. H.—Transferred from Royal Niger Company's service to C.O. as supplementary clerk, Jan., 1900.
JOSEPH, GERARD A.—Priv. sec. to Mr. Just. Clarence, Ceylon, 1891; librarian and sec. Colombo museum, Oct., 1893; is hon. sec. Ceylon branch Roy. Asiatic Soc.

JOSKE, ADOLPH B.—Inspr. native taxes, Fiji, June, 1884; stip. mag. and asst. res. comnr. for Colo. E., and inspr. of native taxes, Oct., 1884; comnr., Colo. N., S.M.Ra., and dep. comdt. armed native constab., 1892; comnr., Colo. E., 1897.

JOYNT, HENRY NOBLE H.—Ed. at Portora Roy. Schl. and Univ. of Dub.; prizeman, royal schlr., 1st honour-man, 1st senr. modir. and gold medallist in nat. sc.; B.A., M.A., M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O., diploma in state med.; L.M., Rotunda Hosp.; ast. med. offr., fever hosp., Dub.; sen. asst. med. hosp., City Hosp., Birmingham; supt., City Hosp., Bradford; gov't. med. offr., Fiji, 1892; J.P. of col.

JULL, MARTIN EDWARD.—Ed. Brighton Gram. Schl.; articled 3 yrs. and afterwards ast. survr. with a firm of survrs. of city of London; clk. P.W.D., W. Australia, Jan., 1887; ch. clk., Jan., 1892; under sec. P.W., July, 1895.

JULYAN, SIR PENROSE G., K.C.M.G. (1874), C.B. (1869).—B. 1816; served as a volr. during Canadian rebellion in 1837-8; splc. comnr. of roads and bridges in Lower Canada in 1839; apptd. to the commissariat in 1845; promoted to rank of dep. ast. comnr.-gen., and received the thanks of col. gov't.; ast. financial sec. to bd. of wks. in Ireland, 1848; dir. of a branch of the royal mint in Australia in 1852, and was employed on mint service until the war broke out with Russia, when he was recalled to the commissariat; employed as an engr. during early part of the war, and then designed and superintended the construction of a marine steam flour mill and a floating bakery for the supply of the army in the Crimea, which was of much service throughout the war. Received the special thanks of the min. for war, and promoted ast. comnr.-gen., Jan., 1856; and created a C.B. in May, 1869; crown agt. for col. in 1858; sent on special coman. of inquiry into the civil establishments and general expenditure of Mauritius, 1873; and again on a similar mission to Malta, in 1878; ret., Nov., 1879.

JUST, ALBERT WOLFGANG, B.A. (1891).—Jun. officer, State of Perak, July, 1892; passed final exam. in Malay, Aug., 1893; ast. to dist. mag. and collr. of land rev., Krian, Jan., 1894; mag., Seremban, Jan., 1903.

JUST, HARTMANN WOLFGANG, C.B. (1902), C.M.G. (1900), B.A. (1877).—B. 1854; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in office of sec. of state for the col., 3rd June, 1878; ed. at Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; was an open scholar on the foundation; 1st class in classical moda., 1874; Taylorian exhib. for German, 1876; 2nd class in final classical school, 1877; ast. priv. sec. to the late Earl of Derby, 6th Feb., 1883, to Col. F. A. Stanley (now Earl of Derby), 24th June, 1885, and to Mr. Stanhope, 3rd Aug., 1886; priv. sec. to Mr. Osborne Morgan, 6th Feb. to 3rd Aug., 1886; ast. priv. sec. to Sir H. Holland (now Viscount Knutsford), 12th Jan., 1887, and to the Marquess of Ripon, 17th Aug., 1892; ast. sec. to the col. confee., 1887; 1st class clk., Apr., 1895; prin. clk. Mar., 1897; accompanied Rt. Hon. Joseph Chamberlain on his visit to S. Africa, 1902-3.

JUSTICE, MAJ.-GEN. WILLIAM CLIVE, C.M.G. (1881).—B. 1835; entered 75th regt. in 1852, col., 1881; has held the following apptmts.: adjt., 75th regt.; adjt., depot batt.; town major, W. dist., England; mil. sec. to comdr.-in-oh., Bombay; ast. adjt.-gen., Bombay; ast. adjt. and qtrmr.-gen., W. Indies; col. on the staff, W. coast of Africa; col. on the staff, Jamaica; served in E. Indies; during the mutiny, 1857-8, and was present during the siege, assault, and capture of Delhi (medal and clasp); in command of an expedit. sent in anticipation of war with Ashanti in 1881; now in command of the troops in Ceylon; was pres. of coman. on Barbados pol. force, 1880; sen. mem. exec. and legis. couns., G. coast, 1881; sen. mem. exec. coun., Barbados, 1883; sen. mem. privy and legis. couns., Jamaica, 1886, and administered the govt., Feb. to Mar., 1887, May to Aug., 1888, Jan. to Mar., 1889; mem. exec. and legis. couns., Ceylon, 1893.

KAYE, A. B. B.—Capt. 3rd batt., Roy. Ir. Fus.; served during Indian rebellion, N.W. Territory of Canada, 1885, attached to Boulton's mounted infantry, present at the Fish Creek engagement, and the taking of Batock; medal; sub.-insp., Br. Honduras constab., 1887; ag. dist. mag., Orange Walk, Apr. to June, 1889, Nov., 1890, to Apr. 1892; insp. Br. Honduras constab., June, 1889; ag. inspr. comdt., Apr., 1892; inspr. comdt., and mem. exec. coun., Oct., 1892; now inspr. of police, Barbados.
KEAN, HON. ABRAHAM.—M.H.A., Bonavista, Newfold., 1885-89, Bay de Verde, 1897; mem. exec. coun. (without portfolio), 1897; ag. min. agricul. and mines, 1898.
KEEFER, THOMAS COLTRIN, C.M.G. (1878), C.E.—B. 1821; engnr. of several rlyws. and other wks. in Canada; was exec. comnr. for the Dominion of Canada at the Paris Exhibn., 1878.

KEITH, A. BERRIEDALE.—Ed. at Royal High Schl. and Univ., Edinburgh; 7 class medals, Van Dunlop scholarship in classics, 1895; M.A.; 1st class hon. classics, 1897; Furguson schl., Glasgow, 1897; Guthrie fellowship in Class. Lit., 1899; open schlship., Balliol Coll., Oxford, 1895; Boden schlship., 1898; 1st class hon. moderations, 1899; 1st class Lit. Ind. (Sanskrit and Pali), 1900; 1st class Lit. Hum., 1901; B.A., 1900; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. clk., C.O., 21st Oct., 1901; sec. to crown agts., July, 1903; called to the bar (1st cls. honours) 1904.

KELK, REV. W. HASTINGS.—Ed. Queen's Coll., Camb.; B.A.; deacon, 1892; priest, 1893; curate of Morley, York, 1892-4; missionary at Ainanoub, Syria, 1894-7; Haifa, Syria, 1897-8; col. chaplain and regiar. of deaths, Accra, 6th Aug., 1899; chrmn. of census comtee. and compiler of census, 10th May, 1901; ag. director of educn., July to Oct., 1901.

KELLY, C. C.—Supernum. marshal, Essequibo, Dec., 1873; ag. clk., Suddie treasy., Oct., 1883; ordinary marshal, provost marshal's office, Georgetown, June, 1884; mag.'s clk., Essequibo, Aug., 1887; ag. ch. clk., pol. mag.'s office, Georgetown, June, 1893.

KELLY, HENRY GREENE.—Ed. Trin. Coll., Dub.; honorman and prizeman in hist. and political science; B.A. and LL.B.; called to the Bar, King's Inn, Dub., Mich. Term, 1884; apptd. a puisne judge in the Niger Territories, 1891; acted on several occasions as Chief Justice; Chief Justice, 1899; Chief Justice of Southern Nigeria, 1900.
KELSON, JACOB WALTER.—Ck., surr.'s dept., G. Coast, 1885; ch. warden and med. dresser, lun. asyl., Accra, 1887.

KEMP, J. H.—B.A., Cape Univ.; cadet, Hong Kong, 1898; land offr., New Territory, 1899; register, land court, July, 1900; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1900 to 1904; asst. sec., sanitary bd., 1904; deputy-registr. and appraiser, sup. ct., 1904.
KEEMPSFORD, GEORGE HERBERT.—Apptd., after exam., 3rd cls. messenger, C.O., 22nd Feb., 1904.

KENNAN, THOMAS PONSONBY K.—Served in F.A.M.P. and C.M.R., 1877 to 1882, during Griqua and Basuto wars (medal); temporary clk. to R.M., Leribe, Basutoland, 1882; sub-inspr., Basuto pol., 1884; inspr., 1886; asst. comsnr., Outhing, 1889.

KENNAWAY, WALTER, C.M.G. (1891).—Mem. prov. coun., Canterbury, N.Z., 1868-74; prov. sec. and sec. pub. wks., Cant., 1870-74; mem. bd. of educn., also bd. of govs., Cant. Coll., 1873-4; sec., N.Z., govt. office, London, 1874; comsnr., Col. and Ind. Exhibn., 1886; apptd. offr. of French Acad. for services at Paris Exhibn., 1889; ag. agt.-gen., 1891; stock and loan agt. for N.Z. govt.; comsnr. under N.Z. Pub. Rev. Acts.; custod. Pub. Trust Securities, London.

KENNY, W. EYRE.—A.M.I.C.E.; jun. asst. engr., Cook county, N. Zealand, Jan., 1883; 2nd asst. engr., Gisborne har. bd., N.Z., July, 1885; ch. asst., Nov., 1887; ag. ch. asst. to col. marine engrnr., N.Z., Dec., 1890; offr. in charge, special survey dept., Sarawak, Sept., 1891; consulting engrnr. to Sarawak govt., Nov., 1892; in addition, engrnr. for water wks., Jan., 1893; asst. supt. of wks., P.W., S.Sttlmts., Jan., 1895; supt. of wks. and surveys, Malacca, Oct., 1897; ag. supt. of wks. and surveys, Singapore, June, 1898, to Feb., 1901; ag. dep. col. engrnr. on three occasions; ag. state engrnr., Selangor, Apl., 1903.
KENT, R. G.—Acctnt., rly. dept., Victoria, 1891.

KER, J. ERRINGTON, M.D. (Edin.).—Was asst. house surg., gen. hosp., Birmingham, and house surg., Hertford Br. hosp., Paris; asst. surg., col. hosp., Gibraltar, 1890; is also pol. and port surg. and public vaccinator; selected for service with the troops in S. Africa, 1899 to 1900; suptdg. med. offr., Jamaica, 1904.

KERNANAHAN, J. B.—Ed. St. Mary's Coll., Trinidad, 1876 to 1883; supry., survey office, Trinidad, 1885 to 1886; acted asst. draughtsman, S.O. Trinidad, 1886; passed survey exam., Univ. Coll., London, 1886 to 1887; priv. survr., Grenada, 1888 to 1890; engaged by St. Vincent govt. on surveys, June, 1890, to Dec., 1894; crown and col. survr., 23rd June, 1890; staff survr., Jan., 1895; apptd. to carry out duties assigned to col. engrnr. under provisions of road order, of 1895, Jan., 1896; staff survr., supt., crown lands, and land comsnr., Feb., 1899; dir. of surveys, still holding other offices above, Aug., 1899; J.P., Dec., 1901; mem. of exec. and legis. couns., Aug., 1902.
KERR, C. M. MACLEOD.—3rd-class clk., constabty. office, Jamaica, Feb., 1863; 2nd-class, Aug., 1869; 1st-class, Oct., 1873; ch. clk., July, 1879.

KERR, LOUIS L.—Clk. imigrn. dept. Br. Guiana, Sept., 1887; clk. pol. dept., Jan., 1888; ag. inspr. pol., 1891-2; ag. paymr., 1892; dist. inspr. and J.P., 1893; co. inspr. pol., Jan., 1897; in charge Aruian frontier station, June to Oct., 1896.

KERR, MARK.—Priv. sec. to gov., Falklands, Sept., 1884, to Apr., 1888; acted also as govt. clk. clk. of couns., shipping master, regisr. gen. and receiver of wrecks from Dec., 1886, to Feb., 1888; on special service to S. Leone, Nov., 1888, to July, 1889; prin. clk. col. sec.'s office, G. Coast, Oct., 1889; chief clerk, July, 1891; asst. col. sec., July, 1892; ag. ch. astt. col. sec., Aug., 1893, to Mar., 1894, and Nov., 1894, to Mar., 1895; clk. of couns., Aug., 1893, to Mar., 1894, and Apr. to Sept., 1895; ag. col. sec., Apr., 1896; astt. col. sec., Lagos, Aug., 1896; mem. exec. and legis. couns. G. Coast, Apr. to Aug., 1896; mem. exec. and legis. couns. Lagos, at intervals from Aug., 1896, to Dec., 1899; col. sec. Northern Nigeria, Jan., 1900.

KERR, THOMAS, C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1818; was employed in the col. sec.'s dept., Barbados, in 1856; pol. mag., 1860 to 1869; judge of the petty debt ct., Bridgetown, to 1874; judge of the ass't. ct. of appeal, to 1880; represented the parish of St. Joseph in the house of assem. from 1862 to 1866; mem. of the legis. coun., 1876; major 11th regt. of militia, 1854; comldg. offr. since 1858; a mem. of the militia board. Author of "Kerr on the Cultivation of the Sugarcane and the Manufacture of Sugar;" administered the govt. of Grenada, Aug., 1878, to Apr., 1879; govr., Falkland Is., 1890-91.
KERR, THOMAS S., M.B., C.M., EDIN.—Col. surg. for Penang, Oct., 1883; coroner, 1884; ag. prin. civil med. offr., S.S., Feb., 1893, to May, 1894, and from June, 1900; confirmed in appt., Sept., 1900.

KERSHAW, CAPT. A. E., 4th Brigade, S. I. div. Roy. Artillery.—Govr. set., S. Australia, 1887; Canada (N.W. territory), 1881; Col. Engrs., Survey Depts., Br. Honduras 1884; priv. sec. to Gov. Sir R. T. Goldsworthy, K.C.M.G.; clk. of couns., 1884; ag. regisr., sup. ct., 1885; A.D.C., 1886; passed school of instruction, Woolwich, 1886; inspr., constab., 1886; dist. mag., Orange Walk, 1887; comdt. and ex officio mem. exec. and legis. couns., 1888; ch. comdt. of pol. and inspr. of prisons, Cyprus, Sept., 1892.

KERSHAW, THOS. HERBERT, C.M.G. (1903).—R.A., Trin. Coll., Oxon.; 2nd-class mod. classics, 1872; 2nd-class finals, 1874; Inner Temple equity scholar, 1876; called to the bar, Apr., 1877; regist. of deeds, Singapore, June, 1887; official assignee, Nov., 1890; ag. atty.-gen., Oct., 1894; legal adviser, Federated Malay States, June, 1896; ag. atty.-gen., S.S., March, 1901, to March, 1902.
KEUN, ALFRED HAVELOCK, M.B., B.CH.(Edin.).—House surg., gen. hosp., Singapore, 1st Apr., 1900; super. col. surg., S. Settlements., 17th Nov., 1900; col. surg., Prov. Wellesley, South, 1st Mar., 1901.
KEYT, F., M.D. (Abdn.).—Licentiate of medicine, Bengal Coll.; med. astt., Ceylon, 1863; astt. col. surg., 1863; col. surg., 1865.

KEYT, FREDERIC T.—Ed. Aberdeen Univ., M.D. (honours), D.P.H., 1932; M.B.C.M., 1888; mem. Br. med. assoc.; astt. col. surg., Br. Hond., 1892; ag. inspr. of schls., Oct., 1892, to June, 1893; dist. comsnr. and J.P., 1893 to 1902; port health offr., Hong Kong, 1902.

KIDD, JOHN, C.M.G. (1882).—B. 1821; priv. sec. to the late Lord Dudley Coutts Stuart, M.P. for Marylebone, 1847; sec. on the staff of Gen. Count L. Zamoyski in H.M.'s Imperial service in Turkey, 1855-56; priv. sec. to the Earl of Carnarvon, 1857; entered the service of the Canadian Govt., 1859; clk., 1858; ch. clk., gov.-gen.'s office, 1875; ag. gov.-gen.'s sec., 1876, 1877, and 1880; ret. on pension.
KIDD, J.—Mem. legis. asmnr., N.S. Wales; postmtr.-gen., in Dibbs' ministry, 1891-4.

KILPIN, ERNEST FULLER, C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1854; clk. of house of assem., Cape; clk. and shorthand writer, col. sec.'s office, Cape, June, 1876; priv. sec. to Hon. J. Gordon Sprigg, premier and col. sec., Feb., 1878, to June, 1880; clk. assist. of the house of assem., June, 1880; clk., 16th July, 1897; has served as sec. on various Cape gov't. comsrs., and acted as priv. sec. to premier, the Hon. Sir T. C. Scanlen, Feb. and Mar., 1883; sec. to the Anglo-German comn. on Angra Pequena and W. Coast claims, 1885; is a J.P. for the col.

KING, CHARLES KEANLEY BERRY.—Ed. Stonyhurst Coll.; matric. Lond. Univ., 1856; 1st clk. and 3rd sub-imigr. agt., Br. Guiana, Nov., 1871; 2nd sub-imigr. agt., June, 1872; sen. ditto, Apr., 1881; acted as imigr. agt.-gen., 1881-2, 1888-9, 1892-3-4-7; mem. of ct. of policy on last three occasions; also mem. poor law bd., and ag. chmn., 1889 and 1894; res. 1896. In 1895, one of the comsrs., to report on the best scheme for commutation by E. Indian immigts. of the right of return passages to India; and in 1896, senior mem. of special comte. to deal with allotment of ld. to E. I. immigts. in lieu of return passage.

KING, F. EUSTACE.—Ck. in col. sec.'s office, Barbados, from May, 1860; transf'd. to govt. sec.'s office, Br. Guiana, in June, 1868; inspnr. of pol. and just. of peace, Br. Guiana, Jan., 1871; comsry. of taxation, 1873; emigr. agt. at Barbados for Br. Guiana, 1875; dist. comsry. of taxation, 1884; treasry. departmental inspnr., Mar., 1896.
KING, GEORGE HENRY.—Ag. clk. reg'rs. office, Nevis, 1897; rev. offr., Nevis, Jan., 1898; govt. offr., St. Kitts, 1899.
KING, HENRY SANDFORD.—Surv'r. temporary staff, W. Aust., 1884; staff surv'r., 1887; inspnr. of mining surveys, Coolgardie goldfields, 1894; under-sec. for mines, 1899.

KING, JOHN.—Ck. to atty.-gen., Gibraltar, Feb., 1866; transf'd. to col. sec.'s office, June, 1870; 2nd clk., Nov., 1877; assisted in audit duties, 1875-83; ch. clk. and sec. to the bd. of health, 1883; asst. col. sec., 1891; has acted as col. sec. on various occasions.

KING, JOHN HAMPDEN.—Entered secretariat, Br. Guiana, Aug., 1880; priv. sec. to Lt.-Gov. Sir C. Bruce, 1887 and 1888; ag. supt. Onderneeming Reformatory schl., Aug., 1894, to Feb., 1895; ch. clk. secretariat, Sept., 1895; ag. supt. penal statlt., and inspnr. prisons, July, 1898, to Mar., 1899; asst. gov. sec., 1st Jan., 1900; ag. auditor-gen., 1900 and 1902. Is a J.P. for the colony.

KING, JOSEPH ARTHUR.—Copyist, reg'rs. office, Br. Guiana, Jan., 1878; ag. 3rd asst. sworn elk., Mar., 1882; 4th asst. sworn elk., Oct., 1882; 2nd ditto, July, 1883; passed exam. for certif. as sworn elk. and notary public, June, 1885.
KING, JOSEPH CHARLES.—Ed. Mt. St. Mary's Coll., Derbyshire; supernum. elk. imigrn. dept., Br. Guiana, Jan., 1888; 5th-cl. elk., Mar., 1893; 4th-class elk., Jan., 1894; 3rd-cl. elk., Apr., 1895.
KING, JOSEPH LEEMAN.—European master, govt. English schl., S. Stltmts., 4th Oct., 1889; asst. treasr., Singapore, 17th Apr., 1899.

KING, M.—Asst. comsnr., Limasol, Cyprus, Nov., 1878; regist., high ct., Mar., 1879; asst. comsnr., Paphos dist., Mar., 1881; local comdnt. mil. pol., and asst. comsnr., Limasol, Mar., 1883; comsnr. and sheriff, Nicosia dist., Apr., 1883; pres. of municipal and water comsrs. of Nicosia, in addition to other duties, 1884-9, and 1891-4; mem. legis. coun., 1893; Br. deleg. of Evcaf, 1894; ag. rec.-gen. and mem. exec. coun., May to July, 1895, June, 1896, to Feb., 1897; sec. Western Pacific coman., 1897.

KING, N. W.—Cler. asst. surg.-gen.'s office, Br. Guiana, Apr., 1893; 6th-cl. elk. off. of insp. of prsns., 1896; 5th ditto, June, 1897; ag. elk. of supplies, May, 1900, to Jan., 1903; ditto, June, 1903.

KING, T. M., I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1842; entered Queensland civ. ser., customs dept., as clk., Sept., 1863; passed through various grades of the dept.; apptd. collr. of customs, Sept., 1882; mem. of the imigrn. bd., Aug., 1884; under-sec. of the treasry., Nov., 1889; aud.-gen., Sept., 1901.

KING-HARMAN, SIR CHARLES ANTHONY, K.C.M.G. (1900), C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1851; ed. Chelt. Coll.; B.A., of Trin. Coll., Camb., 1872; M.A., 1879; priv. sec. to the gov. of the Bahamas, Nov., 1874, to Nov., 1879; priv. sec. to high comsnr., Cyprus, Dec., 1879; asst. comsnr., Cyprus, Dec., 1879; asst. to the ch. sec., Cyprus, Mar., 1881; ag. ch. sec. and mem. of exec. and legis. couns., June to Sept., 1882; auditor-gen., Barbados, 1883; elected mem. of house of assem., 1884-93; ag. col. sec. and mem. of exec. coun., 1884, 1885, and 1886; col. sec. Mauritius, 1893; ag. gov., Jan. to Aug., 1894, and Mar. to Sept., 1896; admnstr., St. Lucia, 1897; gov. S. Leone, Oct., 1900; high comsnr., Cyprus, 15th Apr., 1904.

KINGSTON, Rt. HON. C. C., P.C. (1897), M.P., K.C.—B. 1850; atty.-gen. and premier of S. Australia, 1893-9; pres. of the fed. convention, Sydney, 1897; represented the col. in London at H.M.'s Diamond Jubilee, 1897; one of the delegates sent to England in connection with passing of Federation Act; held office in 1st Commonwealth Ministry as Min. of State for Trade and Customs, Jan., 1901, to Aug., 1903.
KINTORE, Rt. HON. EARL OF, G.C.M.G. (1889).—B. 1852; lord in waiting, 1883; capt. of yeomen of the guard, 1886; privy councillor, 1886; gov. S. Australia, 1889-95; a lord in waiting, 1895.
KIRK, SIR JOHN, M.D., G.C.M.G. (1886), K.C.B., K.C.M.G. (1881), C.M.G. (1879).—B. 1832; formerly H.M.'s polit. agt. and consul-gen. at Zanzibar.
KIRK, JOHN WM.—Chief engrnr., Niger Coast Prot. steam yacht "Ivy," July, 1895; supt. engrnr., So. Nigeria, Oct., 1900.
KIRKHAM, JOHN KARKEEK.—Ag. harbrmr., Barbados, Sept., 1877, to June, 1878; asst. harbrmr., Dec., 1878; shipping master and surv'r. of shipping, Jan., 1881; ag. harbrmr., Oct., 1887.
KIRKPATRICK, IVONE.—Cadet Sarawak civ. ser., Limbang, Aug., 1892; extra offr., Simanggang, Jan., 1893; res. 2nd-cla., Mar., 1898, of Upper Rejang, May, 1899; res. 2nd-cla., Savong, Feb., 1901.
KIRKWOODE, RICHARD H.—Ck. col. sec.'s office, Fiji, 1888-91; 3rd clk., 1891-2; ch. clk. audit dept., 1892-7.

KITCHENER, LT.-COL. ADAM.—Statistical Eastern sys. of rly., Cape Town, Feb., 1881; stores examiner, July, 1882; construction bookkpr., Aug., 1883; transf'd. to Western System, Dec., 1884; head bookkpr., 1888; asst. acctnt., 1892; acctnt. dep. of agricul., Nov., 1896; ch. acctnt., May, 1898; acctntg. offr., May, 1899; served as lieut. with the P.A.O.C. artily. in Bechuanaland, 1897; prin. paymaster, col. def. force, Apr., 1901, with rank of major; lieut.-col., Oct., 1902; accntng. offr. in comsnr. of pub. wks. off., July, 1904.

KITSON, A. W.—2nd lieut Militia, Aug., 1879; lieut., 1881; capt., 1890; served with Roy. Niger Constabulary as sub-commdt., 1890-91; and as ag. commdt., Sept., 1891, to Apr., 1892; comsnr. of civil justice, Gold Coast, June, 1893.

KNAGGS, H. L.—Ck., San Fernando Hosp., Trinidad, Apr., 1885; 4th clk. sec. office, Oct., 1885; 3rd clk., July, 1892; 2nd clk., Nov., 1900; 3rd inspr. of immigrts., Aug., 1897, to Nov., 1900; ag. astl. astl. sec., 1895, 1899-1900; sec. to civ. ser. coman. and to rlys. and roads comsnr., 1894.

KNAGGS, SAMUEL WILLIAM.—Entered Trinidad service, Aug., 1875; ch. clk. comsec. office, July, 1892, and clk. of legis. coun., Jan., 1893; sec. to coman. to inquire into road system, 1886; ag. priv. sec. to Gov. Sir W. Robinson, 1886, 1889, and 1890-91; sec. to bd. of educn. and coll. coun., 1889-91; priv. sec. to Sir F. N. Broome, Aug. to Sept., 1891, and to the gov. of Hong Kong, Oct., 1891, to July, 1892; ag. comsnr., Tobago, 1897; ag. audr. gen., Trinidad, 1895; astl. col. sec., 1900; rec-gen. and M.L.C., 1901; ag. col. sec., 1902; ch. comsnr., Port of Spain, 1899 to 1903; col. sec., Barbados, 1903; ag. gov., 1903.

KNIGHT, F.G.—Jun. clk. prothonotary's office, Barbados, 1874; 4th clk. correspondence branch col. sec.'s office, 1979; 2nd clk. audit office, 1882; ag. clk. ch. on several occasions, and as govt. acctnt. to Barbados rly. co.; ch. clk. record branch col. sec.'s office, 1892; ag. clk. astl. ct. of appeal, 1894 and 1895.

KNIGHT, J. G.—Ed. Lodge gram. schl., and Codrington College, Barbados; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., Jan., 1883; prizeman in jurisprudence, 1884, and in common law, 1885; ag. judge, astl. ct. appeal, Barbados, 1888-9; judge, astl. ct. ap., July, 1889; is registr. of friendly societies, and visiting just. of Glendairy prison.

KNIGHT, JOHN LOCKE.—Ck. and Zulu interp. to the Upper Tugela magistracy, Feb., 1876, to May, 1878; ditto, Umsinga magistracy, May, 1878, to Aug., 1881, during which time he acted for the R.M. and admstr. three times; Aug., 1881, ck. and Zulu interp., and regisr. to the circuit ct., Newcastle dist.; Apr., 1882, ag. res. mag. and admstr. of native law, Newcastle; Feb., 1883, ditto, Umsinga div.; Oct., 1883, admstr. of native law, and Zulu border agt. to Lower Tugela div.; has held the hon. apptmts. of crown prosecutor and offr. for the sale of stamps at the magistracies named; commanded a native levy of over 1,000 strong during the Zulu war; acted for two months in 1884 as R.M., Lower Tugela div.; sub-comsnr., Zululand, Apr., 1887; astl. comsnr. and R.M., Entojaneni dist., June, 1887; R.M., Nkandhla dist., Jan., 1893; mag. Inanda div., 1900.

KNOCKER, FRED. WM.—Ed. Haberdashers' schl., Lond.; studied nat. sci. at Univ. Coll., Bristol; astl. curator, town museum, Leicester, Oct., 1892; sub-curator, civic museum, Bristol, Jan., 1898; settmt. offr., Negri Sembilan, Fed. Malay States, Feb., 1901; curator, state museum, Perak, May, 1904.

KNOLLYS, SIR C. C., K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1888).—B. 1848; ed. at Mag. Coll., Oxon.; open scholarship in 1858; 1st class math. mods., 1870; 2nd class final schls., 1872; B.A., 1873; M.A., 1877; sub-receiver, Trinidad, 1874; audit-gen. of Barbados, 1879; pres. of the educn. bd., 1882-3; col. sec., 1883; administered the govt. of Barbados, 1883; of St. Lucia, May, 1884, to May, 1886, and Nov., 1890, to May, 1891; of Grenada, 1892; of Trinidad and Tobago, 1894-5; 6-7-8-9, 1900-2-3, in the aggregate nearly 3 years; col. sec., Trinidad and Tobago 1894; gov., Leeward Is., 16th July, 1904.

KNOLLYS, MAJOR LOUIS FREDERICK, C.M.G. (1877).—B. 1847; Marlborough school; ensign, 32nd regt., 1866; capt., May, 1880; major, 26th Cameroonians, 1881; A.D.C. to the gov. of Mauritius, 1872-74, to gov. of Fiji, 1875; commanded force sent to reduce mountain tribes, 1876 (promoted to company and C.M.G.); comdt. armed native constab., and comsnr. in mountains, 1877; A.D.C. to gov. N. Zealand, 1880, and to gov. of Ceylon, 1883; inspr. gen. of constab., and dir. of prisons, Jamaica, 1886; inspr.-gen. of pol., 'Ceylon, Oct., 1891; inspr.-gen. of pol. and prisons, Sept., 1898; retired, 1902.
KNOWLES, JOSEPH.—Tide waiter, recvr.-gen.'s dept., Bahamas, 1890; res. just. and school-teacher, Ragged Island, 1897; clk., pol. off., Nassau, Sept., 1903.

KNUTSFORD, 1ST VISCOUNT (creat. 1895, created BARON 1888), THE RIGHT HON. SIR HENRY THURSTAN HOLLAND, BART., G.C.M.G. (1896), K.C.M.G. (1877), C.M.G. (1875).—B. 1825; succeeded to the baronetcy on the death of his father in Oct., 1873; ed. at Harrow and Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Inner Tem., Nov., 1849, and went to the N. Circuit; legal adviser to the sec. of state for the cols., Jan., 1867, and astl. under-sec. of state, Mar., 1870; resign., 3rd Aug., 1874, and elected mem. for Midhurst, 22nd Sept., 1874; mem. of the roy. coman. on the treatment of fugitive slaves, 1876; one of the roy. comans. on the defence of Br. possessions and commerce abroad, Sept., 1879; sec. to the treasur., June, 1885; and vice-pres. of the coun., Sept., 1885, and again in Aug., 1886; returned to Parlmt. as mem. for the Hampstead div. of Middlesex, 1885, and again 1886 and 1887; sec. of state for the cols., Jan., 1887, to Aug., 1892; pres. pol. confere., 1887.

KOCH, REV. C. A.—Astl. missionary to Bishop of Labuan, bishop's chaplain, regisr. of the diocese, 1866; diocesan chaplain, Ratnapura, Ceylon, Mar., 1866; col. chaplain, Jaffna, Aug., 1869; col. chaplain, Colombo, 1880.

KOCH, HENRY C.—Admitted to Natal bar as solnr., 1884, as advoc., 1887; crown prosecutor and legal adviser to Zululand Govt., Apr., 1889; is also regisr. of deeds, regisr.-gen. of births, deaths, and marriages; master, sup. ct., Natal, 1900; spec. comsnr. for Natal to administer oaths to surrendered burghers; resumed duty as mast., sup. ct., 1902.

KOCH, WILFRED VINCENT MILLE, M.B., C.M. (Edin., 1884), M.D. (Edin., 1895, highest honours, gold medal, thesis).—Late astl. in eye dept. of Edin. roy. infirmary; and astl. to the lectures on diseases of the eye and mental diseases, Edin. Univ.; and astl. to the gynecological dept., Edin. schl. of medicine; clin. astl., Royal London Ophthalmic hosp., 1901; clin. astl., hosp. for diseases of ear, nose, and throat, Golden Sq., 1901; ag. astl. med. offr., W. Riding Asyl., Sheffield, 1884; astl. med. offr., Hull Borough Asyl., 1884-88; late surg. to Monckton and Carlton Main Collieries, Barnaley; astl. surg., col. hosp., Port of Spain, 1889; ag. med. supt., Lepre Asyl., Trinidad, 1890-92; dist. med. offr., Diego Martin, 1890-92; ag. dist. med. offr., Port of Spain, 1892-93; ag. med. supt., Lepre Asyl., 1893; ag. dist. med. offr., Diego Martin, 1893; ag. med. supt., lun. asyl., June to Oct., 1894; in charge of col. hosp., San Fernando, June to Dec., 1899; and of col. hosp., Port of Spain, for various periods; ag. dist. med. offr., Couva, 1900; St. Joseph, 1902; lieut., 1891; capt., 1892; comdg. Port of Spain art. volrs.; surg. to permanent staff, Trinidad local forces; major, local force reserve, 1903; asst. surgeon, Hong Kong, 1903; J.P.; ag. med. offr. in charge of gaol and infectious diseases hosp., 1903-4.
KENIG, ETTRIKE.—Crown prosecutor, Mauritius, Nov., 1900; 2nd asst. col. sec., July, 1903; asst. col. sec., 9th Nov., 1903; ag. substitute procureur and advocate-general, 2nd Feb., 1904.

KENIG, LEON.—Senior clk., judicial dept., Seychelles, 2nd June, 1892; ag. 5th cls. clk., col. sec.'s office, Mauritius, in 1897, 1898 and 1899; 5th cls. clk. ditto, 1st July, 1899; 4th cls. ditto, 7th July, 1902; clk. to ex. coun. and coun. of govt., 9th Nov., 1903.

KOLLER, T. W.—Clk., acctns. dept., G.P.O., O.R.C., 1st July, 1900, to 31st Oct., 1902; clk. pub. health dept., 1st to 30th Nov., 1902; examr. of acctns., audit dept., 1st Dec., 1902; is also sec. to tender bd.

KORTRIGHT, L. M.—A.M.I.C.E., M.I.M.E.; surv. of roads and supt. of pub. wks., St. Kitts, 1879; official mem. legis. coun., St. Kitts and Nevis, 1886; J.P., and visiting justice of the gaol; mem. ex. coun., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1901.
KROHN, A. W.—Served in 33rd Co. Army Service Corps, as spec. clk., 1st March to 31st Aug., 1902; stationery dept., O.R.C., 1902.
KYNSLEY, SIR W. R., F.K.Q.C.P., KNT. BACH., C.M.G. 1888.—B. 1840; prin. civ. med. offr., and inspr.-gen. of hospitals, Ceylon, Feb., 1875; ret. 1897.

LABORDE, E. D., I.S.O. (1903).—1st clk., audit office, Tobago, Jan., 1882; dep. regiar., ag. confidential clk., to administrator, and clk. of couns., June, 1882; 2nd clk., administrator's office, St. Lucia, Feb., 1883; ch. clk., Sept., 1883; ag. 2nd clk., gov.-in-chief's office, Barbados, 1884; ch. clk. to gov., Windward Is., 1885; priv. sec. to Gov. Sendall, 1888; ch. of pol. and excise, St. Vincent, 1889; inspr. of prisons, 1889; supervisor of cust. and port offr., 1897; commended in despatches for relief wk. during hurricane, 1898; chmn. town bd., 1899; mem. ex. and legis. couns.; ag. col. sec., Apr. to May, 1901.
LACOSTE, THE HON. SIR ALEXR., K.C., KT. BACH. (1892).—B. 1842; ch. just., Court of King's Bench, Quebec; mem. of the Dominion privy coun., 1892.

LAFFAN, JAMES THOMAS, L.R.C.S.I., L.K., and Q.C.P.I. etc.—Asst.-col. surg., Larnaca, Cyprus, 1883; res. med. offr., Wyndham, W. Australia, 1886; ag. R.M., Wyndham, 1888; res. med. offr., Bunbury, 1889; pol. mag., Blackwood, 1889.

LAGDEN, SIR GODFREY YEATMAN, K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1894).—B. 1851; clerk, G.P.O., London, 1869-77, after compet. exam.; clk. to sec. to govt., Transvaal, 1878; priv. sec. to adminstr., 1878-81; sec. to exec. coun., 1880-81; priv. sec. to Sir W. Bellairs and Sir E. Wood, while administering govt., 1881; sec. to Transvaal sub-comsn. for compensation claims, 1881-82; engaged as war correspondent, Egyptian campaign, 1882-3; present at all engagements, including Tel-el-Kebir and capture of Cairo; on special financial duty, G. Coast, 1883; sec. and secttn., Basutoland, 1884; asst. comsnr., 1886; established magistracy in Masupha's country, 1886; acted as res. comsnr., May to Nov., 1890; ag. Br. comsnr., Swaziland govt. comtee. of adminstr., Apr., 1892; res. comsnr., Basutoland, 1893-1901; comsnr. for native affairs, Transvaal, Aug., 1901; mem. of ex. and leg. couns.; chrmn. of S. African inter-col. native affairs comsn., 1903-4.
LAIDLAW, ROBERT, M.D. (Aberdeen).—Asst. med. offr., Seychelles, 21st Jan., 1902; J.P., and chairman, local bd. of health, Praslin and La Digue Is., Seychelles.

LAING, ERNEST, ALEX. RONALD.—L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S., Edin., L.F.P.S., Glasgow, 1895; L.M. Rot. Hosp., Dub., 1894; (Queen's Coll., Belfast, Surg. Hall, Edin., and R.C.S., Dub.). Health offr., special plague duty, govt. of Egypt; res. pupil, Jervis-st. Hosp., Dub.; res., Rot. Hosp., Dub.; med. offr., Tardis dist., Bombay, and asst. health offr. on spec. plague duty, Calcutta; asst. surgeon, Hong Kong, 1902; and med. offr. in charge of govt. asylums.

LAING, J. E.—A.M.I.C.E., formerly ch. asst. engrnr. in municipalities of Manchester and Leicestershire; dist. survr., Sheffield; col. survr., S. Leone, Sept., 1894; title changed to dir. of P.W., Nov., 1896.

LAIRD, THE HON. DAVID.—Mem. educn. coun., P.E.I., 1872-3; one of the delega. who negotiated the admission of the island into the Dominion; sworn of the Canadian priv. coun. and min. of interior, 1873-6; lieut.-gov., N.W. Territories, 1876-8; was ch. comsnr. in negotiating treaty with Blackfeet Indians, 1877.
LAMB, BENJAMIN.—Lieut., R.F.A.; served in S. African war, Jan., 1900, to Aug., 1902; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to gov., Barbados, Oct., 1902, to Aug., 1903.

LAMB, JOHN CAMERON, C.B. (1895), C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1845; entered the P.O. in 1864; prin. clk., 1877; was one of the Br. delega. to the submarine cables protection confce. at Paris, May and Dec., 1886; asst. sec. to the P.O., 1889; 1st Br. deleg. to the internat. telegraph confce. at Paris, 1890, when he was also one of the representatives of the Cape and Natal; roy. comsnr. on electrical communication with lighthouses and lightships, June, 1892; 3rd sec. to the P.O., Apr., 1896; sen. Br. deleg. and "president de la commission des tarifs" at internat. telegraph confce. at Budapest, 1896, when he was also one of the representatives of the Cape, Natal, and N. Zealand; 3rd sec. to P.O., Apr., 1896; 2nd sec., Jan., 1897; pres., internat. telegraph confce., London, 1903, and sen. deleg. of U.K., Cape and Natal; also sen. Br. deleg. at internat. confce. on wireless telegraphy, Berlin, 1903.

LAMBERT, HENRY CHARLES MILLER.—Ed. at Eton Coll., and New Coll., Oxford; 1st class in classical mods., 1889; 1st class in final classical schls., 1891; entered as student of Lincoln's Inn; apptd. 2nd class clk. in the C.O. after open compet. exam., Feb., 1892; mem. of comtee. emigrts. information office, 1894; chrmn. of the comtee., 1897; acted as priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain for the purposes of the comtee. of the H. of C. on South Africa, 1897; 1st class clk., Nov., 1898.
LAMBTON, STEPHEN HARBORD.—Entered postal service, N.S.W., Sept., 1852; sec., Sept., 1866; dep. postm.-gen.

LAMINGTON, LORD (2nd Baron, creat. 1890), SIR CHARLES WALLACE ALEXANDER NAPIER COCHRANE-BAILLIE, G.C.I.E. (1903), G.C.M.G. (1900), K.C.M.G. (1895).—Only son of 1st baron; born, 1860; dep. lieut. Lanarkshire; lieut. Lanarkshire yeomanry, 1883; M.P. for N. St. Pancras, 1886-90; gov. Queensland, 1895-1901; gov. of Bombay, 1903.

LAMONT, WILLIAM J.—Grad. Moray House Coll., Edin.; entd. imp. custs. by comp. exam.; 1st place in U.K., 1894; superv. of custs., G. Coast, 1896; travelling and inspecting superv., G. Coast, 1901; asst. collr. cust., S. Leone, 1901; ag. collr. cust., 10th May to 17th Oct., 1902; J.P. for Colony.
LAMPSON, P. E.—Ed. Wesleyan mission schl., Anamaboe, G. Coast; asst. master, govt. schl., Accra, 1893.

LANGEVIN, EDUARD JOSEPH, N.P.—Admitted as a notary public, Lower Canada, 3rd Dec., 1858; a mem. of bd. of notaries of Lower Canada for several years; clk. of the Crown in Chancery, Canada, Jan., 1865; to same office for Dominion, July, 1867; dep. regisr.-gen., July, 1868; under-sec. of state of Canada, July, 1873; elected sec. to civ. ser. bd. of Canada, 1876; clk. of the Senate, 1883.

LANGEVIN, THE HON. SIR HECTOR LOUIS, K.C.M.G. (1881), C.B. (1868), K.C., LL.D.—B. 1826; called to the bar of Lower Canada, 1850; created a Q.C., 1864; mayor of Quebec from 1858 to 1861, and as such received H.R.H. the Prince of Wales on his landing in Canada, 1860; entered Canadian parlmt., 1857, and held a seat, first in the old legis. assem. and, since confederation, in the house of commons of the Dominion till 1892; mem. of the exec. coun., Canada, and solr.-gen. for Lower Canada, Mar., 1864; postmr.-gen., Nov., 1866; at confederation, July, 1867, sworn of the Queen's priv. coun. and was apptd. sec. of state of Canada until Dec., 1879, when apptd. min. of pub. wks., and whilst holding that office, was elected as the successor of the late Sir G. E. Cartier, Bart., as leader of the liberal-conservative party of Lower Canada; resig. office, Nov., 1873; postmr.-gen. in Sir John A. Macdonald's 2nd administration, Oct., 1878; then min. of pub. wks.; resig., 1891; was one of the framers of the present Canadian constitution, and in furtherance of the cause of confederation attended the col. confece. at Charlottetown and Quebec in 1864, and in London in 1866, when the Act of Union was carried through the imp. parlmt.; created a Knight Commander of the Roman Order of St. Gregory the Great, 1870; is the author of a prize essay on Canada, and of several other works.

LANGFORD, CHARLES WM.—Issuer, commissariat dept., Barbados, 1868; asst. storekeeper, control dept., Trinidad, Oct., 1869; clk., G.P.O., Trinidad, June, 1871; 2nd ditto, Nov., 1871; 6th clk. rec.-gen.'s office, Jan., 1875; 5th ditto, Feb., 1877; 4th ditto, May, 1878; 3rd ditto, May, 1879; excise officer, Apr., 1884.
LANGFORD, JOHN.—Clk., col. sec.'s office, Fiji, Oct., 1874; ch. clk. and clk. of legis. coun., Jan., 1878; regiar., sup.ct., and curator of intestate estates, June, 1884.

LANGHAM-CARTER, W.—Ed. at Bradfield Coll., Berks.; cadet, S. Sttlmts., 1890; passed final exam. in Malay, Dec., 1892; ag. dist. offr., Malacca, Jan., 1896; dist. offr., prov. Wellesley, Apr., 1897; continued to act at Malacca; ag. collr. of ld. rev. and regist. of deeds, Singapore, Dec., 1898; inspr. prisons, S.S., Jan. to May, 1901; asst. P.M.G., Penang, Dec., 1901; 2nd magis., Sing., Aug., 1902; ag. collr. of land rev., and regist. of deeds, Sing., Sep., 1902; apptd. to cls. III, B, Jan., 1903.

LANSDOWNE, 5TH MARQUIS OF (Gt. Brit., creat., 1784), K.G., G.C.M.G. (1884), G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E.—HENRY CHARLES KEITH FITZ MAURICE, eldest son of the 4th Marquis.—B. 1845; succeeded his father in 1866; was under-sec. for war from Apr., 1872, to Feb., 1874, and under-sec. India Office, May to Aug., 1880; gov.-gen. of Canada, 1883; viceroy of India, Nov., 1888-94; sec. of state for war, July, 1895; sec. of state for foreign affairs, Nov., 1900.
LAPRIMAUDAYE, C., M.V.O. (1903).—Commander, R.N.; capt. of port, Malta, Oct., 1888; supt. of pol., 1890; collr. of cust. with seat in exec. coun. and coun. of govt., 1903.

LARYMORE, CAPT. H. D., C.M.G. (1896).—B. 1867; ed. Westminster; entered Artil. Militia, 1886; lieut., R.A., Dec., 1886; capt., R.A., Sep., 1897; apptd. G. Coast constab., Feb., 1891; adjt., July, 1891; served in Jebu expdn., as staff offr. and sec. to Sir F. Scott; mentioned in despatches, and received thanks of gov. and exec. and legis. couns. of Lagos (medal and clasp); served in Kumasi expdn., 1896, as A.D.C. and headquarters camp commdt., (ment. in desps. and recd. spec. thanks for services; bronze star), ag. res., Kumasi, 1896-97; R.A. India, 1897-1901; R.A., S. Leone, 1901-02; 3rd cl. rea., N. Nigeria, Apr., 1902; 2nd cl. ditto, Oct., 1902; Haiku scholar, Christ's Coll., Camb., 1903.

LASCELLES, ALFRED GEORGE, B.A.—Univ. Coll., Oxon; 2nd class mod. hist.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1885; acted on several occasions as pres. of dist. ct. in Cyprus and as puisne judge; pres. of dist. ct., Papho, 1892; ditto, Larnaca, 1893; ag. puisne judge, June, 1895; ag. Queen's advoc., Feb., 1896; pres. dist. ct., Nicosia, 1896; Queen's advoc., 1898; atty.-gen. of Ceylon, 1902.
LASH, JAR. MILLER.—Ed. upper and middle schls., Peckham; schlmr., Kirkdale prison, Liverpool, 1876; gaoler, Grenada, 1880; supt. of prisons, 1890; gov., Glendairy prison, Barbados, 1894.
LAUDER, CHARLES R.—Clk., pres.'s office, Dominica, Dec., 1872; 4th revenue and quarantine offr., Nov., 1874; 3rd ditto, Feb., 1880; 2nd ditto, rum dept. keeper, 1883.
LAUDER, JOHN J.—Ag. 2nd clk., regr. and prov. marshal's off., Dominica, Feb., 1900: 2nd clk., Jan., 1901.
LAURENCE, P. M.—Puisne judge, Griqualand W., Cape Col., 1882; judge pres., 1888.

LAURIER, THE RIGHT HON. SIR WILFRID, P.C., G.C.M.G. (1897).—B. at St. Lin., Quebec, Nov. 20th, 1841; became a B.C.L. of McGill in 1864, and a Q.C. in 1880; entered parlmt., 1871, being elected for Drummond and Athabasca, and was re-elected in 1874 for the same constituency; min. of inland rev. in the Mackenzie ministry, Sept., 1877, which office he held until the resignation of that govt. in 1878. He was defeated at the gen. election of 1878, but was immediately afterwards elected for Quebec E. He was re-elected at the gen. elections in 1878, 1882, 1887, and 1891. Became leader of the Opposition in Canada in 1890, and premier in June, 1896, on the resignation of the govt. of Sir C. Tupper; was present as the representative of Canada in London at H.M.'s Diamond Jubilee; mem. joint internat. high couns., Quebec and Washington, 1898-9.

LAW, A. F. G.—Asst. comen., Cyprus, 1880; pres. dist. ct., Famagusta, 1883; principal forest offr., 1886; mem. legis. coun., 1887; ag. Queen's advoc., 1889; ag. ch. sec., 1890; Queen's advoc., 1892; puisne judge, S. Sttlmts., 1893; ag. ch just., S. S., Apr., 1901; senior puisne judge, Penang, Dec., 1901.

LAWLEY, HON. SIR ARTHUR, K.C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1860; sec. to administrator, S. Rhodesia, May, 1896; deputy-administr., Matabeleland, Nov., 1896; administrator, Matabeleland, Dec., 1898; ag. administrator, Mashonaland, July, 1899; gov. of W. Aust., Jan., 1901; lt.-gov., Transvaal, 15th Aug., 1902; ag. gov. of Transvaal and O.R.C. and high comsnr., 1903, chrmn. of ex. coun., pres. of leg. coun., mem. I.C.C.

LAWRENCE, W. T.—Asst. priv. sec. to Baron Henry de Worms, parly. sec. to the Bd. of Trade, 1885-6; in same capacity at the C.O., Feb., 1888, to Aug., 1892; attached to the internat. confce. on sugar bounties, 1887-88.

LAWRENCE, JOHN DUNCAN.—Ed. at Clepington schl., Dundee, and civ. serv. coll., Aberdeen; prelim. in arts, (higher mathematics and German) Aberdeen Univ.; govt. trav. schl. master, Falkland Is., Sep., 1896, to Jan., 1902; 2nd asst. opt. of schls., B. Guiana, Oct., 1903.
LAWRENCE, W. C.—Apptd. after exam., 3rd cls. messenger, C.O., 15th Apr., 1904.

LAWRIE, JAS. WM.—Asst. govt. engrnr. survr., Singapore, 12th June, 1883; resigned, 10th Oct., 1884; re-appointed asst. govt. marine survr., 26th Sep., 1887; govt. marine survr., Penang, 9th Mar., 1901.
LAZZARINI, MAJ.-GEN. C. C. JAMES, C.M.G. (1882).—B. 1819; of the Royal Malta Fencible Artillery, decorated for services in connection with the expdn. to Egypt, 1882.

LAYARD, SIR CHARLES PETER, KT. BACH. (1903).—B. 1849; Ed. Cheltenham Coll., and St. John's Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, June, 1873; advoc. sup. ct., Ceylon, Aug., 1873; solr. gen., July, 1878; ag. atty.-gen., May, 1891, to July, 1892; puisne judge, sup. ct., Oct., 1892; atty.-gen., Nov., 1892; ch. just., 26th Apr., 1902.
LEACH, JOHN VINCENT.—Ed. Stonyhurst Coll., England; admitted solr. sup. ct., Jamaica, 1881; res. mag., 1888.

LEARMONT, CAPT. JOHN HENRY, late capt. 12th Lancers.—Ed. at Eton; staff adjt., S. Australian mounted forces, 1886-89; A.D.C. to Earl of Kintore, 1889; officer's extra musketry certif., Hythe, 1889; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to Sir C. A. Moloney, gov., Lagos, and clk. to exec. and legis. couns., Feb., 1890; inspr. Bahamas constab. (which force he raised in Barbados), Aug., 1891; inspr.-gen. Leeward Is. pol., 1895, and inspecting offr. of defence forces; J.P., Antigua, St. Kitts, and Montserrat.

LEASK, J. T., M.B., Ch.M. (Edin.) 1879.—Col. surg., Malacca, Jan., 1891; ditto, Penang, Apr., 1893; ag. col. surg. res., Singapore, May, 1895, to Mar., 1896; ag. col. surg. Penang, Mar., 1896; col. surg. res., Singapore, Jan., 1897; ag. prim. civ. med. offr., S. S., Aug., 1902.

LE CAMP, CHARLES LOUIS THEODORE ADLER.—Ed. South African Coll.; passed Cape civ. ser. exam., Dec., 1888; apptd. to gov.'s staff on probation, Jan., 1889; on fixed estab. as 3rd-cl. clk., July, 1889; res. Cape serv. and apptd. to high comsnr.'s staff, Oct., 1890; 1st clk. to high comsnr., 1890; transf. to Bloemfontein as ch. clk. and regnr. to sec., O.R.C. Admintrn., Feb., 1901; ch. clk. to col. sec., O.R.C., June, 1902; clk. to ex. coun., June, 1902; ag. astt. col. sec., April to Aug., 1903.

LECLEZIO, SIR E. P. J., KNT. BACH. (1887), C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1832; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., 1858; graduate of the Faculty of Laws, Paris, 1858; ag. substitute procureur and advoc.-gen., Mauritius, in 1868, 1869, and 1870; mem. of the coun. of govt., 1869-1878; 1st puisne judge, 1879; ch. justice, 1883; ret., 1898.

LEE-JONES, JOHN WM.—Called to the Bar, Gray's Inn, 1903; astt. master, Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, Jan., 1885; ag. ch. clk. at the magistracy and clk. to the justices, Mar., 1894; ag. dep.-registrar and acctnt., sup. ct., Hong Kong, Sept., 1897; ag. dep.-registrar and appraiser, May, 1900; J.P., 1900; dep.-registrar and acctnt., sup. ct., June, 1902.

LEFEVRE, PHILIP LLEWELLYN.—Ed. at Victoria Coll., Jersey, and Brighton Coll., Sussex; clk., G.P.O., Cape Town, Feb., 1895; clk., atty.-gen.'s off., Feb., 1896; clk. to res. mag., Glen Grey, Feb., 1896; clk. to res. mag., East London, Sept., 1896; clk. sol.-gen.'s off., Grahamstown, Apr., 1899; clk., atty.-gen.'s off. (treason branch), Cape Town, Sept., 1900; B.A., Cape Univ., 1900; ch. clk. to atty.-gen., O.R.C., June, 1901.

LEFROY, GEORGE ANTHONY, A.M.I.C.E.—Asst. survr., W. Australia, 1879-80; priv. sec. to Sir J. H. Lefroy as admstr. of Tasmania; joined pub. wks. dept., Perak, 1882; ag. supt., pub. wks. and res. engrnr., Larut, 1885-7; ag. res. engrnr., Perak state rly., 1886-7; ch. survr. and in charge survey dept., 1887.

LEFROY, HON. HENRY B., C.M.G. (1903).—Elected M.L.A., W. Australia, 1892; re-elected, 1894 and 1897; min. of educn., May, 1897; min. of mines, May, 1898; ag. gen. for W. Australia in London, 1901; resigned, 1904.
LEGGE, C. A. S.—Clerical asst., B. Guiana, May, 1897; 6th class offr., cust., June, 1899; 5th class clk., pol. dept., Dec., 1900; lieut., B. G. Artillery, 1901.

LEGGE, CHAS. HENRY GLADMAN.—Clk. to the atty.-gen., Trinidad, Sept., 1867; 3rd clk. in the audit office, Apr., 1872; was clk. of the col. hosp., Port of Spain, Oct., 1873; 6th clk., rec-gen.'s dept., Nov., 1874; 2nd clk., audit office, Jan., 1875; ch. clk., patent office, 1880; 2nd clk., audit office, Demerara, May, 1880 (designation altered to "Inspr. of Accounts," Jan., 1881); ch. clk., Jan., 1882; ag. auditor-gen., 1883-6-8-9.

LEISK, JAMES RANKINE—Chartered acctnt., Glasgow; astt. recvr. of rev., Johannesburg, 22nd Sep., 1900; ch. clk. to cont. of treasry., Transvaal, 21st May, 1901; astt. sec. treasry., 1st Apr., 1902; sec. to treasry., 1st July, 1903; (Queen's S. African war medal with 3 clasps).
LE JUGE DE SEGRAIS, PAUL.—Assoc. M.I.C.E., govt. survr. and supt. of pub. wks., Seychelles, June, 1891; govt. engr. and archt., Mauritius, 14th Sept., 1896; ag. survr.-gen., 1896-97.

LE MESSURIER, H. W.—3rd landing waiter and astt. tide survr. of cust., Newfdld., 1894 to 1897; astt. collr. and sec. to bd. of rev., 1897; dep. min. of cust., with title of astt. collr., 1898 (commissariat clk. under astt. commissaries prior to the removal of the Imperial troops).

LEMON, ARTHUR HENRY.—Ed. at Merchant Taylors' Schl. and Exeter Coll., Oxford (scholar); B.A., 1887; cadet, Straits, 1888; priv. sec. to gov., Sept., 1889, to Jan., 1890; attached to Chinese protectorate, Penang, Aug., 1891; sec. to currency comte., June, 1893; ag. dist. offr., S. Malacca, Dec., 1893; passed in Malay, 1894; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, Apr., 1894; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., May, 1895; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., June, ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Aug., 1895; ag. dist. offr. Malacca, Oct., 1895; dist. offr. Prov. Wellesley, June, 1896, but continued to act at Malacca; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, July, 1898; ag. asst. P.M.G., Penang, Aug., 1898; 2nd asst. col. sec., Jan., 1902; ag. collr. of land rev. and reg. of deeds, Penang, April, 1903.

LENNOX, ARKILL NAPIER OLIVER.—Sixth clk., immigr. dept., Br. Guiana, 1882; 5th clk., 1884; 4th clk., 1885; awarded 2nd class certif. in Hindustani, 1885; acted as 1st clk., 1887, and July, 1889, to Jan., 1890; 3rd class clk., land dept., Jan., 1891; 2nd-class clk., Apr., 1891.

LEVEY, GEORGE COLLINS, C.M.G. (1878).—B.1835; mem. leg. coun., Victoria, 1861-8; was sec. to the Victorian comsn., and ag. comsnr. in charge of Victorian exhibits at Paris Exhibn., 1878; sec., Melbourne Exhibn., 1880-1; exec. comsnr., Victoria, Amsterdam Exhibn., 1883; sec., Adelaide Jubilee Exhibn., 1887, and Tasmanian Exhibn., 1894.
LE VIEUX, LEON.—Asst. survr., pub. wks. dep., Seychelles, 24th May, 1902.

LEWIS, ARTHUR KENNEDY.—Ed. at Wesleyan high schl. and C.M.S. grammar schl., S. Leone; med. dresser attached to Anglo-French boundary comsn., Dec., 1891 to Apl. 1892 (recd. thanks of govt.); clk. to D.C.; clk. of ct. of requests; registr. of births and deaths, W. dist., 1893 to 1895; 3rd clk. regiar.-gen.'s dept., 1895 to 1897; 2nd clk., atty.-gen.'s off., 1898; travelled with and clk. to atty.-gen. during trial of insurgents by Judge G. A. Bonner, 1898; transport clk., N.A. dept., 1899; ditto P. W. D., Jan., 1900 to July, 1901; offr. in charge of transport on govr.'s tours, 1899-1901; registr. offr., G.P.O., Freetown, Aug., 1901 to Jan., 1903; asst. postmr., Gambia, Feb., 1903.

LEWIS, E. CORNEWALL.—Entered Govt. office, St. Vincent, as supernum., May, 1877; clk. to Kingstown pol. mag., 1880; ag. col. regist., 1883; acted as provost-marshal, 1884; ag. col. postmr., 1885; ag. regist., 1886; dep. coroner, Kingstown, 1886; acctnt. of pub. wks. dept., Grenada, 1887; col. postmr., St. Vincent, 1889; ag. col. postmr., Grenada, Jan. to Mar., 1889; ag. regist. sup. court, St. Vincent, Nov., 1889, to Nov., 1890; ex officio inspr. of prisons, regist. of the vice-adm. court, ag. col. postmr., Grenada, Aug., 1891; resumed col. postmr., St. Vincent, Jan., 1892; J.P., 1892; asst. postmr.-gen., Hong Kong, 1899; J.P., 1899; ag. postmr.-gen., 1900, 1901, 1902, and 1903.

LEWIS, CAPT. E. H.—Joined 21st Lancers in India, 1892; present with that regt. at battle of Omdurman, 1898 (medal and Khedive's medal); served with W.A.F.F. from July, 1899, to May, 1903; served with expedn. for relief of Kumasi, 1900 (Ashanti medal); present at capture of Kontagoro, 1901 (ment. in despa., medal with clasp for N. Nigeria); present at capture of Kano (clasp for N. Nigeria) 1903; apptd. 3rd cls. res., N. Nigeria, May, 1903.

LEWIS, JOHN CHRISTOPHER.—Ent. Trinidad service, 1861; postmr., San Fernando, Sept., 1869; received the thanks of inhabitants for his services in 1882; ag. 2nd clk., G.P.O., 1868; ag. P.M.G., 1886 and 1891; comsnr. for affidavits and J. P.; received thanks of inhabitants of San Fernando in 1882 for his zeal in discharge of his duties, and the legis. coun. in Jan., 1893, passed a unanimous resolution that his long services were deserving of recognition.

LEWIS, JOHN E. A.—B.A., Caius Coll., Camb., 1883; extra offr., Sarawak civ. ser., Oct., 1886; mag., and manager Sarawak govt. printing off., Aug., 1888; ag. princ. insp. of pol. and prisons, Nov., 1889, to May, 1890; Sept., 1894, Dec., 1895; clk. to gen. coun., July, 1897; res., 2nd class, Sept., 1898.

LEWIS, JOHN PENRY.—Ed. at Mill Hill Schl. and Queen's Univ.; B.A., 1876; M.A., 1882; writer, Ceylon civ. ser., July, 1877; ag. pol. mag., Matare, Jan., 1881; ditto, Pet. Pedro, 1883; ag. office ast. to govt. agt., Jaffna, N.P., 1883; pol. mag., Gam-pula, 1884; ag. office ast. to govt. agt., cent. prov., Kandy, 1885; ag. comsnr. of requesta, Colombo, 1886-6; office ast. to govt. agent, C.P., 1887; ag. dist. judge, Matare, 1888; ast. agt., Negombo, 1891; ditto Vavuniya Vilan Kulam, Apr., 1896; ag. ast. govt. agt., Chilan, Apr., 1896; ag. dist. judge, Kurunegala, June; Negombo, Sept., 1896; special offr. under waste lands ordnec. No. 1 of 1897; ag. govt. agt., N. prov., 19th May, 1902; ditto, Cent. prov., 7th Nov., 1902; ditto, N. prov., 13th June, 1903.

LEWIS, HON. SR NEIL ELLIOT, K.C.M.G. (1902), C.M.G. (1901), B.A.—B.1858; atty.-gen. in Dobson-Hervy ministry, Tasmania, 1892-4; premier and atty.-gen. in Lewis ministry, 1900; joined lat Commonwealth ministry as min. without portfolio, Jan., 1901.

LEWIS, SR WALTER LLEWELLYN, KT. BACh. (1904) M.A.—Gold Medallist, Queen's Univ. (Ireland), 1869 and 1871; called to the bar, Md. Tem., Jan., 1876; South Wales and Chester circuits; stip. justice, Trinidad, Jan., 1883; chmn. of coman. on agricultural contracts; chmn. of directors, public service widows' and orphan fund, 1890-1900; puisne judge, 1893; ch. justice, British Honduras, 1900.

LEY, ANDREW K.—Priv. sec. to gov. Labuan, 1882; ag. col. treas. and auditor, 1882 to 1885, and judge gen. ct., 1884-5; mag., ct. of requests, and P.M.G. Sarawak, Oct., 1886; also shipping master and coroner, 1st div.

LEYS, PETER, C.M.G. (1890).—B.1851; ed. at Univa. of Glasgow and Edinburgh, of which latter he is a graduate in medicine; entered col. service, Labuan, Feb., 1876; adminstd. the govt. 1881-7; held a coman. as H.B.M.'s consul-gen. for Borneo; ret., 1889.

LIGHTBOURN, JAMES THOMAS.—Ed. at Bahamas collegiate institute; supernumerary, treasy. and cust. dept., Bahamas, Jan., 1885; 3rd clk., treasy., Dec., 1885; 2nd clk. and bookkeeper, treasy. dept., 1895; has held several acting appointments.
LINCOLN, GABRIEL.—Copyist, col. sec.'s off., Mauritius, 24th June, 1884; clk. to ex. coun. and coun. of govt., 1st June, 1901; ch. clk., col. sec.'s off., 9th Nov., 1903.

LINDER, CONRAD.—Served under late O.F.S. govt. as postmr. and J.P. at Wepener, 1873; resident J.P. at Wepener, 1874; landdrost's clk. at Bethulie, 1878; govt. claim inspr., Oliphantston-tein, 1880; landdrost, Boeshof, 1882; regist. of deeds, 1894; receiver of rev., Bloemfontein, O.R.C., Apl., 1900; regist. of deeds, Mar., 1901.
LINLITHGOW, 1ST MARQUIS OF (U.K. creat. 1902), 7TH EARL OF HOFETOUP (Soot.), P.C., K.T. (1900), G.C.M.G. 1889), G.C.V.O. (1900).

LISTER, JOSEPH STORR, L.S.O. (1903).—Asst. to man. of Tukva tea and cinchona estate, Darjeeling, May, 1870; passed lower standard exam. in Hindustani and an exam. in land surveying and levelling; apptd. to the forest dept., Punjab, in charge of Changa Manga, Lahore, and Umballa plantations, 1871; transfd. to Hazara, to assist dep. conservator in exploring and reporting on hill forests of Khagan; on special duty to Kulu and Kangra in charge of Nurpur sub-division; left India owing to ill-health, 1874; supt. of plantations, Cape, Jan., 1875; J.P. and road mag., Aug., 1875; in conjunction with other duties, custodian of ex-chief Langalibalele and ex-king Cetewayo during their detention at Uitvlugt and Oude Molen; established plantations at Worcester, Beaufort West, Tokai, and Kluitjes Kraal; sec. to sub-committee, for wood and vegetable products, Indian and Col. Exhib., 1886; conservator of forests, E. diva., Sept., 1888.

LLEWELYN, Sir ROFT B., K.C.M.G. (1898), C.M.G. (1889).—B. 1845; extra clk., C.O., London, from 1868; regis. in col. sec.'s office, Jamaica, Nov., 1869; was nominated as one of a comsnr. apptd. to inquire into prison discipline in Jamaica, 1873; priv. sec. to Sir J. P. Grant, July, 1873; clk. of priv. coun., Jamaica, 1877; comsnr. for Turks Is., 1878; admstr. of Tobago, 1885, admstr., St. Vincent, 1888; ag. admstr., Grenada, 1888; admstr., St. Lucia, 1889, with dormant comsnr. as admstr., Windward Is.; admstr., Gambia, 1891; African war medal with two clasps, 1891-2, and 1893-4; gov. Windward Islands, Oct., 1900.
LOYD, AUGUSTUS GEORGE.—Ch. clk. and cashier, col. treas., S. Leone, Dec., 1896; ag. col. treas., Apr. to July, 1899; ag. asst. col. treas., July, 1899.
LOYD, JAMES S.—Ed. at Codrington Coll., Barbados; apptd. to 3rd clerkship, customs, Barbados, Nov., 1876; 2nd indoor offr., customs, Dec., 1890.

LOCKE, R. F.—Apptd. cons. agt. in Oil Rivers (now S. Nigeria) Protec., July, 1892; ag. v.-cons. and dep. comsnr., Benin river, May to Nov., 1894; Brohemie expdn., 1894, mentd. in Adml. Bedford's desps., med. and clasp; apptd. dist. comsnr., Apr., 1896; ag. v.-cons. and dep. comsnr., Warri, Sapele, and Benin Riv. Dista., May to Dec., 1896; one of two survivors Benin massacre, Jan., 1897; apptd. dep. comsnr. and v.-cons., Apr., 1897; now divisi. comsnr., West Div., S. Niger Protec.

LOCKHART, J. H. STEWART, C.M.G. (1898); F.R.G.S., M.R.A.S.—B. 1858; Edin. Univ.; Greek medallist, etc.; apptd., after compet. exam., to a Hong Kong cadetship, 1878; attached to the C.O. for one year, 1879; passed cadet, 1882; supt. opium revenue, Mar., 1883; asst. col. sec. and asst. auditor-gen., Aug., 1883; ag. regis. gen., 1884 and 1885; regis.-gen., 1887, and M.L.C., 1889; mem. exec. coun., 1891; chairmn. bd. of examiners in Chinese, 1891; ditto govrmg. body Queen's Coll., 1894; ag. col. sec., Apr., 1894; col. sec. and regis.-gen., Mar., 1895; special comsnr. to report on the extension of the bndry. of H. Kong, 1898; representative of Gt. Br. to delimitate bndry., for both of which reed. thanks of S.S., 1899; rector of coll. of medicine for Chinese, 1895-1902; comsnr. of Weihaiei, 1902.

LOCKHART, NORMAN.—Fourth revenue offr., Dominica, Feb., 1880; ag. postmr., 1881; ag. excise offr., May, 1882; 3rd revenue offr., 1883; quarantine offr., 1886; ag. mag., Nevis, 1891; ditto, Dist. E., Dominica, 1892, 1895, 1896; mag., Anguilla, 1896; ag. mag., Dominica, 1897; asst. treas. and regis. of shipping, St. Kitts-Nevis, 1897; ag. treas., 1899, 1900, 1901, and 1902. treas., Grenada, 1903.
LOCKYER, E.—Examr. of accts., audit dept., O.R.C., 26th June, 1903.
LONG, THOMAS.—Topographical survr. in the survr.-gen.'s office, Newfild., Mar., 1872; 1st clk., Sept., 1882; dep. min. agricul. and mines, 1898.

LONGLEY, HON. J. WILBERFORCE.—B.A., Acadia Coll., N. Scotia, 1871; M.A., 1875; called to the bar, N.S., Sept., 1875; comsnr. sup. ct., 1876; elected to Prov. Parlmt., June, 1882; sworn in mem. of exec., July, 1884; atty.-gen., May, 1886; comsnr. for revising and consolidating the law and framing Judicature Act, Oct., 1882; editorial writer "Acadian Recorder" since 1873.
LORANGER, L. O., K.C.—Atty.-gen., prov. of Quebec, Canada; apptd. a judge of sup. ct. of Quebec, 1882.

LORANS, HENRI, M.B.C.M., and D.P.H., Edin.—Police and prison surg., Mauritius, 1884; poor law med. offr., Port Louis, 10th Jan., 1884; med. inspr., 15th Nov., 1895; ag. diretr., med. and health dept., 1900 and 1902; nom. mem. of coun. of govt.

LORNIE, J., M.A., B.Sc. Edin.—Cadet, S. S., Nov., 1899; passed final exam. in Malay, Apr., 1901; ag. head of Malay Coll., Malacca, May, 1901; ag. asst. regis., sup. ct., and mag. and sheriff, Malacca, Apl., 1903; ag. dist. offr., Penang, Jan., 1904.

LOVEGROVE, CHARLES ARTHUR, A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Charterhouse; asst. to Mr. W. Wilson, C.E., Westminster, 1879; contractor's engrn., Coleford and Monmouth rlyv., 1881; asst. to Mr. W. Wilson, C.E., 1884; dist. engrn., P.W.D., Ceylon, 1886; ag. prov. engrn., W. Prov., 1897; ditto, N.W. Prov., 1897; ditto, S. Prov., and mem. mun. coun., Galle, 1898; ag. prov. engrn., E. Prov., 1898; ag. asst. dir. of pub. wks., Ceylon, 1900; prov. engrn., Uva, 1900; asst. dir., P.W., 1903.

LOVELL, SIR FRANCIS HENRY, KT. BACH. (1900), C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1844; col. surg., S. Leone, Dec., 1873; med. offr., imigrn. dept., Mauritius, 1878; ch. med. offr. and pres. gen. bd. of health, 1879; mem. coun. of govt., 1885; surg.-gen. and med. offr. of health, and mem. legis. coun., Trinidad, Sept., 1893-1901; also mem. exec. coun., 1894; ret., 1902.

LOW, ALFRED M.—Ed. at Aldenham; open scholarship at Pem. Coll., Camb., 1890; B.A. class. tripos, 1893; ag. prof. of classics, Codrington Coll., Barbados, 1896; 3rd master, Queen's Roy. Coll., Trinidad, June, 1897.

LOW, SIR HUGH, G.C.M.G. (1889), K.C.M.G. (1883), C.M.G. (1879).—B. 1824; sec. to gov't. of Labuan, 1848; pol. mag., 1850; admnstr. the govt. of Labuan five times between 1885 and 1876; Br. ree., Perak, 1877; negotiated treaty of Protection with Sultan of Brunei, 1888; ret., 1889.

LUCAS, CHARLES PRESTWOOD, C.B. (1901), B.A.—B.1863; ed. at Winchester Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; open exhibitioner; 1st class in mods., 1873, and 1st class in final classical schools, June, 1876; Chancellor's prize for Latin essay, 1877; called to the bar by the Soc. of Lincoln's Inn, Easter, 1885; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in the office of the sec. of state for the cols., Apr., 1877; priv. sec. to Sir R. Herbert, Dec., 1881; Earl Granville, Feb., 1886, and again to Sir R. Herbert, Aug., 1886; also chairman of comttee. of emigrants information office till 31st Dec., 1896; 1st class clk., Feb., 1892; author of "Historical Geography of the British Colonies"; prin. clk., Sept., 1896; asst. under-sec. of state, June, 1897.

LUCIE-SMITH, A. VAN W.—Ed. at Rugby; solr., Br. Guiana, 1877; called to bar, Mid. Tem., 1881; ag. solr.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1882 to 1887; pres. dist. ct., Famagusta, Cyprus, 1887; Limasol, 1892; ag. Queen's advoc., 1893; ag. judge, cons. ct., Constantinople, 1896; res. mag., Kingston, Jamaica, Nov., 1896; puisne judge, B.G., Jan., 1898; ag. ch. justice, 1900-01; one of the compilers of the "Rules of Court," 1900.

LUCKIE, D.M., F.S.S.—Trained in a lawyer's office in Montrose, Scotland; in 1862 emigrated to N. Zealand, as editor and part proprietor of the "Nelson Colonist," which he largely popularised; in 1872 was elected mem. of parl. for Nelson City; in 1873 became editor of the "Southern Cross, and when it was amalgamated with the "New Zealand Herald," the leading journal in the col., became ch. editor of the combined papers; asst. comsnr. of govt. assurance, 1878.

LUGARD, BRIG.-GEN. SIR F. D., K.C.M.G. (1901), C.B. (1895), D.S.O.—B. 1858; ed. Rossall and Sandhurst; 1st comn., 9th foot, May, 1878; capt., Aug., 1885; major, Aug., 1896; temp. col., 1898; to India, Sept., 1878 active service, Afghanistan, Sept., 1879-80; medal, Soudan, Feb. to Nov., 1885; medal, clasp, "Tofrek" Khedive's Star; despatched to Birmah, Oct., 1886, to Aug., 1887; mentioned in despatches, D.S.O. and medal. To Africa, Feb., 1888; commanding expdn. against slave traders; very severely wounded. To East Africa, Nov., 1889; exploration of Sabakh; admnstd. Uganda; wrote "Our East African Empire."
