July, 1894, went to Borgu in comd. expedition, and made treaties; hit by arrow, returned, Apr., 1895; Feb., 1896, led expedition across Kalashari for Br. Charterland Co.; apptd. comn., and comdt., West African frontier force, Aug., 1897; went out Mar., 1898; high comn. for Northern Nigeria, 29th Dec., 1899.

LUMB, CHARLES FREDERICK.—Ed. at Liverpool Inst. (Yates exhibitioner), and Downing Coll., ('sch. (scholar and prizeman in maths, nat. sci., and law); B.A., math. tripes, 1870; M.A., and LL.M., 1877; LL.D., 1892; barrister of Linc.'s Inn, 1874, and practised on N. circuit; vice-pres. of Liverpool inst., and mem. of Liverpool schl. bd.; prosecutor for the mint, Feb., 1886; 2nd puisne judge, Trinidad, 1887; mem. educn. comn., 1887; of bd. of educn., 1887; of Roy. Coll. coun., 1888; of library comn., 1889; drafted dist. cts. and educn. ordinances and rules thereunder; puisne judge, Jamaica, 1892; mem. of bd. of educn., 1893-4; ag. ch. justice, 1895-6; chmn. of the education comn., 1897-8.
LUSCOMBE, CAPT. G. A.—Local comdt. of pol., Cyprus, 1898.

LUSHINGTON, CHARLES MORANT.—Ceylon writer, 1876; mag., 1878; asst. to govt. agt. N.W. prov., 1883; dist. judge, Kegalla, Mar., 1889; ditto, Ratnapura, July, 1889; ag. asst. govt. agt., Negombo, Oct., 1890; at N'Ellya, 1892; at Matale, 1894; at N'Ellya, 1895; at Trincomalee, 1896; ag. govt. agt., E. prov., 13th June, 1903.

LYNCH, G. W. A.—Ed. at Westminster, Genville and Caius Coll., Camb. (B.A., 1883), and St. Thomas's hospital, Lond.; M.R.C.S. Eng.; L.R.C.P. Lond., 1885; M.B., B.C., Camb., 1886; govt. med. offr., Fiji, 1890; sen. med. offr., 1898; ag. ch. med. offr., Oct., 1899, to Jan., 1900; ditto, and ag. agt.-gen. for immigr., Mar., 1903, to Jan., 1904.

LYNE, SIR WILLIAM JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1900).—B. 1844; elected to N. Wales parlmt., 1880; sec. pub. wks., 1885-7, and again 1891; mem. exec. coun.; prim. and col. treas., Sept., 1899; joined first Commonwealth ministry as min. of state for home aff., Jan., 1901.

LYONS, CAPT. F. W.—Ed. at Portarlington school, and Trin. Coll., Dublin; joined 80th regt. as 2nd lieut., 15th Feb., 1878; capt., 1884; adjutant, 1885; adjut. of volunteers, 1887-90; capt. reserve of officers, 1890; ag. asst. sup. of pol., S.S., 5th Mar., 1891, to 31st Mar., 1893; apptd. asst. comn., Perak Sikhs, 1st Apr., 1893; acted as capt., sup. of pol., and prisons, Selangor, Aug., 1893, to Feb., 1895; served in S. Africa, 1878-80, medal with clasp; served in Soudan, 1886-86, medal and Khedive's Star; served in Pahang during disturbances, and recd. thanks of S. of S., 1894; acted as chief pol. offr., Perak, 1900-2; apptd. deputy sup. of pol., Hong Kong, 7th Sept., 1902; ag. capt. sup. of pol., etc., 1903-4.

LYTTELTON, RT. HON. ALFRED, P.C., M.A., Camb.; Hon. M.A., Oxford; K.C.—B. 1857; ed. at Eton, and Trin. Coll., Camb.; barrister: bencher, Inner Tem., 1899; legal priv. sec. to Sir H. James, attorney-gen., 1882 to 1886; recorder of Hereford, 1894; recorder of Oxford, 1895; dep. high steward of Univ. of Camb., 1899; M.P. for Leamington, Warwick, 1895; chmn. of Transvaal concessions comn., S. Africa, 1900-1; apptd. by Newfd. govt. as arbitrator in case of the Reid railway contract, 1902; S. of S. for the colonies, Oct., 1903.
MAASDORP, CHRISTIAN GEORGE.—Atty.-gen., Transvaal, Sept., 1878; resign., Jan., 1880; puisne judge, sup. ct., Cape, Nov., 1885; Superiorct., Cape Town, 1896.

McALLISTER, ANDREW.—Asst. survr., Oi Rivers Prot. (now So. Nigeria), Sept., 1892; ag. survr.-gen., Feb. to Oct., 1894, and Aug., 1897, to Dec., 1898; survr.-gen., Jan., 1899; dir. of pub. wks., Jan., 1900.

MACARTHUR, MALCOLM STEWART H.—Ed. Queen's Coll., Oxford; cadet S. Stiltns., Nov., 1895; ag. dep. registr. of deeds, Penang, 1896; passed final exam. in Malay, Apr., 1897; ag. priv. sec. to gov., S.S., Jan., 1897; ag. dist. offr., Balik Pulau, Aug., 1897; and 3rd mag., Penang, 1898; ag. 2nd astt. col. sec., Apr., 1899; ag. astt. sec. to gov't. Selangor, Apr., 1900, to Jan., 1901; dist. offr., Penang, Jan., 1901; ag. 2nd astt. col. sec., Jan. to May, 1901; ag. collector of land rev. and registr. of deeds, Sing.; also ag. sec. to high comn. of F.M.S., May, 1902; ag. comn. of ct. of requests, Sing., Aug., 1903; astt. supt. of Indian immigts, in addition to other duties, 13th Nov., 1903; ag. 2nd astt. col. sec., Nov., 1903; confirmed, July, 1904; seconded for spec. serv. under F.O. in Brunei, Apr., 1904.
MACARTHUR, WM. HENRY, M.R.C.V.S.—Govt. vet. surgeon, Penang, S. Stiltns., 21st May, 1892.
MACASKIE, JOHN COCHRANE.—Pres. dist. ct., Cyprus, 1895.
MACAULAY, JOHN DAVIDSON.—Extra clk. col. sec.'s office, S. Leone, 1874; in customs, 1875; tide waiter, 1877; landing waiter, 1879; clk. of customs, 1883.

MACBRIDE, ROBERT KNOX, C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1844; suptg. offr., pub. wks. dept., Ceylon, 1866; irrigation asst., 1872; prov. asst., N.-Cent. prov., 1874; ditto, Uva, 1876; S. prov., 1878; and Cent. prov., 1882; dir. of pub. wks., Oct., 1885.

McCALL, GEO. H.—Ed. at Queen's Roy. Coll., Trinidad; ag. sub-collr., No. 4 dist., Tobago, Aug., 1895; clk. to comsnr. financial bd. and bd. of educn., Jan., 1896; asst. clk. to warden during expenditure of loan, Oct., 1896; ag. postmtr., Dec., 1896; clk. to comsnr. financial and educn. bds. and asst. clk. to warden, Jan., 1897; twice ag. harbourmr., excise offr., and landing waiter, Tobago, 1898; landing waiter and clk. treasry., Jan., 1899; transf. to cust., Trinidad, Feb., 1899; cust. offr., July, 1899.

McCALLUM, COLONEL SIR HENRY EDWARD, R.E., G.C.M.G. (1904), K.C.M.G. (1898), C.M.G. (1887), A.D.C. to the Queen (1900).—B. 1852; entered the Roy. Mil. Acad., Woolwich, July, 1869; passed first out of 52 cadets, 1871; after two years' practical training at Chatham, proceeded to Portsmouth (1874), and apptd. supt. of telegraphy, S. dist.; transf. to office of inspr.-gen. of fortifications; priv. sec. to gov. S. Settlements, Sir William Jervois, and attended him on various missions to the native states of the Malay Peninsula; mentioned in despatches for the assistance given in preparing a project for the defence of Singapore, and again in Dec., 1876, for services in connection with Perak comsh. of inquiry; suptg. engnr. admiralty works, Hong Kong, Apr., 1877; in Apr., 1878, selected to proceed to Singapore to adopt measures for the defence of that port; attached to the office of inspr. of wks., roy. arsenal, July, 1879, to July, 1880, when apptd. dep. col. engnr., S. Sttlmts.; is a Pollock medalist of 1871; the annual Fowke medallist for 1874; is an assoc. of instt. of civ. engnr., etc.; col. engnr. and survr.-gen., S. Sttlmts., 1884; men. of the exec. and legis. couns.; pres. Singapore municipality, Feb., 1881, to July, 1886; apptd. to construct new fortifications of Singapore, Mar., 1885; comdt. Singapore volr. art., 1888; special comsnr. in Pahang during disturbances, 1891; received thanks of S. of S.; gov. Lagos, 1897; gov. of Newfoundland, 1898; gov. of Natal, 1901.
MACDOUGALL, JOHN LORNE, C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1838; Auditor-gen. of Canada, Aug., 1878.

McDOUGALL, JOSEPH EASTON.—Son of Wm. McDougall, C.B.; ed. U.C. Coll.; called to bar, Feb., 1870; lecturer on criminal law and torts to law society of Ontario, 1879-83; Q.C. 1883; jun. judge ct. st. York, Mar., 1883; sen. judge, Sept., 1885; judge maritime ct., Ontario, Sept., 1885; chmn. of pol. bd. for Toronto, 1886.

MACDOUGALL, THE HON. WILLIAM, C.B. (1867).—Ed. at Victoria Univ., Cobourg; admitted an atty., Upper Canada, 1847; called to the bar, Hilary, 1862; founded (1848) the “Canada Farmer,” subsequently merged in the “Canadian Agriculturist,” which he published and edited until 1858; founded (1850) the “North American,” a Reform newspaper, of which he was chief editor until merged in the Toronto “Daily Globe” in 1857; leading political writer on the “Daily Globe,” from 1857 to 1860; represented Canada at the New York Exhibn., 1853; sec. of the Constitutional Reform Assoc. of Upper Canada, 1859; mem. of the exec. coun. and commr. of crown lands, Canada, May, 1862, to Mar., 1864; prov. sec. from June, 1864, and ag. min. of marine from July, 1866, until the union of the provinces, 1867; sworn of the Queen’s privy coun. in Canada; min. of pub. wks. for the Dominion from July, 1867; lieut.-gov. of Rupert’s Land and the N.W. territories, Oct., 1869; chmn. of the coun. to open trade relations with the W. Indies, Mexico, and Brazil, 1865-6; deleg. to the Charlottetown Union confce., 1864; to that at Quebec in the same year; and to the col. confce. in London to complete terms of union of B.N.A. cols., 1866-7; deleg. to England, with late Sir G. E. Cartier, to confer with imperial authorities on the defences of the Dominion, and for the acquisition of the N.W. territory, 1868; commr. for Ontario, for the settlement of N.W. boundary of the prov., 1871; sent to England by Dominion govt., 1873, as special commr. to confer with imperial govt. on subject of fisheries; returned to Canadian legislature in 1858, where he sat until the union of 1867, when returned to house of commons; elected to Ontario assem. in May, 1875; resig. Sept., 1878, and again returned to house of commons; ret. from public life, 1882, and resumed practice of his profession in Ottawa. Is a K.C.
McDOWELL, DONALD KEITH, C. M. G. (1901).—Prin. med. offr. of W.A.F.F.; accompanied Ashanti expdn., 1900, in that capacity; prin. civ. med. offr., Straits Settlements, Mar., 1903.

McEWAN, THOS. SMITH.—Assoc. M.I.C.E.; asst. construction engrn., Cape govt. rlyws., 1874; maintenance engrn., 1877; dist. engrn., 1880; res. engrn., Bethulie junction, Jan., 1891; East London, 1st Jan., 1893; Port Elizabeth, 1st May, 1894; res. engrn., western system, and dist. engrn., Dist. No. 1, 13th July, 1896; res. engrn., Cape Town, 1st July, 1897; ch. res. engrn., Port Elizabeth, 1st Apr., 1899; asst. engrn.-in-chief, 15th Mar., 1901; asst. gen. man. of rlyws., 16th May, 1901; gen. man., 12th Aug., 1902.
MACFARLANE, WM. HOULSWORTH, M.B., Ch. B.—Supt. and med. offr., hosp. for insane, New Norfolk, Tasmania, Oct., 1880.

McGEE, JOHN J.—Ed. at St. Peter’s Coll., Waterford, affiliated with the Catholic Univ., Dublin; was medallist in physical science; employed by the govt. of Canada on special service in dist. of Algoma, and on various surveys; subsequently professionally engaged under the Dominion govt. in the construction of the inter-colonial rly., and in connection with the rly. coun. of the house of commons; 1st class clk. in charge of survey branch of Dominion lands, Dept. of the Interior, 1879; clk. of the privy coun., Canada, May, 1882.
McGOWAN, JAMES.—B.A., Lond., and fellow of the Inst. of Actuaries, Lond.; acted as exam. to that inst., and engaged for many years in life assurance business; govt. actuary, Cape, 1890.

MacGREGOR, D. S.—Fellow of soc. of accnts. and auditors; ch. audit clk., Leeward, Apr., 1895; federal accnt., Jan., 1896; ag. treasr., St. Kitts-Nevis, Dec., 1896; ag. aud.-gen. and mem. of Leewards and Antigua leg. couns. and Antigua exec. coun., Sept., 1897, to Jan., 1898; on special mission to investigate and adjust accnts. of Trinidad, Dec., 1899, to May, 1900; auditor, British Honduras, June, 1900; ag. reg.-gen. and reg. of sup. ct. on two occasions in 1904.
McGREGOR, JAMES COMYN.—2nd lieut. 7th battn. K.R.R., 1881; lieut., 1891; sub-instr. Basutoland mtd. pol., 1884; asst. commr., 1893.

MacGREGOR, SIR WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1899), C.M.G. (1881), C.B. (1897).—B. 1847; ed. at Aberdeen, Glasgow, Berlin, and Paris; M.B., Abdn., 1872; M.D., 1874; Watson gold medallist, 1872; formerly res. surg. and res. physician, Glasgow Roy. Infirmary; res. physician, Roy. Lun. Asyl., Aberdeen; asst. govt. med. offr., Seychelles, 1873; supt., lun. asyl., and res. surg., Civil Hosp., Port Louis Mauritius, 1874; ch. med. offr., Fiji, Mar., 1875; also receiver-gen., and mem. of the exec. and legis. couns., Jan., 1877; has acted as regisr.-gen., agt. gen. of immigr., and commr. of lands; engaged, 1876, in the suppression of the disturbances in the mountains of Viti Levu, for which he was voted a gratuity of 200L; joint commr., 1877, for the settlement of debts due from natives and Europeans, and for the settlement of all pecuniary claims against the late govt. of Fiji; mem. of the Native Regulation Board, 1877; proceeded to Tonga, 1879, to report on the financial condition of that country; ag. col. sec., Nov., 1883, to June, 1884, and Oct., 1874, to Jan., 1875; admstr. of the govt., and ag. high commr. and consul-gen. for the W. Pacific, Jan. to Aug., 1875; representative of Fiji at the first session of the Fed. Coun. of Australasia, at Hobart, Jan., 1885; Albert medal of the 2nd class (1884), with the Clarke gold medal of the Roy. Hum. Soc. of Australasia in 1885, for saving life at sea; dep. admstr. of Fiji, Sept., 1885, and Aug., 1886; admstr. Br. New Guinea, 1888; lieut.-gov., 1895; gov. Lagos, 1899; gov., Newfoundland, 23rd July, 1904.

McGUIRE, WM.—Appkd., after open compet. clk. in the lower div. of the civ. serv., and assigned to the audit office, Nov., 1884; transf. to valuation office, Ireland, Nov., 1890, and to C.O., June, 1891; temp. staff officer, 1903.
MACINTYRE, D. C.—Harbour-master, Penang. S. Settmts., Sep., 1903.
McINTYRE, JOHN.—Apptd. puisne judge, Tasmania, 1899.
McINTYRE, PETER ADOLPHUS, M.D.—Lieut. govt., Prince Edward Island, Canada, 23rd May, 1899.

McINNIS, Lieut.-Col. EDWARD BOWATER (late 9th Lancers), C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1847; entd. army, 1865; adjt. 9th Lancers for several years, including campaigns of 1878-9-80; Khyber Passa, under Lt.-Gen. Sir F. Maude, K.C.B., 1878; actions of Dei Khazi Killa, Dec., 1879; Siah Sung, Dec., 1879; others at Kabul; siege of Sherpur under Lt.-Gen. Sir F. Roberts, K.C.B.; with the 9th Lancers on the march from Kabul to Kandahar; at battle of Mazra (Kandahar), Sept., 1880; twice mentd. in despatches; bronze star, med. and two clasps; inspr.-gen., G. Coast constab., Mar., 1887; mentd. in despatches by Sir R. B. Griffith, K.C.M.G.; comdt. local forces, Trinidad, June, 1890; mem. exec. coun., Sept., 1890; inspr.-gen. of Br. Guiana pol., May, 1891; comdt. Br. Guiana militia, Nov., 1892, with rank of col.; on special duty to Uruan on Venezuelan frontier, Jan., 1896; commanded cavalry of the colonial contingent at H.M.'s Diamond Jubilee, June, 1897; ret., 1902.
MACKAY, G.—M.A., Aberdeen, asst. master, Victoria Sch., Seychelles, 23rd Aug., 1903.

McKEAN, Lieut.-Col. ALEXANDER CHALMERS, C.M.G. (1891).—B. 1852; cornet, Inniskilling Dragoons, Oct., 1871; A.D.C., Bengal, 1880; served in Transvaal war, 1881; staff off., Zululand war (Eshowe column), 1883; D.A.A. and Q.M.G., S. Africa, 1884; ag. D.A.A.G., Natal, 1886; survr. to Zululand and New Republic boundary comm., 1886; asst. comsnr., Zululand, 1887; comsnr. of Basuto levies in Zulu disturbances, 1888; was honourably mentioned and made brevet lieut.-col. for services; asst. mil. sec. to gov., Malta, 1889.
MACKENZIE, DONALD.—Ch. inspr. of pol., Penang, Dec., 1887; ag. survr. pol., Sungei Ujong, Apr., 1890.

MACKENZIE RIVER, Bishop of, THE RIGHT REV. WM. D. RERVES.—C.M., Coll., Isl., 1865; consec. 1891; hon. D.D., St. John's Coll., Manitoba, 1891; missionary, (C.M.S.) at Fort Simpson, Mackenzie River, &c.; chapl. to Bishop of Athabasca, 1874-84; archd. Chipewyan, 1883-98.

McKINSTRY, W. L.—Barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn; ed. Harr. Coll., Barbados; passed civ. ser. test exam. 1882; supernum. coll. sec.'s office, 1883; ag. clk. audit office, exec. comte.'s office, col. sec.'s office, and as sec. to poor law bd., 1886 and 1888; clk. in petty debt ct., Jan., 1889; elk. to inspr.-gen. of pol., June, 1889; ag. inspr. of pol. and asst. of fire brig., 1889-91; int. rev. offr., 1892; transf. to customs, 1896; ag. gov., Glendairy prison, May to Nov., 1904.

McLEAN, THE HON. ALLAN.—M.L.A., Victoria; pres. of bd. of land and wks. and comsnr. of crown lands and survey, Nov., 1890, to Feb., 1892; min. of agric., Nov., 1890, to Apil., 1891; chief sec., Apil., 1891, to Feb., 1892; ch. sec., pres., bd. of land and wks., comsnr. of lands and survey, Feb., 1892, to Jan., 1893; premier and ch. sec., Dec., 1899, to Nov., 1900; min. for trade and customs, commonwealth parl., Oct., 1904.
McLELLAN, DAVID.—Men. of house of assem. and exec. coun., New Brunswick; prov. sec. and rec.-gen.; pres. of exec. coun., 1890.
MACLEOD, NEIL.—Called to the bar, P.E.I., 1872; mem. of house of assem. since 1879; prov. sec. and treas., 1879 to 1882; atty.-gen. and premier, 1889.
MACLEOD, NORMAN MAGNUS, C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1839; late capt. 74th Highlanders; was comdt. of Swazi contingent against Sikukuni, 1879.

MACLURE, WILLIAM GRANT.—Admtd. Bahamas Bar, 1889; asst. clk., House of Assem., 1890; J.P. for col. and prov. res. just., Gov.'s Harbour, 1894; acted as stip. and circuit mag. and coroner, Is. of New Providence; regist., sup. ct., Bahamas, 1898.

MCMILLAN, SIR DANIEL HUNTER, K.C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1846; Ed. Canada; served with Canadian Volunteers on Niagara frontier, 1864; during Fenian raid, 1866; Red River expdn., 1870; N.W. rebellion (medal), 1885; mem. for Winnipeg in Manitoba legislature, 1886; mem. of Manitoba govt., 1889; lt.-gov. of Manitoba, 6th Sept., 1900.

MCMILLAN, ROBERT FURSE.—Queen's scholar, Westminster school; scholar, Trin. Hall, Camb.; senior in law tripes, 1879; holder of law studentship at Trin. Hall; Inns of Court studentship and Inner Temple common law scholarship; called to the bar, 1881; joined western circuit and Devon and Exeter sessions; puisne judge, W. Australia, 1903.

McNAIR, J. F. A., C.M.G. (1879).—B. 1828; major, Roy. (late Madras) Artill. (retd.); ed. at King's Coll., Lond.; and student at the Schl. of Mines; entered the Madras Artill. in 1845; served in India, the S. Sttlmtns., and Labuan; priv. sec. to the gov. S.S., 1857; and subsequently exec. engrn. and supt. of convicts, Singapore; ch. engrn., S.S., on their transfer to the crown in 1867; accompanied the gov. to Siam, Aug., 1867; survr. gen. S.S., 1872; ch. comsnr. of Perak throughout the disturbances in that country in 1875-6, and engaged in the affair at Kotah Lama, on the Perak river; special comsnr. to Selangore to inquire into piracy, and to Perak in connection with the Pangkor treaty, 1874; again to Siam, in Aug. of that year, on special mission; again, in 1875, with Sir A. Clarke, in connection with unsettled state of affairs there; special mission to the State of Sungei Ujong, 1878; sec. to Sir W. Robinson's mission to Siam in Nov., 1878, to present the G.C.M.G. to the king; author of "Perak and the Malays," &c.; ag. lieut.-gov. and res. councillor of Penang from Feb., 1881, to Aug., 1884, when he ret.

MACNAGHTEN, T. C.—Ed. Charterhouse and Oxford; scholar of Hertford Coll., 1892; 1st class classical mods., 1894; B.A., 1896; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd class clk., Local Govt. Bd., Feb., 1896; 2nd class clk., C.O., Sept., 1896; priv. sec. to Earl of Selborne, Dec., 1899, to Earl of Onslow, Nov., 1900, to Duke of Marlborough, July, 1903; 1st cl. clk., 24th Mch., 1904.

MCNEIL, MAJ.-GEN. SIR JOHN CARSTAIRS, G.C.B. (1882), K.C.M.G. (1880), C.M.G. (1870), V.C.—B. 1831; served during campaign of 1857-58 in India as A.D.C. to Sir E. Lugard during the siege and capture of Lucknow; in 1861-5 A.D.C. to Sir D. Cameron in N. Zealand; commanded Tipperary flying column during Fenian disturbances, 1866-67; served on the staff of Red River expdn. under Sir G. Wolseley in 1870, and in the Ashanti war, 1873-74; A.D.C. to H.R.H. the Duke of Cambridge, Jan., 1874, and an equerry to Her Majesty in Aug. following; served in the Egyptian expdn., 1882, personally attached to the Duke of Connaught; served in the Soudan expdn., 1885; commanded troops at the action of Tobrek, for which clasp was granted.

MOWAN, ISLAY.—3rd clk., col. sec.'s off., Fiji, Sept., 1892; stip. mag., asst. to native comsnr., and tax inspr., Lau, May, 1896; stip. mag. and tax inspr., Navua, and comsnr. for Namasi, Feb., 1900; stip. mag., Lomaiviti and Tailevu; comsnr. and tax inspr., Lomaiviti; offr. in charge, Levuka gaol, Jan., 1904.

MACPHERSON, A.—Served during S. A. war in Lord Dunraven's sharpshooters; civilian warrant offr. and paymaster, Imperial remount farm, 1st to 14th Dec., 1902; ch. clk. and sen. examr. of accts., audit dept., O.R.C., 15th Dec., 1902.
MACPHERSON, LT.-COL. JOHN.—Acctnt. and ch. clk. store branch dept. of militia and defence, Canada, Sept., 1872; dir. of stores and keeper of militia properties, Nov., 1880.

McTURK, MICHAEL, C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1843; revenue offr. and asst. survr., Essequibo, 1872; comsnr. of taxation, Essequibo dist., 1873; special mag. and supt. crown lands and forests, Essequibo, 1878; govt. gold mining offr., Br. Guiana, 1887.
MACWILLIAM, DONALD KIRTON.—2nd clk., treasry., St. Kitts, 1894; rev. offr. and quarantine offr., Apr., 1894.
MADDEN, SIR JOHN, K.C., K.C.M.G. (1899), KT. BACH. (1893).—B. 1844; ch. justice of Victoria, 1893; administered the govt., 1893, 1895, 1897, and 1898, and again, as lieut.-gov., Jan., 1900-1.
MADDEN, W.—Pres. bd. of land and wks., and comsnr., crown lands and survey, Victoria, Aug., 1881, to Mar., 1883.
MADLEY, LEWIS GEORGE.—Col. S. Australian Militia Infantry; principal of training coll., educn. dept., S.A., 1876; also D.A.A.G. of S.A. mil. forces, 1895; comsnr. of pol., 1896.
MAGRATH, THOS. HENRY.—Jun. clk., P.O., Tasmania, 1853; ch. clk., 1869; comptroller of money orders, 1879, and of savings bank, 1882; sec. to P.O., 1888.

MAGRO, PROF. E., M.D.—Grad. Malta Univ., 1877; examr. in arts and sciences, 1882; examr. in med. and surgery, 1884; prof. of math. and physics, Oct., 1885; examr. in land surveying and archt., Oct., 1885; ag. princ. and sec. to the univ., 1887; dir. of educn. and sec. to the senate, 1888-97; ag. inspr. in charge of elemen. schls., Jan., 1898; sec. to Oxford local exams. in Malta, 1898; mem. ex. coun. and of coun. of govt., 1902.

MAHAFFY, ARTHUR W.—Ed. at Marlborough, and Magdalen Coll., Oxford, demy, 1889; B.A., Dublin Univ. (Trin. Coll.); 2nd lieut., 1st Batt. Royal Munster Fusiliers, 1892-1896; gov. agt. Gilbert Is. Prot., Oct., 1896; asst. to res. comsnr., Solomon Is., 1897; res. mag., W. division, Solomon Is., and dep. comsnr. for W. Pacific, 1899; col. sec. and recvr.-gen., Fiji, Oct., 1904.
MAHONY, LIEUT.-COL. JOHN, C.M.G. (1879).—B. 1824; paymr., army pay dept., in the Zulu war; ret. with the hon. rank of lieut.-col., Mar., 1884.

MAITLAND, ANDREW GILBERT.—Reed. his educn. as a geologist and civil engr. at Yorkshire Coll. of Science, Leeds; graduated, 1885; asst. govt. geologist, Queensland, 1888; his official duties taking him as far afield as Br. N. Guinea; govt. geol. of W. Australia, 1896; has written several works on the geology, features, and mineral resources of Queensland and W. Australia.

MAJOR, CHARLES.—Called to the Bar (Mid. Temp.), June, 1887; mem. of Leeward Is. bar, Sept., 1887; chancellor of the diocese of Antigua, Oct., 1889; nominated mem. of legis. coun., Antigua, 1895 to 1899; nominated mem. of gen. legis. coun., Leewards, Nov., 1896, and pres. of same, Dec., 1896, to 1899; mem. fed. ex. couns., Leewards, Mar., 1897, to June, 1901; vice-pres. legis. coun., Antigua, May, 1897; ag. sol.-gen. and atty.-gen., Leewards, 1897, 1898, 1899; capt. commanding Antigua volunteer defence force on its establishment, 1897; J.P., 1897; ag. 2nd puisne judge, Leewards, June, 1899; atty.-gen. of Grenada, 1901; ch. just. of Fiji, and chief judicial comsnr. of Western Pacific, 1902; admnstd. govt., Mar.-Oct., 1904.

MAJOR, F. W.—5th clk., treasry., Trinidad, Oct., 1885; 2nd clk. to supt. of prisons, Jan., 1886; 9th clk., audit office, Aug., 1887; 8th clk., 1888; priv. sec. to Sir W. Robinson, 1888; super. of customs, G. Coast, Feb., 1891; ass. comptroller of customs, 1891; asst. treas., Aug., 1891; ag. comptroller of customs, various dates from 1891 to 1895; comptroller of customs, Nov., 1895; rec.-gen., Bermuda, 1899.

MALCOLM, DOUGAL ORME.—Ed. at Eton and New Coll., Oxford; 1st cl. mods., 1897; 1st cl. lit. hum., 1899; B.A., 1899; Fellow of All Soul., 1899; M.A., 1902; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. clk., C.O. Oct., 1900.

MALCOLM, HARCOURT GASTON.—Pri. sec. to Sir Henry Jackson when admnstr. of Bahamas, 1892, 1893, 1895; ditto to Sir W. F. Hayne-Smith, gov. of Bahamas, 1895; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, June, 1899; ditto, Bahamas, July, 1899; mem. of house of ass., Bahamas, 1900; deputy-speaker, 1901; re-elected, 1904.

MALCOLM, SIR ORMOND DRUMMIE, KT. BACH. (1898).—B. 1839; called to the bar of the Bahamas, July, 1861; ag. pol. mag. in 1864 and 1866; elected mem. of house of assem. in 1865; speake., 1868; ag. judge, court of common pleas, 1875; Q.C. in 1876; ag. atty-gen., Mar., 1882; ag. admnstr., June, 1896; ch. just. of Bahama Is., 1897.

MALING, CAPT. IRWIN CHAR., C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1841; ed. at Wimbledon schl. and Roy. Mil. Acad.; late 23rd Roy. Welsh Fus.; dep. mag. and collr., Nuddes dist., Bengal, 1857; served throughout the Indian mutiny, 1857-59; present in many engagements (medal); ensign, 1861, 89th Princess Victoria's Regt.; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to the gov., W. Coast of Africa, 1868; lieut. 36th Roy. Sussex Regt., 1870; priv. sec. to the Marquis of Normanby, gov. of Queensland, 1871, and New Zealand, 1874; col. sec. and regir. gen. of Grenada, 1879; administ. the govt. in 1880, 1882, 184-47; ag. col. sec., Leeward Is., 1889; and represve. at quarantine confce. at Demera, 1888; ag. admnstr., 1889; adminstr. of St. Vincent, 1889; ret. 1893.
MALONEY, HENRY.—Curator, Botanic Station, Montserrat; sanitary offr., Jan., 1897.

MALSCH, CHARLES CHRISTIAN, A.M.I.C.E.—Draughtsman, works dept., admiralty, 1866; ast engnr., German admiralty wks., Kiel, 1873; survr. state engnr. dept., California; surveying Sacramento and Fraser Rivers, etc., California and Br. Columbia, 1875; exploring Formosa for Chinese govt., 1881 to 1883; ast. engnr., P.W. dept., Hong Kong, 1884; supt. of crown land, 1892; ag. assessor of rates, 1894; J.P., 1894.

MANN, MAJ.-GEN. J. R., R.E., C.M.G. (1881).—B. 1823; entered R.E., 1840; survr.-gen. of Mauritius, 1856 to 1861; dir. of roads and survr.-gen. of Jamaica, 1867; resig. 1873, and re-apptd. in Feb., 1874; official, M.L.C., 1874; ret., 1886.
MANNING, ALBERT EDWARD.—Copyist in registrar's office, Br. Guiana; ast. sworn clk., 1887; sworn clk. and notary public, 1898; comsnr. to administer oaths and affidavits, 1900.

MANSEL, GEORGE, C.M.G. (1891).—Served in Weenen Yeomanry through the Langalibalele rebellion in 1873; served as sub.-inspr. and inspr. in Natal mounted pol. through Zulu war, 1879 (medal with clasp), and Boer war, 1880 and 1881; raised and commanded the Reserve Territory Carbineers from 1883 to 1887, when the force was changed into the Zululand pol.; served in this force through all disturbances in Zululand from 1883 to 1888; twice mentioned in despatches as having shown conspicuous coolness and gallantry.

MANSERGH, CORNEWALL LEWIS WARWICKSHIRE.—Ck., crown lands office, Capetown, May, 1880; 2nd cl. ck. and priv. sec. to comnr. of crown lands and pub. wks., May, 1884; 1st cl. ck., Apr., 1892; ch. ck. to the ast. comnr. of rly. and pub. wks., Oct., 1892; sec. pub. wks., July, 1898.

MANSON, SIR PATRICK, K.C.M.G. (1903); C.M.G. (1900); M.D., F.R.C.P., LL.D.—B. 1844; late dean and lect. on med. coll. of med. for Chinese, Hong Kong; phys. Seamen's Hospital Soc. at Albert Docks branch; lect. on trop. diseases, St. George's and Charing Cross Hosps.; apptd. med. adviser to C.O., 1897.

MARKS, OLIVER.—Supt. govt. plants, Perak, Mar., 1891; 2nd cls. mag., Apl., 1894; ag. ast. mag., Kinta, July, 1894; ast. mag., Kinta, July, 1896; ag. ast. sec. to govt., Perak, Sep., 1896; ast. sec. to govt., Perak, May, 1897; passed Malay, Mar., 1898; 1st cls. mag., June, 1898; ast. sec. to res. gen., Fed. Malay States, Nov., 1898; ag. sec. to res. gen., F. M. S., Mar., 1899; sec. to high comnr., F. M. S., Aug., 1903.

MARLBOROUGH, 9TH Duke OF (created, 1702), CHARLES RICHARD JOHN SPENCER-CHURCHILL, P.C., K.G.—B. 1871; Chancellor of Primrose League, 1897 and 1898; served with yeomanry cavalry, S. Africa, 1900, and A.D.C. to Lieut.-Gen. Sir Ian Hamilton, K.C.B.; pay-master-general, 1899; under-sec. of state for the colonies, July, 1903.

MARRIOTT, H., B.A., 1st cls. math. tripso, Cantab.—Cadet S. Sttlmts., Nov., 1896; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, May, 1897; passed fin. exam. in Malay, May, 1898; dist. offr., Malacca, Feb., 1899; ag. 2nd mag., Sing., Feb., 1902; ast. P.M.G., Penang, Sept., 1903; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, July, 1904.

MARSH, EDWARD HOWARD.—Ed. at Westminster and Cambridge; scholar, Trin. Coll., 1891; 1st cl. classical tripso, 1893; sen. chancellor's medal for classics, 1895; 1st cl. with distinction classical tripso, Part II., and B.A., 1896; M.A., 1897; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. ck., C.O., Sept., 29, 1896; ast. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, July, 1900; to Mr. Lyttleton, Oct., 1903.
MARSH, HERBERT.—Cadet, S. Sttlmts., 27th Nov., 1903.
MARSH, T. R., C.M.G. (1901), on retirement from post as head of the contract branch, crown agents' office; B. 1847.

MARSH, SIR WILLIAM HENRY, K.C.M.G. (1887), C.M.G. (1881).—B. 1827; Clk. to procureur-gen. Mauritius, Feb., 1848; crown solr., Apl., 1854; ch. ck., col. sec.'s office, Oct., 1859; special immigr. comnr. to India, May, 1865; jun. ast. col. sec., Apl., 1866; sen. ast. col. sec., 1867; sole ast., 1870; ag. col. sec., 1875-6; audr.-gen., Apl., 1876; col. sec. and audr.-gen., Hong Kong, 1879; ag. audr. and acctnt.-gen., Cyprus, 1881; adminstr. govt. Hong Kong on six occasions; ret., 1887.

MARSHALL, ARTHUR LACY.—Clerical ast., immigr. dept., Br. Guiana, Dec., 1875; supernumerary clk., 1876; clerical ast. to col. sec., Tobago, 1879; 2nd rev. offr., May, 1882; 1st rev. offr., Apl., 1885; ch. ck., treas., July, 1885.

MARSHALL, COLONEL ROBERT SETON.—Lieut. 3rd brig. Scottish div. R.A., 1882; (lieut. reserve of offrs., 1885); capt., 1885; instructor of artillery, 1887; (capt., rea. of offrs. 1896); major Edin. artill. militia, 1896; (hon. major in the army, 1900); hon. lt.-col., 1903; lt.-col., 1903; retired with hon. rank of col., 1903; served with the 1st brig. W. div. R.A., 1885; staff offr. local forces, Trinidad, Sept., 1888, to Dec., 1891; sec. local defence comtee., 1888; command of Trinidad artill. volrs. for purpose of organising the corps, 1888; A.D.C. to offr. admnstr. govt. of Trin. and Tobago, May to Dec., 1889; ag. commdt. Trinidad local forces, 6th-19th Nov., 1890; adjt. and musketry instr., Br. Guiana pol., Dec., 1891; ag. adjt. Br. G. militia, May to Oct., 1894; and Aug. to Sept., 1897; ag. inspr. of Br. G. pol. during period of special employment at Ururan frontier station, Oct., 1895, to Mar., 1896; adjt. of Br. G. militia and police, Dec., 1897; ag. commdt. Br. G. militia, 9th May to 22nd Aug., 1900; sent to frontier to hand over Amacura and Barima Sands to Venezuela, Oct., 1900; sent to Trinidad for special duty with police, after the riot, 2nd to 27th May, 1903.

MARTIN, H. A.—Ag. ck. to auditor, Grenada, Aug., 1884, to June, 1885; ck. to promarshal, June to July, 1885; ag. ch. to audr., May to Nov., 1887; ast. ck., treasy., Jan., 1889; ag. wks. acctnt., Jan. to Oct., 1-90; ch. ck., regiar., Nov., 1891; treasy. acctnt., St. Lucia and Grenada, Apr., 1894; supt. prisons, Feb. to May, 1897; 2nd financial ast., P.W.D., Ceylon, June, 1897.
MARTIN, JAMES H.—Apptd., after exam., messenger Admity., Aug., 1875; transf. C.O., Sept., 1877; Queen's meas., June, 1897.
MARTIN, JOSEPH.—Mem. of legis. assem., and exec. coun., Manitoba; atty.-gen. and comnr. of rlys. and lands.

MARTIN, LOUIS.—Volr. in survr.-gen.'s dept. at Mauritius, Dec., 1859; ck. in audit office, Jan., 1860; jun. examnr., Mar., 1862; sen. examnr., Sept., 1869; ch. ck., immigr. dept., Mar., 1870; col. postmr. and supt. of telegraphs, May, 1890.

MARTIN, COL. SIR RICHARD EDWARD ROWLEY, K.C.M.G. (1895), C.M.G. (1888), Inniskilling Dragoons.—B. 1847; commanded mounted infantry at Cetywayo's installation, Zululand, 1883; commdt. of gen. depot, Bechuanaland field force, 1884; major Bechuanaland border pol., 1885 to 1887; was Br. comnr.-pres., Portuguese-Swazi bdry., comnr., and Br. comnr. on special mission to Tongaland, 1888; proceeded on visit of inquiry to Swaziland, 1889; sec. to Br. comnr. to Swaziland, 1889; Br. mem. of temporary joint coman. of adminstr., 1889-95; commdt. of pol. and dep. coms., Rhodesia, Mar., 1896.
MARTIN, S. B.—Ed. Wesleyan Mission Schl., C. Coast; 6th class offr., customs, G. Coast, Sept., 1890; ck. to dir. of telegraphs and store-keeper, Aug., 1891; ck. and acctnt., July, 1893.
MARTINS, FRED. G.—Ed. C.M.S. Coll. Inst., Lagos; jun. bailiff, sup. ct., June, 1884; ck. to Queen's advoc., Aug., 1886; storekeeper, P.W. dept., Jan., 1891; audit ck., Jan., 1895.

MARTINS, G. N.—Ed. C.M.S. Grammar Schl., Lagos; messenger, audit, and P.O., 1889; customs, outdoor offr., 1890 to 1893 (during which time attached to revenue office); ag. ck., P.O., 1893; 4th ck. of customs, 1893; 3rd ck. and statistician, Mar., 1894; transf. to the revenue dept., 1900; deputy for cashier and ch. ck. on various occasions; 2nd cls. ck., customs, 1901.

MARWOOD, HORACE RICHARD, A.M.I.C.E.—Junr. ast. engrnr., Taff Vale rlyws., 1877; supernumerary 2nd cl. ast. engrnr. Cape Govt. rlyws. (Eastern system), 1882; 1st cl. ast. W. system, 1883, also in charge of district, 1884; ast. engrnr. rlyws. service of H.H. the Sultan of Selangor, S.S. 1885; chef-de-division, Pyraeus Larissa rlyws., Greece, 1890; engrnr. of wks. and maintenance Trinidad govt. rlyss., 1894.
MASKELL, THOS. AUGUSTUS CORNELIUS.—Cler. astt. to inspr. of schls., B. Guiana, June, 1873; clk., Apl., 1891; 3rd cls. clk., Apl., 1893; sec. to educn. comsnr., 1902.
MASON, ARTHUR WIER, B.A.—Puise judge, sup. ct., Natal, 1896; ag. ch. just., 1900-01; comsnr. of spec. treason ct., 1900-01; pres. of ditto, 1901-02; puise judge, sup. ct., Transvaal, 1902.

MASON, CLAYTON T., M.I.C.E., J.P.—Ed. King Edward's Schll., Birmingham; from 1874 to 1877 was engaged in the rly. branch of the dept. of pub. wks., N. S. Wales; res. engrnr. of the Geraldton and Northampton rly., W. Australia, 1877; suptd. the construction of Point Moore Lighthouse and other pub. wks. in the Victoria dist.; afterwards res. engrnr. for four years of the E. rly. in the same col.; ag. comsnr. of rlys. on two occasions during 1882 and 1883; was provisionally apptd. in 1884 dir. of pub. wks. and comsnr. of rlys.; mem. of the exec. and legis. couns., gen. man. and maintenance engrnr., June, 1885; comsnr. of rlys. and mem. of exec. coun., Jan., 1890; after the introduction of responsible govt. was apptd. collr. of customs, June, 1891.

MASON, GERALD BOVELL.—Ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; matric., London Univ., 1890; joined London hosp., 1890; passed prelim. scientific, 1892; M.R.C.S.(Eng.), L.R.C.P.(Lond.), 1896; jun. ast. med. offr., St. Marylebone infirmary, 1896; clin. astt., aural and ophthalmic depts., Lond. hosp., 1897; house physician, Brompton hosp., 1898; priv. sec. to Sir C. Quilter, Bart., M.P., on voyage to W. Indies, 1891; ag. med. offr., Three Counties asylum, 1891; civ. surg. in charge, Station hosp., Woking, 1892; apptd. med. offr., No. 4 dist., Antigua, 1903; ag. comsnr., Virgin Is., 1904; surg.-lieut., Antigua dist. force, 1904.

MASSON, HON. LOUIS FRANCOIS RODERIQUE.—Called to the bar of Lower Canada, 1859; brigade-major, 8th military dist. of Lower Canada, from 1863 to 1868, and holds the rank of lieut.-col. in the militia; entered Canadian parlmt., 1867; sworn of the privy coun. of Canada and apptd. min. of militia and defence, 19th Oct., 1878; ret., 1880; called to the senate, 1882, and to the legis. coun. of the province, 1890; Quebec, 1884; gov. of Quebec, 1884-7; senator, 1890.

MATHEWS, HENRY ADOLPHUS.—Passed through govt. training instr., B. Guiana; 1st cls. certificated schlinstr.; head-master, grant-in-aid schl., B. Guiana, 9½ yrs.; educntl. dist. offr., 15th Nov., 1893; acted as 2nd astt. inspr. of schools on various occasions, 1895-1903.

MATHEWS, THOMAS VINCENT, I.S.O.(1903).—Outdoor offr., receiver-gen. and treas.'dept., Bahamas, Oct., 1874; 3rd clk., Mar., 1875; ag. priv. sec. to admstr., E. B. A. Taylor, from Sept. to Dec., 1879; ag. collr. of customs, 1883; 2nd clerk and book-keeper, 1885.
MATHIEU, M.—Puise judge, sup. ct., Quebec, 1881.

MATHEWS, MAYNARD R. NELSON.—Jun. clk. telegraph office, Durban, Natal, 1878; tel. offr. and postmr., Bothas Hill, Verulam, and Stanger, 1879; 3rd cl. clk., R.M.'s office, Durban, 1880; field cornet, Durban, and sec. wreck comsnr.'s ct., 1886; 2nd class clk., 1886; ag. clk., mag.'s ct. and registr. circuit ct., Durban and Umlazi, 1886-7; clk., mag.'s ct., registr. circuit ct., sub-distributor of stamps, and dep. clk. of peace, Klip River div., Dec., 1887; J.P., Ladysmith, 1888; has frequently acted as R.M., Klip River, 1888; ag. mag., Inanda, Feb., 1890; J.P. for col., 1890; Weenen, June, 1896; astt. mag., Durban, Nov., 1896; mag., Weenen, Apl., 1897; re-thanks of Gen. Sir Redvers Buller for service to Intelligence Dept. during the Boer war, 1899-1900; ag. mag., Newcastle, 23rd Sept., 1900; mag., Dundee, 9th Dec., 1901.
MATTHEWS, WM. HENRY.—Inspector of mines, S. Aust., 1699; warden of mines, 1900.

MAURICE, MORGAN SYDNEY.—Passed ser. exam., 2nd grade, in Mauritius, 1874; confidential clk. to the roy. comsnrs. to enquire into the treatment of Indian imigrts. in Mauritius, 1873; acted as sen. astt. dist. clk. in the dist. astt. stip. mag. of Black River in 1874; clk. and inter. to the Br. consul, Island of Reunion, 1875; read trans. of Tamil to the sup. ct., Mauritius, 1876; employed in col. engrnr.'s dept., Natal, 1878; subsequently confidl. clk. on staff of Major-Gen. Clifford; audit clk. in the office of the gen. man. of telegraphs, Cape, Oct., 1880; confidl. clk. to Sc. G. Strahan; 3rd class clk. on the fixed estate, July, 1881; 2nd class clk., Oct., 1882.

MAVROGORDATO, ALEXANDER STEPHEN.—Dispenser, civ. hosp., Nicosia, Cyprus, June, 1873; clk. to prin. forest offr., June, 1881; acted as forest offr., May, 1883, to Dec., 1884; was engaged on the locust destruction as paymr., 1883 to 1896; and as astt. to comsnr., Famagusta, 1889-92; exam. of acct., audit dept., May, 1886; transfid. to rec. gen.'s dept., Apr., 1891; astt. in survey and forest depts., Dec., 1892; pres. surv. delimitation comm.; supt. locust destruction operations, 1895-6; astt. in forest and agricultural depts., June, 1895; financial astt., P.W. Dept., October, 1896.

MAVROGORDATO, THEODORE E.—Ed. at Berlin; clk. to comsnr., Famagusta, 1881; clk. and interp. to Nicosia detachment of pol., 1882; astt. inspr., pol., Limassol, 1883; inspr. pol., Krenia, Sept., 1884; joined metrpr. pol., London, for six months' course of instr., 1894; in charge special pol. force, 1895; local comdt. of pol., astt. comsnr., gov. of prison and dep. cornr., 1896; ag. comsnr., Papho, May to Nov., 1897; ag. comsnr., Limassol, May to Dec., 1900; astt. comsnr. of pol. and J.P., Johannesburg, Dec., 1901.

MAXWELL, F. M.—Ed. Balliol Coll., Oxford; 1st cl. honours jurisprudence, 1896; M.A. 1891; called to bar Linc.'s Inn, 1894; 1st cl. studentship, 1882; 2nd cl. intrmdt. exam. in laws, Lond. univ., 1885; ag. atty.-gen., Br. Honduras, Feb. to Dec., 1890, and May to July, 1896; atty.-gen., Br. H., July, 1896; ag. ch. just., Br. H., 21st June to 29th Dec., 1899; ditto, 15th Mar. to 17th Oct., 1902; ditto, Apl., 1904.

MAXWELL, GERALD VERNER.—Ed. Cheltenham and Camb. Univ.; cadet, Fiji, Apl., 1888; ag. reg. genl., ag. reg. of titles and ag. dep. comsnr. of stamps, Dec., 1898, to June, 1899; astt. stip. mag., Macuata, Dec., 1900; ag. comsnr., Naitasiiri, and stip. mag., Rewa, July, 1901; ag. ch. clk., C.S.O., Mar., 1902; ag. govt. storeroom keeper and recvr. nat. taxes, Sept., 1902; ag. P.I. Nadroga, Feb., 1903; astt. dist. comsnr., Jan., 1904.
MAXWELL, JOSEPH P., M.I.C.E.—Pub. dept., N. Zealand, 1874; gen. man., N. Z. rly., 1886; rly. comsnr. under Rlys. Act, 1887, Jan., 1889.

MAXWELL, THOMAS DOVETON.—Ed. Tiverton Blundell scholar, Balliol Coll., Oxford, 1892; B.A. (honours, jurisprudence), 1896; called to the bar (Gray's Inn), 1903; astt. dist. comsnr., Sa Nigeria, 1902; ag. dist. comsnr., Degema, 1903; ditto, Calabar, Mar., 1904.

MAY, ALFRED JOHN, M.R.A.S., F.E.I.S.—Ed. St. Saviour's Gram. Schl., New Shoreham, and Training Schl.; St. John's Coll., Hurstpierpoint; headmr., St. Paul's Coll., Hong Kong, Jan., 1875, to Jan., 1879; asst. master, govt. cent. schl., Jan., 1879, to Sept., 1881; prin., govt. normal schl., Sept., 1881; 3rd master, govt. central schl. (now Queen's Coll.), Sept., 1883; passed II. stand. higher grade Chinese, 1886; 2nd master, 1888; ag. headmr., Apl., 1890, to Oct., 1891, and May, 1900, to Nov., 1901; J.P., 1890.
MAY, A. W. J.—Entered col. office after exam., May, 1882; asst. clk., May, 1892; asst. to supervisor of copying, Mar., 1900.

MAY, BARRY.—Clk. to res. mag., Vryburg, Brit. Bech., 1st June, 1888; clk. to res. mag., Kuruman, 1889; clk. to cv. comsr., Vryburg, 1890; acctnt. to recvr. gen., 1894; acctnt. to res. comsr., Brit. Bech. Prot., 1895, and in addition, govt. sec., 1902; is also ch. asst. offr. and mast. of res. comsr.'s court.

MAY, C. G.—Apptd. asst. supt. of works, Straits Settlements, May, 1899; ag. supt. of works, and surveys, Malacca, June, 1899, to Sept., 1901, and Oct., 1902, to Apl., 1903; supt. of works and surveys, Sing., July, 1903.
MAY, CECIL.—Clerical asst., treasy., Br. Guiana; 4th class clk., Apl., 1889; 3rd class, June, 1892.

MAY, FRANCIS H., C.M.G. (1896).—B.1860; ed. at Harrow and Trin. Coll., Dub.; 1st honour-man and prizeman in classics and modern languages; apptd., after compet. exam., to Hong Kong cadetship, 1881; ag.-asst. regir.-gen., Apl., 1886; ag. asst. col. sec., Jan., 1887; sec. to bd. of examrs., Jan., 1889; asst. col. sec., Jan., 1891; priv. sec. to Gov. Sir W. Des Voeux, to Admstr. Sir F. Fleming, and Maj.-Gen. Barker, Apl., 1889, to Dec., 1891; ag. col. treas., Apl. to Oct., 1892; mem. legir. coun., May, 1893; capt. supt. of pol. and fire brigade, 1893, and of Victoria gaol, 1897; ag. col. sec., Feb. to Sept., 1900; col. sec., 1902; admntd. the govt., Nov., 19-3, to July, 1904.

MAY, GEORGE HERBERT.—Ag. apptmt. in jegir.'s off., Br. Guiana, Nov., 1889; admstr.-gen.'s off., on staff, June, 1892; pol. dept., Apl., 1893; 2nd lieut., Br. Guiana militia, Sept., 1894, and obtained certif. for drill and musketry from schls. of instruc., Chelsea Bks., Lon. and Hythe; ret. from mil. as lieut., and apptd. sub-inspr. of Br. Guiana pol., May, 1897; adjt. and musketry instr. of local forces, Fiji, with rank of capt., Oct., 1898; sup. of pol., ag. sheriff, 12th Dec., 1900; dep. comdt., armed constab., 24th Apl., 1901, also commanded volunteers; comdt., armed constab., supt. of pol., ag. sheriff, 24th June, 1902; A.D.C. to O.A.G., Fiji, July, 1901, to Sept., 1902; A.D.C. to Sir Henry M. Jackson, K.C.M.G., gov. of Fiji, 4th Nov., 1902.

MAY, HENRY FREDERIC P.—Extra clk., treasy., Br. Guiana, 1877; clk., savings bank, 1880; 3rd cl. clk., 1881; 2nd cl. clk., 1884; clk. in charge of Fellowship branch, 1888; 1st cl. clk., 1883; ag. accnt., G. S. bank, 1893-4; cashier of the treasy., 1895; acted as sub-accnt. of Suddil branch, and clk. in charge of Belfield sub-treasy.; Hindustani interpreter for the treasy. and S. bank.
MAZERE, LOUIS JOSEPH.—Clk., survr.-genl.'s dept., Mauritius, 4th Jan., 1873; pay clk., ditto, 26th Mar., 1884; ch. clk. and accntn., 3rd Dec., 1902.

MEADEN, C. W.—14th Hussars and 5th Dragoon Guards, 1870-6; convict service, England, 1877; supt. of Chaguanas convict depot, Trinidad 1885; J.P. for co. Caroni, 1887; asst. supt. prisons, 1890; mem. cent. agricult. bd.; carried out special agricult. experiments by prison labour, 1887 to 1892; twice ag. supt. of govt. farm and pastures, and sec., cent. agricult. bd.; warden of Chaguanas; lieut. and adjt., Trinidad Light Horse, Dec., 1894; man. govt. stock farm, and supt. of pastures, Mar., 1895; and adjt., Trinidad Light Horse, Dec., 1894.
MEANEY, S. J.—Entered col. office, after exam., Apl., 1881; asst. clk., May, 1892; supt. of copying dept., 1898.
MEARES, GEORGE, C.M.G. (1882).—B. 1825; late Mayor of Melbourne and comsnr. and mem. of exec. comtee. of the Melbourne Internat. Exhibn., 1880-1; died, 1903.
MERLING, E. J.—Apptd. accntn., repatriation dept., O.R.C., 1st July, 1902; clk. treasy., 1st Nov., 1903.

MELBOURNE, CHARLES ALEX. DICK.—Ed. privately and Trin. Hall, Camb.; called to bar, Inner Temple, Nov., 1891; admitted to practice in Brisbane, Queensland, Nov., 1892; and in Hong Kong, Nov., 1895; apptd. 1st clk., magistracy, Hong Kong, Sept., 1900; J.P., 1900; ag. asst. supt., Victoria gaol, 1903 and 1904.

MELVILLE, SIR GEORGE, K.C.M.G. (1900), C.M.G. (1894).—B. 1842; late lieut., Kent Artil militia; ed. Edinburg Acad.; clk. in the solr.'s dept., inland rev., after compet. exam., June, 1862 afterwards in the legacy and succession duty dept.; ch. clk., treasy., S. Leone, Nov., 1874; acted as col. treas., Dec., 1874, to Feb., 1876, during which period also acted as col. sec., July to Sept., 1875; acted also as collr. of customs and col. survr., and held comsrs. as dep.-coroner and J.P.; 1st clk., govt. sec.'s office, Br. Guiana, 1877; asst. govt. sec., 1879; acted as govt. sec., 1881, 1882, 1887, and 1888, and also as audr.-gen.; col. sec. audr.-gen., and regir.-gen., Br. Honduras, 1889; admstr. of govt., 1890 and 1891; admstr. of govt. of Falklands, 1893; col. sec., Bahamas, 1894; adminstd. govt., 1894 and 1896; col. sec., Leewards, 1895; adminstd. govt. under dormant commission, 1895, 1896, 1897, 1899, 1900; admstr. and col. sec., Saint Lucia, Sept., 1902.

MENENDEZ, M. RAYMOND.—Ed. Emman.Coll., Camb.; LL.B., Camb., 1890; barrister-at-law, Inner Tem.; ag. stip. and circuit mag., and judge of com. pleas, Bahamas, 1892-4; dist. comsnr., Lagos, Aug., 1894; ag. Queen's advocate, and mem. exec. and legis. coun., Lagos, Dec., 1896, to Aug., 1897; ch. judicial offr., Niger Coast Protectorate, Aug., 1897; puisne judge, S. Nigeria, Jan., 1, 1899; also puisne judge, N. Nigeria; ag. ch. just., 8 Mar. to 27 Oct., 1900, 28 Dec., 1901, to 25 Sept., 1902; reed. thanks of gov., for spec. services in reorganising native courts of prot., Jan., 1903.

MERCER, WILLIAM HEFWORTH, C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1855; open scholar and Hody exhibnr., Wadham Coll., Oxford; 2nd cl. classical mods.; 2nd cl. final classical schl.; 2nd cl. clk. in the C.O. after open compet., 30th June, 1879; awarded 1st prize in common law offered by council of legal education, 1883, and a pupil scholarship of 100 guineas, in real property law, by the soc. of the Inner Temple, 1885; called to the bar, 1886; sec. to the Earl of Jersey; when ag. as Br. delegate at col. confere., Ottawa, 1894; on special mission to Hawaii with Mr. (now Sir) Sandford Fleming, Sept. to Nov., 1894, negotiating for lease of landing station of proposed Pacific cable; asst. priv. sec. to Lord Ripon, Apr., 1896; to Mr. Chamberlain, June, 1896; 1st class clk., May, 1896; sec. to Pacific cable comtee., 1896; prin. clk., Oct., 1898; sec. to Pacific cable comtee., 1899; crown agt. for the colonies, June, 1900; mem. of Pacific cable bd., 1901; apptd. director of scholars sent to England from certain crown colonies, 1902.

MERCIER, GUY HENRY VIENOT.—2nd clk., regisr. and provost-marshal's office, Antigua, 1895; 4th clk., col. sec.'s office, 1897; clk. to regisr. and provost-marshal, Nevis, 1898; dep. coroner, Nevis, 1901; rev. offr., Nevis, 1901.
MEREDITH, O. R.—For many years leader of opposition in Ontario legis. assem.; ch. just. ct. of com. pleas, Ontario, 1894.

MEREWETHER, EDWARD MARSH, C.V.O. (1903); C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1858; ed. Harrow; cadet, S.S., Oct., 1880; passed final exam. in Malay, May, 1882; ag. collr. of land rev., Singapore, and J.P., S.S., May, 1882; confirmed, Jan., 1883; Malay interp. with H.M.S. "Pegasus" to Tenom in connection with the "Niseru" case, June, 1884; ag. mag. and offr. in charge treas., Malacca, 1886; dist. offr., Dindings, 1886; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, 1888; ag. 1st mag., Penang, 1888; 2nd ast. col. sec., June, 1890; supt. of census, 1891; ag. ast. col. sec., Aug., 1891; collr. of land rev., and in charge of treas., Malacca, July, 1892; ag. res. coun., Malacca, Sept. to Dec., 1893; inspr. of prisons, S.S., 1893; ast. col. sec. and clk. of coun., May, 1897; ag. res. coun., Malacca, May, 1897, to June, 1898, and Sept., 1900; ag. col. treas., S.S., Jan., 1900; ag. res. coun., Malacca, Sept., 1901; ag. Br. res., Selangor, Oct., 1901; confirmed, Dec., 1901; lieut.-gov. and ch. sec. to govt., Malta, Aug., 1902.

MERRIMAN, THE HON. JOHN XAVIER.—Comsnr. of pub. wks. and crown lands, C. of Good Hope, 1875-8; has for several years represented the electoral div. of Dordrecht in the Cape legis. assem.; again comsnr. of crown lands and pub. wks., 1881 to 1884; treas.-gen., 1890-3; mem. of Jameson raid comtee., 1896; treas.-gen. in the Schreiner ministry, 1898 to 1900; mem. for Victoria West, 1904.

MESSER, C. MCI.—Ed. Merchant Taylors' schl., and Emmanuel Coll., Camb.; exhibitioner, 1892; scholar, 1893; 16th wrangler math. tripos, 1896; B.A.; 1896; cadet, Hong Kong, 1897; ag. land offr., New Territory, 1899; J.P., 1900; ag. col. treas., 1901; passed cadet, 1902; ag. regisr.-gen., 1902; ast. land offr., N.T., 1903; and mem. of land ct., 1903-4.

MESSERVY, CHARLES.—Ed. at Victoria Coll., Jersey; ast. col. engr., Lagos, Nov., 1870; ast. to supt. of pub. wks., Trinidad, Nov., 1871; supt. of pub. wks., Barbados, Sept., 1881; dir. of pub. wks., Grenada, 1885; ret. on pension; re-employed as engr. in charge of pub. wks., Falklands, 1898 to 1901.

METHUEN, LIEUT.-GEN. LORD, K.C.B., K.C.V.O., C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1845; entered army, 1864; served in Ashanti campaign, 1873-4; in Egypt, 1882; in the Bechuanaland expdn., 1884-5; inspected Bechuanaland pol., 1890; commanding a div. in S. Africa, 1899.

METZGEN, CARL A.—Temporary clk., survr.-gen.'s office, Br. Honduras, 1855; clk. to att.-gen., 1887; ag. clk. to clk. of ct., Sept. to Nov., 1887; rev. offr., 1888; 3rd clk., treas., customs, and post office, 1889; 2nd clk., treas., etc., Feb., 1891; capt., Belize fire brigade, June, 1889, to Feb., 1891; clk. to auditor, Dec., 1891; 4th clk., treas., Sept., 1892; ag. 3rd clk., Nov., 1893; audit examr., col. sec. dept., Feb., 1894; ch. clk., treas. and customs dept., May, 1897.

METZGER, S. ABIBU.—Apptd., after compet. exam., ast. master model schl., S. Leone, Aug., 1875; resig., and joined mercantile service; extra offr. H.M. customs, July, 1884; extra clk. gov't. office, June, 1885; with Sir S. Rowe to Yor. exped., 1885; clk. sup. ct. and sheriff's offr., 1886; dep. and ag. master and regisr., sup. ct., great part of 1892-7; ag. sol. and advoc., sup. ct., 1897; ag. regisr.-gen., 1895-6; ag. curator intesta. estates, 1897; under-sheriff, Dec., 1897; sec. examn. comtee., 1896; ag. dist. comsnr., Waterloo dist., June to Dec., 1899; J.P., S. Leone.
MEWS, ARTHUR.—Accnt., gov. telegraph., Newfulld., 1893; clk. to fincl. sec., 1894; fincl. clk., col. sec.'s office, 1895; dep. col. sec., 1893.

MIALL, EDWARD.—Entered public serv., Canada, 1870; ast. comsnr., inld. rev., 1872; attended fishery comsnr., Halifax, 1877; mem. of Pacific rly. comsnr. of inquiry, 1880-1; dep. min. comsnr. of inld. rev., and comsnr. of standards, Jan., 1883.

MICALLEF, RICHARD, C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1846; entd. the Malta serv., Nov., 1863; clk. in office of charitable institutions, Dec., 1864; clk., sanitary office and sec. to the med. bd., Jan., 1881; dep. recr.-gen., dep. comptroller of charitable instit. and dep. comsry., Monte di Pietà, Gozo, Apr., 1884; ast. sec. to govt. for Gozo, Sept., 1885; comptroller of charitable instit., with seat in coun. of govt., Nov., 1886, and a seat in the exec. coun., Mar., 1888.

MICHELL, ROLAND LYONS N.—Ed. Chr. Ch. Oxford (open studentship, 1865); tutor to Prince Ibrahim Pasha, son of the Khedive of Egypt, 1870; received decoration of the Osmanieh (4th class) from the Khedive Ismail, 1878; and of the Medjidieh from the late Khedive Tewfik, 1879; chief of statistical dept., Cadastral survey, Egypt, 1879; comsnr. Limasol, Cyprus, Aug., 1879; also dep. comsnr. high court, and pol. mag.; is sheriff of dist.

MICHELL, WALTER CECIL.—Ed. Godolphin schl. and Merton Coll., Oxon.; B.A., 1887; cadet, S. Sttlmts., Oct., 1887; ag. collr. and mag., Ula Pahang, 1888; passed Malay final, Sept., 1889; dist. offr., Balik, Pulau, Oct., 1891; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, June, 1895; also dep. regisr., sup. ct., Penang, Oct., 1896; 2nd mag., Singapore, May, 1897; ag. 2nd ast. col. sec., S.S., Aug., 1897; ast. collr. of ld. rev., Singapore, Sept., 1897; ag. sen. dist. offr., P.W., Dec., 1898, to Dec., 1899; official assignee, S.S., Dec., 1901; ag. comsnr., ct. of requests, Singapore, Jan., 1902; ag. 1st mag., Sing., Aug., 1903; comsnr., ct. of requests, Sing., Sept., 1903, but continued to act as 1st mag.

MIDDLETON, JOHN PAGE, B.A.—Ed. Uppingham and Trin. Hall, Camb.; called to the bar, Mid. Term., June 1874, Norfolk and S.E. circuits; ag. Queen's advoc., G. Coast, Jan., 1882; pres. of dist. ct., Limasol, Cyprus, Sept., 1882; puisne judge, sup. ct., 1892; ag. ch. just., June and July, 1893; ag. judge of consular ct., Constantinople, May to July, 1894; ag. ch. just., May to Oct., 1895; received thanks of S. of S. Foreign Affairs in connection with Ottoman Dominions (Courts) O. in C., 1895; puisne judge, Ceylon, 1902.

MILLER, F. A.—Ch. clk. crown law office, S. Leone, 1891; dep. master, sup. ct., Sept. to Oct., 1895; 1st clk. col. secretariat, July, 1896; ch. clk. col. secretariat, Apr., 1879; gov.'s clk. and clk. of legis. coun., 1888; J.P. for colony.
MILLER, GRAVILLY G.—Master of titles, Queensland, Nov., 1882; dist. ct. judge, Aug., 1879.

MILLER, WILLIAM AKERMAN.—Assoc. M. Inst. C.E.; ed. King Henry VIII.'s school, Coventry, and Mason's Coll., Birmingham; pupil and ast. to E. Pritchard, M.I.C.E., Birmingham; 2nd cls. supt., P.W.D., Jamaica, 1891; 1st cls. ditta., 1896; col. engr. and survr. gen., Dominica, 1902; CHRNMN., telephone bd., Roseau, 1902; mem., Roseau town bd., 1902; sub-intendant, crown lands, 1903.

MILLS, SIR RICHARD, K.C.B. (1901), C.B. (1893); V.D.—B. 1830; acctnt. to the treasy., 1859-72; treasy. offr. of accs., 1872-88; astt. comptroller and auditor, Sept., 1888; comptroller and auditor-gen., Apr., 1896, to 1900; late lieut.-col. comdg. (hon. col.) 12th Middlesex (Civ. Ser.) R.V.; was hon. auditor of certain crown cols. ag. on behalf of the S. of S. for Cols.; retired 1900.

MILNER, THE Rt. HON. VISCOUNT (1902), 1st Baron (U.K., creat. 1901). P.C., G.C.B. (1901), G.C.M.G. (1897), K.C.B.—B. 1854; ed at Oxford; scholar Balliol; 1st class lit. hum., 1876; Hertford scholar, 1874; Craven scholar, 1877; Eldon scholar, 1878; Derby scholar, 1878; Fellow of New Coll.; called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1881; priv. sec. to Rt. Hon. G. J. Goschen, Chanc. of Excheq., 1887-9; under-seo. of finance in Egypt, 1889-92; chmn. bd. inland rev., 1892; author of "England in Egypt"; gov. Cape (1897-1901) and High Comnr. S. Africa, 1897; admnstr. of Transvaal and Orange River Colony, as well as High Comnr. S. Africa, Jan., 1901.
MILNER, P.A.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the Civil Serv., and assigned to the C. O., July, 1900.

MILTON, SIR W. H., K.C.M.G. (1903), C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1854; clk., col. sec.'s office, Cape, Apr., 1878; 3rd class clk., 1st Jan., 1879; 2nd class clk., Jan., 1881; 1st class clk., Apr., 1883; sec. to the tender bd., 1881; clk. to exec. coun., 1885; ch. clk. and acctnt., col. sec.'s office, 1891; sec. to prime min., Mar., 1894; detached for serv. in Rhodesia as ch. sec. and sec. for native aff., Aug., 1896; transf'd. to Rhod. serv., Oct. 1897; ag. admr. Rhod., July, 1897, to Nov., 1898; apptd. admnstr. Mashonaland and senr. admnstr. S. Rhodesia, Dec., 1898.

MINNOW, JOSEPH LAZARUS.—Extra clk., customs, Elmina, 1872; scouts' interp. and commissariat issuer during Ashanti war, 1873-4; office keeper, customs, Cape Coast, 1874; clk. col. sec., 1874; 1st landing waiter and dep. regar. and interp., Axim, 1875; clk. and examining offr., Salt Pond, 1880; sub-clk. of customs, Chamah, 1884; Cape Coast, 1886.

MINTO, THE RIGHT HON. THE EARL OF (4th Earl, cr. 1813), VISCOUNT MELGOUND (U.K.), BARON OF MINTO (1797, Gt. Brit.), Bart. (1710, Scot.), P.C. (1902), G.C.M.G. (1896), GILBERT JOHN ELLIOT.—B. 1845; succeeded 1891; ed. at Eton and at Trin. Coll., Camb.; B.A. 1866; ensign and lieut. Scots Guards, 1867-70, is col. comdg. S. Scotland inftry. vol. brig. (V.D.), and a D.L. for cos. of Roxburgh and Selkirk, served in Kurram Valley, Afghan campaign, 1879 (medal); accompanied Sir Fredk. (now Lord) Roberts to S. Africa as priv. sec., 1881; served in mounted inftry. in Egyptian campaign, 1882 (wounded Aug. 24th); afterwards commanded mntd. inftry. in Cairo (mentioned in despatches, medal and 4th class Medjidie); was sec. and mil. sec. to Marquis of Lansdowne, gen. gen. of Canada, 1883-6; served as ch. of staff in N.W. rebellion, Canada, 1885 (mentioned in despatches and medal); gov. gen. of Canada, 1898-1904.

MITCHELL, G. P. S.—Ast. clk., educn. dept., Trinidad, Aug., 1880; 2nd clk. to astt. dir. of pub. wks., Feb., 1884; clk., N. div. of the P.W.D., May, 1886; clk. to warden of St. Ann's and Diego Martin Warder Unions, July, 1893; bandmr. Trinidad Field Artill. Volr. band, June, 1896.

MITCHELL, ROBERT W. S., C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1840; astt. clk., immgr. dept., Trinidad, 1858; warden of the dist. of Montserrat, and comanr. of crown lands, 1867; sub-intendant of crown lands, 1869; was a J.P., mem. of the cent. road bd., and of the bd. of health; astt. protector of immgrs., Mauritius, 1872; received the thanks of H.M.'s roy. comnrs. for special services rendered during their inquiry into the treatment of Indian immgrs. in that col.; acted as astt.-gen. of immgr. in Trinidad in 1873; govt. immgr. agt. in India, Oct. 1873; ag. immgr. agt. for Jamaica, 1876; astt.-gen. for immgr., Br. Guiana, 1881; immgr. agt. for Br. Guiana, in Calcutta, 1884; in 1885 modified immgr. rules under Act of 1883, and in 1886 obtained extension of Indian P.O. money order system to Br. Guiana and other W. Ind. cols.

MOFFAT, JOHN B.—Priv. sec. to col. sec., Cape, 1890; prin. clk. col. sec.'s office 1894; ag. ch. clk., 1896, and again 1896; ch. clk., native affairs offr., and accounting offr., prime minister's dept., Oct., 1897; civ. comnr. and res. mag., Mafeking, Nov., 1900; comnr. of taxes, July, 1904.

MOFFAT, REV. JOHN SMITH, C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1835; native comnr., N.W. Border, Transvaal, 1890-1; R. M., Basutoland, Mar., 1892, to Apr., 1894; R. M. Taunga, Br. Bechuanaaland, 1885; astt. comnr. for the protectorate, 1887.

MOFFETT, FRANK J.—Ed. Yorkshire Coll., Leeds, Univ. Coll., N. Wales, and schl. of elec. engineering, Hanover Sq., W.; B.A. Univ. Lond.; A.I.E.E.; astt. elec. engnr. to the col. of Lagos, Feb., 1897; chief elec. engnr., Nov., 1897.
MOIR, CHAS. EDWARD.—Ag. 4th landing waiter, customs, St. Kitts, July, 1873, to Jan., 1875; 3rd landing waiter, May, 1875; 2nd ditto, Dec., 1878; rev. offr. and quarantine offr., Aug., 1883.
MOLESWORTH, H.—Judge, ct. of insolvency, Victoria, 1896; formerly co. ct. judge.

MOLONEY, SIR C. ALFRED, C.M.G. (1882), K.C.M.G. (1890).—B.1848; entered Mil. Coll., Sandhurst 1860; ensign, Feb., 1867; lieut., Dec., 1864; capt., Nov., 1874; acted as civil comdt. of the Quiah dist. S. Leone, 1867-8; A.D.C. to Sir James Walker, C.B., gov., Bahamas, Feb., 1871; priv. sec. to Capt. G. C. Strahan, admstr. Bahamas, Apr., 1871, to May, 1873; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to Gov. Hennessy, June to Nov., 1873, when he embarked for service on the G. Coast; served in the Ashantee campaign, 1873-4 (medal); priv. sec. and A.D.C. to Gov. Strahan, July, 1874; ag. auditor, G.C. Col., Feb., 1875; ag. inspr.-gen. of constab., 1875; astt. col. sec., 1877; col. sec., 1879; admnstd. gov. of Lagos, 1878 to 1880, and again in 1881; admstr. of the govt. of the col., 1882; reamed the admnstr. of Lagos, 1882-3; admstr. of the Gambia, 1884; admstr. of Lagos, 1885; gov., 1887; gov., Br. Honduras, 1891; gov. of Windward, 1897; gov. Trinidad and Tobago, 1900-1904; author of "Forestry of West Africa."
MOMPLE, FRANCOIS JOSEPH ROBERT.—M.B.C.M. D.P.H. (Edin.); health offr., Mauritius, 7th Sept., 1898; ag. astt. sanitary warden, 4th Apr., 1900.

MONCREIFF, HON. FREDERIC.—Ed. at Edin. Acad., Glasgow Univ., and New Coll., Oxford; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., Apr., 1874; went N.E. circuit; puisne judge, sup. ct., Mauritius, Nov., 1895; ag. ch. justice, Feb., 1900; puisne judge, sup. ct. Ceylon, Apl., 1900; ag. ch. justice, Aug.-Nov., 1901; and March-Apr., and May-Oct., 1902.

MONCRIEFF, ALEXANDER BAIN, M.I.C.E., M. Amm. S. E.—Entered pub. wks. dept. of S. Australia as engnrng. draftsman, Nov., 1874; rly. res. engnr., Dec., 1879; engnr.-in-ch. for rly. and water wks., also engnr. for harbours and jetties, May, 1888; mem. of rly. advisory bd. and chmn. of S.A. supply and tender bd.; engnr.-in-chief and chmn. supply and tender bd., 1896-1899.

MONPLAISIR, VOLMAR JOSEPH.—Entd. Saint Lucia serv. as super. clk., P.O., 21st July, 1896; clk., P.O., 9th Mar., 1899; ag. postmr. in 1901 and 1902; ch. clk., P.O., Grenada, 8th Jan., 1903; dep. postmr., 5th Aug., 1903; postmr., Saint Vincent, 22nd Oct., 1904.
MONTAGUE, AUBREY.—Ed. City of London schl.; M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.B. (Lond.), 1898; med. offr., Fiji, Sept., 1898.
MOOR, THE HON. F. R., M.L.A.—Sec. for native affairs, Natal, 1893; also in 1899.

MOOR, SIR RALPH DENHAM RAYMENT, K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1896).—B. 1860; apptd. deputy comsnr. and vice-consul. in Oil Rivers (Niger Coast) Protectorate, and adjoining native territories, July, 1892; ag. comsnr. and consul-gen. from Aug., 1892, to Feb., 1893, Apr. to Nov., 1894, July to Dec., 1895; promoted consul, Jan., 1896; comanr. and consul-gen., Niger Coast Protec., and adjoining native territories; consul in Cameroons, and consul in Isl. of Fernando Po, Jan., 1896; received Jubilee medal, 1897; high comsnr. for Southern Nigeria, 1900-3.
MOOR, WM. HENRY.—Member of Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1885 to Aug., 1902; asst. col. sec., Transvaal, Aug., 1902.

MOORE, GEORGE, I.S.O. (1903).—Survr. to comanr. for quieting land titles, Sept., 1875; 1st govt. survr., col. of Fiji, Oct., 1876; staff survr., Jan., 1880; ag. crown survr., July, 1884, to Feb., 1885; comsnr. of lands, wrks., and water supply, and crown survr., 1899.
MOORE, H. H. K.—Ed. Farra school, Ireland, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. G. Coast cadet, Sept. 4th, 1901.

MOORE, LINUT, HUGH G., R.N.R.—3rd cls. offr., Niger Coast Prot., 14th Apr., 1897; 2nd cls. offr., 1st July, 1897; in command of armed steam yacht "Ivy," Oct., 1897; served with Benin Territories expdn., Apr. and May, 1899 (ment. in desps., medal and clasp); served in Aro expdn., 1901-02 (medal and clasp, and complimentary record on service sheet); asst. marine supt., marine dept., So. Nigeria, Aug., 1902.

MOORE, HON. W.—Min. of lands and wks., Tasmania, Aug., 1873, to July, 1876; col. sec., Aug., 1877, to 20th Dec., 1878, and again Oct., 1879, to 1881; pres. of legis. com.; afterwards chief sec.; resig. 1899.
MORCOM, HERBERT C.—Employed in traffic dept., L. & N. W. rlyw., England, 1890-1898; apptd. asst. traffic manager, govt. rlyws, Trinidad, 1898.
MOREHEAD, B. D.—Postmr.-gen., Queensland, Dec., 1880, to Aug., 1883; col. sec., June, 1888; premier, 1889-90.
MORETON, THE HON. BERKELEY BASIL.—Postmr.-gen., Queensland, Mar., 1886; min. of public instruction, Apr., 1886-8.

MORGAN, RICHARD HENRY.—Sub. asst. survr., Ceylon, 1st Mar., 1876; asst. survr., 1st Jan., 1877, to 31st Dec., 1880; asst. survr., Singapore, 1st Jan., 1881; transf. to Penang, 1st Jan., 1885; senior survr., Penang, 30th July, 1902.

MORGAN, STANLEY WM.—Ed. Bromsgrove schl.; dist. comanr., G. Coast, June, 1892; ch. regiar. sup. ct. and regiar. of deeds, Jan., 1893; clk. of councils, July, 1898; asst. col. sec., Aug., 1898; puisne judge of sup. ct., Aug., 1899.

MORINE, HON. ALFRED B., K.C., LL.R.—Born in Nova Scotia; removed to Newfoundld. u 1883; became editor of "Mercury" in 1883, and of "Herald," both published in St. John's; admitted to bar of N. S., 1894, and in same year to bar of Newfoundld.; was elected M.H.A. for Bonavista, Newfoundld., in 1886, 1889, 1893, 1897; was mem. of people's delegation to Gt. Brit. in 1890 on French treaties question, and mem. of legis. delegtn., to Gt. Brit. in 1891 and 1898 on same question; col. sec., Apr. to Dec., 1894; recvr.-gen., 1897; min. of finance and cust. and chmn. treasry. bd., 1898; resig. same year; min. of marine and fisheries, 1899; again resig. 1899.
MORPHEIT, JOHN CUMMINGS.—Ed. St. Peter Coll., Adelaide, and Pemb. Coll., Oxon; part. librn., S. Austr., 1873; clk. astt. H. of A., 1886; ditto, L.C., 1888; ch. H. of A., 1901.
MORRAH, A.—Sec. for lands, Victoria, 1st June, 1878; comanr. of audit, 1890.

MORRIS, SIR DANIEL, K.C.M.G. (1903); M.A., D.Sc., C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1844; ed. at Cheltenham and Roy. Schl. of Mines, London; late sen. moderator and 1st gold medalist, natural science honours, Trin. Coll., Dub.: B.A. (1876), M.A. (1880); astt. botanical gardens, Ceylon, Aug., 1887; on special duty, "coffee leaf disease inquiry," Ceylon, 1879; received thanks of planters' assoc. of Ceylon, as also special commendation from Sir M. Hicks-Beach, and honorarium equal to one year's salary; dir., pub. gardens and plantations, Jamaica, Nov., 1879; on special mission to Trinidad and Grenada to report on the cultivation of cacao, 1882; reported upon the economic resources of the col. of Br. Honduras, 1882; chmn., Jamaica comtee., Amsterdam exhib., 1883; reported on resources of St. Helena, 1883; prepared report upon planting enterprise in the W. Indies, presented to parlmt., 1884; chmn. bd. of govt. of the Inst. of Jamaica, 1884-86; comanr. for the W. Indies at the World's Exposition, New Orleans, 1885; chmn. of local comsn. for Col. and Ind. Exhibition, 1886; astt. dir., Roy. Gardens, Kew, May, 1886; represented India at Ramie Confce. at Paris, 1888 and 1899; on mission to W. Indies to organise botan. stations in Windward and Leeward Isls., 1889-90; visited Bahamas in 1896-6, with the object of advising and aiding the col. govt.; impl. comanr. of agricul. for W. Indies, stationed at Barbados, 1898; author of numerous pamphlets on pure and applied botany, and of papers and reports relating to distrih. and cultiv. of tropical economic plants.

MORRIS, HON. E. P., K.C.—Mem. Newfoundland assem., 1885-1900; ag. atty.-gen. for Newfd., 1890-91-95; delegate to Ottawa on confederation question, 1894; delegate to London, 1899, with Rt. Hon. Sir W. V. Whiteway, on matters then before the col. office; leader independent party, Newfd. assem., 1898-1900; mem. Newfd. cabinet, 1889-1897-1900; master in chancery, and re-elected for St. John's West, 1900; editor Newfd. Law Reports Revision, 1820-1900.

MOSELEY, C. H. HARLEY.—C.M.G. (1903); col. sec., Lagos, 1901; spec. serv. offr., S. Leone, 1881; astt. treas., Sherbro, 1882; astt. col. sec., S. Leone, 1884; J.P., S. Leone, 1882; civil comdt. and coroner, Sherbro, 1886; spec. mission to interior with Sir S. Rowe; treas., Gambia, 1891; mem. ex. and leg. couns., Gambia; administered govt., 1891, 1892, and 1893; mission to Fogue country, 1891; Gambia expedition, 1894, thanked for services; transf. to Lagos, 1894; mem. ex. and leg. couns., and bd. of educ.; mem. of comm. on trade; ag. gov., Lagos, 1902.

MOSELEY, FRANCIS ARNOLD.—Admitted to Bahamas bar, 1874; ag. clk., col. sec.'s dept., Bahamas, Apr., 1872; priv. sec. to Gov. Pope Hennessy, Nov., 1873 (ex officio clk. of exec. coun.), and to Admstr. J. D'A. Dumaresq, June to Dec., 1874; astt. priv. sec. to Gov. Robinson, Dec., 1874, to June, 1879; priv. sec. to Admstr. E.B.A. Taylor, June, 1879; to Gov. Robinson, Dec., 1879, to June, 1880; also to Mr. Taylor from that date to Dec., 1880; J.P. for the col., and ag. pol. mag. for the island of New Providence, Sept., 1887; regiar. of titles, W. Australia, 1881; admitted to W. Australia bar, 1883; dep. commnr. of titles, 1881-2-3, and 1888; J.P.; 1883; regiar. sup. ct., 1889.

MOSSE, CHARLES BENJAMIN, C.M.G. (1897), C.B. (1874), A.M., T.C.D., M.R.C.S., L.R. and K.C., Ph. and L.M., F.R.Z.S., dep. surg.-gen., A.M.D.—B. 1830; served in med. charge of expeditionary force up the River Gambia, W. Africa, June 1866; present at assault and capture of the stockaded Mandingo town of Tubaroolong (mentioned in despatches); staff surg. (1867) for "valuable services" during epidemic of yellow fever at Bathurst; held the acting appointments of Queen's advoc. (mem. of coun.), ch. mag., col. surg. and inspr. of prisons, when serving at Gambia and on the G. Coast; served throughout Ashanti war, 1873-74; present at action of Essaman, at battles of Amoasul and Ordahau, and at capture of Coomassie (mentioned in despatches, C.B., and medal with clasp); suppdg. med. offr. for Jamaica, June, 1896; ret., 1904.
MOUNTSTEPHEN, 1ST BARON, SIR GEORGE STEPHEN (creat. 1891), Bart. (1886).—B. 1829; was chmn. of Canadian Pacific Ry. to 1888.
MOURITZ, GEORGE A.—Treas., Melbourne harbour trust, 1877; sec., 1884.

MOYSEY, HENRY LUTTRELL, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1849; ed. at Cheltenham Coll.; a writer in the Ceylon civ. ser., Sept., 1870; pol. mag., Kayta, Oct., 1872; ag. astt. gov't. agt., Kurunegala, Apr., 1873; ag. astt. gov't. agt., Batticaloa, July, 1873; astt. agt., Kurunegala, 1876; pol. mag., Kandy, 1876; astt. agt., Matale, 1878; dist. judge, Kurunegala, Dec., 1891; ag. dist. judge, Galle, Mar., 1894; Batticaloa, July, 1895; gov't. agt., prov. Sabaragamuwa, Sept., 1897; prin. collr. of cust., July, 1899; P.M.G., and director of telegraphs, Jan., 1900.

MOYSEY, COL. CHARLES JOHN, R.E., C.M.G. (1884).—B. 1840; ed. at Cheltenham Coll.; special comsrr. Keate Award dist., S. Africa, 1880-1; assisted in defining the S.W. boundary of the Transvaal, under the Pretoria convention of 1881; astt. dir. of wks., W.O., 1884-9.

MUGLISTON, THOMAS CRIGHTON, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. and L.M. (Edin.).—Entered R.N. med. service, 1876-81; med. offr. to French and U.S. consulates, Singapore; J.P. and coroner, 1883; col. surrg., S.S., 1886; ag. prin. med. offr., Singapore, Feb. to Aug., 1890, July-Aug., 1896, and from March, 1901 to Apr., 1902; col. surg., Penang, March, 1901.

MUIR, THOMAS, C.M.G. (1901), M.A. (Glas., 1869), LL.D. (Glas.), F.R.S.E., F.R.G.S.—B. 1846; mem. Lond. and Edin. Math. Soc., etc.; sub-warden, St. Leonard's Coll. Hall, St. Andrews, 1869; astt. prof. math. Glasgow Univ., 1871; head math. and sci. master, Glasgow high schl., 1874; examr. in math. and natl. phil. to Glas. Univ., 1880-84; Keith medallist, R.S.E., 1881; examr. secondary schls., Scotch educn. dept., 1885-87; examr. in maths. and nat. phil., St. Andrews Univ., 1884-86; supt.-gen. of educn., Cape, Apr., 1892.
MULCAHY, HON. EDWARD.—Minister of Lands and Works in the Lewis Ministry, Tasmania, 1899-1900.

MULOCK, THE HON. SIR WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1902), K.C., LL.D., M.A.—B. 1843; graduated at Toronto Univ., 1863; gold medal in modern languages; called to the Ontario bar, 1868; vice-chanc. of Toronto Univ. in 1881; sat in parlmt. since 1882, representing N. York; postmr.-gen. in Sir W. Laurier's govt., June, 1896.
MUNRO, THE HON. JAMES.—Premier and treas. of Victoria, 1891; agt.-gen. for the col. in London, 1892-3.

MURPHY, MATTHEW ALEXANDER, A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados, and Glasgow and W. of Scotland Tech. Coll.; pupil, Messrs. Kyle, Dennison, and Frew, C.E., Glasgow, 1891; junior astt. engrnr. (with contractors), Lanarkshire and Dumbartonshire rlyw., 1894; astt. engrnr. for new works, Trinidad govt. rlyws., 1896; astt. maintenance engrnr., 1898; ag. ditto, Nov., 1900, to Apr., 1901, Feb., 1904, to June, 1904; seconded for serv. on road scheme, P.W.D., Arima dist., Jan., 1903, to Feb., 1904; supt. of pub. wks., St. Lucia, 1905.

MURRAY, THE HON. ALEXANDER, C.E. (Glasgow Univ.), A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Hyde Abbey Schl., Winchester, Pioneer offr., P.W.D., Ceylon, 1871; supt., 1871; ch. astt. headqrs., 1874; ag. irrig. ass't., 1876; mem. of comen. to enquire into col. store dept., 1877; prov. engrnr., 1886; voted bonus of 5,000 rs. by leg. coun. for invention of concrete sluices for irrign., May, 1890; seconded for spec. serv., att.-gen.'s dept., 1896; ag. dir., P.W., July; astt. dir. Nov., 1897; col. engrnr., survr.-gen., and comptlr. of convicts, S. Sttlmts., Jan., 1898; sent to Perak to report on Krian irrign. scheme, July, 1898; mem. loc. jnt. nav. and mil. def. comtee., Sept., 1898; comdt. Singapore vol. artillery, Apr., 1899; sent to enquire into P.W. Dept., Nigri Sembilan, Nov., 1899; maj. and comdt. Singapore vol. corps., Nov., 1900, lieut.-col., 1902; on spec. duty in Ceylon, 1903; mem. of exec. and leg. couns., S. Sttlmts.; served in Ceylon vol. force 18 years.
MURRAY, ALEXANDER.—Mem. of legis. assem., Manitoba, 1874 to 1878; pol. mag. E. Marquette, 1878; mem. of house of assem., 1879; speaker, 1883 to 1886; mun. comsrr., Winnipeg, 1878.
MURRAY, CHARLES.—M.A., Univ. of Cape, 1884; astt. prof. lit. and math., Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, Jan., 1888; dep. inspr. schls., Jan., 1889; sec. educn. dept., Cape Col., Nov., 1891.
MURRAY, C. E. R.—Crown prosecutor, S.W. dist., N.S.W., June, 1878; dist. ct. judge, N.W. dist., 1878; of N. dist., 1880.
MURRAY, THE HON. CHARLES GIDEON.—Asst. priv. sec. and A.D.C. to lieut.-gov. Br. N. Guinea, 1898; clk. in col. sec.'s office, 1899; res. mag., Western Division, B.N.G. 1900.
MURRAY, T., Junr.—Govt. med. offr., Tacarigua dist., Trinidad, Jan., 1876.
MURRAY, THE HON. SIR THOS KEIR, K.C.M.G. (1901), C.M.G. (1896).—Min. of lands and wks., Natal, 1893; col. sec., Feb. to Oct., 1897; M.L.A. to 1902; census comsrr., 1904.

MUSGRAVE, ANTHONY, Junr., C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1849; priv. sec. to Gov. Musgrave in Newfoundland, from July, 1868, to July, 1869; served in same capacity in Br. Columbia, Aug., 1869, to Apr., 1872; priv. sec. and clk. of exec. coun., Natal, July, 1872, to 1873; priv. sec. and clk. of exec. coun. in S. Australia, June, 1873; priv. sec. to gov. of Jamaica, Aug., 1877, to June, 1883; also acted as sec. to the local comtee. of the Internat. Fisheries Exhibn., 1883; priv. sec. to Sir A. Musgrave in Queensland, Nov., 1883; asst. dep. comnt., New Guinea, 1885; govt. sec., 1888, and local auditor, Nov., 1890; mem. exec. and legis. couns.

MUSGRAVE, CHRISTOPHER.—Jun.clk., regir.'s office, Antigua, Dec., 1874; clk. to mag., dist. A, Apr., 1877; acted as ch. clk., regir.'s office, Jan., 1879; acted as mag. and coroner for dist. B, July, 1882; dep.-coroner for dist. A, Aug., 1882; clk., col. sec.'s office, Mar., 1883; priv. sec. to admr., Lceward Is., Aug., 1883, to Jan., 1884; ag. mag. and coroner for dist. B, Dec., 1884, and for dist. A, Feb. and June, 1885; sec. to local comtee. of the Col. and Ind. Exhibn., Jan., 1886; ag. mag., Nevis, 1886; mem. of infirmary bd., 1886; ag. mag. and coroner, Barbuda, Oct., 1888, to Nov., 1889; and for dist. A, Antigua, Dec., 1889, to June, 1890; ch. clk. regir.'s office, Antigua, Aug., 1890; regir. and pro-marshal, Dominica, July, 1892; mem. exec. coun., 1895; offr. in care of pris. discipline, 1895; supt. crown lds., 1897.
MUSSON, JOHN TURNBULL.—Ed. King's Coll. Schl., Lond.; admitted solr., sup. ct. Jamaica, Oct., 1875; notary public, 1883; res. mag., St. Andrew, Nov., 1888.

MUSSON, SAMUEL PAYNTER.—Clk. in the gov.'s sec.'s office, Jamaica, Apr., 1866; 2nd class clk. in office of financial sec., Sept., 1866; 1st class clk. in col. sec.'s office, Oct., 1872; ch. clk., 1884; has repeatedly acted as asst. sec. and as auditor-gen., June, 1885, to May, 1886; treas., Jamaica, 1896; nominated mem. of legis. coun., 1:97.
MYLNE, THOMAS.—Regir. of titles, Queensland, 1884; clk., regir-gen.'s office, 1864; ch. clk., 1873; 2nd deputy, 1878; prin. dep.-regir., 1879; mem. civ. ser. bd., 1890.
NASON, W.—Apptd., after exam., 3rd cls. messenger, C.O., 15th July, 1904.

NATHAN, MAJOR SIR MATTHEW, K.C.M.G. (1902), C.M.G. (1899).—B. 1862; entered R.E., 1880; capt., 1889; maj., 1898; served in Nile expedn., 1885; Lushai expedn. (medal with clasp), 1899; sec. col. defence comtee., 1895; adminstr. govt. of S. Leone in 1899; gov., Gold Coast, Oct., 1900; gov., Hong Kong, 21st Sept., 1903.

NATHAN, SIR N., KT. BACH (1903).—B.A., Lond., 1861; barr., In Temp., '66; Mid. circ., Yorks. and Birrn. Sessions; res. mag., Jamaica, St. Thomas, '88, Kingston, '90; ag. judge, supr. ct., '91-2; judge, supr. ct., Trin., '93; att.-gen., 1st Jan., '98; ag. ch. just., 1900-01; int., 1903.

NAUDI, ALFREDO, C.M.G. (1901), LL.D. (1877).—Received at the bar, Malta, 1878; elected mem. coun. of govt., 1889; prof. of law, Malta Univ., 1892; crown advoc. and govt. legal adviser, with seat in exec. coun. and council of govt., 1895.
NEGUS, FRANCIS H. D.—Local auditor, Lagos, Dec., 1893; ditto., G. Coast, June, 1894.
NEIL, A. T.—Ed. St. Paul's School and Corpus Christi Coll., Camb.; apptd. G. Coast cadet, July 24th, 1901; ag. ch. clk., Oct., 1901.

NELSON, THE RT. HON. SIR HUGH M., P.C., K.C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1835; vice-pres. of coun., premier, ch. sec., and treas. of Queensland, 1893 to 1895; represented the col. in London at H.M.'s Diamond Jubilee; pres. of legis. coun., 1898; lt. gov. of Queensland, 1904.
NEVILL, EDMUND NEVILLE, F.R.A.S., F.C.S., etc.—Govt. astronomer, Natal, 1882; also govt. chemist and official assayer, 1886.

NEWCOMBE, EDMUND LESLIE.—Grad. arts of Univ. of Dalhousie Coll.; LL.B., Univ. of Halifax; called to the bar Nova Scotia, Jan., 1883; Ontario, Dec., 1893; Q.C., Nov., 1893; reprent. of govt. of Canada to confer with H.M.'s govt. of Canadian copyright, 1894; dep.min. of just. of Canada, Mar., 1893.

NEWYE, JOSEPH.—M.I.C.E.; engnr. on staff ch. inspr., Capetown, Nov., 1872; had charge of Buffalo Comtee.'s and Great Kei bridges, and reconstruction of all E. prov. bridges destroyed by 1874 flood; special service in mil. engnr. work during Gealeka and Basuto wars and Tembu rebellion; in charge of Aliwal N. and Kraai River bridges and other wks. and surveys, Nov., 1877; engnr. asst., Capetown, Sept., 1881; dist. inspr., 1882; ch. inspr., June, 1893.

NEWFOUNDLAND AND BERMUDA, FOURTH BISHOP OF (founded 1839), Rt. REV. LLEWELLYN JONES, D.D.—Ed. at Trin. Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1862; M.A., 1866; rector of Little Hereford, with Ashford Carbonell, 1874-1878; consec. bishop, 1878.

NEWLAND, HARRY OMAN.—Asst. supt. pol., Singapore, Dec., 1882; ag. supt. of pol., Singapore, Apr., 1894, to Apr., 1895; ag. supt. P.W., Apr., 1898; ag. supt., Penang, Nov., 1899; confirmed, Dec., 1901.

NEWMAN, GEORGE HENRY, J.P., Queensland, 1877; trustee in insolveny, 1878; curator of intestate estates and in insanity, 1882; commandant corps' of engnr., 1876-88; is a lieut.-col., Queensland defence forces.

NEWTON, FRANCIS JAMES, C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1857; ed. at Rugby and Univ. Coll., Oxon.; B.A., 1880; M.A., 1890; barrister, Inner Temple; lieut., 3rd batt. Hampshire regt., and extra A.D.C. to Sir H. Robinson, gov. Cape, 1891; priv. sec. to Sir Thomas Scanlen, prime minister of Cape, 1883; and to Sir H. Robinson, 1884-86; sec. to Mauritius roy. comm., 1886; ag. admstr. and ch. mag., Br. Bechuanaland, 1888; col. sec. and rec-gen., 1889; ag. admstr. and ch. mag., 1892; res. comsnr., Bechuanaland Protectorate, Dec., 1895-7; col. sec., Br. Honduras, 1898; ditta. Barbaros, 1901; treas., S. Rhodesia, 1903.
NIBLETT, CHARLES HERBERT.—Apptd., after a compet. exam., clk. of the lower (now 2nd) div. of the civ. ser. and assigned to the C.O., Sept., 1879; promoted to higher grade, Apr., 1891.
