NICHOLAS, CYRIL BAILEY, B.A.—Ed. at the Roy. Coll., Colombo, and at Christ's Coll., Camb.; Ceylon govt. univer. schlr.; classical exhibtn. and modern history prizeman of Christ's Coll.; historical tripes; principal asst., Roy. Coll., Colombo.

NICHOLLS, HENRY ALFRED ALFORD, C.M.G. (1896), M.B. (Honours), C.M. (1873), M.D. Abdn. (1875); M.R.C.S. Eng., F.L.S. and C.M.Z.S.—B. 1851; correps. mem. N. York Acad. of Sci., Jamaica Inst., centl. agric. bd. of Trinidad, and chamber of agric. of Guadeloupe; hon. mem. Roy Agric. and Com. Soc. of Br. Guiana; crown nominee, in legis. assem. of Dominica, 1875-7; med. supt. of Dominica Yaws Hosp., 1877; surg. Roseau Infirmary, 1879; med. offr., pub. instn., 1880; chmn., poor law bd., 1885-91; local comsnr. Col. and Ind. Exhibn., 1896; ag. curator, Dominica Bot. Station, May to Oct., 1890; gained prize of 100l. for best text book of Tropical Agriculture offered by the govt. of Jamaica, 1890; special comsnr. to inquire into prevalence of Yaws in W Indies, 1891, for report on which (pub. as Blue

NICHOLSON, COL. JOHN SANCTUARY, C.B., D.S.O. (brevet lieut.-col.).—Served in 7th Hussars, 1884; commdt., B.S.A. police, 1896-1900; operations in Matabeleland, 1896; S. African war, 1899-1902; ch. staff offr., S.A.C., 23rd Oct., 1900; inspr.-gen., ditto, 1st May, 1903.

NICHOLSON, LT.-GEN. SIR W. G., K.C.B. (1898); C.B. (1891).—B. 1845; entered R.E., 1863; col., 1891; served in Afghan war, 1878-80 (ment. in desps.); Cannahar field force, 1879 (ment. in desps.); march to Cannahar (desps., brevet major, medal with 3 clasps, bronze decoration); Egyptian war, 1882, including Tel-el-Kebir (4th cls. Osmanli, Khedive's star); Burma expedition, 1886-7, as A.A.G. army headquarters (desps., brevet lieut.-col., medal with clasp); Tirah expeditionary force, 1897-8, as chief of staff (desps., medal with 2 clasps); served in S. African war as mil. sec. to commdr.-in-chief and dir. of transport at headqtrs., 1890-1900, (desps. twice, promoted maj.-gen., medal with five clasps); dir.-gen. of mobilisation and mil. intell., W.O., 1901-4; gov. and commdr.-in-chief, Gibraltar, 1905.

NICOLL, WM., M.A., LL.B.—Forensic prize-man, Edin. Univ., 1882; mem. faculty of advocs., Scotland, 1882; asst. prof. civ. law, Edin. Univ., 1883; stip. mag., Br. Guiana, Apr., 1-88; ag. ch. just., Br. Honduras, 1891-2; ag. puisne judge, Br. Guiana, 1893-4; ag. solr.-gen., 1-94-5; mem. of comsn. on the provost-marshal's office, 1896; dir. of the B.G. bank, 1897; Queen's advoc., Lagos, 1897; puisne judge, G. Coast, 1897; ch. just., Lagos, 1902.
NICOLLE, H. C.—Asst. aud., Cyprus, Feb., 1880; aud.-gen., 1883; Br. deleg. of Evkaf, in addition to his other duties, June, 1886; local auditor, Hong Kong, Jan., 1890.
NICOLLS, ERNEST H. DYNLEY.—Divisional engrn., P.W., Cyprus, 1893.

NIGHTINGALE, THOMAS SLINGSBY.—Clk. to inspr.-gen. war dept., King William's Town, 1883; transf. to customs, 1885; res. mag. and sub. coll. of customs, Port Nolloth, 1891; 1st cl. clk., East London, 1896; ch. clk., agt.-gen.'s off., 1894; asst. sec., April, 1902.
NIMMO, JOHN.—Commdt. of pub. wks., Victoria, Feb., 1886-90.
NOALL, W. E.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the Civil Ser., and assigned to the C.O., June, 1898.
NOCK, W. C.—Clk. pub. wks. dept., Trinidad, 1881; road acctnt., Sept., 1883; storekeeper, May, 1886; acctnt., special pub. wks. and road loan bd., Sept., 1890.
NOEL, ARTHUR BAPTIST.—Eldest son of Judge Noel, Victoria; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Jan., 1877; acted for some time as a crown prosecutor in Queensland; dist. ct. judge, Queensland, 1883.

NORMAN, ARTHUR C. A.—Articled to A. Norman, F.R.I.B.A., Plymouth, 1874-8; assoc. of Roy. Inst. of Br. Architects, 1881; fellow, 1896; ch. draftsman and asst. survr., Plymouth municipality, 1878-83; asst. supptd. P.W.D., Selangor, Federated Malay States, Oct., 1883; ag. supt., Nov., 1888-9; architect, Mar., 1890, to Jan., 1896; ag. state engrn. and chmn. sanitary bd., 1894; sen. dist. engrn., Jan., 1896; ag. deputy state engrn., 1897-9.

NORMAN, GEO. WM., jun.—Clk., regar.'s office, Antigua, May, 1871; ch. clk., marshal's office, Dec., 1874; ditto, regar.'s office, Oct., 1877; ag. prov. marshal and regar. in 1878 and 1880; marshal V.-A. ct., Aug., 1870; sec. to law library, sup. ct., 1878-80; ch. clk., G.P.O., Trinidad, Apr., 1880; surg.-gen.'s dept., May, 1865; ag. warden of St. Ann's and Diego Martin ward union, July, 1888, to Jan., 1889; supervisor for the col., Feb., 1889; 2nd lieut., Trinidad R.V., Sept., 1890.
NORQUAY, HON. J.—Prov. treasy. premier prov. of Manitoba, Canada, June, 1878; rly. comsnr. and premier until 1887.

NORRIS, FREDERICK LAING, M.B.C.M.—Ed. Glas. high schl. and Glas. univ.; asst. to med. offr. of health for various sanitary authorities, 1890; res. clinical asst., Shoreditch infir., 1891; jun. houseurg., Birkenhead hosp., 1892; med. offr. for Nos. 3, 5, and 7 dists., St. Christopher, Sept., 1892; do., for 2 dists., Montserrat, Jan., 1895; off. M.L.C., Nov., 1896; mem. bd. of health, Jan., 1897; J.P., 1898; visiting just. of gaol, 1898; ag. comsnr., etc., in July, 1898; road survr. for dist. No. 1 North, Jan., 1900; res. med. offr., Holberton Instn., Antigua, Sept., 1900.

NORTH, FREDERICK DUDLEY, C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1866; Ed. at Rugby; clk. lands off., W. Aust., 1887; clk. of the ex. coun., 1891, and priv. sec. to Sir John Forrest, G.C.M.G.; under sec., premier's dept., 1898; represented W. Aust. as member of royal celebrations commonwealth comte., in Melbourne on occasion of opening of first commonwealth parlmt. by H.R.H. the Duke of Cornwall and York, May, 1901; under-sec., col. sec.'s dept., 1902.

NORTHCOTE, 1ST BARON (created 1900), SIR HENRY STAFFORD NORTHCOTE, 1ST BART. (created 1887), G.C.M.G. (1904), G.C.I.E., C.B.—B.1846; ed. Etton; Merton Coll., Oxford; M.A.; clk., Foreign Office, 1868; priv. sec. to late Lord Salisbury, Constantinople Embassy, 1876-77; chancellor of exchequer, 1877-80; financial sec. to W.O., 1885-86; surv.-gen. of Ordnance, 1886-7; charity comsnr., 1891-92; M.P., Exeter, 1880-99; gov. of Bombay, 1889; gov.-gen. of Commonwealth of Australia, Aug., 1903.

NORTHCOTE, ERNEST AUGUSTUS.—Ed. at Westminster and Trin. Coll., Camb., LL.B. (hon.), 1873; called to the bar, Mid. Term, 1875, Oxford circuit and Worcester and Usk sessions; stip. mag., Br. Guiana, 1882; sheriff of Essequibo, 1884; ag. puisne judge, Apr., 1884, to Sept., 1885; puisne judge, Jamaica, 1886; ag. ch. just., June, 1892, Sept., 1893, to June, 1894, and Aug., 1894, to Apr., 1895, and on subsequent occasions; ch. just., Trin., 1903.

NORTON, 1ST BARON (U. K., created 1878), RIGHT HON. SIR CHARLES BOWYER ADDERLEY, K.C.M.G. (1869).—B.1814; ed. at Ch. Ch., Oxford, B.A.1838; pres. of bd. of health and vice-pres. of bd. of educn. from Mar., 1858, till June, 1859; M.P. for Staffordshire N., for which he was first retnd. 1841; was under-sec. of state for the col., July, 1866, until Dec., 1868; pres. of the roy. comsn. on sanitary laws, 1868; pres. of the bd. of trade, 1874 to 1878.
NOVA SCOTIA, 6TH BISHOP OF, THE RIGHT REV. FRED. COURTNEY, D.D.—Consec. 1888.

NOWELL, EDWIN CRADOCK, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1831; Clk., commissariat dept., Tasmania, 1852; clk. to gov., 1857; to exec. and legis. coun., 1864; govt. statistician, 1867 to 1892; clk. of fed. coun. of Australasia, 1st session, 1886, and 2nd session 1888; is author of a party handbook.

NUGENT, GEORGE OLIVER.—2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, Leeward Islds., Nov., 1893; ag. 1st clk., Dec., 1894, to June, 1895; on reorganisation of office became 3rd clk., Apr., 1897; ag. 2nd clk. and ex. co., Apr. to Dec., 1897, and June, 1899, to Aug., 1900; ag. ch. clk. and clk., fed. ex. and leg. co., and Antigua leg. co., Aug., 1900; ag. priv. sec., Dec., 1897, to Jan., 1898; Jan. to June, 1899, Oct., 1899, to Aug., 1900; 2nd clk., July, 1901; priv. sec., Nov., 1900, to July, 1901; latit., defence force, Aug., 1901; A.D.C. to gov., 1903.

NUNAN, JOSEPH JOHN.—Ed. Univ. Coll. and Trin. Coll., Dublin; fellow of roy. univ. of Ireland, Blake hist. schlr. and 1st vice-chancellor's prizeman, Dublin Univ.; 1st mod. language schlr., roy. univ., Ireland; Bacon schlr., Gray's Inn; gold medallist in history, literature and composition; won exhibitn. and cert. of honour at honour examn. for call to the bar, King's Inn, 1898; H.M.'s vice-consul, and ch. judicial offrr., B.C. Africa Prot., 12th Oct., 1899; judge of high ct., B.C.A., 11th Aug., 1902.
NUNES, W. G.—Clerk in secretariat, Jamaica, 1875 to 1878; clk., internal rev., 1880; clk. parochial bds., 1882; inspnr. of poor, St. Ann's, 1896.
NUNN, BERNARD—Cadet S. S., Nov., 1900; ag. 4th mag., Sing., July, 1904.

NYLANDER, A. W. M.—Apptd. temporary clk., col. sec.'s office, S. Leone, Aug., 1889; asst. police clk., May, 1891; 2nd clk., crown law off., June, 1896; 1st clk., July, 1896; 1st clk., col. sec.'s off., Dec., 1897; chief clk., Mar., 1898; ag. charity comanr., Sept., 1898; J.P., July, 1899; ag. asst. col. sec., May, 1901; and again from May, 1902, to end of year; charity comanr., Apr., 1902.

O'BRIEN, ANDREW, S. M.—Indentured clk., publ. hosp., Bahamas, June, 1889; 3rd clk. gen. post off., March, 1893; clk. registr. of records dept., March, 1897; customs off., Apr., 1901; ag. res. just. and rev. offrr., Ragged Is., Feb. to May, 1902; ditto, Rum Cay, June to Dec., 1902; clk. police ct., Jan., 1903; res. just., Ragged Island, July, 1903.

O'BRIEN, CHARLES ANDREW.—Ed. at St. Stanislaus Coll., Tullamore, and Roy. Univ. of Ireland (LL.D. 1889); called to Irish bar, 1888; English bar, Mid. Temp., 1892; won 1st and 2nd schol. in corn. and crim. law, Mid. Temp., 1889-90; exhibns. in English law, Roman law and jurisprudence, Queen's Coll., Cork, 1887-8; apptd. dist. comanr., G. Coast, Apr., 1895; crown prosecutor, W. prov., 1895-96, 1897, 1898, 1900, 1901, 1902; comanr. of stamps, W. prov., 1900 to 1902.

O'BRIEN, Sir GEORGE THOMAS MICHAEL, K.C.M.G. (1894), C.M.G. (1889)—B. 1844; ed. at Westminster and Trin. Coll., Camb.; writer, Ceylon service, attached to col. sec.'s office, 1867; additional pol. mag., Kurunegalla, June, 1867; pol. mag., Harrispattu, Jan., 1869; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Feb., 1870; ag. pol. mag., Colombo, Oct., 1870; asst. govt. agt., etc., 1871; 2nd asst. col. sec., 1874; prin. asst., Sept., 1876; treas., Aug., 1886; acted several times as col. sec.; auditor gen., 1890; col. sec., Cyprus, 1891; ditto, Hong Kong, 1892-5; ret., 1895; gov., Fiji, 1897 to 1902.

O'BRIEN, H. L.—Ed. Queen's Roy. Coll., Trinidad, and Roy. Naval Schl., New Cross, Kent, England; 3rd clk., registr.-gen.'s office, Trinidad, Apr., 1881; 2nd clk. to the asst. dir. of pub. wks., July, 1881; extra clk., N. Div., Aug., 1881, to July, 1882; ag. during same period as clk. to the gen. supt. of rlys.; 1st clk. to asst. director of pub. wks., July, 1882; transf'd. to the financial branch, Oct., 1883; 2nd clk., registr.-gen.'s office, Sept., 1884; ag. ch. clk., Mar. and Apr., 1888; ch. clk., registr.-gen.'s office, Oct., 1889; ag. dep. registr.-gen., Mar. and Apr., and June to Nov., 1892; Jan. to Apr., 1893; capt., Trinidad Lt. Infy. Volrs., Feb., 1891.

O'CONNOR, OWEN L.—Inspr. of pol., Mauritius, Oct., 1862; poor law guardian, district of Moka, 1866; was hon. sec. of the "Moka special fund committee" for the relief of the sick poor during the fever epidemic of 1867, and was presented with silver plate by inhabitants for his services; poor law guardian, dist. of Flacq, Jan., 1872; ditto, Grand Port, Jan., 1874; sanit. guardian, Savanna, June, 1874; poor law guardian, Grand Port, Jan., 1875; ag. inspr. of imigrts., 1877; inspr. of imigrts., Feb., 1883; mem. of dist. prison comte., 1868-76, and of local bd. of educn., 1868-74; dir., widows' and orphans' fund, Jan., 1885; ag. protector of imigrts. and mem. of com. of educn., 1889; chmn., Rodriguez Inquiry Comsn., 1889; ag. prot. of imigrts., 6th May, 1894; ag. storekeeper gen., 14th to 26th Aug., 1898; ag. prot. of imigrts., 1st July, 1904.
O'CONNOR, THE HON. RICHARD E., M.A.—Min. of just. in Dibbel ministry, N. S. Wales, 1891-94; joined the commonwealth ministry, 1st Jan., 1901; apptd. justice of the high ct., Sept., 1903.

O'DONOVAN, DENIS, C.M.G. (1893), F.R.S.L., F.R.G.S.—B. 1846; knight of the Legion of Honour (1897).—Ed. partly in Ireland, partly in Paris; for some time on the French press, and prof. in one of the Colls. of the Univ. of France; in Melbourne, sec. in roy. coman. on federation of Australian cols. of 1870; parly. librarian of Queensland, 1874; is author of "Memories of Rome," and other literary works, and has produced an analytical and classified index of the library over which he presides; is a fellow of the incorp. soc. of authors (England), mem. of the soc. of arts (England), mem. of the librar. assn. of United Kingdom, fellow of the soc. of literature (England), officier de l'instruction publique (1896), and hon. mem. of the societe d'anthropologie of Paris; also a corrpdg. mem. (causd honoris) of the societe de geographie commerciales de Paris and Havre.

O'DWYER, ARTHUR W.—Ed. at Wesleyan H. schl., Freetown, S. Leone; apprentice outdoor offr. custs., S. Leone, 1879; res. and joined mercant. serv.; apptd. clk. to H.M. cons. for Rights of Benin and Biafra, May, 1888; served throughout blockade Opobo, 1889; paymr. yacht "Whydah," Dec., 1891; clk. gen. post off., Niger C. Protec., 1894; ch. clk. 1877; postmr. O. Calabar, 1901; ch. clk. to paymr., So. Nigeria regt., W.A.F.F., 1902.
O'DWYER, RICHARD H.—Mem. Newfoundland exec. coun., and rec.-gen., 1889-93; comanr. of poor, 1893; comanr. of pub. charities, 1898.
O'HALLORAN, GEORGE FINLEY.—B.A., B.C.L.; graduate in arts of McGill Univ., 1883; grad. in law, 1885; called to the bar of prov. of Quebec, 1885; dep. min. of agric. of Canada, 1892.

O'HALLORAN, JOS. SYLVESTER, C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1842; clk. in audit office, S. Australia, 1859; clk. of exec. coun., and clk. to the ct. of appeals, 1869; acted also as priv. sec. to the Rt. Hon. Sir Jas. Fergusson, bart., gov. of S. Australia, from Feb. to May, 1870; asst. sec. roy. col. int., Apr., 1881; sec., Jan., 1884.
OKEDEN, W. E. P.—Formerly imigr. gen., Brisbane; under-col. sec., Queensland, 1890.
OKES, H.—C.C. and res. mag., Victoria K. div., Cape Col., Apr., 1880.

OLIVIER, SYDNEY, C.M.G. (1898), B.A.—B. 1859; ed. at Tonbridge schl.; open exhibt., Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; 2nd class classical mods., 1878; 2nd class in final classical schl., 1881; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in the office of the S. of S. for cols., 17th Apr., 1882; honourably mentioned for Cobden prize essay, Oxford, 1883; ag. col. sec., Br. Honduras, Oct., 1890, to Apr., 1891; ag. auditor-gen., Leeward Is., Oct., 1896, to Feb., 1896; priv. sec. to the Earl of Selborne, Sept., 1896; sec. to the roy. commr. apptd. to inquire into condition of the W. Indies, Dec., 1896; 1st class clk., June, 1897; sent to Washington on special service in connection with W. Indian reciprocity negotiations, 1898; col. sec., Jamaica, 1900-1904; ag. Gov., 1900, 1902, and 1904; returned to C.O. as prin. clk., Oct., 1904.
OLLIFFE, CHAS. THOMAS.—Asst. treas., Oil Rivers Prot. (now S. Nigeria), Nov., 1892; ag. treas., Apl. to Sept., 1900; transport offr., 1904.

O'LOGHLEN, THE HON. SIR BRYAN, BART.—B. 1828; atty.-gen., treas. and premier of Victoria, Australia, July, 1881; ret., 1883; M.L.A. for Port Fanny, 1889; mem. of Patterson ministry, 1893-4, as atty.-gen.

O'MALLEY, SIR EDWARD LOUGHLIN, KT. BACH. (1891).—B. 1842; ed. Trin. Coll., Camb., grad. B.A., 1864; M.A., 1868; called to the bar (Mid. Temp.), 1866; joined Norfolk circuit; atty.-gen. of Jamaica, 1876; atty.-gen., Hong Kong, Nov., 1879; ch. just., S. Stlthms., 1889; ret., 1892; ch. just., Br. Guiana, 1895; judge cons. ct., Constantinople, 1897.

OMMANNAYE, SIR MONTAGU FREDERICK, G.C.M.G. (1904), K.C.B. (1901), K.C.M.G. (1890), C.M.G. (1882), I.S.O. (1903), late capt. R.E.—B. 1842; ed. at Chelt. Coll., and R.M.A., Woolwich; entd. the roy. engnr., Jan., 1864; employed under the W. O. and admity., and at R.M.A., from 1867 to 1874; priv. sec. to Earl of Carnarvon, S. of S. for cols., Mar., 1874, to Jan., 1877, when he was apptd. a crown agt. for the cols.; a commr. for the col. exhibn., 1887; mem. roy. commr. for Paris exhibn. of 1900; permanent under S. of S. for the colonies, June, 1900; sec. of the Order of St. Michael and St. George, July, 1900.

ONGLEY, FREDERICK OLIVER JONES.—Cadet, R.N., 1875; clk. Cyprus high ct. just. and commr.'s office, 1879; ch. sec.'s, 1884; aud.'s, 1886; rec-gen.'s, 1886; passed higher standard Turkish, 1886; transl. to high commr., 1889; acct. imp. bank of Persia, 1890; ch. clk. custs., 1890; transld. Ott. land code, 1892; asst. to dir. of surv. and prin. for offr., 1892 (now styled asst. reg.-genl. and inspr.); ag. commr., Kyrenia, 1893; again, and loc. commt. and cornr., 1898; ag. asst. to loc. commt., Nicosia, 1897; ag. reg.-genl., 1899.

ONGLEY, PERCY ALGERNON.—Ed. Roy. Nav. schl., New Cross, and Gothio House Coll., Clapham-rise; sub-lieut. and lieut., 5th Royal Lancashire Militia, 1877 to 1882; clk., commr.'s off., Nicosia, Cyprus, 1879 to 1882; ag. sec. to locust destructn. commr.; coll., cust. and ex., Limassol, 1882 to 1893; ag. local commt. of police, gov. of prison, and asst. to commr. of Limassol, Nov., 1886, to Feb., 1887; again, Mar. to Apr., 1887; ag. dist. treas. at Lim., for three months; ag. local commt. of pol., etc., Famagusta, Apr. to Oct., 1887; ag. commr. of Famagusta, July to Aug., 1887; on spec. serv., Papho, 1887 to 1888; passed exam. and commended for colloquial knowledge of mod. Greek; ag. local commt. of pol., Famagusta, June to Dec., 1893; on spec. pol. serv., Papho, Jan. to May, 1894; confirmed loc. commt. of pol., Fam., May, 1894; ag. commr., Fam., 1895, 1896, and 1897; dep. coroner, 1897; ag. commr., Larnaca, May to Oct., 1898; apptd. to command of spec. detach. of Cyprus mil. pol. sent to Crete (the Candia province), Nov., 1898, to Feb., 1899; ag. commr. of Kyrenia prov., July, 1899, to May, 1900; ditto, Papho, June, 1900, to May, 1901; ditto, Fam., May to Nov., 1902; ditto, Kyrenia, Apl. to Nov., 1903; thanked by the govt. for services during relief works, Fam., 1897; read thanks of S. of S. for services, Papho, 1887-1888; thanked and commd. by Sir H. Chermside for spec. serv. in Crete, 1898-1899; ch. of pol. and excise, Grenada, Dec., 1903; M.L.C., 27th Jan., 1904; ag. col. treas. and comptroller, cust., Feb. to Mar., 1904; in addition to substantive duties.

ONSLOW, EARL OF, P.C., G.C.M.G. (1889), K.C.M.G. (1887), WILLIAM HILLIER ONSLOW, VISCOUNT CRANLEY, 4TH EARL (U.K., created 1801).—B. 1863; ed. at Eton, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; J.P. and dep.-lieut. of Surrey, and high steward of Guildford; parly. under-S. of S. for the cols., Feb., 1887; parly. sec., bd. of trade, Feb., 1888; vice-pres. of the col. confce., 1887; a deleg. to sugar bounties confce., 1887; gov. N. Zealand, 1889; resig., 1892; under-S. of S. for Ind., 1895 to 1900; again parly. under-S. of S., C.O., Nov., 1900; pres. bd. of agric., 20th May, 1903.

ONSLOW, SIR ALEXANDER CAMPBELL, KT. BACH. (1896).—B. 1842; ed. Westminster, and Trin. Coll., Camb., B.A. in 1864; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1868, home circuit and Surrey sess.; atty.-gen. Br. Honduras in 1878; ditto, W. Australia in 1880; ch. just., July, 1883; adminstr. the govt., 1885; and again in 1891, 1896, and 1900-1; ret., 1901.

ORD, DUNCAN BERESFORD.—Clk. crown lands office, W. Australia, 1882; clk. treasy., 1882; clk. to mag., postmr., etc., Derby, 1883; 2nd clk., pol. dept., 1884; clk., govr.'s office, 1887 and 1889; priv. sec. to Sir W. C. F. Robinson, 1890 to 1895; priv. sec. to Sir G. Smith, K.C.M.G., 1897; ch. clk., col. sec.'s dept., 1901; ag. under-sec., 1904.
ORME, G. N.—Ed. Cheltenham Coll., and Hertford Coll., Oxford; cadet, Hong Kong, 1901; ag. ass't sec., sanitary bd., 1904.

ORMSBY, A. V. BEDE.—Ed. Christ's Hosp. and King's Coll., London; clk., dist. commr., Belize, Br. Hond., Dec., 1898; sec., electric lighting comtee., 1901; sec., land tax commr., 1901; clk., reg.-gen., Belize, Nov., 1902; commr., sup. ct., B. Hond., 1902; ag. dist. commr., Stann Creek, B. Hond., Mar. 1903.

ORRORKE, THE HON. SIR GEORGE MAURICE, KT. BACH. (1880).—B. 1830; ed. at Trin. Coll., Dub., of which he is a graduate; emigrated to Victoria in 1852, and settled in N. Zealand in 1854; elected to House of Representatives in 1861 for the town of Onehunga, which he represented until it was merged in the elective dist. of Manukau, for which he has sat since 1882; elected speaker of the House of Representatives, July, 1879; and a dissolution taking place in Aug., was re-elected speaker in Sept. following; on both occasions was elected unanimously; previous to this he had been thrice elected chmn. of comtees. of the House of Reps., viz., in 1871, 1875, and 1876; had been also speaker of the Auckland prov. coun. for 12 years, from 1865 to the abolition of the province of Auckland in 1876; entered the Waterhouse ministry in 1872 as sec. for crown lands, and min. for immigr. On Mr. Waterhouse resigning the premiership in 1873, he continued in the ministry of Sir Julius Vogel until 1874, when he resig.; is a mem. of the N. Z. bar, and in 1879-80 was chmn. of the roy. coman, to inquire into the operations of the Univ. of N. Z., and the secondary schls. of the col. in their relation to the Univ.; was for the 3rd and 4th time unanimously elected speaker of the House of Representatives, May, 1882, and Aug., 1884, respectively, and again in 1887; was elected as first chmn. of the coun. of the Auckland Univ. Coll., founded in 1883; also chmn. of the bd. of govrs. of the Auckland gram. schl.

ORPEN, C. E. H.—Temporary clk. to C.C., Griquatown div., Cape Col., 1872; clk., P.O., Barkly; clk. to survr.-gen., 1873; Griqualand W., 1874; sec. to coman. on Griqualand claims, 1875; clk. to C.C., Barkly, 1876; re-appointed to survr.-gen.'s office, Sept., 1876; sec. to special comanr. on native claims, 1877; served as volr. in Griqualand Field Force, 1878; S. Afr. war medal; clk., staff paymstr.'s off., Mar., 1879; inspr. of native locations, div. of Hay, 1882; inspr. of natives, Herbert div., 1883.

ORPEN, HENRY MARTYN HERBERT, C.M.G. (1898).—B. 1831; capt. comdg. Colesberg native levies, from Jan., 1851, to Aug., 1853, during the Kaffir war, also as dist. adjt. of N. Victoria from Apr., 1852, to Mar., 1863; held several other mil. apptmts. during same period; Kaffir war medal; apptd. to customs dept., Port Elizabeth, Dec., 1853; landing survr., Aug., 1857; sub-collr. and survr. and comptroller of H.M. customs and navigation laws, Capetown, Oct., 1867; temporary charge of customs dept., Apr., 1868; asst. treas., acctnt.-gen., and stamping comanr., May, 1876; recr.-gen. and paymr.-gen., 1881; ret., 1902.
ORPEN, RICH. THEODORIE, B.L.—Pol. mag., So. Nigeria, Aug., 1903; ag. atty.-gen. and regist.-gen., Aug., 1903, to Feb., 1904.

OSBORN, JOHN FREDERICK.—Asst. engrnr. on construction, Manchester Ship Canal, Nov., 1887, to Dec., 1891; attached to P.W.D., Jamaica, July, 1892, to Nov., 1896, on surveys for and construction of bridges; agr. irrigation engrnr., Rio Cobre canals; col. survr., and inspr. of P. W., Turks and Caicos Islds., Nov., 1896; J.P. 1901; ag. asst. comanr., Salt Cay, Apl., 1902, to July, 1903, and from May, 1904.

OSBORNE, ALGERNON WILLOUGHBY.—Ed. at Winchester and Hertford Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1886; M.A., 1892; admitted a solr. of sup. ct., England, Nov., 1892; enrolled as barr. and solr. of sup. ct., G. Coast, May, 1896; unofficial M.L.C., G. Coast, July, 1901; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, June, 1904; atty.-gen., G. Coast, Jan., 1903.
OSLER, FEATHERSTON.—Puisne judge, Ontario, 1879; promoted to court of appeal, 1884.
OUIMET, G., D.C.L., K.C.—Premier of Quebec, 1873; supt. of pub. instruction, 1876.
OUIMET, THE HON. JOSEPH ALDRIC, K.C.—Speaker of the House of Commons of Canada, 1890-91; min. of pub. wks., 1892-6; judge, ct. of appeal, Montreal, May, 1896.
PACKER, HARRY E.—Ent. govt. ser., 1877; sec. to pub. wks., Tasmania, 1897.
PADDLE, JAMES ISAAC.—F.R.C.S., Eng.; M.D., Lond.; asst. govt. med. offr. and comanr. in lunacy, Mauritius, 8th Sept., 1891; med. supt., lunatic asylum, 15th June, 1898.

PAGDEN, A. S.—Ed. Wellington Coll. and King's Coll., Camb., scholar of King's Coll., 1st class, classical tripus, 1881; writer, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1881; pol. mag., Panadure, June, 1887; office ast. to the govt. agt., Colombo, Dec., 1890; dist. judge, Badulla, 1896; ag. dist. judge, Kurunegala, Mar., 1896; ag. comanr. of requests, Colombo, June, 1896; pol. mag., Colombo, June, 1900; chmn., municipal coun., and mayor of Colombo, June, 1901.

PAGE, GEORGE WILLIAM.—Asst. dist., comanr. S. Leone Prot.; copyist, science and art dept., Apr., 1881, to Dec., 1884; 2nd cl. clk. prsn. dept., Jan., 1885, to Dec., 1896; keeper of gaol, Free-town, S. Leone, Jan., 1897, to Apr., 1901; asst. dist. comanr., S. Leone Prot., May, 1901; acted as curator of intestate estates, Nov., 1900, to Apr., 1901; is J.P. for the colony.

PAINE, JOHN JAMES.—Served with 19th (Princess of Wales's Own) Hussars in Egypt, and gained medal and 3 clasps and Khedive's star for Suakin and Nile expeditions, 1884 and 1885; apptd. coal porter, C.O., 1st Jan., 1893; placed on estab. as office porter, 15th Apr., 1904.

PAIRAUDEAU, G. H.—Entd. P.W.D., Br. Guiana, as jun. clk., Nov., 1879; served in several capacities as clk., record clk., Mar., 1886; 3rd cl. offr., 1892; 2nd cl., Apr., 1902; sec. to the coman. on values of govt. properties, etc., 1894.

PALMER, ALFRED FELIX.—Auditor's clk., Grenada, Sept., 1879; rev. offr., St. Lucia, 1882; ag. ch. rev. offr., 1882-83; ag. ch. clk., treasy., 1883 and 1884; sub-collr. customs and inland rev., and clk., 2nd dist. ct., Oct., 1884; acted twice as mag., and as coroner, dep. coroner, and J.P., ag. ch. clk., govt. office, 1883.

PALMER, CHAS. STUART RUSSELL, M.I.C.E.—Ed. at Roorkee Civil Eng. Coll., India; ent. P.W.D., India, Oct., 1878, as asst. engrnr.: spec. duty in England, 1886 to 1887; asst. to ch. engrnr. and asst. sec. to ch. comanr., cent. provs., up to 1889, when apptd. exec. engrnr. of Hoochangabad div., and in 1892 of Nagpur div.; ret. from Indian serv., May, 1894; asst. survr., W. Aust., Oct., 1894; engrnr. in charge, roads and bridges, and harbours and rivers branches, Aug., 1897, of water supply, 1898, and sewerage, 1900; engrnr.-in-chief, June, 1902; inspct. engrnr. for W. A. in Lond., 1904.

PANTON, JOS. ANDERSON, C.M.G. (1866).—B. 1831; comanr. of crown lands and goldfields, Victoria, 1862; res. comanr., Bendigo and Sandhurst goldfields, 1864; pol. mag. in various parts of the col. from 1862; first metropolitan pol. mag., Melbourne, coroner, guardian of minors, etc., 1874; also pres. of mil. ct. under Defence Act.

PANZERA, LT.-COL. FRANCIS WM.—Govt. engrnr. offr., 21st Jan., 1893; served for some years with R.A. and artillery militia, ordnance dept., etc., inspr., science and arts dept.; apptd. as a capt., reserve of officers, as acting engrnr. to staff of R.E. dist., 1888, to organise, raise and train the Harwich div. submarine minera, R.K. militia and 1st cls. army reserve; major, 1st Apr., 1890; commanded troops, Harwich and Landguard, 1890-1; nominated by D.A.G., R.E., for service under the C.O., 1892, and apptd. engrnr. offr. to govt. of Bech. and Bech. Prot., Jan., 1893, and to the Bech. border pol.; mem. of Bech. Prot. concessions ct., 1893; mag., Macloutsie and dist., 1893; commanded Impl. base, Mataebek border, and L. of C., Mataebale war, 1893-4 (medal and promoted major, reserve of offs.); organised and in charge of pub. wks. dept., 10th Mar., 1893; sec., Khama So. boundy. comanr. in 1894; g.v.t. engrnr. and Impl. representative under rlyw. agreement (Bech. rlyws.) of Aug., 1894; expert to treasy. for Jameson trial, 1896, for preparation of maps, etc.; mag. for Bech. Prot.; 2nd in command, Prot. div., B.S.A. pol.; British mem. and pres. of British-Transvaal joint boundy. comanr., 1897; ag. asst. comanr., Palapye, June to Aug., 1897; spec. comanr., Ngamiland, to delimit and demarcate boundaries of the country, etc., Feb., 1898, to Mar., 1899; served in S. African war, 1899-1902; commanded artillery; brigade major; D.A.A.G., etc.; defence of Mafeking; afterwards D.A.A.G. on staff of an A.I.G.; Imp. mil. mem., war losses compensation, coman.; commdt., N. Bech. Prot. (ment. in desps., Queen's medal with 2 clasps, King's medal, 2 bars); promt. lieut.-col., reserve of offrs.; astt. comsnr., Bech. Prot., 1st Aug., 1901; spec. comsnr. for administg. oaths of allegiance, 1901-2; ag. res. comsnr., Bech. Prot., 16th May, 1904.
PARDOE, AVERN.—Ed. Stratford-upon-Avon Collegiate Gram. Schl.; on staff of "Toronto Globe," 1875-89; managing editor, 1883-9; librarian of Ontario legislature, 1888.

PARKER, FREDERICK HARDYMAN, B.L., Edin. Univ., M.A., Edin., 1877, F.R.G.S.—Scholar in property law, Mid. Tem., 1879; called to the bar, Trin., 1880; employed in Canada; admitted to the bar, Br. Honduras, 1881; dep. atty.-gen., Corosol circuit, 1882; notary and standing conveyancing counsel, 1883; regiar. of the sup. ct., keeper of the records, and provost-marshal, Sept., 1884; also registr., Admty., lands, titles, a J.P., etc.; acted as dist. mag. and coroner, Belize, and as atty.-gen., 1883-7; ag. ch. just., 1886-7; pres., dist. ct., Papho, Cyprus, 1890; ag. pres., Limassol, 1892, and Laracca, 1893-5; pres., Famagusta, 1892; passed in modern Greek, 1894; pres., Limassol, 1897; pres., Nicosia, 1902; ag. pres., Kyrenia; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., Cyprus, 1903 and 1904.

PARKER, HENRY, A.M.I.C.E., F.Z.S.—Entd. Ceylon civ. serv., May, 1873; irrigtn. offr., July, 1873; irrigtn. astt., 1st Jan., 1883; sent by S. of S. to report on irrigtn., Gambia, 4th Dec., 1902; resumed duties, 8th Oct., 1903.

PARKER, S. H.—Called to the bar, 1868; Q.C., 1890; mem. of legislature of W. Australia from 1878 to 1897; col. sec., 1892 to 1894; represented W. A. at the C. O. and gave evidence before sel. comtee. of H. of C. relative to the grant of self-govt. to the Colony, 1890; represented W. A. in London relative to the federation of Australia, 1900; justice of supreme ct., 1901.

PARMELEE, WILLIAM GRANNIS, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1833; astt. comsnr. of customs, Canada, Jan., 1885; comsnr., Mar., 1892; ch. controller of Chinese immigrn. since 1885; dep. min. of trade and commerce of Canada, 1893.

PARODI, ERNEST VICTOR.—Ed. Stonyhurst Coll., Lancs.; called to the bar, In. Temp., June, 1893; solr.-gen., N. Nigeria, 4th July, 1903; ag. atty.-gen. and comsnr. of stamps, 2nd Aug. to 22nd Dec., 1903.

PARR, LT.-COL. HENRY HALLAM, C.M.G. (1880).—B. 1847; mil. sec. to Sir B. Frere at the Cape; served in the Egyptian expdn., 1882; dep. asst. adjt. and qtrmr.-gen., 1882; A.D.C. to Her late Majesty Queen Victoria.

PARRY, JAMES ROWAN.—Capt., 90th Rifles, Canada; lieut., S. Nigeria regt., W.A.F.F., Dec., 1904; served with Aro expdn., 1901-2 (medal with clasp); inspr. of pol., Nov., 1902; ag. gov. of gaols, Jan. to Aug., 1903.
PARRY-OKEDEN, W. E., I.S.O. (1903).—Inspr. of border patrol, Queensland, 1870; pol. mag., 1872; immigr. agt., Brisbane, 1886.

PARSONS, HAROLD GEORGE.—Ed. Melbourne and Oxford, senior scholar, Wadham Coll., Oxford, 1887; 2nd class mods, 2nd cls. greats., 1890; under W. E. Henley, "National Observer," 1891-2; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1893; M.L.C., W. Aust., 1897; lieut., 53rd (East Kent) I.Y., S. Africa, 1901-2 (wounded, medal, 5 clasps); dist. comsnr. Lagos, 1903; ag. pol. mag., 1903; manager, 1st agric. show, Lagos, 1903.

PARRIDGE, CHARLES, M.A. (1900), F.S.A. (1904), F.R.G.S. (1903), Fell. of Anthorp. Inst. of Great Britain and Ireland.—Ed. Christ's Coll., Camb., and in France; 1890-92, read law in solr.'s chmbrs.; 1892-3 at Camb.; 1897-1900, priv. tut. to inter alios, Sir S. Shuckburgh, Bart.; joined 2nd V.B. Suffolk R., Jan., 1900; founder and edtr. of "East Anglian Miscellany," Mar.-June, 1901; elected mem. of coun. of Suffolk inst. of archaeology, Apr., 1901; apptd. astt. dist. comsnr. in Southern Nigeria, June, 1901; in treasy. at Calabar, July, 1901; in charge of Idah sub-distr., Aug., 1901, to May, 1902; Calabar, June-Sept., 1902; elected fell. of anthorp. inst., 1903; ag. dist. comsnr., Obubura Hill, from May, 1903, to Aug., 1904; has done lit. work of various kinds, including complete verbal cross-index (40,000 slips) to Yule's "Gloss. of Ang.-Indian Words"; article on "Igaraland" in "Blackwood's Mag.," 1904; "Cross River Natives," Jan., 1905.
PATTERSON, THE HON. JOHN COLEBROOKE.—Sec. of state for Canada, 1892; min. of militia and defence, Dec., 1892; lieut.-gov., Manitoba, 1895.
PATTERSON, L. J.—Ch. clk. to audr.-gen., Barbadoes, 1868; has acted on several occasions as auditor; govt. audr., Barbados rly., May, 1883.
PAUL, G. W.—Dist. ct. judge, Queensland, Aug., 1874.

PEACE, SIR WALTER, K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1893), F.S.S.—B. 1840; emigrn. agt. for Natal, Jan., 1881; London agt. for Natal harbour bd., Sept., 1881; author of "Our Colony of Natal"; chevalier de l'ordre de Léopold, for services as Belgian consul in Natal, 1870-9; agt.-gen. for Natal, 1893-1904.

PEACOCK, WALTER.—B.A., Trinity Hall, Camb.; cadet, S. Sttlnts., Nov., 1895; passed fin. exam. Chinese, June, 1901; ag. 2nd astt. protect. Chinese, Penang, July, 1901; ag. dist. offr., Christmas Is., Dec., 1902; ag. 3rd mgr., Singapore, June, 1903; apptd. to cls. V., Sep., 1903.

PEARCE, MAJOR FRANCIS BARROW, C.M.G. (1904).—Entd. army, 25th Aug., 1886; capt., 1893; major (brevet), 1900; astt. dep. comsnr. and vice-consul, B. Cent. Africa Prot., 1st Nov., 1897; dep. comsnr., 1st Oct., 1901; ag. comsnr., 1903.

PEARCE, FRANK LEOPOLD.—Sorting clk., col. sec.'s off., Jamaica, 19th Aug., 1884; seconded as astt. sec. to Jamaica exhibtn., Mar., 1890; sec. to winding-up comtee., May, 1891; 3rd cls. clk., Apr., 1890; 2nd cls. clk., Sep., 1896; prot. of immigrts., 1st July, 1901.
PEARCE, G. H., I.S.O. (1903).—Clk. in penitentiary, Jamaica, 1860; in priv. sec.'s office, 1864; in P.O., 1865; ch. clk. P.O., 1875; ag. postmr., 1874 to 1875, and 1882; now postmr.

PEARCE, THOS. WM.—Missionary of Lond. Miss. Soc., Canton, 1879-1890; Hong Kong, 1890-1902; ag. inspr. of schools, 5th Sept. to 9th Oct., 1898, again in 1900; examiner, Queen's Coll., 1897 and 1898; mem. of comte., on interpretation, 1899-1900; apptd. mem. of bd. of examiners in Chinese, 14th Jan., 1897.
PEARCE, WM. PHILIP.—Ck. to postmr., St. Kitts, 1883; ck., P.O., 1887; ag. postmr., 1889; postmr., 1892.

PEARSE, WILFRED WILLIAM.—M.B., C.M., Aberdeen, 1893; D.P.H., Aberdeen, 1894; 2nd ast. med. offr., Shoreditch Infirmary, 1894-95; ast. med. offr. of health, Hong Kong, 1901; J.P., 1902; ag. med. offr. of health, 1903.

PEARSON, ARTHUR A., C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1847; ed. at Rugby; apptd., 1867, after a compet. exam., ck. in the office of the S. of S. for ocls.; 2nd-class ck., Sept., 1872; ast. priv. sec. to the Earl of Carnarvon, Mar., 1874, to Feb., 1878; ast. priv. sec. to Sir M. Hicks-Beach, Feb. to Mar., 1878; priv. sec. to Earl Cadogan, July, 1879, to Apr., 1880; priv. sec. to the Right Hon. M. E. Grant Duff, Apr., 1880, to Dec., 1880; 1st-class ck., Jan., 1881; principal ck., Apr., 1895; repres. crown ocls. at postal confce., London, June and July, 1898; one of the Brit. delegates at the Brussels sugar conference, 1901-2; ast. delegate, permanent sugar commission at Brussels, 1903.
PEARSON, MAJOR GEN. SIR CHARLES K., C.B., K.C.M.G. (1879).—For distinguished service in the Zulu war; B. 1834.

PEARSON, CHARLES W., I.S.O. (1903).—Sub-collr. of customs at Port Alfred, Cape, Apl., 1869; ch. ck. in gen. management dept. of customs, Cape-town, Sept., 1880; sub-collr. and survr., controller of cust. and navigation laws, and regsr. of shipping.

PEARSON, TURNER.—Ck. to govt. reformatory, Stony Hill, Jamaica, 1869; transf. to Queen's Coll., Spanish Town, Aug., 1873; ch. ck. and purveyor, pub. hosp.; and ck. to the Lock hosp., Apl., 1875; ch. ck., island med. dept.; sec. cent. bd. of hth., and to quarantine bd., 1897.

PEEL, RIGHT HON. SIR FREDERICK, P.C. (1857), K.C.M.G. (1869) (2nd son of Sir Robert Peel, 2nd Bart.).—B. 1823; ed. Harrow, and at Trin. Coll., Camb., 1st class in classics, 1845; called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1849; under-sec. for the cols., Nov., 1851, to Mar., 1852 and Dec., 1852, to Feb., 1855; under-sec. for war, 1855 to 1857; is a dep. lieut. of Warwickshire; was M.P. for Leominster, Feb., 1849, to July, 1852, and for Bury, Lancashire, July, 1852, to Apl., 1857; re-elected for Bury, May, 1859, and apptd. sec. to the treasy., 1860; rly. comsnr., 1873.

PEEL, W.—B.A., Queen's Coll., Cambridge, 11th wrangler, math. tripos., 1896; cadet, S. Stltmts., Oct., 1897; ag. dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Aug.-Dec., 1898; and from Mar., 1899; passed fin. exam. in Malay, Dec., 1899; dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Dec., 1901; ag. 2nd ast. col. sec., May, 1902; ag. ast. col. sec. and ck. of couns., Nov., 1902, to Jan., 1903; and in Aug., 1903; ag. ast. supt., indian immigrts., Sing., in addition, Aug., 1903; ag. 2nd ast. col. sec., Sept., 1903.

PEIRCE, T. ESTWICK.—Ed. Harrison's Coll., Barbados; ck. to col. sec., Barbados, 1880; 5th ck., col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1881; 4th ck., audr.-gen.'s office, Mar., 1881; excise offr., St. Lucia, 1882; special ck., audr.-gen.'s office, Barbados, 1884; offr. of customs, 1887; supervisor of customs, G. Coast, Feb., 1892; ag. travelling and inspecting supervisor, May to Aug., 1892; cashier, treas., 1893; ast. treas., Feb., 1894; ast. comptroller of customs, G. Coast, Nov., 1896; ag. treas., and collr. of customs, Gambia, July, 1896; collr. of customs, Gambia, Sept., 1896; mem. exec. and legis. coun., comsnr. of requests; J.P.

PELLETIER, HON. SIR CHARLES ALPHONSE PANTALEON, K.C.M.G. (1898), C.M.G. (1878).—B. 1837; pres. of the exec. comte. for the Dominion of Canada at the Paris exhibn., and late min. of agricul. and statistics, and comsnr. of patents for the Dominion; mem. of priv. coun. for Canada.

PELLETIER, PHILLIPE.—Called to the bar, Quebec, 1875; mem. of the coun. of the bar of Montreal, and sec. 1878; a fire comsnr. for city of Montreal, 1879 to 1887; unsuccessfully contested St. John's in provincial elections, 1886, and Beauharnois in Dominion elections, 1887; ch. ck. to sec. of state, Canada, 1888.

PEMBERTON, SHOLTO RAWLINS.—Ed. Downing Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1882; called to the bar (Inner Tem.), 1883; admitted to the bar, Leewards Is., 1883; elected mem. legis. assem., Dominica, and of gen. legis. coun., Leewards, 1885 to 1895; mem. educn. coman., 1886; of road bd., Dominica, 1889; nominated mem. legis. assem., Dominica, and mem. exec. coun., 1885; comsnr. of valuation, 1896; comsnr. of assessed taxes, 1896; J.P., 1896; chmn. bd. of guardians, 1897; vis. just. of gaol, 1897; mem. quarantine bd., 1898; nom. mem. Dominican legis. coun. and of gen. legis. coun., Leewards, 1898; ag. 1st puisne judge, Leewards, Aug. to Nov., 1898; ag. astt.-gen., 1899-1900; ag. 2nd puisne judge, 1899.
PENDLETON, A. G.—Gen. traffic man. S. Austl.y. dept. Nov., 1876; comanr. of rly., 1886.
PENNEFATHER, LT.-COL. E.G.—Inspr.-gen. of pol., S. Stltmts., Apl., 1896.

PENNEFATHER, FREDERICK WILLIAM.—Ed. Trin. Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1874; LL.D., 1891; called to bar, Linc.'s Inn, 1877; priv. sec. to gov. of S. Australia, 1881-3; priv. sec. to gov., N. Zealand, 1883-85; mem. of N.Z. coman., Ind. and Col. Exhibn., 1886; lecturer at Adelaide Univ., 1888-9; prof. of laws, 1889-96; sec. to Lambeth confce., 1897; puisne judge, N.Z., 1898.

PENNEFATHER, JOHN PYNE.—Apprentice, survey dept., Singapore, 1st Jan., 1875; 2nd ck. survr., 1st Dec., 1878; overseer and survr., 1st Dec., 1881; dist. survr., Oct., 1882; transf. to Penang, 13th Jan., 1883; ch. survr., Singapore, 11th July, 1888; sen. survr., 1st Jan., 1891; ditto, Malacca, 25th Oct., 1893.
PENNEY, ALFRED.—M.H.A., Carbonear, Newfild., 1885-9; mem. exec. coun., and survr.-gen., 1885-9; judge dist. ct. Harbour Grace, 1898.

PENNEY, F. GORDON.—Ed. Trin. Coll., Glenalmond, and Edin. Univ., M.A., 1876; cadet, S.S., Sept., 1876; mag. for Singapore and a J.P. for the col., Sept., 1878; ag. coroner, Dec., 1878; ag. collr. land rev., Singapore, Jan., 1879; collr. land rev., Penang, 1880; ag. 2nd mag. Penang, 1882; ag. supt. educn., Penang, 1882; 2nd mag. and comsnr., ct. of requeste, Singapore, May, 1883; ag. sen. mag., 1884-5; ag. 2nd ast. col. sec., 1886 and 1888; is a licensing just.; 2nd ast. col. sec., 1888; mag., Malacca, Jan., 1890; ag. ast. col. sec. and clk. of coun., Apl., 1890; sen dist. offr., P.W., June, 1890; 1st mag. Singapore, Apl., 1897; ag. col. treas., S. Stltmts., May, 1897; col. treas., Aug., 1898; res. coun., Malacca, July, 1904, but cont. to act as treas.; ag. col. sec., Sep., 1904.

PENNEY, WILLIAM HUGHES.—Ed. Hymer's Coll., Yorkshire; apptd. to treas. dept., B. N. Borneo, July, 1888; ag. treas.-genl., 1891; P.M.G., 1895; dis. mag., 1896; ag. res., Prov. Alcock, 1898; prot. of Chinese and mem. of coun., 1901; supt. of customs, 1902; resident and dep. gov., Labuan, Jan., 1903.
PENROSE, FRANK PHILIP.—Ag. supt. of wks. and surveys, Malacca, S. Sttlmte., 2nd Aug., 1894; ast. supt. of wks., Prov. Wellesley, 18th Oct., 1897; supt. of wks. and surveys, Malacca, 1st Oct., 1903.

PERCEVAL, SIR WESTBY BROOK, K.C.M.G. (1894).—B. 1854; was mem. for Christchurch City in legis. assem., of N. Zealand; chmn. of comtees. in 1890; agt.-gen. for the col. in England, 1891-96; called to bar, Mid. Tem., 1878; roy. comsnr. for Chicago Exhibn., 1893; agt.-gen. for Tasmania, 1896 to 1899.

PEREGRINE, LAWSON NIVEN.—Called to the bar, Mid. Temple, July, 1886; dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, Oct., 1888; acted sheriff, 1890 to 1891; apptd. member and acted as hon. sec. census committee, 1891; crown prosecutor, western prov., from 1894; pres. Accra town coun., 1898; comsnr. of stamps, western prov., from 1898; chief civil officer, base of operations, Ashanti Field Force, 1900 (medal); apptd. ag. puisne judge, 1902; comsnr. of cent. prov. comprising districts of Elmina, Cape Coast, Saltpond and Winneba, 1901.

PERKINS, ARTHUR JAMES.—Diplôme de l'école Nationale d'Agriculture de Montpellier, 1890; management of farm estates, Tunis, 1890-2; prof. of viticulture and oenology, S. Aust., 1892; sec. for agriculture, 1902-1904; prin. of agric. coll., and prof. of viticulture and oenology, 1904.

PERKINS, H. INNES, I.S.O. (1904), F.R.G.S.—Ck. to dir. of surveys, Trinidad, 1890-81; 3rd ast. crown survr., Br. Guiana, Dec., 1881; 2nd ditto, Sept., 1884; accompanied E. F. Im Thurn on an expdn. to ascend Mount Roraima in 1884-5, and furnished from actual survey a map of the mountain and the country in its vicinity to the Roy. Geog. Soc.; isa J.F.; author of papers on natural history, geography, and mining industries of Br. Guiana; ast. crown survr., July, 1889; ag. crown survr., 1890, 1892, and 1893; apptd. a comsnr. on scheme for settlement of Indian immigrants, April, 1895, and mem. of comtee. to settle immigrants; ag. comsnr. of mines, July, 1895, and ag. crown surv. in addn., Sept. to Dec., 1896; joint author with Prof. Harrison, in reports of geology of Barima, Barama Rivers, 1897, Essequebo, Potaro, and Demerara Rivers, 1898, and Mazaruni River, 1899; F.G.S., 1898; comsnr. for delimitn. of Br. Guiana boundary, Nov., 1900; is mem. of institution of mining and metallurgy, London.

PERKINS, THOS. LUFT.—Divsnl. survr. and engr. for county of Middlesex; dist. engr. for city dist. of Bristol; ditto for Clifton dist., Bristol; exec. engr. under pub. health and buildings ordce., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1904.
PERRIN, GEO. SAMUEL, F.L.S., F.R.S. (Tasm.).—Forester, woods and forests dept., S. Australia, 1880; chief forester, Wirraba, 1885; conservator of forests, Tasmania, 1886; ditto, Victoria, June, 1888.

PERRY, SIR ALLAN, KT. BACH. (1904); M.D. (Durham); D.P.H.(Lon.); M.R.C.S. Eng.; L.S.A. Lond.—Prin. civ. med. offr. and inspr. gen. of hosps., Ceylon; prin. of Ceylon med. coll., 10 Dec., 1897; major R.A.M.C. (retired); mem. municipal coun., Colombo; late pres. Ceylon branch Br. med. assoc.

PERRY, GEORGE McREDDIE, M.B., C.M., Glasgow, and St. Mary's hosp., London, F.R.G.S.—Ast. col. surg., Lagos, Oct., 1893; ag. col. surg., Feb.-Mar., 1894; ag. ast. col. sec., May, 1894; med. offr. to expedn. to Idanam mountains; sen. ast. col. surg., Aug., 1895; ag. col. surg., 1895.

PEAR, SIR JOHN BUDD, KT. BACH. (creat. 1877), M.A., F.G.S.—B. 1825; ed. at Pembroke Coll., Camb., and grad. 6th wrangler in 1847; fellow and ast. tutor of Clare Coll.; called to the bar, 1854; puisne judge, high ct., Calcutta, 1864; ret., 1876; ch. just. of Ceylon, 1877; resig., 1879; J.P. and chmn. of quarter sess., Devon; unsuccessfully contested the Honiton div. of Devon in 1885, and the Tavistock div. in 1886; author of "Elementary Hydrostatics," "The Aryan Village," "International Trade," and other works.

PHILLIPS, H. DENBIGH.—Supernum.clk., col. sec.'s office, Br. Honduras, 1887; ag. clk., col. engrn.'s office, 1888; ag. rev. offr., July, 1888, to Feb., 1889; clk. to att.-gen., May, 1889; ag. clk. to regiar. of sup. ct. and keeper of records, 1889, 1890, 1891, 1892, 1893 and 1894; clk. to regiar., sup. ct., 1894; comsnr. of sup. ct. and a comsnr. of deeds, Aug., 1894; ag. regiar.-gen., Feb. to Sept., 1898; ag. dist. comsnr., Stann Creek, Apl. to Oct., 1899; ditto, Toledo, Mar. to Dec., 1900; dist. comsnr., Toledo, 1st Jan., 1901.

PHILLIPS, HAROLD ERNEST.—Ed. in London; temporary clk. to auditor, Br. Hondura, Oct., 1892; 5th clk., treasy., customs, and P.O. dept., June, 1893; 4th clk., Feb., 1894; 3rd clk., treasy. and customs dept., May, 1897; 2nd clk., June, 1898; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, Sept., 1900; ag. ch. clk., C.S.O., and clk. of couns., Apr. to Sept., 1901; ag. ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, and clk. of couns., Mar., 1902, to July, 1904.
PHILLIPS, THOMAS B.—5th clk., treasy., customs, and P.O., Br. Hondura, Sept., 1891; 3rd clk., treasy. and customs, 1894; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, 1897.
PHILLIPS, W. L. C.—B.A., Dunhelm; dep. clk., gen. assembly, Barbados, 1879; comsnr. of probate, 1882; clk. gen. assem., 1891; col. treas., 1899.

PICKERING, WILLIAM ALEXANDER, C.M.G. (1884).—Resided in Formosa, 1863 to Dec., 1870; received the thanks of the U.S. govt. for assisting in the negotiation of a treaty with the savages at the S. of that island; also thanked by the Br. min. at Pekin for securing Spanish subjects from slavery; Chinese interp., S. Sttlmte., Dec., 1871; during 1874 was engaged in the native states, and twice received the thanks of S. of S. for Cols. for conducting the negotiations which terminated in the pacification of Larut, and for services in Sungai Ujong; J.P. and pol. mag., Singapore, Mar., 1874; prot. of Chinese, Apr., 1877; ret., 1889.
PICKWOAD, CECIL ARTHUR.—3rd customs offr., Br. Hondura, June, 1895; 2nd customs offr., Nov., 1895; 3rd clk., treasy. and customs, and clk. to quarantine bd., May, 1897; 2nd clk., Sept., 1900.
PICKWOAD, HOWELL.—Clk. att.-gen.'s office, Br. Hondura, Nov., 1895; entd. customs dept., 12th Aug., 1898; 4th clk., treasy. and customs, 27th Dec., 1901.
PIEREZ, G., M.B., C.M.—Med. offr., Antigua, Feb., 1882.
PIERIDES, LUKE G. Z.—Clk. to comsnr., Larnaca, Cyprus, Dec., 1884; treasy. clk., Larnaca, June, 1887; official mem. of local admstrve. coun.

PIERIS, PAULUS EDWARD, Samarasinha Sriwardhana, M.A., Trin. Coll., Camb., 1896. Barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1896; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 1896; asst. to govt. agt., Colombo, 1898; pol. mag., Panadura, 1899; dist. judge, Ratnapura, 1899; asst. to govt. agt., Galle, 1901.

PIERRE, LOUIS PHILIP.—Clk. of the peace of the co. of Caroni, Trinidad, Nov., 1860; clk. of the peace of the co. of Victoria, with the town of San Fernando, in 1869; clk. of the peace of Port of Spain, and the W. dist. of the co. of St. George, in June, 1869; ag. stip. mag. of the co. of St. David, and ward of Blanchisseuse, in the co. of St. George, and warden of the Toca ward union, in June, 1872; and in Jan., 1874, stip. mag. of the co. of St. David and ward of Blanchisseuse, warden of the Toco ward union, and ag. stip. mag. of Arouca and Arima; stip. mag. of the town of Arima and the ward of Arima, 1878.

PIERS, GEORGE.—Civ. comsnr. and res. mag., Herbert, Cape Col., 1886; Bedford, 1890; ch. clk., judicial branch, att.-gen.'s office, 1894; supt., convict stn. and Porter's reformatory, Tokei, 1895; comsnr.-in-charge, Robber's Is., 1895; civ. comsnr., Cape div., July, 1904.
PIERS, W. R.—Res. mag., Murrayburg div., Cape Col., 1883; master and regis. of E. dist. ct., Feb., 1880; res. mag., Peddie, Aug., 1884; Albert, Jan., 1893; Paarl, July, 1897; Kimberley, 19-2.
PIESSE, HON. F. W.—Minister without portfolio in the Lewis ministry, Tasmania.

PIGGOTT, FRANCIS TAYLOR, M.A.—Barrister-at-law; employed by F.O. on special service in 1887; legal adv. to prim. min., Japan, Nov., 1887; sec. to att.-gen. Sir C. Russell (afterwards Lord Russell of Killowen) during Behring Sea arbitrn., 1893-4; proc. gen., Mauritius, 1894; ag. ch. just., 1895 to 1897; recd. thanks of govt. for serv. in connection with planters' loan, 1902; author of "Foreign Judgments," "Services out of the Jurisdiction," "Exterritoriality," "Law of Torts," "Imperial Statutes applicable to the Colonies," "Nationality and Naturalization, and English Law applicable to the High Seas and beyond the Realm."
PIGOT, JAMES LOUIS.—Conservator of forests, Ceylon, 22nd Jan., 1903 (services lent temporarily by govt. of India).
PILE, SIR GEO. CLARKE, KT. BACH. (1892).—B. 1821; pres. of legis. coun. and mem. of exec. coun., Barbados.

PILE, THEODORE CONRADE.—Clk. col. sec.'s office, Trinidad, June, 1892; asst. sec. judicial enquiry comsn., 1892; priv. sec. to gov., Aug. to Dec., 1892; sec. to road loan bd., 1892-5; to Chicago exposn. comsn., 1893-4; 2nd asst. harbrmr., Aug., 1893, to Dec., 1894; ag. sec. to quarantine bld., June to Oct., 1894; asst. harbrmr., Jan., 1895; ag. harbrmr., July to Sept., 1896, and June to Sept., 1898; ag. warden St. Anne's and Diego Martin, Jan., 1898; ch. clk. and cash sub-treas., Tobago, Apr., 1897.

PILOT, WILLIAM.—I.S.O. (1904), D.D., D.C.L., F.R.G.S.; fell. St. Aug. Coll., Cant.; canon Cath. St. John Bapt., St. John's, Newfild.; ordained by Bishop Willerforce, Oxon, 1867; prin. Queen's Coll., St. John's, 1867; supt. Ch. of Eng. schls., 1875.

PINDER, JOHN HOTHERSALL.—Ed. Marl. Coll. and Caius Coll., Camb., M.A. (2nd cl. class trip.); admitted solicitor (Eng.) 1887; ch. regiar., Lagos, 1901; called to the bar, Linc. Inn, 1904; dist. comsnr., Lagos, 1904.
PINNETT, F. P.—Solr., admitted 1892; 1st class clk., secretariat, Lagos, 1895; dist. comsnr., 1897; trav. comsnr., 1903.

PISANI, PROF. S. L., C.M.G. (1895), M.D.—Grad. Malta Univ., 1850, Edinburgh Univ., 1853; L.R.C.S.E., 1853; B. 1822; prof. of anatomy and histology, Malta Univ., Mar., 1858; prof. of anatomy and midwifery, Dec., 1859; prof. of anatomy and surgery, Oct., 1869; of surgery, Oct., 1876; ch. govt. med. offr., July, 1885.
PITOT, LEON EMILE.—Govt. survr. and super. of pub. wks., Seychelles, 11th Apl., 1889; gen. manager of rlyws. and engineer, Mauritius, 14th Sept., 1896.
PITTS, HON. JAMES S.—Appkd. to legis. coun., Newfoundland, 1883; mem. of exec. coun., 1888 and 1889; again Apr. to Dec., 1894; mem. of legis. coun., 1898.

PLAGEMANN, CONRAD O.—Clk., Basseterre waterwks., St. Kitts, 1882; clk. S. P. waterwks., 1885-1894; asst. surv. of roads and pub. wks., 1888-1891; clk., Basseterre town improvem., comsnr., St. Kitts, 1891; ag. supt. pub. wks., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1893; inspr. of weights and meas., 1896; clk., Basseterre town bd., 1903.
PLAMONDON, M.A.—Puissane judge, super. ct., Quebec, 1874.

PLANT, GEORGE FREDERIC.—B.A., scholar of B.N.C., Oxford, Oct., 1896; graduated in classical honours, 1900; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1901; ag. office asst. to govt. agent, N. W. prov., Jan., 1903.

PLAYFORD, THE HON. THOMAS.—Mem. house of assem., S. Australia; held office as comsnr. of crown lds. and immigr. at various periods between Feb., 1876, and June, 1881; comsnr. pub. wks., June, 1884, to Feb., 1886; comsnr. of crown lds. and immigr., Feb., 1885, to June, 1885; treas. and premier, 1887 to 1889, and again 1890 to 1892; mem. of fed. convention, 1891; mem. of Kingston ministry, 1893; agt.-gen. for S. Australia in London, 1894 to 1898, and representative of col. at col. conf., Ottawa, 1894; mem. H. of Assem., S. Australia, 1899 to 1901; mem. of senate, Australian parliament, 1901.

PLOWMAN, G.T.—Clk. and shorthand writer, col. sec.'s office, Natal, 1890; 1st clk., 1891; ch. clk., 1894; sec. to civ. ser. bd.; asst. under-sec., 1898; sec. to prime min., July, 1901; sec., lands and works, Nov., 1901; mem., civ. serv. bd., 1901.
PLUMPTRE, GEORGE B.—Ed. Trent Coll.; served with 33rd squadron, I.Y., S. African war, Jan., 1900, to Sept., 1902 (medals, Queen's, 4 clasps; King's, 2 clasps); clk., land settlement dept., O.R.C.

PLUNKET, 5TH BARON (created 1827), WM. LEE PLUNKETT.—B. 1864, K.C.V.O., J.P., Dublin and Wicklow; honorary attache to the embassy at Rome, 1889-92; Constantineop., 1892-4; priv. sec. to Lord-Lieut. of Ireland since 1900; gov. and commdr.-in-chief, New Zealand, 9th Mar., 1904.

POGUE, ROBERT H.—3rd class clk., col. sec.'s office, Gibraltar, Feb., 1883 (after compet. exam.); transf. to pol. office, Jan., 1886; 3rd class clk., P.O., 1889; 2nd class, Jan., 1891; 1st class clk., col. sec.'s office, Nov., 1893; 1st class clk. treas. and port dept., and regiar. marine ct., July, 1895.

POIRIER, FREDERIC.—Apr., 1870, volunteer, proc.-gen.'s office, Mauritius; 1870, copying clerk; ditto; 1872, ag. clk., ditto; 1873, asst. clk., jun. dist. magistracy; 1874, 2nd clk., proc.-gen.'s dept.; ch. clk., 1886; in 1876, sec. to a comteo. to inquire as to the central gool; in 1882, sec. to a comteo. of inquiry as to the working of the central civil status depts.; curator of vacant estates, June, 1890.

PONTIFEX, S. C. O.—Apptd. to cust., Br. Guiana, 1891; 5th class offr., 1892; 4th ditto, 1894; 3rd ditto, 1898; transf., to cust., So. Nig., 1899; astt. collr. cust., Feb., 1901; ag. collr. of cust., July, 1902 to Feb., 1903, and from Apl. to Nov., 1904.

POPE, JOSEPH, C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1854; entered civ. ser. of Canada in 1878 as priv. sec. to min. of marine and fisheries; priv. sec. to the late Sir John A. Macdonald, prime min. from 1882 till his death in 1891; astt. clk. of the Queen's Privy Coun. for Canada, 1889; under-sec. of state and dep. regisr. gen. of Canada, 1896; author of the Memoirs of Sir John A. Macdonald; Royal Tour in Canada, 1901, and other works; attached to the staff of the Br. agt. on the Behring Sea arbitration at Paris, 1893; agt. of Canadian govt., at joint high comsn., Quebec and Washington, 1898-99; deputed by govt. to arrange Royal tour in Canada, 1901; assoc. sec. to Alaska boundary tribunal, London, 19.3.

PORRAL, ALBERT, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1846; Superint. clk., crown land office, Gibraltar, Nov., 1862; 2nd class elk., Jan., 1875; 1stclass clk., Jan., 1883; sec. to comsn. to inquire into the organisation of the pol. force, 1887; contracting offr. in 1890; sec. to the crown lands bd., with a seat at that bd., Jan., 1891; ag. contracting offr., June to Oct., 1892; comsn. of crown lands, 1894.

PORRAL, JOHN.—Clk., port office, Gibraltar, Aug. to Sept., 1874, and July, 1877, to May, 1878; writer in H.M.'s dockyard, Sept., 1874, to July, 1877; 3rd clk., col. sec. office, June, 1878; 2nd clk., 1883; 1st class clk., Jan., 1890; ch. clk., Dec., 1891.
PORTAL, Dr. E.—Astt. govt. med. officer, Seychelles, July, 1892; govt. med. officer, Mauritius (Rodrigues), 1901.
PORTELLI, COL. A. M., of the Royal Malta Fencible Artillery, C.M.G. (1882).—For services in connection with the Egyptian expdn., 1882.

PORTELLI-CARBONE, ALFONSC. M.D. (Malta Univ., 1883).—L. M. (Rot. Hosp., Dub.), 1890; police physician for Zurrico, Safi, Crendi, and act. pol. phys. for Micabiba and Chircop, and sanitary inspr. for the 6th country dist., Aug., 1884; dist. med. offr. for Valetta, Sept., 1885; is a mem. of the Br. Med. Assoc. (Mediterranean branch), 1888; fell. Br. Gynacol. Soc., and mem. Internl. Med. Cong., Berlin, 1890, and Rome, 1894 (obst. sec.).

PORTER, ALEX.—J.P., Br. Honduras, 1879; foreman, pub. wks., Dec., 1886; commr. of sup. ct., 1888; dist. mag., coroner, and registr. births and deaths, W. dist., 1889; ditto, Stann Creek, 1890; dist. commr. and coroner, Belize, offl. admstr., and offl. recvr., June, 1903; ag. dist. mag. and coroner, Orange Walk, 1886; col. engineer, 1887; dist. mag., W. dist., 1887-1888; Toledo, 1888-1889; dist. commr., Corozal, 1894, and Belize, 1895, 1896-1897, 1902-1903.

PORTER, SIR NEALE, K.C.M.G. (1894), C.M.G. (1888).—Capt. 1st Warwickshire regt. (Mil.); pres. mag. in charge of Anguilla, Mar., 1869, to May, 1871; ag. col. sec. of Antigua, May, 1871, to Oct., 1871; ag. lieut.-gov. Dominica, Oct., 1871, to Mar., 1872; ag. pres., Apr. to June, 1872; pres., Montserrat, June, 1872; ag. pres., Dominica, June to Nov., 1882; col. sec., Leeward Is., Apr., 1883; adminst. govt., June, 1883, to Jan., 1884, July to Sept., 1884, and July to Dec., 1887; col. sec., Jamaica, 1887-95.

PORTER, W. HENRY.—Clk., pres.'s office, Dominica, Nov., 1873; treasy. clk. and excise offr., Nevis, 1878; 1st landing waiter and quarantine offr., St. Kitts, May, 1881; ag. treas., Dominica, Feb., 1884, to Oct., 1887; ag. mag., dist. F., Nov., 1887, to Feb., 1888; treas., Dominica, July, 1890.

POTTER, THOMAS IRWIN.—Ed. Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad; ag. asst. clk., col. hosp., 18th Dec., 1882; clerical asst., crown lands off., 10th Apl., 1883; clk. survey dept. (in addition to former post), 1st Dec., 1884; ch. clk., crown lands off., 16th Aug., 1897; ch. clk. and astt. inspr. of schls., 1st Nov., 1898; warden, La Brea and Oropuche (owing to spec. knowledge of asphalt question); ch. offr. of customs, Brighton, La Brea, 1st Jan., 1899; harb.-master, Brighton harbour, La Brea, in addition to wardenship, 1st July, 1899; J.P. for county of St. Patrick; mem. of society of arts, and dormant mem. Zoological society of Lond.; ag. sub. int., crown lands, 1st Sept. to 31st Oct., 1901.

POTTS, MOSES A.—Ed. S. Leone Gram. Schl.; served in H.M.'s commissariat, S. Leone, June, 1853, till Jan., 1860; ag. 2nd clk., treas., July, 1865, to Jan., 1866; extra clk., Jan., 1866; house and land tax clk., Aug., 1869; 2nd clk., treasy., after a compet. exam., Sept., 1870; census master, 1871; ch. clk. and cashier, 1877; ag. aud.-gen., Dec., 1886, to Feb., 1887; charity comsn., 1890.
POWELL, COL. W.—Adjt.-gen. of militia at headquarters, Canada.
POWELL, ROBERT ALBERT, A.M.I.C.E., assoc. roy. coll. of science, Dub.—Dist. engrn., P.W.D., Ceylon; ag. 2nd financial and office asst., Nov., 1892; dist. engrn., Oct., 1893.

PRATT, CHARLES E. J. BLYTH.—Ed. Trent Coll.; 2nd lieut., 4th batt. Sherwood Foresters Derbyshire regt., Jan., 1887; lieut., 4th batt. Prince of Wales North Stafford regt., May, 1888; instructor of musketry, Jan., 1890; lieut., Falmouth div. submarine minera, R.E., Apr., 1893; astt. inspr. Sierra Leone frontier pol., Aug., 1895; inspr., Apr., 1896; J.P. for S. Leone.

PRENDERGAST, SIR JAMES, KT. BACH. (1881).—B. 1828; is grad. of Univ. of Camb.; called to bar (Mid. Tem.), 1857; admitted a barrister of the sup. ct. in N. Zealand, Dec., 1862; atty.-gen., 1865; ch. just. of N.Z., 1875-1899.

PRENDERGAST, JAMES EMILE PIERRE.—Ed. at Seminary of Quebec, and Laval Univ., Quebec; B.A., 1878; LL.B., 1881; returned for La Verendrye, Manitoba, Aug., 1885; again Dec., 1886; on formation of cabinet by Hon. Thomas Greenway, Jan., 1888, was given portfolio of prov. sec.; re-elected in said constituency; elected for Woodlands, 1888.

PRICE, FERDINANDO HAMLYN.—Ed. at Rossall, and Queen's Coll., Camb.; open scholarship, 1875; writer to the govt. of Ceylon, Mar., 1878; extra office asst. to the govt. agt. for the central prov., Sept., 1879; ag. astt. collr. customs, Trincomalee, May, 1880; ag. astt. govt. agt., Nuwara Eliya, Nov., 1880; priv. sec. to lieut.-gov., Mar., 1881; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., cent. prov., June, 1881; priv. sec. to lieut.-gov., July, 1883; ag. astt. govt. agt., Negombo, Dec., 1883; ag. astt. col. treas., Jan., 1884; ag. office asst. cent. prov., Dec., 1885; ag. astt. govt. agt., Kegalle, Mar., 1886; ag. chmn., mun. coun. and mayor, Colomba, Sept. to Dec., 1889; astt. govt. agt., Kegalle, Dec., 1891; chmn. mun. coun., and mayor, Colombo, June, 1896; sent on special duty to Bombay, Feb., 1897; ag. govt. agt., Sabaragamuva, Apr., 1898; again mayor, Colombo, Oct., 1898; spec. serv. as astt. govt. agt., W. prov., Nov., 1900; govt. agt., prov. of Uva, Oct., 1901.

PRICE, THOS. REES., C.M.G.—Served in various positions in the Vale of Neath and G.W. railway companies, England, 1863 to 1880; dist. traffic supt. at Grahamstown, Cape govt. railways, July, 1880; ag. ast. traffic man., West system, C.G.R., Dec., 1880; ditto, Port Elizabeth, Feb., 1881; traffic man. of East system, 1st July, 1882; ag. ch. traffic man., 11th Nov., 1891; traffic man., North system, 1892; C.G.R. agent in Transvaal and O.F.S., 7th Oct., 1892; ch. traffic man., 1st Nov., 1893; ag. gen. man., 1896; gen. man., C.G.R., 16th May, 1901 to 11th Aug., 1902; now gen. man., C.S.A. railways.

PRINSEP, HENRY CHARLES.—Ed. Cheltenham Coll.; went to W. Australia, 1866; apptd. J.P. in 1867; ag. clk. and draftsman in lands dept., 1874; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to admstr., Nov., 1884, to June, 1885; ch. draftsman, lands dept., 1890; ch. clk., 1892; under-sec. for mines, 1894; ch. prot. of aborigines, May, 1898.

PRIOR, WM. BURGESS.—Mem. provisional N. Borneo Assoc.; res. E. Coast dist. Br. N. Borneo, Aug., 1881; prot. of coolies, E. Coast, 1884; mem. E. Coast coun., 1883; Br. consular agt., 1880; res. and mag., Gold Field dist., Feb., 1887.
PRITCHARD, MAJOR C. G.—1st coman. R.A., 23rd Nov., 1891; promoted capt., 4th Dec., 1899; adj., Hong Kong volunteers, Jan. to July, 1901; comdt. and adj. (local major), 2nd Apl., 1902.

PRITCHARD, PERCY HERBERT.—M.A., Ed., 1894; attach. premier's off., Cape, Nov., 1899, to June, 1900; on h. commr.'s staff, June, 1900, to Feb., 1901; clk. to sec. O.R.C. admn., Feb., 1901; clk. to col. sec., June, 1902; editor, O.R.C. govt. gazette, July, 1902; acted as ch. clk., Apl. to Aug., 1903.

PROBYN, LESLIE, C.M.G. (1903).—Ed. at Charterhouse, and in France and Germany, called to the bar, Mid. Tem., 1884; joint author of "The Jurisdiction and Practice of the Mayor's Court," and other law books; atty.-gen., Br. Honduras, 1893; atty.-gen. of Grenada, May, 1896; also ag. col. sec. in 1897-8; ag. admstr. on several occasions; gov. secretary, S. Nigeria, 12th Apl., 1901; gov., Sierra Leone, 14th July, 1904.

PROCTOR, S. F., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.E., and L.M.—Ed. Univ. Edin. and St. Thomas's Hosp., Lond.; apptd. ast. med. offr., Grenada, June, 1878; house surg., Col. Hosp., Grenada, Nov., 1879; resig., Sept., 1880; ag. medical officer, Trinidad, June, 1881, to May, 1882, during yellow fever epidemic; ast. col. surg. and imgrm. med. offr., St. Lucia, Oct., 1882.

PROUT, W. T., M.B., C.M., Edin.—Ast. poor law med. offr., Mauritius, 1885; surg. supt., immigrant ship "Dundee," 1887; ast. col. surg., G. Coast, 1888; dist. comanr., G. Coast, 1890; col. surg., Gambia, 1893; ag. ch. mag., Gambia, 1893; med. offr. in charge of base hosp. during campaign against Foday Sila; col. surg., S. Leone, 1895; title altered to prin. med. offr., 1901; J.P.; pres., quarantine bd.; nominated mem. of city coun.; med. offr. of health, Freetown; M.L.C.; fellow of roy. med. soc., Edin., and corresponding mem. of the epidemiological soc. of Lond.; author of article "Yaws" in "Diseases of Warm Climates" (Davidson); Fil. Voloulous (Archives de Parasitologie, May, 1901); "Filariasis in S. Leone" (Brit. Med. Journal, 1902); and other papers.
PROWSE, D. W.—Central dist. ct., judge, Newfoundland, 1869; author of a "History of Newfoundland," 1895.
PUGHE, S. R.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil ser., and assigned to the C.O., Aug., 1897; promoted, with gratuity, Aug., 1904.

PURCELL, GILBERT KENHELM TREFFRY.—Ed. Exeter Coll., Oxford; B.A. 1888; M.A. 1893; called to bar, Linc.'s Inn, Nov., 1890; mem. of western circ., also practised at cent. crim. ct.; apptd. ast. to atty.-gen. Leeward Islda. (St. Kitts), Mar., 1898; official mem. of exec. and legis. couns. of the Presidency of St. Christopher and Nevis; ag. atty.-gen. Leewards, Aug.-Oct., 1900; apptd. ag. 1st police judge, June, 1901; puisne judge, G. Court, 11th June, 1901.

PURLAND, CAPTAIN THOMAS TERENCE CONSTANTINE, F.R.G.S.—Entered Cape civ. serv., Aug., 1879; clk. control and audit office: treas. custs.; lieut. and adjut. "Prince Alfred's Guard," Basuto campaign, 1880-81 (severely wounded); medal and clasp; rec. rank of capt. in recog. of services; pens. for wounds; clk. civ. com. and rea. mag., Mossel Bay, 1884; Wodehouse, 1885; Albert, 1885; ast. res. mag., Simon's Tn., 1886, also exam. off., cust., 1890; supdt., Shark's River convict stn., 1894; ast. supdt., Breakwater convict stn., Cape Th., 1895; dep. insp. of prsna., 1896; prin. clk. convict dept., 1899; direc. of prsna., Transvaal, 1900; J.P. for Cape Col. and Transvaal Col.

QUARTEY, R. E.—Outdoor offr., customs, Accra, 1881; 4th clk., customs and treasy., 1883; 2nd clk., audit office, 1885; 1st clk., 1887; audit clk. to local auditor, Jan., 1889; has several times acted as local auditor.
QUEBEC, 5TH BISHOP OF, RT. REV. A. H. DUNN, D.D.—Consec.1892; late schol. of C.C.C., Camb. (29th wrangler); M.A. 1866; hon. D.D. Camb.; vicar of All Saints, S. Acton, Middx., 1872-92.
QUENTRALL, THOMAS.—Mining engrnr., Kimberley, Cape, July, 1889; inspr. of mines, Kimberley, July, 1891.
RADCLIFFE, CHAS. AMYAS.—Lieut., R.N., ret.; dep. mast. attendant, S. Stltlmt., Jan., 1903; ag. habmr., Penang, Apl., 1903; ag. mast. attendant, S.S., Dec., 1903.
RADFORD, HENRY WYAT.—Clk. ast. legis. assm., Queensland; clk. of legis. coun., 1881.

RAE, JAMES MACLURE.—Admitted to Bahamas bar, 1877; notary public same year; J.P., 1878; res. just., Crooked Is. dist., Oct., 1878; ast. comanr. at Cockburn harbour, Turks and Caicos Islda., July, 1880; ag. judge of the sup. ct., and ex officio auditor of the Turks and Caicos Islda., Aug., 1882, to May, 1883, and Mar., 1884, to Oct., 1886; ast. comanr., Grand Turk, 1885; stip. mag., Bahamas, Nov., 1887; ag. atty.-gen., July, 1893, to Feb., 1894; ag. ch. just., Feb. and Mar., 1894; ag. atty.-gen., June, 1896, to Jan., 1897; June, 1897, to Feb., 1898; Sept. and Oct., 1899; June to Oct., 1900; June 11-24, 1901; 15th May to 3rd Aug., 1902; ag. ch. just., 4th Aug. to 9th Nov., 1902; ag. att.-gen., 16th June to 31st Oct., 1904.

RAE, WM. CHARLES.—Clk. col. sec.'s office, Mauritius, after exam., Nov., 1876; clk. of the exec. coun., and of coun. of govt., Mar., 1894; compiler of Handbook on the constn., practice, and proceedings of the Coun. of Govt., 1896; ch. clk. col. sec.'s office, Aug., 1900; 2nd ast. col. sec., 9th Nov., 1903.

RALPHS, EDWIN.—F.R.G.S.; F.C.S.; A.C.P.; Ed. St. Mark's Coll., Chelsea; 1st cls. in Queen's scholarship, in final teachers' exams. (education dept.), and in archbishop's divinity examn.; honours in science, South Kens. exams.; ast. master (junior) Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, 1896; senior grade, 1901; ag. second master, Apl., 1903. to Mar., 1904; ag. mast., police schl., Hong Kong, May, 1902, to Mar., 1904; hon. examnr. in botany and chemistry to Hong Kong Coll. of Med. for Chinese, 1901, 1902 and 1903.

RAM, GEORGE EDWARD—Lieut.—4th batt., Prince of Wales's, North Staffordshire Regt.; served in S. Africa, Oct., 1902, to July, 1903 (medal, with 3 clasps); ag. priv. sec. and A.D.C. to gov. of Newfoundland, 9th Sept., 1903.

RAMA-NATHAN, PONNAMBALAM, C.M.G. (1889).—B. 1851; ed. at the Acad., Colombo, and Pres. Coll., Madras; barrister-at-law, In. Tem.; advoc., sup. ct. Ceylon, 1873; mem. of the legis. coun., 1879; mem. of the law commn. apptd. to report upon certain codes dealing with the laws of Ceylon, 1879; served also on the commn. apptd. at his instance to report upon the Thoroughfares Ordinance, 1881; obtained the introduction of P.O. savings bank in Ceylon, 1881-83; was one of the select commn. of the legis. coun. apptd. to report upon retrenching the public expenditure of the island, 1882-83; a mem. of the coun. of legal educn., 1884; mem. of the Ceylon commn. of the Col. and Ind. Exhibn., 1886; mem. of the Cent. Irrig. Bd., 1888; and of the select commn. to report upon the incidence of the grain taxes, 1889; mem. of the commn. to inquire into the extension of the rly. to the N. parts of the island, 1890; solr.-gen. of Ceylon, 1892; ag. atty.-gen., 1894.
RANFORD, HENRY SAMUEL.—Contract survr., W. Australia, 1872; employed on admiralty survey, 1875; staff-survr., 1884; sen. ditto, 1886; govt. ld. agent., Kattanning, Aug., 1897; ch. ld. agt., 1902.

RANFURLY, 5TH EARL OF (SIR UCHTER JOHN MARK KNOX, G.C.M.G.), Viscount Northland and Baron Welles of Dungannon, co. Tyrone, Ireland, Baron Ranfurly of Ramporlie, co. Renfrew, United Kingdom.—Born 1866; succeeded his brother 1875; ed. Trinity Coll., Cambridge; J.P., D.L. for co. Tyrone; Knight of Justice of the Order of St. John of Jerusalem; Lord-in-Waiting to the Queen, 1895-7; Governor of New Zealand, 1897-1904.

RANKIN, E. B. W.—Entd. service, Br. Guiana, 1885; 5th asst. survr. clk., regar.'s off., 1892; passed exam. as sworn clk. and notary public, 1895, and acted as such on various occasions; in charge of Berbice off., Aug. to Dec., 1896; 4th asst. clk., 1896; clk. to atty.-gen., 1897; sec., special commn. of legislature on Demarara-Essequibo rly. tariff, 1899.

RANKINE, R. S. D.—Entd. civ. serv., London, 1890; treasury, Fiji, Feb., 1894; priv. sec. to Sir George O'Brien, Sept., 1897, to 1901; acted clk. to ex. coun. Sept., 1899, to Mar., 1900; apptd. acct. nat. taxes, treasury, 1901; priv. sec. to Sir Henry Jackson, July, 1902.
RANNIE, CLARENCE RUPERT.—Clk. to col. engr., Antigua, Mar., 1891; ag. clk. to audr.-gen., Sept., 1894; 2nd clk. to audr.-gen., Leeward Is., Jan., 1895.

RAPINET, CHARLES.—Entered colonial service, Oct., 1853; clk. in office of controller of charitable insttns., Malta; transfd. to ch. sec.'s office in 1869; ch. clk. in audit office in 1877; acted as audr.-gen., May to Nov., 1878; and as audr.-gen. and dir. of contracts, June to Oct., 1880, Oct. to Nov., 1881, May to Nov., 1882, and July to Dec., 1883; commr. of the monte di pietà and savings bank, 1885.
RASON, CORNWALTH HECTOR, J.P., M.L.A.—Min. for pub. wks., W. Aust., Dec. 1901; min. for pub. wks. and for rly., July, 1902; resigned, 1904.

RAT, JOSEPH NUMA.—Colonial scholarship, Trinidad, 1866; Warneford scholarship, King's Coll., Lond., 1867; seven times prizeman, literature, divinity and medicine in King's Coll., 1867-1871; matric., Lond. Univ., 1868; prel. science, M.B. Lond. Univ., 1869; M.R.C.S. (Eng.), 1873; asst. col. surg., G. Coast, Feb., 1887; dist. med. offr., Dominica, Aug., 1889; ag. med. offr., public institutions, Dominica, 1890; dist. med. offr., St. Kitts, Oct., 1892; mag. and med. offr., Anguilla, Dec., 1897; dep. judge, court of summary juris., Leeward Is., 1899; M.L.C., Dominica, 1888-1889; chmn., town improvement commn., St. Kitts, 1896-1897; mem. poor law bd., St. Kitts, 1896-1897; has translated several military drill books into the Hausa language for the G. Coast govt.; has also written numerous papers on Leprosy, Yaws, etc., and a grammar of the Carib language.

RATTRAY, W. WALLACE.—3rd clk., gov.'s office, Windward Is., 1883; transfd. to Grenada as 2nd clk. in gov.'s office on separation of Barbados from Windward Is., June, 1885; supervisor of customs, G. Coast, Nov., 1891; ag. controller of customs, May to Sept., 1892, and Feb. to May, 1894; asst. treasr., G. Coast, Apl., 1895; ag. treasr., May to June, 1898, and Dec. to Mar., 1900, with a seat in the exec. and legis. councils; sent to Gambia on spec. serv., April, 1901, as ag. treasr., collr. of cust. and postmr., J.P., and commr. of requests, and commr. of affidav.; mem. ex. and legis. couns.; asst. coll. of customs, Lagos, 1902; ag. collr. of cust., 1902; asst. treasr., May, 1904; ag. treasr., May to Sept., 1904; asst. compt. of cust. G. Coast, Sept., 1904.

RAWSON, ADMIRAL SIR HARRY HOLDSWORTH, K.C.B. (1897); C.B. (1882).—B. 1843; naval cadet, 1857; sub. lieut., 1863; lieut., 1863; commander, 1871; captain, 1877; rear-admiral, 1892; vice-admiral, 1898; admiral, 1903; hoisted the British flag at Nicosia, Cyprus, 1878, and was for one month mil. comdr. of that place; principal transport offr. in Egypt, 1882 (medal, star 3rd cls., Osmanieh, C.B.); A.D.C. to H.M. Queen Victoria, 1890 to 1892; in cmd. of expdn. against M'Barack and capture of Mwale, 1896 (medal); of bombardment of Sultan's palace, Zanzibar, 1896 (1st cls. Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, and promoted to Order of Hamoudieh), and of Benin, 1897 (clasp and K.C.B.); apptd. command-in-chief, Cape of Good Hope and W. Africa Station, 1895; in command of Channel Squadron, 1898-1901; gov. of N. S. Wales, 12th Feb., 1902.

RAWSTORNE, HENRY CLANCY.—Asst. clerk, civil status, Mauritius, Jan., 1874; clk., stip. ct., Flaqc., Mar., 1875; asst. clk., dist. ct., Port Louis, July, 1875; joint clk., ditto, Oct., 1876; inspr. of pol. force, May, 1877; inspr. inland rev., Mar., 1880; and supt. of distilleries, Mar., 1893; ag. supt. inland rev., May to Oct., 1895.

RAYNER, SIR THOMAS CROSSLEY, KT. (1899).—B. 1860; ed. at Owens Coll.; matric., Lond., 1878; barf., Mid. Tem., 1882; dist. commr., G. Coast Col., 1887; acted as judge, 1890; mag., Trinidad, 1890; ag. puisne judge, June to Oct., 1891; chmn. of commn. of inquiry into registry of sup. ct., Jan., 1892; ag. commr., Tobago, Sept. to Dec., 1892; ag. puisne judge, Trinidad, Dec., 1892, to Sept., 1893; puisne judge, G. Coast, Jan., 1894; ch. just., Lagos, 1895.

READ, EDWARD INSKIP, F.R.C.S.I.—Entered govt. service, Trinidad, Feb., 1882; ag. dist. med. offr., S. Naparima and town, and Port of San Fernando, 1882-3; asst. surg., col. hosp., Port of Spain, 1884; surg.-in-charge, San Fernando hosp., 1887; ag. health offr. of shipping, inspr. of imigrts. and dist. med. offr., Port of Spain, 1891; in charge of coll. hosp., Port of Spain, Nov., 1892; clinical ass't., Royal Westminster Ophthal. Hosp., 1893; dist. med. off'r., Santa Cruz; examiner to widows and orphans' civ. ser. fund, 1897; ophthalmic surg. to health office dept., 1900.

READ, HERBERT JAMES, B.A.—Brazenose Coll., Oxford; 1st class math. mods., and 1st class math. finals; clk., higher div., W.O., Feb., 1887; 2nd class clk., C.O., Jan., 1889; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, Sept., 1896; priv. sec. to Mr. (now Sir E.) Wingfield, Feb., 1898; 1st class clk., Oct., 1898.

READ, THE VEN. ARCHDN. J. H., D.D.—1st Archdeacon of P. E. Is., 1860; ecclesiast. comary., 1858; formerly of St. John's Coll., Camb., and B.D., 1852; D.D., 1857; of King's Coll., Fredericton, N.B.; rector of Milton, 1865.
READ, WILLIAM HENRY MACLEOD, C.M.G. (1886).—M.L.C., S. Stltns., 1867-86.
REAL, C.—2nd puisne judge, Queensland, 1890.

REECE, EARLIEY B.—Ent. pub. service, St. Lucia, Apl., 1892, as supernmry. clk. govt. off.; 3rd clk., Apl., 1893; 2nd clk. and acct. treas'y., Apl., 1894; ag. ch. clk. on six occasions, 1895-9; honours graduate, 1900 (diploma), Ont. com. coll., Canada; affil. inst. ch. accts., Canada; apptd. chief clk. and book-keeper, treasury, Gold Coast, 26th April, 1901; introduced double entry system of accounts in the treasury in conformity with fin. inst., June, 1901.
REECE, FREDERICK ST. AUBYN.—Clk., P.O., Barbados, 1873; 2nd clk., 1875; resig. 1878; landing waiter, St. Lucia, 1883; col. postmr., St. Lucia, 1885.

REECE, REV. J. E.—Ed. at St. John's Coll., Camb.; B.A. in 1868; M.A. in 1874; ag. curate of St. Michael's Cathedral, Barbados, and priv. sec. to Bishop Parry, 1872; asst. master of Harrison Coll., and curate of St. Michael's Cathedral, 1877; curate of St. Bartholomew's, 1878; curate of St. Luke's, 1882; bishop's chaplain, 1884; inspr. of schls., 1885.

REECE, J. RICHARD.—Ed. at St. John's Coll., Camb.; B.A. in 1870; called to bar, Inn. Tem., 1871; J.P., Barbados, 1873; coroner for parishes of Christchurch and St. George, 1873; ag. provost-marshal and serjt.-at-arms, 1874; ag. pol. mag. of St. Michael's parish, 1875; ag. judge of asst. ct. of appeal, 1875-79; comsnr. of probates, 1877; ag. solr.gen., 1880; regiar. of friendly and benevolent societies, 1880; ag. judge of the asst. ct. of appeal, 1880-82; ag. atty.-gen., Grenada, 1883; judge of asst. ct. of appeal, 1883; ag. ch. just. St. Vincent, 1884-5; local comsnr. of incumbered estates ct. (St. Vincent), Oct., 1884; res. mag., Jamaica, 1888.

REEVE, HENRY, C.M.G. (1900), A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1854; passed C.S. exam., Victoria, 1868; instld., Melbourne Univ., 1870; cadet, Victorian survey dept., 1873; cadet, N.S.W. survey dept., 1874; survr., 1878; survr., Fiji survey dept., May, 1880; staff survr., Jan., 1881; ag. comsnr. for wks., July, 1884, to Feb., 1886; col. engrnr., St. Lucia, 1887; dir. of survey, Windward Is., 1891; mem. legis. coun., St. Lucia, 1891; special mem. exec. coun., St Lucia and St. Vincent, 1891; col. engrnr., Gambia, 1894; mem. exec. coun., 1895; ch. English comsnr., Anglo-French bndry. coman., 1895-6, 1898-9; director of pub. wks., Lagos, Aug., 1901; ag. col. sec., Jan. to May; ag. gov'r., May to July, 1902; ag. col. sec., 1902 and 1903; ag. gov'r., 1902; deputy gov'r., 1903; ret., 1904.

REEVE-TUCKER, W. R.—Lieut. D.C.O. Hants and Isle of Wight Arty., 1885; capt., 1890; hon. maj., 1900; attd. three yrs., 1887-8-9, as subaltern to service batteries, Roy. Arty.; inspr. Gold Coast Hausas, 1891; asst. inspr. Lagos Hausas, 1892; inspr., 1896; commanded Lagos Hausas, Ashanti expdn., 1895-6; wing off'r., Malay States Guides, 1896; comsnr. of police, Lagos, 1898; travelling coman., 1899.

REEVES, HON. WILLIAM PEMBER.—Born Canterbury, N.Z., 1857; ed. in Colony; Some Scholar, Christ's Coll., Canterbury; first elected mem. of H. of reprntves., 1887; joined Bellance Min., Jan., 1891, holding the portfolios of educn., just and labour; contd. in off. in Seddon Min. to Jan. 1896; author of following N.Z. Acts relating to labour:—Employers' Liability, Contractors and Workmen's Lien, Workmen's Wages, Conspiracy Law Amendment, Factories, Shipping and Seamen's Amendment, Shops and Shop Assistants, Industrial Conciliation, Servants' Registry and Wages Attachment, the most important being the Indust. Concil. Act, providing for compulsory arbitration in labour differences when negotiations between employers and employed fail: agt.-gen. for N.Z. in London, 1896; holds also the offices of stock and loan agent for N.Z.; comsnr. under N.Z. Public Revenues Act; custodian of N.Z. Public Trust Fund Securities, &c., &c.; represented N.Z. at Internat. Commer. Congress at Philadelphia, 1899; mem. of Pacific Cable Comtee., 1899; author of the "Long White Cloud," and other publications relating to N.Z.

REIBBEY, THE HON. T.—Speaker of house of assem., Tasmania, mem. for Westbury; mem. of the exec. coun., July, 1876; was leader of the Opposition, Mar., 1875, to July, 1876; and Aug., 1877, to Dec., 1878; premier and col. sec., July, 1876, to Aug., 1877; col. sec. in Mr. Crowther's ministry, Dec., 1878, to Oct., 1879; elected speaker, July, 1887.
REID, ALEXANDER, I.S.O. (1904).—Ent. the govt. ser. Tasmania, 1858; under treasurer, 1895.

REID, THE RT. HON. G. H., P.C.—Min. of pub. instrctn., N.S.W., Jan., 1883, to Mar., 1884; M.L.A. for E.
