Sydney; leader of Opposition, 1891; premier, 1894-9; leader of Opposition, 1st, 2nd and 3rd Commonwealth ministries; premier and min. for external affairs, 4th ditto, 1904; represented N.S.W. in London at H.M.'s Diamond Jubilee.

REID, IRVINE KEMP.—M.B.C.M. (1884), M.D. (1887), D.P.H. (1st class honours in practical hygiene) (1891), Abdn. Univ.; J.P. (1904); mem. of Roy. Med. Soc., Edin.; mem. of Soc. of Med. Offrs. of Health; house surg. and sec., W. Norfolk and Lynn Hosp., King's Lynn, 1886; surg., res. med. staff, Col. Hospital, Georgetown, Br. Guiana, 1887-89; govt. med. off'r., Is. of Wakenasa, Rio Essequibo, 1889; med. off'r., penal statmnt., Massaruni; and res. surg. pub. hosp., Bartica, 1892; med. off'r., Mara dist., 1893; ditto, Peter's Hall dist., and mem. med. bd., 1894; med. off'r., Cotton Tree dist., 1896.
REID, WALTER SCOTT.—Barrister of sup. ct. of N. Zealand, admitted in 1866; asst. law off'r. to govt. in 1871; solr.-gen. (non-political) in 1875.

RENNER, WILLIAM, M.D. (Brux.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.K. and Q.C.P., L.M. (Ireland).—Ed. at the Liverpool Coll., and Univ. Coll., Lond., and Liverpool, Rotunda, Dublin, and St. Jean Hosp., Brussels; mem. of the Pathological Soc. of London; late ophthalmic asst. to Prof. Streathamfield, Univ. Coll. Hosp., Lond.; ag. surg. S. Leone, 1882-83; asst. col. surg., 1884; in charge of lun. asyl., and incurable and smallpox hosp. Kissy; ag. col. surg., 1887.

RENNIE, ALFRED HERBERT.—Ed. Hamilton Gram. Sch., and Upper Canada Coll., Toronto; confill. clk. to Hon. John Norquay, premier and prov. treas., Manitoba, 1885 to 1889; deleg. to England and New York to negotiate a loan for Manitoba, 1887; correspond. clk., pub. wks. dept., Hong Kong, 1890; ag. astt. harbrmr. and ag. supt., water pol., 1892; ag. sanitary supt., and sec., sanitary bd., Hong Kong, 1893.

RENTON, ALEXANDER WOOD, M.A., LL.B. (honours), Edin. — Forensic prizeman; Lord Rector's essay prizeman; Bacon scholar, Gray's Inn, 1882; called to the bar, 1885; author of "Wood Renton on Lunacy"; joint author of first edition of "Edmunds on Patents"; editor of "Law Journal", editor of "Encyclopaedia of the Laws of England"; editor of "The English Reports"; puisne judge, Mauritius, Aug., 1901; chrmn., local comte. of soc. of comp. legis., 1902; chrmn. of mountain reserves comsn., 19-34; ag. procureur and advocate-gen., Aug., 1904.

RENWICK, SIR ARTHUR, KT. BACH. (1894), B.A. (Sydney), M.D. (Edin.), F.R.C.S.E.—B. 1837; min. of pub. instrctn., N.S.W., Feb., 1886; sec. for mines, Oct., 1881, to Jan., 1883; exec. comsnr. for N.S.W. at Melbourne Internat. Exhibtn., 1880-81; vice pres., Amsterdam Internat. Exhibtn., 1885; mem. of med. bd. of N.S.W., 1873; pres., state children's relief dept., 1881; fellow of the senate, Sydney Univ., 1872; pres., Sydney hosp. benevolent society of N.S.W., and of deaf, dumb, and blind asylums; comsnr. for N.S.W. at Chicago exhibition, 1893.
REYNOLDS, A. E. — Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Aug., 1900.

REYNOLDS, GEORGE ALEXANDER.—Entered audit office, Cape, 1863; transf'd. to office of C.C., Colesberg, Jan., 1868; 2nd class certif. in law and jurisprudence; astt. to C.C. and R.M., Namaqualand, Feb., 1869; acted as C.C. and R.M., 1869-71; as sub-collr., Port Nolloth, in 1871; and as R.M. and sub-collr., Hendeklip Bay in 1871; investigated state of R.M.'s office, Hendeklip bay, 1875, and took over duties, Feb. to May; transf'd. to audit office as inspr., and acctnt. of the gen. and revenue branches, Aug., 1875; ag. C.C. and R.M., Namaqualand, Nov., 1875; ch. clk., office of master of sup. ct., Dec., 1889; mast. of sup. ct., Mar., 1890; ag. high sheriff, June, 1901.

REYNOLDS, G. LEWIS.—Served in 23rd Co. I.Y. (Duke of Lancaster's Own), 25th Jan., 1900, to 15th Nov., 1900; clk., dir. of rlyws. labour depot, Bloemfontein, O.R.C., 16th Nov., 1900, to 31st July, 1901; native refugee dept., 1st Aug., 1901, to 12th Mar., 1902; army pay corps, Bloem., 12th Aug., 1902, to 31st Oct., 1902.

REYNOLDS, THE HON. W. H.—Mem. of prov. parlmt., Otago, N.Z., 1853 to 1876, having been eight years a mem. of the prov. exec., and four years speaker; mem. of col. parlmt. from 1863 to 1878; mem. of legis. coun., 1878; has held various offices in col. ministry; delegate to Australasian confce. in 1873.

RHODES, FRANK.—Joined post and tel. dept., O.F.S., Sept., 1888; postm. and telegraphist, Boshof, Sept., 1888; ditto, Bethlehem, Apr., 1893; ditto, Jagersfontein, Mar., 1895; 2nd supervisor, G.P.O., Bloem., Dec., 1895; inspr. of telegraphs, Jan., 1898; sec., posts and telegraphs, O.R.C., Mar., 1900.

RICE, CECIL N.—Ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; supernum. clk., admstr.'s office, St. Vincent, 1890; ag. 2nd clk., Apr. to July, 1891; Dec., 1892 to Feb., 1893; 2nd clk., June, 1893; is also sec. to gen. bd. of health; ag. ch. clk., 1895; clk. of exec. and leg. couns. on several occasions, 1895-1901; assisted in relief work after hurricane, Sept.-Dec., 1898; port offr. and cust. offr., 1898; ag. sec., Hurricane Loan bd., 1899, 1900 and 1901; 2nd clk. to gov., Windward Is. (Grenada), Aug., 1901; ch. clk., Nov., 1901; relieving offr. and sec. to Capt. Young on special mission to St. Vincent after volcanic eruption, 1902.

RICHARDS, T. H. HATTON, F.R.G.S.—Associate to ch. justice, W. Australia, 1881; clk. to ch. judicial comsnr., Western Pacific, 1883; assoc. to ch. jus. and clk. sup. ct., Fiji, Nov., 1883; also marshal, vice-admiralty ct., 1884; ag. priv. sec. to Sir W. C. F. Robinson, govrn. of S. Australia, and clk. to exec. coun., July, 1885; A.D.C., May, 1886, and also in Melbourne, Mar., 1889; priv. sec. to Sir W. Macgregor, admstr. of Br. N. Guinea, and clk. to exec. and leg. couns., Aug., 1889; also a res. mag.; treasr. and collr. of customs and a mem. of the exec. and leg. couns., 1890, and mem. native regulation bd.; regar.-gen., 1891; agt. govt. sec., Feb. to Sept., 1892; astt. col. sec., Gold Coast, May, 1893; ag. ch. astt., col. sec., Mar. to July, 1894, and clk. to exec. and leg. couns. again from Sept., 1895, to Feb., 1896; clk. of couns., Oct., 1896, to Mar., 1897; invalided, 1897; astt. sec. to govt. Cyprus, 1900.

RICHARDSON, EDMUND L.—Jun. clk., col. sec.'s office, Tobago, May, 1874; ch. clk., Oct., 1879; ag. registr. of deeds, sup. ct., July, 1880, to June, 1881; ch. clk., audit office, July to Dec., 1881; 2nd rev. offr. and landing waiter, St. Lucia, Dec., 1881.

RICHARDSON, THE HON. EDWARD, C.M.G. (1879).—B. 1831; entered the N. Zealand parlmt. in 1871 as representative for the city of Christchurch; min. for pub. wks., 1872; held that office through various changes of ministries till Jan., 1877, when he was compelled to retire on account of ill-health; again took office as min. of pub. wks., Sept., 1884, to Oct., 1887.
RICHARDSON, GEORGE.—Entered govt. service, 1861; comsnr. of police, Tasmania, 1899.
RICHARDSON, H. G.—Entered C.O., Dec., 1890; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser. and assigned to bd. of educn.; transf'd. to C.O., Oct., 1902.
RICHARDSON, LT.-COL. HUGH.—Stip. mag. of N.W. Territories, Canada, 1876; presided at trial of Louis Riel, 1888; sen. puisne judge, sup. ct., N.W.T., 1887.
RICHARDSON, JAMES A.—Copyist in the regist.'s office of Br. Guiana, Dec., 1878; 1st sworn clk., July, 1883.
RICHEY, MATTHEW HENRY, K.C., D.C.L.—Mayor of Halifax, N.S., 1864-7, and again 1875-8; sat in the commons for Halifax 1878-83; lieut.-gov. of N. S., 1883-8.

RICHMOND, DOUGLAS CLONE, C.B. (1902), M.A.—B. 1839; late fell. of Peterhouse, Camb.; Bell univ. schlr., 1858; chancellor's med., 1861; called to the bar, Linc.'s Inn, 1874; astt. sec., 1869; sec., 1872, endowed schla. comsn.; sec., 1875; comsnr., 1883; charity comn.; astt. comptroller, and auditor, 1896; comptroller and auditor-general, 1900; is hon. auditor of certain Crown Colonies and Protectorates; ag. on behalf of the S. S. for the cols.

RICHMOND, JAMES, M.I.C.R.—Engrn. irrigation wks., Jamaica, 1874-90; dist. engrnr., 1876-90; astt. dir. pub. wks., 1890; acted as dir. on several occasions; is a J.P., and also man. comsnr., Kingston improvement works.
RICKETTS, OSWALD F.—Cadet, Sarawak service, Oct., 1881; asst. rea., 4th div., June, 1881; res., 2nd class, 4th div., Jan., 1888; of Limbang, May, 1890.

RIDDELL, HELENA.—Certif. obst. soc., Lond.; trained, St. Thomas's hosp., 1895-9; Bombay municipal plague hosp., Arthur road, and the Sarvajenick plague hosp., Parel, 1899-1900; priv. nurse in England, 1900-1; matron, Victoria hosp., St. Lucia, June, 1902.

RIDLEY, HENRY NICHOLAS.—M.A. Oxon.; asst. in botanic dept., Br. Museum, 1880 to 1888; dir. of gardens and forests, S. Sttlmts., 25th Sept., 1888; sent on visit of inspection to Coos-Keeling and Christmas Is., 1890.

RIDGEWAY, THE RT. HON. SIR J. WEST, G.C.M.G. (1900), K.C.B., K.C.S.I., P.C. (Ireland).—B. 1844; ret. from the army with the rank of col., 1889; under-sec., foreign dept., India, 1881-6; comsnr. for delimitation of Afghan frontier, 1885-7; under-sec. to lord-lieut. of Ireland, 1887; lieut.-gov. of the Isle of Man, 1893; gov. of Ceylon, Dec., 1895.

RIND, WALTER LOCKHART.—Ed. Rossall schl. and Univ. Coll., Oxon.; 3rd cl. classical mods., Mar., 1897; 3rd cl. litt. hum., June, 1899; B.A., 1899; priv. sec. and clk. to admstr., and clk. to councils, Seychelles, June, 1900.

RIPON, 1ST MARQUESS OF (created 1871), GEORGE FREDERICK SAMUEL ROBINSON, K.G. (1803), P.C. (1863), G.C.S.I., C.I.E.—Son of 1st Earl of Ripon; born 1827; succeeded his father as Earl of Ripon and Viscount Goderich, 1859, and his uncle as Earl de Grey and Baron Grantham the same year; created a marquess in 1871, in recognition of his services as chmn. of the Joint High Coman. which arranged the Treaty of Washington in 1871; is lord-lieut. and C.A., N. Riding of York; dep. lord-lieut. of Yorkshire and Lincolnshire; under-S. of S. for War, June, 1859; removed to India Board, Feb., 1861; returned to W.O., July, 1861; and was S. of S. for War, Apl., 1863, to Feb., 1866, when he resumed his position at the India Board; finally resign., June, 1866; lord pres. of the coun., Dec., 1868, to Aug., 1873; gov.-gen. of India, 1880-4; first lord of the admiralty, Feb. to Aug., 1886; S. of S. for the cols., Aug., 1892, to June, 1895; was M.P. for Hull, 1852-3; for Huddersfield, 1853-7; and for the W. Riding of York from 1857-9; hon. D.C.L., Oxford, 1870.

RISLEY, JOHN SHUCKBURGH.—Ed. at Marlborough and Magd. Coll., Oxford (classical exhibitor); 2nd class classical mods., 1888; 2nd class jurisprudence, 1890; open studentship, Inns of Court, 1892; called to the bar, 1893; M.A. and B.C.L. 1894; author of "The Law of War," 1897; editor of Dale's "Clergyman's Legal Handbook," 7th edition; joint editor of Waterlow's "Companies Acts Manual," 11th and 12th editions; legal assistant, C.O., March, 1901.
RITCHIE, JOSEPH NORMAN, M.A.—Ed. at King's Coll., Windsor, N.S., 1857; Q.C., 1872; was recorder of Halifax; is lieut.-col. militia; asst. judge, N.S., 1885.
RITTER, GUSTAVE A., C.M.G. (1902).—Cik. to the judge of the sup. ct. of Mauritius, 1864; regis. V.-A. ct. 1873; marshal of V.-A. ct., 1882; unoff. nominated mem. of legis. coun., May, 1891.
RIVERS, GEORGE F.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv. and assigned to C.O., 16th July, 1902.
ROBB, DAVID.—2nd clk. asst. and shorthand writer, legis. assem., Natal, Feb., 1894; clk. asst. and Hansard reporter, legis. coun., Mar., 1894; has acted as sec. to govt. comans.

ROBERT, FREDERIC.—Admitted a solicitor of sup. ct. of Mauritius, 1885; ag. mag., Rodrigues, from Nov., 1895, to Dec., 1896, June, 1897, to Sept., 1898, and Apr., 1900, to June, 1901; dist. clk. and sen. civil status offr., Plaines Wilhema, Curepipe division, 15th July, 1901; ag. reglar.-gen., May to Oct., 1903; ag. mag., Rodrigues, 1st Apl., 1904; ag. dist. and stip. mag. of Mauritius, 20th July, 1904; ag. mag., Lesser Dependencies, 19th Sept., 1904.

ROBERTS, A. TEMPLE, M.A.—Ed. Charterhouse and Magd. Coll., Oxon.; 1st class in classical mods., and a 2nd class in classical finals; was "proxime accessit" for the Hartford scholarship, and was awarded the "Gaisford" Greek prize in 1879; asst. master in Winchester Coll., 1883; 1st class prof., Roy. Coll., Mauritius, 1886.

ROBERTS, CHARLES JAMES, C.M.G. (1882).—B. 1846; ed. at Sydney Gram. Schi.; alderman of Sydney, 1877; J.P., 1878; mayor and ch. mag., 1879; comanr. at the Sydney Internat. Exhibn., 1879-80; comanr. for N.S. Wales at Melbourne Exhibn., 1880-81; at Amsterdam Exhibn., 1883; at Calcutta Exhibn., 1883-84; and at Col. and Ind. Exhibn., 1886-7; M.L.A., 1886 and 1888; postm. gen., 1887; in the Parkes ministry; mem. Centennial Celebration Coman., N.S.W., 1888.
ROBERTS, J. C., F.I.P.S.—Offic. shorthand writ., Barbados, June, 1896.

ROBERTSON, ABRAHAM FAURE.—Ag. 1st clk. to C.C. and R.M., Swellendam, June, 1870; clk. audit office, Feb., 1872; 2nd clk. to C.C. and R.M., George (ag. as ch. clk.), May, 1872; clk. to C.C. and R.M., Prince Albert (2nd class clk.), Dec., 1872; ditto, Bredasdorp, July, 1874; asst. acctnt., treasy., Jan., 1876; ditto, col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1877; ditto, native affairs office, Apr., 1879; acctnt., July, 1879; acted as C.C. and R.M. at George in 1872, Prince Albert, 1873-74, Bredasdorp, 1874-76, Stellenbosch, 1878, and Robertson, 1878; sec. to the tender bd., Apr., 1877, to Apr., 1879; was mem. of the 2nd deptnl. comtce. on system of books and acts, in 1879; at request of impl. govt. acted as C.C. and R.M., Vryburg, Br. Bechuana land, Oct., 1885, to Apr., 1886, when he resumed duties as acctnt., native affairs office: C.C. and R.M., Colesberg, July, 1890; ditto, Robertson, 1890; Beaufort West, Oct., 1895; Ceres, Nov., 1900; Swellendam, Sept., 1902.

ROBERTSON, W. C. F.—Ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin; exhibitioner, 1886 and 1887; classical scholar, 1888; classical moderator, 1899; inspr. of schls., G. Coast, 1898; director of educn., 1899; ag. comptroller of cust., 1899; compiled census rept., 1901.

ROBINSON, FRANKLYN.—M.A. Durham Univ., cadet, S. Sttlmts., Oct., 1903; ag. head of Malay Coll., Malacca, Apr., 1903; demarcation offr., Malacca, 4th Dec., 1903; passed final exam. in Malay, Jan., 1904.

ROBINSON, GEORGE GEOFFREY.—Ed. at Rtoo and Magdalen Coll., Oxon.; 1st class classical mods., 1895; 1st class classical finals, 1897; B.A. 1897; fellow of AllSoul's, 1898; apptd. clk. in sec. off., G.P.O., after open compet. exam., Oct., 1898; M.A. 1899; 2nd cl. clk., C.O., Mar., 1899; ag. asst. priv. sec. to Lord Milner in S. Africa, 1901.

ROBINSON, J. ALEX.—J.P. and notary pub. for the col. (Newfild.); ed. Vict. Coll., Jersey, and New Kingwood, Bath; prin. of Carboner gram. schl., 1883-92; elected for Bonavista, 1897; col. sec. of Newfild. and mem. of exec. coun., 1897; accepted leadership of legis. coun., Dec., 1897; May, 1898, returned to H.A. for dist. of Trinity; mem. treasy. bd., 1898.
ROBINSON, T. M.—Postmr., telegraphist, gaoler, and offr. for the sale of ammunition, Ixopo, Natal, Nov., 1883; sub-distributor of stamps, Nov., 1885; J.P. for Ixopo div., 1902.

ROBINSON, W. A.—Ed. Appleby Schol. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; 1st classical honours, 1893; 1st cl. lit. hum., 1896; B.A. 1896; 2nd cl. mod. hist. 1896; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd class clk., C.O., Oct. 11, 1897.
ROBINSON, MAJOR W. ARTHUR.—Roy. Art., lieut., 1884; capt., 1893; temp. major, 1897; W. African Frontier Force, Dec., 1897.

ROBINSON, SIR WILLIAM, G.C.M.G. (1897), K.C.M.G. (1883), C.M.G. (1877), F.R.G.S.—B. 1836; clk. in the C.O., Jan., 1864; asst. junior, Nov. 1864; 3rd class, June, 1868; was priv. sec. to Mr. Herne Merivale from June, 1858, to May, 1860; to Lord Blachford from May, 1860, to Mar., 1862, when he was promoted to be an asst. senior; priv. sec. to the Right Hon. Edward Cardwell from Apr. to June, 1864; apptd., Nov. 1869, to represent the C.O. on the E. African Slave Trade Coman.; sen. clk., July, 1870; prtn. clk., Sept., 1872; one of the col. comtees for the Vienna Universal exhibn. of 1873, and special comsnr. for the crown cols., and supt. of the col. ct. at that exhibn.; edited the report upon the Br. cols. represented there; was one of the comtees for securing a permanent col. museum in London; lieut.-gov. of the Bahama Is., Oct., 1874, and gov., 1875; lieut.-gov., Windward Is., 1880; gov., 1881; gov. of Trinidad, 1885; gov. of Hong Kong, 1891; received thanks of bd. of trade, 1877, and of H.M.'s gov't., Nov., 1878, in reference to administration of Bahamas; received also thanks of H.M.'s gov't., Dec., 1887, for satisfactory settlement of Venezuelan difficulty arising out of the "Henrietta" and "Josephine" cases; ret. 1898.

ROBINSON, WILLIAM VALENTINE.—Ck. of the legis. assem. of Victoria; ed. at T. C. Lockwood's schl., Leeds, and at the Leeds Gram. Schl.; reporter for the "Leeds Intelligencer" (now "Yorkshire Post"); shorthand writer and occasional reporter for, as well as correspondent for and contributor to, various Yorkshire newspapers; prov. correspit. for the "Morning Herald," "Standard," and other London journals; left England for Melbourne, Oct., 1861, under engagement with the proprietors of "The Argus" to be one of their staff of parly. reporters; resig. his connection with that newspaper at the beginning of 1866, to assist in the production of official reports of the debates and proceedings of both houses of the Victorian parlmt.; clk. legis. assem., May, 1891.

ROBISON, CHAS. CARE.—Ed. Dulwich Coll. and St. Paul's, Stony Stratford; served with several banking companies, etc.; attached to treasury, Sarawak, 1901; supt. of cust., 1902; postmr.-gen., shipping-mast. and supt. of cust., 1904.
ROBITAILLE, THE HON. THEODORE.—Lieut.-gov. of prov. of Quebec, Canada, July, 1879, to 1884; senator, 1885.
ROBSON, WM. JOHN.—Asst. auditor-gen., Transvaal, Apr., 1901; aud.-gen., 1st Sept., 1902.
ROCHA, J. G. DA.—Entered the P.O., Hong Kong, Feb., 1865; acctnt., July, 1872; ag. asst. postmr., Sept., 1881, to May, 1883.
ROCKETT, E. D.—Apptd., after a compet. exam., clk. of the lower (now 2nd) div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O. Nov., 1880; supt. of printing dept., 1894.
ROCKS, M. J. W.—Formerly clk. to various distr. comsners., S. Leone afterwards gaoler, Gambia, 1891-8; 1st clk., traff. branch, S. Leone govt. rly., 1901.

RODEN, ROBERT B.—Called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1902; clk., public library, Antigua, 1880; 2nd clk. to the gov't., Leeward Is., 1881; acted on several occasions as ch. clk., and as clk. to the legis. and exec. coun., and as a dist. mag., Antigua; also acted as priv. sec. to gov', Leeward Is., in 1883, 1890, 1898 and 1896; mag., coroner and J.P., Nevis, 1890; mem. St. Kitts and Nevis legis. couns., 1891; mem., fed. leg. coun., Leeward Is., 1898-9; mag., Bridgetown, Barbados, 1903.
RODESSE, MAURICE.—Priv. sec. to several puisane judges and ch. justices, Straits Sttlmts., from 20th May, 1889; sheriff and dep. regier., Sing., 1st Jan., 1902.

RODGER, SIR JOHN PICKERGILL, K.C.M.G. (1904), C.M.G. (1899).—B. 1851; ed. at Eton and Ch. Ch., Oxon; called to the bar, Inner Tem. certif. of honour), Jan., 1877; ch. mag. and comsnr. of lands, Selangor, Sept., 1882; ag. Br. res., Mar., 1884; Br. res., Pahang, Sept., 1888; ag. res., Selangor, Apr. to Dec., 1891; res., Selangor, July, 1896; Brit. res., Perak, Sept., 1901; gov. Gold Coast Colony, 21st Sept., 1903.

RODGERS, JAMES LUKE.—Joined the Leeward Is. pol., Nov., 1877; served in Antigua, Dominica, and Montserrat; corporal, Jan., 1888; ret., Nov., 1889; 3rd cl. warder, Montserrat gaol, Dec., 1889; ag. gaoler, June, 1898.
RODWELL, CECIL HUNTER.—Ed. Eton and King's Coll., Camb., B.A., 1897; served on staff of high comsnr. for S. Africa since Dec., 1900; Imperial sec., Mar., 1904.
ROE, AUGUSTUS S.—Called to the bar, Perth, W. Australia, 1872; special comsnr., sup. ct., 1872; pol. mag., Perth, Oct., 1897.

ROGER, CAPT. ARCHIBALD.—Ensign 2nd roy. Lamark militia, Aug., 1859; gazetted to W. I. regt., Nov., 1860; lieut., Aug., 1861; capt., Oct., 1866; exchanged to the 1st batt., 17th regt., Feb., 1867; sold out of the service, Jan., 1870; was priv. sec. to Mr. Bayley, gov. of the Bahamas, Dec., 1860, to July, 1863, and fort adjt., Jamaica, Dec., 1863, to Sept., 1864; pol. mag. of the rural dist., St. Christopher, June, 1873; dist. mag., May, 1874.

ROGERS, THOMAS.—Employed by Cape gov't., 1874 to 1889; by Main Reef G.M.C., Transvaal, 1889-90; in naval dockyard, Simonstown, 1890-91; re-appd. by Cape gov't., Feb., 1891, to Mar., 1897; instr. of drills and diamond setter, late O.F.S., Mar., 1897, to Oct., 1899; ditto, Imperial military gov't., 1st Oct., 1900; ditto, civil gov't., O.R.C., 1st July, 1902.

ROHRWEGE, FRANK, C.M.G. (1899).—B. 1859; ed. at Radley and Brasenose; called to the bar, Inn. Tem., hilary, 1886; joint author of "Parochial Assessment Rules"; a dist. comsnr., Lagos, 1894; ag. Queen's advoc., 1894; D.C., E. dist., 1895; pol. mag., 1886; resigned.
ROLANDO, FRANCOIS ADOLPHE—Regiar. and offr. of the civil status, Seychelles, 6th Jan., 1890; crown atty., Mauritius, 10th Sept., 1897.

ROLLESTON, THE HON. WILLIAM.—Ed. at Rossall; grad. at Emmanuel Coll., Camb.; 2nd cl. in the classical tripes in 1855; was apptd. a mem. of an educn. coman. which framed the educational system adopted in the prov. of Canterbury, N.Z., 1863; prov. sec. of that prov. and mem. of the bd. of educn., 1864; under-sec., native dept., and instr. of native schls., 1865; elected supt. of the prov. of Canterbury, 1868; was again elected supt. at two consecutive elections, and held the office at the time of the abolition of the provs., 1876; sat in the col. partly from the year 1868 to 1887; is a mem. of the senate of the Univ. of N. Zealand; min. for educn., lands, and immigr., 1880; also, temporarily, min. for native affairs, 1881; min. for lands, mines, and immigr., 1882 and 1883, and till the accession of the Stout ministry in 1884; leader of the Opposition, 1891-93.

RONAYNE, THOMAS.—Ed. Wakefield, Yorks.; apprentice at Inchicore works, Dublin, of Gt. S. and W. rly. of Ireland; employed subsequently at Atlas loco. wks., Manchester; emig. to N. Z., 1875; employed in various capacities on N. Z. rlys. up to 1886, when apptd. res. and loco. engrnr. on Wellington section, where he effected improvements on the Rimutaka incline; subsequently transfd. as loco. engrnr. to Addington, and later to Greymouth, to put the Grey-Brunner line into working order. Is now gen. man., N. Z. rlys.

ROOKS, CHARLES JOHN.—Ch. clk., col. sec.'s off., Trinidad, and clk., leg. coun. (27th Nov., 1901), and supt., govt. printing off. (1st June, 1901); ed. at N. London Coll. Schl.; entd. civ. serv., 15th Aug., 1881; junior clk., col. sec.'s off., 1st Oct., 1881; has acted as ast. col. sec. and clk. ex. and leg. couns. on various occasions, 1892-1900; wrdn. of St. Ann's and Diego Martin (1893); mag. and wrdn. of Arima and Blanchisseuse (1900); mag. of St. Joseph, Tunapuna and Arouca (1900); has held the following apptmts.—hon. sec., coun. of managt. of Queen's Roy. Coll.; sec., spec. loan for P. W. and rds.; sec., civ. ser. widows' and orphans' fund; sec. to coman. on proposed lines for rly. extension; sec. to Central rd. bd.; joined Queen's West Rife Vols., 1879; holds coma. as maj. in infy. (vols.) of loc. forces; commanded loc. detachtn. to England, Diamond Jubilee, 1897; long serv. med.; has acted as col. A.D.C. to Sir W. Robinson, Sir F. N. Broome, Sir C. C. Knollys, and Sir H. E. H. Jerningham, and priv. sec. to Sir C. C. Knollys.

ROPER, H. B., I.S.O. (1903).—Ck. in treas., and sec. to govt. office, Griqualand W., 1873; ag. postmr.-gen., 1874; C.C. and R.M., Hay, 1876; ditto, Herbert, 1878; ag. R.M., Kimberley, 1881; ag. C.C. and registr. of deeds, 1882; chmn. of municipal and mining areas coman., 1882; inspr. of Sihune's Diggings, 1883; held inquiry into detective dept. of Kimberley, 1884; ch. of detective dept., and comanr. of pol., Kimberley, 1885; comanr. of pol., 1888; inspr.-gen. of gaols and convict stations, 1888.

ROPER, W. H.—Sub-inspr. of schls., Dominica, Aug., 1882; ag. dist. mag., coroner and registr. dist. F., June, 1886; mem. bl. of poor law guardians; nominated mem. legis. assem., 1886; ag. inspr.-gen. of schls., Leewards, July, 1887.

ROSBOROUGH, J.—Ed. at Trin. Coll., Dublin; B.A., Univ. of Dublin, 1893; capt. 6th R.I. Rifles, 1896; served with Gambia expdn., 1901; Somaliland, 1902-1904 (medal with two clasps); capt. 1st Batt., King's African Rifles, British Central African Prot.

ROSS, A. E. CLARENCE.—2nd clk., surg.-gen.'s off., Trinidad, 1875; clk. to dir. pub. wks., Feb., 1876; savings bk. clk., recvr.-gen.'s dept., Nov., 1880; acctnt., savings bk., Oct., 1882; sec. to trade and taxes coman., 1886; acctnt., recvr.-gen.'s dept., May, 1889; ch. mag., savings bk., Jan., 1901; P.M.G., Trinidad and Tobago, Sept., 1903.
ROSS, DAVID ALEXANDER.—Admitted to the bar, Quebec, 1884; Q.C., 1873; in 1878-9 mem. legis. assem. for co. of Quebec; mem. of the exec. coun., atty.-gen., and in 1887-8, mem. legis. coun.

ROSE, JAMES FREDERIC.—1st cls. certd. schl. mast., B. Guiana; ast. mast., normal schl. for 2 years; clk. to inspr. of schls., July, 1873, to May, 1877; jun. educnt. dist. offr., May, 1877, to 1882; sen. educnt. offr., 1882 to 1891; 2nd ast. inspr. of schls., 1891 to 1903; acted as 1st ast. ditto on various occasions, 1892-1903; 1st ast. ditto, July, 1903.

ROSS, G. W., LL.D.—Held seat in house of commons, Ontario, 1872 to 1883; min. of educn. in 1883; was inspr. of pub. schls. from 1871 to 1883; comanr. for Ontario and also for the Dominion to the Ind. and Col. exhibtn., 1896.

ROSS, JOHN KENNETH MURRAY.—Clerical ast. natve dept., Fiji, Sept., 1879; stip mag., Aug., 1880; ag. res. comanr., Colo West, Oct., 1882; mag. Tailevu and Ra, Apr., 1887; called to the bar, Fiji, Mar., 1888; ag. res comanr. Lomaiviti, Aug., 1888; proceeded in July, 1893, to Rarotonga and Penrhyn Is. as special judicial comanr., under the W.P. high comanr.; collr. of customs, ch. inspr. of charities, etc., Dec., 1896; ag. atty.-gen., May to Sept., 1897; called to the bar, Inner Tem., Jan., 1900; ag. ch. just., June, 1901, to Jan., 1902, and from May to Nov., 1902; ag. atty.-gen., Jan. to May, 1903; mem. exec. coun., Aug., 1903.

ROSS, O. B., B.A., Owens Coll., Manchester.—Cadet, S. Sttltn., Oct., 1897; ag. 3rd mag. Penang, Jan.-Nov., 1900; and from Feb., 1901; passed final exam. in Chinese, Jan., 1901; 4th mag. Sing., Jan., 1902; ag. dist. offr., Penang, Sept., 1902; passed cadet, 11th Feb., 1901; 4th mag. Singapore, 1st Jan., 1902; ag. 2nd ast. prot. of Chinese, Penang, 1903.

ROSS, S. B. C.—Ed. Uppingham Schl. (1889-93); Owens Coll., 1893-96; B.A., 1896; (Eng. lang., lit. honours); univ. schlr., 1896-7; prize for Roman Law, Constitutl. Law, 1898; cadet, F.M.S., 1899; sent to Canton to study Chinese, 1900; transfd. to Hong Kong, 1901; ag. registr., land ct., 1902 and 1903; ag. ast. postmr. gen., 1903; sec. to comanr. of inquiry into sup. ct. registry, 1903; ag. ast. P.M.G., 1903; ag. P.M.G., 1903-4; ag. ast. col. sec., and clk. of couns., 1904.

ROUILLARD, JEAN EDMOND.—Ed. Roy. Coll., Maur., and King Henry VIII.'s Schl., Coventry; called to bar, Mid. Tem., 1891; licent. laws, Paris, 1891; ag. dist. mag., Plaines Wilhelms, Maur., Oct., 1899; ag. crown pros., Feb., 1900; crown pros., leg. advr., pol. mag., etc., Seychelles, 4th Sept., 1900; mag., Rodrigues, July, 1902.
ROULEAU, CHARLES B.—Ed. at Laval normal schl., Quebec; advoc., Quebec, 1868; dist. mag., Ottawa, 1876; stip. mag., N.W. Territories, 1883; judge, N.W. Territories, 1887.

ROUND, FRANCIS R., M.A., C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1845; ed. at Marlborough and Balliol Coll., Oxford, where he obtained a double 2nd class in mods., and a 2nd in classics in final exam., 1868; apptd., after a compet. exam., clk. in the office of the S. of S. for the Cols., Feb., 1869; 2nd class clk., Sept., 1872; accompanied Sir P. Julyan to Mauritius in 1873 as sec. on special mission of inquiry into the civil estabmt. and gen. expenditure of that col.; ast. priv. sec. to the Rt. Hon Sir Michael Hicks Beach, Mar., 1873; priv. sec., 1st Apr., to 27th Apr., 1880; priv. sec. to Mr. Herbert, Jan., 1881; 1st class clk., and financial clk., Dec., 1881; on special service to Br. Bechuanaland and Mauritius, 1886; acted as col. sec., Mauritius, 1886-7; prin. clk., C.O., Mar., 1896.

ROUPELL, ERNEST P. S., D.S.O.—B. 1870; ed. at Marl. Coll.; 2nd lieut. R. Welsh Fus., 1888; transf. to Milford Haven div. R. Engrnr. Militia, 1891; cons. agt., Oil Rivers Prot., 1893; transf. to Oil River Irreg., 1894; served in Benin R. expdn., 1894 (mentd. in des.), and in minor operations; asst. comnrr., Anglo-German Bound. comnrr., 1896; political offr., 1897-8; divisional comnrr., Brass River div., So. Nig., 1898-1900; Ashanti expdn., 1900-1901; temporary 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 15th July, 1902.
ROUSSEAU, JAMES TOP, M.A., Edin.—Ent. civ. ser., 1882; asst. sub-intendant of crown lands, Trinidad, 1897; magistrate and warden of Tobago, 1899.

ROUTLEDGE, ROBERT M.—Ed. at Abdin Univ.; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, Nov., 1879; judge and mag., Falklands, June, 1891; judge, mag., and col. sec., Dec., 1891; stip. mag., Trinidad, 1893; acted as puisne judge on several occasions, 1894 to 1901; puisne judge, Mar., 1901; ag. ch. just., 1903.
ROWDEN, ERNEST GEORGE.—Special mast., roy. coll., Mauritius, Jan., 1898; head-master, Victoria schl., Seychelles, 27th Dec., 1901; inspr. of schl., Seychelles, 1st Mar., 1902.

ROWELL, THOMAS IRVINE, C.M.G. (1890), M.D., C.M., Aberdeen.—B. 1841; prin. civ. med. offr., S. Sttlmts., July, 1877; and health offr., port of Singapore, J.P.; acted as col. surg. on several occasions from May, 1868, to Mar., 1876; accompanied Sir W. Robinson on mission to invest king of Siam with G.C.M.G., Nov., 1878; regis. of births and deaths, Dec., 1882, and of Mohammedan marriages, Dec., 1883; pres. fisheries inquiry comnrr., 1887; pres. Singapore mun. coun., 1888-9; ret., 1890.
ROWLAND, J. W., C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1852; asst. col. surg., G. Coast Col., May, 1880; dist. comnrr., Lagos, 1887; col. surg., 1887; retired, 1897.

ROWLEY, THOS. WM.—Ed. Cheltenham Coll.; 2nd lieut., 2nd L.I. Regt., Rev. Guernsey Militia; attached to 106th Regt., L.I., at Guernsey, for two trainings, and passed school; lieut., Mar., 1879; passed prelim. exam., R.M.C., Sandhurst, Apr., 1880, and qualifying exam. for line, July, 1881; joined armed constabulary, N.Z., 1886; transf. to mtd. pol. Jan., 1886; and to N.Z. Artill., June, 1886; resig., Aug., 1888; inspr. of roads, Perak, Nov., 1889; asst. coll. land rev., mag. and J.P., Perak, Dec., 1890.

ROXBURGH, T. LAWRENCE.—Ed. Edin. Univ.; clk. petty sess., parish of St. Elizabeth, and asst. clk. to dist. ct., Jamaica, Jan., 1882; clk. of cta., St. Eliz., Apr., 1888; 1st class clk., col. sec. 'office, Feb., 1890; asst. col. sec., 2nd Jan., 1902; ag. col. sec., July to Nov., 1902.
ROZAN, JULES.—Spcl. clk., audit. off., Mauritius, 1st Jan., 1870; ch. clk., ditto, 19th June, 1897.

RUDOLPH, GERHARDUS M., C.M.G. (1882).—B. 1828; clk., and Dutch and Zulu interp., and postmr., Weenen, Natal, 1855; J.P., 1870; landdrost, Utrecht, and comnrr. of Swazi and Zulu borders, Jan., 1874; rendered valuable services to British commanders in Zulu war; mem. Transvaal-Swazi bdry. comm., 1880 (mentioned in despatches); R.M., Klip River, Natal, June, 1881; ret., 1899.

RUMSEY, ROBERT MURRAY, I.S.O. (1903).—Entered R.N., Sept., 1862; lieut., Apr., 1873; served on N. American, W. Indian, E. Indian, Pacific and Home stations; comnrr. of Volta dist. (i. Coast, May, 1880; ret. commander R.N., Jan., 1882; ag. asst. col. sec., Lagos, and dist. comnrr., Jan. to July, 1883; ag. asst. col. sec., G. Coast, Jan., Feb., 1884; dep. gov., Lagos, Mar. to Aug., 1884; asst. harbrmr., Hong Kong, 1884; harbrmr., 1888; mem. of legis. coun., 1892; mem. of exec. coun., 1897.
RUSHMER, JAMES.—Entered C.O., Oct., 1893; apptd., after exam., library attendant, 1st July, 1896.
RUSHTON, CHARLES BERKELEY.—Clt., comnrr. of rlyws. dept., W. Aust., Apr., 1896; ch. clk., July, 1896; ag. sec. to comnrr. of rlyws., 23rd Aug., 1901; sec. to ditto, 18th Dec., 1901.

RUSSELL, LIEUT.-GEN. SIR BAKER CREED, G.C.B., K.C.M.G. (1880), 13th Hussars.—B. 1837; was in command of the troops employed against Sikukuni in S. Africa; A.D.C. to Her Majesty Queen Victoria, 1880; asst. adjt. gen. for cavalry in the expdn. to Egypt, 1882, where he also commanded the 1st Cavalry brigade.
RUSSELL, CONRAD.—Ed. at Balliol coll., Oxford; apptd. asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, S. of S. for the Colonies, Apr., 1903; ditto to Hon. Alfred Lyttelton, Oct., 1903.

RUSSELL, H.C., B.A., F.R.S., C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1836; govt. astronomer, N.S. Wales, July, 1870; author of memoirs on Australian eclipse expdn., 1875, and of numerous other papers on astronomical subjects, and has designed a variety of instruments for use in the observatory.

RUSSELL, JAMES GEORGE, I.S.O. (1903).—Master of the sup. ct., S. Australia, Dec., 1878; regis. of V.-A.ct., of companies, of trades unions 1878; comnrr. of inland rev. and ag. regis. of probates, 1884; comnrr. of taxes, 1884; of stamps, 1886; comnrr. of insolvency, 1889; special mag. local ct., Adelaide, 1889; pres. of state bd. of conciliation, 1895; ag. judge of sup. ct., Mar. to Oct., 1897.

RUSSELL, ROBERT, JUN.—Natal exhibn. from Pietermaritzburg high schl. to Merton Coll., Oxford, 1885; B.A., M.A., and B.C.L., 1893; called to the bar by the In. Tem., 1893; acted as clk. in atty.-gen.'s office, Natal, 1891; asst. inspr. of schl., 1892; sec. to the agt.-gen. for Natal in London, Dec., 1893; acted as priv. sec. to the Rt. Hon. H. Escombe, ex-premier of Natal, during his visit to England, 1897.

RUSSELL, WESTERN FRANCIS, B.A.—Ed. at Cranbrook schl. and Christ Church, Oxford; 2nd cls. math. mods.; 1st cls. math. finals, 1884; 1st cls. prof. (math.) Royal coll., Mauritius, Aug., 1886; ag. sen. prof., 12th Apr. to 28th Nov., 1894; and 1st Aug., 1901, to 17th Oct., 1902; ag. rect., 18th Oct. to 30th Nov., 1902; ag. sen. prof., 27th Mar., 1903, to 8th Feb., 1904; ag. asst. col. sec., 24th Feb., 1904.

RUTHERFORD, G., C.M.G. (1888).—B. 1818; landing waiter, Grenada, 1841; landing survr., St. George's, 1845 to 1847; sub-collr. of customs, Falmouth, Jamaica, 1850; collr. of customs, Natal, 1853; also comptroller of customs and navigation laws, 1854; ret., 1889.
RUTLEDGE, THE HON. SIR A., KT. BACH. (1902).—B. 1843; barrister, Queensland, 1878; mem. of legis. assem. since 1878; atty.-gen. from 1883 to 1888.

RYAN, SIR CHARLES LISTER, K.C.B. (1887), C.B. (1881), J.P., Berks.—B. 1831; apptd. asst. examr., impl. audit office, Mar., 1831; jun. clk. in H.M.'s treasy, Whitehall, Feb., 1852; was priv. sec. to Mr. Disraeli, Sir Stafford Northcote, and Mr. Gladstone; apptd. to audit office as sec., May, 1865; asst. comptroller and auditor, Mar., 1873; comptroller and audr.-gen., June, 1888; was hon. auditor of certain crown cols. ag. on behalf of H.M.'s S. of S. for the cols.; ret. 1896.

SABBEN, FREDERICK.—Ed. Royal Naval Sch., govt. agt., Fiji, July, 1882; inspr. of imigrts., Jan., 1883; boarding offr., Nov., 1883; bondkeeper, 2nd class clk., and magazine-keeper, Jan., 1884; inspr. of pol., and offr. in charge of gaol, May, 1886; gaoler, Aug., 1887; supt. prisons, Aug., 1903.
SALOMONS, SIR JULIAN EMANUEL, K.C., KT. BACH. (1891).—B. 1834; vice-pres., exec. coun., N.S. Wales, 1891; resig., 1893; mem. of legis. coun.; London agent-gen., N.S.W., 1899-1900.

SAMUEL, SILVERIC, L.—Ed. at St. Gregory’s Rom. Cath. gram. schl., Lagos; meser. and copyt., educ. off., Oct., 1897; clk. to inspr. of schls., Mar., 1899; sec. to comte. for exhibtn. of schl. wks., 1902 and 1903; clk. and interpreter to trav. comsnr., Ilesha interior dept., Feb., 1904; 3rd cls. clk., govr.’s off., Aug., 1904.

SAMUELSON, S. O.—Ag. regisr. native high ct., Natal, Feb., 1881, to Apr., 1882; ag. Zulu interp., sup. ct., May to June, 1882; clk. and Zulu interp. to R.M., Umlazi div., Oct., 1882; Zulu interp., sup. ct., Feb., 1888; under-sec. for native affairs, 1893; mem. of civ. ser. bd.; 1894-1901: J.P. for colony, 1894.
SANDERS, WM. RUTHERFORD BOGLE.—Cadet, Ceylon, 4th Oct., 1879; pol. mag., 1st Jan., 1888; dist. judge, 1st May, 1896; ag. govt. agt., N. prov., 21st Apr., 1902; officer of cls. II., 18th July, 1902.

SANDERSON, JAMES.—Clik. to ch. just., Barbados, 1867; clk. to gen. bd. of health, Barbados, Apr., 1871, also clk. to quarantine bd. in 1880; sofr., atty., and proctor, Barbados, in 1873; was deleg. for Barbados to quarantine confce. at Demerara in 1888; govt. dir. on bd. of Barbados rly. co. in 1891.

SANGSTER, G. H.—Served in 2nd Dragoons and 91st Highlanders; asst. supt. pol., Gambia, 1897; ag. supt. of pol. inspr. of prisons, Gambia, 1897; asst. inspr., S. Leone frontier force, 1898; Mendi expdtn., 1898, mentioned in despatches; ag. dist. comsnr., Karene, 1899; inspr., S. Leone F.F., June, 1899; W. Africa meda and clasp, S. Leone, 1898-9; Royal Humane Soc.’s bronze medal, 1898; asst. dist. comsnr., S. Leone, Dec., 1899-1900; travelling comsnr., Gambia, Nov., 1900; field treas., Gambia field force, 1901; special mention in despatches; priv. sec. to ag. govr. of Gambia.

SANGUINETTI, FREDERICK S., I.S.O. (1903).—1st class clk. in col. sec.’s office, Jamaica, Aug., 1878; clk. in office of main road comsnrs., Sept., 1863; clk. in office of exec. comte., Feb., 1864; 2nd class clk. in financial sec.’s office, 1866, and now in col. sec.’s office; was sec. to a coman. apptd. “to inquire into the condition of the juvenile population of Jamaica,” 1877; one of the compilers of the “Handbook of Jamaica, 1881;” ag. comsnr., Turks and Caicos Islda., 1883; and again, 1886; ag. col. sec., treas., and pol. mag., Falklands, July, 1890-1; admstr. of the govt. for a short time in 1891; ag. asst. col. sec., Jamaica, 1894-7; ret. on pension, 1898; comsnr. of Cayman Is., 1898.

SANKEY, JOHN.—Customs clk., England, Dec., 1886; excise offr., Jan., 1887; 2nd div. clk., June, 1887; ag. asst. P.O. survr., 1897; acctnt. dept. of posts and telegraphs, O.R.C., Sept., 1901; ch. acctnt. ditto, July, 1903.

SARTORIUS, MAJ.-GEN. REGINALD WILLIAM, C.M.G., V.C. (1874).—B. 1841; 6th regt. of Bengal cavalry; apptd. to the order of St. M. and St. G. for service in the Ashanti war, 1873-4, under Sir J. H. Glover, and received the V.C. for an act of conspicuous gallantry in saving a wounded sergt.-maj. of Houssas.

SARWAR, HAFIZ GHULAM.—Cadet, S. S., Sept., 1897; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, Jan. to June, 1900; and from Sept., 1900, to Nov., 1901; passed final exam., Chinese, July, 1900; 3rd mag., Sing., 29th Apr., 1902; ag. sherif, deputy-regar., and asst. official assignee, Penang, May, 1902.

SAUER, THE HON. J. W.—Sec. for native affairs in Scanlen ministry, Cape pol., May, 1881; ret. 1884; col. sec., July, 1890, to Jan., 1896; comsnr. of pub. wks. in the Schreiner ministry, Oct., 1898, to 1900; mem. for George, 1904.

SAUNDERS, CHARLES J., B.A.—Ed. Merchant Taylors, and St. John’s Coll., Oxon.; cadet, S. S., 1891; passed final exam. in Chinese, Feb., 1896; ag. 2nd asst. prot. of Chinese, Feb., 1896; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, May, 1895; ag. dist. offr. Penang, Mar., 1897; Malacca, May, 1897; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Aug., 1897; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, and supt. of Ind. immigr., Penang, June, 1898; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Aug., 1898; also ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, Sept., 1898; 2nd asst. prot. of Chinese, Penang, Aug., 1898; still ag. at Singapore till Feb., 1899; ag. 2nd mag., Singapore, May, 1900; ag. offl. assignee, S. S., Jan., 1901; sherif, dep. regar., supreme court, and asst. official assignee, Penang, May, 1902; munic. comsnr. for Singapore, Sep., 1903; asst. prot. of Chin., Penang, but cont. to act as offl. assignee, Sing., May, 1904.

SAUNDERS, C. J. R., C.M.G. (1896).—B. 1857; clk. of the ct. and Zulu interp. to the R.M., Alexandra co., Natal, Nov., 1876; to the R.M., Durban, 1878; to the R.M., Alexandra, Nov., 1879; ag. ch. clk., office of sec. for native affairs during Zulu war, for the purpose of registering native levies, Apr., 1883; Zulu interp. sup. and circuit cte., Dec., 1883; ag. regisr. to the native high ct., Apr., 1884; asst. admstr. native law, Umlazi, Jan., 1886; ditto Inanda, June, 1886; proceeded to Amatongaland to ratify treaty with Queen of that country, Sept., 1887 (mentioned in despatches); R.M., Eshowe, Zululand, Jan., 1888; apptd. to define bndry. between Amatongaland and Zululand (again mentioned in despatches), July, 1889; asst. comsnr. and R.M., Lubombo, Aug., 1890; R.M., Eshowe, 1891; spec comsnr. to territories of Samban, Umbegiza, and Mohlatene, N. of Zululand, Apr., 1895; ch. mag. and civil comsnr., prov. of Zululand, 1897; col. govt. representative, Zululand delim. coman. 1902.

SAUNDERS, SIR FREDERICK RICHARD, K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1838; asst. survr., Ceylon, 1857; asst. agt., Trincomalee, July, 1862; asst. to govt. agt. at Kandy, Aug., 1865; dist. judge, etc., Ratnapura, Nov., 1865; asst. to the govt. agt., W. Prov., at Ratnapura, Oct., 1867; inspr. gen. of prisons, 1874; agt., E. Prov., 1878; agt. W. Prov., 1879; M.L.C., 1879; ag. audr.-gen., 1890; treas. and comsnr. of stamps, Oct., 1890; ag. audr.-gen., 1896; ret., 1898.

SAUNDERS, JAMES B.—Harbrmr., Trinidad, Sept., 1894; also J.P. and mag. and regisr. of births and deaths for waters of the col.; coroner for Gulf of Paria; mem. of quarantine bd., and shipping master, etc.
SAUVAGE, DANIEL.—Govt. pilot and asst. port offr., Seychelles, Feb., 1894, and port offr., May, 1897.
SAVAGE, W. D.—Apptd. bookkeeper, funds dept., treasy., O.R.C., June, 1888.

SAXTON, GEORGE SHADWELL.—Ed. at Clifton Coll.; writer, Caylon civ. ser., 1878; pol. mag. Haputale, 1887; appt. gov't. agent, Mannar, 1887; Chilaw, 1888; dist. judge, Tangalla, 1890; apptd. gov't. agent, Matale, 1890; Matara, 1890; Kalutara, 1902; gov't. agent, Sabaragamuwa, 1902.

SAYER, W. F.—Ed. Univ. Coll. Schi., Lond.; solr., 1879; bar. and solr. in W. Australia, 1891; sec. law dept., Mar., 1891; ag. comsnr. of titles, Mar., 1899; atty.-gen., 1901; parly. draftsman, 1902; crown solr., Dec., 1902; atty.-gen., 1904.

SCANLEN, THE HON. SIR THOMAS CHARLES, K.C.M.G. (1884).—B. 1834; mem. house of assem., Cape, since 1869; dep. sheriff for Cradock, 1875-66; J.P. for Cradock, May, 1869; mem. of the divisional coun. of Cradock, 1867-77; comsnr. and chmn. of the municipality of Cradock, 1870-77; apptd. by sup. ct. examr. for admission of attorneys and notaries, May, 1887; mem. of the exec. coun., May, 1881; atty.-gen. and premier, May, 1881, to June, 1882; col. sec. and premier, July, 1882, to May, 1884; proceeded to England to negotiate loan for pub. wks., Oct., 1883; as a priv. mem., carried through the Executors and Trustees Accounts Act, 1873; Titles Registration and Derelict Lands Act, 1881; prepared and carried during term of office, General Loans Act, 1881; Postal Act, 1882; Lands Clauses Act, 1882; Municipal Act, 1882; Police Regulation Act, 1882; Aliens' Naturalisation Act, 1883; Public Health Act, 1883; Liquor Licensing Act, 1883; legal adviser, Rhodesia, 1894; legal adviser, Rhodesia Administrative Staff, 1895.
SCARTE, WILLIAM BAIN.—Dep. min. of agricnl. and statistics, Canada, Dec., 1895; dep. commnr. of patents, Mar., 1897.

SCHOLITZ, CLERKE BURTON.—Comsnr., Lichtenburg, Transvaal, 1877; lieut., Bechuana-land field force on Sir C. Warren's staff; mem. of commn. on Stellaland claims, 1886; ch. clk. and asst. mag., Vrijburg, 1888; R.M., Kuruman, 1889.

SCHOMBURGK, OTTO HEINRICH.—Ck., engrn.-in-chief's office, S. Aust., Jan., 1874; ck., ch. sec.'s office, Aug., 1877; ck., regisr.-gen. of deeds office., July, 1880; ck., sheriff's office, Nov., 1882; dep. sheriff, 1891; also returning offr. W. Adelaide, Jan., 1893, to June, 1902; sheriff, July, 1903; also Commonwealth electoral offr., for the state of S. Aust., Aug., 1903; major, comdg. field batt. of artillery (retired).

SCHOOLES, HENRY RAWLINS PIPON.—Ed. Marl., called to the bar, Mid. Tem., Hilary, 1873; admitted to bar, Leeward Is., May, 1873; mem. for Sandy Point legis. assem. of St. Kitts, Mar., 1874, to Apl., 1876; ag. atty.-gen., Leeward Is., Apl. to Sept., 1876; re-elected mem. for Sandy Point, Jan., 1877; mem. of new legis. coun. of St. Kitts, 1879, and elected by the legis. coun. as sen. mem. for St. Kitts of fed. coun. of the Lee-ward Is.; June, 1880, atty.-gen., Br. Honduras; June, 1881, apptd. provisionally as ch. just. of Br. Honduras; atty.-gen., Grenada, Apl., 1883; ditto, Jamaica, 1896.

SCHREIBER, COLLINGWOOD, C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1831; emigrated from England, 1852; entered service of Hamilton and Toronto Rly., and became asst. engrn., 1852; suptding. engrn. of wks., N. Rly. of Canada, 1860-64; div. engrn., Nova Scotia govt. service, on Truro and Picton Rly., 1864-5; survey engrn., Dominion govt. service, on inter-col. rly., 1886; suptding. engrn., N. Brunswick E. Rly., 1869-71; ditto, inter-col. railway, 1871-3; ch. engrn. of govt. railways in operation, 1873-8; also gen. man., 1878-80; also engrn.-in-chief, Canadian Pacific Rly., 1880; now engrn.-in-chief, govt. rlys. and dep. min. of rlys. and canals.

SCHREINER, THE HON. W. P., C.M.G. (1890), K.C.—B. 1857; ed. S. A. Coll., Cape town, Camb. Univ., and London; advoc. of sup. ct., Cape, 1882; legal adviser to high commr., 1887-93; atty.-gen. in Mr. Rhodes's ministry, 1893; mem. of Jamieson Raid Comtee.; premier and col. sec., 1898-1900.
SCOFFHAM, W. H.—Apptd., after compet. exam., ck. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to census office, 20th Feb., 1901; transf'd. to C.O., 17th Dec., 1902.

SONCE, HERBERT W.—Trin. Coll., Camb.; B.A. math., tripos, 1886; M.A. 1897; asst. master, Faversham Gram. Schi.; Godolphin Schi., Hamersmith, and Grocers' Co.'s Schi., Hackney, 1887 to 1893; asst. mr., Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana, Apr., 1893; asst. inspr. of schls., July, 1895; ag. inspr., May to Nov., 1898; June to Nov., 1900; July, 1902, to Apl., 1903; inspr. of schls. and off. visitor of Onderneeming Indust. Schi., Apl., 1903; mem. bd. of agric. for educntl. matters; examr. for prelim. exam. for govt. clerical serv.

SCOTT, J. H.—Mag., Tembuland, Aug., 1876; capt. in Tembu levies, Sept., 1877; ag. ch. mag., Tembuland, Apr. to July, 1878; ch. clk., native affairs dept., Oct., 1878; sec. to atty.-gen., on special duty to N. Border during war there, Feb. to Apr., 1879; ag. special commnr., N. Border, May to July, 1879; special commnr. and special mag. of N. Border, July, 1879; res. commnr., Pondoland, July, 1888.

SCOTT, RALPH.—B.A. Lond. Univ.; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1895; ag. collr. of land rev., Singapore, Mar., 1897; passed final exam. in Malay, Sept., 1897; ag. dist. offr., Malacca, Sept., 1897; confirmed, Apl., 1898; apptd. to Cl. IV., Jan., 1903; ag. dist. offr., Dingdings, Apl., 1903.

SCOTT, THE HON. RICHARD WILLIAM, K.C.—Called to the bar of Upper Canada, 1884; created a Q.C., 1867; sat in the legis. assem. of Canada from 1867 to 1873, and while there carried the Separate School Act of Upper Canada; returned to the Ontario assem. at confedn., 1867; ret. on his entrance into the Dominion Govt., Nov., 1873; called to the senate, Mar., 1874; was speaker of the Ontario assem. from 7th to 21st Dec., 1871; mem. of exec. coun. and commnr. of crown lands for same prov., from later date until Nov., 1873, when sworn of the Queen's privy coun.; S. of S. of Canada, Jan., 1874; ret. on resignation of Mackenzie govt., Oct., 1878; is now a senator; was the framer of the Canada Temperance Act, known as the Scott Act; joined Sir W. Laurier's govt. as S. of S., June, 1896.
SCOTT, ROBT.—Actnt. to director, army telegraphs, O.R.C., July, 1900; asst. acctnt., dept. of posts and telegraphs, Aug., 1902.
SCOTT, WALTER.—Apptd., after compet. exam., ck. of the lower (now 2nd) div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., 22nd Jan., 1883; promoted to higher grade, 2nd div., 3rd Nov., 1896.
SCROGGIE, J. A.—Apptd. 1st ck., treasy., O.R.C., 20th May, 1900.
SCROGGS, Lieut. Harold Chas., R.N.—Harbour mast. and supt. of merc. marine, Mauritius, 23rd June, 1903.
SEALY, A.—Pol. mag. of dist. B, Barbados, Sept., 1873.

SEALY, Frank Dean.—Supernum. clk., imigrn. dept., Br. Guiana, Jan., 1884; 4th class clk., Apr., 1891; 3rd class, Jan., 1893; has acted as ch. clk.; 2nd class clk., Apr., 1895; ag. imigrn. agt., 1896 and 1898.

SEALY, Gordon Courland Miller.—Clk. to the atty.-gen., Tobago, Jan., 1882; clk. in col. sec.'s dept., 1883; acted as confid. clk. to the admstr. and clk. of couns., 1883 to 1886; 3rd clk., surg.-gen.'s dept., Br. Guiana, 1887; 2nd clk., 1887; has acted as ch. clk.

SEALY, Herbert Hamilton.—Confid. clk. to gov., and to exec. and legis. couns., Tobago, Jan., 1878; ag. audr. and regist. of deeds and of the sup. cts. from Apr. to May, 1882, and June, 1882, to Feb., 1883; ag. pol. mag., Windward dist., Oct., 1883, to Jan., 1884; is a J.P.

SEALY, J. Ernest Cameron.—2nd clk., audit and registry dept., Tobago, Feb., 1888; clk. to commrs. and financial bd., Nov., 1890, and Oct., 1893; sec. to bd. of educn., Sept., 1892; ch. clk. treas., June, 1893; postmtr. of Tobago, Dec., 1895.

SEARCY, Arthur.—Boarding offr., cust., S. Aust., 1873; clk., cust., 1874; clk., audit off., 1875; clk., cust., 1876; tide inspr. and measurer of ships, cust., 1889; correspdg. clk., cust., and sec., marine bd., 1890; ag. dep. comsurr. of taxes and stamps, 1891; correspdg. clk., cust., and sec., marine bd., 1891; sec., cust., and sec., marine bd., 1894; dep. comsurr. of taxes and stamps, 1896; comsurr. of ditto, 1897; president, marine bd., 1902; also inspr. of kerosine, 1902; also comsurr. of trade marks and regist. of copyrights, 1904.

SEARLE, H. L.—Served in 12th Sudanese Egyptian army, medal and clasp, Khedive's star and clasp; apptd. to Royal Niger Co.'s constab., 1892; took part in the expeds. of 1892-3; apptd. to Niger Coast Protectorate force, Sept., 1893; present at Brass River expedit., 1895 (med. and clasp); Cross River expedit., Aug., 1895; Qua Iho expedit., 1895; Benin City expedit., 1897 (clasp); apptd. collr. of customs and postmtr.-gen. Southern Nigeria, Feb., 1900; Aro expedit., 1901-2 (medal with clasp).
SEATON, G. L.—Apptd., after exam., 3rd cl. mess., C.O., 17th May, 1899; 2nd cl., 18th Feb., 1900; King's mess., 10th June, 1904.

SECCOMBE, G. S., L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), 1874.—Mem. Medico-Psychological Assoc.; sen. asst. med. offr. and dep. med. supt. of Caterham Asylum, England, 1874; med. supt. of lun. asyl., and med. offr., roy. gaol, Trinidad, Sept., 1882.
SEDDON, The Right Hon. Richard J., P.C. (1897).—B. 1845; premier of N. Zealand, 1893; represented the col. in London in the Diamond Jubilee, 1897.
SEDGEWICK, Robert, K.C.—Dep. min. of just. and solr. of Indian aff airs, Canada, 1888; now a puisne judge, sup. ct.
SEE, The Hon. Sir John, K.C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1845; mem. legis. assem., N.S.W.; treas. in Dibbs's ministry, 1891-4; ch. sec. in Sir W. Lyne's ministry, 1899; premier, 1901.

SEIGNORET, C. A.—Supernumerary treas., Dominica, 1890; sec. to special yaws comm., Mar., 1891, to Sept., 1892; ch. clk., regist.'s office and clk. pub. market, 1892; asst. sec. to Sir R. Hamilton during his inquiry in Dominica, 1893; govt. offr. treasy., 1895; sec. bd. of health and quarantine, 1895; ag. clk., admstr.'s office and exec. coun., 1895; ag. treas., Dominica, July, 1901.
SEIGNORET, G. B.—Clk. to postmr., Dominica, 1889; govt. offr., treasy., 1893; ch. clk., regist.'s off., 1895; ag. postmr., 1896-7; clk. govt. offr., Roseau, 1897; govt. offr., treasy., 1901.

SELBORNE, 2nd Earl of (creat. 1882), Viscount Wolmer, Baron Selborne (U.K.), P.C. (1900), The Rt. Hon. Wm. Waldegrave Palmer, B. 1859; ed. at Winchester and at Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A. (1st cl. hist.), 1882; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Childers, Chanc. of Excheq., 1882-85; was mem. of parlmt. for E. Hants, 1885-92, and for W. Edinburgh City, 1892-95; succeeded his father, 1895; Under S. of S. for Cols., June, 1895; chmn. Pacific cable comttee., 1896; mem. of Pacific cable comttee., 1899; first lord of the admiralty, Nov., 12th, 1900.

SELBY-LOWNDES, R. C. W.—Ed. at I. of Wight Coll. and Tamworth Coll.; served in B.S.A. pol. during Mashonaland rebellion, 1896-7; chartered company's civil serv., 1898-1900; served during S. Africa war, Rhodesia regt., Rhodesian field force, and French's scouts, 1900-1902 (medals: Mashonaland, 1897, Queen's medal, S. A. war, 4 clasps, King's medal, 2 clasps).

SELLS, H. C., B.A. (Oxon.).—Cadet, S. Sttlms., Oct., 1897; ag. priv. sec. to gov., Nov., 1898, to Nov., 1899; ag. 4th mag., Singapore, Jan., 1900; passed fin. exam. in Malay, Apr., 1900; ag. dist. offr., prov. Well., May, 1901; supt., money order branch and govt. savings bank, Sing., May, 1902; head of Malay coll., Malacca, July, 1904.

SELWYN, Alfred Richard Cecil, LL.D., F.R.S., F.G.S., C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1824; on the geol. survey of Gt. Brit., 1845-52; dir., geol. survey, Victoria, 1853-68; dir., geol. and nat. hist. survey, Canada, 1869-95.

SEMPER, D. H.—Clk. to atty.-gen., Leeward Islands, 1893; dep. coroner for dist. B., Oct., 1898; ag. Federal treas., Oct., 1902; fed. treas., Mar., 1903; acted as clk., leg. coun., Antigua, Dec., 1902; ditto, ex. coun., Montserrat, Mar., 1903.
SEN, Nirmal Chandria.—Cadet, S. Sttlms., 27th Nov., 1903.
SENEVIRATNE, Alexander de Alwis.—Mem. of legis. coun., Ceylon; advoc. of the sup. ct.; mem. mun. coun., Colombo, and coun. of legal educn.; chmn. Ceylon national assoc.

SENIOR, Bernard.—Clk. to bindry. coman. (Redistribution of Seats Act), 1884; attached to Scotol educn. dept., 1885, and again 1888; priv. sec. to Sir F. R. Sandford, under-secr. for Scotland, 1887; 1st class clk., col. sec.'s office, G. Coast, 1888; local auditor, Lagos, Aug., 1889; ditto, G. Coast, Dec., 1889; ditto, Br. Bechuana land, Feb., 1894; ditto, Cyprus, 1895; and-gen., O. R. C., Sep., 1902; is also chrmn. of tender bd.; ag. col. treas., O. R. C., from 12th Aug., 1902; aud. gen., Oct., 1902; ag. col. treas., 12th Aug., 1903, to 15th Feb., 1904; M.L.C., May, 1904; author of "A Guide to the examination of Government Accounts in Cyprus."

SETH, Arathoon.—Called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, June, 1893; Hindustani interp., magistracy, Hong Kong, Feb., 1868, to Dec., 1869 (in the serv. of the P. & O. Co. in the interim); re-entered magistracy as 3rd clk., Sept., 1872; 2nd clk., Sept., 1873; 1st clk., Feb., 1875; clk. of couns., and ch. clk., C.S.O., Feb., 1881; valuer under the "Rating Ord., 1875," 25th June, 1881, to 19th Mar., 1883; J.P., Jan., 1882; supt. of the opium revenue, Aug., 1883, to Feb., 1885; ditto, imports and exports, June to Oct., 1887; sec. to bd. under "Taipinghain resumption order," 1894-1895 (recd. thanks of govt.), sec. to insanitary properties comsn., July, 1896; ag. deputy land offr., Nov., 1894, to Jan., 1895; ditto, Mar., 1895, to Nov., 1896; dep. regisr. and appraiser, sup. ct., and comnr. for oaths, Mar., 1895; comnr. for taking acknowledgments of married women, Oct., 1896; sec. squatter's bd.; offl. recvr. in bankruptcy, Mar., 1895, to Nov., 1896; ag. asst. regisr.-gen., July to Sept., 1897; ag. regisr., sup. ct.; ag. land offr.; ag. regisr. of companies; offl. admstr., and offl. trustee, 15th Dec., 1898, to 26th Jan., 1900; ag. regisr., sup. ct., 1903-04; confirmed, 1904.

SETH, G. G.—B.A., Calcutta; cadet, S. S., Nov., 1901; ag. sheriffl, Singapore, Jan., 1903; passed final exam. in Malay, Jan., 1903; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, 5th Jan., 1904; ag. dep. regisr., sup. ct., Singapore, July, 1904.

SHAND, CHAS. ARTHUR.—Mem. local assembly, Antigua, 1876-1891; ditto, federal coun- 1877-1891; J.P., Antigua, 1885; mem. ex. coun., Antigua, 1887; chrmn. P.L. guardians and of several local boards, Antigua; dist. mag., Nevis, Feb., 1903; and offl. mem. leg. coun., St. Kirits-Nevis.

SHARE, HAMNET HOLDITCH.—Entered Royal Navy, 1880; served in H.M.S. "Tourmaline" and numerous other ships (Egypt, medal, 1882, and Khedive's Bronze Star); sec. to Comdr-in- Chief, Pacific station, 1899-1900; ditto, Australia station, 1901-1903; priv. sec. to gov. of N. S. Wales, May, 1903.

SHARPE, SIR ALFRED, K.C.M.G. (1903), C.B. (1897).—B. 1853; H.M.'s vice-consul, Nyasaland, 14th Feb., 1891; ditto, for territories under British influence North of the Zambesi, 18th Feb., 1893; consul for same territories, 1st Feb., 1894; ag. comnr. and consul-gen. at Zomba, 7th May, 1894 to 3rd May, 1895; given rank of deputy comnr., 1st Apl., 1896; and again acted as comnr. and consul-gen., 16th Apl., 1896 to 14th July, 1897; comnr. and consul-gen., for territories under Brit. infi. N. of Zambesi, 15th July, 1897; recd. Jubilee medal, 1897; comnr., commdr.-in-chief, and consul-gen., B. Cent. Africa Prot., 1st Jan., 1902; recd. Coronation medal, 1902.

SHARPE, W. S., C.M.G. (1900).—B. 1860; capt. 4th batt. R.I. Rifles, 1893; major, Oct., 1903; instr., Sierra Leone frontier force, Mar., 1894; 3rd comnr. to Anglo-French bdry. coman. and delimited frontier from Atlantic coast to Little Mola River, 1896; dist. comnr., Karene dist., Jan., 1897; F.R.G.S., and J.P. for the col.; transfd. to N. Nigeria as asst. res., Dec., 1900; apptd. 2nd class res., Oct., 1901.

SHAW, CAPT. BERNARD VIDAL.—Late capt. rifle brigade; priv. sec. to dir. of crim. investig., London; comnr. of pol., Capetown, 1882 to 1888 (office abolished); visiting mag., Breakwater convict station (Cape); author of "Epitome of the Criminal Law of the Cape;" sub-imigr. agt., Br. Guiana, 1889; supt. H.M.'s penal stintm., Massarum, Jan., 1894; stip. mag., 1897.
SHAW, COURTENAY.—Clk. in off. of sec. to Transvaal adminstrn., Mar., 1901; ag. priv. sec. to ditto, Mar. to June, 1902; priv. sec. to col. sec., Transvaal, July, 1902.

SHAWE, HENRY BENJAMIN.—Clk. at Clanwilliam, Cape Colony, 12th May, 1883; col. secy.'s off., Cape Town, Dec., 1883; sen. examnr. of acctnts., 14th Apl., 1890; chief ditto, 1st July, 1894; acctnt., 10th July, 1895; acctntg. offr., 31st Mar., 1899; ch. clk. and acctng. offr., 1st Mar., 1901; ag. under col. sec., 1st Apl. to 30th Sept., 1901; asst. under col. sec., 1st Apl., 1902.

SHEA, THE HON. SIR AMBROSE, K.C.M.G. (1883).—B. 1818; mem. of assem., Newfoundland, 1860-87; deleg. to Washington on reciprocity treaty, 1854; speaker, 1855-61; organised plan for water supply for St. John's, 1860; deleg. to London on French fishery treaty, 1861; deleg. to Quebec on confederation of provs., 1864; and on trade convention, 1865; comnr. at fishery exhib., London, 1883; deleg. to Washington on fishery treaty, 1885; deleg. to col. confce., 1887; gov., Bahamas, July, 1887-94.
SHEA, THE HON. SIR E. D., KT. BACH. (1902). —Col. sec. and clk. of coun., Newfoundland, Jan., 1874; cashier of savings bank, and pres. of legis. coun., Feb., 1886.
SHEA, THE HON. GEORGE.—M.H.A., Ferryland (Newfd.), 1889-93; again in 1897; mem. exec. coun. (without portfolio), 1897; mem. of treasy. bd., 1898.
SHELFORD, SIR WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1904), C.M.G. (1901), M.I.C.E.—B. 1834; consulting engnr. for railways to the Crown agents for the cols.

SHENTON, THE HON. SIR GEORGE, KT. BACH. (1893), J.P.—B. 1842; mem. of the legislature of W. Australia, 1870-74, 1875-90; on the introduction of respon. govt., col. sec. in the first ministry formed by Sir John Forrest; pres. of the legis. coun., Oct., 1892 on the death of Sir T. Campbell, Bart.; is senior mem. of the metropolitan prov.; has been mayor of Perth on eleven different occasions.

SHEPPARD, WALTER SYDNEY.—B.A., M.B., B. Ch., Cantab., civil surg. in charge of H.M.'s troops, N.E. dist., Eng., May, 1898 to May, 1899; spec. plague med. offr., India, May, 1899 to Nov., 1900; house surg., gen. hosp., Singapore, 19th Nov., 1900; super. col. surg., S. Stltms., 1st Mar., 1901.

SHEPSTONE, H.C., C.M.G. (1895).—Priv. sec. to the lieut.-gov., Natal, 1857; clk. to exec. coun., 1863; Indian imigr. agt. and J.P., 1864; R.M. and admstr. of native law, Alfred co., 1870; ditto, Durban co., 1878; acted as sec. to Mr. Justice Phillips, C.M.G., on special service to Barbados, 1876; served on the staff of Sir Theophilus Shepstone, K.C.M.G., H.M.'s special comnr. in S. Africa, 1877; sec. for nativo affairs, Transvaal, July, 1877; attended Cetywayo on his visit to England, 1882; sec. for nativo affairs, Natal, 1884; ret., 1883.

SHEPSTONE, JOHN WESLEY, C.M.G. (1888). —B. 1827; July, 1846, govt. interp., Natal; July, 1847, accompanied Capt. Kyle on special mission to Zulu king; Dec., 1850, accompanied Capt. Gordon on special mission to Pondos; Oct., 1851, interp. to recorder and mag.'s offices; Dec., 1851, asst. R.M., Pietermaritzburg co.; Aug., 1852, R.M., Lower Umvoti, and govt. agt., Noniti country, and J.P.; 1857, commanded native forces against Chief Usidoi and tribe, and against Chief Matyana and tribe; Nov., 1858, supt. of the cultivation of cotton; Apr., 1861, ag. sec. for nativo affairs and judicial assessor; July, 1861, commanded a native force in the Fort Buckingham expdn.; May, 1862, ag. R.M., Inanda div.; Aug., 1863, asst. R.M., Pietermaritzburg co.; ag. R.M., Umvoti co., Sept., 1867; May, 1874, comnr., Klip River and Weenen counties; July, 1874, ag. sec. for nativo affairs and judicial assessor; Oct., 1874, J.P. for the col.; Sept., 1876, sec. for nativo affairs and mem. exec. and legis. couns.; Mar., 1878, mem. of the bndry. comsn.; Dec., 1878, delivered ultimatum to Zulu delega. at Lower Drift, Tugela; Aug., 1879, accompanied Sir Garnet Wolseley to arrange the settlement of Zululand; mentioned in despatches; Dec., 1882, Br. comsnr., Zululand; Jan., 1884, judge, native high ct.; ret., 1896.
SHEPSTONE, THEOPHILUS, C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1843; was in command of a Basuto regt. in the Zulu war; mem. of Natal legis. coun. until 1887; counsellor with Umbandeen, King of Swaziland, 1887-91.

SHERIFF, PERCY MUSGRAVE CRESSWELL.—Called to the bar, Mid. Temple, June, 1892; admitted to the bar of B. Hond., 1893; of Jamaica, 1895; ag. dist. comsnr., Belize, for six months in 1898; apptd. mag. and coroner, So. dist., Grenada, 1900; visiting just. of prisons, 1900; chairman of St. George's Road Board, 1901; ag. mem. legia. coun., June, 1902; ag. ch. just. and prov. mem. of ex. coun., St. Vincent, Dec., 1902; admtd. govt. and acted as col. sec. and treas., Aug., 1903; ch. just., St. Vincent, Oct., 1903; appt., confirmed, Dec., 1903; M.L.C., Apr., 1904.
SHIELS, THE HON. WILLIAM.—Atty. gen., Victoria, 1891; premier and treas., 1892-3.
SHIPWAY, WALTER ROBERT.—Apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd div. clk., and assigned to C. O., Dec., 1898.

SHOLL, L. H., I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1844; clk., P.W. office, S. Australia, July, 1858; clk., waterwks. dept., Jan., 1859; clk., audit office, Sept., 1868; ch. clk., Jan., 1872; ch. clk. and acctnt., treasy., July, 1874; acctnt. and receiver of rev., treasy., July, 1876; cashier and acctnt., treasy., July, 1879; under-treas., July, 1883 under-sec. and govt. statist., May, 1890; also clk. of exec. coun., July, 1894.

SHOLL, R. A., I.S.O. (1903).—Probation clk., P.O., W. Australia, 1863; 3rd class clk., 1864; 2nd class clk., 1869; ch. clk., 1873; ch. clk. and acctnt., treasy., 1879; ditto, P.O., 1881; postm. gen., 1889; also lieut. metropolitan rifles (W.A.), 1875; capt., 1883; capt.-comdt., 1888; ret., 1904.

SHORT, EDWARD MORRISON DE COUBOY.—Ceylon writer, 1878; pol. mag., 1883; asst. to govt. agt., S. prov., 1886; asst. collr. of cust., and landing surrv., cust., Galle, May, 1886; ag. asst. govt. agt., Mannar, Jan., 1889; Hambantota, Sept., 1889; Kegalla, May, 1890; Vavuniya and Mullaitivu, Dec., 1890; Kalutara, Apr., 1894; Chilaw, Dec., 1895; Vavuniya, Apr., 1896; Matara, Nov., 1897; asst. govt. agt., Matale, Jan. 1900; Nuwara Eliya, 15 Aug., 1900; ag. pol. mag., Galle, Oct., 1890; dist. judge, Matara, Feb., 1892; asst. govt. agt., Matale, 16th Jan. 1900; ditto, Nuwara Eliya, 15th Aug., 1900.
SHOWERS, EDWARD MELIAN.—Served in Bengal police, Feb., 1867 to Sept., 1901; comsnr. of pol., Transvaal, 8th Nov., 1901.
SIDNEY, T. S., M.A.—Ed. Harvard, and Trin. Hall, Camb.; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., June, 1885; enrolled adv. to high ct. of Madras, 1894; apptd. dist. comsnr., Epe, Lagos, Jan., 1901.

SIERRA LEONE, BISHOP OF, Rt. REV. EDMUND HENRY ELWIN, M.A., Merton Coll., Oxon, cur. of St. Peter-le-Bailey, Oxford, 1894-6; chaplain at Fourah Bay Coll., S. Leone, 1896-8; principal, 1898-1901; chapln. to Bp. of S. Leone, 1898-1901; Bishop of Sierra Leone, 1901.
SIMPSON, GEORGE.—Inspr. of schls., Bermuda, 1880; also inspr. of dockyard schls.

SINCKLER, E. G.—Entered col. sec.'s office, Barbados, Nov., 1874; acted as clk. to lieut.-gov., 1878; 2nd clk. corrpdce. branch, col. sec.'s office, 1879; ag. asst. clk. to Gov. W. Robinson, 1889; 1st clk., record branch, col. sec.'s office, and clk. courts of ordinary and error, Jan., 1883; ag. ch. clk., 1886; comsnr. of census, 1891; ch. clk. to judges, 1892; J.P., Oct., 1892; ag. regnr., friendly socs., June, 1893; ag. sen. pol. mag., Bridgetown, and dist. A, Aug. to Oct., 1893; ag. pol. mag. and judge, dist. B, Sept. to Oct., 1894; has acted several times as coroner, dist. A; ag. pol. mag. and judge, dist. F, July, 1896, to Jan., 1897; ag. pol. mag. judge, and coroner, dist. B, Sept. to Oct., 1897; ag. pol. mag. and judge, dist. D, Mar. to Apr., 1898; ag. coroner, St. Michael, 1898 (at time of hurricane), and has repeatedly acted as such; ag. pol. mag. and judge, dist. B, 1899; ag. pol. mag. dist. A, Feb., 1900; ag. comsnr. of probate, May to Nov., 1900; ag. pol. mag., dist. A, July, 1900, to Sept., 1901; pol. mag. and judge, dist. E Oct., 1901.

SINCLAIR, WM.—Clk., stores dept., L. and N.W. rly., Jan., 1863; stores auditor, July, 1873; rly. storekeeper, Midland system, Cape govt. rly., Aug., 1878; ch. rly. storekeeper, Jan., 1886; storekeeper, C.S.A.R., 1904.

SINGAPORE, LABUAN, AND SARAWAK. 3rd BISHOP OF LABUAN, THE RIGHT REV. GEORGE FREDERICK HOSE, D.D.—Ed. St. John's Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1861; M.A., 1867; D.D., 1881; curate of Roxton with Great Barford, Beds., 1861-65; curate of Trinity, St. Marylebone, London, 1865-67; col. chaplain, Malacca, Nov., 1867; ditto, Singapore, Feb., 1874; regnr. of the diocese of Labuan, Apr., 1874; archdeacon of Singapore, Dec., 1874; bishop of Singapore, Labuan, and Sarawak, 1881.

SIVEWRIGHT, THE HON. SIR JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1892), C.M.G. (1880).—B. 1848; gen. man. of telegraphy, C. of Good Hope, Apr., 1878; ret., 1885; M.L.A., 1889; mem. of Rhodes's ministry without portfolio, July, 1900, and subsequently as comnr. of crown lands and pub. wks.; resig., 1893; resumed office, Jan., 1896.
SKEEN, G. J. A.—Extra asst. govt. printer, Ceylon, 1869; asst. govt. printer, 1872; govt. printer, 1881.

SKEETE, ELLIOT L., B.A., Durham, 1883.—Asst. to supt. bot. station, Barbados, Sept., 1890; ag. supt., Dec., 1890, to Jan., 1891; ch. offr. reformatory, May, 1892; ag. supt. reformatory and bot. station, July to Dec., 1894, and Apr. to May, 1896; and other occasions.

SKERRITT, CHRISTOPHER MARTIN.—Supernumerary clk., regnr. and provost marshal's office, Dominica, July to Dec., 1891, and Feb. to Sept., 1892; asst. clk. to govt. offr., Roseau, Sept., 1892, to Mar., 1893; aid waiter, treasy., Aug. to Oct., 1893; tax collr., treasy., Nov., 1893, to Apr., 1894; asst. clk., tax dept., treasy., 1895; asst. to admstr., Jan. to Mar., 1896; 2nd clk., regnr. and prov. marshal's off., Dominica, Mar., 1896 to Dec., 1899; 1st clk., Jan., 1901; is a comnr. to admstr. oaths, Dominica; and supt. of pub. cemetery; has held numerous "acting" appointments.
SKINNER, WM., M.A.—Ed. Bedford gram. schl.; scholar St. Catherine's Coll., Camb. B.A. (math. tripes), 1882; M.A., Oct., 1886; headmr., Govt. gram. schl., Dominica, Dec., 1892.

SLATER, A. R.—Ed. at King Ed. schl., Birmingham, and Emm. coll., Camb. (B.A. 1897); cadet, Ceylon, Nov., 1898; extra offr., asst. govt. agent, W. P., Aug., 1899; 2nd asst., P.M.G., Mar., 1900; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Dec., 1901; on spec. duty at camp for prisoners of war. Diyatalawa, July, 1902; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Jan., 1903; clk. leg. coun., May, 1904.
SLATTERY, THE HON. T. M.—Mem. legis. assem., N.S. Wales; sec. for mines and agricul. in Dibbs's ministry, 1891-94.

SLOLEY, HERBERT CECIL.—Served in C.M.R. in campaigns against Griquas and Moirosi (medal with clasp, 1877-8-9); capt. in native contingent, Basuto war, 1880-1; mentioned in despatches; sub-instr., Cape police, 1883; ditto, Basutoland, 1884; instr., 1886; asst. commr., Basutoland, 1889; ag. res. commr., July to Dec., 1895; govt. sec., 1896.

SLYNE, D.—Barrister-at-law; Lord O'Hagan medallist and David Lynch medallist, King's Inns, Ireland; entered Imperial civ. serv., open compet., 1880; until 1900 attached successively to Inland Rev. Dept. at Dublin, Sligo, Chester, Oxford, Leicester, Birmingham, Dublin, Dundee, and Birmingham; apptd. supervising excise offr., Trinidad, 1900; ag. receiver-gen., May, 1902; receiver-gen., Feb., 1903.

SMALLWOOD, HENRY ARMSTRONG.—Entered navy, 1885; on board H.M.S. "Victoria" at time of disaster, June, 1893; asst. commr., Colo West, and stip. mag., Apr., 1894; ag. stip. mag., Ba and Yasawa, Apr., 1896; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, and priv. sec. to gov. Fiji, Apr., 1896; ag. sec. to W. Pac. high comm., May, 1897, to May, 1898; stip. mag., Savu Savu and Taviuni, Jan., 1899; island treas., Cyprus, Mar., 1900.

SMART, ARCHIBALD WM.—Clk., dept. of trade and cust., Melbourne, 1863; landing waiter, 1868; landing survr., 1890; senior ditto, 1893; collr. of cust., Victoria, and regis. of shipping, Melbourne, 1903; ch. Commonwealth survr., 1903.

SMARTT, JAMES A.—Alderman of Brandon, Manitoba, 1882-84; mayor, 1886-87; mem. judicial bd., 1886; mem. of prov. legislature, 1886; min. of pub. wks., 1888; dep. min. of the interior of Canada, Apr., 1897; dept. supt.-gen. of Indian affairs, July, 1897.

SMARTT, CAPT. J. P. (3rd Batt., South Lancs. Regt.)—Public wks. and roads dept., G. Coast, 1893-96; local auditor, N. C. Prot. (now S. Nigeria), Oct., 1896; chief asst. treas., Dec., 1901; ag. treas., Dec., 1901, to June, 1902, and June to Dec., 1903; lieut. G. C. rifle volrs., May, 1893; capt., Nov., 1893; ag. adjt., May to Nov., 1893; ag. comdt., Oct., 1896, to Mar., 1896; attached for duty with Niger C. Protec. force, May to July, 1897, May and Aug., 1900; ag. treas., So. Nigeria, June to Dec., 1903, and in Sept., 1904; served with expdt. Benin territories, Apr. and May, 1899; mentd. in desps., medal and clasp; bronze medal R. Hum. Soc., 1887.

SMEETON, S. P., I.S.O. (1905).—B. 1842; extra clk. in office of S. of S. for Cols., June, 1862; 1st class clk. in col. sec.'s office, Jamaica, June, 1870; regis.-gen. of births, deaths, and marriages, 8th Feb., 1878; suptd. the taking of census of Jamaica, 1881 and 1891; dep. keeper of records (on amalgamation of registration and record depts.), May, 1899; nom. mem. of comm. to inquire into marriage and regis. laws, May, 1903.
SMITH, ALLAN F.—Clk. in P.O., Bermuda, 1879; clk., col. sec.'s office, 1886; asst. col. sec., 1895; col. postmr., 1896; ag. col. sec., July and Aug., 1899, and from May, 1900, to July, 1901.

SMITH, SIR C. ABERCROMBIE, KNT. BACH. (1903).—M.A. (Camb. and Glas.), fellow of St. Peter's Coll., Camb., 2nd wrangler and 2nd Smith's prize-man, 1888, &c.; mem. of the house of assem., Cape, 1866-75; mem. of the exec. coun., 1872; commr. of crown lands and pub. works, 1872-75; mem. of comm. under Ordinance 97, 1872-5; commr. of the sinking fund, 1872-4; mem. of the Univ. coun., 1873; chmn. of the meteorol. comm., 1874; comptroller and audr.-gen., 1875; chmn. of the tender bd., 1875; mem. of the surety bd., 1875-86; vice-chancellor, Cape Univ., 1877-9; commr., civ. ser. exams. bd., 1886; chairman, 1887; ret., 1904.

SMITH, C. A. JEFFREY.—Apprentice, pub. wks. dept., Jamaica, Aug., 1870; asst. to ch. supt., 1875; 3rd class ditto, 1879; 2nd class ditto, 1881; ag. dist. engnr., W. dist., 1886; ditto, Kingston dist., 1888-9; overseer of wks., prisons dept., 1889; 1st cls. supt., pub. wks., and supt., St. Catherine waterwks., 1896; also acted on several occasions as govt. assessor, and supt. irrigation wks.

SMITH, SIR CECIL CLEMENTI, G.C.M.G. (1892), K.C.M.G. (1886), C.M.G. (1880).—B. 1840; ed. at St. Paul's Schl. and Corp. Chr. Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1862, M.A., 1868; elected student interp., Hong Kong, Mar., 1862; regr.-gen., Mar., 1865; ag. pol. mag., June, 1865; ag. col. sec., 1867; mem. of exec. coun., 1869; ag. col. sec., Feb., 1872, to Dec., 1873; treas., 1871; acted as col. sec., Oct., 1874, and also from Mar., 1875; col. sec., S. Stilmta, July, 1878; apptd. under the F.O. to be Br. commr. to settle certain marine claims at Manila, Sept., 1878, and received the thanks of H.M.'s govt.; also for services in connection with the "Nisero" case, 1884; administered govt. S.S., Mar., 1884, to Nov., 1885; lieut.-gov. and col. sec., Ceylon, Nov., 1885; gov. of S.S., 1887; also consul-gen. and high commr. for Borneo and Sarawak, 1889; ret., 1893; pres. of comm. to inquire into Trinidad riots, 1903.

SMITH, SIR CHARLES HOLLED, K.C.M.G., C.B.—B. 1846; ensign, 1863; lieut., 1869; capt., 1877; S. African war, 1879-1881; Zulu campaign; Transvaal cam.; battles of Laing's Nek, Ingogo, and Majuba Mountain; despatches, Lon. Gaz. May 8, 1881; Egypt. exped., 1882; at Ramleh, Tel-el-Mahuta; action at Kassassin; battle of Tel-el-Kebir, Lon. Gaz., Nov. 2, 1882; major (brevet), Nov., 1882; with Egyp. army, Jan., 1883, to Aug., 1892; Sudan exped., 1884-5; lieut.-col., June, 1886; col., Dec., 1888; gov.-gen., Red Sea littoral, and comdt., Suakin, Sept., 1888, to Aug., 1892; Sudan, 1888-91; action of Gamaizah, Lon. Gaz., Jan. II, 1889; occupation of Handoul, Jan., 1891; major-gen. comding Victorian mil. forces, Dec., 1894; several medals, clasps to bronze stars, and mentioned in numerous despatches.

SMITH, EDWIN MITCHELL.—Entered survey, crown lands dept., S Australia, as asst., June, 1862; cadet and jun. survr.; survr., Jan. 1869; draftman, Jan., 1874; steward and survr. of educnl. lands, Feb., 1882; ch. clk., land office, Jan., 1886; dep. survr.-gen., July, 1894; mem. of central pastoral and central lands bds.
SMITH, FRANCIS.—Ch. mag., Gambia, 1879; puisne judge, G. Coast Col., 1887.

SMITH, FRANCIS JAGOR.—M.A. Oxon.; ed. Merchant Taylors schl.; scholar, St. John's Coll., Oxford, 1892; 2nd cls. class. moda., 1894; 2nd math. mods., 1894; 2nd Lit. Hum., 1896; B.A., 1896; M.A., 1902; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Oct., 1896; attached to col. sec.'s off., 1896; office asst. to govt. agt., and deputy fiscal, Cent. Prov., Apl., 1898; commr. of requests and pol. mag., Balapitiya, Aug., 1900; ditto, Galle, June, 1901; landing survr., customs, Colombo, Dec., 1901.
SMITH, THE HON. SIR FRANCIS, KNT. (creat. 1862).—B. 1819; B.A. Lond. Univ., 1840; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., May, 1842; solr.-gen.,
SMITH, FRANK BRAYBROOKE.—Director of agric., Transvaal, and agric. adviser to the govt., Apr., 1902; M.I.C.

SMITH, FREDERICK BONHAM, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1837; entered col. sec.'s office, Barbados, 1869; in office of gov.-in-chief of the Windward Is., 1860 to 1866; pol. mag. of Christchurch, Barbados, 1866; sen. pol. mag. of Bridgetown and St. Michael, 1873; ag. judge of the ass't. ct. of appeal, 1878 to 1880; inspr. of prisons, 1878, held with office of provost-marshal from 1882; ag. col. sec., 1879.

SMITH, FREDERICK STANLEY.—Ed. at Sherborne Coll., Eng.; called to the bar, In. Temp., Jan., 1892; ag. pol. mag., dist. "B," Barbados, Aug. to Oct., 1893; ag. pol. mag., dist. "D," Apr., 1894, to July, 1896; and from Dec., 1895, to Oct., 1896; ag. pol. mag., dist. "C," Jan. to Feb., 1897; J.P., Apr., 1897; ag. judge-ass't., ct. of appeal, Apr. to Sept., 1897; ag. pol. mag., dist. "E," Jan., 1898; ag. pol. mag., Bridgetown and dist. "A," Feb. to May, 1898; ag. pol. mag., dist. "F," Sept. to Oct., 1898; apptd. pol. mag., dist. "D," Jan., 1899; ag. pol. mag., Bridgetown and dist. "A," Nov., 1901, to Apr., 1902.

SMITH, GEORGE.—Transf'd from W.O. to ch sec.'s office, Cyprus, Nov., 1879; ch. clk., 1881; asst. to ch. sec., 1883 to 1891; clk., legis. coun., 1886 to 1891; sec. to the comteer for the representation of Cyprus at the Col. and Ind. Exhibition; ag. ch. sec., May to Oct., 1888; comsnr. of Papho, 1891; regia-gen. and mem. legis. coun., 1895.

SMITH, GEORGE.—Ed. at Galway House Schl., Leyton, Essex, and at Morden House, Greenwich; ag. dist. comsnr., Anamaboe, G. Coast, 1877; dist. comsnr. Dexcove, 1878; ditto, Palma and Leckie, Lagos, 1880; sub-collr. customs, Palma and Leckie, 1883; ch. clk. and warehouse keeper, customs, Lagos, Aug., 1889; ag. dist. comsnr., E. dist., Lagos, Mar., 1892.

SMITH, GERALD STANLEY WELLS.—Graduate Ontario business coll., Canada; supry. clk., regis.'s office, Grenada, Jan., 1890; asst. clk., post office, Mar., 1890; 4th clk., treasy., June, 1890; asst. clk., col. sec.'s office, Oct., 1890; 2nd clk., treasy., Nov., 1891; audit clk., Nov., 1897; has held numerous acting appointments.

SMITH, Lieut.-Col. Sir GERRARD, K.C.M.G. (1895).—B.1839; late lieut.-col. Roy. Scots Guards; groom-in-waiting to Her Majesty, 1883-5; M.P. for High Wycombe, 1883-5; J.P. for E. Riding of Yorkshire; gov. of W. Australia, 1895 to 1900.
SMITH, HENRY.—Joined Mauritius pol. force, Feb., 1886; inspr. of police, Seychelles, Nov., 1897.
SMITH, HENRY J.—Apptd., after exam., 3rd cl. messenger C.O., 12th Nov., 1883; 2nd cl. ditto, 1st June, 1897; Queen's mess., 1st Apr., 1898.
SMITH, JAMES ALFRED.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv., and assigned to the C.O., Apr., 1891; promoted to higher grade, 1902.

SMITH, Jas. C.—Ch. clk. and storekr., Imperial treasy. and commissariat depts., Bahamas, June, 1876, to Aug., 1889; mem. bd. of educ., 1886 to 1892; postmr., 1889; J.P., 1890; elected mem. of Bahamas House of Assembly in 1882-89 and 96; resigned 1897; asst. postmr. gen., Sierra Leone, Dec., 1896; J.P., 1897; ag. postmr. gen., 1897, 1899, and 1900; ag. curator of intestates, S. L., from Jan., 1899, to Jan., 1900.

SMITH, JAMES PARKER, M.P.; P.C. (1904).—Ed. Winchester, and Trin. Coll., Camb. (4th wrangler and 2nd Smith's prizeman, 1877; fellow of Winchester and late fellow Trin. Coll.) called to bar. Lincoln's Inn, 1881; J.P. and D.L., Renfrewshire and co. of city of Glasgow; J.P., Lanarkshire; dir. of Union Bank of Scotland; elected for Partick div. of Lanarkshire, 1890; parly. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, 1901-3.

SMITH, SIR JOHN SMALMAN, KT. BACH (1896).—M.A., St. John's Coll., Camb.; b. 1847; called to the bar, In. Temp., 1872; special pleader Oxford circuit; puise judge, G. Coast Col., 1883; judge, Lagos, 1886; ch. just., Lagos, 1889; ret., 1896.
SMITH, LINDSAY LEA.—Acting clk. to comsnr., Turks Is., 1894, 1896, 1900, and 1903; confirmed 1st July, 1903.

SMITH, RICHARD WILLIAM, A.M.I.C.E., B.A., B.A.I.—P. W. Dept., Ceylon; ed. at the Grammar Schl., Chard, Somerset, 1874, and Dublin Univ. (Trinity College), 1875-79; employed in engineer's off., D.W.W. Rlwy., 1879; res. engrn., Wicklow Harb. works, 1880-83; asst. to Mr. J. Lanyon, Belfast (rlwys., waterworks, drainage, etc.), 1883-85; res. engr., Cleggan Harb. Works, 1885-88; asst. res. engr., St. Helier's Harb. Works (Jersey), 1888-89; aspt. P.W. Panang, S.S., 1889-92; D.E., P.W.D., Ceylon, 1892-94; ag. prov. engrn., N.W.P., 1894-97; dist. engrn. and ag. prov. engr., S.P., 1898; seconded to railway extensions staff, 1898; seconded to "Water Supply Schemes," 1898-1900; charge of Boer Camp Works, 1900; "Water Supply Schemes," 1901-02; seconded as res. engrn., proposed convict prison works, Seychelles, 1902; returned to Ceylon 13th Aug., 1902.
SMITH, ROBERT MURRAY, C.M.G. (1894).—B. 1831; agt.-gen. for Victoria in United Kingdom, 1881-86.
SMITH, HON. SYDNEY.—M.P. for Macquarie, New South Wales; postmr.-gen., Commonwealth of Australia, in Reid-Turner ministry, 1904.
