SMITH, THOMAS SERCOMBE, B.A., LL.B. (Lond.).—Hong Kong cadet, 1882; attached to C.O., 1883; passed cadet, 1886; ag. asst. regist.-gen., 1886-90, except for five months as ag. pol. mag.; asst. regist. gen., 1891; called to the bar. Mid. Tem., Nov., 1893; ag. puise judge, various dates, 1895-7, and in 1900, 1901 and 1904; treas. Dec., 1896; pol. mag., July, 1898; ag. col. sec., 1898-9, and for 3 months in 1901.

SMITH, WM. EDWARD.—Employed on L. & N.W. Rly., 1872-82; asst. traffic man., Indian guaranteed rlys. (Scinde, Punjab, and Delhi), 1883-86; ag. district traffic man., Lahore and Delhi div.; gen. man., Barbados Rly., 1889; gen. and traffic man., Trinidad and Govt. Rly., Feb., 1894; received special allowance of 100/ in 1896 for reorganising rly. dept.; is a J.P.

SMITH, SIR WILLIAM FREDERICK HAYNES, K.C.M.G. (1890), C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1839; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., June, 1863; solr.-gen., Br. Guiana, Oct., 1865; atty.-gen., Oct., 1874; admstr. the govt., Apr. to Sept., 1884, and in 1887; gov., Leeward Is., Nov., 1888; gov., Bahamas, 1895; high comsnr., Cyprus, 1898-1904.

SMITH, SIR WILLIAM JAMES, KT. BACH (1896).—M.A., LL.M. Trinity Hall, Camb.; b. 1853; acted as puise judge of the G. Coast Col. from Dec. 1880, to July, 1881; puise judge of the sup. ct. of Cyprus, 1882; ch. just., 1892; ch. just., Br. Guiana, 1898; puisne judge, sup. ct., Transvaal, 1st Mar., 1902.
SMITH, WILLIAM RAMSAY.—B.Sc., M.B., M.S., Edin.; chmn. cent. bd. of health, coroner, vaccination offr., and inspr. of anatomy, S. Australia, 1899-1903; chmn. cent. bd. of health, and coroner, 1903.
SMITH-STEINMETZ, GERARD ARCHIBALD.—Ed. at Stonyhurst coll.; cadet, Straits Settlements, Oct., 1902; in charge of S.S. emigr. depôt, S. India, June, 1904; passed final exam. in Tamil, July, 1904.

SMUTS, JOHANNES.—Ck., treasy., Cape, 1882; acted as priv. sec. to the Hon. J. Gordon Sprigg, 1885-6; to Sir H. A. Smyth, 1889; to Lieut.-Gen. H. E. Cameron, and to Sir H. Loch, 1891; sec. to Hon. J. H. Hofmeyr (Swaziland Convention), 1890; asst. despatch ck., govt. house, 1886; 2nd class clk., 1887; ag. priv. sec. to Lieut.-Gen. Cameron and to Sir H. B. Loch, 1891 to 1896; ch. clk. to high commr., 1891; acted as olk. of exec. coun., 1891 and 1896; Br. consul in Swaziland, Aug., 1896-9.
SMYLY, PHILIP CRAMPTON.—Ed. at Trin. Coll., Dub., B.A., LL.B.; called to the bar, King's Inns, Dub., 1888; LLD., 1891; Queen's advoo., S. Leone, 1896; atty.-gen., 1896.
SMYTH, C. E. OWEN, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1861; ed. at Erasmus Smith Sch., Dub.; specification clk., S. Australia, 1876; profnl. clk., 3rd class, 1878; 1st class, 1883; sup. of pub. bldgs., 1886.
SMYTH, GEN. *SIR HENRY AUGUSTUS, K.C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1825; in command of the troops, S. Africa, 1889; gov. of Malta, 1890-3.
SNELLGROVE, J.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv., and assigned to C.O. 22nd June, 1903.
SNOWBALL, JABEZ BUNTING.—Lieut.-gov., New Brunswick, Canada, 6th Feb., 1902.

SOLOMON, JOHN.—2nd-class asst. of excise, England, Apr., 1868; 1st-class asst., June, 1870; ride offr., June, 1871; resig., Jan., 1876; ag. 3rd clk., immigr. dept., Br. Guiana, July, 1877; ag. cashier in rec.-gen.'s office, 1878; comr. of taxation, 1879.
SOLOMON, MICHAEL CLAUDE.—Ag. 3rd-cl. clk., island med. dept., Jamaica, Mar., 1885; 3rd-cl. clk., Oct., 1888; 2nd-cl. clk., July, 1891.

SOLOMON, HON. SIR RICHARD, K.C.M.G. (1901), C.B. (1903), K.C.—Ed. S. African Coll., Capetown; St. Peter's Coll., Camb., 23rd wrangler; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1879; legal adviser to late Lord Rosemark on his mission to Mauritius, 1886; mem. of native law comsn.; chmn. of mining comsn.; mem. of House of Ass. for division of Tembuland, Cape; atty.-gen., Cape, 1898-1900; legal adviser to Transvaal adminstrn., 1901-1902; atty.-gen., Transvaal, 19th June, 1902; mem. ex. and leg. couns.; mem. I.C.C.

SOLOMON, WM. HENRY.—B.A., Cantab; admitted to the bar, Inner Temple, 1877; practised in Cape Colony; asst. legal adviser, Cape, 1883; puisne judge, sup. ct., 1887; pres., spec. treason court, 1900; 1st puisne judge, Transvaal, 1902.

SOUZA, SIMON I., DE.—Ed. at the R. Catholic gram schl., Lagos; 1st despatch clk. and shorthand writer, col. sec.'s office, Lagos, June, 1895; acted as clk. of the legis. coun., 1900; regis. of correspondence, June, 1900; conf. clk. to gov. and clk. of leg. coun., July, 1901; ag. priv. sec. to gov., and clk. to ex. coun., Feb., 1902; in charge of Ibadan residency, Nov., 1903.
SORZANO, TILDEBER.—Draughtsman, crown lands office, Trinidad, 1874; first ditto, survey dept., Aug., 1881.
SPALDING, Lieut.-Col. WARNER WRIGHT, C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1844; N. S. Wales Artill., 1876; in command of the detachment which served in the Soudan, 1884; res. mag. Norfolk Is., 1896.

SPEED, EDWIN ARNFT.—M.A., LL.B.; ed. at Rugby, and Trin. Coll., Camb.; major scholar and senior exhibt., Rugby schl.; scholar and prize-man of Trin. Coll., Camb.; 1st cls. class. tripes, 1890; 2nd cls. law tripes, Part I., 1891; called to the bar, Inner Temple, June, 1893; dist. commsnr., G. Coast, 1899; atty.-gen., Lagos, 1900; edited a revised edition of Lagos laws, 1901; has acted on numerous occasions as ch. just. and also as col. sec., and has held a comsn. as dep. gov't.

SPENCE, FRANK.—3rd clk. col. sec.'s office, Fiji, 1880; clk. to commsnr. of wks., Oct., 1884; ag. priv. sec. to gov., 1884, 1885, and 1887 to 1896; sec. to consul-gen. for W. Pacific, 1889 to 1896; stip. mag., Cakandrove and Busa, 1896 to 1898; provincial inspr. and stip. mag., Cakandrove and Buia, 1899; ditto, Cakandrove, 1901; stip. mag., Navua, Jan., 1904.
SPENCE, J. B.—Med. supt. of Colombo lun. asyl., Ceylon, 1886.

SPENCE, R. O. H.—Cler. asst., P.W. dept., Br. Guiana, 16th Aug., 1886; ag. 2nd clk., P.W. dept., Aug., 1889; ag. 3rd-cl. clk., treasy., Jan., 1890; gov. offr., govt. ld. dept., Apr., 1890; clk., govt. ld. dept., May, 1890; sec., Bartika commsnr., June, 1891 to May, 1893; clk., dept. mines, Oct., 1892; ag. 3rd-cl. clk., dept. mines, Apr., 1893; govt. offr., dept. mines, July, 1893; J.P., May, 1894; comrn. to admr. oath, June, 1894; dist. govt. offr., dept. mines, Aug., 1896; warden, dept. mines, Nov., 1896; obd. certif. as sworn land surv., June, 1898; ag. ch. clk., dept. mines, Feb., 1900.

SPENCER, CYRIL E.—Ck., ch. sec.'s office, Cyprus, 1st Aug., 1892; passed in Mod. Greek, lower standard, Nov., 1893; clk., legis. coun., Mar., 1894; ag. priv. sec. to O.A.G., Jan.-Apr., 1898; inspr. of pol., May, 1898; ag. priv. sec. and clk. to ex. coun., Feb., May, 1899, and Dec., 1899, to May, 1900; ag. local comdt. of police, Papho, July, 1900, to June, 1901; Limassol, from June, 1901.

SPICER, QUINTEN HERBERT.—2nd clk., col. sec.'s dept., Tobago, 1884; 1st clk., audit and registry dept., 1884; 2nd rev. offr., 1895; harbrmr. and landing waiter, 1890; supervisor of excise 1892; inspr. of pol., 1896; in charge of prison, 1897; ast. harbrmr., Trinidad, 1899.
SPIRE, JOHN.—Apptd., after exam., 3rd-class messenger, C.O., Jan., 1883; 2nd class, July, 1896; Queen's mess., Nov., 1899.

SPRIGG, THE RT. HON. SIR JOHN GORDON, P.C. (1897), G.C.M.G. (1902), K.C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1830; has represented E. London in Cape assem. since 1869; col. sec. and premier, Feb., 1878, to May, 1881; col. treas., May, 1884, to Nov., 1886; premier, Nov., 1886; apptd. comdr. legion of honour for services in Paris Exhib., 1889; resig. office, July, 1890; again treas., 1893, and also premier, 1896; represented the col. in London at H.M.'s Diam. Jub.; resig. premiership, Oct., 1898; again premier 1900.

SPROULE, PERCY JULIAN, B.A., Camb.—Bar-rister-at-law (Mid. Tem.); cadet, S. S., Nov., 1896; ag. dep. registr., sup. ct., Penang, Mar., 1897; passed final in Malay, Mar., 1899; ag 3rd mag., Penang, June, 1899; dep. registr., sup. ct., Penang, Sept., 1899; ag. asst. regisr., sup. ct., Penang, May, 1903; ag. 2nd mag., Sing., Sept., 1904.
STAGG, SIDNEY.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. 2nd div. civil ser., and assigned to board of agriculture, Dec., 1897; transf. to C.O., Mar., 1899.

STANFORD, WALTER ERNEST MORTIMER, C.B. (1900), C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1850; mag.'s elk., Cape Col., 1863; mag., 1876; capt. of levies in Gcaleka war, 1877-8 (medal); mem. native laws and customs comm., 1880-2; comdt. in war of 1880-1; on special service to Pondoland, 1884; ch. mag. Griqualand E., 1885; negotiated treaty with Pondoos, 1886; sec. nat. affairs dept., 1888; ditto and ch. mag., July, 1904.

STANMORE, THE RIGHT HON. SIR ARTHUR HAMILTON GORDON, 1st Baron (creat. 1893), G.C.M.G. (1878), K.C.M.G. (1871), C.M.G. (1859).—B. 1829; priv. sec. to his father, Earl of Aberdeen, when 1st lord of the treas., 1852 to 1855; M.P. for Beverley from July, 1854, till Mar., 1857; accompanied Mr. Gladstone on his special mission as lord high commr. extraord. to the Ionian Is., Nov., 1858; capt. comdt. of the 1st Aberdeenshire R.V., Feb., 1860; lieut.-gov. of N. Brunswick, Oct., 1861; gov. of Trinidad, Nov., 1866; gov. of Mauritius, 1870; ret., 1874; gov. of the new col. of Fiji, Jan., 1875; H.M.'s high commr. and consul-gen. for the W. Pacific, 1877; gov. of N. Zealand, 1880; gov. of Ceylon, 1883; ret., 1890.
STANTON, LIONEL WM.—Inspr. of schls., S. Aust., 1876; asst. inspr.-gen., 1892; chmn. of bd. of inspr., 1896; inspr.-gen. of schls., 1902.

STEELE, WALTER MATTHEW.—Clerical asst., treasry., Br. Guiana, Feb., 1884; 5th class clk., Feb., 1886; 4th class, July, 1888; 3rd class, Apr., 1890; ag. clk.in-charge, sub-treasy., and savings bk., Dec., 1891, to June, 1893; 2nd class clk. (acctnt.), govt. land dept., April, 1896; asst. treas., So. Nigeria, July, 1904.

STEEN, DAVID MILLER, B.A.—Scholar, Queen's Coll., Belfast; 1st class exhibitioner, Roy. Univ.; B.A. 1896; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., 1887; pol. mag., Balapitiya, 1889; asst. collr., customs, Trincomalee, 1890; ditto, Galle, 1892; pol. mag., Haldemulla, 1893; ag. dist. judge, Badulla, 1893; ag. dist. judge, Tangalla, 1895.

STEERE, C. J. LEK.—Probation clk., col. sec.'s off., W. Aust., Feb., 1885; jun. clk., Aug., 1885; ag. clk., gov.'s off., 1889-1900; clk. of leg. coun. and clk. of parlts., Dec., 1890, to June, 1901; clk. of leg. assem., June, 1901.

STEIN, JOHN.—Ed. at Christ's Coll., Finchley, and at Neuwied, Germany; sec. to consulates for Germany, Sweden and Norway, and Denmark, in Mauritius, May, 1889, to Dec., 1896; apptd. by S. of S., F.O., an asst. dist. commr. in H.B.M.'s Niger Coast Protectorate, April, 1897; ag. dist. commr., Opopo, June, 1897, to June, 1898; Bonny, Jan., 1899, to Jan., 1900; promoted dist. commr., Jan., 1900; asst. sec. to govt., Southern Nigeria, June, 1900; ag. sec. to govt., Sept., 1900, to May, 1901, and from June to Nov., 1901; West African medal with clasp, 1899; ment. in desps. for services in connection with Aro expedition, 1901-2; ret., 1904.
STEPHEN, CHELVUM.—Ind. interp. to res. mag., Durban, Natal, Jan., 1875; clk. and Ind. interp. to res. mag. Umlazi div., co. of Durban, Mar., 1876; Tamil interp., sup. ct., 1895.

STEPHEN, GUY NEVILLE.—Ed. Paris, Marseilles, London; M.R.C.S., Eng., 1881; licentiate of medicine, Franco, 1882; ag. house surg., Lincoln co. hosp.; ditto, asst. med. offr., Middlesex co. asylum (Colney Hatch); surg. to the consulate of Norway and Sweden, Marseilles, 1881; surg. to the Br. Consulate and Seamen's Home, Marseilles, 1882; dist. med. offr., Nicosia, Cyprus, 1883; med. offr., central prison, gen. and ophthalmic hosp.; med. offr. of health, Nicosia; pres. of the municipality, Nicosia, 1889; ag. ch. med. offr., 1885 and 1890; surg. to Smyrna Hosp., 1894.
STEPHEN, SIR MATTHEW HENRY, K.T. BACH. (1904).—Mem. legis. assem., N.S.W., 1869-71; judge, sup. ct., May, 1887.
STEPHEN, WILLIAM WILBERFORCE.—Clk., col. secretariat, N. S. Wales, 1852; priv. sec. to various premiers, 1856-58; clk., land dept., 1858; under-sea. for lands, 1870; sec., atty.-gen.'s dept., 1880.

STEPHENS, THOMAS NOAKES.—Jun. offr. S. Australia; customs, 1864; clk., marine bd., 1865; boarding offr., customs, 1866; asst. landing waiter, 1867; confid. clk., 1870; ch. clk., treasy., 1875; sec. marine bd., 1877; sec. of customs, 1889; under-treas., 1890; collr. of customs, regisr. of shipping, ch. inspr. of distilleries and excise, pres. of marine bd., 1894; is a J.P.; transf. with cust. dept. to serv. of Commonwealth of Aust., 1st Jan. 1901.

STEVENS, FRED. G.—B.A., Oxon.; ed. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; cadet, Strait Settlements, Oct., 1902; demarcation offr., Sing., 15th June, 1903; dep. coll. of land rev. and regisr. of deeds, Sing., in addtn. to other duties, Oct., 1903; ag. dist. offr., Malacca, June, 1904; passed final exam. in Malay, July, 1904.

STEVENS, PERCIVAL.—Asst. M.I.C.E.: asst. engnr. govt. rly., Trinidad, 1874; dist. engnr., Couva extension rly., 1878; req. engnr. in charge of construction, San Fernando and Guaiacara rlys., 1879; 1st asst. engnr. P.W. dept., Jan., 1885; has acted as asst. dir. of P.W., 1886, 6, 8, 9, and 90; engnr., N. div., P.W.D., Jan., 1892; 1st engnr. in charge of rds. and bdges., P.W.D., Jan., 1894; engnr. in charge of rds. and bdges., rds. and bdges. dept., Jan., 1895; ag. dir. pub. wks., with seat in legis. coun., May, 1894, to Feb., 1896.

STEVENS, THOMAN HAMILTON.—2nd clk. in treasy., Antigua, 1873; clk. in treasy., St. Kitts, 1874; supervisor of customs, G. Coast Col., 1880; in charge of special expenditure in connection with Ashantee difficulty, 1881; acted as paymr., G. Coast constab., 1882; commr. of Elmina, 1882; in charge of dist. chest, Accra, 1884; paymr. of constab., 1885; dist. commr. of Dixcove, 1886; Elmina, 1886 and 1887; financial asst. to pol. Singapore, 1888; ag. asst. audr.-gen., S. S., Nov. to Dec., 1896.

STEWARD, MAJ.-GEN. EDWARD HARDING, C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1836; entered army, Roy. Engrs., Oct., 1854; employed from 1865 to 1879 in the fortification branch of the W.O.; services lent in 1866 to 1869, and in 1877 to the S. Australian govt., and in 1877 till 1879 to the govt. of Victoria. In charge of the defence of the Natal frontier in 1879, and comdt. of Natal and mem. of exec. coun. of the col. in 1880; services lent to govt. of Victoria and N. S. Wales in 1883; and in 1884, on retirement from the army, became military adviser to the agts.-gen. of Victoria, N. S. Wales, Queensland, S. Australia, N. Zealand, and Tasmania.

STEWARD, GEORGE.—Ck., Tasmanian govt. rly., June, 1892; ch. ck. and acctnt., education dept., July, 1893; sec. to premier of Tasmania, Aug., 1894; ck. to ex. coun., Jan., 1895; and sec. to defence comtee., June, 1895, in addition; under sec. for Tasmania, Jan., 1896; and ch. inspr. of explosives and magazines, 1st Sept., 1897, in addition; raised and commanded first company of mtd. infantry in Tasmania, and holds rank of captain; ch. ck., dept. of external affairs, Commonwealth govt., 18th May, 1901; offi. sec. to gov.-gen., and sec. to fed. ex. coun., 24th Dec., 1902.
STEWART, A.—Rea. mag., Victoria E., Cape Col., Jan., 1882; transf'd to Albert, 1884.

STEWART, CAPT. SIR D., K.C.M.G. (1902), C.M.G. (1897).—Joined 92nd Gordon Hinds. 1879; served in Afghan war, 1879-80; present at Lord Robert's march from Cabul to Candahar (severely wnd.), med. and clasp, star for the march; served with 92nd in Transvaal war, 1881; A.D.C. to comdr.-in-chief in India, 1882 to 1884; served with mtd. infnty., Soudan campgn. of 1884-5, med. and clasp, Khedive's star; served as political offr. with Ashanti expdn. of 1896, star; Res. of Ashanti, 1896; served in ntnrn. territories G. Coast, 1897, at the occupn. of Gambaga and connected operations; comsnr. and commdr.-in-chief, B. East Africa Prot., 1904.

STEWART, JOHN MARCUS.—Ck., pol. ct., Queensland, 1887; ditto, pol. dept., 1888; sec. pol. investmt. bd., 1898; acctnt., pol. dept., 1899; sub-lieut. in Queensland and Commwth. of Aust. naval forces, 1st Nov., 1899, to 31st Jan., 1904; asst. treas., N. Nigeria, 6th Aug., 1904.

STEWART, MAJ.-GEN. SIR ROBERT MACGREGOR, K.C.B. (1902).—B. 1842; served in Hazara campaign, 1868; Afghan war, 1878-9; Soudan, 1885; A.D.C. to Queen Victoria, 1887-97; late commdr., R.A., southn. dist., Portsmouth; gov. Bermuda, 19th Mar., 1904.

STIGAND, ALMAR GORDON.—2nd ck. to res. comsnr. for Bech. Prot. at Mafeking, 1898; 1st ck. and regis. to res. comsnr., 1899; served in town guard during siege of Mafeking, Oct., 1899, to May, 1900; ck. of ct. and ck. to asst. comsnr., Gaberones, 1902; J.P.; Bech. Prot., 1903; ag. asst. comsnr., Jan. to Feb., 1904.
STILES, ALFRED.—Apptd., after exam., messenger C.O., Dec., 1875; Queen's mess., Aug., 1882; asst. off. keeper, 17th Feb., 1900.
STILWELL, E. A.—Sarawak civ. ser., roads and bridges dept., Mar., 1888; asst. res., Baram, Oct., 1894; of Sadong, May, 1895; res., 2nd class, Jan., 1897.
STIRLING, SIR JOHN LANCELOT, KT. BACH. (1902), B.A., LL.B.—B. 1849; mem. leg. coun., S. Aust., 1891; chief sec., 1899; pres. leg. coun., 1901.
STIRLING, RIGHT REV. W. H., D.D.—Bishop of the Falklands.

STOKER, WILLIAM HENRY, K.C.—Called to the bar, Mid. Temp.; atty.-gen., Leeward Is., Mar., 1898; mem. fed. and island ex. and leg. couns.; ag. 1st puisne judge conjointly with atty.-gen., Nov. to Dec., 1901; atty.-gen., Barbados, July, 1902; chrmn., Barbados quarantine coman., 1902-3; K.C., Barbados, 3rd April, 1903; M.L.C., Apl. to Oct., 1903; ag. pres. of educn. bd., May to June, 1903; ag. ch. just., 1st Aug. to 30th Oct., 1903; deleg. for Barbados, and elec. pres. at Brit. W. India conf. on quarantine, Apl. to May, 1904.

STONE, SIR E. A., KT. BACH. (1902).—B. 1844; ck. to atty.-gen., W. Australia, 1860; called to bar, 1865; ck. to legis. coun., 1870; nominee mem. of ditto, 1880-2; crown solr., 1882; puisne judge, 1884; acted as ch. just., 1880, 1881, 1887, and 1889; ch. just., 1901; admstr., 1901-2.

STONE, E. C. M.—Ck. to regis., Trinidad, 1875; ch. ck., 1878; ck. of complaint ct., 1878; ch. ck. to regis., sup. ct., 1880; regis., ct. of survey, 1883; ag. regis., sup. ct., 1880, 1881, 1883, and Apr., 1884, to July, 1885, and June to Sept., 1886; ch. ck. to regis.-gen., Sept., 1887; dep. regis.-gen., Oct., 1889; ag. regis.-gen., 1892-93; is a comnr. of affidavits, and sup. regis. of births and deaths for Port of Spain.

STONE, HERBERT C.—Entered Trinidad service, Apr., 1863; ast. clk. of the peace, Port of Spain, May, 1864; ag. clk. of the peace, W. dist., co. of St. Patrick, July, 1865; 3rd ck., immigr. office, Mar., 1866; 2nd ck., June, 1867; ag. ch. clk., 1868-9; ag. stip. mag., co. of St. David's, and ward of Blanchisseuse, and warden of the Toco ward union, Feb., 1872; inspr. of immigrts., Nov., 1872; ag. warden and supervisor of the Caroni ward union, Feb., 1876; J.P. for the town of Port of Spain and counties of St. George and Caroni, May, 1876; inspr. and sub-prot. of immigrts., 1st Jan., 1882; ag. prot. of immigrts., 1882, 1887, and 1889-92, with seat in legis. coun.; mem. bd. of educn., June, 1892; ag. rec.-gen., with seat in legis. coun., 1892.

STOUT, THE HON. SIR ROBERT, K.C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1844; admitted a barrister of sup. ct., N. Zealand; entered the prov. coun. of Otago, N.Z., 1872; prov. solr., 1873; elected to the gen. assem., 1875; atty.-gen., Mar., 1878, to June, 1879; min. of lands, etc., for immigr., 1878; pres. of Dunedin Freethought Soc.; prime min. and atty.-gen., and min. for educn. 1884-87; fellow of N. Z. Univ.; chief justice of N.Z., 1889.

STRACHAN, W. HENRY W., C.M.G. (1902), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), F.L.S.—Jun. res. med. offr., pub. hosp., Kingston, Jamaica, 1882; sen. res. med. offr., 1885; sen. med. offr., 1892; mem. of bd. of govrs., Inst. of Jamaica, 1892; chief med. offr. of Lagos, Dec., 1897; acted as col. sec., Dec., 1899, to Mar., 1900.

STRACHEY, CHARLES.—Apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. jun. clk., F.O., 2nd Apr., 1886; 1st cl. jun. clk., 1st May, 1883; sec. Uganda rly. comte., 10th Sept., 1895; 1st cl. clk., C.O., 20th Nov., 1898.

STRANGE, WM. LUMISDEN, M.I.C.E.—Served in pub. wks. dept., India, 1879 to 1903; undersec., ditto, July, 1901; dir. of irrigtn. and water supply, Transvaal, 25th Apl., 1903; author of "Indian storage reservoirs with earthen drains."

STRATHCONA AND MOUNT ROYAL, LORD, 1ST BARON (U.K. creat. 1897), SIR DONALD A. SMITH, G.C.M.G. (1896), K.C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1820; Gov. of Hudson's Bay Territory; special commnr., Red River, 1869; mem. of Canadian parlmt., 1871, dir. of Canadian Pacific Rly., 1880; high commnr. for Canada, 1896; mem. of Pacific Cable Comtees, 1886 and 1899; Lord Rector of Aberdeen Univ., 1899.

STRATTON, GEORGE BERNARD.—Ed. Warwick and Durham Univ.; cadet, S. S., Nov., 1899; passed final exam. in Malay, Dec., 1900; ag. aspt. govt. printing office, Jan., 1901; ag. 4th mag., Sing., Mar., 1901; ag. aspt. of education, Penang, Dec., 1901; on spec mission to Pulau Weh, Jan., 1904; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Apl., 1904.

STRAWBRIDGE, WILLIAM.—Entered survey and crown lands dept., S. Australia, as cadet, June, 1862; after several promotions, was apptd. examr. of licensed survr.'s work and draftsman, June, 1872; and ch., draftsman, Apr., 1877; dep. survr.-gen., July, 1886; survr.-gen., July, 1894; chmn. of pastoral, central pastoral, and central land bds.

STRICKLAND, SIR GERALD, COUNT DELLA CATENA, K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1889).—B. 1861; ed. Oscott Coll., Birmingham; lieut. C.U.R.V., and elected mem. coun. of govt., Malta, 1886; B.A. and LL.B., Trin. Coll., Camb., honours law tripoe; attended col. conf., 1887, on behalf of Malta; called to the bar, Inn. Temp., and pres. cholera coman., Malta, 1887; thanked by govt. for negotiations on Imp. veto of appts. to See of Malta; unoffic. mem., ex. coun., and ag. ass't sec. to govt., 1888; vice-pres., bd. of health, pres. comtee. of privileges, ch. sec. to govt., and major, Royal Malta militia, which he established, 1889; re-organized Malta rlyw., 1891; planned breakwater, 1894; pres., coun. of Malta univ., 1900; chrmn., mil. and civ. drainage bd.; mem. comtee. on Malta naval reserves; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Leeward Is., 1902; estab. central factories for sugar and cotton in Antigua, 1903; gov., Tasmania, 23rd July, 1904.
STRICKLAND, R. B., M.A. (Dublin).—Inspr. of schools, Ceylon, 29th Aug., 1902.

STRONG, THE RT. HON. SIR SAMUEL HENRY, P.C. (1897), KT. BACh. (1893).—Born in Dorsetshire, Eng., 1825; called to bar of Ontario, 1849; created Q.C., 1863; elected a bencher of the Law Soc. of Upper Canada, 1860; a comsnr. for consolidating the statutes of Upper Canada and Canada respectively, Dec., 1866; a mem. of the law reform coman., 1871; vice-chancellor Ontario, Dec., 1869; puisne judge of the ct. of error and appeal, same prov., 1874; puisne judge of the sup. ct. of the Dominion, 1875; ch. just. of Canada, 1892; apptd. to the judicial comtee. of H.M.'s privy coun., 1897.
STROUD, BENJ.—Suptdng. sergt., Barbados; pol. supt., 1876; 3rd class landg. waiter, Sept., 1881.
STUART, ALEXR.—Ed. St. Andrew's Univ.; regiar. of impta. and expts., Singapore, Sept., 1890; temporarily attached to patent office, London, 1897-8.

STUART, JAMES.—Ed. St. John's Coll., Hurstpierpoint; matric. Lond. and Cape; silver medal, Natal, Eng. lit. exam., 1891; clk. G.P.O., Pietermaritzburg, Feb., 1888; clk. and interp., R.M., Eshowe, May, 1888; 1st clk. and interp. to res. comsnr. and ch. mag., Zululand, Feb., 1889; acted on various occasions as sec., res. comsnr. and res. ch. mag.'s ct., Zululand; interp. to Br. comsnr., Swaziland, 1894 to 1896; and in charge of Swazi deputations to Capetown and England in 1894; acted as Br. consul, Swaziland, 1895; R.M., Ingavuma dist., Zululand, 1895; mag. Lower Tugela div., 1899; asst. mag., Durban div., 1901; mag. for colony, 1902.
STUART, JAS. MARTIN.—Comsnr. of insolvency, S. Australia, June, 1883; stip. mag., Port Adelaide, July, 1881; crown solr., 1890.

STUBBS, REGINALD EDWARD.—Ed. at Radley and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford (exhibitor); 1st cl. classical mods., 1897; 1st cl. lit. hum., 1899; B.A., 1899; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. clk., C.O., Jan., 1900.

SULLIVAN, ADMIRAL SIR FRANCIS W., BART., R.N., K.C.B. (1879), C.B. (1864), C.M.G. (1878), B. 1834; entered the royal navy, 1848; comdr. of "Harrier" in N. Zealand, 1862-4, during Maori war; present at Koheroa, Rauarangi and other minor affairs; promoted capt., 1863, and rear-admiral, 1878; commodore on the Cape and African station, 1877 to 1879, during operations in the Cape and Zululand; received the thanks of both houses of parlmt. for his services in connection with the Egyptian expdn., 1882; vice-admiral, 1886; dir. of transports, admiralty, 1883.

SULLIVAN, W. W., K.C.—Atty. gen. and premier of Prince Edward Is., Canada; born in P.E.I., 1843; called to the bar 1867; elected to legislature in 1872, and has continuously represented the same constituency; created a Q.C. by the govt. of P.E.I., 1876, and by the govt.-gen. of Canada 1879; is a dep. judge in the admity. ct., pres. of the bd. of educn., and pres. of the bd. of trustees of P.E.I. Hosp. for the Insane; was a mem. of the exec. coun., holding the office of solr.-gen., Apr., 1873, till Sept., 1876; leader of the Opposition in the legislature in 1877; became prime min. and atty. and advoc.-gen. in 1879, which position he continues to hold; has been a deleg. representing the prov. govt. on several occasions in Canada, and was deleg. to England in 1886, to confer with the impv. govt. regarding the terms of confederation between P.E.I. and Canada; ch. just., P.E.I., 1889.

SUTER, WM. CHAR.—Aest. master, govt. English schla., Singapore, 22nd Nov., 1884; ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, 1st July, 1888; shorthand reporter to leg. coun. in addition to other duties, 1st July, 1887.

SUTHERLAND, WM.—Apptd. to Fiji civ. ser., Jan., 1880; clk. recr.-gen.'s office, Sept., 1880; clk. regiar.-gen., Jan., 1882; stip. mag. of the col., Apl., 1883; ag. collr. of cust., Sept., 1897; ag. recr.-gen., Mar., 1898; native comsnr., 1898; ag. col. sec. and recr.-gen., Jan., 1903.

SUTTOR, THE HON. SIR FRANCIS B., KT. BACh. (1903).—Postmtr.-gen., N.S. Wales, Aug., 1890, to Nov., 1881; min. of pub. instruction, 1881 to 1883. and again, 1891-4; representative of col. at col. confere., Ottawa, 1894.

SWAIN, ALFRED WILLIAM.—Clerical ast., regisr.'s off., Barbados, 1879; Jun. clk., G.P.O., Br. Guiana, June, 1881; 3rd cls. clk., Jan., 1883; 2nd cls., Dec., 1889; 1st-class, Apr., 1900; ch. elk., Sept., 1900; ag. postmtr.-gen., May, 1901, to May, 1902; and Aug. to Dec., 1904; 2nd lieut. Br. Guiana volunteers, 1889, ditto militia, 1891; lieut., 1892; capt., 1896; maj., 1900; ag. comdt., of local forces, July, 1901, to Feb., 1902; Hythe certif., 1898; Colonial and Aux. forces long service medal; recd. thanks of govt. on retiring in 1902, and retains rank of Major.
SWAIN, ARTHUR CLAUDE.—2nd elk., crown lands dept., Br. Guiana, Oct., 1879; ast. comnr., May, 1882; dist. ditto, July, 1881; is J.P.

SWAIN, GEORGE DOUGLAS.—1st elk. and bookkeeper, pol dept., Br. Guiana, Apr., 1882; inspr. of pol., May, 1884; ch. inspr., Mar., 1892; ag. dep. inspr.-gen., July to Sept., 1893; on special service with Mr. McTurk, Feb., 1896, to take over Ururan from Venezuelana, remaining in command there till July, 1895; vol. in Ashanti expdn., Nov., 1895, to Mar., 1896; mentioned in despatches (star); two months' training with R.I.C. at Dublin, 1890; Hythe, P.S., and 1st cl. ambulance certif.; ag. dep. inspr.-gen., Aug., 1896, to Jan., 1899; promoted ch. county inspr., and 2nd in command, 18th Nov., 1899; ag. insp.-gen., 10th May to 21st Aug., 1900; served on comm. apptd. to inquire into adminstr. of poor law relief, 1900; ag. inspr.-gen. of police, and col. comdt. of militia, 8th May to 3rd Sept., 1902; deputy inspr.-gen. of police, Trinidad, 30th September, 1903.

SWAMY, PONNAMBALAM COOMARA.—Mem. legis. coun., Ceylon, and mun. coun., Colombo; proctor, sup. ct., and notary public; served on comm. to inquire into and report on financial condition of Colombo municipality.

SWAN, ROBERT A.—Matric. at Madras Univ., Dec., 1868; passed special law test prescribed for unconvicted supts. and ast.-supts. of pol.; 2nd-class inspr. of pol. in the Cuddapah dist., Madras, May, 1870; in Aug., 1871, 1st-class; May, 1874, passed in Telugu; Oct., 1876, special ast. to sub-collr. and joint mag. of Cuddapah for famine relief duty, and in same month apptd. mag., and placed in charge of the Kadri and Madnapully Taluqs of Cuddapah dist.; in Feb., 1877, stip. mag. Mauritius; Jan., 1878, stip. mag. of Flaoc; and in Aug., 1879, Grand Port and Savanne; mem. of comm. to frame regula. under "labour ordinance of 1878"; member of Lincoln's Inn, 1882; called to the bar, 1887; stip. mag. Br. Guiana, Nov., 1883; in July, 1884, sent on special duty to Madras to organise an emigrn. agency for Br. Guiana; sheriff, Essequibo co., Dec., 1887; chmn. lands titles inquiry comm., Feb., 1890; a revising barrister under Br. Guiana Constnt. Order, 1891; sheriff of Berbice and mag. New Amsterdan, 1892; acted as pol. mag. Georgetown, Feb., 1890, and Oct., 1894, to Apr., 1895; mem. of comm. on poor-relief, 6th Apr., 1900; chairman of ditto, 7th Sept., 1900; appt. as pol. mag. Georgetown, confirmed by S. of S., 1st Oct., 1900; ag. pulse judge, sup. ct., 1st Oct., 1900; pulse judge, Trinidad, 3rd Aug., 1903.

SWAYNE, CHARLES RICHARD.—Stip. mag., Fiji; inspr. of native taxes, 1876; stip. mag. and commr. of sup. ct., Lau prov., 1878; sent to Kadava with special authority in native matters, 1879 and 1880; inspr. of Indian and Polynesian labourers, Lau prov., 1883; stip. mag., regisr., and commr. of sup. ct. at Levuka, 1885; returned to Lau, 1886; ast. nat. commr., Lau, 1888; ag. Br. res., Gilbert and Ellice Is., and special judicial commr. for trial of certain cases, Oct., 1893, to Nov., 1895; stip. mag., Rewa, and commr., Naitasiri, 1898; ag. nat. commr., and mem. ex coun., July, 1901, to Sep., 1902; commr. Naitasiri, Sep. 1902; ag. commr., Rewa, July-Dec., 1903.

SWEET-ESCOTT, SIR E. R., K.C.M.G. (1904), C.M.G. (1896).—B. 1857; ed. Somersetshire Coll., Bath, and at Balliol Coll., Oxon; exhibitioner, Worce. Coll., Oxon, June, 1876; 2nd class classical moda, June, 1878; 3rd class in hist. finals, June, 1880; B.A., July, 1880; classical prof. at the Roy. Coll., Mauritius, June, 1881; précis writer, col. sec.'s office, Feb., and 2nd ast. col. sec., Apl., 1886; ag. col. sec., 1889; ag. col. sec., Br. Honduras, Mar., 1893; admtd. the govt. of that col., Apr. to Nov., 1893; col. sec., Br. Honduras, 1894; admtd. govt. Apl. to Nov., 1895, again in 1897; ag. 1st-class clkr., C.O., Jan., 1898; admstr. of the Seychelles Is., Aug., 1899; assumed govt., 20th Nov., 1899; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Oct., 1903; gov., B. Honduras. 15th Apl., 1904.

SWEETENHAM, SIR F., ATHELSTANE, K.C.M.G.(1897), C.M.G.(1886).—B.1850; cadet, S. Stiltsnts, July, 1870; passed final exam. in Malay, July, 1872; ast. collr. of land rev. for Penang and Prov. Wellesley, Aug., 1872; collr. of land rev., July, 1873; J.P. and mag. and commr., et al. of requesta, Penang, May, 1874; sent on special missions to Perak in Jan., June, and July, 1874; sent to reside with the Sultan of Selangor, Aug., 1874; ast. res., Selangor, Dec., 1874; gazetted to act temporarily as res. of Selangor, 22nd Oct., 1875; took charge of the residency in Perak on the murder of the res., Nov., 1875; dep. commr., Perak, Nov., 1875; mentioned in despatches; ast. col. sec. for native states, Mar., 1876; ast. col. sec., July, 1881; Br. res., Selangor, Sept., 1882; commr. for S. S. and Malay States, Calcutta Exhibtn., 1883-84; ag. Br. res. Perak, Mar., 1894, to Jan., 1896; exec. commr. for S. S. and Malay States, Col. and Ind. Exhibtn., 1896; Br. res., Perak, 1898; res.-gen. Malay States, Jan., 1896; admtd. govt. S. S., Feb., 1900; gov., 1901; ret. 1904.

SWEETENHAM, SIR J. A., K.C.M.G. (1898), C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1846; ed. Clare Coll., Camb.; scholarship, May, 1867; writer, Ceylon service, 1868; ag. pol. mag., Harrispattu, Feb., 1870; pol. mag., Kayta, Sept., 1870, continuing to act at Harrispattu; ag. astt. govt. agt., Galle, Dec., 1871; ag. landing and tide survr., Galle, June, 1872; ast. collr. of customa, Jaffna, Oct., 1872, to continue to act at Galle; ag. dist. judge, Matara, Apr., 1873; 2nd ast. col. sec., and clk. of the legis. coun., 1876; rec.-gen., Cyprus, 1883; audr.-gen., Ceylon, 1891; col. sec., S. S., 1895; admtd. govt., June to Aug., 1895, from Mar. to Dec., 1898, and from Dec., 1899, to Feb., 1901; gov. Br. Guiana, 1901; govr., Jamaica, 14th July, 1904.
SYDNEY, BISHOP OF (AUSTRALIA, METROPOLITAN AND PRIMATE OF) (Bishopric founded 1847), MOST REV. W. SAUMAREZ SMITH, D.D.—Consec., 1890.

SYMON, SIR JOSEPH HENRY.—K.C.M.G. (1901); K.C.; mem. of H. of Ass., S. Australia, 1881-1887; atty.-gen., 1881; Q.C., 1881; representative to Aus. fed. conven., 1897-8, and chrmn. of its judiciary comte.; pres. of fed. league of S. Aust.; pres. of Commonwealth league; mem. of univ. coun.; senr. for S. Aust., Commonw. parl., Mar., 1901; atty. gen., ditto, Oct., 1904. SYMonds, J. D. T.—Served in different regts. of Lancers, 1876-90; asst. inspr., G. coast constab., Mar., 1890; asst. suplt. of pol., S. S., 1891.

TAITt, ALFRED.—Supernumerary clk., Trinidad, Mar., 1889; extra clk., savings bank dept., 1st Sept., 1890; 3rd clk., royal gaol off., 1st Jan., 1891; 3rd clk., educn. off., Apr., 1891; 3rd asst. clk. of the peace, Port-of-Spain, 1st June, 1891; 4th clk., col. sec.'s off., 12th July, 1892; 3rd clk. col. sec.'s off., 16th Aug., 1897; ag. ch. clk., col. sec.'s off., and clk. to leg. coun., Nov., 1895; June, 1897; Nov., 1898; and Nov., 1900; sec. to the col. coun.; priv. sec. to Govr. Sir F. Napier Broome; sec. to the municipal inquiry commn., 1896; clerical asst. to comm. apptd. to inquire into admn. of just. in Trinidad; sec. to industrial educn. commn.; priv. sec. to Sir H. E. H. Jerningham.

TALBOT, MAJ.-GEN. HON. SIR REGINALD ARTHUR JAMES.—K.C.B. (1902); C.B. (1885); B. 1841; Ed. Harrow; formerly A.D.C. to Queen Victoria; M.P. for Stafford, 1869-74; serv. in Zulu War, 1879; Egyptian Campaign, 1882; Nile expdn., 1884-85; lt.-col. com. 1st Life Guards, 1886-88; mil. attache, Paris, 1889-95; comg. cavly. brig., Aldershot, 1896-99; comd. army of occupation, Egypt, 1899-1903; gov. of Victoria, 16th Feb., 1904.

TALMA, EDWY L., B.A. Christ's Coll., Camb., 14th Wrangler, Math. tripos, 1895—Cadet, S. S., Nov., 1896; ag. asst. prot. imigrts., Penang, Aug., 1897, to Mar., 1898; and from Oct., 1898; confirmed Jan., 1901; passed final exam. in Tamil, Feb., 1889; ag. asst. P.M.G., Penang, Apr., 1902; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, May, 1903; ag. 2nd ditto, Nov., 1903; asst. supt. of Indian imigrts., in addition to other duties, Nov., 1903; dep. registr., sup. ct., Singapore, July, 1904.

TANNER, J. E., M.I.C.E., C.M.G. (1894).—B.1834; ed. at the Coll. for Civ. Engrs., Putney; pupil to Messrs. James and William Simpson, and to Mr. (afterwards Sir) Charles Hutton Gregory, on the Graissacq rly. in the Cevennes, France; joined the army works corps before Sebastopol as asst. supt., 1836; and served with the force until the peace, 1856; asst. engr. on the Ind. Guaranteed rly. (Scinde and Punjab), 1856; mem. of the Anarkulle European volrs. throughout the Ind. mutiny; dist. engr., 1858; and engrn.-in-charge of the Sutlej div., Delhi rly., including the Sutlej bridge, 1862; apptd. to survey for a rly. in Trinidad, 1871; and to superintend its construction, 1873; dir. of pub. wks. and gen. supt. of rly., 1876; M.L.C., 1886; ret. 1894.

TARRING, CHARLES J.—Ed. City of Lon. Sch., and Trin. Coll., Camb.; Ist-cl. classical tripos; B.A., 1871; M.A., 1881; called to bar, Inner Tem., 1871; vice-consul and asst. judge, Constantinople, 1883; ag. consul-gen. at various periods from 1883 to 1885; consul, 1887; ag. judge, on occasions from 1884 to 1895, when he became judge of H.M. sup. ct. of Constaple; ch. jus., Grenada, Dec., 1897; author of "Chapters on the Law relating to the Colonies," "A Practical Turkish Grammar," "British Consular Jurisdiction in the East," etc.

TARTE, HON. J. ISRAEL.—A notary by profession, but only practised two years; editor of Le Canadien since 1874; sat in the legis. assem. of Quebec, Feb., 1887, to Dec., 1891; first returned to parlmt. in 1891, and again in 1896; min. of pub. wks. in Sir W. Laurier's govt., 1896-1902.
TASCHEREAU, RIGHT HON. SIR H. E., KT. RACH. (1902), K.C., LL.D., P.C. (1904)—B. 1836; puisne judge, sup. ct., Quebec, 1871; puisne judge supreme ct. of Canada, 1878; ch. just. of Canada 1902.

TASKER, HUGH POLLOCK, B.E., A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Queen's Coll., Cork; prizeman in engrng. 2nd year, and scholar in 3rd year; grad. B.E., Oct., 1879; articled to borough engr., Salford, Apr., 1880; asst. engr., to mun. coun., Salford, Apr., 1881; asst. engr. and survr., mun. coun., Birkenhead, Apr., 1883; exec. engr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, Mar., 1890; ag. dir. pub. wks., May, 1894.
TAUBMAN-GOLDIE, P.C. (1898), THE BR. HON. SIR GEORGE DASHWOOD, K.C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1846; founder and dep. chmn. of the Royal Niger Co.; chmn., 1895.
TAYLOR, A. W. FORBES.—4th clk., treas., Natal, June, 1878; 3rd clk., Apr., 1879; 2nd clk., July, 1881; 1st clk., audit office, Mar., 1884; inspr., audit dept., 1897; ch. inspr., 1903.

TAYLOR, EDWARD B. ANDERSON, C.M.G. (1884).—B. 1827; mem. Bahamas bar; entered civ. ser., 1847; was priv. sec. and col. A.D.C. to Mr. Mathew, when gov. of the Bahamas, also to Mr. Gregory, Sir A. Bannerman, and Mr. Bayley; sec. to bd. of educn., 1861 to 1861; capt.-comdt. New Providence volr. rifle corps, 1853; clk., pol. ct., 1851; pol. mag., 1862; ag. rec-gen. and treas., Oct., 1862, to July, 1863; ag. col. sec., Apr. to July, 1867; ag. asst. just., Sept., 1869; ag. ch. just. from Oct., 1869, to Jan., 1870, and July, 1873, to Jan., 1874; col. sec., Dec., 1874; is ex officio sen. mem. of the exec. coun.; admnstd. the govt. in 1879-84 and 1886; ret. 1890.

TAYLOR, FRANK.—Architectural ast., P. W. D., Cape Town, 3rd Nov., 1900, to 8th Jan., 1901; on active serv., lieut. in irregular corps, 8th Jan., 1901, to 7th Apr., 1902; chief draughtsman, P. W. D., O. R. C., 18th Apr., 1902.

TAYLOR, JOHN.—Entd. Royal Navy, 1883; served in Soudan war, 1884-5 (Medal and Khedive's bronze star); warder, Pentonville prison, Lond., Dec., 1887 to June, 1897; ch. warder, Belize pris., B. Hond., July, 1897; keeper of prisons, 1898.

TAYLOR, R. H. BASIL, F.R.A.S.—Kn. R.N. 1878; served in Egyptian war, 1882 (medal and clasp, Khedive's bronze star); lieut., 1888; served on Mediterranean, N. America, and W. India, China, and home stations; res. commn., 1898; asst. harbmr., Hong Kong, July, 1899; ag. harbmr., Mar., 1900, to June, 1901; and Sep., 1903, to Feb., 1904; M.L.C., June, 1900, to June, 1901; and Sep., 1903, to Feb., 1904.

TAYLOR, W. T., C.M.G. (1895).—B. 1848; collr. of customs and excise, Larnaca, 1879; ch. collr. of customs, Cyprus, 1882; ag. rec-gen., Nov., 1883, to Feb., 1884; ag. commr., Larnaca, in addition to his own duties, 1883 and 1885; ag. recov.-gen. in addition to duties as ch. collr. of cust., Jan. to Nov., 1887; recvr.-gen. and ch. collr. of cust. and excise, 1891; audr.-gen., Ceylon, May, 1895; ag. col. sec., Ceylon, Oct., 1895, to Feb., 1896, Mar. to Dec., 1896, Mar. to Nov., 1899, and Apr., 1900 to Nov., 1901; col. sec., S. Settnsts., June, 1901; admnstd. govt. of S. S., Oct., 1903, to Apr., 1904; ag. rec-gen., F.M.S., Sep., 1904.

TEMPLER, FREDERIC GORDON.—Ed. at Harrow and at Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Inn.Tem., Nov., 1872; went the W. circuit; apptd. dist. judge, Kyrenia, Cyprus, 1882; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., May to Oct., 1888; Nov., 1888, to Feb., 1889; Jan. to Mar., 1890; and May to Sept., 1891; dist. judge, Larnaca, 1891; Queen's advoc., Cyprus, 1893; county ct. judge, York, 1898.
TEMPLE, John Harvey, B.A.—Office asst. to atty.-gen., Ceylon, 18th Jan., 1881; crown counsel, 24th Sept., 1892; has acted as dist. judge, atty.-gen., and solr.-gen. on several occasions.

TENNANT, The Hon. Sir David, K.C.M.G. (1892), Kt. Bach. (1877).—B. 1829; elected speaker of the house of assem., C. of Good Hope, 1874; has for several years represented the div. of Piquetberg in the assem.; mem. of coun. of Cape Univ., 1873; agt.-gen. for the Cape, 1896; ret., 1902.

TENNANT, Hercules.—Barrister-at-law, Inn. Tem.; advoc., sup. ct., Cape col., 1873; sec. of law comsn., 1879; represented Caledon in house of assem., 1879-81; lieut. D.E.O.V.R., 1878-81; extra A.D.C. to gov. and commdr.-in-chief, July, 1879; served in Basuto war, 1880-81 (medal), with rank of capt., as ast. staff offr. to Gen. Clarke, C.B., and afterwards to Col. Carrington, C.M.G.; sec. to ch. just., 1882; librarian of the sup. ct., 1882; ast. registr., sup. ct., and official reviser of authorised edition of Cape statutes, 1884; ast. registr., ct. of appeal, Mar., 1886; J.P. for Cape-town and dist., and Capt. dist., July, 1886; ast. taxing offr., sup. ct., Sept., 1884, and Aug. to Dec., 1885; Apr. and Oct., 1886; Mar. and June, 1887; and Oct., 1887, to Jan., 1889; taxing offr., Feb., 1889; high sheriff, Oct., 1889; registr. of sup. ct., 1894; sec. to law dept., Transvaal, 1st June, 1901; J.P., 1901; advocate of sup. ct., 1902; examiner of candidates for admission as conveyancers, 1902; editor and compiler of "The Notary's Manual;" "The Justice of the Peace's Manual;" "Rules of Court;" "Chronological Table and Index of the Statute Law of the Colony, 1714-1883;" "Masters and Servants Laws of the Colony;" joint editor (by authority) of a revised edition of "The Cape Statutes, 1652-1886, and of the Griqualand W. Statutes;" also compiled (under authority), "The Index of Government Proclamations and Notices, 1863-1881."

TENNYSON, Rt. Hon. Lord, 2nd Baron (U.K., creat. 1884), G.C.M.G. (1903), K.C.M.G. (1899). HALLAM TENNYSON, succeeded 1892 (on the death of his father), Alfred Lord Tennyson, poet laureate, 1850-92.—B. 1859; ed. Marlbr. Coll., Trin. Coll., Camb., and Inner Temple; J.P. for Hants; mem. of Marlbr. Coll. exec. coun.; prepared his father's memoir, published in 1897; gov. S. Australia, 1899; gov.-gen., Commonwealth of Australia, 1902-1904.
THOMAS, E. J.—Entered Victoria service, 1852; receiver of revenue, etc., 1863; acted as sec. to numerous boards and comsns.; priv. sec. to Sir B. O'Loughlin, Bart., 1881; sec., premier's dept., 1883.
THOMPSON, ALFRED.—Apptd., after exam., 3rd cl. messenger, C.O., May, 1880; 2nd cl., July, 1891.

THOMPSON, AUGUSTUS WM.—Asst. clk. of ct. G. Coast, 1873; clk. to Queen's advoc., 1874-5; dep. registr., cent. prov., 1877; ch. registr., 1880; postmnr., Cape Coast, Mar. to Aug., 1884; registr. and interp., W. Prov., 1884; registr. of deeds and taxing master, W. Prov.; distr. comsnnr., G. C. col., May, 1889.

THOMPSON, JOHN.—Ent. Royal Irish constab., Belfast, 1864 to 1870; obtained appttn. in the convict ser., Woking, England; ast. in reforming prison ser. in Jamaica, Oct., 1883; prin. warder and storekr., gen. penitentiary, to July, 1888, then dep. supt. of same; ag. supt. to Dec., 1894; supt. of St. Catherine dist. prison, Spanish Town, Jamaica, Nov., 1897.

THOMPSON, THOMAS AUGUSTUS.—Called to bar, Oct., 1872; in Easter term, 1874, received comsn. to act as public prosecutor in the crim. ct., Bahamas; acted as judge of court of common pleas from June to Dec., 1875; pol. mag., Bahamas (being still allowed priv. practice), Jan., 1876; chmn. of man. comtee. of prisons, and presides over investigations into cases of wreck and other marine casualty; mem. of the Bahamas legislature, and trustee of the Nassau Museum and Library, 1881; stip and circuit mag., 1886; ag. atty.-gen., 1887; ag. ch. just., July, 1890, June to Oct., 1891, and June to Nov., 1892; ag. col. sec. and chmn. of bd. of educn., Oct., 1890; judge and col. sec., Falklands, 1893; admstd. govt., July to Nov., 1894; regar. and marshal, sup. ct., Trinid., 1897.

THOMPSON, W. A.—2nd cl. supervsr. G. Coast, Apr., 1894; ag. ast. treas., Cape Coast Castle, Mar., 1895; cashr., Accra, Mar., 1896; ag. ast. treas., Apr., 1896; 1st cl. supervsr., Sept., 1897; sent on serv. in hinterland, Feb., 1898; invalided and retd from West Afr., Feb., 1900; treas., etc., Falklands, 1901; ag. col. sec., May to Nov., 1902, and Apr., 1903, to Jan., 1904.

THOMPSON, WM. WARDLAW.—Apptd. to pub. wks dep., Cape, 1878; 1st clk., correspondce branch, 1882; ch. record clk., crown lands office, 1885; 1st clk., 1889; dept. lands, mines and agricul., 1892; ag. prin. clk., dep. of agricul., 1896; ag. prin. clk., July, 1897; was on active service with the D.E.O.V.R. in Transkei from Feb. to May, 1879, and in Basutoland, from Sept., 1880, to Mar., 1881.

THOMPSTONE, SYDNEY WILSON.—F.R.C.S., Edin., M.R.C.P., Edin., D.P.H., Camb.; form. house surg., Roy. Infir., Glasgow; apptd. dist. med. offr., Oil Riv. Protec. (now S. Nigera.), Mar., 122nd, 1893; ast. prin. med. offr., 1897; ag. prin. med. offr., Aug.-Nov., 1898; Nov., 1899, to June, 1900, and June, 1901, to Oct., 1902; prin. med. offr., N. Nigeria, May, 1903.

THOMSON, ALEX. MCDONALD.—Ed. Abdin. Univ., M.A.; 1st class hon. math., 1883; ast. prof. of math., Aberdeen, 1886-7; Beacon scholar, Gray's Inn, 1888; cadet, Hong Kong, 1887; passed cadet, Dec., 1890; ag. supt., Victoria gaol, Apr., 1891, to Mar., 1892; ag. clk. of couns., Jan., 1891, to Oct., 1892; ag. ast. col. sec., Mar. to Oct., 1892; ag. registr.-gen., and provisional mem. exec. and legis. couns., 1893; ag. ast. col. sec., 1894; ag. col. treas., Mar., 1895; and provisional mem. exec. and legis. couns.; mem. governing body of Queen's Coll., 1894; postmnr.-gen., 1897; col. treas., 1898; mem. exec. and legis. couns., 1899; ag. col. sec., Apr., 1902; and Nov., 1903, to July, 1904.
THOMSON, HON. DUGALD.—Mem. Leg. Ass., New South Wales, 1894, 1895, and 1898; mem. of Commonw. parl., Mar., 1901, and Dec., 1903; min. for home affairs, ditto, Oct., 1904.

THOMSON, JOHN CHRISTOPHER.—Ed. Edin. Univ.; M.D., 1892; M.B., C.M., with 2nd cl. honours, 1888; M.A., 1884; Edin. Univ. cert. in diseases of trop. climates, 1904; D.P.H. (Edin. and Glas.), 1904; dip. in trop. med. and hygiene (Camb.), 1904; formerly suptd. of Alice Memorial hosp. and Nethersole hosp., Hong Kong; hon. sec. and lecturer on tropical diseases, Hong Kong Coll. of med. for Chinese; ent. H.-K. civil service, med. dept., Jan., 1897; gaol surg., med. offr. in charge of infective diseases hospital, and inspctg. med. offr. of Tung Wah hosp.; J.P., 1898; pres. H.-Kong and China branch of Br. med. assocntn., 1899; also med offr. in charge of post-mortem exams., 1897 to 1901.

THORBURN, HON. SIR ROBERT, K.C.M.G. (1887).—Emig. to Newfoundland, 1852, where he has been engaged in mercantile pursuits; M.L.C., 1870; premier, 1885; deleg. to col. conf., 1887; defeated at genl. election, 1889, and resig. office.

THORBURN, J. JAMIESON.—Writer, Ceylon, Oct., 1886; agt. govt., N. cent. prov., Mar. to Aug., 1889; agt. pol. mag., Galle, Dec., 1893; agt. off. ast. to govt. agt., in various dists. from Oct., 1889, to 1895; agt. 2nd ast. col. sec., Jan., 1896; 2nd ast. col. sec., Jan. and Dec., 1898, and June, 1899; agt. ast. postmr.-gen., Jan., 1899; 2nd ast. col. sec., June, 1899; agt. sec. central irrigation board, Jan., 1900; principal ast. col. sec. and joint sec., loan board, May, 1900.

THORNTON, RIGHT REV. SAMUEL, D.D., 1ST BISHOP OF BALLARAT, VICTORIA.—Consec. 1875; scholar and fellow, Queen's Coll., Oxon; B.A., 1856; incumbent of St. Jude's, Whitechapel, 1860; rector of St. George's, Birmingham, 1864.

THORNTON, SWINFORD LESLIE, R.A.—Lincoln Coll., Oxon; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, Nov., 1877; admitted to the bar, S. Settlimts., Nov., 1886; regis.; sup. ct.; comanr.; ct. of requests and collr. of stamps, Malacca, June, 1887; agt. sen. mag., Singapore, July, 1888; regis.; sup. ct., and mag., Malacca, July, 1892; atty.-gen., St. Vincent, 1894; agt. admstr., Nov., 1894, to Mar., 1895; agt. ch. just., Nov., 1895; rec. mag., Jamaica, 1896; mem. comittee, to revise rules and form rec. mag. cts., 1897; mem. parochial bds. comanr.; and chrmn. house and land tax comittee, 1899; agt. puisne judge, 1901.
TIBBETTS, A. C. K.—Employed col. sec.'s off.; treas. and aud. off., Antigua, Apr. to Dec., 1894; clk. sav. bank, Jan., 1895; 1st clk., post off., Jan., 1898; agt. postmr., Apr., 1899, to Feb., 1900.
TICE, W. G.—Apptd., after exam., 3rd cl. mess., C.O., 29 Feb., 1900; 2nd cl. 1 Feb., 1902.

TILLARD, RICHARD.—Fifteen years' service under Cape govt.; was R.M. and collr. of customs, Port Nolloth, when he retired in 1875; road mag. and J.P. for the several dists. of Cape Col.; served as an offr. of volrs. in the Gaika and Basuto wars; Kaffir war medal, 1877-8; R.M., Mafeking, Jan., 1886; C.C., Dec., 1886; supt. of tola, Br. Bechuanaland, Oct., 1886; R.M., Vryburg, 1889; civ. comanr. and rec. mag., Fort Beaufort, 10th May, 1898.
TIMPERLEY, WILLIAM HENRY.—Ed. at Shrewsbury Gram. Schl., Univ. Berne; entered W. Australian pol., 1856; inspr., 1870; supt. of Rottnest Is., 1880; R.M., Bunbury.

TODD, SIR CHARLES, M.A., K.C.M.G. (1893), C.M.G. (1872).—B. 1826; Fell. Roy. Meteor. Soc.; F.R.A.S.; Fell. Soc. of Tel. Engrs. and Electricians; ast. astronomer, Cambridge, 1848; ast. astronomer, Roy. Observatory, Greenwich, 1854; observer and supt. of telegraphs, S. Australia, 1856; postmr.-gen., supt. of tels. and govt. astronomer, 1870-1901; dep. postmr.-gen., Austral. Commonwealth, 1st Mar., 1901.

TODD, JOHN SPENCER BRIDGES, C.M.G. (1878).—B. 1840; jun. clk., col. sec.'s office, Cape, Aug., 1860; 2nd clk. to C.C. and R.M., Swellendam, Dec., 1862; 2nd cl. clk. to C.C. Robertson, Nov., 1864; 1st clk. to C.C. Swellendam, May, 1867, to Aug., 1874, but was agt. C.C. and R.M., Swellendam, June, 1870, to Jan., 1872, and again during Sept. and Oct., 1872; 1st corrpdg. clk. to the rly. engr. of the col., Aug., 1874, to Mar., 1875; clk. in charge of money orders and stamps, G.P.O., Capetown, Apr., 1875; employed on special service as sec. to a govt. comanr. investigating the acc'ts. and balances of the col. treasy-chest, Capetown, May to Dec., 1875; was exec.-comanr. for the col. at the Paris Exhibition of 1878; ast. col. sec.'s dept., Jan., 1876; in June, 1880, served with Sir H. White and Mr. Lawson (W.O.) and Sir W. B. Gordon (Treasy.), on a mixed comanr. to investigate the expenditure incurred by the impl. and col. govt., in the Transkei war of 1878; proceeded in June, 1881, on special service to Kimberley, with a view to adjusting the acc'ts. of the prov. of Griqualand W.; acted as C.C. and regis. of deeds at Kimberley from Sept. to Dec., 1881; and as ast. comanr. of crown lands and pub. wks. in Capetown from Jan. to Apr., 1882; sec. and ast. to the agt.-gen. for the col. in London, Oct., 1882; agt.-gen., 1895-6.

TOMALIN, H. F., F.R.I. Br. Architects, M.I.C.E., and F.G.S.—Ed. Northampton Gram. Schl.; ast. to C.E. and on L. and N.W. rwy. wks., 1880-6; dist. engrnr. P.W.D., Ceylon, 1886-90; dist. engrnr. Colombo harb., foreshore, and drainage wks., 1887-8 and 1890-2; 2nd final and office ast. P.W.D., Ceylon, 1890-6; designed and constructed post and teleg. bldngs., Colombo, 1892-5; dist. engrnr., Colombo, 1893-6; dist. engrnr. scheme for water supply to the town of Jaffna, Nov., 1896, to Apr., 1897; engrnr., N.W. Prov. and E. Prov., Aug., 1899; prov. engrnr., Cent. Prov. Mar., 1903; ditto, W. Prov., Mar., 1904; ast. D.P.W., Jan., 1905.

TOOKE, W. H.—Porter gold medallist, 1879; Eldon prize essayist, 1890; ch. clk., dept. of agricul., Cape, Sept., 1893; Kaffir war, medal with clasp, 1877-8 campaign; ast. under sec., ch. clk. and accounting offr., Nov., 1901.

TOOKER, H. P.—Ed. at Queen's Coll., Cork, Ire.; grad. bach. of engrnr. at Queen's Univ. (now Royal Univ. of Ire.), 1897; artic. to borough engrnr., Salford, 1890; apptd. ast. engr. to munl. coun., Birkenhead, 1883; apptd. exec. engrnr., p.w. dept., H. Kong, 1890; filled ast. of ag. dir. p.w., May to Aug., 1894, and again in 1900; apptd. ast. dir. p.w.; apptd. assoc. mem. int. civ. engrnr., Dec., 1892; mem. of sec. arts., Feb., 1901.

TOPPIN, CHARLES SAMUEL.—Ed. at Diocesan schl., Waterford, Ireland; entd. W. Australian; serv. as sub-ast. rwy. dept., 1st Mar., 1897; ast. ast. acctnt., 1st July, 1900; ast. ch. acctnt. rwy., 1st July, 1903; aud.-gen. for State of W.A. and deputy ast.-gen. for commonwealth, Feb., 1904.

TORONTO, BISHOP OF (RT. REV. ARTHUR SWEATMAN, D.D., D.C.L.).—Headmnr., Collegiate Inst., London (Ont.), 1865; ast. science master, Upper Canada Coll., Toronto, 1871; rector, Gracechurch, Brantford, 1872; headmnr., Hellmuth Coll., Toronto, 1874; bishop of Toronto, 1879.

TOTHILL, FRANCIS JAMES.—Extra ast. to postmr.-gen., Ceylon, 28th Oct., 1898; ast. inspr. of post offices, Jan., 1899; ast. sec. cent. irrigtn. bd., 15th Jan., 1900; sec. ditto, and office ast. to dir. of irrigtn., 15th May, 1900.
TOURIS, F.—Govt. printer, Seychelles, Jan., 1892; postmr. and rev. stamps offr., Jan., 1894.
TOWAR, JAMES DE LOS.—Principal, Agricultural Coll., Roseworthy, S. Aust., 1902.

TOWNSHEND, CHARLES JAMES.—Ed. Univ. of King's Coll., Windsor, N.S.; grad. with high honours in 1862; B.A. 1863, and B.C.L. 1873; admitted to the bar of N.S., Apr., 1866; Q.C. 1880; elected to the legislature of N.S. in 1878; re-elected in 1882; resig. in 1884, and elected mam. House of Com. of Canada same year; mam. of the exec. comm. of N.S., 1878 till 1882; ast. judge, N.S., 1887.
TOZER, HON. SIR H., K.C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1844; formerly col. sec., Queensland; agt.-gen. for Queensland in London, 1898.

TRANCHELL, E. C. J.—4th astt. to supt., Lower Perak, 1886; 2nd astt. to dist. mag., Kwite, 1886; astt. collr. land rev., Larut, 1883; astt. mag., Ipoh, 1890; ch. astt. to dist. mag., Kwite, 1897; mag., Larut, 1898.

TRAVERS, A. K.—3rd clk., col. sec.'s office, Hong Kong, Oct., 1874; priv. sec. to Admstr. Austin, Mar. to Apr., 1877, and to Gov. Hennessy, Apr. to July, 1877; ag. 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, July to Nov., 1877; 2nd clk., Dec., 1881, to May, 1883; priv. sec. to Admstr. Marsh, Apr., 1882, to Apr., 1883; astt. P.M.G., May, 1883; J.F., Dec., 1883; ag. P.M.G. and collr. of stamp rev., June, 1888.
TRAVERS, B.—Dist. mag., W. dist., Br. Honduras, June, 1882; ditto, Toledo, Mar., 1884; Corosol, Jan., 1886, and Orange Walk, Aug., 1886; comsnr., Famagusta, Cyprus, 1892.

TREACHER, SIR W. H., K.C.M.G. (1904), C.M.G. (1890), M.A., Oxford.—B. 1849; ag. pol. mag., Labuan, Nov., 1871, to June, 1872; ag. col. sec. and audr., Oct. 1873; col. sec., audr., and pol. mag., Apr., 1877, admst. the govt., Dec., 1876, to Feb., 1877; and Oct., 1877, to Apr., 1880; mem. of the legis. coun., Dec., 1874. During the periods he admst. the govt. he acted as consul-gen. in Borneo, and visited Sulu and N. Borneo in connection with Spanish claims; apptd. (1881) the first gov. of Br. N. Borneo, to Dec., 1887; again acted as admstr. of Labuan and consul-gen. in Borneo, from Feb., 1884, to Nov., 1886, and was instrumental in saving Brunei from an attack of the Limbang rebels; sec. to Perak, June, 1888; ag. res., Sept., 1888; res., Selangor, July, 1892; res., Perak, July, 1896; res.-gen., Fed. Malay States, Sept., 1901.

TREGEAR, EDWARD.—Emig. to N. Zealand in 1863; apptd. Goldfields survr., 1867; in command Waikato native contingent, 1875; sec. labour dept. and ch. inspr. of factories, 1891. Is author of "The Maori-Polyesian Dictionary," "The Paumotuan Dictionary," "Fairy Tales of the South Seas," etc., etc.

TRENDELL, SIR A. J. R., KNT. BACH. (1900), C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1836; clk. in science and art dept., London; took part in admstrn. of Col. and Ind. Exhibn., 1886; astt. sec. S. Kensington dept. of science and art.
TRESIDDER, CAPT. TOLMIE JOHN, R.E., C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1850; was engrn. for Malta drainage wks.
TRICKETT, W. J., M.L.C.—Postmr.-gen., N.S. Wales, May, 1883; min. of instruction, May, 1884, to Nov., 1886.

TROTTER, H. B. NOEL CORTLANDT.—Ch. clk., G.P.O., and sec. to govt. savings bank, Singapore, 1877; acted as postmr., Penang, 1890; ag. P.M.G., of S.S., 1883, and 1884 to 1888; J.F.; astt. P.M.G., Penang, 1884; passed in Tamil; temporarily attached to G.P.O., London, 1889; mag., 1890; P.M.G., 1896; also man., govt. savings bank, S.S.; temporarily attached to G.P.O., London, 1897-8.

TROTTER, J. F.—Ast. surv., great trigonometrical survey of India, for seven years; ensign W.I. Regt., 1870; lieut., 1871; 2nd sub-imigrn. agt. of Br. Guiana, Feb., 1872; sen. sub-imigrn. agt., Nov., 1872; in charge of imigrn. dept., Feb. to Sept., 1873; and ag. imigrn. agt.-gen. on several occasions; prot. of imigrts., Mauritius, Apr., 1881; special imigrn. comsnr. to India, May, 1883, to Apr., 1884, and May to Dec., 1889.
TROWELL, WM. JOHN.—Engineer and shipwright, survr. and examnr. of engineers, Liverpool dist., Feb., 1898; inspr. of bd. of agric., Dec., 1898; inspr. of marine surveys, S. Sttlmnts., 16th Jan., 1903.

TRUMP, J., A.M.I.C.E.—Pioneer office, pub. wks., Ceylon, 1878; suptdg. offr., 1879; in charge of convicts, Colombo Breakwater, 1880-3; engaged in rly. and wharf constr. in native states, S.S., 1883-6; dist. engnr., Kuila, Perak, 1886; dep. state engnr., Feb., 1887; ag. state engnr., Oct., 1886, to Oct., 1887.

TRUTCH, SIR JOSEPH W., K.C.M.G. (1889), C.M.G. (1877), F.R.G.S., and M.I.C.E.—B. 1826; ch. comsnr. of lands and wks., and survr.-gen. of Br. Columbia, May, 1864; was a mem. of the exec. and legis. couns. of that col.; was apptd. in 1870 to proceed to Canada as one of the delegs. from Br. Columbia to confer with the govt. on the terms on which the col. should be admitted into the Dominion; lieut.-gov. of the prov. of Br. Columbia, July, 1871, to July, 1876; res. agt. of Dominion govt. in Br. Columbia, 1879-88.
TUCKER, A. L.—Pres. of bd. of land and wks., and comsnr. of crown lands, Victoria, 1883 to 1885; M.L.A. for Fitzroy.

TUDOR, DANIEL THOMAS.—Called to the bar, Gray's Inn, Nov., 1890; eq. dr.; also practised on West. Circ., Bristol, Bath, Somerset, C.O.C., Middx., and N. Lond. Sessions; served as one of the comsnrs. on the Saint Vincent pol. enquiry coman., Sept., 1903; atty.-gen. of Grenada and St. Vincent, July, 1903; also ag. col. sec., Grenada, Apl. and May, 1904; ag. ch. just., Grenada, May to Oct., 1904.

TUPPER, THE HON. SIR C., BART. (creat. 1888), G.C.M.G. (1886), K.C.M.G. (1879), C.B. (1867), M.D., L.R.C.S., Edin. (1843).—Born 2nd July, 1821; is an M.A. and D.C.L. of Acadia Coll., Nova Scotia; is gov. of Dalhousie Coll., Halifax (apptd. by Act of Parlmt., 1862); pres. of the Canadian med. assoc. from its formation, 1867, until 1870; mem. of exec. coun., and prov. sec. N.S., 1867-1860; and from 1863 to June, 1867; prime min. of that prov. from 1864 until he ret. from office with his govt. on the Union Act coming into force, 1st July, 1867; deleg. to England on public business from N.S. govt., 1868 and 1866; from the Dominion govt., Mar., 1868; leader of the delegn. from N.S. to the union confce. at Charlottetown, 1864; to that in Quebec in same year, and to final confce. in London, to complete terms of union, 1866-7; holds patent of rank and precedence from Her Majesty Queen Victoria as an exec. coun., N.S.; sworn of the P.C., June, 1870, and pres. of that body until 1st July, 1872, when apptd. min. of int. rev.; min. of customs, Feb., 1873; resig. office with Sir John Macdonald, 5th Nov., 1873; min. of pub. wks., Oct., 1878; min. of rly. and canals, 1879; represented Cumberland in parlmt. for 29 years in N.S. Assem., from 1836 until confederation in 1867, and in the Commons from that year until he resig. his seat in the cabinet and was apptd. high comsnr. for Canada, in London, May 24th, 1884; acted as exec. comnnr. for Dominion at the Antwerp Exhibn., 1885, and the Col. and Ind. Exhibn., London, 1886; resumed office in Canadian ministry as finance min., 1887; one of H.M.'s plenipotentiaries at fisheries confce. at Washington, 1887; re-apptd. high comsnr., May, 1888; joint plenipo., with the Marquis de Dufferin and Sir J. A. Crowe, for negotiating a commercial arrangement between Canada and France, 1892; S. of S. for Canada, Jan., 1896; premier, Mar. to June, 1896.

TUPPER, Hon. Sir Chas. Hibbert, K.C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1855; son of Sir C. Tupper, mem. of N.S. bar; grad. Harvard Law schl.; mem. house of commons, Canada, since 1882; min. of marine and fisheries, 1888; deleg. to Washington in connection with Behring Sea difficulty, 1890; Br. agt. for the arbitration with the U.S. on the Behring Sea question, 1892-3; min. of just., 1890; resig. 1896.

TURNER, The Rt. Hon. Sir George, P.C., K.C.M.G. (1897), LL.D.—B. 1851; premier and treas. of Victoria, 1894-9 and 19-0; represented the col. in London at H.M.'s Diamond Jubilee 1897; pres. of conf. of premiers at Melbourne, 1899; joined 1st Commonwealth Ministry as treasurer, Jan., 1901; treas. in 2nd (Deakin) ministry; treas. in 4th (Reid-Turner) coalition ministry, 1904.

TURNER, GEORGE.—M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.B., D.P.H. (Cantab.); lecturer on Hygiene, Guy's hosp., 1882-1896; examnr. for D.P.H., roy. coll. of phys. and surge., Lond., 1890-1895; employed by L.G.B. to report upon diphtheria, 1884; and by L.C.C. to enquire into epidemics of enteric fever and the sanitation of River Lea, 1894-95; med. offr. of health, Cape, 1896-1900; mem. of Cape med. coun., 1898-1900; dir. of govt. rinderpest experimental statn., Kimberley, 1897-98; med. offr. of health, Transvaal, 1st Aug., 1900; M.L.C.; census commnr., Transvaal, 1904.

TURNER, WILLIAM.—Ed. at Abdn. and Edin. Univs.; M.A. Abdn., 1876; M.B. Edin., 1879; M.D., 1881; res. phys., roy. hosp. for sick chldn., Edin., 1879; non-res. phys., roy. infrm'y., Edin., 1880; res. accoucheur, Glasgow maternity hosp., 1881; mem. roy. med. soc., Edin., fell obstetrical soc., Edin.; asst. surg. civil hosp., Gibraltar, 1882; also surg. civ. prison, med. offr. lunatic asyl., and dist. med offr., 1882; pub. vaccinator, pd. surg., 1888; surg. to col. hosp., Oct., 1889.

TUTCHER, W. J.—Ed. at Merchant Venturer's schl., Bristol; entered Royal Gardens, Kew, 1888; asst. supt., botan. and afforestation dept., Hong Kong, 1891; actg. supt. in 1893, 1894, 1897, 1900 to 1901, and 1902 to 1903; hon. lecturer on botany, coll. of med. for Chinese.
TWYNNAM, E.—Entered the survey dept. of N.S. Wales as a rly. survr. in 1855; appd. dist. survr. at Goulburn, 1863; ag. surv.-gen., 1889.

TYSER, C. R.—Ed. at Rugby and Trin. Coll., Camb., M.A. (1872); called to bar, In. Tem., 1873; asst. ag. judic. commr. and leg. adver., Cyprus, 1880-1; atty.-gen., Leeward, 1886-9; pres. dist. ct., Kyrena, Cyprus, 1890; puisne judge, Cyprus, Jan., 1902; author of "Marine Insurance Losses," 1894; wrote translation of Omar Hilmi Effendi's Evlifak. Law, 1899; and of the Mejellé, 1901.
TYTLER, R. DUFF.—Supt. of immigrtn., Ceylon, 1st July, 1903.

UDAL, JOHN SYMONDS.—Called to the bar, In. Tem., Michaelmas term, 1875, W. circuit; J.P. for co. of Dorset; dep. county court judge, 1885-6-7; atty.-gen. of Fiji, Aug., 1889; Admty. advoc., Sept., 1889; ag. ch. justice for short periods in 1891-2-3; ag. legal asst. C.O., Feb. to Dec., 1900; ag. ch. justice, Leeward Islands, Dec., 1900; ch. just., June, 1904.
USHER, C. R., F.S.I. (1897).—Asst. survr.-gen., Br. Honduras, Sept., 1890; survr.-gen., Apr., 1895.

USHER, Henry Chas., I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1838; Clk., col. sec.'s office, Honduras, June, 1859; in public treasy., July, 1865; provost-marshal, July, 1869; pol. mag., now called dist. mag. Belize dist., Nov., 1878; in charge of the public treasy., June, 1866, to July, 1867, and Apr. to June, 1869; and of Belize gaol, May to Aug., 1872; has acted as regis. of shipping, survr. of shipping, inquisitor of wreck and casualty, and inspr. of pol.; was marshal of the V.-A. ct., is ex officio judge of the summary ct., and also coroner of the col.; commnr. of currency under Ord No. 32 of 1894; ag. col. treasy., collr. of cust. and postmr., harbrmr., and suptdt. of mercantile marine on many occasions from 1883 to 1900; ex officio regar. of births and deaths, 1885 to 1892; suptdt. fire brigade, 1886 to 1896; commnr. on special duty to the Cays, Aug. to Sept., 1889; ag. col. sec., Aug., 1890; ret., June, 1903.

VALANTIN, W. ADOLPHE.—Asst. clk. and messenger, Queen's advoc.'s chambers, S. Leone, Jan., 1878; acted as asst. sec. to Br. commnr. on the N.W. Liberian Bndry. Commn. in 1879; clk. to crown sovr., June, 1880; acted as clk. to ch. just., 1882; clk. to Queen's advoc., 1883; dep. sheriff, Mar., 1890; clk. to ch. just., Aug., 1891; dep. coroner, July, 1895; ch. clk. atty.-gen.'s chambers, Dec., 1897; curator, intestate estates, Jan., 1902.
VALENTINE, CHAS. JONAS.—Chief inspr. of sheep, S. Australia, 1865; ch. inspr. of stock and regist. of brands, 1879.

VANDER BECK, J. N., I.S.O. (1903).—Extra clk., col. secy.'s office, Singapore, Sept., 1871; 8th clk., Jan., 1873, and attached to govt. house; 6th clk., Dec., 1875; guardian of govt. house, May, 1877; 5th clk., col. secy.'s office, Apl., 1879; 4th clk., June, 1879; 3rd clk., June, 1880; clk., govt. house, Dec., 1883.

VANE, F. W., I.S.O. (1903).—Employed in the G.P.O., Mar., 1871; asst. P.M.G. and inspr. of post offices, Ceylon, Apr., 1872; acted as sec., loan bd., Apr., to Dec., 1874; acted as P.M.G., and dir. of telegs. on several occasions; commnr. of loan bd., Apl., to Dec., 1897; controller of govt. stores, 20th Jan., 1900.

VANE, HENRY G. B.—Asst. audr., Perak, Dec., 1884; ag. asst. audr.-gen. S.S., Apr., 1887; audr. Sungai Ujong and Negri Sembilan, May, 1888; treas., pol. mag., etc., Sungai Ujong, Oct., 1890; audr., Selangor, Dec., 1892; state audr., Perak, Apr., 1895.

VAN NOOTEN, E. H. H.—4th clk., Savings bank, Br. Guiana, Aug., 1881; 6th clk., treasy., May, 1884; 5th clk., Feb., 1886; 3rd class clk., July, 1888; 2nd class, Apr., 1891; 1st class, June, 1897; ag. acct. sup. ct. of civ. justice and acct. registr.'s office, Apr., 1898, to May, 1899.
VAN RENEN, H.—2nd asst. survr.-gen., Cape, Sept., 1897; ag. survr.-gen., May, 1904.
VAN ZIJL, JAMES STRATEN.—Controller, P.O. savings bank, late O.F.S., Sept., 1897; ditto, O.R.C., Mar., 1900.
VARDON, JOSEPH.—M.L.C., S. Aust., 1900; commnr. of pub. wks. and min. of industry, 1904.

VAUDIN, WILLIAM MARSHALL.—Ed. Royal Coll., Maur.; writer, P. W. D., Maur., Jan., 1888; sworn ld. surv., May, 1890; ag. asst. govt. surv., May to Oct., 1890; 2nd learner, P. W. D., April, 1891; ag. asst. surv., May, 1891, to July, 1892; first learner, P. W. D., June, 1893, asgt. asst.
GOVERNMENT SURVEYORS, Dec., 1897, to Sept., 1900; ass't surv., Seychelles, Dec., 1900; superint. of wks. and surveys, Aug., 1901; chmn., local bd. of health, central dist., Apr., 1902.
VECCIO, THOMAS JOSEPH.—Marshal, sup. ct., bkptey. ct., and V.-A. ct., Gibraltar, 1866; also interp., 1866-76.
VELGE, C. E.—Called to the bar, Mid. Tem., Michaelmas, 1870; reg'ar., sup. ct., Singapore, May, 1875; ditto, S. Stiltns., 1st Mar., 1896.

VELLA, FRANCESCO, C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1837; entered the col. serv., Malta, Dec., 1851; was a clk. in the land rev. office; transf'd to the ch. sec.'s office in 1858; ch. clk. and ass't clk. of the coun., 1870; ass't sec. to gov't., and clk. to the coun. of govt. of Malta, Nov., 1871; collr. of customs and supt. of ports, with a seat in the exec. coun. and a seat in the coun. of govt., ex officio; retired, 1902.

VELLA, TOMMASO.—Entd. Malta service, Dec., 1865; clk., May, 1866; dep. collr. of cust., 1892; dep. postmtr., 1896; collr. of cust. and supt. of ports, Sept., 1902; postmtr.-gen., May, 1903; mem. of ex. coun. and of coun. of govt., 1903.

VENN, HARRY WHITALL.—Mem. of the legis. assem. of W. Australia; joined the 1st ministry under responsible govt., Dec., 1890, as comsnr. of rly's. and dir. of pub. wks.; returned in 1890 for the Wellington dist. as mem. of the legis. coun., and has since represented that constituency; chmn. of the comsnr. on agricul., 1887-91; left office, 1896.

VENNING, ALFRED REID.—Unoffl. J.P., and dep. coroner, Mataledist., Ceylon, 1876; treas. and collr. of customs, Selangor, Oct., 1884; also chmn. sanitary Bd. Kuala Lumpur, June, 1890; special comsnr. to report on Cocoons and Keeling Is., 1893; ag. govt. sec., Selangor, Oct., 1893, to July, 1894; ag. clk. mag., Selangor, Mar., 1897; sec. to govt., Perak, Sept., 1897; fed. sec., Fed. Malay States, Aug., 1903.
VENNING, EDW., A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Univ. Coll., Lond.; supting. offr. P.W. dept., Ceylon, 1870; ag. prov. asst., 1878; prov. engnr., Sabaragamuwa, 1893; financial assist. to dir. of wks., 1896.

VENNING, G.—App'td., after compet. exam., asst. clk. and assigned to local gov't. bd., May, 1899; after further compet. exam., apptd. clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv., and assigned to W.O., 8th Jan., 1900; transf'd to C.O., 16th Apr., 1903.

VERNON, HON. F. G.—Ed. in Eng. for R.E.; came to Br. Columbia, 1863; elected to local legislature, 1875; mem. exec. coun. and ch. comsnr. of lands and wks., 1876; ret., 1878; re-elected, 1878, and remained in legislature till 1882, then ret.; re-elected in 1887, and again apptd. ch. comsnr. of pub. wks. and lands and mem. of exec. coun. for prov.; agt.-gen. for Br. Columbia in London, 1895.

VERNON, ROLAND VENABLES.—Ed. at Clifton and Oxford; scholar of Balliol, 1895-9; 1st cl. Mods., 1897; prox. acc. Hertford schlr.; Craven schlr., 1897; Jenkyns exhib., 1899; 1st cl. Lit. Hum., 1899; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in Sec.'s dept. inland revenue, Nov., 1899; 2nd cl. clk., C.O., Aug. 13th, 1900; sec. to Trinidad riot comsnr., 1900.
VICKERS, EDWARD.—Emmanuel Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1864; called to the bar, In Tem., Jan., 1868; advoc. in the high ct., Bombay, Dec., 1868; res. mag., Jamaica, Mar., 1888.

VIGORS, CHARLES THORNTON DOONE.—Ceylon writer, 1878; pol. mag., 1883; asst. collr. of customs, Trincomalee, 1884; ditto, Galle, 1886; ag. off. asst. to govt. agt., S. prov., from Sept., 1886, to Mar., 1888; ag. dist. judge, Kegalla, Apr., 1888; Tangalla, Dec., 1889; landing survr. cust., Colombo, Nov., 1888, resumed duties, Feb., 1890; ag. pol. mag., Kandy, May, 1894; asst. govt. agt., Matara, May, 1894, resumed same, Jan., 1896; Kalutara, May, 1898; govt. agt., Uva, Aug., 1900; asst. govt. agt., Kalutara, 13th May, 1902.

VILLIERS, FRANCOIS JOHN, C.M.G. (1880).—B. 1851; entered the C.O., Sept., 1869; priv. sec. to the admtr. of Griqualand W., Oct., 1875, and clk. of the coun.; ag. col. sec., May, 1877, to Oct., 1880; mem. of legis. coun., 1879; engaged on special service in connection with the annexation of the prov. to the Cape Col. from Nov., 1880, to May, 1881; audr.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1882; ag. govt. sec., at various times between 1883 and 1896; ret., 1898.

VIRET, ALFRED PERCIVAL.—Rev. offr., Dominica, May, 1883; sec. bds. of health and quarantine, Aug., 1884; sec. to local comtee., Ind. and Col. Exhibn., 1886; confid'l. clk. to pres., and clk. exec. coun., May, 1886; govt. offr., treas. and quarantine offr., June, 1891; 1st cl. supervisor of cust., G. Coast, Oct., 1897; asst. col. treas., S. Leone, July, 1899; acted as col. treas., S. Leone, from July to Oct., 1899, and from Jan. to Sept., 1901; acted as col. P.M.G., from June to July, 1900, and from Oct. to Mar., 1902; apptd. collector of customs, 3rd Nov., 1902.
VITALIS, NICHOLAS.—Ch. interp. and clk. to comsnr., Larnaka, Cyprus, July, 1878; ditto, Nicosia, Mar., 1879; clk. and interp. to Queen's advoc., Feb., 1892.
VON DOUSSA, LOUIS.—Mem. of H. of Ass., S. Aust., 1899-1902; M.L.C., 1903; atty.-gen., 1903-4.

WACE, HERBERT, C.M.G. (1903).—B. 1861; ed. at Westminster Schl.; Ceylon writer, 1873; asst. collr. of customs, Trincomalee, 1876; asst. govt. agt., Matara, 1886; N'Eliya, 1887; ag. govt. agt., Ratnapura, 1889; 2nd asst. col. sec., 1889; comsnr. of requests, Colombo, 1890; govt. agt., Ratnapura, 1891; ag. govt. agt., Matara, May, 1896; 2nd asst. col. sec., Mar., 1899; com. of requests, Colombo, Dec., 1890; govt. agt., Sabaragamuwa, Feb., 1892; ag. govt. agt., Kurunegala, 1896; govt. agt., C. Province, Jan., 1900.
WAINWRIGHT, HAMILTON.—Supernum. clk., audit office, Trinidad, 1872; in treasy., 1873; 7th clk., treasy., 1874; ch. clk., col. hosp., 1874; ag. sup't lun. asyl., 1878-9; 2nd clk., customs, 1883.

WALCOTT, H. B.—Excise and customs offr., St. Lucia, 1884; treasy. acctnt., 1890; ag. ch. clk., treasy., 1891; ag. ch. clk. and acctnt., St. Vincent, 1892; ag. treasy. acctnt., Grenada, 1892; ch. clk., audit office, Dec., 1893; ag. postmtr., 1895-6; ag. ch. of pol. and excise, 1897; 2nd audit clk. col. sec.'s off., Br. Honduras, 1897; audr., Feb., 1898; ch. asst. treas., Gold Coast, Jan., 1900; ag. treas., and mem. ex. and leg. couns., dir., pub. offr.'s guarantee fund, mem. bd. of educn., July, 1900, to Jan., 1901, and May to Oct., 1902; ag. comsnr. of police and supt. of prisons, 1901-1902; pres. Accra town coun., 1901-1902; treas. and collr. of cust., regiar. of shipping, pres., quarantine bd., chmn. poor relief comtee., mem. of bd. of educn., mem. ex. and leg. couns., Br. Hond., Mar., 1903; J.P., Apr., 1903.

WALCOTT, REV. J. EVANS, S.C.C.—Ed. Codington Coll. (Barbados); theol. exhib., 1865; Rawle scholar, 1867, etc.; headmr., St. Lucy Mid. Schl., 1869; curate, St. Silas and St. Albans, 1872; public librarian, 1877; compiled classified catalogue, 1879.
WALCOTT, RICHARD AQUINTUS.—Solr., Jamaica, 1883; also notary public, 1885; res. mag., St. James, Jamaica, 1888.
WALKEM, G. A., K.C.—Atty.-gen. and ch. commr. of lands and wks., Br. Columbia, Canada, 1874 to 1878; apptd. to the bench of sup. ct., Br. Columbia, 1882.

WALKER, SIR EDWARD NOEL, K.C.M.G. (1888), C.M.G. (1885).—B. 1842; ed. Chelt. Coll.; in office of gov.-in-ch. of Windward Is., Feb., 1862; priv. sec., May, 1866; previously priv. sec. to the lieut.-gov. of St. Vincent from Nov., 1864; asst. gvt. sec. and clk. of the ct. of policy and combined ct., Br. Guiana, Feb., 1867; acted as gvt. sec. on several occasions; asst. col. sec., Jamaica, May, 1874; col. sec., June, 1883; ditto, Ceylon, 1887-1901; admtd. gvt., Mar., 1888, Jan., 1889, Apr., 1893, Dec., 1894, Oct., 1895; lieut.-gov., Dec., 1890; resumed duties as col. sec. at various dates from July, 1883, to Feb., 1896; admtd. gvt., Mar., 1899; retd., 1901.

WALKER, Lieut.-Gen. Sir F. W. E. F., G.C.M.G., K.C.B., C.B. (1879).—B. 1844; entered army, 1862; A.D.C., Mauritius, 1866-7; mil. sec., Cape, 1873-8; served in S. African wars, 1878-9 and 1899; and in Bechuanaland expln., 1884-5.

WALKER, HENRY.—2nd ass't., land dept., Br. N. Borneo, Jan., 1883; asst. supt., lands and pub. wks., Kudat, July, 1883; commr., land, and recorder of lands, May, 1884; mem. coun., Oct., 1883; ag. supt., P.W. dept., Jan., 1887.
WALKER, Hopson Puckney, B.A.—Jesus Coll., Camb., 1863; LL.B. (Lond.), M.A. (Melb.); barrister-at-law (Mid. Tem.), 1888; ch. clk., sup. et., Melbourne, Dec., 1883.

WALKER, JAMES HUTCHINSON.—Ed. Abdn. Univ., M.A. (nat. sci. hon.), 1876; M.B., C.M. (highest hon., Murray scholar, and gold medal), 1878; M.D. 1884; asst. prof., botany, Abdn., 1876; prin. med. offr., N. Borneo, Aug., 1881.

WALKER, JOHN BAYLDON.—Called to the bar, In. Tem., Nov., 1875; went N.-E. circuit; pol. mag. and coroner, S. Leone, 1890; ag. judge, sup. ct., 1891; ag. Q.A., June, 1892; and extraord. mem. legis. coun., Sept., 1892; received thanks of coun., 1893, for preparing Freetown municipality order; ag. Queen's advoc., May, 1894; judge, Turks Is., 1896; ag. commr., 1895; ch. just., island of St. Vincent, Feb., 1898; S.J.P., Port of Spain, Trinidad, 1901; ch. just., St. Lucia, 1902.

WALKER, LT.-COL. S.F. FROWD, C.M.G. (1891).—B. 1860; entered Sandhurst, 1869; ensign 28th foot, 1871; capt., June, 1891; hol. lieut.-col., 1889; A.D.C. to gov., S.S., 1878-9; attached to Perak armed pol., Mar., 1879; dep. commr. of the force, Feb., 1880, and in command from Nov., 1882; acted on various occasions as pol. mag., asst. res., and as sec. to gov.; has received the thanks of gov't. several times for his action in dealing with Chinese and coolie disturbances; in command of Pahang expdny. force, 1892 and 1894; ag. sec. to gov't., Perak, Oct., 1894, to Aug., 1895.

WALL, J. DARRELL.—Clk. and storekeeper, Holberton Instlt., Antigua, Nov., 1891; shorthand reporter to W. Ind. Roy. Coman., 1897; aud. def. force accts.; ag. ch. clk. to aud.-gen., Leeward and Fedrl. accts., June, 1899; also clk. to trade and income tax commsnr., Sept., 1899; ch. clk. to aud.-gen. and Fedrl. accts., May, 1900.

WALLACE, WM., C.M.G. (1897).—Senior ex. offr., Roy. Niger Co., 1886; later as agt.-gen., until 1900, when Niger territrs. were handed over to H.M. gov't.; oomld. numerous treaties with native potentates, one with the Sultan of Sokoto, 1894; commanded or served with over 30 expds.; deputy high commr. and senior res., N. Nigeria, 1900; admtd. gvt., May to Dec., 1901, and May to Nov., 1903 (3 W. African medals, 5 clasps, coronation medal).
WALLACE, WILLIAM HENRY.—Ent. gov. ser., 1882; sec. of mines, Tasmania, 1898.

WALLIS, C. BRAITHWAITE, F.G.S., F.R.G.S., etc.—Ed. privately from Eton, and at Shrewsbury and Mil. Coll., Oxford; 2nd lieut., 4th Manchester regt., 1894; lieut., 1895; instr. of musketry, 1896; capt., 1897; "Special mention" certif., schl. of instruction, and "extra" certif., schl. of musk., 1896; instr. of musk. to 3rd Border regt., 1896, and to 3rd Roy. Welsh Fus., 1897; specially commended by G.O.C. dist. for performance of duties; nominated for admission to R.I. Constab., 1897; apptd. to S. Leone Front. Force, Jan., 1898; ag. dist. commr., 1899; Mendiland, Sherbro', and Karene expdns., 1898; Protectorate expedn., 1899; twice mendt. in despatches; in command of a column; rec. thanks of gov't., West Africa medal and clasp; transf'd. to 1st batt., Cameronians, 1899; to India, Oct., 1899; selec. for serv. in Somaliland, 1901; asst. dist. commr. S. Leone, 1901; ag. commr., Apr., 1902, to Mar., 1903; awarded silver medal and certif., R. Humane Soc., Dec., 1901, for saving life in West Africa; again selected for service in Somaliland, June, 1903; J.P. for S. Leone; F.R.C.I.; author of "The Advance of our West African Empire," and "West African Warfare" (Unwin, 1903).

WALLIS, HENRY RICHARD.—Asst. agt., Chinde, 15th Sept., 1893; Brit. vice-consul, Fort Johnston, 1st Oct., 1897; 1st clk. asst., B. Coli. Africa Prot., 1st July, 1898; ag. judicial clerk, Jan. to Nov., 1899; rec'd. commr. as H.M. vice-consul, 9th June, 1900; ag. consul, Mozambique, Feb. to Dec., 1901; apptd. vice-consul, Tete, 1st Apl., 1901; ag. dep. commr., B. C. Africa Prot., July, 1902, to Feb., 1903; asst. dep. commr., 1st Apl., 1904.

WALPOLE, SIR CHARLES GEORGE, M.A., Kt. BACH (1897).—B. 1848; ed. at Eton and at Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, In. Tem., Jan., 1873; went the home (afterwards the S.E.) circuit; prov. dist. ct., Larnaca, Cyprus, 1882; atty.-gen., Lee-ward Is., 1889; mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; acted as ch. just., June to Sept., 1890, and June to Nov., 1890; ag. ch. just., Gibraltar, Nov., 1892, to Nov., 1893; ch. just., Bahamas, Jan., 1894; ret., 1897.
WALSH, FRANK.—Entered N.S. Wales civ. ser., legis. assem. dept., 1874; partly, librarian, 1889.

WALTER, W. G. C.—Served in R. Navy from July, 1877, to Feb., 1886; apptd. to Roy. Niger Co.'s constabulary, Nov., 1886, to Aug., 1894; took part in expdns. of 1886-1894, Nigeria med. and clasp; harbour mr., Klang, Selangor, F.M.S., Jan., 1895; dep. regist., imports and exports, Klang, Feb., 1895; 1st cl. mag., July, 1895; conservator of the port of Klang and regist. of imports and exports, Dec., 1897; ag. dist. offr., Klang, on occasions from Feb., 1897, to Feb., 1901.

WALTON, GEORGE O'DONNELL—Matriculated a student of Lond. Univ., June, 1891; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., 17th Nov., 1893; ag. on several occasions as pol. mag., Barbados; judge of the petty debt court of Bridgetown; judge of the ast. ct. of appeal; ag. pol. mag., Bridgetown and dist. "A" from 21st July, 1902, to 14th Feb., 1903; pol. mag., dist. "F," 9th Dec., 1902.
