WARD, HENRY THOS. SIMPSON, M.I.C.E.—Suptdg. offr. of irrigtn., Ceylon, 21st May, 1876; dist. engr., June, 1886; prov. engr., 1st Nov., 1894; financial and office asst., 7th May, 1895; asst dir. of pub. wks., 23rd Jan., 1898; dir. of irrigtn., 15th May, 1900.

WARD, W. F.—Assoc. Roy. Schl. of Mines, Lond. (1876).—Analyst, etc., to the govt. of Tasmania, 1882; asst. to Dr. T. Percy and Prof. W. C. Roberts-Austen, etc., in chemical and metallurgical laboratories of Roy. Schl. of Mines, Royal Mint, and Normal Schl. of Science, S. Kensington (1876-80); special apptmt. to Potosi Gold Mining Co., Venezuela (1881); in charge of chemical testing of smoke-consuming appliances at exhibition, S. Kensington (1881).

WARE, FABIAN.—Ed. priv. and Unis. of Lond. and Paris (B.-es-Se., 1894); asst. mast. in secondy. schl., Bradford gram. schl., 1895-9; examnr. in French to civ. serv. comm., 1898-1901; prov. inspr. of secondy. schl. to bd. of educm., 1901; representative of educm. comttee of royal comn. at Paris exhbntn., 1900; ag. asst. dir. of educm., O.R.C., 28th June, 1901; asst. dir. of educm., Transvaal, 17th Oct., 1901; dir. of educm., 1st July, 1903; corres. mem. of nat. educm. assoc. of U.S.A.; author of articles on German educm. in spec. rpts. of bd. of educm., 1898-9; translatn. of "Mon Testament" (Père Hyacinthe), 1895; "Educml. Reform—The Task of the Bd. of Educm.", 1900; "Educml. Foundtns. of Trade and Industry", 1901.

WARNER, GEORGE BRUNTON.—Extra clerical asst., G.P.O., P.M. Burg., from Sept. 1st, 1881, to Feb. 16th, 1882; 3rd class clk. civ. ser., and filled the post of 7th clk., G.P.O., Feb. 16th, 1882; as 6th clk., Jan. 18th, 1883; clk., ool. engineer's dept., Jan. 13th, 1886; border customs offr., De Jager's Drift, Jan. 8th, 1887; issuer of native passes, Dundee div., Jan. 16th, 1888; sheep inspr., De Jager's Drift, May 7th, 1888; J.P., Dundee div., Dec. 13th, 1892; chief examining offr., Customs Point, Durban, Nov. 21st, 1896; ag. clk. of the court, Newcastle, June 16th, 1900; clk. of the court, Newcastle, Aug. 9th, 1900; J.P., Newcastle div., Aug. 16th, 1900; deputy registrar special court, Newcastle div., Aug. 23rd, 1900, in terms of sec. 25 of Act No. XIV., 1900; has acted as asst. and ag. mag. of Newcastle on many occasions; also for the following periods:—Act. asst. mag. from Nov. 19th, 1900, to March 24th, 1901; ag. mag. from June 6th, 1901, to Sept. 8th, 1901; ag. asst. mag., Sept. 9th, 1901.
WARNER, GEORGE VALENTINE.—Clerical asst., comnr's off., St. Kitts, 1891; 2nd clk., treasy., Apr., 1896; rev. offr., 1896; acctnt., treasy., 1897.
WARREN, A.—Apptd. asst., treasy., O.R.C., 23rd July, 1900; asst. bookkeeper, funds dept., 1st July, 1900.

WARREN, LIEUT.-GEN. SIR CHARLES, R.E., K.C.B., G.C.M.G. (1885), K.C.M.G. (1883), C.M.G. (1878)—B. 1840; ed. Chalt. Coll.; was specially employed in various military and administrative capacities in Griqualand W. and the Cape Col. in 1876-8; lieut.-col. for distinguished service; adminstr. govt. of Griqualand W., 1879 to 1880; served in Egyptian campaign, 1882, and brought to justice the murderers of Prof. Palmer and his party; H.M.'s special comnr. in Bechuana land, 1884-5, with local rank of maj.-gen., comdg. expedn.; apptd. to command at Suakim, with rank of maj.-gen., and as gov.-gen., 1886; comnr. of metropol. pol., 1886-8; C.R.E., and in command of troops with rank of maj.-gen., Singapore, 1889 to 1894; commanding a div. in war against Transvaal and Orange F. S., 1899-1900.

WARREN, COL. FALKLAND GEORGE EDGEBORTH, R.A., C.M.G. (1880)—B. 1834; ed. King William's Coll., Isle of Man, and R.M.A., Woolwich; 2nd lieut., R.A., 1852; capt., 1859; major, 1871; lieut.-col., 1877; col., 1881; served in Indian Mutiny campaigns, 1857, 1858, and 1859; Umbeyla campaign, 1862 and 1863; Bhootan campaign, 1865 and 1866. Six times mentioned in despatches; medals and four clasps; asst. comnr., Larnaca, Cyprus, July, 1878; comnr., Limassol, Oct., 1878; ch. sec. to govt., Ang., 1879; mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; ret. 1890.
WARREN, PHILIP DAVID, F.R.G.S.—Dist. surv., Ceylon, 1st Oct., 1879; office asst., 16th June, 1886; 4th ch. survr., 1st Jan., 1896; asst. survr.-gen., 26th Mar., 1897; has acted as survr.-gen.

WATKINS, FREDERICK HENRY, I.S.O. (1904).—Acted as rev. offr., St. Lucia, 1882; inspr. of schl., St. Vincent, 1883; headmr. of gram. schl., St. Vincent, 1887; inspr. of schl., Leeward Is., 1889; ag. pol. mag., Grenadines, 1886; reported on St. Lucia schl., 1888; adjt., St. Kitts-Nevis volr. forces, 1897-9; comnr. of Montserrat, Jan., 1900.
WATSON, E. C.—M.H.A., Trinity, Newfild., 1885-9; supt. fisheries, 1899.
WATSON, JAMES.—Col. treas., N.S.W., Dec., 1878, to Jan., 1883.
WATSON, J. J.—Res. mag., Jansenville div., Cape Col., Jan., 1890.

WATSON, REGINALD GEORGE.—Ed. at Haileybury; cadet, S.S., 1883; priv. sec. to Sir C. C. Smith, 1884-5; attached to land office, Malacca, 1887; ag. 2nd asst. prot. Chinese, Singapore, 1888; ag. mast. ditto, 1888-9; dist. offr., South, prov. Wesley, 1889; ag. dist. offr., Balik Pulau, 1889; 2nd asst. prot. of Chinese, Penang, 1888; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, July, 1890; prot. of Chinese, Perak, Oct., 1891; ag. sec. to govt., Perak, Sept., 1896, and Mar., 1898; sen. mag., Selangor, July, 1896; sec. to govt., Selangor, Apr., 1899; ag. sen. mag., Perak, June, 1899; senior mag., Sept., 1901; ag. sec. to res. Perak, May, 1902; comnr. of lands and mines, F.M.S., 27th Feb., 1904.
WATT, JAMES.—Ed. at Oxford; classical schlr., Balliol Coll., 1889; B.A. 1896; apptd. asst. dist. comnr., Southern Nigeria, 1899; dist. comnr., Feb., 1902.

WATTLEY, ERNEST CHAS.—Ed. Gram. Schl., St. Kitts, Crawford Coll., Maidenhead, and London Internat. Coll.; called to bar (Leeward Is.) Aug., 1877; J.P., St. Kitts, 1885; loc. comnr. of incumbered estates ct., 1886; visiting just. of the gaol, 1887; escheater-gen., St. K.-N., 1889; ag. asst. to attorney-gen., 1900; mem. ex. and leg. couns., and asst. atty.-gen., Aug., 1901.

WATTS, FRANCIS, C.M.G. (1904), D.Sc., F.C.S., F.I.C.—Analytical chemist, Antigua, Jan., 1889; mem. leg. couns., 1897; analytical and agricultural chemist, Jamaica, May, 1898; Leeward Is., May, 1899 (in connection with the Imperial dept. of agriculture for the W. Indies); mem. exec. couns., Leeward Is., Oct., 1899; mem. ex. couns., Antigua, Dec., 1903.

WAY, RT. HON. SIR SAMUEL JAMES, Bart. (1899).—B. 1836; ch. just., S. Australia, Mar., 1876; lieut.-gov., Jan., 1891; Hon. D.C.L. (Oxon.), 1890; LL.D. (Adelaide), 1892; hon. LL.D. (Queen's Univ., Kingston, Canada), 1895; (Cantab.), 1897; (Melbourne), 1901; called to the bar, S. Australia, 1861; Q.C., 1871; mem. of house of assem., and atty.-gen., 1875; vice-chancellor of the Univ. of Adelaide, 1877 to 1883, and chanc. from 1883; admstd. the govt. S. Australia nine times—1877 (twice), 1878, 1883, 1889, 1893-4, 1895, 1898-9; mem. of the judicial comte. of H.M.'s P.C., 1897.

WEBB, FREDERICK WILLIAM, C.M.G. (1894).—B. 1837; sess. clk., legis. coun., N.S. Wales, 1851; elk., G.P.O., 1853; elk., legis. assem. dept., 1860; ag. elk. of assem., 1877-86; elk., 1888; apptd. J.P. of col., 1888; sec. to National Australasian Convention, 1891.
WEBB, T. PROUT, of Lincoln's Inn.—Master of equity and lunacy, Victoria, Oct., 1884; comsnr. of titles, June to Dec., 1885.
WEBER, ARTHUR F. C.—Ed. Merton Col., Ox., B.A., 1896; called to bar, Inn. Temp., Feb., 1896; ag. stip. mag., 1898, and March to Oct., 1900, Br. Guiana; stip. mag., Essequibo, Oct., 1900.

WEBER, OSCAR WM.—Clk. asst., prisons office, B. Guiana, 1887; 5th cls. clk., P.O., 1889; transf. to secretariat, 1895; 4th cls. clk., 1896; 3rd ditto, 1899; 2nd ditto, 1901; is also sec. to bd. of agric.
WEDDERBURN, W., Q.C.—Prov. sec. and rec.-gen., N. Brunswick; ct. judge, 1882.

WELBY, FRED. E., F.R.C.S.E.—Ed. Diocesan Coll., Rondebosch, Cape; King's Coll., Lond., and Edin. Univ.; 1st class honours in medicine, medal in botany, 1st class honours in midwifery and diseases of women, asst. demonstrator of anatomy, Edin., L.R.C.P.E., L.R.C.S.E., etc.; fellow of Obstetrical Soc., Edin.; col. surg., St. H.lena, 1889.

WELDON, HORACE.—Served in S. African war, 1899-1901 (medal and 2 clasps); ch. inspr. of mines, Transvaal, 12th Apl., 1901; govt. mining engnr., 1st Mar., 1902; ag. comsnr. of mines, M.L.C.; while ag. comsnr. of mines serves as mem. ex. coun.

WELMER, GERARD WILFRED.—Ed. Oscott Coll., Birmingham; asst. mag., Matang, Perak, May, 1883; mag., Thaiping, 1884-9; ag. state treas., Nov., 1886, to Mar., 1887; ag. collr. and mag., Krian, Jan., 1889, to May, 1890; ag. chief mag. and comsnr. of lands, Selangor, May, 1890; govt. sec., Selangor, Nov., 1890.

WENDT, HENRY LORENZ.—Advoc. sup. ct. Ceylon, 1880; unof. mem. of legis. coun., 1895-1900; mem. incorpd. coun. of legal educn.; ag. atty.-gen., Apr.-Nov., 1900, and from Dec., 1900; puisne judge, 15th Oct., 1901.

WENTZEL, CHAS. AUGUSTUS.—Legal adviser to mil. govr., Pretoria, June, 1900; res. mag., Johannesburg, Apl., 1901; senior mem. of spec. crim. ct., J'burg., Apl., 1901-3; mem. gold law comsn., 1902; chrmn. of J'burg. insanitary area improvmt. scheme comsn., 1902-3; ch. mag. for Witwatersrand dist., May, 1902; advocate of sup. ct., Transvaal.

WERRY, CAPT. FRANCIS EDWARD.—Ed. at I. of Wight Coll. and Uppingham schl.; capt., 4th batt. Essex regt.; dist. comsnr., Lagos, July, 1902; ag. comsnr. of pol., Aug. to Oct., 1902; ag. trav. comsnr., N.E. dist., Jan. to June, 1903.
WESSELS, J. W.—Legal adviser to commdr.-in-chief, July, 1900, to Apl., 1901; puisne judge, sup. ct., Transvaal, May, 1902.
WESTBROOK, WALTER FRANCIS.—Apptd., after compet. exam., elk. of the lower (now 2nd) div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Nov., 1880; supt. of the registry, 1892; ch. registr., 1896.

WESTON, SAMUEL MARTIN.—Clk. to warden, N. and S. Naparima ward unions, Trinidad, 1874; elk. to comsnr., S. prov., 1879; sec. to dist. agricul. bd., Naparima ward union, 1886; acted as warden of the union, Oct., 1887; additional supervisor of the union, Feb., 1888; elk. to warden, Naparima, 1890.
WETMORE, A. R.—Atty.-gen., N. Brunswick, Sept., 1867; judge, sup. ct. of judicature of the prov., May, 1870; also sole judge, ct. of divorce causes, 1881.

WETMORE, EDWARD LUDOW.—Ed. at King's Coll., Fredericton, 1859; barrister, N. Brunswick, 1864; dep. elk. of crown, 1869-72; mayor, Fredericton, 1874-77 Q.C., 1881; elected to legislature, 1882-6; leader of Opposition, 1883-6, when he was not re-elected; judge, N.W. Territories, 1887.
WHEELER, CHARLES HENRY, M.D. (Queens Univ., Ire.), L.M.R.H.D.—Surgeon in the navy, Aug., 1881; dist. surgeon, Krian, Perak, Jan., 1887; J.P. for Krian, 1887.
WHEELER, R. P.—Apptd. typist, Imperial military railways, O.R.C., 1st Nov., 1900; ditto, refugee camps, 17th July, 1901; ditto, treasury, 15th Nov., 1902.
WHEELER, W.—C.M.G. (1904); Treas. Br. Cent. Africa Prot., 28th Feb., 1592.

WHITE, LT. COL. FREDERICK, C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1847; elk., dept. of just., Canada, 1869; transf. to dept. of S. as elk.-in-charge of mounted pol. branch, Apr., 1876; ch. elk., Sept., 1878; comptroller of N.-W. mounted pol. force of Canada, Nov., 1878; dep. head, July, 1883; priv. sec. to Rt. Hon. Sir John Macdonald, 1880 to 1882.

WHITE, FIELD-MARSHAL SIR GEORGE S., D.C., G.C.B., G.C.S.I. (1898), G.C.M.G. (1901), G.C.I.E. (1893), G.C.V.O. (1900).—B. 1835; commander-in-chief, India, 1893-8; Q.M.G., 1898-9; commanding troops in Natal at outbreak of S. Afr. War, 1899; defended Ladysmith, Nov., 1899, to end of Feb., 1900; gov. Gibraltar, 1900-1905.

WHITE, HERBERT.—Ed. Shrewsbury Schl., cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., 1879; pol. mag., Avissawela, 1886; asst. agt., Nuwara Eliya, 1885; ag. gov't. agt., Ava, 1896; 2nd asst. col. sec., and sec. cent. irrng. bd., 1896; ag. gov't. agt., S. prov., 1896 to Jan., 1897; prin. asst. col. sec., and sec. cent. irrng. bd., May, 1899; mayor of Colombo, May, 1900; comsnr. of requests, Dec., 1901; ag. pol. mag., Colombo, 20th Feb., 1903; registr.-gen., 30th Mar., 1903; comsnr. of requests, 27th Nov., 1903.

WHITE, LIBUT.-COL. WILLIAM, C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1830; elk. in G.P.O., London, Feb., 1846; res. Apr., 1854; ch. elk. in money order branch of P.O. dept., Canada (on its establishment), Dec., 1854; sec. of the dept., Jan., 1861, and a comsnr. to inquire into the organisation of the civ. ser., June, 1880; author of a "Post Office Gazetteer" of the Dominion; and of the "Annals of Canada," published in the "Canadian Monthly"; is lieut.-col. comdg. 43rd batt. of Canadian militia; and a J.P.; commd. Canadian team sent to Wimbledon in 1884; dep. P.M.G., July, 1888.

WHITE, W. ARTHUR.—Apptd. after open compet. exam. for home civil serv. to P.O., Jan., 1890; 2nd div. elk., acctnt.-gen.'s dept., July, 1892; elk., controller's office, Mar., 1898; supt. of registtn., G.P.O., Singapore, May, 1903; ag. supt. of mails, Oct., 1903.

WHITEHOUSE, ALFRED ASHMALL.—Consular agt., Niger Coast Protectorate, Oct., 1894; dist. comsnr., 1896; served with Benin City expdn., 1897 (medal and clasp); received thanks of S. of S. for work in Kwa Ibo country, 1897; ditto, for work along German bndry., and in Ekoi country, 1899; trav. comm., S. Nigeria, 1900; polit. offr. with expdn., in Ubinni country, 1901; served with Aro expdn., 1901-2 (medal and clasp); polit. offr. with expdn. against Bibi-Kala, 1903; polit. offr. Ibibio expdn., 1904; has acted on several occasions as vice-consul and divisional commr.

WHITETWAY, THE RT. HON. SIR WILLIAM V., P.C., K.C.M.G. (1880).—B. 1828; atty.-gen. and premier of Newfd., 1878 to 1884, and again 1889; offl. deleg. to London on Fisheries question, 1890; deleg. from legislature on same question, 1891; resig. with his ministry, Apr., 1894; again took office Feb., 1895; represented the col. in London at H.M.'s Diamond Jubilee; resig., 1897.
WHITFIELD, H. S.—Clk. and examnr., audit dept., W. Australia, 1886; ch. clk., Aug., 1891.
WHITING, JOHN BEEBY.—Entd. public serv., S. Australia, 1873; sec., state children's dept., 1886; sec. and registr. of stock in ag.-gen.'s dept., London, 1903.

WHYHAM, WILLIAM HENRY.—Entered R.N., 1864; ret., July, 1873, and joined the Leeward Is. colonial yacht "Union"; dist. mag., Dominica, Nov., 1874; nominated mem. of legis. assem., Mar., 1877; dist. mag. and inspr. of prisons, Antigua, Feb., 1878; ag. pres. of Nevis, 1882; hon. commr. for Antigua at Col. and Ind. Exhibn., 1886; mem. legis. coun., Antigua, 1887; ag.-pres., Dominica, Apr. to Nov., 1889; escheator-gen., 1890; ag. commr., Montserrat, May to Sept., 1894; May to June, 1898; pres. legis. coun., and mem. exec. coun., Dec., 1895; ag. admstr., Dominica, July, 1896, to Feb., 1897, and Apr. to Sept., 1899; visiting just. gaol, 1896; offl. mem. legis. coun., July, 1898; non-elective mem. legis. coun., L. Is., Dec., 1898; ag. admstr., Dominica, July to Nov., 1900; ag. aud.-gen., L. Is., Nov., 1899, to May, 1900; June-July, 1900; Nov., 1900, to Mar., 1901; apptd. mem. exec. coun., L. Is., 1st Mar., 1902.
WHYTE, J. W.—Solr. to lands titles commrs., Tasmania, 1888; recorder of titles; regis. of public trusts, collr. of stamp duties, and dep. regis. of deeds, 1884.

WIDDUP, JOHN CHAS. PONSONBY.—L.R.C.S., 1869; L.M., and L.R.C.P., 1870; supernum. med. offr., Br. Guiana med. ser., 1881; govt. med. offr., Caye dist., 1886; health offr., pol., prison, and port surg., New Amsterdam, 1889; mem. Br. med. assoc.

WIGLEY, F. S.—Mem. of assem., St. Kitts, Mar., 1886; notary public, Aug., 1886; called to the bar, June, 1887; master and examr. in chancery, Oct., 1887; ag. pol. mag. and coroner for the Basseterre dist., in 1870 and 1872; ag. atty.-gen. in 1870 and 1872; mem. of the exec. coun., Oct., 1870; visiting just. of the gaol, Apr., 1872; nominated mem. of the legis. assem., May, 1873; dist. mag. for dist. C, 1877; offl. mem. legis. coun., Jan., 1879; now mag. and coroner, dist. C; ag. pres., 1885-86; ag. pres., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1885, 1886, 1887, 1888; mem. exec. coun., 1893; ag. commr., 1893 and 1895; ag. admstr., 1898, 1899, and 1900; ditto (under dormant commr.), 1901-1902.
WILBRAHAM, D. F., B.A. (Oxon.)—Master sup. ct. and registr.-gen., S. Leone, 1896; ag. ch. just., June to Nov., 1902; also ag. atty.-gen., May to Oct., 1902.
WILHELM, THOS. A.—Entered col. service, S. Leone, 1878; chief draftsman, survey dept., Mar., 1896.
WILKIE, DAVID, F.I.P.S.—Ed. at Lincoln middle schl., and Chr. hosp., London; served in S. African campaign, 1900-1; corporal, S.A.C., 1903; clerk to lieut.-gov., O.R.C., 1903.

WILKINSON, DAVID.—Ch. interpreter of Fiji, 10th Oct., 1874, to Sept., 1875; sec. to land claims comm., Dec., 1876; native commr., Nov., 1876; mem. native registr. bd., 1877; accompanied Dep. High Commr. Maudelay as special political agt. to king and chiefs of Tonga, 1878; accompanied Sir A. Gordon to Tonga, 1879; resig., 1882; commr. native lands, Fiji, June, 1894.

WILKINSON, ERNEST EDWARD.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk., 2nd div., civ. ser., and assigned to the bd. of agriculture, May, 1894; transf. to C.O., June, 1896; temp. staff offr., ch. clk.'s dept., 1902.

WILKINSON, ERNEST FRED. WHALLEY.—C.E., demonstrator and lecturer in engineering, Mason Coll., Birmingham; ch. asst. engnr., Hull corpnr.; ch. engnr., P.W.D., Siam; apptd. sen. supt. of wks., G. Coast, July, 1897; apptd. ag. 1st asst. dir. of wks., Nov., 1897, to May, 1898; ag. dir. pub. wks., Dec., 1899, to June, 1900; extraordinary M.L.C., Feb., 1900; ex. mem., exec. coun., Feb., 1900; apptd. asst. dir. pub. wks., Oct., 1899; ag. dir. pub. wks., June, 1901, to Mar., 1902.

WILKINSON, J. E.—Clk., stores acctng. dept., Cape govt. rlya., Jan., 1901; joined impl. mil. authorities, 30th Apl., 1901; clk. and typist, land stimlt. dept., O.R.C., Jan., 1902; asst. acctnt., 1902; examnr. of accts., audit dept., 1st May, 1903.

WILKINSON, RICHARD JAMES.—Ed. Trin. Coll., Camb. (exhibitioner); cadet, S.S., Oct., 1899; passed final exam. in Malay, July, 1891; ag. dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, Dec., 1890, to Aug., 1891; ditto, Nibong Tebal, June to Aug., 1892; ag. asst. postmr.-gen., Dec., 1892, to Mar., 1893; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Sept., 1893; ag. sheriff, Singapore, Sept., 1894, to May, 1895; ag. asst. audr.-gen., July, 1895; ag. collr. land rev., Singapore, Mar., 1896; 3rd mag., Singapore, Mar. to Apr., 1896; ag. collr. land rev., Penang, Apr., 1896; also ag. supt. of educm., Penang, July, 1896; ag. 2nd mag., Singapore, Apr., 1897; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of coun., July, 1897; ag. dist. offr., P.W., Aug., 1897; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, Apr., 1898; ag. inspr. of schl., S. Titlima, Aug., 1898; apptd. asst. postmr.-gen., Penang, but still ag. inspr. of schl., Jan., 1899; ag. supt. educm., Penang, Aug., 1900; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Sept., 1900, to Jan., 1901; dist. offr., Dindings, Jan., 1902; ag. 2nd mag., Sing., Apl., 1903; inspr. of schl., F.M.S., Oct., 1903.

WILCOCKS, LIEUT.-COL. SIR JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1900, after relief of Coomassie), C.M.G. (1899); D.S.O.—B. 1857; Leinster regt., 1st commr., Jan., 1878; capt., 1884; major, 1893; tempy. lieut.-col., 1897; tempy. col., 1898; station staff offr., 1st clk., Bengal, July, 1890, to June, 1893; D.A.A.G., Bombay, Aug., 1894, to Nov., 1897; West African F.F., Nov., 1897. Active service: Afghan war, 1878-80, medal; Mahood Wuzereee expdn., 1881; Soudan expdn., 1885, Suakin, med. with clasp bronze star; Burmese expdn., 1885-89, as transport offr., and in charge of field commr., also acted as rd. commdt.; desig. Lon. Gaz., Sept., 2, 1887, med. with 2 clasps, D.S.O.; Chin-Lushai expdn., 1889-90, clasp; expdn. to Munipoor as transp. offr.; desig. Lon. Gaz., Aug. 14, 1891; N.W. frontier of India, 1897; A.A.G., Tochi field force, desig. Lon. Gaz., Feb. 11, 1898; 2nd in commd. W. African F.F., Nov., 1898; officiating commdt., Sept., 1898.
WILLETT, ARTHUR.—D.Sc. (Lond.); F.R.S.; Director of Colombo museum, Ceylon, 25th Apl., 1902.

WILLIAMS, ARTHUR HENRY.—Writer, crown lands office, W. Australia, 1879; 2nd clk., 1881; clk., gov.'s office, 1883; acted as priv. sec. to Sir Frederick Broome in 1883 and 1884; clk. to coun., May, 1884; ch. clk., P.O., 1889; ch. clk., premier's dept., 1900; clk. to exec. coun., 1902.

WILLIAMS, A. JAY.—Appkd. by F. O. Br. Cent. Africa Prot., 5th Jan., 1895; asst. sec. (new post), 20th Aug., 1897; ag. sec. to adminstr., May to Oct., 1897; and June, 1899, to Apr., 1901; sec. to adminstr., 1st Apr., 1901.

WILLIAMS, C. RIBY, C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1857; supervisor of customs, G. C. Col., 1884; on special mission to Aquamoo and Crepea, 1886; dist. commr., 1887; in charge Salagha recruiting expdn.; commr. with Tavievo expeditionary force, and on special mission to King of Buem, 1888; on special mission to E. Wassaw, 1889; controller of customs, Dec., 1890; on special mission to Krobo, 1891; ch. commr., Anglo-German bdry., commr., 1892; treasur., Nov., 1896; ag. col. sec., Dec., 1897.

WILLIAMS, GEOFFREY CHAMPNEY.—Ed. Rugby and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; grad. with hons., 1897; priv. sec., Barbados, 1898; attached to high commr.'s staff, Cape Town and Johannesburg, 1900-01; clk. to sec., O.R.C., admin., Feb., 1901; sec. for permits, Jan., 1902; clk. to col. sec., June, 1902; ch. clk. and editor, Agricultural Journal, July, 1903, and sec. agric. bd., Oct., 1903; also ag. regiar. of brands.

WILLIAMS, SIR HARTLEY, KT. BACH. (1894).—B. 1844; second son of Sir E. Eyre Williams, a former judge of Victoria; ed. at Repton and Trin. Coll., Oxon; B.A. 1865; called to the bar, In. Tem., 1867, and immediately went to Australia and commenced practice; judge of the sup. ct. of Victoria, 1881, and is now the sen. puisne judge; was a vice-pres. of the Victorian free educ. league; and is pres. of the Victorian league for promoting the opening of museums, etc., on Sundays; ret. on pension, 1896.

WILLIAMS, JAMES ALEXANDER, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1856; messenger and copyist, secretariat, Laogs, Dec., 1871; clk. and keeper of debtors' prison, June, 1875; 2nd clk., gov.'s office, G. Coast, Dec., 1875; ch. clk., 1877; clk., leg. coun., 1886; dist. commr., Dec., 1890.
WILLIAMS, JOHN, F.C.S.—Asst. analyst, Br Guiana, 1893.

WILLIAMS, JOSHUA STRANGE, M.A., L.L.M.—Ed. at Harrow and Trin. Coll., Camb.; 1st class in law tripes, 1858; jun. optime and chancellor's legal studies medallist, 1859; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, Nov., 1859; dist. land-regar., Canterbury, N.Z., 1871; regar.-gen. of land under the Land Transfer Act, 1873; puisne judge, sup. ct., Otago, Mar., 1875.
WILLIAMS, MYLES.—Clk. to res. commr., Bech. Prot., 1902.

WILLIAMS, RALPH CHAMPNEY, C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1848; ed. at Rossall; explored parts of Patagonia and Terra del Fuego, 1873-4; and Cent. Africa, 1883-4; head of civil intell. dept., Bechuanaland expdn., 1884; author of "The British Lion in Bechuanaland"; Br. consul. offr., S. African republic, 1887; Br. agt. there (with letter of credence), 1888; col. treas., and mem. of sanitary comm., Gibraltar, 1890; also contract offr., 1891; and also capt. of the port, 1895; recd. certif. and med. from the Italian govt. for services in connection with the wreck of the "Utopia," Mar. 17, 1891; col. sec., Barbados, 1897; res. commr., Bechuanaland Protec., 1901.
WILLIAMS, W. R.—Med. offr., Dist. C., Dominica, Jan., 1881.
WILLIAMSON, G. ALEXANDER.—M.A., M.B., M.D., Aberd. univ.; dist. med. offr., Cyprus, 1896.

WILLIAMSON, VICTOR ALEXANDER, C.M.G. (1882).—Ed. at Westminster, and Chr. Ch., Oxford, of which he is M.A.; barrister-at-law of the In. Tem., and goes the N.-E. circuit; one of the royal commrs. who in 1872 and 1873 inquired into the treatment of Indian imigrts. in Mauritius; served for more than two years in Fiji without remuneration as chinm. of commr. for settling titles to land; was mem. of exec. and legis. coun., 1879-81; sec. to roy. commr. on London bd. of wks., 1888.

WILLIS, JOHN CHRISTOPHER, M.A.—Gonville and Caius Coll., Camb.; held for 3 yrs. from 1890 the Frank Stuart studentship for botan. research; was sen. asst. to regius prof. of botany, Glasgow Univ., and lecturer in botany, Queen Margaret's Coll., Glasgow; dir. of royal bot. gardens, Ceylon, Aug., 1896; on special duty to Fed. Malay States, Mar., 1904; author of "A Manual Dictionary of Flowering Plants and Ferns," and numerous scientific and economic papers; editor of "Annals of the Royal Botanical Gardens, Peradeniya," and of "Circulars and Agricultural Journal of Royal Botanical Gardens, Ceylon."

WILMOT, ROBERT DUNCAN.—Mem. of exec. coun., N. Brunswick, from 1861 to 1854, and from 1856 to 1857; again in his own govt. from 1866 until the union; survr.-gen. from 1851 to 1854; prov. sec. from 1856 to 1867; mayor of St. John, 1849; deleg. to confederate coun. of trade, Quebec, 1865; and to the col. confere., for the union of the B.N.A. cols., London, 1866-7; commr. on behalf of Canada to the Philadelphia Exhibn., 1875; sat for St. John (city and co.) in N. B. assem. from 1846 until 1861, and from 1865 until the union; called to the senate, May, 1867; speaker of the senate, 1878; lieut.-gov., N. B., 1880 to 1885.
WILSON, A.—Engr. in charge of ports and harbours, and imigrn. agt., Victoria, July, 1882.

WILSON, SIR DAVID, K.C.M.G. (1890), C.M.G. (1891), V.D. (1896).—B. 1838; ed. Trin. Coll., Glenalmond; home civ. serv., 1866-61; priv. sec. to lieut.-gov. of N. Brunswick, 1861-6; special commr. to report upon the salmon fisheries of N.B.; capt. and adjt. 1st York militia, N.B., 1863; also at camp of instruction, 1865-6; major, St. John volr. batt. when on service on the American frontier, 1866; priv. sec. to gov. of Trinidad, 1866-9; supt. of prisons, 1869-70; stip. mag. E. dist. of St. George, 1870-8; ag. S.J.P., Port of Spain and W. dist. of St. George, Sept., 1873-4; commr. of the N. prov. and sub-intendant of crown lands, 1878 to 1897; mem. of exec. coun., 1894; ag. col. sec., Trinidad, various dates from Apr., 1876, to Jan., 1897; raised and organised Trinidad volr. force, 1879 to 1890; col. comdg. L.I. volr., 1890-7, and is now hon. col.; gov. of Br. Honduras, Jan., 1897; ret. May, 1903.

WILSON, G. GORDON.—Cadet, S.S., Nov., 1899; ag. 4th mag., Sing., Apr., 1902; passed final exam. in Chinese, Aug., 1902; ag. supt., money order branch and savings bank, Singapore, May, 1903; ag. asst. postm.-gen., Penang, 2nd Oct., 1903; supt., money order branch and sav. bank, Sing., but cont. to act in Penang.

WILSON, HENRY FRANCIS, C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1859; ed. Rugby and Trin. Coll., Camb.; foundation scholar, 1878; sen. Bell's Univ. scholar, 1879; Chancellor's medal for English verse, 1890; 6th in 1st-class classical tripes, and distinguished in exam. for Chancellor's class, med., 1883; fellow of Trin. Coll., 1884; M.A., 1886; called to the bar (Lincoln's Inn), 1888; sec. to Trinidad judicial enquiry comm., 1892; sent to Malta to collect evidence with the Protestant communities in connection with the Marriages Case, 1893; priv. sec. to the Rt. Hon. J. Chamberlain, M.P., S. of S. for cols., June, 1895; legal ast. in the C.O., 1897; went to S. Africa as legal ast. on high comsnr.'s staff, Feb., 1900; sec. to administration of Orange River Colony, 1901; col. sec., June, 1902; acted as lieut.-gov., Apl. to Aug., 1903.

WILSON, LESLIE ORME.—R.M.L.I.; joined Royal Marines as 2nd lieut., 1st Feb., 1896; lieut., 1st Jan., 1896; capt., 1st Apl., 1901; served in H.M.S. "Hibernia" and H.M.S. "Australia"; ast. instructor of musketry, Gravesend, 1901; served with naval brigade in South Africa, 1899-1900 (ment. in desp., D.S.O., medal with five clasps); A.D.C. to gov. of N. S. Wales, 12th July, 1903.

WILSON, T.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in the lower (now 2nd) div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the G.P.O., Apr., 1882; transf'd to the C.O., Dec., 1883; ast. regis., July, 1896; higher grade, Jan., 1898.

WILSON, T. R.—Ck. to comsnr. of crown lands, co. of Bourke, Victoria, Dec., 1852; ch. clk., med. dept., Mar., 1855; sec. to cent. board of health, Aug., 1865; ch. clk., ch. sec.'s office, Jan., 1881; under-sec. Victoria, 1881; J.P., 1883; hon. mem., Br. Med. Assoc., 1890.

WILSON, HON. WALTER HORATIO, C.M.G., M.L.C., Queensland.—Born Ruabon, N. Wales, 1839; ed. Ellesmere, Shrop.; arrived in Victoria, 1853; solr. sup. ct. 1863; admitted in Queensland, 1865; called to legis. coun., 1885; postmnr.-gen. in ministry of Sir W. Griffith; leadership of legis. coun. without portfolio in the Griffith-McIlwraith govt., Aug., 1890; postmnr.-gen. and sec. of pub. instruc., May, 1893; continued as such in the Nelson ministry, to Oct., 1894; acted as asty.-gen., Mar., 1897, to Jan., 1898; retained position of postmnr.-gen. in the premiership of the Hon. T. J. Byrnes; min. of just. and postmnr.-gen. in the ministry of the Hon. J. R. Dickson; subsequently min. of educ.

WINDHAM, WM.—Ck., res. comsnr.'s office, Zulu native reserve, Apr., 1883; studt. interp. native affairs office, Natal, Mar., 1884; clk. and regis. to special comsnr. for Zulu affairs, Oct., 1889; clk. and interp. to res. comsnr. and ch. mag., Zululand, June, 1887; sec. to ditto, Jan., 1889; clk., exec. coun., Natal, Sept., 1889; priv. sec. to gov., Natal, Oct., 1889; sec. for Zululand, Dec., 1889; priv. sec. to gov., Zululand, Feb., 1890; gov't. sec., Zululand, Feb., 1894; ast. under-sec. for Zululand affairs, 1897; regis. of deeds and regis.-gen., Natal, Mar., 1898; (compiled abstract of opinions and court decisions upon transactions in deeds office, 1878-1898); sec. to native affairs dept., Transvaal, 23rd Sept., 1901.

WINGFIELD, SIR EDWARD, K.C.B. (1899), C.B. (1889).—B. 1834; ed. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford, B.C.L. and M.A. (Oxon); called to the bar by the Soc. of Lincoln's Inn, 10th June, 1859; went the home circuit; apptd. ast. under-S. of S. for Cols., 19th July, 1878; permanent under-S. of S., 1st Mar., 1897, to 28th Feb., 1900; sec. to Order of St. Michael and St. George, 1897-1900.

WINGFIELD, MAURICE E.—Ed. at Winchester and Trin. Coll. Camb.; M.A. 1896; sec. to royal comsnr., hut tax, S. Leone, 1898; ag. treas., collr. of cust., postmnr., and mem. ex. and leg. couns., Falkland Is., 1899-1900; priv. sec. to gov., of Queensland, 1902; priv. sec. to gov.-gen. of Australia, 1903.

WINKFIELD, JOHN, M.A. (Oxon) (Worcester Coll.)—Called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, Jan., 1889; dist. comsnr., Lagos, 1896; ag. Queen's advocate, July, 1898, to May, 1899; rly. comsnr. Aro, Jan.-July, 1900; ag. asty.-gen., July-Sept., 1900; asty.-gen., So. Nig., Dec., 1900; compiled laws of So. Nig., 1900-1901.
WINSTON, J. H.—Sub-inspr. of roads, Dominica, Mar., 1874; inspr. of roads and ranger of crown lands, Sept., 1882; dist. gov't. offr.

WINTER, SIR FRANCIS PRATT, KT. (1900), C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1848; ag. asty.-gen., Fiji, 1887-88; ch. judicial offr. and mem. exec. and legis. couns., Br. N. Guinea, 1888; ag. admistr., Sept., 1894, to June, 1895, and Oct., 1898, to Mar., 1899; ret., 1903.

WINTER, SIR J. S., K.C.M.G. (1888), K.C.—B. 1845; speaker, house of assem., Newfoundland, 1877-8; mem. exec. coun., Feb., 1879; solr.-gen., Oct., 1882; asty.-gen., 1886; agt. for Newfoundland at fishery confce., Washington, 1887; resig. office of asty.-gen., 1889; puiana judge, 1893; resig. 1896; prime min., 1897-1900; asty.-gen., 1897; mem. of delegn. to imperial gov't. re French treaties and other matters, 1898; min. of just. and mem. of treasy. bd., 1898; high comsnr. to internat. confce. between Gt. Brit. and U.S.A. at Quebec and Washington, 1898.

WISE, ALFRED GASCOYNE, LL.B.—Ed. at Repton, at Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn, Jan., 1878; elk. to ch. just. at Hong Kong, 1882 (resig., 1883); pol. mag., Jan., 1884; ag. regis. of sup. ct., official trustee, regis. of companies, and land offr., Mar., 1886; regis., sup. ct., 1892; ag. puiana judge, Apr., 1889, to Sept., 1890; ag. asty.-gen., Apr. to Aug., 1890; puiana judge, Hong Kong, 1895; ag. ch. just., June, 1901, to Mar., 1902; ag. asty.-gen., Apr. to June, 1902; chairman, squatter's board, Aug., 1902.
WISE, BERNHARD RINGROVE.—Atty.-gen., N.S. Wales, May, 1887, to Feb., 1888.
WITHERS, A. K.—Ed. at Bedford schl.; served in survey dept. under Gov't. of Egypt; confdl. clk. to admstr. of Gambia, 1897.

WITTEENOOM, HON. SIR E. H., K.C.M.G. (1900).—B. 1854; elected M.L.C., W. Australia, 1883; resig. in Dec.; re-elected 1884; resig. 1886; elected, M.L.C., July, 1894; min. for mines, Dec., 1894; agent-gen., July, 1898; resigned, 1901.

WOODEHOUSE, CLARENCE B.—Entd. Sandhurst Coll., 1876; gazetted to 77th Regt., 1876; resigned coman., 1880; local commdt. of police, Cyprus, 1st Jan., 1894; ag. coman., Limassol, 12th July, 1894; ditto, Papho, 1st Apl., 1895; comanr. Papho, 7th June, 1895.

WOODEHOUSE, H. E., C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1845; ed. Repton Schl.; Hong Kong cadet, 1867; student interp., 1869; sup. of Chinese pol., 1870; astt. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1875; pol. mag., 1880; hon. sec. to the local coman. in connection with the Indian and Col. Exhibn., and special comanr. in London, 1886; mem. exec. coun., May, 1895; retired.

WOODEHOUSE, P. P. J.—Ed. Eliz. Coll., Guernsey; first clk., reg.-gen.'s dept., Hong Kong, 1897-1900; passed in Chinese, 1899; in charge of census, Dec., 1900; astt. supt. of police, 1901; attached Punjab pol., 1901-2; passed in Urdu, 1902; J.P., 1902.

WOLFE, E. D. C.—Tonbridge Schl. (1890-94); exhib. in mod. langs., Caius Coll., Camb. (1894); exhib. from Tonbridge Schl. (1894); 3rd class mod. langs., tripos. (1896); 1st class Pt. I, and 2nd class Pt. II, Law Special (1897); B.A. (1897); cadet, F.M.S., 1898; sent to Canton to study Cantonese (1899); transf. to Hong Kong, 1901; passed cadet, Sept., 1901; registr., land ct., Oct., 1901; ag. 2nd pol. mag., Oct., 1902; ag. astt. reglar-gen., Nov., 1902; sec. to pub. wks. enquiry coman., 1902; collr. of crown rent, New Territory, Feb., 1903; registr., land ct., Mar., 1903; ag. pol. mag., N.T.; and astt. supt. of pol., 1903-4; collr. of crown rent, N.T., 1904.

WOLFERTSTAN, LITTLETON EDWARD PIPE.—Ed. Eton, and Clare Coll., Camb.; B.A. 1888; cadet, S.S., Oct., 1889; passed final exam. in Malay, July, 1891; ag. priv. sec. to Sir C. Smith, Jan. to Apr., 1890; ag. dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, Feb. to Mar., and June to Nov., 1892; ag. dist. offr., S.W. dist., Penang, Mar., 1893; 3rd mag., Penang, Apr., 1895; ag. dist. offr., prov. Wellesley, June, 1897; ag. 2nd mag., Singapore Aug., 1897; sheriff and dep. registr., sup. et., Singapore, 1898, but still ag. 2nd mag., Singapore; ag. dist. offr. Dindings, Feb., 1900; ag. collr. ld. rev., Penang, May, 1900; ag. sec. to H. Cr. for Fed. Malay States, May, 1901; ag. sen. dist. offr. prov. Wellesley, May, 1902; collector of land rev. and registr. of deeds, Penang, Aug., 1902; offl. assignee, S.S., Sept., 1903.

WOLFF, Rt. Hon. Sir Henry Drummond, P.C. (1885), G.C.B., G.C.M.G. (1878); K.C.B. (1879); K.C.M.G.(1862).—B. 1830; entered the foreign service in 1846; was attached, 1852, to H.M.'s legation at Florence, where he served for a short time as ag. chargé d'affaires; attached, 1856, to the Earl of Westmorland's special mission to Brussels; astt. priv. secretary to the Earl of Malmesbury, Feb., 1858, S. of S. for foreign affairs; in Nov., priv. sec. to the Rt. Hon. Sir E. B. Lytton, S. of S. for cols.; C.M.G. in Apr., 1859, and hon. king-at-arms of the Order of St. M. and St. G.; public sec. to the lord high comanr. of the Ionian Islands, June, 1859; in the same year a mem. of a coman. to inquire into the state of pub. depts.; in 1860, mem. of a coman. to inquire and report on state of Ionian finances; represented Ionian govt. at the internat. statistical congress, London, 1860; was apptd., 1861, by the Ionian govt., vice-pres. of a coman. to organise, in 1862, an exhibn. of the works of Ionian art and industry, and in the same year mem. of a coman. to inquire into and prepare a project of law for regulating system of public instruction in the Ionian Is.; comanr. for the Ionian Is. internat. exhibn., 1862; unsuccessfully contested Christchurch, 1863; elected for that borough, 1874; apptd. in 1878 to be H.M.'s comanr. on the European coman. for the organisation of E. Roumelia; mem. for Portsmouth, 1880 to 1885; was apptd. envoy extraord. and min. plenipio. to the Sultan on a special mission, with particular reference to affairs of Egypt, 3rd Aug., 1886; high comanr. in Egypt, 1885-87; min. at Teheran, 1888; ambassador Madrid, 1892-1900.

WOLHUTER, C. P.—Ck. to the registr. of the sup. ct., Natal, Nov., 1864; acted on several occasions as registr.; 2nd ck., registr. of deeds office, Feb., 1876; ck. to res. mag., Pietermaritzburg, city div., May, 1881.

WOLLASTON, H. N. P., I.S.O. (1903), LL.D., J.P.—B. 1846; apptd. to dept. of trade and customs (after exam.), 1863; dep. registr. of shipping, 1886; grad., with honours, inlaw, Melb. Univ., 1884; called to bar same year; promoted to be ch. ck., customs dept., 1889; is also paid counsel to marine board of Victoria; apptd. permanent head of Victorian dept. of trade and customs, 1891; apptd. comp. gen. of commonwealth dept. of trade and customs, and permanent head, 1901.

WOLSELEY, FIELD-MARSHAL VISCOUNT (created 1885), SIR GARNET JOSEPH WOLSELEY, G.C.B. (1880), K.C.M.G. (1870), G.C.M.G. and K.C.B. (1874), BARON (1882), K.P. (1885).—B. 1833; ensign, Mar. 1852; capt., Jan., 1855; major, 90th foot, Mar., 1858; lieut.-col., Apr., 1859; col., June, 1865; maj.-gen., 1874; lieut.-gen., Apr., 1878; gen., 1882; field-marshal, 1895; served with 80th foot in the Burmese war of 1852-53, severely wounded (medal); served with distinction in the Crimea and taking of Sebastopol, with the 90th L.I., and was severely wounded in a sortie, after which he received the Legion of Honour, the 5th class of the Medjideh, etc.; was also at the siege and capture of Lucknow and defence of Alumbagh, and numerous actions in Oudh, when he was made brevet lieut.-col.; served in China on the staff of the qrtmr.-gen. throughout the Chinese campaign of 1860, for which he also received a medal and two clasps; dep. qrtmr.-gen. in Canada, Oct., 1867; astt. adjt.-gen. at headqrs., 1871; apptd. for special service on the G. Coast in connection with the Ashantee war, Aug., 1873; brought the Ashantee war to a successful conclusion, and received the thanks of the House of Lords and the House of Commons for his services; was made a G.C.M.G. and a K.C.B., and a major-gen.; and inspr.-gen. of auxiliary forces, Apr., 1874; specially apptd. to administer temporarily the govt. of Natal from Feb. to Sept., 1875; acted as vice-pres. of the London S. African confce., Aug., 1876; mem. of the Indian coun., 1876; comanr. and comdr.-in-ch. Cyprus, July, 1878; gov. and comdr.-in-ch. Natal, and of the Transvaal, and comdr. of the forces in S. Africa, May, 1879, and high comanr. for S. E. Africa; qrtmr.-gen., Apr., 1880; adjt.-gen., Mar., 1882; commanded the forces in Egypt, with the temporary rank of gen., in 1882, and received the thanks of both houses of parlmt., and a peacage; and from the Khedive of Egypt the Grand Order of the Osmanich; commanded the Nile expdn. and other operations in Egypt and the Soudan for the relief of Gen. Gordon, 1884-5; received the thanks of both houses of parlmt.; was raised to the rank of viscount, and made K.P.; is author of "The Soldier's Pocket Book for Field Service;" comdr.-in-ch. in Ireland, 1890; comdr.-in-ch., 1895-1900.

WOOD, DAVID.—Ed. Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen; ch. acctnt., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1891; supt. of accts., corres., and stores, P.W.D., 1894; was sec. to coman. on housing the Chinese during the plague, 1894; ag. govt. assessor of taxes, July, 1900, to Nov., 1901, and from May to Oct., 1902.

WOOD, FIELD-MARSHAL SIR HENRY EVELYN, V.C., G.C.B., G.C.M.G. (1882).—B. 1838; entered the navy 1852, and the army, 1855; served in the Crimea; received the V.C. for his gallantry in hunting down the rebels in the Seronge jungles; admstd. the govt. of Natal, from Feb. to Dec., 1881; commanded the forces in Natal and the Transval, and was a mem. of the roy. coman. for the settlement of the affairs of the Transvaal, 1881, but dissented from the report; commanded the 2nd brigade, 2nd div., in Egypt, 1882; apptd. by the Khedive to the command of the Egyptian troops, 1883; served in the Nile expdn., 1884; comdr. of the E. dist., 1886; qrtmr.-gen. of the forces Oct., 1893; apptd. to command of army corps, 1901.

WOOD, JOHN R.—Ed. City of Lond. schl. and Camb.; foundation scholar of Jesus Coll., 1886; 1st class clas. tripus, 1899; B.A., 1899; cadet Hong Kong, 1899; registr. of land ct., Nov., 1900; ag. pol. mag. in New Territories, May to Jul., 1902; J.P., 1902; mem. land ct., 1903-4.
WOOD, JOHN T.—M.A. (Oxon); F.R.G.S.; registrar of mining rights, Transvaal.

WOODCOCK, GEORGE ALBERT, A.C.P.—Ed. at St. John's Coll., Battersea; apptd. astt. master Victoria Coll., Hong Kong, 15th Mar., 1890; final bar, Gray's Inn, 1897; ag. sec., sanitary bd., Mar., 1900, to July, 1901; sec. of san. bd., July, 1901.

WOODE, BENJAMIN TREGASKIS, M.R.P.S.—Ed. at Wesleyan High school, Freetown, S. Leone; jun. clk., Queen's advocate off., S. Leone, Apr., 1890, to July, 1891; cust. offr., Forcados, Benin and Warri dists., in Oil Rivers Prot., Aug., 1891, to July, 1892; cust. offr., Bonny and New Calabar dists., July, 1892, to Aug., 1892; postmnr., Bonny, N.C.P., from Aug., 1892, to Nov., 1894; beachmaster, storekeeper, and supervisor of workmen at Bonny and Cawthorne channel, Jan., 1893, to Nov., 1894; transf'd to Old Calabar, to take up duty there, Mar., 1895; postmnr., Brass, May, 1895, to Apr., 1899; postmnr., Opobo, Apr., 1899, to Apr., 1901; postmnr., Bonny, from May, 1901; postmnr., Opobo, Dec., 1901.
WOODFORD, C. M.—Acted as Br. consul at Apia; Br. resident Solomon Islands, 1897.
WOODFORD, WILLIAM.—M.H.A., Harbor Main, Newfd., since 1889; finan. sec., 1894: chmn. B.W., 1897; min. P.W., 1898.

WOODMAN, LEONARD CECIL.—Ast. dist. comsnr., Niger Coast Prot., Aug., 1898; ag. res., Benin City, July, 1900; dist. comsnr., Jan., 1901; ag. div. comsnr., Eastern div., 1903; ditto, Western Div., 1904.
WOODS, HENRY J. B.—Mem. exec. coun. and survr.-gen., Newfoundland, 1889-1893.
WOODS, W. W.—Ed. St. John's schl., Leatherhead, and Oxford, B.A. (1901); clk. col. audit branch, E. and A. dept., Somerset House, 2nd Sept., 1901; astt. auditor, G Coast, 22nd Feb., 1902.

WOODWARD, LIONEL MABBOT.—Ed. at Harrow, and Trin. Coll., Camb. (scholar); 1st class honours, classical triposes; B.A., 1886; M.A., 1892; cadet, Straits, 1888; passed final exam. in Tamil, July, 1890; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, Nov., 1890; ag. dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, Apr., 1891; 3rd mag., Penang, Apr., 1891; 3rd mag. and astt. Indian imigrn. agt., Singapore, July, 1892; ag. dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, Mar., 1893; sheriff and dep. registr. sup. ct., Singapore, Mar., 1896; 2nd mag., Penang, July, 1898; ag. offl. assignee, S. Stiltna, and registr. of deeds, Apr., 1898, to June, 1899; ag. gen. dist. offr., Prov. Well., Oct., 1900; astt. regiar., supreme court, Penang, May, 1902; ag. solr.-gen., May, 1903, dep. pub. proser., Mar., 1904.

WRAGG, SIR W. T., KT. BACH (1891), M.A., D.C.L., Oxon (1894)—B. 1842; open classical exhibn., Linc. Coll., Oxon, Apr., 1863, and open classical scholarship at Worcester Coll., May, 1863; B.A., 1stclass honours, 1867; mem. of the In. Tem., Apr., 1866; writer in the Ceylon civ. ser., Jan., 1868; comsnr., of requests, Harrispattu, July, 1868; in charge of the office of the dir. of pub. instruction, Mar., 1869; pol. mag., Colombo, July, 1869; pol. mag., Panwila, 1869; pol. mag. of Gampola, Pussellawa, and Nawalapitiya, 1871; dist. judge, Chilaw, June, 1872; called to the bar by the In. Tem., Jan., 1879; ag. dist. judge, Kurunegala, June, 1879; ditto, Badulla, 1879; puisne judge, sup. ct., Natal, July, 1883; pres. of comn. to inquire into the working of the Ind. imigrn. laws in Natal, 1886-7; pres. of special judicial comn. for trial of Zulu chiefs, 1888-9; ret., 1898.

WRAGGE, CLEMENT L., F.R.G.S., F.R. Met. Soc.—Born 1852; ed. at Uttoxeter gram. schl., and in London as a solr.; went to Adelaide in 1876, and was engaged in the survr.-gen.'s dept. on surveys in the Flinders Ranges and the Murray Scrub; gathered a large collection of specimens in ethnography, conchology, geology, etc., which he presented to the town of Stafford, where it is known as the Wrage museum; devoted his attention to meteorology, and established three observatories in the Churnet Valley and moorlands of N. Staffs., chiefly to investigate problems in climatology; in 1881-2 established and worked the first observatory on summit of Ben Nevis, 4,406 feet high, and a station in connection at Fort William; in Jan., 1884, he established the Torrens Observatory at Wakerville, near Adelaide, and in Oct., another on the summit of Mount Lofty, S. Australia; apptd. meteorl. observer of Queensland, Jan., 1887.

WRAY, CECIL, F.G.S., M.R.P.S.—Local corres., Anth. inst.; entered pub. wks. dept., Perak, Oct., 1881; ag. ch. clk. of treasy., May, 1882; astt. to mag. and collr., Kinta dist., 1882; ag. collr. and mag. B. Padang, 1886; collr. and mag., Batang Padang dist., 1887; ag. supt., Lower Perak, Aug., 1889; ag. mag. and collr., Kinta, Apr., 1892; mag. and collr., Krian, June, 1892; ag. dist. mag., Lower Perak, Dec., 1894; ag. dist. mag., Kinta, Apr., 1896; ag. sen. mag., Perak, Apr., 1897; ag. chief mag., Selangor, Sep., 1897; ch. mag., Selangor, Apr., 1899; dist. mag., Kinta, Jan. 1900; dist. offr., Kinta, Jan., 1903; ag. British res., Pahang, 3rd Mar., 1904.

WRAY, CHARLES JAMES HILL, L.R.C.F., L.R.C.S. (Edin.).—Surgeon, imigrn. service, Queensland, 1876; health offr., Brisbane, 1879; med. offr. penal establt., St. Helena, and boys' reformatory, Lytton, and visiting surg. benevolent asylum, Dunwich, 1879; inspr. of orphanages, 1882; sec. to central bd. of health, 1884.

WRAY, LEONARD, I.S.O. (1903), M.I.E.E., F.Z.S., M.R.P.S., cor. M.P.S., local corres. A.I., A.M.A.—B. 1852; entered pub. wks. dept., Perak govt. service, June, 1881; supt., Govt. Hill Garden, Larut, Jan., 1882; and curator, Perak state museum, Jan., 1883; also state geologist, Jan., 1890; dir. of museums, F.M.S., Mar., 1904.

WRENFORDSLEY, SIR HENRY THOMAS, KNT. (1883).—Ed. in France, and at Trin. Coll., Dub.; was a mem. of the old Norfolk circuit; dep. cty. ct. judge for the metropol. dists. of Marylebone, Brompton, and Brentford, 1876; 2nd puisne judge, Mauritius, 1877; procureur and advoc.-gen., 1878; ch. just., W. Australia, 1880; repres'tl. col. at intercoln. confce. of the Australian cols., held at Sydney, 1881; ch. just. of Fiji and ch. judicial comn. of the W. Pacific, Oct., 1882-3; admtd. govt. of W. Australia from Feb. to June, 1883; acted for some time as judge, sup. ct., Tasmania; puisne judge, Victoria, 1888; ch. just., Leeward Is., Oct., 1891; retired, 1901.
RIGHT, ARTHUR—Supt., printing dept., Niger Coast Prot. (now So. Nigeria), 1893.

RIGHT, G. H. BATESON.—Queen's Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1875; Denver theological schol.; Kenicott Hebrew schol., 1876; Houghton Syriac prize; Pusey and Ellerton Hebrew schol., 1877; M.A., 1878; B.A. and D.D., 1891; apptd. headmr. Queen's College, Hong-Kong, Nov., 1881; ag. inspr. of schla., Feb. to Dec., 1883; author of "Critical Translation of Book of Job;" "Was Israel ever in Egypt?" and various school books; J.P., 1886.

WRIGHT, Hon. J. Arthur, C.E., M.I.C.E., C.E.I.—Comsnr. of rly., dir. of pub. wks., and engnr.-in-ch., mem. of legis. coun. and exec. coun. of W. Australia, 1885-90; temporary collr. of internal and cust. revenue at Albany, W. Aust., Nov., 1897; ag. govt. res., Albany, Jan., 1898; govt. res., Apr., 1899.
WRIGHT, R. T.—Inspr. of pol., Br. Guiana, Mar., 1883; inspir. of pol. prot. of imgrts., and sec. to the bd. of health, Grenada, Apr., 1886; harbrmr., Georgetown, Br. Guiana, 1892.
WRIGHT, Wm.—Ck. to pub. trustee, S. Aust., 1885; ch. clk. to pub. trustee, and ch. clk. succession duties dept., 1891; pub. trustee and curator of convicts' estates, 1903.

WRIGHTSON, Walsh, C.M.G. (1900), M.I.C.E.—B. 1852; served articles with Chas. Fowler, Leeds; asst. to boro' engineer, Leeds, 1872; dist. engineer, P.W.D., Ceylon, 1875; seconded for serv. on Nanuoya Rly. extensions, 1883; spec. serv. to restore Kalawewa irrigation tank, 1884; on successful completion, promoted prov. engr. N.W. Prov., 1888; asst. to dir. pub. wks., 1892; prov. engr., E. Prov., 1894; dir. pub. wks. Trinidad, and offl. mem. legis. coun., Jan., 1895; apptd. mem. ex. coun., June, 1895; res. engr. harbour, water and sewerage wks., 1896.

WRIXON, The Hon. Sir Henry John, K.C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1839; atty.-gen., Victoria, Feb., 1886; resig., Nov., 1890; mem. of Federation Confce., 1891; represented Victoria at Col. Confce., 1894; author of "Socialism: Notes on a Political Tour."
WROUGHTON, Lewis.—Sub.-inspr. Basutoland mtd. pol., 1884; ag. asst. comsnr., Quthing, 1890; asst. comsnr., Basutoland, 1891; ag. govt. sec., 1901; govt. sec., 1902.

WURTELE, J. S. C., D.C.L.—Treas. of prov. of Quebec, Canada, 1882-3; speaker Quebec legis. assem., 1884 to 1886; judge of the sup. ct., 1886; offr. of the legion of honour, 1882; offr. of public instruction 1881; judge, ct. of appeal, Quebec, Sept., 1892.

WYATT, Robt.—Lond. Metro. pol., 1891 to 1895; B. Hond. pol., June, 1895; asst. supt., Feb., 1902; ag. supt., May, 1902, to May, 1903; ag. dist. comsnr., Orange Walk, July to Aug., 1903; J.P. for Colony; Hythe extra certif., 1899.
WYLIE, Thos. Alexander.—Ast. traffic supt., Ceylon rlyws., 17th May, 1888; acctnt., 11th May, 1894; now ast. gen. magr. and acctnt.

YOUL, Sir James Arndell, K.C.M.G. (1891), C.M.G. (1874).—B. 1810; a respected Australian colonist who, after a series of patient and very interesting experiments, succeeded in introducing salmon and trout into the rivers in Tasmania, and also made the first shipment of salmon eggs to Otago, N.Z.; received a vote of thanks from the prov. govt., accompanied by a handsome silver vase; political agt. for Tasmania, 1861-3; was seven years hon. sec. and treas. to the Australian Assoc., which succeeded in prevailing on the Imp. Govt. to establish a mail service to Australia, via Red Sea, and in getting the Australian sovereign made legal tender throughout the Br. dominions; ag. agr.-gen. for Tasmania, Feb. to Oct., 1888.

YOUNG, Alfred Karney.—B.A., Magd. Coll., Oxon.; called to the bar, In. Tem., May, 1889; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, Br. Honduras, Oct., 1890; ag. dist. mag., Toledo dist., Aug., 1891; priv. sec. to Sir A. Maloney, and clk. of councils, 1892; priv. sec. to admnstr. of the govt., 1895; ag. registr.-gen., 1895-7; dist. comsnr., Orange Walk, 1898; registr.-gen., June, 1898; ag. suptdt. of police, May to Nov., 1899; J.P. for col.; crown prosecutor, legal adviser, and police mag., Seychelles, 23rd Sept., 1903.

YOUNG, Capt. Arthur H., C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1864; late 27th Inniskillings; ed. at Edin. Acad., Rugby, and Sandhurst; local comdt., mil. pol. Kyrena, Aug., 1878; asst. comsnr., Paphos, Nov., 1878; comsnr., Paphos, Sept., 1879; ditto, Famagusta, Jan., 1882; dir. of survey and prin. forest offr., 1892; mem. legis. coun., 1892; ch. sec., 1894; admtd. the govt., July to Dec., 1895, Jan.-Apr., 1898; sent on special mission to St. Vincent in connection with relief work, Nov., 1902.

YOUNG, Roft. Heyden.—Sub-survr., survey dept., S. Sttlmts., 27th Jan., 1878; computor and draftsmen, Malacca, 1st May, 1881; dist. survr., Oct., 1882; ch. survr., 12th July, 1888; senior survr., Jan., 1891; in charge of re-survey of Singapore, 4th May, 1903.

YOUNG, William Douglas.—Ed. at Charterhouse; clk. in govt. sec.'s office, Br. Guiana, Jan., 1877; ch. clk., 1889; ag priv. sec. on several occasions, 1877-1893, to govs. of B. Guiana, Trinidad, and G. Coast; ag. ast. govt. sec., B Guiana, 1892-3-4-5; ag. govt. sec., Dec., 1892; ag. govt. agt. N.W. dist., 1895; ast. col. sec., Mauritius, 1895; ag. col. sec., 1896-7-8, 1900-01; admtd. govt., Mauritius, Aug., 1897; comsnr., Turks and Caicos Islands, 1901. W. F. STANLEY & Co., Ltd., Surveying and Drawing Contractors to H.M. Government, India, and the Colonies.

THEODOLITES, SEXTANTS, LEVELS, COMPASSES AND EVERY DESCRIPTION OF INSTRUMENT FOR FIELD AND OFFICE. Of the first quality, highest finish, and most moderate price. Manufactured largely by SELF-ACTING MACHINERY, in GUN METAL and ALUMINIUM ALLOYS.
